# Thread: accounta-freakin-bility
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/accounta-freakin-bility.1338/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2012-05-08T06:24:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/accounta-freakin-bility.1338/post-22129>

I'm not a fan of journaling. Even as a child I'd get a cool diary with a lock and write in it feverishly like Jan Brady then go back and read it years later and think that is some boring crap and throw it away.

I am going to give this a shot simply for the accounta-freakin-bility. It seems I need a bit more of that.

In March I did 5 weeks of the leptin rx and started CT- week 2-5. I hadn't felt that good in quite some time. I saw changes in my body so swiftly it reminded me of when I had tried (and failed) HCG. I stood in front of the mirror naked and staring at parts of my hips I swear it had been ages since I'd seen. My skin changed drastically. After a few weeks of being smelly and flaky with dry skin I emerged fresh as a daisy with tight pores. I looked like I had turned back the hands of time.

I can only imagine where I'd be right now if I hadn't succumbed to the girls weekend full of cinnamon rum and chocolate cake. Followed by the New Jersey Baptism weekend....Italian, catholic....need I say more?

No denying how good I felt. It's good to be back

### cantweight — 2012-05-08T16:42:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/accounta-freakin-bility.1338/post-22200>

Not really sure what I should be doing....leptin rx again? keto paleo? paleo?

Going to start with keto paleo. I guess the biggest difference will be timing. No morning rush to force down 50 grams of protein. Going to eat when I am hungry, keto foods, stick with 2 meals, and CT the hell out of this body.

This is the only place I don't feel like a freak for being excited about my baths! lol

# Thread: JohnnyB's Life Optimizing Goals
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### johnnyb — 2012-06-30T20:47:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-34642>

I never really introduced myself. I have experiences to share and questions to explore. I figure starting here will get this done even if only a little at a time....

A couple years ago I decided I was tired of being obese and didn't want to see my blood pressure slowly keep creeping up. I started bringing lunch to work but it was still SAD. That didn't work very well. I started low carb Atkins style. Joe Branca on this forum suggested Paleo eating and Mark's Daily Apple and other sites. Primal eating has been a very good thing. Later I made it here and to Leptin Rx and an optimal journey.

### johnnyb — 2012-07-05T03:43:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-35579>

I was diagnosed with obstructive sleep apnea a few years ago. Here are a few quotes from Dr. K's blogs...

> People with sleep apnea have some of the lowest levels of growth hormone measured and this helps partially explain why most of them have body composition issues.
>
> > The normal large circadian prolactin surge we should see at around midnight, after leptin enters the brain, does not happen if the patient has leptin resistance, sleep apnea, or has eaten food too close (within 3-4 hours) to bedtime
> > Cognitive decline is obvious with sleep deprivation, and learning is impaired. Sleep apnea is a known killer in humans and generally appears in those with obesity, again linking energy metabolism to sleep.
>
> Sometimes I think sleep should be levee #1 of the quilt and not #28. Of course, that's only because sleep issues are a huge mismatch for me. I'm surprised apnea doesn't come up more here.
>
>
>
> Would you know if you have apnea? I didn't. I was a snorer, but snoring may not mean anything, but apnea often means snoring. I am a slow, steady breather so "breathing pauses" are tuff to spot. Even now my CPAP can think I'm taking to long to breath even when I'm awake and fine relaxing as I go to sleep. I can pass an PulseOx test staying above 95% O2 saturation even with 60 apnea events an hour. Sleep Doc says my brain is very good at waking me up. DW says she never heard me make gasping for air sounds. It was the cognitive decline and general lack of patience and mental ability that drove me to the doctor. One major symptom I totally missed was waking at night. I started waking more nights then not. I easily dismissed this as a need to urinate or stress as my mind would start reeling with yesterdays problems. I often ended up sleeping in an easy chair downstairs. Within 2 days of CPAP treatment that stopped. I didn't realize how much sleep deprivation builds up over time. Bedtime meant nothing. I was usually stupid with the need for sleep long before bed. After sleeping well now I can pretty much spot a bad nights sleep before my head leaves the pillow in the morning.
>
>
>
> If you are leptin resistant for any reason, have sleep apnea, you will always have an altered body composition because of a low GH level and an altered sex steroid profiles on testing. The reason is because DHEA is the immediate precursor for those hormones and is always low in people with bad sleep efficiency. Most VLCers who are warm adapted face this very problem today.

This is a very interesting quote as well as the recent DHEA blog. I need to work out what to test and where to optimize next. I think I have DW on board for education consults for each of us. Too bad I didn't get her onboard last weekend for the 2 for 1 steal, I mean deal.

### chocolate — 2012-07-05T04:29:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-35581>

My apnea got better with the leptin reset and a mouthpiece. I bet the dhea would kick butt.

### johnnyb — 2012-07-05T05:28:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-35587>

I had lost a good 60lbs by the time of my last sleep Doc visit. They said keep up the good work. I may be a candidate for a mouthpiece next time. I was pretty disappointed to be told I had apnea from being fat and still having it after so much weight loss. My brother has OSD too. They visit later this month. I'm hoping to get him interested in a more optimal path.

When I was a child and got braces one orthodontist said my jaw needed to be pulled forward. My parents went with the one who only moved my teeth around. I can tell my airway is more open with my jaw forward even a little bit. I'm very hopeful a mouthpiece will work well.

### johnnyb — 2012-07-11T08:21:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-36763>

> **http://jackkruse.com/primal-cortisol-response wrote: You must â€œtightenâ€￾ your retinal exposure to sunlight, because this sharpens our brainâ€™s perception of our circadian cycle of melatonin in the pineal-thyroid neural pathways in the brain.\* When you pay attention to the AM light cycle, it helps tremendously optimizing your appropriate diurnal cortisol levels during the day, and minimizes cortisol production at night. It is critical to avoid living and working in semi-darkness during the day**.

I spend much of my time reading computer screens during the day. Until a few years ago my office was a cave with only a desk lamp on to keep glare down and monitor contrast up. We changed buildings and I scored a windowed office. Looking back that was probably another unintended brick in my road to optimal. Looks like it's time to brighten the office. I stopped wearing my hat for the walk to lunch. Time too lose the sun glasses too.

### kathylu — 2012-07-11T15:03:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-36787>

Thanks for digging up those quotes. I have OSA too, but I tend to ignore it because I adjusted fairly well to CPAP use. So, I generally feel rested in the morning, though not always. However, my hormone panel is s\*\*t and Dr. K thinks I would benefit from growth hormone injections. Trying to find an anti-aging doctor to get things started. Not trivial when you think about the cost involved and the lack of expertise out there....

### johnnyb — 2012-07-15T06:22:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-37986>

Chickens come from eggs and eggs come from chickens, but which came first. Classic grade school paradox.

We repeatedly do things because it's a habit, and it's a habit because we repeatedly do them. But how to form a new behavior? How to shed the Neolithic paths for optimal ones?

I decide to take a step like eating a brazil nut everyday. Pick up a bag. At one a day they go into the freezer for better keeping. I didn't eat one yesterday, so I forget to eat one today too. Repeat. I'm so clever I put several in my chocolate stash, so I'll grab one right after dinner. That's all good except I don't actually eat chocolate everyday. If only thoughts and intentions were actualizatons. I'm sure before long it will be an old habit too....

Maybe if I start eating mackerel smoothies.

### johnnyb — 2012-07-16T16:02:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-38338>

I didn't get the giveaway consult but I did sign up for the email consult and send in the labs I have and a medical history. I forgot to mention magnesium under on the supplement list. Funny how the mind works. Most supplements are taken with BAB or dinner. Magnesium I try to take avoiding a meal. I complete missed them. I probably should have mentioned increased sleep weirdness too. I keep waking up at 4 or 5 generally heading to bed asap after 10. I slightly increased the CPAP pressure in case its apnea events, not helping so far. I see this especially on nights I got to do a real CT session in the ocean.

### johnnyb — 2012-07-17T16:28:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-38707>

Leptin may be the one hormone to rule them all! but sleep is what I covet. Sleep, my precious, gollum gollum....

My brain ever mindful of the sting of sleep deprivation I value sleep like little else. My sleep, DW's sleep, the neighbors sleep (I despise the overly loud car alarm chirp of one of our cars on a rare night we are out late enough to wake a neighbor). What is one willing to do get good sleep? Go to bed at the same time each night; separate beds; separate bedrooms; lock the pets out, [Ssscat](http://http://contechstayawaymotion-activatedpetdet.blogspot.com/2011/05/innotek-ssscat-cat-training-aid.html) any one? This was true before blue blockers, SCN, melatonin, hormone cascade, autophagy, prolactin surge, HGH release, or any of the other whats, whys and golden nuggets to be mined from the blogs here.

I'm starting to ask myself if the clock is to me what a weight scale is to someone with an ED. If I wake up and it's dark I try to go back to sleep with much success I should add. If I wake for too long I look at the clock which I have to grab and move so I can see it since I have its leds point at the wall. I find I wake quite a bit between 2-2:30 which seems part of the 2 phase sleep pattern I have read about. Waking at 4-4:30 pretty much sucks. That can be ~6 hours of sleep which can be too close to the 7-8 hours of sleep I covet to go back to sleep, but it's also too early for breakfast within an hour of waking. I'd love to go for a walk but would hate to raise cortisol. At this point I think the clock is still a tool and not my nemesis. I guess I'll keep it for now.

Sleep, my precious, you will be mine every night. gollum gollum

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-07-17T19:32:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-38774>

I'd say don't look at the clock!! If you are waking between 2 and 3 o'clock, it's mostly a problem with cortisol and/or insulin. Looking at the clock and telling yourself you HAVE to go back to sleep will only raise cortisol and make matter worst. You should cover that alarm to make sure it is very dark in your room.

I was having the same problem: waking up in the middle of the night and having difficulties to go back to sleep. I would turn on the light, do some reading, sometimes surf the web. ALL WRONG. Stay in the dark. Do some meditation or listen to calm music, and you'll fall back to sleep.

After testing, I found out that DHEA was in the toilet. I took high dose of DHEA to help me find sleep again, and lowered it after a few days. I'm now sleeping much better. I sometimes wake up in the night to use the bathroom, but I fall right back to sleep.

Have a good day...and a good night sleep

Even with the few number of days since I started with the blue blockers I now sleep all night. I expect that will continue. Covering my head with a T-shirt keeps it dark. The CPAP keeps lots of fresh air under the shirt and the white noise it makes covers the noise of the open window too. Now if only the T-shirt provided cool to my head.

### johnnyb — 2012-08-01T06:38:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-42216>

I had an email consult with Dr. K. reviewing my history and suggesting blogs and tests. Very interesting reading the blogs with the new perspective from his suggestions and the experience of writing up my medical history. Actually writing it up opened my eyes to some stuff like the probability of leaky gut. Not having any (recognized) food intolerance I kinda panned leaky gut. Rereading blogs my medical history is littered with leaky gut red flags.

I met with my PCP to talk over recent issues and to see which of the recommended tests I could get. I like my doc very much but she is a CW doc and my interface to a CW system. Some of the tests are not done by the lab. I'll probably have to get one of the saliva cortisol test done else where. She did order a blood cortisol test and 24 hour urine cortisol test. I'll get at least TPO Ab, TSH and T4 but apparently not T3 or rT3. No vit D because it was normal in Feb. She wasn't going to do the hsCRP because the last two CPR tests were lower then the tests range. She asked why get the hsCRP in my case? I was at a bit of a loss other then its importance as an inflammation marker. She put it on the lab sheet so I'll get it.

I think I learned a key element to getting tests and working with her. One of the criteria is what action do you take based on test results. I didn't really want to explain that if I have a high hsCRP I'll break out a big can of CT woopass. That'll have to wait for a followup. The follow up consult with Dr. will likely clue me in a bit to work better with her too. I'm some what hopeful if I can record the skype session I may get her to listen it or maybe selected important parts of where Dr. K says to head.

### johnnyb — 2012-08-02T06:56:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-42456>

The vampires took 8 or more vials of blood this morning and supplied a large orange vessel for a 24 hour urine test. I hope test results return quickly. One of the tests was fasting so breakfast was late. I figured out how to make some breakfast at work so I didn't have to go back home. I missed bringing lunch since I had planned to make that after breakfast.

Fast food is getting harder to go clean. The food is the same but my awareness and desire for clean food increases. I started to like the occasional Asian food lunch choice after I figured out I could get vegatables as the side instead of rice or noodles and a shrimp entree. The shrimp while looking sauteed on closer inspection have a light flour or starch coating. I wish they have scampi. The soy sauce is crap of course. A friend suggested a hack at the fish taco/buritto place. Sounds like I can go off menu, get a bowl with the fish and some Teriyaki vegatables sans the Teriyaki.

Mothers Market had 6 or more flavors of Kavita to try. The coconut flavor was OK with dinner. I missed the gentle part of "shake gently" but without incident  They had Bubbies pickles and sourkraut and as a bonus their horseradish. The regular store brands all had serveral kinds of crap with them. I do like some horseradish on cold shrimp. Mother even had organic Tamari sauce in the little pouches.

I eliminated night shades a couple days ago in the hopes of seeing if shoulder issues are related. Shrimp at lunch came with bell peppers. Picked them out. I hope thats clean enough. You never know how many tomatoes and peppers you eat until you avoid them. Starting to look at the leaky gut Rx too which reminds me I need to get that them bones in the slow cooker.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-02T17:56:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-42517>

### johnnyb — 2012-09-04T19:53:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-49398>

When the time changed in the spring I started riding my bike to work. That start was soon thwarted with a crash preventing maneuver that injured my knee. Some time and lots of ice later I resumed riding. My initial riding goal was to get more NEAT, Non-Exercise Activity Thermogenesis, and now to rehab my knee. Ironic that the activity the lead to the injury was also a good one to build up my knee again. I was very concerned about avoiding chronic cardio on the ride. It's a very nice ride. Down hill all the way to work along a river trail, then up the hill to the office. I actually started walking up one short hill/path instead of gearing down and grinding up it. The days I ride into work I generally feel great and ready to take on the world.

So while I though the ride was all about getting increased daily NEAT I now think that is only a bonus. What I'm really getting is a 25 minute morning dose of sun light to entrain my SCN to circadian rhythms. Yup, I get a steaming hot cup of Good Morning! for my brain. I could swear I have read get morning light to increase your morning cortisol and also get morning light to reduce morning cortisol. I suppose the answer is entrain the brain, get the correct amount of cortisol at the correct time of day. Which reminds me, that ASI should be coming back this week....

### johnnyb — 2012-09-08T08:07:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-50204>

ZRT labs results came back. The good news they appear very "normal". Vit D was normal but not high in the range like I hoped. Gotta get consult going. I'm happy that I most likely need to optimize from mediocre and not some of the suspect issue like Hashis. That's 2 TPO negative results.

### johnnyb — 2012-09-19T06:31:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnybs-life-optimizing-goals.2125/post-52640>

So much goes on and I don't make time to write......

I finally had my consult with Jack. He really gives a rip about each person. I figured he would have a high level of competency and professionalism but he really is trying to put statfish back in the water even one at a time.

I got to hear him say "you are an easy case". The short version is I'm a functional hypothyroid. I have mild pregnenolone steal and mild andropause. All in all I'm pretty happy. He even had a compliment about one of my Doc choice of labs. I think he said, good she's a thinker. My labs were odd and constrained by what the medical group's lab offers supplemented by ones I got done. It will be interesting to see how my optimal desires play out with a good CW Doc.

I scored a bunch of size large tshirts to replace the XLs I'm starting to swim in. Some with very retro themes. DW had saved a bunch of my shirts that I became far too obese to fit. Its almost like a new wardrobe. Thanks honey! Now lot of clothes that are too big are going bye bye. Those clothes will **never** fit again.

I scored lots of seaweed both dried and fresh, and a jar of oysters at Hmart. The white kimchee is good which is convenient given they only had it in a 1.6kg jar. It took 2 days to get it all home in my back pack from work. I scored it on a lunch run to Hmart and I have been riding my bike to work.

Life is good.

# Thread: B's on the train to optimal : )
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-07-03T03:33:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/post-35195>

Ok, so here goes nothing! Iâ€™ve been thinking about doing a journal for a long time, but I kept resisting. I think it scares me in a way to be so open and honest about everything, but Iâ€™ve realized that not only do I need and want to be held accountable I also want to be able to paleo it forward somehow. I try to do that in my everyday life, but Iâ€™ve learned so much about my journey to optimal by reading othersâ€™ stories and posts and it occurred to me that I need to put myself and my story out there too so that maybe even one person can find something helpful in it.

**Background**

For as long as I can remember Iâ€™ve always had less energy and just generally dealt with more fatigue than others and was finally diagnosed with hypothyroidism around age 16-17 and put on synthroid. â€œIrritable bowel syndromeâ€ runs in my family and I dealt with that a bit growing up as well, but nothing really major. PMS was pretty bad for me with lots of cramping and moodiness, but overall I actually had a fairly healthy and unremarkable childhood.

I have two beautiful kids and felt the best Iâ€™d ever felt in my life while pregnant . . . both times my husband joked he was going to keep me pregnant forever! Too bad for him my libido took a major nosedive after the second one ; ) (I told this to Dr. K prior to my consult and it was a major clue for him on what was happening with me.)

Onto the good stuff: It was about 2.5 years ago that my health majorly crashed. I began eating a vegetarian diet and was doing a super intense workout program. I had never really exercised in my life; hated any form of it really. Thankfully Iâ€™d never had a weight problem so I didnâ€™t have to worry much about it, but I had a bit more trouble taking the weight off after my second baby so I actually put some effort into it ; ) For a while I felt wonderful â€“ I remember waking super early every morning and feeling like I could take on the world. Things were fantastic!

Then all of the sudden I started to develop numerous food intolerances and crazy reactions â€“ rashes, stomach pains, etc, etc. First it was soy, then quinoa, gluten, corn and then dairy. It seemed like every 6 weeks or so there was a new food intolerance creeping up. I began an elimination diet and literally felt like I was reacting to EVERYTHING! It was terrifying. I didnâ€™t know what to eat.

Long story short â€“ I ended up on an anti-candida diet and then GAPS. I definitely got worse eating the allowed grains on the anti-candida diet. I do think GAPS helped in some areas, but also set me back in some ways too. Saw some ridiculous doctors â€“ told I had early signs of MS, muscle wasting disease, all kinds of horrific things. One doc sent me away and said there was nothing he could do for me â€“ the last few years have been rough to say the least. My main problems were (and I say were because things are finally looking up!) :

â€¢ Major fatigue and muscle weakness â€“ so bad that I could barely lift my arms to wash my hair at times or even get one leg in front of the other to walk

â€¢ Memory loss â€“ this has been a gradual thing for the last 10 or so years

â€¢ Impaired cognitive function

â€¢ Gain weight anytime my carbs go above 30-40 grams/day

â€¢ Complete lack of libido â€“ I wish it were just a decrease, but I got nothinâ€™ right now

â€¢ Constipation â€“ never had this in my life until I started GAPS

â€¢ Food intolerances mentioned above, i.e. EXTREME leaky gut â€“ so bad that I couldnâ€™t tolerate hardly any foods / supplements for a very long time

â€¢ Fat malabsorption

â€¢ Oxalate issues

â€¢ Histamine reactions â€“ couldnâ€™t have bone broths, cured meats, avocados, etc.

â€¢ Rosacea â€“ I look like a tomato when Iâ€™m hot! A tomato with really bad acne.

â€¢ Bizarre facial swelling

â€¢ Havenâ€™t had TOM since Feburary and only had it once in the 10 months prior to that

â€¢ Low body temps

â€¢ Low blood pressure

â€¢ Always freezing

â€¢ All kinds of problems with my teeth after having 8 amalgams removed â€“ infections, general pain, etc, etc.

â€¢ Mold issues â€“ triggered breathing problems; eventually did some remediation in my basement that helped a good bit with this

Iâ€™m sure thereâ€™s more, but thanks to the memory loss and impaired cognitive function thatâ€™s all I can remember right now!!

So, what Iâ€™ve done:

â€¢ Started leptin rx on 1/1/12

â€¢ Started CT in February â€“ currently do 5-6 baths/week at 50 degrees

â€¢ Eat a super clean diet following the AI protocol

â€¢ Consulted with Dr. K â€“ best decision yet!

â€¢ Found a decent doctor that will actually work with me

â€¢ Started taking pregnenolone and DHEA

And now Iâ€™m well on my way to optimal!!

Improvements Iâ€™ve been noticing:

â€¢ Sleeping much better, but I still wake not feeling totally rested and very stiff/sore in my hips

â€¢ I think Iâ€™m tolerating more foods finally! My diet has been so restricted Iâ€™m ready to add a bit of variety!

â€¢ Definite upswing in energy â€“ I think itâ€™s related to the DHEA that Dr. K recommended I increase during my consult

â€¢ Muscle weakness is very mild these days, if at all

â€¢ Just had a meeting at work and I rocked it, so Iâ€™m hoping the cognitive function is coming back. The last few years Iâ€™ve totally laid low on the job because I couldnâ€™t follow half of what was happening and didnâ€™t want to show everyone just how stupid Iâ€™d become, but I had confidence today and spoke up about an issue and got major positive feedback! Canâ€™t wait to feel productive again : )

â€¢ Just a general sense of well being. I didnâ€™t realize how â€˜downâ€™ I felt all the time until lately when I just feel positive and want to share it with everybody

â€¢ Skin is much smoother and I barely break out anymore unless Iâ€™m having a rosacea flare, which unfortunately still happens frequently

â€¢ I seem to be tolerating cold temps a bit better too . . . though that may have something to do with the heat wave weâ€™ve been experiencing lately!

**My Plan to continue to get my health back:**

â€¢ Implement the things Dr. K discussed during my consult:

o I ordered T3 â€“ just need to wait for it to get here from Mexico!

o Still working on my doc to prescribe some HC

o Continuing to increase my DHEA

o Adding a Brazil nut a day for selenium

o Getting my iodine tested â€“ if needed, add it

o Just ordered Mg Threonate

â€¢ Continuing with the baths 5-6 times/week

â€¢ Getting in a little extra CT by swimming 2-3 times/week - although the water is close to 80 degrees

Yes, I wrote a book, but thatâ€™s all I got for now. Stay tuned for any updates

~ B

### Eve — 2012-07-04T03:09:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/post-35398>

Thanks for laying it all out

I'll be following along as I'm in a similar boat

### BJK77 — 2012-07-04T04:36:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/post-35414>

**News for the day:**

\- Got my T3 in the mail and started on it right away. I should probably research dosage, timing, etc a bit more, but I tend to dive head first into things, especially when I think it's gonna help and I have BIG hopes for this!! Funny side note: the family is referring to it as my "Mexican drugs!" Most people around me already think I'm a quack . . . can't imagine what they'll say when the kids jokingly call it that in front of them!

\- Still continuing to up the DHEA and am finding it strange that I suddenly feel more feminine. I've never had a boyish figure, but never much of a pin-up figure either. I'm suddenly getting curves in places I never had them - hips and boobs have found me! Trying to accept them and be happy and in some ways I am, but if I'm being brutally honest, in a way I also hate that half my clothes no longer fit me!

\- I don't own a scale, so only know what I weigh when I'm at the doctor's, but I've been eating like a pig lately and oddly I really don't think I'm gaining weight! Yes, I know I should be 100% focused on health and not so much weight, but reality is that I've always been known as the petite, thin girl and it's part of my identity now. I'm happy to say I think my body comp is improving - I'm noticing more muscle definition in my thighs and arms - that along with the new curves may give me a confidence boost I desperately need! I assume this is all due to the increased DHEA. Who would have thought something so simple could cause such radical changes.

\- Picked berries in the hot, humid 90-degree weather today for well over an hour with the kids. Just a few weeks ago I would have been exhausted, felt sick from the heat and probably would have crashed the rest of the day. Today I outlasted the kids! I came home and made blueberry muffins (not for me of course!), dehydrated and froze a bunch of berries, cleaned the house and then took the kids swimming. Amazing what some hormones can do : )

I think that about covers my updates for today. Thanks for reading Eve!

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-07-04T05:45:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/post-35425>

Wow, sounds like you've come quite a ways and are on a great track! Could your doc not prescribe you t3 to get here?

### BJK77 — 2012-07-04T19:09:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/post-35487>

> **Trisha;35588 wrote:** Wow, sounds like you've come quite a ways and are on a great track! Could your doc not prescribe you t3 to get here?

Hey Trisha,

My current doctor who thankfully does have a clue about all of this is actually a chiropractor, so he can't prescribe. He does have an MD that he works with who will prescribe the t3, but I couldn't get into him until the end of August and I'm too impatient to wait that long, so I took matters into my own hands

### BJK77 — 2012-07-06T04:04:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/post-35786>

My poor family . . . I'm finding my emotions all over the place right now. Not sure if it's the T3 (taking 50 mg total in 2 doses), pregnenolone (200 mg total in 2 doses) or DHEA (160 mg total in 2 doses). I just noticed tonight that I'm pretty much making a big deal out of something that really shouldn't bother me quite so much and I'm crying my eyes out just thinking about it!

My hormones are definitely out of whack . . . just wish I could figure out which one is the problem!

### BJK77 — 2012-07-07T04:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/post-36036>

Emotions seem a bit more under control today thankfully, but CT has become much more difficult. I posted a q in another forum about it and Dr. K responded that it could be my new T3 being forced into rT3 by CRH or ACTH. I'll give it 10-14 days like he said and see how I feel and run a thyroid panel to see what's actually going on.

Leaving for the beach tomorrow for a few days and can't wait to get in the ocean! Hoping this T3 mess won't make it too challenging! I think the water temps are in the mid-60's, so much warmer than my bath water, but cooler than my pool water!! I'm planning on total relaxation. Now if only I could just make that happen . . .

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-11T00:58:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/post-36662>

> **BJK77;35577 wrote: News for the day:**
>
>
>
> \- Got my T3 in the mail and started on it right away. I should probably research dosage, timing, etc a bit more, but I tend to dive head first into things, especially when I think it's gonna help and I have BIG hopes for this!! Funny side note: the family is referring to it as my "Mexican drugs!" Most people around me already think I'm a quack . . . can't imagine what they'll say when the kids jokingly call it that in front of them!

My family thinks I'm nuts to order meds from Mexico, too! LOL! Doesn't matter though...t3 makes me feel much better!

I read a book by Paul Robinson on dosing methods that was really helpful. You should check it out. [http://www.amazon.com/Recovering-T3-Journey-Hypothyroidism-Thyroid/dp/0957099304](http://http://www.amazon.com/Recovering-T3-Journey-Hypothyroidism-Thyroid/dp/0957099304)

### BJK77 — 2012-07-12T03:52:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/post-37044>

> **PaleoCowgirl;36828 wrote:** My family thinks I'm nuts to order meds from Mexico, too! LOL! Doesn't matter though...t3 makes me feel much better!
>
>
>
> I read a book by Paul Robinson on dosing methods that was really helpful. You should check it out. [http://www.amazon.com/Recovering-T3-Journey-Hypothyroidism-Thyroid/dp/0957099304](http://http://www.amazon.com/Recovering-T3-Journey-Hypothyroidism-Thyroid/dp/0957099304)

Thanks PCG. I've been reading so much conflicting info about how and when to take my thyroid meds I'm totally confused. Just ordered this book. Hopefully it will clear things up for me.

I wish I could say I feel improvement with the t3. I thought I did initially, but now I wonder if it was just because I so desperately wanted to feel better with it. I'm actually afraid it's converting to rt3 as I'm finding CT to be extremely difficult these days. I'm having a thyroid panel drawn tomorrow, so I guess I'll know soon enough . . .

### BJK77 — 2012-07-12T04:00:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/post-37046>

Spent a few days at the beach and actually felt pretty good! Did my CT in the ocean - although it really wasn't very cold! I think the water temps were 78-79 degrees while we were there, but I went in everyday and stayed in for at least an hour, so better than nothing.

And of course now I'm back to reality. First day back at work today in the sweltering office and my rosacea returned. Thankfully it wasn't quite as bad as it has been in the past, but still very noticeable. I'm hoping the DHEA I'm taking will eventually fix it.

Got my cortisol saliva results back and didn't think they looked too bad, but I'm not really sure what we're aiming for with them.

Any thoughts?

Cortisol Morning: 6.1 (Range 3.7-9.5)

Cortisol Noon: 3.0 (Range 1.2-3.0)

Cortisol Evening: 1.5 (Range 0.6-1.9)

Cortisol Night: 0.4 (Range 0.4-1.0)

Definitely still noticing some improvements with the DHEA/pregnenolone supplementation, but they seem to come and go. Libido returned for a few days only to drop again. Constipation became a thing of the past with daily BM's, but now that's not so great either. I feel like I'm on a constant roller coaster. For every up, there must be a huge down

### BJK77 — 2012-07-15T03:23:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/post-37898>

I haven't been updating this the last few days because I'm going through one of those lovely "low points" and honestly just have no motivation to do anything! I'm back in that depressed stage and questioning "why me?!?!" Trying desperately to pull myself out of it, but it's not so easy.

I think I may just be going through some sort of detox, possibly an oxalate dump. This seems to happen to me every few months - extreme fatigue and muscle weakness sets in along with insomnia, headaches, nausea and increased food intolerances. My face feels like it's on fire most of the day. It gets bright red and just burns up! I also get super bloodshot eyes - ocular rosacea maybe?!

I've been trying to re-read several of Dr. K's blogs, but my brain fog just makes it difficult to take too much in. I have realized though that I definitely had/have an ED. Not AN or bulimia, so I guess you could say nothing major, but I totally had a tendency to overeat/binge, eat compulsively . . . whatever you want to call it.

My diet is still super tight. Thankfully I can control and ignore any cravings I have, which usually are pretty sparse, but I still find myself gorging on certain foods. Bought a purple cabbage yesterday and almost have the whole thing eaten already! I know it doesn't set well with my stomach, but I gobble it up anyway. I just need to stop buying it, but for some reason that's harder than you'd think!

To further keep me accountable, I'm gonna start putting down here what I eat every day. Yes, my diet is boring, so don't anyone get too excited  I think it might help me with the whole overeating thing though if I force myself to write down everything that enters my mouth. We'll see anyway!

So, today was:

**Breakfast

Salmon

Burger

Cabbage - too much!

Kale - just a little pureed in with my broth to give it flavor - YUM!

Lots of coconut oil and beef tallow

**Lunch**

Chuck Roast

More cabbage

And my beloved CO/tallow

Dinner**

Mussels

Chuck Roast

And more cabbage - I know, I know!

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-07-17T06:50:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bs-on-the-train-to-optimal.2177/post-38653>

I love purple cabbage too! Yum, I could eat half a head in one sitting. But better that than half a pizza, right?!

# Thread: Keeping Kim Honest
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 58
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NWgirl — 2012-07-15T20:48:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-38091>

I decided that I really need to have a place to write some things down and have a record do how I'm feeling and what I'm doing. A place to "keep Kim honest". I have a huge record of trying things and not sticking to them long enough to see if they are even working. I'm so tired of not feeling well and having so many 'ups and downs' that effect my mood. I want to get better!

Here is a short synopsis of where I'm coming from:

\- I'm 43 with husband and twin 16 year old daughters (I adopted them from Korea). My parents also live with us. They are healthy which is good!

\- currently 245 pounds but my highest was 400. I had weight loss surgery 11 years ago (duodenal switch) and got down to 205 so I've regained 40 pounds.

\- I've been on LR a couple weeks and I'm also doing CT baths. I'm very familiar with primal and paleo... Have tried it multiple times over a few years without success. I found it difficult to stick to long term.

\- history of PCOS, on thyroid meds (armour).

\- I had excess skin surgery in 2006 to remove off tummy, breasts and arms. I don't gain where I had the surgery so now I have an upper "man tummy" which I hate!!!

\- I got into "real food" about six years ago through the Weston A Price foundation and I've started a couple blogs based on Sally Faalon's books.

\- as a result of my WLS surgery I have had trouble with absorption of iron and vitamin D. Spent some time extremely anemic but now I receive iron infusions every two years which has kept me non-anemic for about 6 years thank god. I have had trouble getting my vitamin D level above 32.

That is enough history for now, if you have specific questions let me know! Feels weird to write all of that down...

Kim

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-15T23:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-38118>

You/Me = Peas/Pod

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2377-A-fat-kid-s-tale](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2377-A-fat-kid-s-tale)

We are almost identical on age, weight, bypass. I had no PCOS and not on any thyroid stuff. No surgery for skin here. Would love to figure out how you got someone to do the iron infusion - no luck with my docs here as I'm just below range with the sublingual supps. Same Vit-D issue but I've recently started sunbathing in addition to taking it.

I got up to about 400, lost to 220, stalled out and yo-yoed from 220 to 300 several times. Basically since early 20's I've been either gaining weight or losing weight. Never stable until the Leptin RX + CT.

### NWgirl — 2012-07-16T00:14:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-38144>

> **Cavemam;38287 wrote:** You/Me = Peas/Pod

wow Cavemam you are correct! We r definitely two peas in a pod. I too would NOT change the result of the bypass. Because of that I actually had the confidence to date (yes via Internet) and met my husband. That would not have happened if I was still 400.

Re: iron infusions. I struggled almost one year until my then doctor "let" me have one. He kept saying how "drastic" the infusions would be. At the time my ferritin stores were almost at zero (had to fight to get that lab done) and I was VERY anemic. Finally I put my foot down and my quality of life was back within 5 days of the first infusion. I never went back to that doc after that. Now I see a naturopath that is 1000 times easier to work with.

For me I think the iodine supplementation (and supporting supplements) may be the next step. I have some small goiters I'm hoping will shrink with that protocol. Oh and the LR and the CT of course!

### NWgirl — 2012-07-17T04:06:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-38601>

Thanks Cavemam for reminding me that I actually have vit D drops already!!

My DIM came today, hopefully it will help with the detox problems I've been having! They seem to be better today though.

Quick synopsis of yesterday and today:

Yesterday took my CT bath in the morning for the first time. I washed my hair and then got in the tub, which wasn't ideal but better than I thought. My body got cold faster because my head was cold/wet. I could only stay in 30 minutes but did ok considering. I liked taking it after BAB will definitely try that again on a weekend. I was pretty tired after CT though and I realized that I'd normally be sleeping through that!

Ate pretty well and we even went out to dinner. Had more trouble when we got back. I'm having trouble not eating after dinner. This has typically been my "fruit" time - I have an apple with Celtic sea salt or a pint of berries or cantaloupe, whatever is in season. But my go to is an organic fuji apple with salt. Is it really imperative for the program for me to give this up? I guess I could eat it as part of my dinner if I really needed to.

Today I went to work and did really well. Our cafeteria is really good and they have a ton of veggies and good local meat choices. They will serve me any of the dishes with extra veg instead of starch or will let me get a side salad. And the soups are homemade and twice in the last week they haven't had grains in them! Score. Good way to get more veggies in.

Only bad thing today - it's after 6 and I have no idea what I'm eating for dinner. Better work on that now!

Thanks for reading...

Kim

### NWgirl — 2012-07-19T04:58:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-39281>

Last couple days have been good. Did my 10th CT bath last night and got it down to 51 degrees with some ice. Did about 40 minutes and it was the first time that my skin got really pink. I did put topical magnesium all over 15 minutes prior to getting in, not sure if that helped the pink color.

Food has been good but not perfect. I found that I get really nervous if I think that I should eat to keep myself from getting hungry. Weird, I know. So I've been eating some snacks but I do want to get to to 3 meals a day thing.

Yesterday I came home from work and was exhausted. After CT I went to bed at 8:30pm! I slept straight until 6:15 (before my alarm) and felt much better today.

Apparently I save all of my really good writing for my day job. My brain is fading!

### NWgirl — 2012-07-20T07:58:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-39616>

First day that I didnt get hungry between meals, yaah! Also did CT for 30 minutes 53 degrees. Almost sat down right away, the fastest that I've gotten into the water. Still having problems with getting neck and shoulders into the water though.

### NWgirl — 2012-07-21T20:15:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-40032>

Yesterday I had the most fabulous day. Took the day off of work and took one daughter and nephew raspberry picking. It started raining a very warm rain... I felt so 'back to nature' and peaceful. Tons of berries (for freezing) was only $7. Then went to lunch and took them to a place to play. Then a nice nap before going to dinner with my husband and friends. And the best thing, I did all of this while sticking to the LR. I even waited 7 hours to eat between lunch and dinner, without killing anyone! There was one moment of frustration when they all ordered a huge thing of fried tater tots for appetizer. But I know that they always made me feel sick, and I wanted to keep feeling good and thinking clearly. I chose health!

Here's a picture of my daughter Allie and nephew:

### NWgirl — 2012-07-23T02:12:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-40312>

Yesterday was NOT a good day. I really had a 'mood' problem until I did CT in the early evening. Had good CT did 40 minutes then followed it up with my first ever cold shower. Of course my body felt fine but my shoulders and head were not used to it, so I think it was really good for me. At dinner ate too much well done steak (restaurant cooked it wrong) and had trouble digesting. Slept well though. Ate too many raspberries.

Today, slightly better than yesterday but had major attacks of diarrhea this morning. Not sure if it was the raspberries or the too done steak or what. But my stomach didn't settle down until noon. Mood is better but it still lingers. Making 'no bean' chili tonight for dinner and I need to be strong to resist the corn tortillas.

I'm starting to get really tired of this and wondering if it will even help. No one in my house is eating this way so it seems to be a constant challenge. Also I realized that I get 'bored' at home or unhappy or something and I tend to think about food more there. Maybe it's just because I'm not distracted by work though.

### NWgirl — 2012-07-23T20:55:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-40430>

I resisted the corn tortillas fine last night although it was tough. They were chips fried in coconut oil and looked yummy. Slept weird last night didn't go to sleep until 11:30pm and got up for work at 6:30 just fine. I will probably be tired tonight due to that!

Had two baked tilapia filets for breakfast. Having nausea and diarrhea at work but not as bad as yesterday. I hope the day gets better!

I'm considering going off coffee completely and just taking a caffeine pill for awhile to see if that helps me. I am wondering if the acid is what is killing my stomach. I do have to mention I drink green iced tea (unsweetened) at work all day too.

### NWgirl — 2012-07-24T01:15:08+0300

Just realized I have lost 9 pounds since starting LR beginning of July! So maybe this is working. I take back what I said last night.

Still doing good. Great thing is that I feel better in the morning and have more energy. I had two tilapia filets again for breakfast but then ate cherries between breakfast and lunch, which made me hungry. Duh!! Yeah need to work on the no snacks thing.

Why don't you get everybody at home eating your way? It would be really good for your daugthers, too.

### NWgirl — 2012-07-31T07:38:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-42022>

Working on sticking to 3 meals with NO snacking. This is harder than I thought but I'm making progress.

The malbec on Friday night was a total mistake for me. It of course knocked me out of ketosis but the worst part was I was hungry between meals both Saturday and Sunday even though I stuck to the plan. Still even today I don't have my previous satiety back yet.

Bf - 2 tilapia filets with salsa, coconut oil

Lunch - pork roast and pork veggie broth, cherries

Dinner - spoon of coconut butter, half roasted chicken (dark meat), full fat Greek plain yogurt with cherries

Snack- 5 brazil nut kernels

Ok I may be eating too much fruit, will work on that! Hey at least I'm eating it with meals now instead of as a snack. The fruit thing is hard for me this time of year due to all of the good local berries, cherries and cantaloupe.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-31T14:31:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-42033>

> Dinner - spoon of coconut butter, half roasted chicken (dark meat), full fat Greek plain yogurt with cherries

I would test your bs after eating yogurt too. Might not be on your protocol and might stop you from getting LS someday.

Fruit and yogurt were tough for me to give up cuz I just love that stuff but somehow I managed.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-05T03:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-42994>

Vkiernan, thanks yes I know you are right. Funny I haven't had yogurt since that one night and I'm suddenly fine to not eat it! The fruit however is still a daily occurrence although I am eating it only with my meals now, yaah!

I'm still doing really well, which is completely surprising to me. I'm not trying to be negative but I don't have the best track record sticking to 'paleoish' eating. But I feel so good that I just want to keep going. I still am not losing weight very fast but I really think I'm changing on the inside.

Some good things about this is that my behavior is better. I don't get as frustrated and annoyed and I'm more even than before. Plus I am not hungry between meals and my energy is good. Also my thinking capacity is just amazing now. At work I'm doing well and am so productive and thinking of new ways to do things (I'm a IT systems analyst). I'm only working about 40-45 hours a week but my work output is like I work so much more. And when I get home I actually STILL have something in my brain leftover for my family most nights. We even are going swimming after work as a family two nights a week (too warm to be actual CT but oh well). I sleep like a rock most nights and mostly wake up clear headed. It is still a struggle not to eat at night (habit).

I'm still having some issues I would like to work out, the main one is diarrhea. Also I'm getting a lot of allergy type symptoms and sneezing, weird. And if I go more than one day in between CT sessions some of my joint pain comes back. Who would of thought cold baths could be so helpful?

But for one month in, I've lost 9 pounds and I feel good.

### vkiernan — 2012-08-05T19:51:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-43088>

Yay! Good for you. You know for me this wasn't a perfect start and finish; well I am still not finished even after a year. I was really strict and then had a slip up, really strict and then a slip up. But, I kept doing more things that were for the better. You are still pretty new to the game. Everyday just keep getting up and doing the best you can and if you slip or "eat fruit" eventually you will change that thought and keep moving toward optimal if that is your goal. I am happy you are having successes. Tells you that you are on your way!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-05T20:20:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-43092>

I agree with Vicky, each time you are not perfect - just make it worth it by thinking on it, and getting back up the next day. I think for most humans, "directionally accurate" is pretty good! I think as you make each change, you will find the next one easier. . probably as our brains heal it gets easier.

A year ago I would not have thought I'd be taking ice baths, wearing funny glasses, sleeping with the sun and eating ketogenic most of the time - and without much pain.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-05T20:59:37+0300

8am BAB: taco meat w/3eggs, tiny bit of cheese, green salsa, coffee w heavy cream and CO

Iced coffee with heavy cream (Starbucks)

1pm Lunch (persian food out): 1 lamb shank, 2 bites feta w basil, cucumber tomato salad w olive oil and lemon

Homemade iced coffee w heavy cream, cocoa powder, CO and stevia

8pm Dinner (out-benihana): 7 pieces sashimi w tamari. SautÃ©ed onions, zucchini and mushrooms. Sauteed shrimp with cucumber avocado tomato yellow pepper asparagus. Green tea.

Before dinner got really dehydrated and low blood sugar but felt better after 3 glasses of water and sashimi appetizer. 7 hours is still pretty long for me to go between meals although I'm getting better.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-09T06:51:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-43826>

Thank god I started to feel better Tuesday after two not so great days. The neck and shoulder pain was kind of like a headache but not in my head. I wish I knew what caused it because I hate it so much, and it seems to always rob me of a weekend day.

Been doing good today, but the last two evenings were difficult food wise. I had a few bites of things I shouldn't have and also ate snacks, and too close to bedtime. My husband brought home pirate's booty and I ate two stale pieces sitting on the counter. How weird is that?? Also partook in some fresh pineapple which I probably should not have since it's a higher sugar fruit. My husband brought his in and the smell was so strong and good that I had a very hard time resisting it. My sense of smell seems to have really increased and I had a great nose before!!

Oh great my daughter is now making cookies and it smells heavenly. I really wish my whole family was doing this.

Today's food

Bf - chicken thighs, coffee with CO and HWC, cucumbers

Lunch - ground lamb and chicken kabobs, zucchini, cucumber tomato salad, onions

Dinner - shrimp w basil and butter, cucumber, some yellow pepper, pineapple, 2 string cheese sticks

NWgirl..

Yep I'm taking iodoral. And a 'yes' on the Celtic sea salt too that one is so easy I love that stuff. Probably a good sign that i really need it. I'm also taking the ATP. Selenium is coming in the mail and I take magnesium topically before CT.

Thanks for the kind words!!

### NWgirl — 2012-08-11T05:46:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-44269>

Bf 8am: BAB shrimp and 2 eggs, coffee w CO and HWC

Lunch (out) 2:30pm: BBQ brisket and pulled pork, coleslaw (didn't especially enjoy the meat, didn't eat it all)

Dinner 7pm: a cucumber, pepper, onion, tomato feta salad with lemon & olive oil and half an avocado

I lost 1 more pound AGAIN! Total of 11 pounds lost since beginning of July. I'm now at 239 which is the lowest weight I've been in a couple years. Breaking the 240 barrier is a huge step for me.

I liked the eating schedule today of BAB then 6 1/2 hours later have a medium lunch and then a lighter dinner. I didn't eat any meat at dinner, didn't feel like it. I guess we'll see how that went tomorrow. No cravings today at all, and I don't feel especially susceptible to cravings for the rest of the evening.

I don't have to go to work until Monday 8/20!! Yaah.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-16T18:31:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-45216>

I posted yesterday here but the server change lost it.

I've had some issues with eating at the Oregon coast on vacation, but I moved on. Overall I'm not doing badly. Getting a lot of seafood including steamer clams, crab, smoked oysters, scallops and prawns.

My CT yesterday was amazing. We're renting a house in Barview and I went to the beach of the bay that is essentially filled with ocean water, it is so close (Tillamook bay). The water temp kept changing, but I got my skin temp down to 49. I must have stayed in half hour to 45 min. the 3 teen girls (two are mine) went with me and they went in too but they didn't stay in long and were impressed that I did.

At one point an old fashioned steam engine went by and we waved. Near the end I settled into a hole in the sand in the water that was shaped like an arm chair, faced the sun and watched a pelican hunt for dinner.

Best CT ever!

### NWgirl — 2012-08-16T18:32:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-45217>

When I get back home I'm following the plan to the letter including the early dinner!! Need to get more disciplined about this to see the most benefit.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-19T02:38:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-45642>

Something weird but good has happened! My appetite is really low today.

Bf: tilapia and salmon in fish broth, two bites of Asian pear, coffee w CO and cream

Linner: carnitas bowl at chipotle (no rice or beans of course) but I only ate less than half of it!!!! Will save the rest for BAB tomorrow.

I'm not sure why this is happening but I love it. Maybe this is what it feels like to be normal???

### NWgirl — 2012-08-19T06:30:16+0300

### LinD — 2012-08-19T06:34:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-45660>

Do you work on a computer for work? Or use one at home a lot? You're neck position is critical. Sewing, reading, or anything that.keeps your neck hovering/hanging down? Ride bikes a lot?

### NWgirl — 2012-08-19T06:45:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-45661>

> **LinD;46034 wrote:** Do you work on a computer for work? Or use one at home a lot? You're neck position is critical. Sewing, reading, or anything that.keeps your neck hovering/hanging down? Ride bikes a lot?

Yes, work on a computer for work and also read a lot.  I use iPad after work for reading and Internet browsing.

### LinD — 2012-08-20T03:26:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-45774>

> **NWgirl;46035 wrote:** Yes, work on a computer for work and also read a lot.  I use iPad after work for reading and Internet browsing.

This could very well be the reason for your neck/shoulder pain. If you were here, I would loan you my book by McKenzie, "7 Steps to a Pain Free Neck and Back."

[www.amazon.com/gp/aw/d/0452282772](http://http://www.amazon.com/gp/aw/d/0452282772)

You would see how very important it is the position we stand, sit, and sleep in.

I have C5/C6 herniation, so I know the pain it can cause when I've let down my guard and improperly get my neck in the wrong position for too long.

I hope you look into it and consider the possibility.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-21T06:50:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-46011>

Thanks LinD! I will check that out for sure.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-21T07:05:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-46013>

I've lost 2.2 more pounds! And that is over vacation! Total lost 12.8 pounds since 7/1.

Bf: tilapia, coffee w CO and cream

Lunch: curry veggie soup with a bit of beef, chicken, cherries

4pm- got hungry, had a pluot and a small bite of coconut butter

Dinner: grass fed beef steak, mushrooms, green beans & zucchini, homemade sauerkraut, sour cream

Watermelon

Ate way too much at dinner, not sure why. I had beef left on my plate and was going to stop but then I just went on. And then I ate waaayyyyy too much watermelon. Damn those summer fruits. Tomorrow I will be eating half he serving that I had tonight.

Poop talk alert...... Don't read on if you don't want to....

Was starting to get the stiff neck and shoulders again yesterday. This is weird because it always seems to happen on a Sunday and then lasts two days. Well yesterday I started thinking, maybe my liver is clogged with toxins? So I did a coffee enema. I immediately felt better after I was done. The pain was still kind of in the background at first then it just dissipated. And today I have been have the most wretched awful BM's like burnt rubber or something. I'm definitely getting rid of something bad!

### NWgirl — 2012-08-24T04:25:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-46640>

My weight has been up a few pounds even though I haven't cheated at all, kind of discouraging. I'm hoping it's just something hormonal and it will go away again.

I haven't had as much of a problem not eating after dinner lately, which is good!

Realized that I haven't had my TOM in like 3 months. Never thought I would miss it but I do. At least I knew when thing were happening then. I'm too young for menopause (43)!

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-08-24T21:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-46799>

Don't bother with the scale Kim, it's normal that is goes up and down. No TOM is not fun  Did you get tested lately for hormones? Seems that something is wrong, and that will likely slow down your weight loss.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-24T22:25:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-46816>

> **AbetterME;47209 wrote:** Don't bother with the scale Kim, it's normal that is goes up and down. No TOM is not fun  Did you get tested lately for hormones? Seems that something is wrong, and that will likely slow down your weight loss.

Just for fun, I'm going to put it out there that for Kim (just kim, context ya know!  might benefit from facing the scale. When someone has been very overfat before, and can be in denial while putting on 20, 30 - 50 lbds - setting the habit of monitoring weight can be a useful tool.

I'm finding that once I embraced the discomfort of it and now just do it every morning, and face it - it's getting easier and easier and I'm also mainting a weight range in a way I've never been able to do before.

I think for those that are obese or have been obese, the use of scale might be a good "lab". Clearly for someone who has a ED of a different sort - it can be a complete negative.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-25T04:08:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-46929>

Caveman, for us that have been overfat I totally agree with you. Your words on this a few weeks ago really hit me, thank you! I was used to just eating and not facing it, but weighing for me is GOOD and makes me face whatever it is that day.

That being said, I don't have a scale at home right now and have been weighing at work on their medical grade scale. So I guess I'm doing it 4 or 3 times per week or so.

I'm considering getting that scale that will go with my fitbit but it is $125!!  anyone have that and like it?

### NWgirl — 2012-08-25T04:10:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-46931>

> **AbetterME;47209 wrote:** Don't bother with the scale Kim, it's normal that is goes up and down. No TOM is not fun  Did you get tested lately for hormones? Seems that something is wrong, and that will likely slow down your weight loss.

### NWgirl — 2012-09-05T03:02:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keeping-kim-honest.2454/post-49506>

Still doing well! I've been really into lamb lately, had a major craving for it. Not sure what that's about but I'm enjoying it along with fish for most meals.

I ate 6 oysters for breakfast on Sunday and ended up in urgent care with food poisoning... sucked. Only good part, I lost 5 pounds and I've kept 3 of the 5 pounds off. lol. It's going to be a LONG time before I try oysters again. I'm not sure if my current gut flora can handle it. I don't even think that the oysters were 'bad' I think it was just me (but I could be wrong).

Had my first consult with Jack on Monday and it was great! I'm still feeling a little overwhelmed by all of the info, but hoping to fit in more researching in my free time to understand the next steps I should take. I think I will wait about a month and then repeat labs again, then have another consult.

Apparently I may have hashi's plus PCOS, and I need BHRT. I'm not sure what move to make next on that.

Still no TOM and very little sex drive which is weird for me. Still doing CT. Did 45 minutes last night, kind of dreaded getting in but it was fine when I was in it (typical).

> I ate 6 oysters for breakfast on Sunday and ended up in urgent care with food poisoning... sucked.

Interesting. I had raw oysters yesterday and I was sick like a dog all night and day. I stuck it out at home but my PCP said I was going to have to come in for fluids if I didn't stop throwing up and running to the loo by 3pm. But I had them for a couple days before as well and didn't get sick.

I can usually tell what I got sick from but not so this time. I had my last food yesterday at 4pm and that was liver and bacon (cooked!) and I didn't get sick until 1:30am.

# Thread: Waging war on WAT!!!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waging-war-on-wat.4067/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lorrainemartinek@gmail.com — 2012-10-02T17:47:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waging-war-on-wat.4067/post-55834>

I need a journal. It isn't something I usually do but I can't seem to keep my self in line. So feel free. To give me a verbal ass kicking when needed.

So here are the facts:

I'm 5'9" about 225 lbs, female 42 years young

Can't eat grains

Doc said I am peri-menopausal (dude! Really? I didn't start me mid life crisis!)

I am recovering from ankle surgery. I broke my fibula 7 April. After months of me explaining something is wrong and FINALLY getting a cat scan..... Oh hey! Look at whose right?!?!? Bone partially healed. So now I have some fabulous hardware and a so sexy big ass scar on my ankle.

I bought the cold fat burner because I cannot take a bath with a cast. I tried. Didn't work. A wet cast is not fun and I got a talking to because I needed a new cast.

My issues that need some serve working on:

To say I love red wine is an understatement. I love it too much. So I want to cut it out till further notice

I most likely cannot get any testing done. I am in the Air National Guard. Since I hurt myself while on military orders I stay on them till I am all better. I can tell you that the chances of asking for and getting any of these test will be near impossible. Even though I go to West Point, I think they will look at me like I am insane. So I have to wait till I go back to my civilian job and use that insurance. At this point that could be another 4-6 months.

Plan of action:

Eat seafood. I am getting over my ick factor with the clams and what not. I never liked the texture. But I am getting over it. I love fish lobster crab

No wine

Do my CT with my ice pack vest nightly. I have to say it is SO EASY with the vest.

Actually take my supplements that I bought from Jack's top 10 list. I have most

Go buy some sea veggies and check out my local seafood store. Apparently they go to the seafood market nearly daily down in NYC.

Instead of weighing myself I am going to use a pair of pants to gauge weightloss

Well let me get my gimpy butt out of bed and get going with the day....

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waging-war-on-wat.4067/post-55836>

I can tell you that the chances of asking for and getting any of these test will be near impossible. Even though I go to West Point, I think they will look at me like I am insane. So I have to wait till I go back to my civilian job and use that insurance. At this point that could be another 4-6 months.
>
>
>
> Plan of action:
>
> Eat seafood. I am getting over my ick factor with the clams and what not. I never liked the texture. But I am getting over it. I love fish lobster crab
>
> No wine
>
> Do my CT with my ice pack vest nightly. I have to say it is SO EASY with the vest.

>

>

good news on the wine. . . over time the desire for it changes. If you are doing CT/Wintery lower carb stuff your taste for it will diminish. I'm both happy AND sad over this. I was a total wino foodie and now if I order wine it's with the idea that I will just have a few sips and hubby will finish my glass. Now, if you love wine in the "code for alcoholic" sense, that's a whole other matter

# Thread: LinD's Back to the Future journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 54
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LinD — 2012-10-05T17:02:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56478>

Back in October 2010, I considered hCG as a method of losing weight. My neurologist agreed I would be better off with my multiple sclerosis. She didn't seem to have any issues with the hCG protocol.

It wasn't until June 2011 that I actually started hCG. To make a long story shorter, by November 2011, my weight actually went below the neuro's suggestion (since I was off by 5# due to not remembering correctly. I lost a total of 42#. Today, I am 6# over LIW, but that really isn't my driver--I just want to be optimal.

I was maintaining the weight loss fine after LIW (last injection weight), but after about six weeks (and following Leptin Reset), the scale started trending upwards a little. Correction days didn't seem to help much. This was January 2012.

Due to multiple levels of stress at that time (that hasn't changed too much and now it's October), I wasn't going to let the increase stress me out further. Plus, I figured the vitamin K was doing some good and maybe re-mineralization was taking place and potentially adding to the small gain.

Moving on, I decided to see a new doctor to have some labs completed to see where I am with hormones. I had bloodwork taken earlier this week and was going to do the ZRTlab saliva test, but I was getting conflicting info on whether I could have water the morning of the test or not. Instructions say rinse your mouth with water five minutes beforehand, but a friend who did the same test from the same doctor says no. Ack! I'll call the office and see what they say. I cannot do the test tomorrow and hope my cycle doesn't start before Sunday.... Otherwise, I'll have to wait another 18, 19 or 20 days.

I am 50 years old with a regular TOM (generally every 22-24 days with normal flow).

Now let me call the office to see about the water.....

### LinD — 2012-10-05T20:08:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56504>

Dr office says no rinsing... They do not follow zrtlab instructions. Also, I may have been one day too soon, so good thing I didn't do the test, though it feels a little suspicious that I might start TOM sooner than later--hopefully not before Sunday!

### LinD — 2012-10-05T23:29:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56529>

Oh dear! Now they say I should do the test 7-9 days BEFORE the first day of TOM. So the 5 panel saliva test will be done later this month.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-05T23:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56530>

This drives me nuts, specially when I'm paying out of pocket. I've had that thing where you get results back and someone says "if you did it this way instead of what you did it would have been better". So WTF! What if I were dying of something?!!

### LinD — 2012-10-05T23:40:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56532>

> **Cavemam;57792 wrote:** This drives me nuts, specially when I'm paying out of pocket. I've had that thing where you get results back and someone says "if you did it this way instead of what you did it would have been better". So WTF! What if I were dying of something?!!

Exactly the same thoughts I was just having! I nearly wasted $150. Really surprised I was advised incorrectly... I wasn't expecting that from her.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-05T23:49:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56533>

> **LinD;57794 wrote:** Exactly the same thoughts I was just having! I nearly wasted $150. Really surprised I was advised incorrectly... I wasn't expecting that from her.

From the outside, one would just assume that there are some minimal standards in the health fields, like offices dealing with LIVES would take the time to figure out how best to do these things . . .but no, I'm constantly amazed as the lack of professionalism and lack of basic "try to do a good job" mentality. It's like there is nobody that has responsibility when we are dealing with "offices".

I wish I could go back in time and become a physician. And be independently wealthy so I didn't have to play the games of course.

### Endless — 2012-10-06T01:07:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56540>

I ordered a saliva test from ZRT through my chiropractor...I thought with saliva it didn't matter when in the month you used it. Is it an ASI or a female hormone panel?

### LinD — 2012-10-06T01:14:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56541>

> **Endless;57802 wrote:** I ordered a saliva test from ZRT through my chiropractor...I thought with saliva it didn't matter when in the month you used it. Is it an ASI or a female hormone panel?

It is for five tests:

Estradiol

Progesterone

Testosterone

DHEA-S

Cortisol

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56542>

### LinD — 2012-10-07T23:50:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56814>

Today is Day 21 and feeling a little crampy, so given the signs, I bet I start TOM real soon. Glad there was a clarification on the saliva test timing or the results would have been off.

The weather changed somewhat dramatically on Friday and it is cooler. The gray sky yesterday and today is making me blue. It's either because of that or because our new home closing was delayed (and the builder is trying hard to make things right, but so far I'm not happy!), AND, I get an MRI on my left knee tomorrow. I THINK I remember hitting my knee hard--not once, but twice, before we moved from the other house. X-ray did not show any issues or arthritis (which I thought for sure it could be that), so I'm not sure what will be found. I'll find out Wed.

### LinD — 2012-10-12T06:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-57701>

We finally closed on our new home today, so now the second move begins. Too much mental trauma to discuss right now with the closing process, but the end result is we have the keys.

I found out yesterday I do have a small meniscus tear. Will treat conservatively with physical therapy, though this hasn't started.

I have some of my lab results too. Dr seemed concerned about my cholesterol.... 213 total, BUT HDL=126, LDL=79, Trig=38. Don't know what VLDL is, but it is 8. I'll share later on the other stuff when I can. I am really curious what the rest means in terms of thyroid and some of the hormones. Saliva test next, but I started TOM today.

My knee is hurting a little tonight, but after running into the baby gate (used for our dog) and knocking it down last night (and wrecking my pedicure, no less), it's no wonder.

LinD - Your ratios are very good! Calculated using Iranian Equation:

LDL/HDL os 0.37 (Ideal = 4.3 or below)

### nonchalant — 2012-11-14T17:08:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-64254>

> **LinD;65803 wrote:** You are right... I'm not worried about the cholesterol---in the least.

I'm a bit worried about it, at least the doctor's response to the TC of 213. You have awesome HDL, awesome LDL, and awesome TG, and she is concerned about TC?? But then, actually, if your LDL were higher, you would have some spare cholesterol to turn into more hormones with the help of T3 and vitamin A.

About the cortisol, I would want a 4x cortisol test, to see how it is later in the day. Your "high" cortisol might be fine, if the rest of the numbers are in line at noon, 6pm, and at bedtime.

As far as some of your other test results, well, I wish I had some of those numbers...

My Free T3 was down at that level, but it has been edging up with a tiny bit of iodine supplementation.

### LinD — 2012-11-16T16:17:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-64730>

Follow up appointment yesterday afternoon.... BTW, she was not concerned AT ALL with my cholesterol and was quite approviing. She said my HDL is higher than hers----in an envious manner.

As far as the rest, I am finding out she might be conservative in dose and which hormone to utilize.

Here is where I start my journey, good or bad---guess I'll find out!

1) Liothyronine sodium, 5mcg, BUT she wants me to split the pill and only take 1/2 each morning, so 2.5mcg.

2) Testosterone PLO 8mg/1gm gel: Use 1/2 ml

3) DHEA: I am calling back because I didn't write down the quantity! Again, I think it's conservative and she said 5 mg.

I told her I recently started using 7-Keto.......

I really thought there would be more to it. The pharmacist mentioned that this doctor is conservative, but actually that is a good thing so you know what you might be reacting. If too many things in the mix, how would you know?

As far as the higher than normal morning cortisol, she felt my stress levels are likely up with the move and the miniscus tear so she doesn't think the 4X/day cortisol is necessary.

### LinD — 2012-11-16T17:36:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-64741>
