# Thread: Changing just for me for life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Justfourme — 2013-11-04T02:47:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-99760>

I've planned on starting a journal for months and have heard so many recommend journaling, now is the time. The best place to start in any story is the beginning, but first a few basics. 53 yo female, wife and mother, LR, overweight and menopausal.
Born, raised, and living in the Midwest (USAHeartland). For the first 18 years if my life, I lived on a farm where we raised our own beef cattle (mainly grass fed), pigs, chickens (not caged), milk cows, and had a fish stocked pond. We also had a garden that was probably an acre in size and canned or froze just about every type of veggie and fruit. No fertilizer other than what the animals provided! We were outside rain, sun, snow or sleet doing chores or just playing in the sun. We had raw milk straight from the barn - although I've never been a big milk drinker. Most of my carbs came from veggies and fruit. We made everything from ingredients and didn't really eat anything that came from a box. I was probably 16 before I even tasted pizza! We didn't have much TV time as that was reserved for when after all the chores were done. (Yeah, I agree that was pretty sweet). I was typically tan, fit, and never overweight for my height.

Age 18 came with moving, getting a job, getting a loan for college. Married at 19 and a mom at age 20. Never got back down to pre pregnancy weight but was around 10 lbs over that. Last baby was breast fed for a couple months. Most of my 30's and 40's I was eating SAD and my weight stayed between normal to +30.
Tried WW (about 4 times) and even got my lifetime membership status, then gained it back. Tried exercise, weight came off, then back. Had my gallbladder removed. Next we'll fast forward to 2010.

### Justfourme — 2013-11-04T03:14:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-99763>

Fast forward - peri menopausal, overweight, overworked, stressed and bad diet. I gave up soda. But had terrible cravings and replaced soda with chocolate. Fatigue, low energy, some migraines. Weight kept creeping up and nothing would allow weight loss. Everything that I had tried before, now would not budge the scale. I was miserable and decided to try this diet I had never heard of before. That was the beginning of my HCG journey. It was like a miracle! When I was about 15 lbs from goal weight, I started hearing about this new thing called leptin, found Dr. K. And this forum. About 3 months after meeting my goal, I started eating paleo, did the BAB, Leptin reset, avoided night shades. I was feeling the best I had in decades.
Now let's add in some stress - two divorces of very close peeps, lost my job, mom passed away all w/in a couple months. Still felt good and was maintaining. Tried to do CT and just couldn't stand the cold. (Later found out from the wise Dr K that I needed to get labs and get hormones and thyroid fixed). Yes that's right - get your labs and hormones fixed first. I found a wonderful doc and started to get labs.
Add in a new job and more stress, official menopause, and the new job is in a building full of EMF. First 6 mo of new job = + 10 lbs, ditto second six months. Double the work load and I'm at my desk 12 hrs per day. Next 6 mo, 20 lb gain, ditto for the next 6 mo.
Still fixing my low thyroid using supplements, started BHRT, and fixing my low cortisol.
Posting labs next...

### caroline — 2013-11-04T03:15:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-99764>

Hi Justfourme and Welcome!....

I am so happy to see you starting a journal.... Dr. Kruse likes journals so he can get some context. He has no chance of answering any of your questions unless he knows something about you!
He said the other day ..... it is like wanting a mechanic to fix your car - but you leave the car at home!

Also - if we don't ask questions .... he doesn't know what we don't know! This is some pretty tough science stuff for some of us non- geek people. I have to keep reading things over and over and over and that is what he expects us to do - if that is what it takes! My brain is in pain all the time these days .... but I love it .....I love trying to connect the dots. It does get easier and sometimes you will read a blog that you read a year ago ....and the lights will finally go on

Please go to the home page and read the easy start guides- they are excellent.

Have you read Dr. K's book - it is a great way to get to know him and what he is all about and trying to do with his blog and this forum. It is available in Kindle or paper. It has a really good section on labs etc.

Also - listen to his podcasts - they are a quick way to get information and he is easy to listen to.

If you can manage it ..... become a gold member .... you will then have access to all the webinars at anytime that is convenient for you and there is a follow up live Q&A a few days later and you can ask a question on anything - He often goes for a couple of hours - one went for more than 4 hours!

There is a "fix your hormone" bootcamp starting tomorrow with Dr. Tim Jackson. He is one of the good guys! This would be invaluable for you as you are entering the mine field of menopause. You can listen to a free community wide webinar that Dr. Jackson just did a week or so ago. Please do listen to that.

### caroline — 2013-11-04T03:28:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-99765>

Oh - I didn't see you hadn't finished yet!! Please post your labs in the lab section - They might not get seen here in your journal.
Go to the top of this page and click on forum and go down about 7 categories - I think!

How did your pregnancies go? that is a big tell for Dr. K. too.

It sounds like you have a lot of this figured out already - good for you!

Light cycles are really, really important .... as is grounding. You need to rise and set with the sun! Do you have blue blocker glasses?

Non native EMF is hugely important. Can you turn the breaker off to your bedroom? get rid of your TV and alarm clock and anything else that emit EMF where you sleep. Do you have a smart meter?

### Inger — 2013-11-04T09:02:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-99787>

Welcome to journal land Justfourme

Sounds too bad, your job... could you look for another kind of job?

### Justfourme — 2014-02-12T00:11:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-109702>

Help please...heart issues...I normally just read a lot vs posting much, but I really need your input. I mainly follow the paleo diet and have intentions of moving more toward the epi-paleo. Since I do most of the cooking, my husband more or less follows my diet with the exception of some occasional bread. I also try to stay away from all processed foods, dyes, chemicals, preservatives, try to eat local and organic, GMO and gluten free. I also am opposed to using statins. Both of us were "prescription free".

He had a heart attach last week. 100% blockage of the RCA, cardiac cath, angioplasty with one stent.
A few stats: age 53 male, non smoker, BMI of 31, total cholesterol 200, LDL 131, HDL 48, trig 105. BP around 120/78.
He doesn't eat as much of the healthy fats as I do on a daily basis.
So now we are trying to navigate thru the throngs of cardiac rehab and dietitians -who of course are pushing the normal food pyramid, wanting to eat lots of grains, eliminate fats, butter, coconut oil.
They are very concerned where we are getting our fiber from...uh...fresh veggies? Fruit? No breads??
I did get a small concession on their part on omega 3's. They want breads, grains added for fiber.

They also automatically put him on a statin to get the LDL lower. Thank goodness I already had him on co q 10. They have agreed to discuss the possibility of reducing the statin in 6 mo and seeing if he can eventually go off of it.

So here am I, the bad guy who wants to give her spouse healthy fats, no grains and no statins.

Advice on how to navigate thru this mess?

### Justfourme — 2014-06-19T04:08:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-131340>

Test, test and more tests. Have you ever read things on the forums and thought "well, I don't have that"? Guess what - tests reveal leaky gut and problems with methylation. So in addition to no gluten, add no dairy, eggs and most beans to the list of foods to avoid. Eggs were probably the hardest to deal with. Hardly had much dairy, except for some cheese, and didn't eat many beans before so those weren't very hard to give up.
Brain fog, fatigue and choc/carb cravings are now gone. Thyroid function is back up. Eating more seafood, leafy greens, And taking glutathione and methyl B's, Resveratrol and probiotics.
Going back to re-read all the blogs for leaky gut.

Now if someone could point me in the right direction to tell when the leaky gut is healed...

### Josh — 2014-06-19T04:31:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-131350>

> [Justfourme said:](/goto/post?id=131340)
>
> Test, test and more tests. Have you ever read things on the forums and thought "well, I don't have that"? Guess what - tests reveal leaky gut and problems with methylation. So in addition to no gluten, add no dairy, eggs and most beans to the list of foods to avoid. Eggs were probably the hardest to deal with. Hardly had much dairy, except for some cheese, and didn't eat many beans before so those weren't very hard to give up.
> Brain fog, fatigue and choc/carb cravings are now gone. Thyroid function is back up. Eating more seafood, leafy greens, And taking glutathione and methyl B's, Resveratrol and probiotics.
> Going back to re-read all the blogs for leaky gut.
>
> Now if someone could point me in the right direction to tell when the leaky gut is healed...

Well "repair" reduces and or ends the persistent chronic version. Our intestines are designed to leak to gather DNA and any stressor that overwhelms mucosal immunity which is redox dependent will open up the guts and possibly the brain anytime for some period of time. Think about a rash on the skin....

### elvey90 — 2013-11-21T10:52:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101233>

Hey All,

Decided to make myself accountable for my own progress hence starting a journal, hopefully this will help me and other people as well in similar positions.
Not sure how this works but I guess I'll start by posting my situation just to provide some context and I'll go from there.

Before getting into Paleo or Epi Paleo, I was eating unwholesome foods, cheap restaurants, soy sauce and lots of carbs (not the good ones), boozing, late nights etc and my energy, mood, inner psychology was going crazy, literally. Also, I was skinny bulk (does that even make sense lol?) for my size, aka, no muscular tone but still noticeable bulk. I was also very active playing soccer, tennis and weight training rotating them every day. However, by not eating right and not recovering body and always pushing my body to do more, I always felt tired and fatigued to the point that it was driving me mad inside because I wasn't able to sustain myself without knowing why. So my diet was a mess, and like I said it didn't help me mentally, moreover external situations made it worse and had bouts of depression. One day I got injured, snapped my foot in half (literally) and I have been out and still to this day (in terms of physical activity). I was still not eating properly until April (2013) when enough was enough and decided consciously to make a decision to better myself physically.

Hence I stumbled upon the Paleo diet, a friend of mine told me a few years ago about it and I couldn't believe that I still remembered it. So ever since I've been eating a Paleo and my relationship with food started to change. In turn I've started to appreciate myself as well, instead of trying to push/exert myself all the time, I started to appreciate my body and started to listen to it more and more. I gradually started to recover, my foot was healing and with a sense of clarity I started to investigate more and more into my relationship with food and in turn realizes the effects that it has on me mentally. Good food = smarter brain! (Yes, that was a huge realization for me....)

Long story short, I continued Paleo on an 80/20 principle cheating on certain days but until recently I made another conscious decision to stop that entirely. To be good hinders the progress of greatness, at roughly around the same time I found out about leangains IF and started to incorporate it into my lifestyle. I tried IF for a few weeks but it didn't feel right, was moody at times and energy was all over the place. Coming back to the present moment, I stumbled across Epi-Paleo and Jack Kruse website and holy heck, I fell in love (yea sounds a bit corny haha). Going through the blogs (not even close to reading and digesting all of it) has definitely been a humbling experience.

So here I am, starting with the Leptin RX, though I may not need it (according to my Labs - see Lab section), I started integrating the principles anyway. Switching from skipping breakfast when on IF and have instead a BAB and that lasts me till 4PM on average then I go home from my job and eat dinner before 6PM at latest when the sunsets (Aussie time). That feels sooo much better, much more fulfilled and my energy levels have improved quite a bit (though it's only been 4 days now...yes getting a bit eager hehe). Started to also be more EMF aware and started to do little things like turning off my phone, laptop when I go to sleep, but minimizing EMF's is difficult since I live in a shared room at the moment. I'm also planning on incorporating CT in my own way, cold showers and swimming in cold pools (looking for a pool now) and gradually move onto bath tubs when I actually find some time.

So far I've been researching and doing everything myself, having a lone wolf mentality. However I've come to realized that sometimes it's better to be vulnerable and accept the fact that I can't do everything myself. So I'm writing this journal with this purpose in mind, not only to improve but also not be afraid to share a bit of myself.
Wow that felt like a touching moment

In terms of physical goals, is to get that 6pac that I've always wanted and pretty much just have a better overall body composition. May sound vain but I do believe that if you really appreciate yourself, you will take care of your body which definitely in turn correlates with mental health.

I'm also hoping to maintain a state of clear, present mindfulness and mental performance through eating authentic and wholesome foods. Everyday is a new day, so why not make it awesome

Cheers All!

### elvey90 — 2013-11-22T12:37:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101332>

Today is a good day until lunch time but we'll get to that later.

So have been eating BAB's every day now, slowly finishing all the poultry I have in the fridge and leaving my organic drumsticks last. It consists of 3x eggs, 2-3 sausages, 2-3 chicken thighs (which I'm removing from diet), 1x avocado, 1 glass of coconut milk with organic cacao powder. Throughout the days I drink Nobles water or if I'm at work I refill with RO water.

I've been feeling good and I'm much more aware of the effects of EMF has on my body and the foods I eat.
Now at lunch today, my colleagues decided to go out for lunch, so out of obligation (worst choice), we went along to a Korean restaurant (Kimchi Hut). Everyone ordered their usual crap (harsh but lets face it, we all know it's crappy food haha, in this case it was rice, vegetable oil cooked meat, probably not pasture fed eggs etc) but I couldn't help but myself and ordered seafood. So here I thought yay something that I can eat and then the dish came out....IT WAS FREAKIN FRIED in what not (yea I don't even know what else was in it)...and you know what, I ate it anyway.

Yes I am kicking myself but at the same time I've accepted what I ate and face the consequences that follows. Was kinda on a high and felt floaty the whole day (but it had no sugar and I was on a high, maybe it was self induced), but thankfully nothing noticeable detrimental but did feel sluggish in a sense like my brain was slow (is this placebo?? or am I taking this out of proportion). Though I'm probably not leptin resistant (according to labs), I still wanted to follow this protocol cause I do have a sense of a somewhat carb craving (or maybe it's my own mentally arrghghghg). So I've added on another 10 days onto the calendar with this protocol to review, and made sure I understood the lessons which also relates to how I approach to life as well:

\- Never ever do things out of obligation, in every situation you have a choice.
\- Learn to accept what you did, there is no change without adversity.
\- To gain optimal health, turn inward and find out the reason why your doing in the first place and let it drive you.

\- And most importantly, ask the waiter/waitress how your food is going to be cooked or served raw (which is what I thought)...

With the third point, as I'm reflecting now, I'm re-questioning and re-affirming my reason of doing this. As JK says, there's no room for good, there's only great (is that what he said? Doesn't matter sounded epic in my head).

Yes this small little plate of fried seaweed plus other crap has got me more focused and discipline into achieving my goal and also my values for health. Ok Rant over

Yet despite this, I chose to have a good day and started researching on where to get seafood, the bracelet encoder that I'm going to purchase (seriously I think it looks good, think I'll even just get it because of that haha) and the Greenwave EMF filter. I've purchased Ghee and Coconut Oil online, will be making ghee myself after this batch thanks to a couple of individuals who showed me how and the best way to do it (thanks guys!). Before I purchase the EMF filter I've sent a couple of questions to them, one of them being whether they ship to Australia with it's relevant plugins, so waiting on that before I purchase it.

Tomorrow is a new day and will be heading into the markets looking for seafood and also the protocol resets

Excited

PS: Dinner, I had 1x GF Porterhouse Steak, with 1x serving of broccoli and coconut butter, made sure I had protein and fats with this meal, and I bought Blue Blocking glasses of Ebay

### sooperb — 2013-11-22T13:46:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101338>

Don't be too hard on your meal choice Elvey. One's life is always bound up with others, some you love, some you don't but all will exert an influence of some sort. Saying no and sticking out for what you believe in is never easy and you will upset even those you love along the way. I fail too often because by nature i want to help and oblige others, putting my wants first is quite difficult, I dislike confrontation. I am managing to make changes and stick to them, blue blocking glasses for a start. So my OH thinks it's bunkum, so what, I'm doing this for me and he can come along for the ride or he can stay where he is, it's about choices isn't it. If my choices aren't his choices I don't care, just as long as he doesn't make my sticking to my choices too difficult. On my side I have all the occasions when I've been right, which helps  Everyone falls off the wagon sometimes, you just need to dust yourself off and climb back on and try harder next time.

### caroline — 2013-11-22T23:20:03+0200

### caroline — 2013-11-22T23:29:43+0200

BTW ..... people do notice what you are doing - even f they don't say much. Your stickwithitnedness \[is that a word?\] is what they are observing and taking note of. My friends now know the way I am eating and living and are seeing all the positive benefits ..... how can you argue with that!

### sooperb — 2013-11-23T01:47:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101417>

I have found that trying to make healthy, informed choices while out dining with friends etc. makes them uncomfortable because they seem to take it as an unspoken criticism of their choices. Dining with anyone who doesn't care what they eat is difficult, more so with some than others. My hubby has just agreed to a venue for a meal out with friends in an Indian restaurant he knows I don't like because I am convinced they use MSG which invariably gives me palpitations and night sweats and they are very heavy on salt, plus I'm convinced that some of their chicken is pre-formed. I could refuse to go but I won't because he will be embarrassed and angry if I do. If I refuse food while I'm there he will be even more annoyed.

Then there's the temptation to eat things that you know you shouldn't like parathas or naan bread. I can fully understand why Elvey gave way, sometimes it's easier even though you know it's bad and you'll pay for it afterwards.

### caroline — 2013-11-23T02:33:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101420>

Sooperb ... just call ahead and ask about their menu and tell them you cant have MSG \[allergic\] and or gluten intolerant.

I wrote a post about this long ago. We had a restaurant for 35 years. Believe me - owners are very happy to comply. The last thing you want is having to call an ambulance ..... besides - you want happy customers!

Pick a time to call when the restaurant will be less busy and ask for the owner/ manager and just ask for help in choosing something from their menu.

Sooperb - you are right - some take it as a criticism ... but I really find that people are impressed that you have very specific things that you are doing for your health and well being and won't be swayed from your commitment. How could we possibly eat that way - now that we know so much better.

I never make a big deal of anything .... and when you say you are allergic - people do understand that.

The one thing I have found over and over......even tho my shape and size has changed considerably .... the thing people always say is that I look so healthy and fit .... my weight is never mentioned.

Make no mistake - they do pay attention....

I must have borrowed your soapbox ....LOL

### prAna303 — 2013-11-23T10:31:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101462>

> I've shut down my TV (hardly use it anyway), turn off my laptops, make sure all switches are turned off, I've unplugged my alarm clock as well so that I rely on my natural circadian clock.

One big thing many forgets is that just because one shuts off the technology with a switch do not mean it does not "live".

A simple way of describing it is like a speaker, when you listen to music thru a set of speakers you do not want to have any other sets in the same room. Even if they just stand in a corner they will start to resonate with the soundsource. Natural frequencies are analog but today we also use man made ones that acts like particles. This makes stand alone gadgets work in weird ways... It is a form of ghosting.

So i mean that you must always remove the complete source of technology, not just kill the power to it.

### prAna303 — 2013-11-23T20:22:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101481>

For example phone and loudspeakers and this can apply to many electronic devices...

> The type of interference can occur if the following things happen
> together:
> 1) a pulsing radio transmitter,
> 2) with relatively strong power,
> 3) in very close proximity,
> 4) to a non-linear circuit element.
>
> The non-linear circuit element is usually some sort of solid state
> device such as a transistor or diode. If the non-linear element is
> subjected to a strong pulsing radio signal, it will act as a rectifier
> and "detect" the pulsating waveform, i.e., convert the pulsations from
> a radio frequency to an audio frequency (if the pulsation rate is in
> the pass-band of audio frequencies.) For example, a hearing aid
> consists of a microphone, an audio amplifier and a small speaker. If
> a strong pulsating radio signal impinges upon the first transistor
> amplifier stage, the transistor will be driven into its non-linear
> range and detect the pulsations. If the pulsation rate is in the
> audio frequency range, the rest of the hearing aid amplifier will
> amplify this and deliver it to the speaker, to the great annoyance of
> the hearing aid wearer.
>
>
>
> Read the full text here: [http://mentalfloss.com/article/1758...speakers-go-blip-blip-blip-buzz#ixzz2lUhtwkR9](http://mentalfloss.com/article/17586/why-cell-phones-make-speakers-go-blip-blip-blip-buzz#ixzz2lUhtwkR9)
> \--brought to you by mental\_floss!

### elvey90 — 2013-11-27T11:47:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101719>

Alright time for an update, I've been busy studying exams and finished my first one this morning, feeling pretty awesome right now haha

Anyway so on the weekend bought 5kg worth of salmon at the markets and going to incorporate more and more seafood. My chicken batch is running out and probably after tomorrow will be all gone so no more chicken. So far my diet is looking good, on the weekend I had my BAB and lunch and didn't feel hungry for dinner, this was the first time that it happened to me and was pretty excited when that happened. My carb cravings seem to be dying down a lot, thought not totally gone, if I do have carb cravings, I usually have 1 glass of coconut milk with cacao powder and maca powder with my meal. Apart from that one day I had breakfast and lunch skipping dinner, every other day I have my BAB, not feeling hungry till 4PM then having dinner when I get home from work.

So far my carbs have been >30-50g (not counting) through green veggies but thinking of lowering it even more. So far my food choices are:

BAB:

2x pork or beef sausages
2-3 hard boiled eggs
2-3 chicken thighs (once these are gone I'll replace it with 200-400g salmon, or whatever fish I can find)
1 glass of coconut milk with cacao powder (I have these at dinner now due to time restraints I have in the morning)
1 cup of Bok Choy dipped in coconut oil and ghee

Dinner

400g GF Steak
Mussels or 200-400g Fish
2-3 eggs
Cabbage or Broccoli dipped in coconut oil or ghee.

I'm still eating my BAB within 30 mins but sometimes it may stretch to 45 mins though doesn't really concern me.
My water source is still RO water or San Vittoria/Pellegrino.

Overall I feel great, one thing I noticed is that when I really fill myself with fats like coconut oil and ghee, my brain just kicks into gear, it's the same feeling when I first got onto Paleo in April but this feeling is just even better, much more energy and just feeling pretty good about myself. Though I must say that I have been involved with heavy personal development and philosophy studies which combined with having a healthy lifestyle living, the process and feeling just accentuates.
I was reading Barry's thread with his keto + endurance + fasting experiments and there was one phrase that really struck me that rings true, it's been in harmony with oneself and understanding that all nature's law's apply, one aspect of life cannot exist without the other.

I'm still at the beginning stages, EMF is one of the issues I'm trying to resolve but quite difficult due to living in a shared accommodation, those Greenwave filters look very tempting to get first thing now.

### elvey90 — 2013-11-28T02:09:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101783>

> [Hemming said:](/goto/post?id=101720)
>
> Holy smokes! That is an impressive amount of food. I don't understand how you can eat that much protein. I thought I was eating a fair amount of food but I'm completely dwarfed by you  Do you know approx. how much fat you eat? Can I ask how tall you are and how much you weigh?

I am about 1.75m high and weigh about 71-72kg (haven't really measured). Though just to provide some context I look a lot thinner than I use to be, when I was gymming and eating non-paleo foods I was fluctuating between 72-75kg but looked much more bulky. Ever since I got onto Paleo I've looked a lot leaner (maybe too lean).

I don't really count fats, I usually have a massive scoop of ghee and coconut oil in every meal and if I do have coconut milk, it's 1 glass so that's about 55g of fat. I do realize I have been eating a bit too much protein and working to getting it down and eating more fatty foods like seafood & offal.

Oh I forgot to add that I have 1x avocado in the mornings usually and if I miss that then I have it for dinner, so that's another 15g of fat approx.

But I honestly don't count to the extreme, it's more of a reference of how much I eat. I just eat whatever I feel like, and at the moment I am working to getting that protein down a bit and increase fats.

### elvey90 — 2013-11-29T12:04:38+0200

caroline is absolutely right, we eat more and we stay lean, how freaking awesome is that haha, if you were here I would hi5 you caroline!

Quick Update:

Food Intake:

Breakfast:
\- 250-400g fish
\- 2-3 eggs
\- 1 glass coconut milk with cacao powder and maca powder
\- 1 avocado
\- 2 sausages
\- liberal amounts of coconut oil

Dinner:
\- 250g minced meat
\- 1 broccoli
\- 1 onion
\- 2 eggs
\- 7 mussels (man these were good)
\- liberal amounts of coconut oil and ghee

Water:
San Vittoria (Italian Sparkling Mineral Water)
**Question: The label shows high amounts of bicarbonate, is this stuff good or bad for you?**

Now this is gonna be where I start experimenting a little, I'm going to try and reduce my BAB by removing sausages altogether, they are around 15g protein each if anyone is curious, but will be consistently having 3 eggs and of course my lovely fatty fats.
For dinner, that's going to stay the same for now but I will add another glass of coconut milk.
I'm also taking cod liver fish oil if I remember to take it about 1 teaspoon.

Still no exercise of any shape of form but I'm leaning towards swimming because it's water and its cold  (I've love being in water, feels good  ) and am going to start to experiment to change my body composition without going to the gym and do weights (been doing that for 3 years with each year plateuing end of each year, but was constantly sore and have niggling injuries), having said that I might go casually and do some kettlebells or battle ropes, something different.

Still figuring out the best EMF situation for my place, definitely going to get Greenwave filters and chuck em all over the place, and I'm going to replace my lightbulb with red amber bulbs if I can find them, at the moment I'm using yellow light bulbs. I am slowly removing the electrical devices that I don't need as well.

Other than that, constantly working on being present and aware.

**Another Queston: I'm getting beef gelatin soon, is it recommended to be taken separately from protein powder or it doesn't matter? And if taken away from meals does that count as a snack or should it be taken with meals?**

### sooperb — 2013-11-30T19:54:34+0200

Bit confused here, I thought the reset was only something like 75g of protein? That's roughly 3ozs which isn't a lot, certainly not as much as you guys and gals are ingesting. Maca powder I thought was a carb? Drinking coconut milk sounds great but again, isn't it a carb in that form? Italian sparkling water has a much higher salt content than most, San Pellegrino was always on our banned list because of this, how times are a changing

### cinnamon — 2013-11-30T20:20:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101906>

Hi sooperb,

The amount they are aiming for is the protein content in the food. For example, 100g of pan fried bacon has 38g of protein (try [this link](http://nutritiondata.self.com/facts/pork-products/7356/2) to nutrition data). What makes up the rest of the 100g is mostly fat (40g) and some water content. So, you would need to eat nearly half a pound of bacon (200g) to get to 75g of protein.

### sooperb — 2013-11-30T20:32:44+0200

### elvey90 — 2013-12-04T01:55:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-102233>

Time for an update,

In terms of food, I've minimized my protein size for my meals (still eating 2 meals) and literally just rub them in Fat!

BAB:
250g of GF minced meat with chopped onions and red cabbage
3x eggs (hard boiled)
1x Avocado
1 Tbsp (approx) of coconut oil (maybe more, I take swigs of coconut oil at times)
1 cup of Coconut Milk with Cacao Powder & Maca Powder (gonna stop the Maca once I finish)

Dinner:
250-400g fish (fresh atlantic salmon)
2x eggs (scrambled)
1x handful of Kale
Loads of coconut oil and ghee
1x cup of coconut milk (if I feel like it)

I definitely feel a lot better than I was a couple of weeks ago and what I mean is by better is not the 'forever delusioned utopian happyness' but rather much more aware and the conscious changes of what's happening within me and around me. I definitely feel the EMF's affecting me, yesterday at my job, a colleage turned on a server right next to me and immediately a massive headache plus feelings of tiredness crept in.

I'm seeing a specialist tomorow for my foot before I start any form of excercises but still looking at swimming. The challenge is how I'm going to time when I swim and when I eat with the constraints of studying and my job.

It's been a heavy few days but it's also been very very fun!!

### elvey90 — 2013-12-05T02:22:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-102359>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=102357)
>
> you are way ahead of me..... didn't take much did it ...

Curiously, there was a LCHF meetup (Low Carb High Fat) meet up (more like a conference) that happened recently, the presenters that were explaining the science did a very good job, explaining how the excess of carbs causes the insulin resistance and how high fat can help a diabetic etc etc. Interestingly none of them really spoke or talked about ketosis and the effect on the brain, also the benefits of seafoods and DHA for brain function, (though they did touch on a little on the benefits of ketosis as energy for the brain). In terms of food choice, it was mainly on finding and eating grass fed meats, poultry, offal, healthy fats and low carbs from greens etc.

What I found interesting was the examples of polysaturated fats (oils coming from plants and seeds), essential the human body was only meant to have 1:1 ratio, until we added grains and wheat it became 2:1. Now what was really scary is that now an average human takes in 36:1 of these plants and seeds, funnily enough these so called 'healthy' foods are approved by the 'Heart Tick Foundation'. I'll link the video once I get it.

Another interesting thing I found was the analogy of a paleolithic diet compared to a keto fuel based lion. A lion when hunts for food and catches its prey, the first thing it eats is the heart, then the liver, the rest of the organ meats and the last thing it eats is the muscle meat.

Food for thought!

Other than that, it was well presented to an audience who were mainly carb based, (can easily tell by the audience). It was pretty funny to see their reactions especially a diabetic doctor announced her disbelief that eating fats can help with diabetes haha

### elvey90 — 2013-12-16T05:46:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-103564>

Heya All, it's been a while since an update, been busy studying and working through some stuff as well!

With regards to the foods I've been eating, I've cut out greens except for red cabbage, mushrooms and onions and I've minimized my protein intake as well (well kinda). I'm starting to notice that I get less and less hungry during the day especially if I drink lots of RO water. This weekend I managed to just eat breakfast and last the whole day without feeling hungry, no snacks, just water throughout. When this happened I got really excited, went to bed early on that day feeling pretty good. Some of my changes include eating more lamb/beef liver and heart, including random mussells here and there and putting more coconut oil on my foods.

On a side note: I go through ghee ridicously fast and yes I shoulda have listened to you guys (the ones in a separate thread that mentioned to make your own ghee), and have decided to make bulk ghee for myself.

The next morning, I had my BAB and I spilled pink salt all over my food and unfortunately ate through it all. Throughout the whole day, I had stomach cramps, constant burping and stomach grumbling. Was seriously such a tough day especially with my mood going out of wack as well. Anyhow, it's starting to reside today and I feel a lot better! I still have acne (white heads) here and there but not as often and severe anymore, probably a detoxing effect.

So far I must say, I'm really happy eating this way, it's definitely much more fulfilling when I eat and feels 'right' to me. Though there is one thing, sometimes my stomach grumbling plus randomly burping after a meal, is that normal? also with this acne, is there something I can take or do to at least minimize the effects much more effectively?

In terms of CT, I'm just taking cold showers and my skin is getting drier but I read up in the forums that it's probably a detoxing sympton. In terms of EMF, I've decided to get rid of my TV, PS3 and my games (not only an EMF reason but something bigger). Getting greenwave filters are much tougher than I thought since they don't ship to Australia, but so far I've got a couple of salt lamps placed around my room and getting a couple more to place on my desk where my laptop sits. This will have to do until I move out!

Until then I've looked at a water resonator to mitigate some risks with EMF - [http://www.greenfieldnaturals.com/resonator-research-program/](http://www.greenfieldnaturals.com/resonator-research-program/)
Anyone tried or have any thoughts?

It feels like there's so much to do and it feels never-ending sometimes but if there's one thing I learnt, is that be patient and enjoy the process!

### Sammy — 2013-12-17T03:02:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-103631>

Elvey, if you have time, go to the beach and just soak in the water for about 20 minutes. You can use the sand to gently exfoliate your skin. Do this about 3 times a week(the more the better) and your skin will thank you! All my life I had horribly dry scaly skin(have a history of eczema).... sure enough epi-paleo and cold showers definitely improved my skin ALOT(I no longer itch like a madwoman after a shower) but it was going to the beach that really took it to another level. I don't even have to use lotion/creams etc.

# Thread: Frankie L's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankie-ls-journal.8839/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Frankie L — 2013-11-22T16:56:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankie-ls-journal.8839/post-101359>

Well here goes...this is my first time really journalling. So I will provide some brief medical and personal history so we have a context of what I am dealing with. Here is my profile:

Athletic Muscular female
40 years old
Tall, Medium Build
I work out 2-4 times per week at he gym - cardio and weight training
No issues with energy.

**Diet**

Vegetarian for 5 years until November 19, 2012 **Had grass feed red meat!**
Started to eat salmon of March 2013 about 1-2 times a week
Don't eat PUFA oils
Lots of olive and flax seed oil
Still ate eggs while a vegetarian about 2 - 3 per day.
Eliminated Dairy accept for organic 2% cottage cheese and flax oil combined
Don't drink pop, coffee, juice only herbal tea (no caffiene)
Only eat Ezekiel Bread and don't eat any carbs with gluten
May occassional have a cookie or some dessert
Don't drink alcohol
Drink plenty of home-made smoothies such has kale, spinach and swiss chard
Drink Reverse Osmosis water with added trace minerals (I do this)
Don't eat fruit accept for raspberries and blueberries
Eat lentils and chickpeas NO RICE
Drink almond Milk
Eat alot of avacados
Eat 4 tablespoons of Organic coconut oil a day
**Just started to eat read meat as a start to PAELO**

**Medical History**

Cancer:
2006 Diagnosed with Non-Hodgkins Lymphoma Folicular B cell
Told it was incurable
Did 8 weeks of CVPR and 2 Years of Rituxan
Tumour came back in 2010
Did 5 weeks of back-to-back radiation-tumour disappeared
Decided to take matters in my own hands and go natural DID A TON RESEARCH OVER A FEW YEARS
Tumour came back in my neck
Changed diet and started to take supplements to change my chemistry
All tumours are gone and I am in complete remission
I will never ever do chemo or radiation again knowing there are other safer alternatives

Anxiety:
Woke up in January of 2013 worrying incessantly about the lymphoma coming back.
Chronic worry, can't stop, was wondering what the heck happened over night?
This persisted for several months and was at it's peak in March of 2013.
Hands started to tingle and feet
Vibration in legs and feet that would come and go.
Slight tremour in my peripherals espeically in the morning.
Developed numbness in hands and tops of my feet.
Self diagnosed PN.
Stopped worrying about my issues, my body still continues in the "sympathetic state"??
Take St.John's wort if anxiety becomes intolerable.
I feel my adrenal glands are taxed and need a break.

**Daily Supplmentation**

Cancer protocol:
B12, 10,000 iu of D3, Beta 3D glucan, Barely grass, CO Q10 and selenium, vitamin multi-pack

Eyes:
Vision essentials -bilberry and luten (really helps!)

Anxiety and Periperal neuropathy:
Just started with protocol about 2 weeks ago.
Liquid Magnesium, 4 tablespoons of coconut oil, Reservatrol, Krill oil, L-Carnitine, R-Alpha Lipoic Acid, Liquid Iodine drops, Liquid B complex

Overall Health:
Sea vegetables (contains iodine), probiotic,

**Non-Native EMF**
Eliminated cell phone from bedroom and unplugged lamp.
Removed landline phone as well.
Room is dark with no artifical light.
Have 2 alarm clocks but are positioned away from the bed
Cell phone is turned off when I 'm not using it. Never in my pocket.

Ok, can't think of anything else at the moment.

### caroline — 2013-11-23T00:56:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankie-ls-journal.8839/post-101409>

Some early thoughts Frankie ......you need to get rid of the alarm clocks. Can you turn off the breaker to your bedroom?

Do you have a smart meter? do your neighbours have a smart meter? Do you use a microwave? It should be unpluged.

Have you considered starting the Leptin reset - using the epi paleo template? C/T will be huge for you.

There are easy start guides on the home page.

You really need to clean up your diet ....ditch the pulses and the ezekial bread and the fruit for now.

just eat simply from the epi paleo template.

Don't hesitate to ask lots of questions ..... there is a ton of stuff here to take on board.

If you can manage - join us for Dr. Tim Jackson's "Heal your hormones" bootcamp. He is giving us an awesome amount of information and he is very easy to understand. Have you listened to his free webinar?

As I said - keep reading and asking questions .... there are lots of great people here to help!

Thanks for starting a journal and sharing all that with us - have a look at other's journals when you have time .....may people are stuggeling with all kinds of stuff and they could benefit from your experience too.

That wasn't so hard was it?

# Thread: Optimization to the Rescue!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimization-to-the-rescue.8938/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal

### ozbuck — 2013-12-02T01:44:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimization-to-the-rescue.8938/post-102001>

Hi,

I'm a late-20's male from Australia.

I recently had a whole bunch of bloodtests and a salivary cortisol test done. My saliva test showed that I had HIGH cortisol levels from 8am-8pm. The rest of the tests my integrative doc said they were fine (I'm in the process of getting her to send me a copy of the results so I can get some further input here).

My symptoms of non-optimized health are:

\* never feel refreshed and rejuvenated upon waking. It takes 30-60mins to get going.
\* wake up during the night a few times to roll over and fall back to sleep again. I don't have any problems falling asleep though.
\* doctor believes I have adrenal fatigue. I struggle to put on weight however - 6'2" and 72kg.
\* acne and rosacea - acne has reduced considerably since eating PHD style and taking daily zinc and b6.
\* chronically stuffy nose - all the mouth breathing could be contributing to high cortisol as it activated the sympathetic nervous system. Sometimes one nostril will open up so I can breathe through it, then close up and the other nostril may open up. But most of the time both nostrils are stuffy (i think the nasal passages are swollen)
\* anxiety and feelings of being down.
\* struggle to build muscle mass - might be just genetic but high cortisol surely isn't helping.
\* struggle to concentrate. some brain fog i guess.
\* sometimes i feel really tired after eating a meal.

Some other info:

\* I've been eating paleo/primal/PHD for around 3 years.
\* My diet before that was bad. Plenty of processed foods, sugar, take away, etc.
\* In high school my diet was shocking. Sugar laden cereal or I would just skip breakfast. Sometimes I would skip eating during the day at school and then come home and eat a frozen meat pie, or buy a milkshake, etc. and then eat dinner. Soft drink was always had nearly every day.
\* Stress - social anxiety especially - has always been in my life. I think a lot of it is psychologically based (I'm going to get the hypnotherapy and NLP done soon). But I also think that imbalanced biochemistry in the brain doesn't help either.

Any thoughts, insights and info from any of you is appreciated!

Thanks

### ozbuck — 2013-12-03T00:54:08+0200

Hi Caroline,

PHD = Perfect Health Diet.

I'm starting the leptin reset tomorrow... I don't feel hungry when I wake up. I've been doing intermittent fasting so I usually don't eat til around 10am (i wake around 6.30am).

My daily meals revolve around this usually.... protein: lamb, beef, occassionally chicken, once a week salmon, cheese, yogurt. fat: from meat, coconut oil, lots of butter, palm oil, eggs. Carbs: potato, white rice, vegetables, fruit, honey.

I know Jack doesn't advise eating potatoes or rice until after leptin rx is completed because it can fuel carb cravings, but I don't have any carb cravings. Im a skinny dude. I used to be VLC for a couple of years and feel much better with 100-150 carbs a day, especially from potatoes with plenty of butter on them!

I definitely need to up my seafood intake more!

Havent had a chance to listen to his podcasts yet. I will though!

Thanks for your help!!

### caroline — 2013-12-03T01:10:42+0200

Oz - please read very carefully about the Leptin reset! Dr. K. has never advised to go back to potatoes etc after the reset!

This is primarily a high fat, medium protein, low carb way of eating. You must be eating seasonally if at all possible.

You need to reset the "Rolex" in your brain. Timing is everything! Huge for you!

just to be clear ... and this is easy but very, very important

within 30 minutes of rising ..... go outside and look at the sun and then have a BAB ... at least 50g. of protein

leave at least 4 hours between meals ..... absolutely no snacking ....and leave at least 4 hours after eating and before bed.

there is a great easy start guide .... have a look and then follow exactly. This definitely 100% works. You will be craving free and you will feel much more satisfied.

You may have to force yourself to eat the BAB within 30 minutes of waking at first - but this is REALLY important. Make no mistake about this.

Have you got Jack's book? It really is all there ........

Keep reading and asking questions!

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-03T19:35:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimization-to-the-rescue.8938/post-102199>

I still haven't had confirmation that I've calculated this correctly but I think your BUN/creatinine ratio is 18.77

S UREA 5.7 mmol/L (3.0-7.5) = 15.96 (BUN)
S CREAT. 75 umol/L (60-110) = 0.85

15.96/0.85 = 18.77

[http://www.endmemo.com/medical/unitconvert/Urea\_nitrogen.php](http://www.endmemo.com/medical/unitconvert/Urea_nitrogen.php)
[http://www.endmemo.com/medical/unitconvert/Creatinine.php](http://www.endmemo.com/medical/unitconvert/Creatinine.php)

If this is correct then you're dehydrated as your result is above 15.

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-03T19:47:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimization-to-the-rescue.8938/post-102202>

RDW 14.4 (11-17)

"Red cell distribution width or RDW is a parameter that measures variation in red blood cell size or red blood cell volume. A high RDW (over 14.5%) means that the red blood cells vary a lot in size. A normal RDW is 11.6 to 14.6%, but researchers from the Intermountain Medical Center Heart Institute found that patients with a RDW level greater than or equal to 12.9% had an increased risk for depression."

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthre...Red-Cell-Distribution-Width-Levels-Depression](http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?8891-Link-Between-Red-Cell-Distribution-Width-Levels-Depression)

This can be caused by deficiencies in b12, folate, iron, selenium, vitamin E and possibly some other things.

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-04T12:04:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimization-to-the-rescue.8938/post-102274>

Your B12 is actually a little low, Jack has said in one of his blog posts he likes B12 to be over 1,000.

> B12 another cause of macrocytic anemia, neuropathy, posterior column dysfunction (vibration sense) and subacute combined degeneration. If any of these occur, it tells you that you are very deficient body wide and in your diet. Most vegans and vegetarians are quite deficient in this. I have actually seen two vegans paralyzed from this in my career. I like people’s levels over 1000 on their blood testing screening. Often times those with a leaky gut can’t absorb it well and it requires injections to elevate. People who are diabetic and who use metformin need higher levels of B12 because metformin uses up B12 to a much greater degree metabolically.

[http://www.jackkruse.com/what-can-you-find-about-your-own-health/](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-can-you-find-about-your-own-health/)

You can also be B12 deficient in the brain but have normal levels in the body. Freddd had degeneration of his spinal cord and needs to take very large amounts of B12 for it to get into his central nervous system (CNS). I believe he's now having problems with the Jarrow Formulas B12 but I've not really been keeping up to date with his protocol.

[http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?threads/active-b12-protocol-basics.10138/](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?threads/active-b12-protocol-basics.10138/)

# Thread: Excited to Join The Optimal Community: A Summary of my first two weeks.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-27T22:28:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104305>

Hello,
My name is Chris Wyllie, I've introduced myself and provided some of my background info in the Meet and Great.

I analyze everything with an open, critical mind, and try to always think horizontally; putting the pieces of the puzzle together to provide a holistic picture from different disciplines - evolutionary bio, cognition, neuroscience etc.

Everything I do revolves around maximizing human performance. I specialize in maximizing brain function and cognition, employing many bio-hacks, including neurofeedback, bio-energetic, biaurnal brainwave entrainment, biofeedback, nootropics, nutraceuticals. Mindfulness, meditation, and Qigong, are critical to cultivating the strength of mind to make things happen in my life.

As an entrepreneur, student looking to embark on a dual M.B.A/J.D degree, and athlete looking to unleash super-human performance for myself and clients....I am completely captivated by the phenomenal knowledge and discussion in OPTIMAL community. Hopping onto the road to Optimal may be the game-changing BIO-HACK to fuel all my endeavors and aspirations.

I've spent the last two weeks, devouring the information that Dr. Kruse has provided. After many re-reads, and contemplation, I am convinced. The path to Optimal is intuitive, and I find that every piece of the puzzle is ultimately tied together. However, I want to put Jack's information to the test, and see what these principles can do for me. It is my mission to maximize every bit of my potential. If I have to deal with a little bit of cold, so be it.

Week 1, 2:
My Diet has always been OPTIMAL. Consuming Superfoods like (Brain On, an algae, which I believe, and I'm sure Jack will concur, is the best food on the planet). I'm an advocate of the Bulletproof lifestyle: IF (coffee with MCT, Pastured butter, Cacao Butter) due to the awesome cognitive enhancement provided by the Ketones and Coffee combo. In the afternoon I usually lift heavy, or perform some very intense HIIT. Followed by some Qigong and Meditation to facilitate recovery. A sizeable portion of grass-fed Offal is usually dinner. I'm going to tailor carb intake even more strictly to the seasons given Dr. Kruse's recommendation.

Overwhelming CT experience: As a young, ambitious guy, reading the CT series, especially CT 6, absolutely blew me away. Immediately after, I went outside in my boxers and started dumping piles of snow in my bath tub: I had to create my own ice packs. On went the cold water and I hopped in the tub for 45 minutes. Strength of mind enabled me grin and bear the violent shivering, but I woke up with some nasty swelling and welts all over my body. Itchiness pervaded my entire body!! During the last two weeks, I've continued with the cold tubs, reducing the duration to 30 minutes. Obviously I started way to quickly, but am happy to say that I am almost cold-adapted. I felt exhilarated after my last 30 minute tub and didn't dread it one minute.

CT appealed so much to me because not only would it facilitate recovery from chronic over-training, but it would enhance cognition and productivity by increasing the current of flow in the brain. Minimizing inflammation in the brain was another critical point for me.

Questions for the community:
1\. To accelerate cold adaptation, minimize inflammation, would a super potent DHA fish oil - say Nordic Naturals Pharma. grade - be advisable? I've self experimented with 0 to 15 grams of high DHA fish oil, and have noticed large improvements with high doses.

2\. As a Qigong practitioner, one element of CT that scared me, is that the cold would literally "dampen my fire" or "Qi / Life Energy" that I work so hard through meditation and movements to accumulate. Or does CT have the reverse effect, by improving conductivity, minimizing inflammation, it improves Qi circulation and accumulation.

3\. Please tell me your top productivity, and brain enhancing BIO-HACKS!!

### caroline — 2013-12-27T23:43:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104307>

So happy to have you here.....can't wait to hear more....

### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-28T02:05:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104314>

Thanks Caroline. What have been the most actionable and rewarding aspects of the information Jack has provided in your life?

### caroline — 2013-12-28T03:07:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104318>

Gosh .... interesting question ...... I will have to think on my feet here ....

I think - when I first ran into JK on MDA and paleo hacks .... it all finally made sense!

I dove in head first and never looked back.

I had been a binge eater most of my life and no matter what I did I could not control it. It is amazing how one can be successful in other parts of your life ... but you can't control your weight no matter how hard you try and you can't stop stuffing your gob. The lack of control made me feel like such a failure.

Within a week of starting the leptin reset I was completely binge free and more importantly - craving free - and not hungry all the time!

I was lucky enough to have a couple of consults with Dr. K. and he helped me straighten out my hormones and I have a lovely Doc here who is willing to play along..... still a work in progress tho.....

I think if you had told me two years ago that changes would happen slowly - I might not have been too impressed!
But having said that ......I love all the changes that are happening to mind, body, spirit and I would not change one single second of this journey.

It is the quantum entanglement and quantum magic that I love the most of all. I can see the joy all around me .... I am having the time of my life ...

### caroline — 2013-12-28T06:31:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104331>

Chris .... Do you know who Erwan Lecorre is? Movnat?

Dr. K. Joined Erwan in Santa Fe for a movnat a year ago. Subsequently Jack did a webinar about stuff that Erwan had triggered - and then Erwan joined Jack for the Live Q&A. I must bore everyone to tears about this .... But it was mind bending for me.

### prAna303 — 2013-12-28T11:34:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104344>

Welcome...

Just how i think and do.

> Questions for the community:
> 1\. To accelerate cold adaptation, minimize inflammation, would a super potent DHA fish oil - say Nordic Naturals Pharma. grade - be advisable? I've self experimented with 0 to 15 grams of high DHA fish oil, and have noticed large improvements with high doses.

I always go for the real thing, fatty fish that is. If i have salmon, raw is how i prefer it. When we use pills i think we perhaps can use it shortly for reversal but only for that. I belive that when we do use supplements we only get a small bit of the information food stuff should supply us. So many small sublime details of data is lost when you only want the "effect" that information should have. Think digital vs analog...

> 2\. As a Qigong practitioner, one element of CT that scared me, is that the cold would literally "dampen my fire" or "Qi / Life Energy" that I work so hard through meditation and movements to accumulate. Or does CT have the reverse effect, by improving conductivity, minimizing inflammation, it improves Qi circulation and accumulation.

That thought is because Qigong and other energy based motion often comes from a warm country, yoga for sure. The idea is quantum but many do not connect the dots on how this should work. It works but nobody knows why, i think Jack can explain this and that is why i visit this place. Remember that in a quantum world the things that may look negative in the end is positive. Like cold, think about the seebeck effect. We are quite warm inside and on the outside we should have a difference, when we are close to our own temperature we start to die in a high speed rate, maximum entropy. In our tissue and other stuff we are made of we have a lot of metals, when we get that difference we create currents (Qi)... Okey, this is the non science way i think, many others can explain it more deeply.

> 3\. Please tell me your top productivity, and brain enhancing BIO-HACKS!!

Keep it simple, i always think how i would do and act IF i did not have any modern stuff around me... Golden rule for me, be the animal i am. Always try to eat in season and what the environment have to offer. Do all my living as much in the wild, running barefeet, climbing, hiking and some freediving.

### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-28T19:56:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104356>

Caroline, I share your same passion to make the most of every moment in life, to optimize my abilities and to bring as much good as possible to the world. I'm currently helping kids with ADHD, Autism and other learning disorders, people with Depression, Anxiety and working professionals looking to optimize performance. Neurofeedback has been a game-changer in terms of enhancing mental performance. I'm also working as a Performance Coach and Personal Trainer. I really heed the work of Tim Ferris, when it comes to maximizing learning.

In order to elevate my career ceiling and implement the type of change in our society's approach to health that is needed, I'm planning on completing an Ivy MBA next year. I've always had a passion for law as well. Ultimately, if I'm going to pay it forward as much as I would like, and achieve significant change in our world, I really do need to be in a position of economic power. Thats just the way the world works... I'm hoping to becoming one of the Jack's shining starfish and enlighten the world to some of his great work. Even if we can't get everyone doing CT - which would be awesome - we can still implement other approaches to optimize human performance. Neurofeedback, improving your neurology, and current of flow in your brain is just one such thing.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-05T02:38:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ricardos.9253/post-104752>

"Lucy are you sure this shampoo works ?"

I have a multi-focal blockage of nerve conduction. I have been on IV Gamma Globulin ever since they began to use it for people like myself as well as MS patients. Basically my immune system is confused.. or abnormally stupid.. depends on how you look at it. I am fortunate for having had great neurologists to help me with my disorder. Currently, how this condition begins is still just a matter of hypothesis. I do better if my inflammation markers are lower.

I think Dr Kruse has a lot of information about lowering inflammation and, in addition, resetting one's biology. I am impressed by the amount and quality of knowledge available here and at Dr Kruse's blog.

I have done a fair amount of research on my own to try to better understand health related matters, so maybe some of what I have discovered or hypothesize might be useful to others.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-05T05:39:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ricardos.9253/post-104779>

At this point I have begun to integrate some practices suggested by Dr Kruse's blog.

Perhaps I should begin by the big picture as I see it, then go through what, at this time, is for me an optimal day.

So big picture first:

Let's consider the body as a work-site, a work in progress. I believe there are 2 main types of workers: probiotics and enzymes ( by that I am referring to all the enzymes in the body.. not just those involved in digestion). So you need sufficient members of both trade unions.. the probiotics and the enzymes to do the work. If you don't have enough of both types of workers your raw materials just sit.. That can cause problems.

I start every day with a good strong array of probiotics. Since I go to hospitals regularly, its important to have the right good guys (probiotics) inside my system to defend my body against the "super bugs" that populate certain hospitals. And btw, these super bugs have killed and will do so again at every opportunity. These "super bugs" are powerful strains that have been created by over consumption of anti-biotics.

So take your cue from the japanese who have their white miso soup in the morning. Same idea.. starting the day with probiotics.

There are probiotics and there are super probiotics. Here is an example of an advanced probiotic: Plantarum OM.

The US patent states.. and I quote...
"The L. plantarum, OM strain has proteolytic, anti-viral, anti-retroviral, anti-bacterial, anti-microbial, and anti-tumoral characteristics and uses."

This is a warrior strain.. one bad-ass probiotic.

[http://patft.uspto.gov/netacgi/nph-...50&s1=5895758.PN.&OS=PN/5895758&RS=PN/5895758](http://patft.uspto.gov/netacgi/nph-Parser?Sect1=PTO1&Sect2=HITOFF&d=PALL&p=1&u=%2Fnetahtml%2FPTO%2Fsrchnum.htm&r=1&f=G&l=50&s1=5895758.PN.&OS=PN/5895758&RS=PN/5895758)

I know of a woman who had a tumor the size of an orange..medication wasn't working so she took this probiotic and in 2 months kapow ! The tumor disappeared.. gone...kaputsky !

There are others. So. Probiotics. Workers. I make sure they show up on the worksite at the beginning of the day to do the work. And I make sure there are plenty of them to do what has to be done.

As for the other crew..the enzymes... that could take a little longer and its getting late.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-13T22:17:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ricardos.9253/post-105578>

Super.. and I send my regards to the didgeridoo guy. The more good vibes we send each other the better the world we live in.

While I'm here in this thread, I should continue with the descrip of a logical, I think, perception of how our biology functions:

So if we consider the body as a worksite, I believe there are two main types of workers: probiotics and enzymes.

Probiotics were thought of as bacteria useful in the GI tract. They are way beyond that now. Thanks to microbial engineers there are now probiotics that are veritable "super beasts" that perform work systemically and can eradicate pathogens that were previously not accessible to probiotics.

There are nutritional strategies to support the work of enzymes in the body.

Fats, more than any other nutrient, support the production of energy, of new cells, and regulate the entire metabolic process. Making the right choices in fats is perhaps the number one key to building health and eliminating disease.

Ingest electron-rich foods. Unprocessed foods: the closer to nature the better. Simply stated, to support the work of enzymes, give your body the right components to work with.

Combining molecules to produce therapeutic and optimal results:

Dr Beliveau is director of the molecular medicine lab here in the city. He was featured as the last speaker in a day long seminar at the Jewish General Hospital. By the end of the afternoon most everybody is starting to yawn and feel sleepy. Well his talk about research into the powerful properties of food compounds got everyone excited and wide awake.

Based on the findings of the MM lab, it would be advantageous to combine foods, herbs and spices that are known to have medicinal properties, in order to form more powerful molecules. Their findings are being documented.

btw, I was happy to see some very appropriate food choices and combinations in the optimal e-cookbook. I particularly like the way the sulfhydryl group of spices is used with fats.

# Thread: Margos leptin reset - Highway from hell!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/margos-leptin-reset-highway-from-hell.9283/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-01-08T10:16:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/margos-leptin-reset-highway-from-hell.9283/post-105051>

Hi hi,

This is going to be very long, this i know! So im gonna try condense it as much as possible, and use the key points.
History = endurance athlete for 14 years.
Bulimic 18 - 25 years
Tried the contraceptive pill around 26 yrs old - had to come off it, got to the point where i could barely leave the house, so exhausted, very reactive to wheat and was never the same on the bike again. (not as strong as pre the pill)
Migraines started age 27. Debilitating pain, always stemmed from left side @ C1. If they were bad then I would vomit, and would continue violent dry retching in excess of 8 x per day (and im talking really violent!) resulting in me on the floor from the pain in my head. Longest one 72 hours. They have reduced by about 98% since going paleo, and the severity has reduced drastically
Was vegetarian / fat phobic for years. Used to get severe abdominal upset some days and thought nothing of the regular 5 bowel movements pday.

@33 years old - started 'losing it'. Very emotional, out of control - crying, sullen, withdrawn and very stressed.
@35 years old - lost the plot. I was at work one day and literally 'felt' a cape of darkness being draped over me. It was all i could do to get through my shift at work (chef). GOt prescribed anti depressants. Did counselling, flicked the loser guy, took some time out.
@37 years - went to thailand for a detox. Ended up going to a second health farm and met the doctor that changed my life - took me from vegan to paleo, tested adrenal function (very poor), and i detoxed from coffee.
After 3 months of paleo / adrenal extract i literally woke up thin! Was amazing, felt amazing, didnt even know i was getting a period, life was easy!
\- back to antarctica to work. (sleep issues had started down there the previous summer, but i was ok when i left). No change to diet etc but weight started creeping up. Stress?
\- I had 3 years of summer in antarctica, and then 4-5 months in asia. Lots of blue light - hours on the computer, lots of flying, weight still going up..
39 yrs - Breast reduction, breasts doubled in size in 2 years. for all those who joke and say they want big ones / what a waste etc - try living with constant back and neck pain, being paranoid, being harrassed.. it sucks.
\- Weight still going up. Ability to get off an 8 hour flight and not be able to get my jacket closed - i swell up that much  that sucks, because its very slow to go down again.
\- Sleep issues at their worst meant i could take a pharma sleeper and lie awake all night. I felt, all along, that it was cortisol related - heart would start thrashing by 10.30pm on the dot.
\- after the breast surgery, self medicated with thyroid extract.
\- Moved back to NZ, continued swelling up, felt some days, that i was too big for my skin. Lost all definition in legs, gained all over body cellulite, including lower back & chest (yeah, effed up, right?!), severe pain in back exacerbated at work (standing on my feet) , plantar fasciaitis.. Not very happy!
\- 40 yrs - started seeing a naturopath here in NZ. onto wilsons adrenal protocol, iodine tested - barely any showed. started iodine supplement.
\- Came off thyroid. (dessicated pig thyroid).

\- Last six months - blacked out windows in room. noticed a change in sleep on night two!
\- body pain drastically reduced.
\- have occasional coffee, love it!
\- have a 'ring' around my neck (like a crease) at the bottom that can flare up at times - not sure what makes it stand out more, but i think its thyroid related?
\- took body temp this week, was 36C - this has come up from the 34-low 35C that i used to sit at constantly

Always had a six pack, was always 'lean' - am now around 25kg heavier.
\- Get bad headaches with period, not always
\- still eat paleo including raw milk
\- current, been doing JK protocol for about two months. not always to the letter, but as best as i can. I do get frustrated still, and worried it wont work (more so before my period). I can get quite down around period time, which seems to have gotten worse lately.

Just waiting on next test kit to arrive. am going to do salivary cortisol, melatonin and DHEA and sex hormones. Then Id like to join this site so i can have a consult with JK and see whats really up.

Im wearing blue blocking glasses at night, look into rising sun in the morning and supplement with oxytocin (when i remember to!) to try help that melatonin reset (looking at the sun)
Swim in the lake over the road for my CT. Its a constant 11-13C. can do up to 28 minutes.

### caroline — 2014-06-20T11:37:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/margos-leptin-reset-highway-from-hell.9283/post-131491>

Hey Margo! you got a heck of a story there girl! where are you now? I can't keep track of you ....lol

### margor_j — 2014-06-20T11:59:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/margos-leptin-reset-highway-from-hell.9283/post-131495>

> [spinergie said:](/goto/post?id=131429)

> Hi what an interesting journey I relate a fair bit with the bike part and weight.
> I am grateful to both C and J for suggesting I get in touch we seem to have some similarities in out journeys.
> Do you currently have time to train much, I imagine your job is taking most of your time.
> I will add you to my follow list if you don't mind

hi hi!
sure you can add me! im back in thailand now. some quiet time! i dont ride anymore. infact, i really watch that old 'chronic cardio'! iv had a few months out of the gym to. just been walking, yoga and now doing some T Tapp again. im thinking of gymming again but im on the fence. im finding now, my periods just knock me back ten steps and it takes like a week to get over it :-/ so im not sure. im interested to hear what your story is, with its similarities!

### madamechickendance — 2014-01-13T04:36:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mcds-journal.9322/post-105519>

In January 1996 I had an epiphany in the bathroom of my college residence hall.

I decided that I wanted to poop without pain. Constipation was a regular occurrence (oxymoron!) for me. I was fed up. I didn't want to deal with this all the time.

My first decision was to eat three meals a day, with no snacking or desserts. I ate the same thing every day: muffin and coffee for breakfast, peanut butter/jelly sandwich and water for lunch, and a plateful of the vegetarian entree for dinner, with a salad and juice (instead of soda). I took psyllium fiber, vitamins, and garcinia camobgia every morning. To exercise was my next decision; I was in class 5 days a week, so this was just another thing I did every day. I chose to swim. Another factor in this routine was my schedule. The semester I focused on my health was the lightest one ever for me at college. My stress level was lower. This was not by design, but it worked out that way.

I got results. My goal of pooping without pain was achieved in short order, but I stayed the course. My clothes fit better. Progress continued till graduation. Then, well, I left the bubble of college. My routines were destroyed, and I didn't adjust. The weight came back.

Four years later I decided to take my mom up on her offer to pay for me to get breast reduction surgery. We met with a surgeon who told me to lose 20 pounds first. Then we could make plans, she said. With my goal clearly defined and my motivation unshakable, I did what it took. I relied on a much more intense exercise routine (lots of cardio) and more "pure" on the eating, even doing a master cleanse (bad idea!). But I did lose the weight and got the surgery. After surgery I focused on maintaining my weight. I kept losing. I got involved with Overeaters Anonymous, and that was great for structure, support, and accountability. My plan of eating was...three meals a day (no seconds) with no wheat, dairy (except butter!), or sugar (of ANY kind). One piece of whole fruit a day. I was getting plenty of calories, probably about 2,200/day, in three large meals. I ate plenty of meat and eggs, veggies, non-wheat starchy carbs, and never restricted my fat intake. It agreed with me. I maintained a rigorous workout schedule, with weights in addition to the cardio. I looked and felt in the best shape of my life. For the first time EVER, I looked "normal," not overweight.

to be continued....

### caroline — 2014-01-13T08:35:57+0200
