Great start Madame! keep it coming....

### madamechickendance — 2014-01-18T02:11:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mcds-journal.9322/post-106080>

I did enjoy looking good. Shopping for clothes became a hobby. But as good as things were on "the outside," my spirit was unsettled. I needed to make a change, a big one. When a co-worker of mine, Ben, told me that he would be resigning in February to go hike the Appalachian Trail, I asked, 'What's the Appalachian Trail?' I'd never heard of it. Ben told me all about the trail, trail culture, the challenge of hiking, how much he was looking forward to it. I was floored. This was it! This was the change I needed. After some days of chewing on it, I told Ben that I'd like to go with him. When he replied, "You can't go with me," I knew what he meant--that he had his hike to hike--but then he looked me in the eye and said with sincerity, "But you can do it. You should do it."

I believed him. I believed him, and I resolved in that moment to hike the Appalachian Trail...on my own. I plunged into research and training. When I told my parents that I was going to hike the AT, they nodded and said, "Sure you are," and proceeded to not pay it another thought. But to everyone's surprise, I kept at my preparations.

A funny thing happened in January of 2003, three months before my anticipated start date. I stopped having my period. I was at a healthy body weight and fat percentage, eating tons of nutritious food with absolutely no junk, and exercising intensely. I knew I wasn't pregnant. In a way, this was a good thing, I told myself. Not having to deal with my period in the woods would be a good thing! I relocated to Maryland in March, 2003 to make my final preparations from the safe haven of my aunt and uncle's place. In this month I did not exercise with as much intensity as I had been, and I began to notice my weight begin to creep up. I began my hike officially on April 2, 2003, headed south from Harper's Ferry toward the Appalachian Trail's southern terminus at Springer Mountain, GA.

As soon as I got into the routine of hiking I quickly tired of all the sugar-, wheat-, and dairy-free meals that I had carefully made myself or purchased at some great expense. The other hikers I encountered ate the typical junk that hikers eat: Little Debbies, Lipton and Ramen noodles, candy bars, etc. After a month I decided that I didn't want to be the girl with the weird diet. I started eating like a thruhiker--bad food in huge quantity. It was absolutely amazing how quickly the weight came back on. In retrospect I know it wasn't just fat--it was basic inflammation. But I looked really different by mid-hike. Months later, I crossed paths with a hiker I had met in May, just 5 or so weeks into my hike. He had started at Springer 60 pounds heavier, hiking with his blood sugar meter and his insulin. In September, when I encountered him the second time, we both stopped in our tracks and stared at each other. He had shrunk to a normal size. I had ballooned. These facts did not escape comment. But suffice it to say that even carrying 30 pounds on your back up and down mountains day after day will not NECESSARILY make you skinny.

After 5 months or so of a highly inflammatory diet and major abuse to my body (long distance hiking is stressful, no matter what precautions you take) I actually started my period again. I got off the trail (that is, I quit) 330 miles shy of completing the whole trail in a year (a widely-accepted definition of "thruhiking"). By the time I had to stand up in my brother's wedding (Thanksgiving, 2003) I was 20 pounds heavier than I had been at the start of my hike. And it was most certainly NOT "all muscle."

to be continued...

### caroline — 2014-01-18T06:25:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mcds-journal.9322/post-106095>

I am so excited to hear more......

I was so bloated and uncomfortable and so disapointed at what I had done to myself - eating what everyone else was eating - being part of the group...stupid, stupid, stupid....I now intend to stick out like a sore thumb every chance I get!

### madamechickendance — 2014-01-28T15:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mcds-journal.9322/post-107150>

My parents had welcomed me home while I pondered and prayed about what exactly to do with my life. For several months I had one routine that stuck: I got up before dawn and headed to gym, where I got a good workout in before heading to the coffee shop with my Bible and my journal. I don't remember what I was eating at the time (healthy or junk), so I can't remember where breakfast fit in or what it was. But at that point in my life, my health/eating was not the MAIN focus of my routine. I was after a spiritual answer, and after I received it.

As I took the next steps to respond to what God was asking me to do, I got back in touch with Zack, a fellow hiker from 2003. He told me that he was free to go back to the trail in May and that he would be glad to accompany me; he also was very frank that I should finish what I started. Of course, he was right. But then there was this job I was trying to make a decision about. Compounding all this was the fact that in the months since we'd been apart or even communicated (this was the era before Facebook, texting-as-a-way-of-life, and smartphones), I had developed feelings for Zack. I had gone from saying, "If I were ever to get married, it would be to a guy like him," to "I think he's the one." Well, the way it all came down was that I confessed my feelings; if we were going to hike together, he needed to know ahead of time how I felt. I also told him to go ahead and be honest with me, that I was an adult and I would get over disappointment and we could hike together just fine without drama. But his answer surprised me. Zack had gone through a similar path of emotions, as I had. He had feelings for me, as I had for him!

Now that I was 30 years old, I knew what I wanted. I wanted to get married and have a family. Zack is 4 years younger than me, but he knew that marriage and family were what he wanted (eventually). The question was: Do _**we**_ get married? We sought out the answer by hiking together, walking and talking as we had the year before. It didn't take long to settle the question. By the time the hike was over, we had the wedding all planned. This was a good thing, because after we finished the hike, we both returned to our jobs. I didn't see him again until a week before the wedding!

After completing the year at the school where I worked, we packed up our lives and headed out west. We hiked the Colorado Trail from Denver to Durango. Then we settled in a southwestern CO town (8,000 ft above sea level) to finish up Zack's masters degree student teaching requirements. I spent as much time as I could maintaining my conditioning from the CT hike (cardio, cardio, cardio!). But I HATED living in the high desert. The sun was too intense, the air too dry, the landscape too brown. When his degree was complete, we journeyed to Southern Appalachia, land of the wet and the green. We love it here. The skies are dark enough that he can stargaze. Our county is rural and poor...and very peaceful and beautiful. No, there's no "culture" here, but if we need to get to "civilization," there are 4 cities with airports within 2 hours drive.

So what does my life story have to do with my Optimal Journey? CONTEXT. I live in a place that I love, with the man that I love, doing work that I love. As far as emotional stressors go, I am very blessed to have very few. I know that each and every time I think on the immensity of God's goodness to me in these things that my "quantum" self (protons and electrons) gets a shot of good.

to be continued....


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Here's my story - where do I go from here?!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heres-my-story-where-do-i-go-from-here.9392/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dreeza — 2014-01-19T21:44:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heres-my-story-where-do-i-go-from-here.9392/post-106224>

Long history, so I'll try to summarize as best as possible:
As a baby: had colic for unknown reasons
~9 months old: dropped on my head on a stone floor...taken to ER, I was 'fine'
4th grade: give IQ test & tested for ADD. I had a high average IQ, and tested positive for ADD
4th grade-8th grade: put on ritalin - my grade and attn improved significantly
9th grade: decided I wanted to be off the meds, so took myself off
10th grade: wasn't doing well, so wanted to go back on meds - re-given an IQ test, and it was found my score dropped significantly (mainly in 2 categories: picture arrangement & block design) - still high average, but ~ 15 points lower overall. My parents have no reason, no known things that could have caused - maybe ritalin?!
All high school/college: Knew my brain didn't work like others - constant brain fog/trouble processing/felt really slow....but always found my way around it, and always got great grades
\--> insert few episodes of mini concussions...never anything too serious, but hit my head way more often than any normal person does
College: on & off ADD drugs (on concerta at this point) & turned to diet...this is when I started researching diet. Did my best with the info I knew, but by no means was a great diet. Through all of this, always had tons of aches & pains/muscle soreness, etc, that I never realized was abnormal...cause, I'd always had it. My sister even remembers me as a kid always complaining about my joints hurting.
Grad School: finally off ADD drugs for good...took out most grains (but def wasn't gluten free, or great overall...but WAY better than the average SAD)
~1 year ago: car accident, sustained a concussion that took everything I had worked on so hard, and amplified it x100. Took about 7-8 months to finally feel back to myself. During this time (and actually about 6 months leading up to the accident), became MUCH more serious about diet. Grass fed beef/dairy, gluten free, high fats, organic when possible, etc etc. Still by no means perfect, but 80% pretty close.
\- Have seen a naturopath & nutritionist. Was told I have an issue with sulfur, so took away vegetables with sulfur which cured many of my aches and pains. I've been put on all kinds of supplements & homeopathic remedies, which has no brought me back to my baseline pre-accident...except for one thing. My vision.
I've been doing vision therapy for about 8 months now, and have a huge issue with central processing. Each eye works fine by itself. I don't really notice I have vision issues (except when I'm driving & all of a sudden a car/person comes "out of nowhere"...when it was clearly an eye shutting down). My deficits are really notable with the exercises she makes me do, but even my vision therapist admits that my issues are a bit weird. We are thinking a lot of it was likely present pre-concussion (which makes sense), but I never knew it.
Anyways, I also still have bad brain fog/issues with processing information (trying to read through the info on this forum is killer...it is like a bad joke trying to research what things would be best to do to do to help with my issue...cause I have such a hard time processing it).

That being said, I am overall a very functional person - I have my doctorate in physical therapy, and do very well. But I feel like there is this huge untapped potential because of whatever the heck underlying issue.

Oh, I also have a grounding sheet that I sleep on every night (didn't notice any different from it). Taking Vit D, Vit K, magnesium, homeopathic supplements, immune support (immuno gg, bio-immunoenzymes),

I am just wondering - where to go from here!? Feel like I've hit a plateau.

Thanks!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### SkylerTrevino — 2014-05-03T23:08:18+0300

# Thread: Ltwo's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ltwos-optimal-journal.9431/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ltwo — 2014-01-22T23:55:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ltwos-optimal-journal.9431/post-106646>

Suffering from IBS for approx. last 10+ years, with bloating and pain in right side just under rib cage. In July 2010, had colonoscopy and doctor found Colon Polyps with Diverticulosis. Had upper GI, ultra sound of abdomen, hida scan, and CT scan. Gastroentrogologist, Internal Medicine Doctor, and OB/GYN Doctor can find nothing else wrong with me, other than IBS and was advised there is no way to prevent polyps.

Since 2010, continue struggling with severe IBS, bloating, leaky gut, fatigue, brain fog, and elevated platelets from 415 in 2010, to 440 - 2 months ago.

After finding Jack Kruse's site in 2013, I have read and incorporated many practices into my life. Eating GF Beef, more sea food and trying overall to live by Brain Gut 6. Purchased his book and have been a Bronze Member since May 2013. July 2013, leaky gut has gotten better following Jack's Leaky Gut Protocol and following the Epi-Paleo Diet. Performed CT during May, June, July, and August 2013 with the Flexi-Ice vests that were on Jack's website. I bought multiply vests and wrapped my body from my waist to my knees, for 1 to 2 hours per day. I felt calm and many days, this CT was the only thing that kept me going forward. I read and re-read the blog and took questions to my doctors, which at one point they offered me antidepressants, which I promptly refused. I need help, not drugs, however there was nothing they could do for me. It was hard to keep moving forward with so much brain fog some days, however I knew that this information is the key to my future recovery, and I continue to read and re-read blogs/information.

In Dec 2013, had follow up colonoscopy, and doctor found additional Diverticulosis and more polyps - one was a sessile serrated polyp approx. 33 cm., removed via Endoscopic Mucosal Resection (EMR). Thank god there was a doctor with this expertise, or it would have been very difficult to get over a full abdominal surgery in my state of health. However, I am very concerned about getting colon cancer or another deadly disease.

In looking for help to prevent getting sicker, met with Functional Medicine Doctor in Nebraska who offers the Patricia Kane Protocol, which uses Phosphatidylcholine, Butyrate (Sodium/Potassium) and Glutathione, via supplement and IV treatment too. Here is a link to PK's latest seminar: [http://www.bodybio.com/Data/Default/...rence-2013.pdf](http://www.bodybio.com/Data/Default/...rence-2013.pdf) - which has an executive summary of the protocol. I am still learning/evaluating if this is the best choice for me at this time.

Most recently I listened to Jack's latest Webinar and Q&A from The Epi-Paleo Diet with Dr. Jack Kruse and found two big pieces of wisdom from this call - 1st: how was my BUN/Creatine level - which Jack stated should be 7 to 1. My recent blood panel from 11.04.2013 for BUN is 12 and Creatine is 0.7. This was surprising to me because I drink 64 to 80 oz. of water per day. Looking for information on how to improve water function in my body.

2nd: info from this Webinar: I get a red face after eating, particularly around my nose and mouth. Wow - how eyeopening. I explained this to my doctor, he just looked at me and had no idea.

Have had Thyroid problems over the last several years, however doctor felt the numbers were borderline, of if needed was given Synthroid. This never helped, because it was always the Free T3 and T4 conversion numbers that were never tested. So in August 2013, ordered my own Thyroid Panel through Lef.org and took the results to my doctor. Now taking Armour Thyroid at 1 mg. This is not the answer, because there are many other issues that prevent my thyroid from working, however it has helped with some of the brain fog.

I am currently reviewing the Brain Gut 6, and other Blogs that Jack Kruse suggested. I have ordered the cookbook and Gerald Pollack's book. I have asked my husband to review the EMF Blog and to find ways to reduce our exposure, particularly with our wireless services in our home.

I take many supplements including curcumin, a good multi vitamin without copper or iron, D3, Vitamin C, Selenium, 1 Charcoal cap per meal (to help with bloating), Resveratrol, Quercetin, Omega 3, and CoQ10. Recently added Body Bio - Butyrate (Sodium/Potassium) and Phosphatidyl Choline.

Looking forward to Jack Kruse's next Webinar this weekend. Any help and suggestions are greatly appreciated.

# Thread: Erik J. Ware's Journal, Beginning 2-2-14
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### erikjware — 2014-02-02T18:21:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-107925>

I really began a ramp up in health & fitness last June when I began training for the Hartford Half Marathon and discovered the Bulletproof Podcast. I finished my 13.1 and then had some blood work done late in the year for surgery. On December 6, I had old hardware, (2 plates and 13 screws; they left one screw in because the bone grew completely around it) removed from my left forearm from a bad break 4 and a half years ago. I had been having pain and soreness and it was discovered one of the screws was backing out of the bone over time. The diet guidelines and experience from rehabbing the arm the first time has me almost all the way back in only 2 months. Of course the bone will take longer to fully regenerate but range on motion in the hand and muscle healing is probably 90-95% back.

The major points of interest on the blood work was a 1:1 ratio of Tri/HDL. Fasting glucose: 89. B12: 442. Total testosterone: 610. Total vitamin D: 34. Nothing to be too concerned over, correct?

I also had a 23andme profile done and I came out as elevated risks for: Atrial Fibrillation (6.7% above avg.), Prostate Cancer (3.7% above avg.) \[Father has had some prostate issues, but not cancer\], Alzheimer's Disease, (5.4% above avg.) \[Aunt on my Mother's side now has our family's first case of Alzheimer's\], Colorectal Cancel (1.2% above avg.), and Chronic Kidney Disease (.8% above avg.).

Honestly though, none of these issues scare me as my Father believes the current cholesterol myth and follows the recommended low fat, high carb diet, and has for a decade, and my Aunt with Alzheimer's eats a SAD diet and has her entire life.

I'm 6'5" and weigh 197. I'm about to start some 2014 fitness goals with my wife and family which will include 1, 3, and 6 month goals. I am going to start religiously tracking what I eat, how I'm sleeping, and all CT I am practicing. I really want to get into and stay into ketosis long-term so I can have direct experience of what is possible on that diet.

And just for fun, my favorite breakfast is 1 avocado (dusted with dulse flakes and chipotle powder), 4 eggs over easy, and 5 pieces of bacon, with a BP coffee! Thanks in advance and I'll be posting more soon.

### sooperb — 2014-02-02T18:51:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-107933>

Welcome Erik, I love the sound of your breakfast.

I'm in 2 minds about the 23andme, how accurate are the results and what are they based on? I can see that if you were going to get really stuck in to self analysis it might be useful to have advance warning of what could be coming your way, on the other hand, who wouldn't start fretting if something really unpleasant was forecast? The more I read, the more I wonder how anyone can achieve optimal when there are so many interconnecting bits and finding the missing or mismatched bits can be such minefield of potential booms (to borrow from Jack lol)?

### erikjware — 2014-02-02T19:49:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-107950>

I wanted the 23andme just in case there was something really unusual or alarming showing up. I don't think that happened. Now, I'm more interested in the relatives/genealogy aspect.

### caroline — 2014-02-02T23:16:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-107988>

Welcome Erik! your breakfast does sound yummy! What is your non native EMF exposure? Are you and your family drinking R/O water?

### erikjware — 2014-02-03T02:04:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108031>

I have begun to address EMF. I cut the breaker box at night. I've cut down on how much I wear socks, especially at home on the hardwood floors. I've started cold adapting in small ways; not turning on the heat in the car (it's winter here in CT!), wearing less clothes, sleeping with less cover, turning down the thermostat, not wearing a beanie and gloves, etc. Tonight I will doing my first face dunking session (after watching the SuperBowl). At this time I have just upped my water intake but my wife and I are looking at a Berkey filter system and will be making some other changes, such as glass bottles, etc. Thanks for the welcome. Do you have any water hints?

### caroline — 2014-02-03T06:30:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108051>

You need R/O water....there are threads about water... Consider carefully before jumping into anything. Dr. Kruse suggests just buying R/O water, unless you have great spring water.

Do you have a smart meter?

Good luck with the face dunking! Is your wife doing this as well?

### sooperb — 2014-02-03T12:38:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108077>

She'll be holding his head under lol.

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-03T12:53:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108078>

> [erikjware said:](/goto/post?id=108031)
>
> Do you have any water hints?

It seems that what you shower or bathe in can be an issue, too -- at any rate if the water is heavily chlorinated in your area.

I don't know if Jack has covered this as yet. David Getoff has done quite a bit on this. I just googled and found a video where he explains to Sean Croxton how to obtain and fit a carbon filter to your shower head. I've not done this -- I'm not sure they're even obtainable in this country (I'm not in the U.S.); didn't seem to be last I looked -- but it might be worth it:

\[video=youtube;7WwnckleYn4\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7WwnckleYn4\[/video\]

You can find more from Getoff by just googling: "david getoff water".

### sooperb — 2014-02-03T14:42:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108087>

Our local water is heavily chlorinated, turn on the tap sometimes and it smells like a swimming pool! We have used a bog standard jug filter for 20+ years. The water isn't fluoridated though, apparently the northwest is one of the few areas in England where it isn't added, maybe because a lot comes from the lakes and may be considered to be purer, till they start messing with it of course. I wonder if you could empty the carbon bits from a jug filter into the shower head, would that work, would it still screw back on and the water still come through?

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/Shower-Head...91431385&sr=8-1&keywords=sprite+shower+filter](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Shower-Head-Sprite-Royale--filtration/dp/B004CEV69E/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1391431385&sr=8-1&keywords=sprite+shower+filter) - there seem to be plenty here in the uk!

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-03T15:13:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108090>

It seems you're right: in some areas of the UK they do add it. Jeez, I didn't think they'd sunk so low in Britain yet. Poisoning the \*\*\*\*\*\* water supply! And, since it's done to you at source (corporate-society enforcement), no opt out. Yes, here's what I found by googling:

> Most water supplies contain some fluoride and around half a million people in the UK receive naturally fluoridated water.
>
> Another **5.5 million people receive water that has had the fluoride concentration raised** to around one part per million (1ppm). ...
>
> The West Midlands has the biggest water fluoridation scheme in the UK, which serves over three-quarters of the local population ...

[http://www.nhs.uk/conditions/Fluoride/Pages/Introduction.aspx](http://www.nhs.uk/conditions/Fluoride/Pages/Introduction.aspx)

IOW, most of the UK water companies are leaving the water alone. 5.5 million is less than 10% of the population (now heading for 80 million). Still, no-one should be doing this. I guess someone's got a fat wallet from this "public health" con.

### sooperb — 2014-02-03T15:23:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108092>

Remind me never to move to the West Midlands lol.

### erikjware — 2014-02-05T17:36:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108481>

A few days in, limiting carbs and increasing veggies is the toughest part, I'm finding. Eating plenty of fat and protein in proportion is okay but I'm only eating one large meal a day; dinner. However, I have 1 or 2 avocado's with breakfast and I'm working on a can of green food powder, so I'm not worried right now. Thinking ahead though, I really like the BP coffee and/or full breakfast in the AM, BP yerba mate around 2/3 PM, then a veggie based meal for dinner, that I've been doing.

So, all-in-all, I feel good about this.

### caroline — 2014-02-06T02:48:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108628>

re consider your veggie based dinner ... how about some seafood and a small salad - maybe a seafood stew?
a can of sardines for breakfast? Why are you so worried about veggies?

have you read Jack's easy start guides?

### erikjware — 2014-02-22T17:26:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-111692>

Worried because I don't get enough. Still working through how to plan and organize in advance to ensure I have the right high quality foods ready when I get hungry.

### erikjware — 2014-02-22T17:31:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-111693>

I also joined the YMCA this month and have been using the steam room almost daily. After only two days of the jacuzzi I had to stop because the chlorine have me a headache that lasted all day. I was only able to get it to go away after I drank over 2 gallons of water, spaced out over the second half of the day. Terrible feeling. Still have not done a full CT session. Getting mentally comfortable with colder temperatures by showering cooler, rolling down the car window when I drive, not turning in the car heat, wearing less clothes outdoors (Winter here in CT), and cold water splashes on the face often.

### sooperb — 2014-02-22T19:34:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-111710>

Don't be in a rush, you can't fix everything at once. You can try but it becomes tricky keeping each bit online so to speak. I dipped my face for ages before I plucked up the courage to step in the bath, I used the shower too but it's not the same.

# Thread: My consult with Dr Tim Jackson
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-consult-with-dr-tim-jackson.9611/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-02-05T08:42:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-consult-with-dr-tim-jackson.9611/post-108417>

Hi hi

So, after hearing about DTJ a couple of weeks back, i decided to have a consult with him. i had been listening to a podcast he was in, while i was doing some grounding at the beach - feeling so fat and frustrated (hello, welcome to how i live!)

He went over labs i had done that are a year old, i wasnt expecting that, but am comfy with him being comfy with using them.. and as much as i rate my GP that has helped me a lot, TJ found things that my GP didnt think were biggies..

So, im waiting on 23andme. The sample was only sent yesterday so i know its a wait away.

Il have an ASI come in soon - i ordered it just before i spoke to TJ - though after talking to him yes, i can see how that test doesnt really tell you jack, because it doesnt tell you WHY levels are munted... anyway, i had ordered it so, well see..

Im on betaine / HCI, and though iv used digestive enzymes in the past, they tended to always be papain based, and im pretty sure my gut feels better on these other enzymes.. hes got me upping the dose each couple of days, to get up to 8-9 pills. im on six atm (400mg betaine each) and i have zero sign of any burning / discomfort / side effects..

zinc was low, ferritin low, bifidus low (im supplementing all now, except the ferritin - getting there!), am off whey to. he didnt say it was bad per se, but you dont always see the 'reactions' your body may be having - infact, hes pro no dairy for me. which im fine with, Im off the raw milk now, (about 3 weeks now), and cheese and even butter. Using some ghee here and there, predominantly its avocados and coconut oil - and im really upping coconut oil to try and get into HFLC zone. which im feeling i am - BAB, 3 slices of cucumber for lunch and small dinners but i always feel i ate too much, still!!

Coffee no, but we knew that, right? unless its optimal / BP - but its not.. needless to say, I was just away at work (bike race - soigneur) and pre menstrual. straight up, first two nights, CRAP sleep :-/ fatigue like nobodies business, tiredness, neck and shoulder tension - i was miserable, i was drinking coffee again.. its never my intention to, but to those who mock me for having 'no will power' i ask you to experience severe menstrual headaches, crap sleep, trying to get thru your days work.. i struggle with it, and also, for headaches, its a good one to ditch them - instead of dropping pills.. tho yeah, maybe the coffee does just as much damage as pills - back in my day of drinkign lots of it, i could easily smash up to 7 cups a day, pmt, get a period and not touch it...

Liver function low - so the stomach and liver share blood supply, and if digestions buggered (hello, mine is!) then the livers going to be robbed of its blood supply. hmmm now im starting to put more 2 and 2s together in my big picture with the likes of chronic elevated estrogen - to name but one.. (struggling to process alcohol another..)

Im taking aloe vera for gut - i couldnt get the blend he recommended. to help heal the lining. im fine with that, makes sense..

im gutted i didnt get time to ask him many questions - like the big health issues i have, an hour flies! but then again, im getting heaps of help from this site (thanks JK!) and the kind people on the forum, so.. another big part of it is learning / practising / sharing...

I did ask him about takin curcumin - his reply? (again..) 'depends on absorption'... so, im not taking it for now - why take something that i probably wont / maybe cant absorb..

I did find a new blend of protein powder here in NZ by nutralife - a mix of pea and rice. organic etc... and freaking tasty! doesnt taste like day old cardboard! add coconut milk, coconut oil, iodine, hemp seed oil (sometimes), avocado.. (sometimes) its amazing! explains why i can get thru to dinner time with no hunger - but thats a welcome change after YEARS of blood sugar issues!

SO now, wait for 23andme results and then we take from there. i am curious about SNPs now, fo sho..

i still go up and down, like, the effort / expense / frustration / agony / time to try and lose at least 1kg is almost staggering.. i cant stand it most days.. and im really thinking that some of the things that contributed to effing me up are: vegetarianism / vegan, soy, bulimia, competitive cycle racing (15 years - HELLO! chronic cardio, lol), lots of trips to antarctica, blue light - technology....

anyway, day at a time. .im off for a walk up the nearby 'thousand steps' which are actually 357 stairs - my energy is SO low still in the mornings, im wondering coconut oil but i can deal with having to take that out of my diet for now - that would leave me like vego protein, pig fat and spinach.. not ready! iv been over a week now since i had eggs, and had no red meat ! (unintentionally) but had seafood..

thoughts / ideas / comments / input welcome!

Mags

### caroline — 2014-02-05T08:57:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-consult-with-dr-tim-jackson.9611/post-108422>

Hey Mags! How are ya doing???
Sounds like you had a good consult ..... good!
Is Dr. jackson okay with the protein powder that you have? What is the name/brand of it? It fills you up all day?
I was confused about the CO - you can or can't have it? You can't have red meat?
enjoy the beach and get lots and lots of grounding and lots of sleep .... and hug a tree .....and a person!

### margor_j — 2014-02-05T09:04:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-consult-with-dr-tim-jackson.9611/post-108424>

hey girl! im ok... better now that my periods been and gone, lol.. it was another weird one tho..
yeah TJ said that rice / pea protein is fine. the brand i found is nutralife.. hes also pro whey, but maybe not for me. I can have red meat, it just so happens in the last week and a half, iv had none! but more fish (mainly raw salmon) and pork..
whats CO darl? coffee? im not meant to have it no - mycotoxins, but its my one vice that i struggle with, cos i HATE feeling like crap.. il keep pressing on with giving it up tho, iv done it before.
yeah re the beach. its a ten min walk away from me, and i spend like 2-3 hours there per day! its not cold enough for CT, tho some days its kinda fresh. i was doing CT in the shower last week when i was away - it was frigging cold - but id do stomach area and face. better than nowt, right? and i was cold when i got out of the shower.
well, i got to hug a person at the race, but i wont say more than that.. LOL!!  but is it a sign of a sex drive coming back?!?! surely not.... lol xx

### tellmisty — 2014-02-05T23:31:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-consult-with-dr-tim-jackson.9611/post-108571>

CDSA is similar to Metametrix, though Metametrix is better. Dr. Tim can tell you a LOT about how you can fix your gut based on the Metametrix test. Did he tell you that he wanted you to do Metametrix, too, Margo?

When you get the 23andme test, it will take you a whole hour to go through that one with him, and you will again get a LOT of great insight into your own n=1 and the supplements you need to take / lifestyle changes you need to make to counteract bad SNPs.

Dr. Tim is good, y'all. He isn't an expert on Redox Potential, but he's kickass with hacking your labs and helping you map a solid, solid path to healing. Combine what he teaches you with what you're learning here, and DUDE. Gamechanger.

# Thread: Time to start over.... Emily's Journey.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-to-start-over-emilys-journey.9778/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 36
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emily Sullivan — 2014-02-15T19:38:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-to-start-over-emilys-journey.9778/post-110443>

Well, I'm starting the Leptin RX four about the 4th time now. The self-sabotaging effects of binge eating and occasional purging and my own negativity have taken their toll again. I'm starting to wonder if it's even worth trying again. Why should this time be different? Because it _has to_ be different. I've been overweight, sluggish and unhappy with myself for too long.
I have PCOS, hypoglycemia, fibromyalgia, fatigue, and anxiety issues. Up until about 2 1/2 years ago I was slim, active, and optimistic. I went to a gyno for some help with some irregularities caused by PCOS, and he put me on a round of clomid for whatever idiotic reason. Long story short it screwed me over. I ended up gaining 30 lbs in about a month. That, combined with some other stressors in my life at the time, caused my attitude and self-esteem to bottom out. It still hasn't completely recovered. My anxiety got so bad I could barely leave the house for nearly a month.
I started to try every diet you could think of. JUDDD, atkins, almased shakes, HCG. None of it worked, mostly because I couldn't stick with it because I'd end up so miserable and fatigued as a result. I tried the Leptin RX about a year and a half ago, but didn't stick to it.
I started LR AGAIN in the middle of January, and have restarted about 4 times since then. Frustrated with myself is putting it lightly. Last night was one of the worst slip-ups I've had in a while. The whole binge/junk food train wreck. It all needs to stop. Now.

I know I have my youth on my side, (I'm 22) if nothing else. Maybe this process won't be as long and arduous as my cynical side tells me it will be. Unfortunately, I only have about 18 weeks to stay on the reset, after that I'm going to a month long program to study theology/philosophy with some students and monks, and, spiritually refreshing as it will be, the very rigorous daily schedule, in which it will be nearly impossible to do the BAB, will set a limit on how long I can do the protocol (I leave for it July 7). I intend to still follow the guidelines in every other way as closely as I can. I'm hoping that this will still be enough time, if I do my absolute best to do a perfect LR.

I guess what I need more than anything right now is some faith that it'll work. So many things haven't worked for me. It's hard to believe that this finally will.

### caroline — 2014-02-15T23:26:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-to-start-over-emilys-journey.9778/post-110478>

Hi Emily. The leptin reset does work - absolutely .....but you have to do it exactly as Dr. Kruse has written it.
Please read the easy start guides a couple of times to make sure you understand exactly. There are lots and lots of success stories here. Please keep reading so you understand why all this works - this isn't another diet.

this should be your day .....
get up and go outside and look at the sun
within 30 minutes of rising start a BAB .... at least 50g of protein - you may need lots more - I did

Leave at least four hours between meals and before bed time. NO SNACKING - very important!

Only dring spring water \[for your water\]

Get outside as much as possible with as little clothes as possible and bare feet

What is your non native EMF exposure?

get rid of everything in your bedroom except the bed .... no iphone, TV, ipad, clock radio etc. can you cut the breaker to your bedroom?

no fake lite after sundown - extremely important! You need blue blocker glasses.

can you reduce the stress in your life? listen to music, hug a tree, hug a friend, laugh!

Please come back here every day and tell us how you are doing. Everyone slips up now and then but that doesn't mean we give up.

Keep reading and asking questions ..... Dr. K. doesn't know what we don't know unless we ask.

Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi-Paleo RX ......available at amazon on kindle or paper. It is a great overview of all of this in easy to understand terms and explains why Dr kruse is doing all this.

Please listen to all his podcasts - another easy way to get quick and easy information! - on the home page

Have you joined the free 90 day optimal reset? you still can. This has been amazing and Dr. K. has given all of us a huge amount of time - there is a live Q&A with the webinars where you can ask him anything. Please don't miss this opportunity.

If you can't find anything - please let me know!

You have made a great start on your optimal journey .....Have you done any testing?

### sooperb — 2014-02-16T02:08:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-to-start-over-emilys-journey.9778/post-110529>

I think the one stellar thing to stand out for me is that 2 1/2 years ago you were in a better place. It's not a long time back and you haven't been stuck in the difficult place you find yourself for years and years. The second plus point is that you have recognised that you need to do something and that you have made a start. Many people live in denial for years.

It isn't easy and it isn't difficult but it may not be quick either. You have found the one place where you can get support, practical help, a wealth of knowledge and experience all just for the asking. I think Caroline is right, read as much as you can and have a good think about it. I do wonder a bit if you are setting yourself up to fail by putting such a tight time limit on it. Progress isn't instant, I've been trying for weeks to make something happen and it hasn't - yet. I get despondent but I figure that I haven't yet found out why, there are so many aspects to sorting out a health/lifestyle problem. It's taken me years to give up sugar and I've fallen off the wagon with that one too, but it's only been a blip not a full scale reversal back to being a sugar muncher so I've learnt something.

If you don't think that you can access what you need to stick with the reset when you go away then why not see what you can take with you that will help, a few jars of coconut oil, a tub or two of ghee, a bag of nuts, tins of fish maybe. There's usually a way round most roadblocks you just need to think laterally. Make solving the problem your project rather than dwelling on what you're trying to fix, make it work for you, you're worth it

### caroline — 2014-02-16T02:20:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-to-start-over-emilys-journey.9778/post-110533>

That concerns me too ..... when you go away. I didn't want to be a wet blanket but ..... this is a journey to Optimal health - not for 6 months or a year .....We are all working on our own masterpiece and it takes work and endurance and a lot of reading and seeing what works for us. We are lucky to have Dr. Kruse shining his flashlight for all of us.
we do have to do the work - but he is always more than willing to answer questions and help when we are floundering with all the science.
I am definitely not saying you shouldn't start - of course you should .... but think about what you are going to do down the track when you go to this place .... will it be vegetarian? You are young and strong with an amazing life ahead of you .....and you can do this....

Dr. K. faces enormous risks in his day job - as do lots of others here ......we just have to do extra ordinary things if we want to have an optimal life ......

### SeaHorse — 2014-02-16T03:24:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-to-start-over-emilys-journey.9778/post-110553>

Emily...binging may mean not enough protein and with hypo and PCOS and all the other things you are dealing with....the BAB has to become the starting point everyday. I hope you have some support around you because when your brain is being overwhelmed with addictive chemicals it is truly a battle. Once you have a few weeks or months of clarity under your belt you won't want to lose that feeling....until then you have my full empathy. And to answer your question...IT IS ALWAYS WORTH IT Are you taking anything to help your neurotransmitters??? all the best, SeaHorse

He deserves someone who can love herself as well.

### caroline — 2014-02-17T02:22:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-to-start-over-emilys-journey.9778/post-110691>

If they will help you with what you need for BAB that is great. Please don't sweat the small stuff for that period of time. Do the best you can do! So you can still do the reset no problem. If you are still having cravings later in the day .. .. Just up the protein and fat for your BAB ..?easy!

Cans of sardines would be easy enuf to take along. Will they rovide protein at every meal? Roasts etc?

This is real life anyway - no matter where we are we have to make better choices for ourself ....and who knows how many will watch what you are doing. You can be a conduit for others to make healthy choices.

My big rec is to stay away from fruit.
.

I expect you already know all this, just don't think this is another diet.....really it has way more to do with how you live your life...all the best, SeaHorse

### Lahelada — 2014-02-17T03:13:05+0200

Emily,let go... If this is a a spiritual retreat you will be nourished. ### Josh — 2014-02-19T21:10:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-to-start-over-emilys-journey.9778/post-111226>

Consider that the brain is the energy hog. On retreat, it rests and the rest of the body gets to catch up and renew. Everyone talks about getting "fit" and it is usually using the body beyond limits that costs cognitive performance and regeneration of the whole system.

On the purging stuff that you struggle with, consider that purging is used therapeutically in Ayurvedic Medicine and by Yogis to clear their bodies of what they do not need. Tigers will also vomit up whatever they have not digested after 2 hours. In life and death situations almost all animals evacuate their bowels and or vomit so they do not use energy to digest food while they are running/fighting for their lives. The refinement that yogic techniques use is that they drink a quart or two of lightly salted water to dilute the stomach acid so it does not burn the upper stomach/fundus and esophagus when it comes back up. It also stimulates the vagal reflex which gets most of the organs and nervous system going and creates endorphin releases like hard exercise. ... Be gentle with yourself, as one gets more centered these things become less necessary. Consider Jesus was probably a yogi based on how he lived and taught, the tradition is about purification and transcendence and not the property of any religion. See:

> **Physical effects**
> When we do kunjal, what happens? We stimulate the sensory channels of our nervous system, which sends it signal to the brain. This in turn sends a signal down the motor system to make the body vomit: the diaphragm, stomach and glottis contract, causing the water to move in the reverse direction.
>
> There are three processes of the body which totally paralyse the brain and mind for one moment, leaving you in a 'selfless' state, reminiscent of states of meditation. These are orgasm, sneezing and vomiting. If you think about it and reflect upon your own experiences, you will remember that at the moment of experiencing one of these three states, you felt a wave of energy rush through your body and mind which momentarily stopped all thought and action.
>
> When the brain feels this rush of energy it is in a state of extreme stimulation. Many of its circuits are temporarily cut, leaving only the few most necessary circuits in action. This situation is analogous, but much more gentle, subtle and effective than ECT (electric shock therapy) as used in hospitals for the treatment of depressed patients. Energy floods into every nerve of the brain, but in the case of kunjal, not ECT, it is pranic energy which floods through, giving life and rejuvenating every cell. Then when the energy subsides, these circuits start up again in a more harmonious fashion.

[http://www.yogamag.net/archives/1977/dapr77/kunjal.shtml](http://www.yogamag.net/archives/1977/dapr77/kunjal.shtml)

### Emily Sullivan — 2014-02-19T21:57:45+0200

Ok. Been going strong since Saturday. A few successful days. Can't complain. ### Josh — 2014-02-19T22:27:34+0200

### sooperb — 2014-02-19T22:28:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-to-start-over-emilys-journey.9778/post-111239>

A tip for the cramps, try ice packs. I think it was Margor who said she'd been wracked with the same pain, had taken ice packs from the freezer and put them on her abdo area and experienced relief within minutes so turned on her stomach and laid on them. She slept five hours and awoke pain free and it didn't come back. Inspired by that and Jack's assertion that CT can be beneficial in lots of ways I applied an ice pack to my toothache, it resolved in minutes.

### Emily Sullivan — 2014-02-20T00:35:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-to-start-over-emilys-journey.9778/post-111269>

I've been fairly low carb for a while, without the anxiety, but I might try adding some more carbs for now and see what it does. I had a fairly severe anxiety attack while I started to walk home from school and I had to find a quiet place to hide I so could have a good cry. I'm trying to really do some inner searching to see if there's a particular cause that I might be able to address, but there seems to be a different cause every week. Relationship stresses are a big one, though.

I'll try the ice packs next time the pain hits. I have't started full-blown CT yet, I don't want to add too much at once, because I don't want to push myself past the point where I just want to drop the whole thing.

### Josh — 2014-02-20T01:27:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-to-start-over-emilys-journey.9778/post-111277>

When the body gets some extra stress such as mensturation, it can shift out of ketosis based on the NPY mechanism with reverse T3 in the brain. This is to help the body calm the inflammation that may accompany the stress. It increases Leptin resistance and potentially insulin resistance as well. Consider that when the brain perceives that it does not have enough fuel in the blood from either glucose or ketones, it signals danger we are in trouble here because it has no reserves of its own..I used to wake up in a panic from hypoglycemia. At that time a hand full of almonds at bedtime or some other fat/protein solved it and I laughed at all of the other scenarios I had created over the feelings...

### Lahelada — 2014-02-20T14:09:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-to-start-over-emilys-journey.9778/post-111351>

Emily , rather than add carbs you might try to balance your fat and protein ratio and tilt it heavier to the fat side. Fat has an even better "anti anxiety " effect than carbs if you get the proportion right and enter ketosis. I would suspect that being fairly new to low carb you still suffer subconsciously from the dogma "fat is bad ,who eats it is bad as they will end up fat". I have a person who is food obsessed, eats only one small meal a day to be able to have a pastry in the morning and loves to look down her nose at me . Lady, I am the one who who lost 50 lbs like this and who now is holding her weight,measuring or restricting nothing but carbs.
Walk tall Emily !

### kovita — 2014-02-20T21:39:27+0200

I hardly eat any carbs and feel good and no cravings and I even managed to put on some necessary weight, mostly in muscles and in overall in better silhouette. Now I can tell you that menstruation and any infection or even potential infection (for exampla kids have some virus) seems to knock down the balance and I start to crave carbs and have a difficult time going on with 3 meals only no matter how big these are. During these episodes I will have great difficulties with CT, mainly because it seems to lower my blood sugar big time. I can have BAB and a cold bath 30 minutes later and when I leave the bath and stop shivering I would be on the verge of hypoglycemia. This would get corrected without any food intervention within the next 20 minutes or so.

# Thread: Goal:  Biohack libido & Drive in general
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goal-biohack-libido-drive-in-general.9820/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pine_Cone — 2014-02-18T19:12:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goal-biohack-libido-drive-in-general.9820/post-111034>

Hello, I'm new to the forum.

From what I've gathered so far, starting an Optimal Journal is a good place to post labs, goals and gather feedback from the community and Dr. Kruse. **I'm attaching my wellness fx from this year and the previous year**. I'll also include a bit more about myself for context/ introduction. Any feedback on the labs, general comments/ questions are welcome. I look forward to interacting with the community and getting to know you.

I'm a 36 yr old male, 189 lbs with a physical build although not 'ripped'. In the right light, I have a 4 pack  I would take healthy over ripped any day of the week, just trying to give you an idea of body composition.
Diet wise, I'm pretty strict with paleo and fall towards the low carb side of the spectrum. In January (the 30 days preceding my most recent labs, I was in ketosis for nearly 3 of the 4 weeks. I had some red wine 5 days before my lab work, which I think explains the whacky triglyceride markers. I also lost about 10 pounds in the 30 days preceding lab work. Also worth mentioning, I got blood work done in the morning (to see my testosterone at its best), fasted and I was in ketosis.

**One of my motivations for posting this is to find ways to optimize my drive. My libido is very low, and I feel like my drive in general is not what it used to be. I sell software for a living, so competitive drive is essential and my wife is too hot for me to be lacking drive in the bedroom**

It looks like I had some T3 down regulation during January due to the low carbohydrates and continuing to workout (short intense HIIT types of workouts).

I'm getting wordy, so I'll leave it there for now. I'm happy to answer additional questions if you have any. Thanks in advance for taking a look at my labs and helping me biohack my drive!

# Thread: Part 2 of my Journal - Margo :) WOW!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/part-2-of-my-journal-margo-wow.9976/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-03-02T02:16:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/part-2-of-my-journal-margo-wow.9976/post-112970>

So,
as per part one - fat, miserable, depressed and all the rest. Im still based at my parents place- which wasnt meant to be for long, but when contracts fall through, then things change. but what did that failing contract allow? Me to go swimming! daily, in the ocean! Its not freezing, we're in summer in nz (officially autum now) but if i dont move around a lot, and stay in the water for an hour or more, trust me, i get cold! so i do that daily, and then sit in the sun to dry, and walk home.

I have just gotten back from ten days work in australia. Pre departure, I had one of my 'leanest' feelings ever! I couldnt believe it, even my parents noticed - face and neck thinner, but the fat rolls that come and go (at will) on my back were barely noticeable (hello, inflammation! finally i get some answers re those!) I really kicked up the fats - to the point where i had to cut back later in the day - I was lying awake at nights and didnt know why - but even the fact that i noticed higher energy from having coconut oil is amaze balls - usually nothing i take makes any change..

I sometimes, not all the tim,e will sit on the lawn in the evenings and read. And weve been turning wifi off at night - though sometimes we forget. Sleep in a black room, drinking a lot of fluids per day, and doing my TJ protocol - off dairy, working on gut and digestive health etc (up to 3200mg of HCI per meal and still no burning feeling!  this really has me wondering now, the more i learn about leaky gut etc... so, really pleased i did my consult with TJ for that fact alone!)

So off i went to aus for work. And I was pre menstrual for most of it. (gonna use some detail here, sorry guys but trust me, periods tell you a LOT about whats going on!) First period in a while that didnt puff me up out of control, bloat me to looking 9 months preggis, make me cry, shitty etc!! I did feel bigger, but not like before, about 90% less! Coffee - yes, i had some. the black heroin, its a bitch for me, but so are changing time zones and working, heat etc - all of which i had in queensland. My whole life, pre period is always the coffee time - i can literally go from one a day to up to 7, dont notice any changes, get my period and cant stand it again.. freaky right?! and there was one day there where i fell for some carb cravings, that i think were part imaginery, and also because i was tired - I kept waking up at 4am the whole time i was away :-/ BUT, in general, i felt so fine the next day so... high fat? and the carbs were NOT worth it. even a work colleague noticed i had 'glazed over' - boy i felt like shite! lesson learnt - im so not into sugar now its not worth it! and its reallly easy to say no!

Im now not on any supplements for mood - been over a month. Id say that i do still get some dark thoughts around my period, but its not for 3 weeks a month, and i do eft when they come up.

Period was a bit hit and miss for two days, but now - no back pain, no nausea, no headaches, no bloating. and i didnt get the hideous gurgling gut for a day before it - that used to do my head in. iv read it was to do with estrogen but... I didnt get it this month!

the other big change for me is getting a sex drive back!  OMG! thats been dormant for far too long now and i cannot help but hi five myself for that! i didnt want to live out my days with sex as a far away memory!!! And yup, took advantage of that while i was away (bit of lovin between friends, whats not to love, right?!)  lets remember tho, my road of hell even took me to breast reduction land, and so much self loathing / hatred and disgust over the past few years, that, while sex may be a daily occurrence for many, well yah, celebrate that!!! I wouldnt say im fully confident of my body, yet, but to have male interest again, to feel more self confident, to be getting complimented on how young i look all the time.... those 'little things' are a big deal on the road to recovery!

While i was away, i was on two meals a day. Some days, im doing 8 massages in the afternoon - so id have my BAB and then eat before i started massages. Usually along the lines of seafood and avocado. Hungry at nite when iv finished work? not even close!! its heavenly!! the boys all go for dinner around 6.30pm - so i know they wont eat til nearer 7.30pm.. too late for me! Oh and to be at a race and not feel hungry is also so so so nice! solid energy = great mood, higher concentration etc..

woohoo me!

oh, but on the not so woohoo side - iv learnt for me that travel / time zones / and pmt (even mild) are my markers for getting me onto the black heroin (coffee) again.. im not sure how else to get thru - i was doing cold showers to wake me up in the morning.. I know i can get bulletproof coffee in aus, and i go back to aus in about a week, so i may get some decaf BPC to try... to shut me up!! first morning in aus i went to a huge park near the hotel and grounded myself on the wet grass / did some breathing / hugged a tree

Looks like Il be in sydney for a few days - staying with a friend in kings cross (ie in town!?) so if any of you are about, pm me, would be awesome to meet up - NOT in a cafe but in a park, where we can earth!

still waiting on 23andme tests to come back.

But hugs and kudos to you Jack Kruse, again (one day i hope to meet and thank you, in person!) - to think a year ago, all i wanted was to not be alive, i was constantly thinking of dying and just so miserable - and to all of you here on the site, sharing ideas / knowledge / advice, wow! thanks !! theres nothing more powerful than feeling change when youve been stuck in shitsville!

hugs to all xx

# Thread: Male, 62 yo with 30 year history of IBS and chronic lower right quad pain
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 40
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rick — 2014-03-06T05:29:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-113710>

Dx = hypogonadism, adrenal fatigue, IBS, internal hemarrhoids and diverticular disease. The latest diverticular flare was in August of 2013, Rx 10 days of Cipro and Flagyl plus one overnight in the ER on IV Cipro and Flagyl.

This cleared the diverticulitis but oddly a new symptom appeared shortly thereafter in September 2013; the chronic lower right quad pain (my view) was gone and I developed and still have chronic lower left quad pain (my view) about 45 degrees to the left and halfway between the lower rib and the iliac crest. There is no sharp pain, distension or fever and at the same time I developed severe constipation requiring daily doses of magnesium citrate, sulfate or a mixture of magnesium, potassium and calcium ascorbates to keep this condition at bay.

I have successfully stopped the colon hydrating minerals and supplement with artichoke leaf extract and oxbile before high protein and fat meals. Every week I eat bacon, eggs, egg salad, chicken livers or leftover dinners for breakfast, a salad for lunch with MCT oil, Bubbies pickle juice, greens, seedless peeled cukes, skinned and seeded tomatoes and jicama or radish with a daily avocado. Dinners are beef, pork, lamb with salmon, shrimp, scallops, oysters or mussels 2 to 3 times along with cooked greens and other non-starchy veg. No dairy except Kerrygold butter, butternut squash once or twice per week and a little 90% Lindt chocolate. No alcohol of any kind and I have just started cold adapting again. I wear wrap-around blublockers at night and in the early morning before sunrise. No wifi at night and grounding outside as much as possible now but more of that in the spring and summer of course with copious amounts of sunbathing from April to November.

Cooking and coffee water is distilled and I drink Pellegrino and Aquafina during the day. No coffee or other beverages made from municipal water at home or at work. I have seen a new gastroenterologist who suggested Miralax for the constipation (which I flatly refused to do) and he thought that the constipation was the reason behind the lower left quad pain but I explained that symptom is not relieved by a b.m.

I had also seen a doctor from the Paleo physicians network in Manhattan and she prescribed b-vitamins, fish oil, adrenal support vitamins, 10 mg sublingual DHEA, 1000mg vitamin D and Bioimmersion probiotics with beta glucans. She also placed me on 50 mg Testim gel daily. The beta glucans with probiotics did nothing for the constipation at the time and the Testim simply increased the hypogonadism symptoms after two months with no improvement to libido or erectile function. Obviously I have left her care and have scheduled a consult with Dr. Tim on March 11 but I do have lab results that I ordered on my own and blood and urine testing done through her office, please see the attached file.

Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi-Paleo RX

### Rick — 2014-03-07T16:21:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-113996>

Hello Caroline, f@h and nonchalant and thank you all for your rapid responses. Please allow me to elaborate so everyone can understand my context a little better:

I am not new to the JK forum, I have been here pretty much from the beginning, before the first takedown and rebuild of JK forum and came here from the Robb Wolf forum. I have read everyone of Jack's blogs new and old incl the RP Rx. I have his book on Kindle, read it twice and refer to it often and constantly search the forum threads, and yes I'm in the optimal reset and am following most of his guest podcasts on other sites etc.

I am friends with Curves64 here and she and I attended U of Western States as cont ed students in 2012 to take Anat & Phys and Intro to Biochem because we both had decided to open our own health coaching businesses. She has already started her biz but I am worried about possible regulations in NJ although right now there are no licensing or credentialing requirements. To cover my ass I enrolled in 2013 at Hawthorn Univ for their MS in holistic nutrition program. The master's degree (I have a BS in Sound Recording Technology from SUNY Fredonia 1980) will allow me to sit for the board certification exam in holistic nutrition given by NANP who the school is affiliated with and then I should have no problem talking to clients openly about diets. This will not happen until 2017 or 2018 at which point I will be ready to retire (I'll be 65-66) from Consumer Reports in Yonkers NY and go full time on my own practice (I will never retire per se but I will and desperately want/need to retire from all corporate employment and commuting).

I think I do understand the EMF situation quite well but I am not an RF engineer; I was a pro audio engineer for 15 years in NYC and gravitated from recording and production quickly into electronic repair, design and fabrication. My partner and I built and installed some high end audio systems based on my proprietary amplifier designs - I won't bore you with the details, but I know my analog and digital electronics pretty well...so the question is what can I do about this issue and I'm looking for detailed electrical engineering type explanations because that is what groks with my technical background and training unfortunately. I did attend Michael's segment on the Opt Reset last week and am thinking of not spending the 200 on the EMF bootcamp but perhaps investing in a consult with him one on one sometime down the road.

So I had spoken with Jack about what he wrote in the RP Rx re: Apple's iPhone modulating the device's RF power in relation to a hidden body temp sensor in the phone. The plan was for me to get Consumer Reports to send me to the EMF bootcamp and then I would write a blog for CR on EMF protection products. Sadly my manager gave notice and is leaving this week so difficult for a 6T like myself to pitch this idea on my own in a union shop where the guild members are already embroiled in a long and bitter battle with management...so that's why I say no bootcamp and invest that money with Michael directly to clarify the EMF questions I want answered...

I was also a personal/private chef (self-taught) for many years in Bergen county NJ and was able to design the menu, cook and serve for 14 guests at a 7-course wine pairing event with Bruce Cakebread of Cakebread Cellars and Rene Schlatter of Merryvale Vineyards. I was dba Chef for the Night and my Paleo/Primal dinner party service was called The Onion Tree Cafe. Those sites are about to go down and I have already opened Evolution for Life, LLC.

So from the first day I found JK's blogs online, I decided I wanted to be a self-styled Paleo nutritionist and that I would have to go back to college. When I read his first blog on osteoporosis, I knew immediately that would be the topic of my master's thesis.

But here's the thing, while I have "hacked" my way into electrical engineering and professional cheffing with little formal training, I'm having a hard time applying the principles Jack talks about and biohacking my own n=1.

This does not bode well for my future business; if I can't gain the skills to biohack myself how will I be able to help clients? My biggest fear is that my MS program will not prepare me to be a really great holistic practitioner. No matter how cutting edge these programs are they do not recognize nor do they teach quantum biology so I look at JK's writings as my graduate level training.

I picked Hawthorn as opposed to Bauman College or the Institute for Integrative Nutrition because they were at least Paleo-friendly, but even they have a lot of CW and other nutritional dogma to sort through. So I truly appreciate what Jack and everyone is doing on this forum and the blogs but the informational format is tough for me to grasp; not so much scientifically but because of my ADD and non-verbal learning disorder - its hard for me to follow what is being said if there are any typos and trying to sort through forum threads to discover the gold nuggets, not so easy for me.

So, I've never done this journal thing before and I never had labs to post before so that's why I'm back here now to get help biohacking and to ask more questions to get the details I am missing. I thank everyone in advance because I will pester you all with dozens of questions and I will track and share my progress with you here, firstly my consult with Dr. Tim on March 11 next week...

Before the advent of toroidal power xfmrs we used mu metal to shield our audio circuits from 60/120 Hz power supply hum but understanding how MHz and GHz signals propagate and how to shield against them is quite new to me...

Hey, while you're here, Jack talks about grounding on airplanes by placing bare feet on the metal seat legs in front of you - while that metal is tied to the aircraft's electrical ground or zero volt ref for its electro-mechanical and comm systems, it is still floating from true earth which is the only infinite source or sink of e-, so how is that any different from trying to ground yourself with wrist straps, mats or sheets that are connected via an open (because bldg ground was tied to a metal cold water pipe which is now PVC) or a very resistive, maybe inductive or otherwise "dirty" ground in your house via 3rd pin which is not zero ohms at HF? Hello Caroline, yes EMF hell, hundreds of cell phones and wifi routers at work, at home we turn it off when not in use but 20 other routers in our neighborhood - yes I need a magnetico most assuredly. $ for that will need to be accrued over time this year but $ for Gold membership, no way, not yet...my goal is that I can join as a healthcare practitioner in 5 years or so. In the car I personally have no EMF locally; no headsets, bluetooth etc but I spend anywhere from 1-3 hours driving un-grounded to and from work depending on the traffic over the damn TappanZee bridge...

What else can you help me with on the forum? I will elaborate ABOVE in my other answer because I got kicked off the internet and didn't finish editing to fully explain my problems, but here is one example from the RP Rx, quoting JK:

"For a low redox potential, we want a low electrical resistance in cells and tissues. Semiconductors are designed to have low resistance by their very nature. A high redox potential robs them of that ability."

I don't understand these statements because in my mind I thought for sure he was saying a high redox potential was good and desirable and here it is now the opposite? So what is this measurement of redox potential (RP)? Is it our cellular charge contained within our aqueous battery (the water EZ, right?) so you want that potential difference to be as high (more voltage) as possible? Or does he mean the redox potential is the actual barrier potential described in biochem re: enzymatic reactions and we really want that barrier potential to be as small (low voltage) as possible so the reaction pathways can occur with the least amount of additional energy expenditure??

This is one example of where I read and reread the blogs and just get more and more confused and go around in circles ad infinitum...maybe it's just me because other folks seem to catch on to what Jack means and are able to apply this knowledge successfully or maybe these are just typos in the blogs...but being very literal myself, it's confusing as hell and uber-frustrating.

So if anyone can help translate this passage, that would be great! In essence here's what I'm asking - is the RP a measure of charge or potential difference (voltage) that indicates how well our water batteries are charged up and therefore the higher the better....or is the RP a barrier potential or threshold that needs to be overcome and therefore we want it to be as low as possible as he seems to be indicating in the quoted passage?

Thank you Caroline!

### Rick — 2014-03-08T21:22:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-114188>

>
> Rick, could the Cipro be the culprit for the new symptoms? I've updated my "avoid" list at all doctors to include fluoroquinolones. Not taking the chance of being floxed.

Hello fitness@home! Not sure if you mean the idiopathic left quad pain or the constipation but it never occurred to me to blame the Cipro or the Flagyl because I have had about 7-8 outpatient occurrences of diverticulitis over the last 10-11 yrs and the standard of allopathic care is always these two antibiotics to wipe out aerobic and anaerobic bacterium; never before have I experienced this pain on the left or constipation after a round of these admittedly very nasty antibiotics - I don't like 'em anymore than u do!

So you really think these drugs could be related to either symptom? Always for 30 years before I have described my issues as IBS-d with lower right quad pain, in fact the antibiotics usually caused BSS to stay around a 4 while on the meds which was actually a pleasant change and then after the scripts were done the diarrhea would make its regular appearance...So then this begs the question if fluoroquinones are responsible for or associated with these kinds of symptoms, what the hell has changed with the last diverticular flare that made this occur this time and not all the others - I would point the finger at increased EMF exposure except I've been at the same workplace for 8 yrs now so this is very curious?

Hi Rick,
My RF career ended in 1995 not long after public TV lost federal funding. At that time I was an engineer for NH public television, prior to that I was a microwave systems technician in the Air Force. I guess it was a blessing in disguise when I lost my job. I work in IT now so I can’t escape all EMF, especially Wi-Fi. I started to plan my escape, but I haven’t figured out what I can do that satisfies my techie-geek side without exposing me to massive EMF… I am open to suggestions…
You are right about it being a floating ground, floating in reference to earths ground. But as long as it is the only ground up there and there isn’t a second ground source in reference to the first ground to cause a ground loop it is ok. You’re definitely not going to receive the same benefits as grounding bare foot to the earth but you will benefit from being at zero reference to the on board electronics.
No, I don’t fly the big planes, sailplanes and ultralights are what peg my fun meter… seat of the pants flying. When I do fly it is only for a short duration of time, usually under an hour and also it is not too far up from earth.
Bruce

### Rick — 2014-03-08T21:55:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-114195>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=113779)
>
> Welcome, Rick. You might want to read the Redox Rx blog, and the other redox blog just before it. Dr. K hasn't gotten to his recommendations for floxies, but he did mention that they have a bad water chemistry. Sounds logical, since fluoride is involved.

Hey nonchalant! Yes I concur that these drugs are reprehensible but faced with the choice of a diverticulum becoming abscessed and leading to life-threatening sepsis, I never know what else to do when this occurs, except of course to go on a liquid diet, then gradually change to soft smooth foods and then solid food with lots of probiotics.

Anyone know of a protocol to stave off these infections with Lugol's? My understanding is that iodine is a powerful and adaptogenic antimicrobial that could wipe out the bad bugs while preserving the beneficial flora but I think I have not the knowledge or skill to implement this therapy on my own; thus the reliance on very bad antibiotics! I certainly don't plan on this happening ever again (I am completely OCD about avoiding seeds, nuts, skins and peels or anything that could get stuck) and yes the previous gastroenterologist and my primary internist all suggested I do a bowel resection surgery to eliminate the pockets in the descending colon. I even saw a surgeon to discuss the possibility and then one morning I got a call from Valley Hosp saying I was scheduled for this procedure without my confirmation! So I got a second opinion from the chief of surgery at VH in Ridgewood NJ and I followed his advice to hold off on the surgery. Sadly the worst culprit in my experience, the one that landed me in the hospital in Teaneck NJ for five days about 14 yrs ago was a piece of gristle from a strip steak that I accidentally swallowed...newer info online now about diverticular disease suggests it has nothing to do with hard crunchy foods getting stuck and other practitioners claim they can cure or prevent an attack with heavy doses of probiotics, not sure about that...The standard recommendation is a high fiber diet but I disagree and try to stay moderate to low fiber now.

### Rick — 2014-03-08T22:08:49+0200
