> My RF career ended in 1995 not long after public TV lost federal funding. At that time I was an engineer for NH public television, prior to that I was a microwave systems technician in the Air Force. I guess it was a blessing in disguise when I lost my job. I work in IT now so I can’t escape all EMF, especially Wi-Fi. I started to plan my escape, but I haven’t figured out what I can do that satisfies my techie-geek side without exposing me to massive EMF… I am open to suggestions…
> You are right about it being a floating ground, floating in reference to earths ground. But as long as it is the only ground up there and there isn’t a second ground source in reference to the first ground to cause a ground loop it is ok. You’re definitely not going to receive the same benefits as grounding bare foot to the earth but you will benefit from being at zero reference to the on board electronics.
> No, I don’t fly the big planes, sailplanes and ultralights are what peg my fun meter… seat of the pants flying. When I do fly it is only for a short duration of time, usually under an hour and also it is not too far up from earth.
> Bruce

Ahha, thank you Bruce!

Yes that's a good question, what could possibly provide technical nirvana because just about anything and everything out there tries to incorporate wireless data xfer or communication of some kind, can't help you there which is why I want to leave CR in the next 5-6 yrs and open my holistic nutrition practice...somewhere in that time frame, Sue and I will sell our Ramsey NJ house and move closer to the shore as our two daughters are 23 and 26 now, just don't know where exactly yet, south NJ, Delaware, VA who knows...Good talking with you,

Rick

PS - Hah, of course there's always high-end class A audio amps and such for a geeky hobbyist but that's no career if you're looking for a new one of those. I test computers for consumer reports and my wife is a senior architect for voice apps at Nuance Communications here in Mahwah NJ.

> [Rick said:](/goto/post?id=114187)
>
> Hello Caroline, yes EMF hell, hundreds of cell phones and wifi routers at work, at home we turn it off when not in use but 20 other routers in our neighborhood - yes I need a magnetico most assuredly. $ for that will need to be accrued over time this year but $ for Gold membership, no way, not yet...my goal is that I can join as a healthcare practitioner in 5 years or so. In the car I personally have no EMF locally; no headsets, bluetooth etc but I spend anywhere from 1-3 hours driving un-grounded to and from work depending on the traffic over the damn TappanZee bridge...
>
> What else can you help me with on the forum? I will elaborate ABOVE in my other answer because I got kicked off the internet and didn't finish editing to fully explain my problems, but here is one example from the RP Rx, quoting JK:
>
> "For a low redox potential, we want a low electrical resistance in cells and tissues. Semiconductors are designed to have low resistance by their very nature. A high redox potential robs them of that ability."
>
> I don't understand these statements because in my mind I thought for sure he was saying a high redox potential was good and desirable and here it is now the opposite? So what is this measurement of redox potential (RP)? Is it our cellular charge contained within our aqueous battery (the water EZ, right?) so you want that potential difference to be as high (more voltage) as possible? Or does he mean the redox potential is the actual barrier potential described in biochem re: enzymatic reactions and we really want that barrier potential to be as small (low voltage) as possible so the reaction pathways can occur with the least amount of additional energy expenditure??
>
> This is one example of where I read and reread the blogs and just get more and more confused and go around in circles ad infinitum...maybe it's just me because other folks seem to catch on to what Jack means and are able to apply this knowledge successfully or maybe these are just typos in the blogs...but being very literal myself, it's confusing as hell and uber-frustrating.
>
> So if anyone can help translate this passage, that would be great! In essence here's what I'm asking - is the RP a measure of charge or potential difference (voltage) that indicates how well our water batteries are charged up and therefore the higher the better....or is the RP a barrier potential or threshold that needs to be overcome and therefore we want it to be as low as possible as he seems to be indicating in the quoted passage?
>
> Thank you Caroline!

You are absolutely right, Rick! I missed that. The passage should read something like this:
For a high redox potential, we want a low electrical resistance in cells and tissues. Semiconductors are designed to have low resistance by their very nature. A low redox potential robs them of that ability. (Or perhaps -- A high resistance robs them of that ability.)

I have sent Jack a correction.

### Rick — 2015-01-17T18:49:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-153162>

Help, any experts out there, it has been suggested that the attached test indicates a poor ratio of firmicutes to bacteroidetes and this may account for my inability to lose weight despite many leptin resets, CT, and ketosis? A further suggestion is that an all vegetable diet would fix the imbalance in gut flora.

Another development is that in November 2014 fasting blood glucose was 127 and HbA1C was 6.0% which is very disconcerting in itself but upon reviewing blood work from 2013 serum ferritin has been 711, and 567 a month later, and in Nov 2014 still high at 490. But with the pre-diabetic numbers, fatigue, low-T and impotence shouldn't I be looking at hemochromatosis as a driving cause and if so what can be done about this condition (I am of northern European descent).

Yes I am going to my PCP on Tuesday to request another iron panel and HFE test and I am also asking to be sent for a hydrogen and methane breath test as I now suspect my 30 yr history of IBS is largely due to SIBO. Stool improves whenever I have diverticulitis but then IBS symptoms return after the ABx are finished which is a classic indicator...

And, yes I've done a little looking out there re: hemochromatosis and vegetarian, low protein or no red meat diets are suggested but there is so much conflicting advice on the subject, if there is anyone here with some expertise or personal experience in this area, I would very much appreciate your help and guidance.

Thank you,
Rick

PS - we did purchase a 5 gauss magnetico last year and it has helped my wife's herniated disc but me, not so much, can't say I've felt any different at all. In fact if anything I'm doing worse now with weight up to 190, blood sugar dysregulation, very high lipids and glycated hemoglobin, still low-T and fatigue and recently hypoglycemia meaning that I need to disobey the rules of the leptin Rx and eat way more often than 2-3 meals/day...but this could all be related to high iron storage yes?

### Rick — 2015-01-18T14:48:31+0200

Thank you for your reply, sorry can't answer your question, this is all very new to me, don't know if I even have this condition and I've never given blood except for lab work draws. Are you suggesting that copper and zinc are lowered due to high iron because as per Dr. Tim I've been supplementing with 50 mg zinc with 2-3 mg of copper and 1500 mcg of chromium tripicolinate? But frankly since trying those no improvement to hypogonadism or blood sugar dysregulation and lately despite ketogenic epi-Paleo diet I am rapidly becoming pre-diabetic with severe hypoglycemia.

Since 2013 I've seen 5 docs but 2 just attributed the high ferritin to inflammation, the one that did labs in Nov 2014 did not look at any iron markers, just FBG, glycated hemoglobin, standard lipid panel, FSH, LH and testosterone. I went to her to continue with T replacement but she refused because I had increased from 200 into the 400s and she thought that was plenty good and only wanted to put me on Metformin...so this January I just visited a DC and nutritionist trained by Dr. Kharrazian and he was the one that suggested the high ferritin was hemochromatosis.

I should probably explain the T levels; the first Doc in Manhattan put me on 50 mg Testim which I did for about 2 months with no effect at all. So I bought the third months supply but stopped using it shortly thereafter and went to another doc in New Jersey and she put me on a compounded Testim cream with chrysin, again with little effect...so I bought some oral chrysin with piperine from LEF.org and double dosed the compounded cream with the leftover Testim and that's what got me the 200 point increase.

Perhaps I will know more details after next week and maybe the PCP will send me for some phlebotomies so I will post more updates asap...

Thank you Da-mo!

Since this occurred I've read sites that recommend vegan, vegetarian or just no red meat diets...or to increase consumption of cabbage or high calcium dairy foods to chelate the iron...or other papers that say the chelators only work on non-heme iron...or that diet has no significant impact and so on...

I really don't think I'm able to sort out all these different opinions (I am the world's worst lie detector, I usually have no idea when people are not telling the truth, have an agenda, or sometimes even joking, I just miss all those subtleties in person or in writing), so if anyone understands where the truth of this controversy lies, I would very much appreciate getting clued in!

Here's another completely different viewpoint I stumbled on regarding vitamin C and antioxidants in hemochromatosis (vit C is typically avoided completely since it enhances iron absorption): [http://www.health-science-spirit.com/haemo2.html](http://www.health-science-spirit.com/haemo2.html)

Any comments on this article would be welcome also and again thanks to all for any input,

Rick

PS - I have stopped using my cast iron cookware and I am transitioning to an all vegetable diet for a few weeks to see if I can alter the balance btwn firmicutes and bacteroidetes. Perhaps without any heme-iron I can get the ferritin down into the 2-300 range...

### JanSz — 2015-01-20T17:06:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-153438>

\============================== =====================
**Hemachromotosis Genetic test (HFE Gene Test)**

HEREDITARY HEMOCHROMATOSIS
\_\_DNA Mutation Analysis (UOM )
Hereditary hemochromatosis (HH) is an autosomal recessive
disorder of iron metabolism that results in iron overload
and potential organ failure. It is one of the most common
genetic disorders in individuals of European-Caucasian
ancestry, with an estimated carrier frequency of 10%. HH is
caused by mutations in the HFE gene. Most individuals with
HH (60%-90%) are homozygous for the C282Y mutation. A
smaller percentage of affected individuals are either
compound heterozygous for the C282Y and H63D mutations
(3%-8%), or homozygous for the H63D mutation (approximately
1%).
\============================== ==

If you test positive,
think of maintainin maximum Total Testosterone at no more than 550ng/dL
phlebotomies if Hgb raises over certain level, say 17.

Minimize foods with iron.

.

Hello Hope,

At the time I was considering writing a small blog for CR magazine to point out that some manufacturers did recognize the dangers of GHz EMF despite the lack of tighter regulations on the RF power by the FCC. Never went anywhere and probably editorial would never have approved of any such statements because CR is careful to not ruffle too many feathers in the marketplace unless a product or car presents a serious risk of bodily injury or death...my understanding via Jack was that the IR detector was to be used to pickup body heat so the phone would automatically reduce its RF output when the phone sensed a human in close proximity. I tried to confirm that design feature in that iPhone release with our own phone testers/engineers but no one knew anything about it and so I could not confirm one way or another; since CR is free from advertising we are not allowed to speak directly with any mfg's so I could not contact Apple directly. Perhaps Jack or someone else on here knows if that feature was ever actually fitted on that iPhone or any other cell phone, don't know...

### nicld — 2015-01-21T16:16:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-153531>

Don’t know if this will help or not but for years and years and years I had horrible gut issues. Was diagnosed with IBS-C maybe 12 years ago then with celiac. I was sometimes taking 2 capfuls of mirilax 2X a day just to stay going. Had GI’s done and colonoscopies done and they never really found anything. About 2 years ago (just before I found this place), I was realized I had SIBO and got a round of antibiotics from my GI doc and that knocked it down. Since then I have been eating Epi-paleo, lots of fermented veggies and home brewed kombucha and other stuff to work on the gut. But most important I got my circadian cycle way better. Now I am pretty darn regular and no more C and I am happy.

Everyone has their own N=1 but just wanted to say that I think gut issues can be fixed.

### Rick — 2015-01-21T19:27:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-153542>

Yes it helps very much nicld, thank you! We are quite similar as I now I believe I have SIBO also and this explains the 30 yr history of IBS but I wrongly described it as IBS-d until the last two years, but all along it was paradoxical-d or really what amounts to simultaneous d and c with chronic abdominal pain and feelings of incomplete emptying, along with the classic gas, bloating, distention and GERD. So what I've learned recently is that gut motility is all brain based so I have some physical exercises to stimulate the vagus to talk to the gut enteric system and I will still get the lactulose test for hydrogen and methane. I have scripts for this test plus labs for an iron panel, neutrophilic hypersegmentation index for folic acid deficiency, a full iron panel, plus 25-OH D3 so my plan is to run those labs later to see if ferritin has come down but yes I will run the SIBO test right away...since I now think this has been around for a very long time off and on, I will likely need rifaximin and neomycin as I bet I have hydrogen-producing bacteria and methane-producing prokaryotes. And I must also do the brain exercises; gargling, lightly stimulating the gag reflex w/a tongue blade, loud humming and singing. After the first round of meds, do the breath test and if the gases aren't down, repeat the Abx. When the overgrowth is gone < 3ppm of methane, < 20 ppm of hydrogen, or combined level < 15 ppm, then add some kind of prokinetic, like Iberogast, and/or huperzine A, or galantamine so the SIBO does not replapse...I learned all this recently from Drs. Siebecker, Sanberg-Lewis, and Kharrazian on Sean Croxton's digestion sessions.

Thanks for your support nicld, appreciate it very much and yes like you I also need to work on light cycles again. I was doing really well with light in 2013 and part of 14 but got out of the habit as I thought it wasn't doing much for my health but I was wrong wasn't I?

So I think I will stay all-vegetables for awhile yet and like I said, see if that brings down the iron labs; then I can go back to epi-Paleo too w/ more seafood, lots more veg and just less iron-rich meats and less often. Glad you are feeling so much better now too, chronic GI problems suck don't they?

TTYL and take care,

Rick

### nicld — 2015-01-21T21:50:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-153551>

Oh GI issues are the worst and my family gives me crap about it all the time. I am just "special"

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-163095>

Hi everyone, so I have been eating tons of seafood prepared with bacon fat and cooked veg with 1lb KG butter/week and it has been suggested that any weight loss efforts will be blocked by the imbalance of firmicutes to bacteroidetes that was discovered on Dr. Tim's referral to do the GI Effects 2200. The solution for this, because there is no such thing as bacteroidetes probiotics that one can take, is to go on an all vegetable diet which I tried for several weeks but now I'm learning this F/B ratio is very slow to change and it may take 3-6 months to reverse and some say to avoid animal fats because those feed the firmicutes...Jack or anyone out there, do u have some definitive info on this conundrum because I want to go back to the LR diet and I've decided I need to do it all with seafood but how can I get keto and burn bellyfat if animal fats promote the firmicutes in spite of large amounts of non-starchy veggie fibers to grow the bacteroidetes - all coconut or palm fats, cooked veg at every meal and 80% fat with no more than 20% total carbs and protein??

Help, any troubleshooting ideas, because it appears the gut flora are blocking my efforts but the fix for that is the antithesis of the LR diet, what to do to reverse this situation, Jack, anyone plz??

Thanks to all,

Rick

### Rick — 2015-05-02T21:16:20+0300

Does having more firmicutes than bacteroidetes promote obesity and if so, does just adhering to a strict epi-Paleo leptin reset w/CT eventually override the imbalance of these commensal flora or do I really have to fix that ratio first with all veggie and all coconut/palm oil diet and butyrate supps before I can get the leptin reset diet to work again for me???

Any sage advice welcome and tia,

R

PS - low redox still, poor adrenal output, weight stuck at 190 now, eating all fish protein, very low carb and very high sat fat (bacon grease & KG butter), no liquid oils except mct or c8 with mostly cooked green veg, sm veg salads at lunch and paying attention to light with red bulbs, blublockers, and sunrise exposure while grounding, been soaking in local quarry for 2 weeks now, early to bed early to rise...

### Christos — 2014-03-10T04:43:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christians-optimal-journey.10090/post-114457>

Hi Christian here from South Aus, 19 years old, currently studying science at Uni.
So let's begin.
I started the CT protocol I while back only to realize head dunking in 10 degree water gave me terrible brain freeze. This meant at least two things, I was severely dehydrated or had high tissue omega 6. However an AA/EPA ratio of 5.3/1 (and hence under 10/1) meant high tissue omega 6 was not the problem. So today I realized that urea on my Australian blood test is the same as BUN just in different units, and hence I worked out my BUN/creatinine ratio, it is 35/1!!!! Thus I have severe celluar dehydration, and hence why I'm getting brain freeze doing CT.

The cause of this is I think, due to a yearly dose of accutane two years ago. Due to the fact that the drug has caused me to have temperature issues (facial flushing/ hot body temperature), it must have caused leptin resistance at the hypothalamus (which regulates body temperature). This I think was caused by the drug specifically altering my photoperiod (due to a massive retinol dose) hence causing leptin resistance at the hypothalamus. This has then caused a lack of coherent water (from CPC 6 post on pseudotumor cerebri). Don't know enough about coherent water yet but perhaps it has something to do with the phosolipids not aligning correctly in the lipid bilayer of membranes (just read this from my BIO text).

Anyway so in order to lower my BUN/creatinine ratio I need to avoid dielectric blockers like the plague. Which brings me to my first question, chloride (comes from chlorine) and fluoride (comes from fluorine), are both halogens and hence dielectric blockers?

What water are people using in Aus to combat the donut whole (I'm doing spring ATM)?

Should I be taking any supplements to help lower BUN/creatinine ratio, magnesium perhaps? I know I'm low in Mg because I sweat like crazy easy.

Beside

Rest of Labs:
Vitamin D 49.6ng/mL
HS-CRP 0.74

Fasting Blood Glucose 90mg/dL
Fasting HDL Cholestrol 1.9mmol/L
Fasting LDL Cholestrol 8mmol/L
Fasting Triglycerides 0.7mmol/L

FBG shows that I'm leptin resistant at the liver, but HS-CRP isn't that high. Just thought I'd post all this up as my first post, any comments appreciated.

We in the southern hemisphere should be drinking imported water from the northern hemisphere. I buy mine at Aldi.

# Thread: Journey to leptin sensitivity, lower WAT and athletic performance.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-to-leptin-sensitivity-lower-wat-and-athletic-performance.10147/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Infinusss — 2014-03-13T19:47:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-to-leptin-sensitivity-lower-wat-and-athletic-performance.10147/post-115221>

A/S: 30M
Height: 5'11.5"
Weight: 209lbs

My goals are to drop BF%, gain strength and explosive movement. I want make those gains for ice hockey performance, and general athletic performance and health (more energy/confidence). I also want to look good at the beach.

-   I haven't got any blood tests done, yet, but I think I might beLR. I'm by no means obese, but my BF% is higher than I want it to be. I do hold an undesirable amount of fat, especially in my gut and chest.

-   I'm feel tired or even exhausted every morning when I wake up, and I feel as though I should have more energy, drive and concentration than I do.

-   I was also diagnosed with moderate ADD after a full day of testing by a professional, a few years ago, after years and years of struggling with attention/concentration/mood problems. I'm not sure if this has anything to do with my western diet history (that I've been trying to get away from for the past 5 or 6 years, though not always consistent) or physical/metabolic state, of if it belongs in this post.

I started the Leptin Rx on Monday, although I've been trying to stay away from grains and dairy for years. Sometimes unsuccessfully (think roller-coaster, cheat days and weeks), that's why I currently have a belly, and also because I'm very sedentary for my job and wasn't exercising or moving much.

**Two issues that I have right now are:**

1\. Jack said to hold off on heavy exercise until more LS. Unfortunately, I started HIIT and conditioning at about the same time I started Leptin Rx. For the first time ever, I feel dedicated to HIIT and staying with it. Since I have an extremely hard time staying with routines, I'm afraid that if I stop, I will have a hard time starting again in 6 weeks. I'm not willing to stop HIIT. Can I continue to train HIIT and be successful with leptin Rx?

2\. I was also thinking about starting CT soon. I read that Jack sates that having higher carbs will make you cold adapt faster, but in the leptin protocol, he states to keep carbs low. Does this mean that I should wait until LS to CT, so that I can up my carbs to 50-150g/day and cold adapt faster? I'm trying to figure out where I should stand with carbs.

I posted a few pictures of myself to give everyone an idea of where I'm at.

I fought Jack for 3mths about not exercising while doing the LR. and I had no success. **As much as HIIT Feels good... its going to slow down your progress**.... Learn from my mistakes (take a few moments to read my N=1 [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/n-1-my-journey-on-the-leptin-reset.94/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/n-1-my-journey-on-the-leptin-reset.94/) ) **the LR is about letting your body heal and resetting your circadian clock**.

Now for CT. You can go ahead and jump right in. Though you might want to consider starting with face dunking, then move on to full body in the water to see how it works for you... I was mostly Keto when I started CT. And CT acutally works best when you're Keto Adapted. I think you miss read about the carbs.

journal. and keep us posted on your successes.

### Josh — 2014-03-13T19:55:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-to-leptin-sensitivity-lower-wat-and-athletic-performance.10147/post-115224>

Nice picture....good luck and welcome. Not exercising is counter to our "more is more" culture. Consider that "less is more" can be a possibility and that the right dose is the right dose for a given N=1. If your body cannot respond with autophagy to the stress of HIIT, then it is just self-destruction, literally. Leptin sensitivity is a key to this. For me, it is essential to see the concept (s) at work here.

>
> journal. and keep us posted on your successes.

From: [http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)
"The beauty of this adaptation is that is does not require any change to your core temps. When you begin to mess with your core temps you can get into trouble with frostbite and freezing injury. The higher your omega six content, the worse cold adapted you will be. The higher your omega three content is, the better you will adapt to cold. The higher protein/fat intake you have, the slower you will adapt to cold. The more carbs your have in diet (LR), the easier you will find it to adapt to cold. If you have a history of smoking, dipping, cigar use you will not cold adapt well. If you are dehydrated (booze/wine) you will not cold adapt fast either."

### nicld — 2014-03-14T17:15:16+0200

Listen to Gretchen, do CT and quit with the exercise!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

If you have any inflammation in your body doing any kind of exercise will probably make it worse, TRUST ME!!! It has clicked for me that excess body fat is a sign that I have inflammation in the body (Jack has said this multiple time) . Exercise is a stressor to the body.

You can get a couple of key tests that Jack says will give a picture of what is going on. I think they might be Highly Sensitive CRP (inflammation marker), bun/creatine level (dehydration), vitamin D and DHEA. If you write these down and take to a doctor and explain why you want these they should order them and be covered. I been taking a list to my doctor and have explained why I want these and I have never been denied a test. You can get some saliva tests (male hormones, cortisol) from ZRT labs for a reasonable price, just google and see what offers are out there.

Nicole

Also, I try to eat 2 big links of pork or chicken sausage (not pastured, but working on getting some) or GF steak, and maybe an avocado or BP coffee. ### caroline — 2014-03-18T23:51:06+0200

### Infinusss — 2014-03-24T19:59:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-to-leptin-sensitivity-lower-wat-and-athletic-performance.10147/post-117641>

Today marks me at two weeks on the reset.

Progress that I've had so far:

-   I'm starting to wake up at 6:30am on my own, before my alarm goes off. When I wake up, I feel more alert and much less groggy than before I started the reset.
-   I am starting to feel more energy throughout the day.
-   I'm down 10lbs

I'm still doing HIIT because I don't feel or see any negative results from it. I don't think that it is effecting my reset at all. My recovery times are getting better, my endurance is increasing, and I don't feel as much dread or despair doing my workouts as I did two weeks ago.

I started CT this morning with face dunks. I have some questions on it, but I'll post them in the CT section instead.

All in all, I feel as though the reset is already starting to do positive things for me. Sometimes I don't get my food down until almost an hour until after I wake up, and sometimes I don't always get 4+ hours in after dinner, but I try to do the best that I can with my schedule and with the other people in my life.

# Thread: About this "Journal" thing....(new here)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-this-journal-thing-new-here.10269/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GGinPG — 2014-03-21T19:02:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-this-journal-thing-new-here.10269/post-116964>

I scanned a few of the recent posts to get an idea what I should be documenting here but I couldn't really glean too much other than it seems fairly informal (?). I looked for a "how to", but didn't see one. So I have to humbly ask for help .

Should I use this space basically like I would a personal diary? Should I use this initial post as the Journal itself or should I open a new post each time I have something to report? Should I use the Journal to wax poetic about my Buster Keaton infatuation? (Probably not that, I'd guess!)

Thanks much!
GG

I

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-21T19:31:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-this-journal-thing-new-here.10269/post-116972>

Context is key. Jack reads these he drops by and will drop nuggets. write about your journey, your medical issues - your back story of what brought you here. Some focus on what their eating, others focus on how they feel, others use this as a place to chart their ups/downs, hormonal swings, responses to eating certain foods. FBG levels etc...

capture what's important to you. If we know your context - many of us "old timers" here can help give you pointers and tips that might help you on your journey...
Gretchen

### Lahelada — 2014-03-21T19:31:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-this-journal-thing-new-here.10269/post-116973>

Hey GG, welcome.
This is basically your spaceer where you share whatever you like on your journey. Drank milk today felt bloated. Yogurt was fine . Slept badly due to staying up late .It is a tool for you to understand if you have patterns to whatever is happening and for someone else to chime in and say hey when I did this....or ..did you remember to?
I understand your confusion and having been here for a while I regret not having made my first posta summary of how I got here.
What I would include is this What kind of child were you ,healthy sick,where did you grow up ona farm in a city ,
how did your life progress and when did you first realise something was off. Did you see a doc,do you have a diagnosis or maybe you you just came here to lose weight. Did these symptoms come on after a traumatic event
Your surroundings , Do you live in an apartment where your PC sees sees 30 wifis from the neighbour or in a tree house in the Serengeti?  Does your job expose you to EMF?
Any medicines,supplement you may be taking

As I said this is not a guideline , you can uuse this journal as you wish but I think an introspection like this would be useful.

### GGinPG — 2014-03-21T20:24:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-this-journal-thing-new-here.10269/post-116983>

Ok, great - gotcha! Thanks to you both

### caroline — 2014-03-22T00:30:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-this-journal-thing-new-here.10269/post-117026>

Yep .... tell us whatever is on your mind ......

This is all about change ....body, mind and spirit .....really open your eyes and see the world as it is - not as you wish it could be and enjoy every single second!

Your life ..... your journey ....make it awesome!

# Thread: Let's get optimal! Adrenal Fatigue, High Cortisol, Reactive Hypoglycemia...Help with labs welcome!

### Tombleweed — 2014-05-23T21:05:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-get-optimal-adrenal-fatigue-high-cortisol-reactive-hypoglycemia-help-with-labs-welcome.10908/post-128112>

I began my paleo diet and bio hacking adventure about 18 months ago, but feel I have hit a brick wall and need some help to better understand my labs and situation. I have reactive hypoglycaemia, elevated morning cortisol, high cholesterol and mild hypothyroidism (low T3 to T4 ratio and slightly elevated TSH).

I am from England, 24 years old, lean with relatively good muscle tone, 6ft and 75kg. I am pretty physically “fit” despite health issues. I was quite severely overweight for a few years as a teenager, but lost the weight in my late teens. I also had neck surgery when I was 11 years old due to an aneurysmal bone cyst and I had to have my C6 vertebrae completely removed. I now have a junkyard of titanium in there and was exposed to some serious EMF with multiple scans, MRI’s and Xrays throughout childhood. I have also snapped my ACl and torn cartilage in both knees from playing sports. However, I feel that these accidents were not bad enough to have merited such damage if I had been healthier.

Since starting paleo and dabbling with a ketogenic diet many of the health issues that I have been dealing with for years have actually worsened considerably. I feel my underlying metabolic/blood sugar/hormonal/gut problem has been brought to light by low-carb living. I see this as a good thing and this is why I have persevered in the hope that this new information and lifestyle would allow me to get to the bottom of it once and for all.

I have bad acne on my face and chest, dark shadows under my eyes, dizziness upon rising or after eating sugar, frequent headaches, reactive hypoglycaemia, digestive issues, food intolerances, constipation, poor concentration and mental acuity, brain fog, excessive sweating, anxiety despite relaxed attitude and general fatigue. However, I am still able to find the energy to exercise (weightlifting, climbing, jogging and biking) and feel much better when I do so long as I eat some carbs for fuel. Nonetheless, deep down I can sense that excessive exercising and high-carb living has only been masking a deeper hormonal imbalance and giving me some temporary adrenaline and endorphins to run off.

When I try to eat a ketogenic diet my energy levels drop severely, my blood sugar gets very low, my blood pressure gets low, I get dizzy a lot and get heart palpitations. A post-prandial reading of 54 mg/dl would be common. I also tend to feel very depressed and find it hard to function efficiently on a day-to-day basis. I get very skinny, can see my ribs, feel like I am loosing muscle and becoming skinny fat. My hunger also completely disappears, my digestion seems to slow and food feels heavy in my stomach. However, my appetite/ cravings for carbs are constant. Cold showers and baths seem to help energy levels, alertness and feeling of wellbeing, but only temporarily.

My naturopathic doctor tells me that I do have a leaky gut and also that my liver is not in good shape, especially phase II function and bile production. I suspect I am not digesting fats well and consequently struggling to use them as fuel.

I have been referred to an endocrinologist who performed an overnight dexamethasone suppression test. However, my cortisol levels apparently remained at such a high level that he questioned whether I had actually taken the drug (I don’t have specific figures yet). He wants to perform further tests to find out why. I feel like he doesn't have a clue and won't be able to help me. I'm pretty sure I don't have Cushing's Syndrome and that the high cortisol levels are more likely linked to blood sugar imbalances.

I also had an oral glucose tolerance test in which three hours after drinking 75g of liquid glucose my blood sugar level dropped to 50 mg/dl and I had all the symptoms of reactive hypoglycaemia. However, my glucose level never went above 110 mg/dl at any point, which according to my endocrinologist suggests that I am highly insulin sensitive despite obvious blood sugar issues. I'm not sure if he is right?

I have been hugely inspired by Dr. Jack Kruse’s incredible work and all the information he has made available. It has shown me what tests I needed to get privately to finally persuade the unsympathetic doctors that something is actually abnormal and that I am not too young to have any issues

Since most of the docs aren’t of much use here in the UK I was hoping for some help to better understand my labs, what could be the real root cause of my issues, whether I am actually leptin resistant or not, and if my neck surgery and knee injuries could tie into my current problems? Any tips, info or advice on how to move forward from here and what JK posts to read would be massively welcomed.

I have already got some good answers on this thread, but thought it was suggested that I start a journal, so that's what I've done! [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...ning-cortisol-secondary-hypothyroidism.10867/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/spinal-surgery-fat-child-reactive-hypoglycaemia-high-morning-cortisol-secondary-hypothyroidism.10867/)

Below are the labs I have managed to get either privately through Genova Diagnostics or through my doctor:

**Adrenal Stress Profile**

Cortisol levels (nmol/L)

8am: 33.3 (12-22) HIGH

12 noon: 10.7 (5 - 9) HIGH

4pm: 5.5 (3 - 7)

12 midnight: 2.4 (1 – 3

DHEA Levels:

12 noon: 0.17 nmol/L LOW

4pm: 0.19 nmol/L LOW

DHEA Mean: 0.18 nmol/L (0.40-1.47) LOW

DHEA Cortisol Ratio: 0.35 nmol/L (2.0-6.0) LOW

Adrenal Stress stage = resistance stage 2 maladaptation

**Thyroid + reverse T3 (Genova Diagnostics)**

Total Thyroxine (T4) 106 nmol/L (58-154)

Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH) 3.87 mIU/L (0.4 – 4) Borderline High

Free Thyroxine (FT4) 16.5 pmol/L (10-22)

Free T3 (FT3) 3.33 pmol/L (2.8-6.5) Borderline Low

FT4:FT3 ratio 5 (2-4.5) HIGH

Reverse T3 0.37pmol/mL (0.14 - 0.54) Normal

(Reverse T3 23.9 ng/dL (9.0 – 35.0) Normal)

**Does this T3 result rule me out from being leptin resistant in spite of hormonal and blood sugar issues?**

**More recent standard Thyroid Function Test**

Serum TSH 2.61 mU/L (0.3 – 5.00)

Serum Free T4 16.4 pmol/L (11.0 – 23.0)

**Vitamin D –** I had previously been taking 5000 IU of D3 per day as I was presuming low-levels based on time of year and ASI results. Could the high cortisol be preventing absorption on a cellular level? I stopped taking supplements after first test.

31st Dec 13 Total Hydroxyvitamin D level 177 nmol/L (50 – 100) HIGH

1st March 14 Total Hydroxyvitamin D level 111 nmol/L (50 – 100) HIGH

**Serum Lipids**

Serum Cholesterol 7.9 mmol/L (305 mg/dl) (<4) HIGH

Serum Triglycerides 0.5 mmol/L (44 mg/dl) (0 - 1.7)

Serum HDL level 3.1 mmol/L (120mg/dl) (>1)

Serum LDL level 4.6 mmol/L (178mg/dl) (0 -3) HIGH

Total cholesterol HDL ratio 3 (0 -5)

**C- reactive protein (CRP)** 0.9mg/L (0 – 5.0)

**Erythrocite sedimentation rate (ESR)** 4mm/h (0-20)

**Secretory IGA** 115.9ug/mL (118-641) LOW

**B12/Folate**

Serum vitamin B12 1248ng/L (172- 1162) HIGH

Serum Folate 8.7ug/L (4.6-18.7)

**HbA1c level** 33mmol/mol (<53)

**Bone Profile:**
Serum calcium 2.37 mmol/L (2.20 - 2.60)

Corrected serum
Calcium level 2.40 mmol/L (2.20 - 2.60)

serum inorganic
phosphate 1.12 mmol/L (0.80 - 1.50)

Serum alkaline
phosphatase 85 U/L (40 - 120)

Serum total
protein 67 g/L (55-80)

Serum albumin 39 g/L (35 - 50)

Serum globulin 28 g/L

**Urea and Electrolytes**
Serum sodium 137 mmol/L (133 - 146)

Serum potassium 4.1 mmol/L (3.5 - 5.2)

Serum urea level 3.5 mmol/L (2.3 - 7.5)

Serum creatinine 96 umol/L (o -135)

**Is there any way to calculate redox potential from these? There doesn't seem to be a BUN on the standard uk test...**

**Liver function test -** all results well within normal range

**Full blood count –** all results now in the normal range. However, a few months ago my neutrophil count was very marginally low and my Mean Corpuscular Volume (MCV) was also marginally high. Anyone know if this can be of significance?

On an Elemental Hair Analysis my mercury was slightly high (2.4ug/g – (<1.32)) while my phosphorus levels were also high (212ug/g – (104-206)

Thanks in advance for any help.

Tom

### Tombleweed — 2014-05-23T21:21:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-get-optimal-adrenal-fatigue-high-cortisol-reactive-hypoglycemia-help-with-labs-welcome.10908/post-128117>

p.s. Dr. JK's insights on my labs on the first thread were as follows:

"DHEA low.......means too many protons in the CSF........IL-6 is high. Means lots of calcium release for some reason.
I bet your zip code is not so hot. You do need a pic for me to go further........."

This is from Quantum Biology 10 : "When melatonin cycles are altered it creates an imbalance in Magnesium-calcium optimization inside the cells of the brain"

I know Jack isn't a big fan of supplements, especially melatonin, but from my labs I am wondering if I might need supplemental DHEA and melatonin to kickstart recovery? Pregnenolone is something else I have been considering taking given my pregnenolone steal...However, I am very wary of messing with hormonal supplements without some expert guidance.

I have been regularly going to bed early, waking early and limiting blue light exposure at night for over a year now, but don't seem to be seeing any improvement, so this is why I am thinking about such options...(There isn't much I can do about where I am living for the moment).

# Thread: Starting over....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-over.10971/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2014-06-01T01:38:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-over.10971/post-129098>

I'm back. Life became too demanding with the care of and subsequent death of one in-law and the subsequent and continuing declining health of the other in-law. They live next door to us, so the primary care falls to us. Very time consuming, with many trips to the hospital and lots of my time spent there helping however I am needed. On a positive note (for me, not so much for my hubby), my MIL is moving to Utah to be with her 2 daughters. It's a permanent move, and for the first time in our 38 years of marriage, our unspoken but entirely understood second responsibility (after our marriage) to watching over my in-laws will finally fall on the shoulders of someone else. I am secretly rejoicing! We can now plan trips w/0 regard to finding someone to watch over my MIL.

Last fall I had 25 blood/hair/saliva tests done after going to my marvelous doc for insomnia and post- cardiac stent implants. The news was not good--every hormone was severely deficient. He put me on a regimen of supplements, a continuation of 3 sleep/restless leg meds, and a ketogenic diet. Here are the critical results then and the new ones as of last month. You can see that the stress of my life has worsened the situation.

Then: CRP, hs: 2.44 now: 2.55 He wants it below 1.0
Fibrinogen 488 now: 504 He wants it below 300
Lipoprotein 136 now: 128.7 He wants it below 70
Homocysteine 14.5 now: 9.2 He wants it at or below 5.7

He said that the only reason I am still alive is because my cholesterol is so good. It's at 232 with HDL at 84.

Other results: BUN/creat ratio is 21/5
DHEA: 78.76
Estradiol: 18
T3 total: 85.7
T4 5.99
Testosterone: 41.46
TSH: 3.03
Growth hormone: .02
Pregnenolone; 32

There are others. Basically I'm a mess. I have lost most of my hair, including eyebrows, pubic hair, and of course head hair. I have to wear a wig. Silent celiac. Insomnia. Tired all the time, foggy brain, and barely function some days.

Even so, I have much to be grateful for!! I have a husband who thinks my bald head is cute and likes my curvyness (although he would like me to lose weight because he knows how much I would like to). I have 5 grown sons who call us every Sunday afternoon. They have wonderful wives and are raising between them 20 children. I have my faith, live in the country with fresh air, have beautiful vistas (2 volcanoes visible from our house here in Washington State), and silence.

Now it's time to get busy again taking care of ME!

### caroline — 2014-06-01T01:56:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-over.10971/post-129101>

So glad you are back Julie .... and beginning to look after you ...sending you a hug xo

### fitness@home — 2014-06-01T02:13:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-over.10971/post-129103>

Welcome back Julie. Once the stress decreases your body should start responding better in conjunction with the protocols you've learned here.

Bet you can't wait for that first vacation trip

### nonchalant — 2014-06-01T03:38:47+0300

So good to hear from you again, Julie!

> He said that the only reason I am still alive is because my cholesterol is so good. It's at 232 with HDL at 84.

Wow, great doc!!

### Jude — 2014-06-01T05:46:17+0300

Good to have you back on board.....2 volcanoes close by...wow that sure could be used to yr advantage with possibly a big collection of earth electrons via grounding, to help in yr healing process.

Don't know if you've been able to keep up with all the reading of late ...but the message has become reduced to a slogan of late...Electrons,electrons,electrons...is what we're missing and we need DHA to catch them..

# Thread: An Update of My Highway To Hell! Tim Jackson, Asia, BPC, Tests..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-update-of-my-highway-to-hell-tim-jackson-asia-bpc-tests.11130/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-06-20T13:34:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-update-of-my-highway-to-hell-tim-jackson-asia-bpc-tests.11130/post-131502>

hi peeps,

So here i am back in asia. Before I left australia, i had a chance to go and 'unplug' - i stayed with a community in north queensland. Solar power, no wifi, barely any cell reception, good ground water, no television, massive organic gardens, pitch black at night, quiet.. what more could you want, right? I prob ate more carbs there than i have in eons. did i feel crap from eating more carbs and less protein? not at all - so im taking it as a good sign that im healing. They do the 'group meal' thing in the evenings, so i was always part of that.

anyhoo, it was nice. and when i left there after my two weeks - ppl commented on weight loss in me.. hmm interesting, definitely WASNT a food thing! (proven to myself, yet again!)

I had two races to work at in aus, and then i came to asia. Also, i made the switch to bulletproof coffee after that retreat. BPC kicks ass!!! and the reason i made the change was mainly because, 'trying to' give up coffee was causing me more mental anguish and torture than im sure it was worth. i got cocoa powder, and decaf, along with daves MCT and regular coffee.. in a nutshell - what am i finding? its now even weaning me off coffee!! some days i WANT decaf version! its easy, its not coming with anguish, torture, or feelings of loss, and its HEAVEN to be able to be at work (races) wake up, make my breakfast in my room ,and go for the day!! i usually add an organic egg, or eat an avocado or some meat as well (i went off eggs for months. i think im ok on them now? ) . personally, my own n=1 shows im better with something extra (fat or protein).. i guess i average 5-9 hours, yeah, it can be pretty random. its best i dont 'aim for 2pm' IFing as is DAs recommendation. if it happens it does, if not, then it doesnt

Im now in thailand. i was just at a race in Indonesia. I got a head cold half way through, this is the second cold iv had since JK protocols in place, and i used to get them for 3 weeks - theyd go nose, throat, chest.. without fail.. this is the second one that was 5-7 days! happy with that! plus, i was also getting a period. i had major fatigue to.. hmmm but i think that was the cold as well.
I always do cold showers, even here in thailand, the hot water doesnt work, so therye cold if i want them to be or not! and definitely earth myself on flights - im not swelling up anymore when i fly!!
theres a park here in the old city that i go to daily to earth. i am also using this time for some work on self - law of attraction, changing beliefs, EFT etc. Im starting to work on changing my 'sick thinking' to that of health.

\*\*\* question: i dont have a bath here. i have tepid to cold water in the shower. id like to do more. im not sure if il find an 'ice vest' per se. even doing cold face baths is not readily available. .. i sleep with air con on. i have no idea where id find a compression shirt hmmm, any advice peeps? i think ice will be easy to find, whats the best way to take advantage of it? '\*\*\*

(sorry guys, girl facts here!) Im still having quite a disaster time with periods. this month, it was carb cravings, a bit moody, couple of nights of insomnia.. and debilitating headaches at the end, and so heavy.. beyond belief! . iv also been sO fatigued since getting back from indonesia (4 days) which i put down to my cycle, and to the fact i had about 3 hours sleep the night before i left - anxiety, period, emotional dramas in my head :-/ but, no discomfort, no aches and pains.. i am booked in for some acupuncture tomorrow re this. not sure what JK says about it. it seems sex hormones are still outta whack.. im still using progesterone cream to (TJs recommendation)

\*\*\* in general? my sleeps improved by about 85% \*\*\*\* my rooms divinely dark at night. i still struggle tho if i go to bed like after 10pm?

i had a few people comment to me in indonesia about how much smaller i am! friends in thailand have made the same comments to.

In general, i find my eatings fine an dandy. some days are better than others, with how i feel, getting hungry etc. sometimes upping water helps a lot. though i try to average 4-6L per day. (im pretty sure its RO water im getting here in chiang mai. theres machines dotted about the old city, and you can refill your bottles. its around 1c per litre.. )

i had my second consult with Tim Jackson before i went to indo. gut gut gut! hes recommended i look into the 'specific carbohydrate diet'. its for restoring bacteria balance in the small intestine. He also wants me to do a metametrix test, (which my regular GP whose here in thailand, has available), a urinary profile test for amino acids and neurotransmitters (which i guess il have to order from the US) and, a slew of tests that can start ruling out viruses / systemic infection.

This week, i rocked off to 'Chiang Rai Ram' hospital with a list of tests, and i added HS-CRP to it as well
there i am, with my wish list... asking for tests. i was told i had to see a doctor tho, so i said 'ok cool' - what i saw was an endocronologist (TJ wants another full thyroid panel, and ACTH) . Actually, she was by far the best one iv seen! she felt around my neck and said she thinks my thyroids definitely 'big' (like my neck) and had no qualms ordering me all the tests, or, testing for cushings! yay! victory!  my last ASI was sky high cortisol day and night, TJ thought about cushings, but said maybe it wasnt high enough.. anyway, i had a test done whereby i took two pills at night, and then they drew blood at 8am the next day, to see how my cortisol was responding.. sorry, i dont have the paper here with me, so cant remember the name. Its not ACTH / synacthen, but its a start. depending on the results, thatll be the next step. So in a week, il go back to get all the results from her. Shes asked for a food diary  and what was my typical breakfast etc, like there and then she wanted an example .. when i started saying about BPC.. of course i got the '' reaction.. so i calmly said 'im not going to argue about calories with you, sorry.. low fat has never worked for me, and i FEEL a lot better doing what im doing now' !! she was fine with that, but yeah.. i can imagine the chat il get.. in saying that tho, she was quite understanding..

\*\*\* OMG, MY WEIGHT!! \*\*\*\*\*\*
so, they do your vital signs when you go in to the hospital. First visit, i was 89.2kg,.. WOW! iv cracked 90kg!!! Now, im not sure of the accuracy of the scales i was using at my place in NZ, i got told unreliable, BUT,, they were getting me up to over 95kg - heavier than most all black rugby players (enter suicidal thoughts here) , and then on wednesday, when i went back to give my blood, they weighed me again and i was 87.7kg!!!  WOW! i woulda jumped for joy if i wasnt stood on the scales!!!!

im thinking of joining the gym again. iv been so whacked since i got back though, im not sure.. its $30 for a month tho, and they have yoga classes twice a day, plus id like to do some weights again? and some sprints on the stationary bike. i think it would be ok?

\*\*\* FOOD IN THAILAND \*\*\*
over the road from me, theres a restaurant that sell bbq fish . the skin is salted and then they slow cook them, so when you buy, you can peel the skin back and eat the flesh. the fish is stuffed with lemon grass. its about $2 per fish  he also sells broth! either pork or beef.. Iv had the beef one, it has chunks of beef in it, and sometimes i add some butter / natto.

SUMMARY -
\*\*\* POSITIVES \*\*\*\*
1) weight loss! very very slow, but happening?
2) less bloating
3) improved sleep
4) improved mental state - havent bawled over the state of myself for a few months now
5) more energy
6) i have wrists again! my forearms were always a tell tale sign of what was going on - i had 'blocks' - straight lines from elbow to wrist, like they werent my hands attached..  and creases in my forearms - from too much 'fat'.. i still have 'cellulite' in my forearms when i squish my skin but.. i have wrists!?!?
7) this is possibly gonna be TMI.. but im sharing because - iv had NO sex drive for the last 4 years. & i felt so unsexy, ugly etc.. lately? iv been getting more attention, AND feeling some primal urges! (HALLELUJAH!) .. long story short, i actually took a selfie of me, topless, and sent it to 1 im connecting with..  ok, shock horror over.. my room was only semi lit, nothing too pornographic.. the reason WHY im sharing this is a) i actually had the confidence to do that?! b) it was fun / reckless / harmless - feelings i havent felt in years, c) i had a breast reduction 1.5 years ago. I lost over 1kg in that operation.. its amazing to have boobs that dont rule your life!! d) when im not pmt i actually have shape coming back to my stomach again - except for the lowest part of my stomach. i still have that annoying 'crease' there.
8) more emotionally stable / stronger
9) i actually wear gym / yoga pants IN PUBLIC now!!

\*\*\* NEGATIVES / UNCHANGED\*\*\*\*
1) i feel like im looking older, not younger...  could be sleep or dehydration i guess.. i feel like its happened like in the last 2 weeks LOL! turkey neck, wrinkles.. noooooooo
2) still feels slow. i started on JKs journey in november.. i can sabotage my own efforts. i worked out though, thats driven by frustration at how messed up i feel i am.. its like a 'whats the point' sort of reaction. and it can REALLY be driven by hormones!
3) i can still get carb cravings. though theyre not out of control at all...
4) its still a shocker to go to a changing room in a clothing store.. the 'cellulite' thats all over me.. even if iv had a good start to the day, thats a sure deal-breaker! lol
5) dont have 'sleeveless shirt arms' yet..  i think thats part of a thyroid issue myself..

ciao ciao!
)

# Thread: Khim's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/khims-journal.11228/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Khim — 2014-06-30T21:41:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/khims-journal.11228/post-132752>

Hi all,

Just got most of my lab results back and I need help. My cortisol levels are high as far as I can tell and low low DHEA. Some labs are still not back yet. But just a little history of my health...

Born 2 months premature.
Very skinny from birth to age 12, never felt hungry, only ate because it was meal time. I have often asked my friends what hunger feels like, weird.
Weight became normal with puberty but started getting cellulite at 14 years old despite being active. Also started having migraines.
Worked night and double shifts at 7-Eleven for a year after graduating high school, insomnia set in and then high blood pressure. Treated with HBP with chinese herbal concoction.
Started gaining weight at college due to stress of working and studying. Developed peptic ulcer, gained a lot of weight from taking Tagamet with milk. Lost weight after college doing karate.

Moved to Australia when our daughter was 3 yo. It was a very stressful first couple of years trying to become permanent residents and that was when my hair started turning grey, gained weight, and insomnia was the worst.
Worked night shift when our daughter started school so I can be home after school and on school holidays. I would also go to the gym after sending her to school. During this period I also developed asthma when our landlady decided to change all the windows in the whole apartment block while we were still staying there. I was put on Ventolin and after the first year and it got worse, so I decided to use alternative medicine to get better. I had started using Bragg's Apple Cider Vinegar after reading a lot of positive feedback from a health website and from that website read about home remedies for asthma and decided to try it (It took about 8 months and asthma is gone, but my lungs are still weak. I would have mild chest congestions and chronic coughing every winter).

I was sleep-deprived, moody, and after 3 years my memory suffered big time. This was when I decided to get a part-time job working school hours and letting my body heal. Went back to full time when she was old enough to walk to school after we bought our house.

Started low cab again in Oct 2013 and have been since then but only lost 3kg after 3 months. Was doing Bulletproof coffee intermittent fasting, lost 2 kegs in 2 weeks and read about Leptin Rx on the BP forum. Started Leptin Reset in April and gained 4kgs after 2 months. I don't have a lot of weight to lose but I am certainly "over-fat". Body comp is horrible, with poor skin elasticity. I used to have most fats in my hips, butt and thighs, but in the last year my upper arms are getting bigger with cellulites.

I hated most vegetables especially green leafy vegetables as they tasted extremely bitter to me, hated all meat, except for white meat chicken, hated shrimps. Loved crabs and white fish. It was until I was in college that I started loving green vegetables and shrimps. I still cannot tolerate the taste of red meat unless well marinated. Absolutely cannot tolerate salmon, tuna and fishy fish.

Here are my lab results. Any advise would be appreciated. Thanks!

Cortisol 6:30 am 46 nmol/L
Cortisol 8pm 30 nmol/L
DHEA 3.4 umol/L
Total Cholesterol 7.4 nmol/L
Hdl. 2.9 nmol/L
LDL 4.3 nmol/L
Trig. 0.4 nmol/L
LDL/ HDL ratio. 1.5
Chol/HDL ratio 2.5
IgG. Positive
IgA. Low positive
Homocysteine. 5.9 umol/L
CRP. 0.7 mg/L
Testosterone. 0.8 nmol/L
Free Testosterone. 5 pmol/L
Prolactin. 4.4 ug/L
Progesterone. 1.4 nmol/L
Estrogen. Still waiting for results
Estradiol. Still waiting for results
Free T4. 17.8 pmol/L
Free T3. 4.7 pmol/L
TSH. 2.81mIU/L
RT3. Still waiting for results
IGF1. Still waiting for results
Platelets. 204
Iron 21.2 umol/L
Transferrin. 2.54g/L
Saturation. 33%
Ferritin. 22 ug/

Issues to be worked on:
Brain fog
Mindlessness
Interrupted sleep
Cellulites
Body composition
Cold hands and feet
Low libido

So far:
Improved sleep but still wake up once to go to the bathroom since it's getting colder.
Brain fog improved but need work.

Oops, time to go. Will continue later...

### Inger — 2014-07-01T09:18:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/khims-journal.11228/post-132845>

Hi Khim! Good idea to start a journal!
The more you share the easier it is to get good advice

Do not stress with the weight - just focus on getting healthy, good body comp will follow, and it probably will take some time..... the most important thing is to heal now, from inside out

### Khim — 2014-07-01T12:13:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/khims-journal.11228/post-132852>

Thanks for your encouraging words Inger
I am naturally tense and do not know how to relax I often find myself clenching my jaw a lot or my shoulders tightening. When I wake up to go to the bathroom, my mind would be so active and then I can't go back to sleep. Right now now what helps is the BAB. All last week I was so busy with work that I didn't wake up early enough to have my breakfast at home and my sleep was shot. Today I was able to have my BAB again so hopefully I will have a good night's sleep. Do you ever have the problem of waking up to go to the bathroom when it's cold? I used to go 3-4 times but got better since starting LR. The problem went away after the first week and then came back when I CT at night, so I stopped CT at night. But now that the temperature has dropped down to around 5-6C I get up once in the night. I need advise on which broken part need to be fixed first.

### Khim — 2014-07-01T12:54:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/khims-journal.11228/post-132855>

My Leptin journey:

BAB at 4am: Eggs and bacon, BPC
LUNCH 10am: Chicken with salad, or a frittata
Dinner 4pm: White fish or hamburger with veggies
Experience:
Week 1: Felt energized and really good after BAB but would yawn non-stop from 8-9:30am everyday, then it was gone. Drowsy and sleepy at 5:30pm, would end up going to bed at 6pm. Deep sleep through the night with dreams. Wake up feeling GREAT.
Weeks 2-4: Same energy and uplifted mood after BAB but yawning has ceased, sleepiness and drowsiness set in around 7pm. Could not stay up beyond 7pm but sleep throughout the night.
Weeks 5-6: Daily cold showers A.M and cold bath in P.M. Deep sleep but had to wake up to go to the bathroom every night.
Weeks 6-8: Cold showers A.M and P.M, cold bath on weekends only. Didn't get up to go to the bathroom on weekdays unless it was a cold night.
Week 9 (last week): Didn't have breakfast until on schedule, didn't sleep well,too tired to get up in the morning.
Back on schedule today

### nicld — 2014-07-01T16:19:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/khims-journal.11228/post-132862>

I agree 200% with Inger, try to let go of the weight component of this and focus on getting the insides better. It will take some time, maybe ALOT of time. I have been following this for a year now and the weight has not gone down but the insides are getting better.

Sleep is huge in all of this. Do you have blueblocker glasses? They have made a huge difference for me.

Just keep journaling and good luck. There is so much to learn here and so much support, it is great.

Today I was able to have my BAB again so hopefully I will have a good night's sleep. Do you ever have the problem of waking up to go to the bathroom when it's cold? But now that the temperature has dropped down to around 5-6C I get up once in the night. I need advise on which broken part need to be fixed first.

Hi Khim! You aren't actually naturally tense, and you don't have to "know" how to relax. Your body will relax naturally, when it has what it needs. For me, tension and anxiety eased with a higher fat diet, and also other forms of additional electrons, such as grounding barefoot, CT, seafood, sunshine, excellent water. Glad you are finding the BAB is helping.

It's interesting to find that many "personality factors" are actually health factors.

### Lahelada — 2014-07-02T14:34:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/khims-journal.11228/post-132986>

Good morning Khim,
You made me giggle. I have the same issues with meditation. I am getting better with a simple trick. Breathe in,breathe out to the count of one without thinking anything. If you think extraneous thoughts you are stuck at1. you only get to count to 2 if your mind was absolutely still at the count of 1. If you are like me you will count to one 567 times hehe.

# Thread: Casey's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/caseys-journal.11350/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caseyjmc — 2014-07-18T09:27:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/caseys-journal.11350/post-134792>

I am 59 but feel more like 40. My wife and I run a home based company that sells one product along with its accessories. We've been married 31 years and have a joy-filled life. We have 2 adult married sons and three grandchildren who live a few blocks from us. We see them during the week and enjoy every minute.

I've spent a lifetime trying to lose weight. At times hugely successful but always started eating poorly and gained it back. 3 years ago I had some kind of epiphany and began eating smaller portions of everything. I went from 235lbs to 170 in a little over a year; then got into the sugar again and gained 15lbs. I started doing low-carb but the weight kept creeping up. At 190lbs I started LCHF according to The Diet Doctor [www.dietdoctor.com](http://www.dietdoctor.com), but the weight kept rising. 8 months into LCHF, some friends who are following Haylie Pomroy's Fast Metabolism Diet, lost 115lbs between them in 8 months. My wife and I decided to join them to see what would happen. In six weeks, my wife has lost 10 lbs. I've lost nothing. So I started trying to find out why, and I ran into The Leptin Diet.

I do not eat sugar or refined carbs. I do not eat fake foods, nor diet foods. I follow the plan I'm following to the T, so I have been frustrated that I didn't lose on Low Carb, LCHF (keto), nor the Fast Metabolism Diet. I drink plenty of water, I sleep well (only need 6-7 hours per night).

Enter the Leptin Diet. There seem to be many different versions. All of them seem to agree with one basic idea—BAB with 50-75 g. of protein. Not certain as to which one to follow, I began with BAB. 4 days later I had dropped 4 lbs. A week later I am up a lb.

I honestly don't know if I have the stamina to follow this plan, but I am going to take your advice and start with baby steps. I am weary of limiting my carbs to <25 per day. It didn't work before, but I'm open to the possibility that I wasn't eating enough protein (in fact, I was guided to reduce protein since it turns to glucose when there is too much, and ends up as if I had been eating carbs, so they say!)

Anyway, that's where I'm at right now. Thanks to all of you who respond to my questions and offer me encouragement.

### bionaut — 2014-07-18T13:51:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/caseys-journal.11350/post-134799>

Do you get sleepy at sunset and getup at dawn feeling refreshed? Does your room have any lights or led's and do you have any electrical equipment in your proximity?

A leptin reset will stimulate autophagy (pruning of bad mitochondria). This takes time. I have been on the reset myself for about 6 weeks. Today was the first day I woke up full of energy. It takes a while but your sleep effieciency and circadian timing needs to improve before exercise and diet will help you. You have come to the right place for help but expect it to be a weight loss over the long haul.

As for the stamina to continue, eating that BAB consistently at the "same time" will reign in your neuropeptides in your brain. This will boost your stamina. You just need to stay the course.

# Thread: Don't let the perfect be the enemy of the good
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 56
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WereBear — 2014-07-18T19:58:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-134812>

That is one of my favorite Voltaire quotes, and it's been a good motto to encourage all kinds of ventures in my life.

Especially something as challenging as an Optimized Life.

Almost two weeks in, and I'm loving the seafood, have bought a can of oysters (will try that this weekend) and am drinking lots of cold water. I need to move up the pyramid and incorporate more crab, shrimp, and oysters. Found a local source for offal, so will explore that next week.

I ordered an EMF blocker for the bottom of my laptop. Just being cognizant of how much EMF there is around me has been very useful, I hope. I have my iPod touch in Airplane mode almost all the time now, got some sunglasses readers for reading in bed, and keep my cell phone far away if I'm not using it. This morning I had to make a customer service call where I was on hold, and I put it on speaker for the duration. Will be experimenting with better ways of handling it, since proximity is such an issue. I have hands free in the car, which is great, and I don't make a habit of talking while I'm driving, either. Parked is another matter

Now my lunch hour is taken in a nearby park, where I found a bench that has no wifi signals reaching it. I take off my shoes and have my feet in the grass the whole time, sitting in the sun.

After researching the earthing mats on the forum, I still went ahead and got a couple, we have thirty days to see if anything good happens, since my husband has a chronic illness and it's difficult for him to get down the three flights of stairs to get outdoors. We are trying to make that situation better, too, but in the meantime, it might help. And I'll try one at my desk at work.

We have winters that get down to -40. Won't be sitting in the park then, I'm sure.

The earthing mats I ordered have arrived, but I've done a lot more research in the interim... and from reactions here, seems like NOT a good idea at this point. I'm able to return them, and I'm flipped into pessimism about them.

On the other hand I've had oysters for dinner.

I need a recipe.

### WereBear — 2014-07-29T03:07:58+0300

My Schumann resonance generator arrived last Friday, and while I can't exactly _afford_ it, it fits in my budget far better than the magnetic mattress pads. I know the effects are subtle, but I basically added an hour to my sleep time since it arrived, and I feel muscles relaxing that really needed it. We'll see how things develop, but this is a move I feel very good about.

I discovered that making my oyster into Angels on Horseback (wrapped in bacon) works very well. Yum! Also have some very nice German prosciutto that I can wrap around oysters, too. I thought I'd be missing dairy, but I don't. Also found a recipe for making my own mayo with olive/hemp oil, and I feel well enough to implement that move. I think this will be a fine upgrade.

### WereBear — 2015-05-06T04:07:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-163417>

Looking back, I've been undercutting my sleep for _decades_:

-   my teens were high stress -- used to stay up late just to get quiet time for myself, then drag myself out of bed early for ungodly early high school
-   20's I spent commuting -- once again, up too early and went to bed too late in order to have my own time
-   30's I was coding and we all know the vampire hours that seems to encourage
-   40's I had another period of very high stress, college and work, and late nights studying

So the good sleep I've getting now is just amazing. It's like an exquisite meal or a wonderful movie. I wake up in the morning feeling rejuvenated!

I have four decades to catch up on

### WereBear — 2015-06-20T19:15:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-169055>

Had a very trying month -- weekend away for work, two long lotsa-driving day trips for work, lots of evening events I needed to attend, and a surprise in-laws visit all used my reserve tank, and I'm back to being tired again. However, I am bouncing back faster than I hoped, so it's just a case of Doing Too Much.

I've let some things slide, too, partly because I was exhausted, and partly because I wasn't sure they were working for me. Now I know I must keep up with:

-   Lipo-C
-   Skipping dinner unless I'm really hungry -- if the feeling passes, it can be ignored
-   Zero balancing (cancelled appointment because of time pressure -- should have kept it)
-   More grounding -- we've had a lot of rain

### Peertje80 — 2015-06-20T20:56:40+0300

I hope you get your energy back soon. Driving a lot is so draining for energy.

May I ask a question about the passing of hunger feeling? When do you know it is passing? I mean when do you decide to eat anyway in the evening?

### WereBear — 2015-06-20T22:07:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-169075>

> [Peertje80 said:](/goto/post?id=169064)
>
> -   May I ask a question about the passing of hunger feeling? When do you know it is passing? I mean when do you decide to eat anyway in the evening?

My bedtime is at 10pm so I need to eat by six at the latest since Dr. Kruse recommends a space of 4-5 hours before sleep. If I'm hungry then, and can't be distracted from it by a fascinating book or interesting project, then I know it will just get worse, so I eat then.

But hunger that comes to be later than six, I can take some niacin and vitamin c, and it goes away. Like my body was saying it just needed nutrients... not food

### Peertje80 — 2015-06-20T23:27:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-169086>

Ok this is interesting. Sometimes I feel at dinner time a bit hungry but at the same time feel I could do without eating. I just feel doubt to skip a meal with resulting to eat later at night because that really trashes my sleep. Does niacin and vit C curb that edge of hunger or is it just something you do?

It is indeed interesting to notice that if you keep you mind occupied hunger will pass. So I guess it is a sense of boredom that triggers eating in the end somehow.

### WereBear — 2015-06-21T17:50:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-169168>

I had an eating disorder as a highly stressed teen, and part of my (self-engineered) recovery was to figure out when I was _really_ hungry. If we are not used to sorting out our body signals, so many things can get mis-interpreted as hunger. Some strategies:

-   Could be thirst. Drink a big glass of water.
-   Could be mineral deficiency. I used chromium picolinate for months, still take chelated magnesium, and use sea salt on my meat and spirulina in my morning smoothie.
-   Could be boredom. Do something fun!
-   Could be lack of pregnenolone (this has been a wonder supplement for me.)

I felt well enough to volunteer for a weekend away for work, and it was probably too much. I also went off my usual routines and found out just how much they do for me. So I wound up, at 2AM on the second night, gripped in an awful panic attack situation, with my mind conjuring up ridiculous scenarios of altered reality to explain why I felt like the next-to-last-teen in a slasher movie. Then I realized I had skipped my pregnenolone for the last two days! 75mg under the tongue, and five minutes later I felt the relief coming at me. Ten minutes later I was asleep! From a state where I was ready to rip out my own organs to _make it stop_.

Niacin and vitamin c are things I take all day, anyway. Dr. Abram Hoffer used high dose niacin for his PTSD patients. While not formally diagnosed, I think I qualify on the chronic stress front, and it does wonders for sleep!

All these strategies helped me realize, as dinnertime approached, that I wasn't really hungry, and I was turning into a dinner skipper, just like Dr. Kruse said I should if I work the protocols correctly. For me, it's having the Big Ass Breakfast, and a late Big Ass Lunch.

### WereBear — 2015-11-04T15:19:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-181136>

Okay, I'm back. Shingles is a hell of a detour.

Don't know what I did wrong, or if it was simply the YEARS of struggling that caught up with me under extra pressure. Our little business had gone LLC, and there was tons of paperwork and delays and then I was hit by a trooper car going way over the speed limit and more stress and money and constant dealing with the fallout like wearing sandspur socks.

The shingles derailed me for weeks, though I only took a week off when it first happened. Amazing thing: I was given gabapentin for the nerve pain, which is lingering. (Of course, of _course_.) But it seems to be "connecting some wiring" for me. A stubborn shoulder problem that occurred when I fixed my posture is responding to my movements again. Partner said the look in my eyes that says ME is back.

I seem to be handling the medication well and that is such a good thing because without it, the neuralgia comes back. So I'm on it for a while yet.

It was helpful to getting me back to 80% which is the best I have achieved, but then I found USP progesterone. Which is another story.

### WereBear — 2015-11-24T02:54:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-182555>

I absolutely adore the progesterone cream. My sleep, my stamina, my stress handling... all have improved.

I was under the erroneous impression (repeated all over the web) that to get bio-identical hormones for menopause issues I had to see a doctor who prescribed such and I also needed a compounding pharmacy. Not so!

It can be ordered over the Internet. I'm greasing up like a Channel swimmer.

### caroline — 2015-11-29T09:48:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-182821>
