### WereBear — 2015-11-29T18:59:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-182838>

I have no local sources, the chain drugstores don't seem to carry it, though I understand GNC does. These chain drugstores carry wild yam tablets and such, and I always avoided them because they messed up my stomach so badly.

I started with ProGest, from Amazon, and then moved to ProgesterAll, from Dr. John Lee's site (he has passed on, but was the first doctor to recognize estrogen dominance.) The ProgesterAll has a much more silky feel and gets absorbed faster, and is appropriate for my face.

I have also been reading the ProgesteroneTherapy.com website, which advises a "loading" technique for faster results, where one uses lots at first. Which is where I'm at right now. She sells NatPro, which is more concentrated, and as soon as her warehouse is re-stocked, I'll try that, too.

I use a blob the size of two large grapes, twice a day, and more when it's stressful. And it seems to work very well!

It has done incredible things for my sleep. That wonderful tingly feeling I got from the Leptin Reset when I first woke up? Now it is intensified and it lasts for hours.

If I have a terrible bunch of frustrations to deal with, I add more cream and feel much better. I was always a cool head in a crisis, handling stress with belated anxiety perhaps, but totally able to cope. The hormone storms that messed me up so badly introduced me to the concept of "GET ME AN AXE" which was quite foreign to my personality.

With the cream, I'm back to easily managing low carb meals and can easily skip dinner. Maybe I can get rid of the 20 extra torso pounds that had refused to budge no matter what I did.

This might be the missing element I needed. I've had a severe case of endometriosis since puberty (sign of estrogen dominance, so I read) and so it's possible I never really had enough progesterone in the first place.

### WereBear — 2015-12-25T23:27:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-184954>

I just love the effects of progesterone. Dagnab! I'm two and a half months into using it.

I've eliminated my use of anti-inflammatories for sleep. I was using white willow bark & devil's claw, then I went to niacin, but since progesterone is supposed to suppress the inflammatory effects of estrogen, I dropped these supplements altogether.

The sleep I've been getting is now flatout incredible. On vacation this week, I'm racking up 10-11 hours a night. Of course I have a deficit. It is also of amazing quality, and I wake up with a "healing glow" that helps me feel so rejuvenated.

I think this might be my missing piece that will get me back to 100% of what I can be. This is also my 1 & 1/2 year anniversary of working on my Optimization, and I have made fantastic progress. I do think the progesterone is mopping up the last of the inflammation problems that had wreaked such havoc on my system.

It is especially welcome because I am working over one job when the organization pulled in a bigger territory and I have been juggling two, along with extra work and the stress. So if things calm down I might do even better.

### WereBear — 2016-04-08T15:51:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-192347>

Well, things haven't calmed down that much. Why do I always think they will? I guess hope is that thing without feathers.

However, this progesterone immersion seems to have paid off. I found a great company, Ora's Naturals, and I get 10%. This is a financial boon and I have also cut down on my dose, because I'm actually feeling better, sleeping great, and seeing how I do with a small maintenance dose.

Started drinking apple cider vinegar diluted in water with all my meals, for the "natural"Metformin effect."

I'm also trying Maca, which has all kinds of confusing information about it out there. But I'm convinced a lot of the confusion out there regarding Adrenal Fatigue is that their true problem is Cortisol Resistance, which was the key to my getting better, and starting the Kruse Protocols. Nothing is going to get better until cortisol actually gets into the cells.

Maca is variously described as a stimulant, a "toner," and an adaptogen. So I'll see how it works for me.

Financial crunch means I'm weeding out my supplements and focusing on ones with a clear benefit I can _feel_.

Doing more walks as the days have been getting longer, and getting my morning light on my face as I walk to work. Sardine and pickle lunches! Cheap and go together wonderfully.

### WereBear — 2016-08-18T16:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-199767>

I have been optimizing as best I can, and I improve, and then I hit a wall. I have a few key supplements that are doing wonders, but I have hit a wall. I have great sleep now, but that also has hit a wall in how much it lets me recover. I even went to therapy, cleaned up some sources of stress, and then I hit a wall again.

Recently, I learned the _name_ of the wall. It is undiagnosed **Asperger's Syndrome**.

When the flow of strangers through my office receded, so did my condition. When the demands of my job pushed me out among lots of people and noise and activity, upping my sensory input, so did my condition worsen. Asperger's was the missing planet that influences the orbits of the entire solar system of my health.

I am in the process of getting a formal diagnosis, which is itself a source of stress. Because I live in a remote area, I had to drive four hours there, recover in a motel room, take the all day assessment tests, recover in a motel room, and then drive back the third day through a driving rainstorm at seventy miles an hour, which wound up taking six hours.

By the next weekend, I was so drained that simply trying to go out to breakfast (I am so good at my job that our first two choices were packed ) I had what is called a **shutdown**, where my brain _simply stopped working_. I was too dizzy to stand up, everything was too loud and bright, it wasn't low blood sugar (though it felt like it) because eating some fruit didn't help, and I had to go home and lie on my bed under a pile of quilts with a pillow over my head for three hours to recover.

Not eager to trip _that_ particular set of events again, I can tell you. Home sensory deprivation chambers are very expensive  and my makeshift one is kind of cumbersome

Personally, I have no problem being an Aspie. I'm a writer and explainer and I had no idea social skills, which came to me in high school, were not the instincts of the Neurotypical, but instead a series of calculations and instructions (much like running a computer program, which is what I used to write) that took up brainpower. I figured it out so well no one could tell, but I also painted myself into a corner at getting so good at social my job pushed me into _more and more_ social, which eventually overwhelmed me.

It is rather common for High Functioning Aspies to not know until midlife, since the diagnostic criteria didn't get out into medical science until 1994, when a lot of us had already passed through the educational system. By this time, we have a enormous number of coping strategies, but this takes a lot of bandwidth. Any ebbing of energy (in my case, the Menopause from Hell,) and this elaborate structure starts to crumble. I need to shore it back up again. Getting a diagnosis means my insurance _should_ pay for a weighted blanket, targeted Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, and I'm also trying to get some massage therapy prescribed for me. I must talk to my boss about ajusting what is expected of me. All of these should help a lot, now that I know.

Most of all; _it explains so much_.

### Debntx — 2017-03-22T16:13:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-210884>

hi, just wanted to chime in on the weighted blanket thing - which i didn't even know existed.
I found out through my youngest child (now 20) who was diagnosed autistic later in life - anyway, i bought one for myself recently - less than 2 weeks ago - immediately made a difference that couldn't be explained any other way- wow, i sleep much better - it's weird how something so simple made a huge difference for me. i didn't study why - maybe later

thank you for validating my thinking - it's not just my imagination - i can trust my experience but i like hearing it from others as well - in regards to weighted blanket.

i just want to add about my office situation - i work in a call center and during the first part of day - people all around me in my small group- talking talking talking very distracting for me - then they leave (they're great people by the way) - but i feel so much better... feels/sounds like cacaphony... wildly overwhelming - then they leave - much better -  strangely i do put on a fan for the noise - but people sounds nah uh (i actually do quite fine)
because my job is on the phone all day talking to people delivering news they may not like (delays in service) - that takes a lot of mental energy - harder to be available for my coworkers too for chitchat but i am trying to be better at it - makes the day go better when i can work it in

i potentially could be in that category not diagnosed but "realizing" later in life- don't care if insurance pays for anything for me - the less information i give them or use the better i am - just the way i see it

at work they see me as "different" - made worse by my new "habits" (remove shoes when outside - get more sun - eat outside - wear long sleeves especially for inside, mostly small things etc...) - - but they probably blame it on age

good luck with everything - i know it's a challenge - it was nice to read about your experiences some of which I can really relate to -

Did you notice a difference using the weighted blanket? quality of sleep?

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-24T13:50:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-let-the-perfect-be-the-enemy-of-the-good.11355/post-210994>

> [Debntx said:](/goto/post?id=210884)
>
> hi, just wanted to chime in on the weighted blanket thing - which i didn't even know existed.
> I found out through my youngest child (now 20) who was diagnosed autistic later in life - anyway, i bought one for myself recently - less than 2 weeks ago - immediately made a difference that couldn't be explained any other way- wow, i sleep much better - it's weird how something so simple made a huge difference for me. i didn't study why - maybe later
>
> thank you for validating my thinking - it's not just my imagination - i can trust my experience but i like hearing it from others as well - in regards to weighted blanket.

I've been fascinated by the senses since DS2 started developing sensory problems at about 2. I've learned from Jack to relate it now to the mitochondria, the hydrogen bonding network, light water and magnetism. I think there's a lot more senses than those we typically think of - including the sense of where we are in time and space, and gravitation sensing. Then I think a sense can by "typical" or hyper or hyposensitive. So for example one person may need a stronger stimulus like a bear hug, another would find that painful. So a weighted blanket could be working on one or several hyper or hyposensitive senses. For example, one possibility is that it could subconsciously make someone feel safer, better grounded, more aware of where they are in time and space, when the visual sense light stimulus relied on in the day is missing. Feeling safer = better sleep.

# Thread: Kristen's Lyme journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristens-lyme-journal.11364/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ksilva — 2014-07-19T22:37:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristens-lyme-journal.11364/post-134935>

Hello anyone reading...

I am new here, so I don't know what I'm doing. I am in college theoretically, had to drop out because of health problems for two years now. It is a long story....but I hope you will bear with me...

I had been on antidepressants for about eight years, stopped them after I thought medical marijuana could help instead, went through withdrawal and was hospitalized and incorrectly diagnosed bipolar. I was put on 73957 medications, gained a lot of weight very quickly, came home a month later and stopped all of them. I tried losing the weight by cutting out all carbs, and quickly lost about fifteen pounds before it came to a sharp halt, and I couldn't lose anything no matter the calorie level, for months. I fell into an eating disorder and depression. I had not slept more than three solid hours for several months by now because my body was a mess as you can imagine. The very-low carb diet seemed to be a final drop kick to my adrenals, as my anxiety and insomnia kicked up several notches, along with a new case of jitters.

I was put on a low dose of mood stabilizer that didn't do much, and a benzo for sleep...which was a humongous mistake. I lost the rest of the weight plus more, and lots of muscle...but had to go to rehab and gain back some weight. Now we are in June 2013, whereas all this started in June 2012. June is not a good month for me it seems. They also took me off two more medications while I was in rehab, so I was in withdrawal the whole time. I came out of rehab and stuck to my plan, but my weight kept creeping up. I tried exercising and it went up faster. I stopped exercising, it still went up. I started exercising again after it seemed stable for a month, and gained six pounds in five days from just walking a mile a day. Of course no one believes me when I say this, I do not blame them necessarily but it hurts all the same...I still cannot walk much without putting on weight...I will get to this in a minute.

I saw a nutritionist every week. I kept to the meal plan, even tried eating more to see if that'd help. No. I tried eating less, and my metabolism decided maintaining my body fat on less food would be just fine (in other words, it just slowed down). I cut out gluten. Then I cut out sugar, real and fake. Then I cut out most fruit (except a handful of berries a couple times a day). My stomach was always bloated, I couldn't and still can't hold down vegetables. I had long since cut out soy. I finally cut out dairy a few months ago. Nothing even made a dint. By now I had gained almost 40 lbs... 30 lbs over what I've weighed most of my life...but everyone said it would swing back.

This whole time I had been shuffled from psychiatrist to psychiatrist, because doctors told me I was fine. I refused all meds, I knew there was something wrong, I still know, but no one believed me... Once you get into the system of psychiatry people tend to do that. In the meantime I had been trying to taper off the benzo, and this, I believe, is what put the nail in the coffin of my long lost health. I developed Paresthesia, my insomnia got worse again, my mood sank, and my weight was shooting up even more...and now I had muscle and nerve pain along with it. My body also developed a bad sweating habit and body odor. But everyone insisted all my symptoms boiled down to my stressing out. What a nice catch-22.

Finally I got an appointment with a PCOS doctor. She took tests and diagnosed me with PCOS in April this year. She told me my only symptom was acne, and refused to work with me, telling me yet again to eat less, and that my symptoms would get better if I lost weight. My depression and morale was (still is) somewhere underneath the sewer lines by now. Low carb wasn't helping...there must have been something else wrong... My friend told me to get tested for Lyme.

I received my diagnosis of Lyme disease two months later, in June this year.

I have been on antibiotics for a couple weeks now and my weight is still rising. I have now gained 55 pounds in one year. I cannot walk, due to severe pain. I also cannot exert myself much without gaining weight, and I have narrowed this down to the high level of cytokines, from both withdrawal and Lyme disease, circulating in my body and being stirred up by movement, causing fat cells to expand and "catch" the toxins. I have also read Jack's post on Lyme and leptin resistance, and this somewhat confirms my theory. I imagine I sound like a lunatic for saying all of this, but at this point no one else knows what is wrong, and not many people seem to want to bother listening, so I am left to my own resources.

I really, really hope someone can help me on this website. I can only sit at home and wait to get better, but the pattern has been such that I only get worse and so I do not see a life ahead of me anymore. I'm sorry to end on a negative note. Thank you for reading.

### Ksilva — 2014-07-19T23:21:35+0300

Lamictal, 50 mg
Wellbutrin XL, 150 mg
Trazodone, 25 mg
Ativan, 1.25 mg

Other:
Cefuroxime, 1000mg, for Lyme

Supplements:

Prebiotic
Probiotic
B-12 methylcobalamin
Digestive enzymes - can only take in morning before breakfast, otherwise stomach ache all day
Berberine
5-MTHF - not a mutation but have trouble processing folate
Inositol - for PCOS, insulin resistance
Alpha Lipoic Acid - insulin resistance
Cod Liver Oil
Omega-3 fish oil with chromium
D3
DIM - for high estrogen
Magnesium - CALM powder and transdermal oil
Vitamin C powder
Melatonin, Sleep Tonight, Doxylamine - all for sleep
Progesterone cream/capsules - for low progesterone
Green powder

### caroline — 2014-07-20T05:41:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristens-lyme-journal.11364/post-134966>

I am so glad you have found your way here. Why don't we work a little on your circadian cycles first....

This is what your day must look like ...

Upon rising .... Go outside and look at the sun

BAB within thirty minutes of rising .... Leave at least 4 hours between meals and before bed ...And absolutely no snacking.

Drink bottled spring water

Get outside as much as possible - bare feet and little clothes

What is your non native EMF and blue light exposure?

No fake lights after sunset

Get all electronics out of where you sleep and turn off the breaker if you can. Make bedroom as dark as possible.

You have to reset the Rolex in your brain .....

Don't underestimate any of the above ... Commit 100%

You need to eat a ton of seafood .... Oysters, oysters, oysters!

### prAna303 — 2014-07-20T11:25:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristens-lyme-journal.11364/post-134980>

Welcome...

> I came out of rehab and stuck to my plan, but my weight kept creeping up. I tried exercising and it went up faster. I stopped exercising, it still went up. I started exercising again after it seemed stable for a month, and gained six pounds in five days from just walking a mile a day. Of course no one believes me when I say this, I do not blame them necessarily but it hurts all the same...I still cannot walk much without putting on weight...

You are loosing energy to the world around you. Something is big on stealing from you and that is perhaps why you gain weight when doing some motion based activity. We gain weight when we are in a bad energy state, often based on the thought that we should eat less and move more. If you gain fast, search where the leakage is.

Circadian is a big one but i think what most get messed up is the modern world around us. If you fix your environment the cycles comes to you natural.

Big questions:

\- Where do you live?
\- How do you live?
\- How does your day look like?
\- What is your relationship to nature?

### prAna303 — 2014-07-20T21:43:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristens-lyme-journal.11364/post-134999>

> What do you mean losing energy. What leakage? I don't mean to sound skeptical but this sounds kind of out there. Who else here has experienced a leakage that caused them to gain weight endlessly, perhaps I can talk to them and figure it out?

Think of it like this, also Jack said it a couple of times...

You have a bucket in which you try to grow a plant... If the bucket has holes you will work the hell out of yourself to fill it with energy, it drains and growth can not be made. Fix the holes and you can start to harvest.

It is all about energy... If you loose energy you will gain mass, universal law and not proven false yet. You loose energy in this modern world because we are living in a constant microwave oven, exposed to things you where not designed for.

In this world today we all loose energy to the world around us, not a single living thing is safe. This is what Jacks methods will offset...

Life is based on three main things, this is not Jacks idea alone but proven by Nasa and others... Simply put: Sun, water and grounding.

Dear Silva.
You need to go outside in the nature. Barefoot, with as little clothes as possible. Get air and sun on your skin. Swim in natural rivers/ocean/lakes, drink plenty of natural water.
Eat lots of wild seafood. Rest. Sleep in the night, have it as cool as possible. Avoid technology and fake light.
You will heal, just give it time!

You can read about it all - the theory **why** \- on Jacks blog. I have read every single post. But it will take you a long time. Until you have gone through it, start doing what we tell you - we have all done it and do every day - you can too! It is very easy. Instead of putting your money on supplements, put them into oysters and seafood. Eat them raw.
Start with small steps if it sounds too heavy and too much of a change to do it all in one. Do step by steps. As much as you can. Just do not forget, the more you do the faster you will heal. Still, you will need lost of patience, healing does not happen in one day.... but you will not regret it, I promise you!

# Thread: Anneh's Journey to Optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/annehs-journey-to-optimal.11512/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Anneh — 2014-08-15T20:45:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/annehs-journey-to-optimal.11512/post-137957>

Hello, my name is Anneh (pronounced Anne). I am a 42 year old woman. I live in British Columbia, Canada. I am a new member and very happy to be here.

I have had chronic daily headache/migraine for the last 9 and a half years. The pain is constant — I have not had one pain free moment for 9.5 years. The pain is also quite high on a daily basis.

I have run the gamut of both conventional and alternative medicine - the drugs, techniques, supplement, and interventions are too long to list here - but nothing has helped, reduced, or eliminated the pain.

The only thing that helps the pain is ice packs wrapped around my head. I cried when I read the blog entry that explained why cold works on head pain. I feel that for the first time since I got sick, finally there is a person, Dr. Kruse, who actually understands what is “wrong” with me.

The migraines began in university in my early 20s, both episodic, and for short (month long) daily/chronic periods. These migraines were mainly episodic until about 12 years ago (age 30), when they began to become chronic, with some intermittent relief, until finally, 9.5 years ago, the migraine settled in and hasn’t left since.

Other symptoms that have accompanied the migraines include “brain fog”, inability to concentrate, increased pain from concentration/thinking/reading. My memory - both short and long term - is absolutely shot. I “lose” words when speaking, and need to stop and “find” them. When there is too much stimulation (noise, lights, voices, people, objects), my head spins and I can’t keep track of everything that’s happening. I am hyper-sensitive to, and triggered by, lights (esp. fluorescent, and sadly also the sun), smells, noise, weather changes, stress, some foods, heat, and exertion (I simply cannot do any exercise other than short walks, and can’t do even the slightest cardio, without exacerbating the headache/migraine).

In addition the the headaches/migraines, I have constant exhaustion. I am not sure if this is “Chronic Fatigue” per se, or simply a part of the migraine condition (and after reading this blog I’m not sure that it really matters in terms of healing). It doesn’t matter how much I sleep, I wake up exhausted. And it seems I only have a certain amount of energy per day, which is “spent” quite quickly, leaving me even more exhausted, and in more pain, and “out of it” by late afternoon every day. The more I try to do in a day the more exhausted I am. Even very simple things take it out of me. If I push myself and do too much, I can end up on the couch for several days to recover.

I also have chronic long-term depression and anxiety. My first depressive episode occurred at age 25, followed about 2 years later by severe anxiety. Several times in the last 15 years I have been suicidal - not because of my life, but simply because of the depression/anxiety. Luckily my mood is being well managed these days by an antidepressant (Cipralex). (I’m not a fan of pharmaceuticals, but this drug has saved my life given how bad the depression/anxiety was.)

I have been diagnosed with PCOS. I had absent/irregular periods immediately upon beginning menstruation, and was first diagnosed at age 17. I took the birth control pill for about 10 years, until about age 29. I currently take Prometrium, which seems to help, (but managing the dosage is like throwing a dart in the dark).

At age 39, I began to experience hot flashes and extreme night sweats. The hot flashes are less now, but the night sweats are still with me. I literally drench my pyjamas and sheets, and need to get up in the middle of the night and change it all, sometimes needing 3 or 4 changes a night.

About the same time the hot flashes and night sweats started 3 years ago, I also developed insomnia. I would wake up at about 4 a.m. and not be able to get back to sleep for about 3 hours. I tried to deal with it without pharmaceuticals for about a year, but it finally drove me crazy. I have been taking 10 m.g. of Amitryptaline before bed since then, and it keeps me asleep through this time period. If I don’t take it, I wake up like clockwork.

I also have chronic adult acne. I treated it in my mid/late 20s with Accutane. (I did not understand at that time the costs to my health of making that choice). I have also treated it with laser therapy. It persists to this day.

Two years ago I was diagnosed with cervical dysplasia and underwent a LEEP procedure (which follow-ups show was successful).

I have low thyroid, both T4 and T3, right at the bottom and just below “normal”, but my doctor does not suggest treatment. I did have another doctor who treated me with supplementation for a short time, which did raise my T4, but after stopping this medication (as I was not tolerating it due to nausea) my numbers have been steadily dropping.

I am freezing cold all the time, especially my feet, but also my core, and in the winter I just can’t get warm. I am not exaggerating — I am cold beyond cold (but don’t have Reynauds specific symptoms).

Every time I increase my water intake to increase hydration, it seems that all I do is pee it out. Really, the amount I have to urinate makes it very hard to hydrate.

I would not have said I have an Autoimmune disorder (unless migraines are “autoimmune”, which none of my conventional doctors classify them as). However, in the last two weeks I have developed pain in my hand and fingers. Today I will go for an X-ray of my hand and a blood test for rheumatoid factor. From what I can glean, my hand pain is presenting very much like RA.

I have learned that I am homozygous for the MTHFR C677T mutation.

I have cravings for sugar that dominate my thinking and my life. It is clear to me that I have been a “sugar addict”, truly, all of my life. I have done regular paleo, or Atkins, sometimes successfully for months or even 1-2 years at a time. But always I come back to the sugar. I feel powerless over it. I gained 55 pounds eating this way, and over the last two years slowly lost that weight by eating correctly. But now the sugar demon is back and I am struggling. I have started to eat epi-paleo about 3 weeks ago, and am following the prescription completely. However, I am still overwhelmed by, and give into, sugar binges (which I know is not part of the prescription). This is devastating to me.

Because of the migraines and associated neck pain/problems, I have undergone several CTs, MRIs, and X-rays.

I have had multiple head injuries (4) from a variety of causes throughout my life.

When I was a child, up until the age of about 10, I had “fits”. I would “drop” to the ground without much warning. When this happened, it felt like blacking out to me. I never got a diagnosis of epilepsy, but from the research I’ve done it sounds like it could have been. These “fits” often also presented as a kind of dissociation, in which I would not drop, but would be “freaking out”, awake and speaking, but I would not remember afterwards what I had said or done or how I ended up where I was. Often the episode would end with me vomiting, and then “coming to”. The last time this happened I was 18 years old.

Also as a young child I had “temper tantrums”. I was a behavioural problem in the classroom, although highly intelligent and successful academically. I’m very glad there were no drugs to put kids on in those days, or I would have been medicated for sure. I slowly grew out of this behaviour.

As a child I had constant nightmares. These decreased somewhat in frequency as I became an adult, but have stayed with me. It is part of why sleep is troubling for me, because the nightmares are very distressing, and it seems to me that they are possibly tiring as well.

As a teenager I had several episodes of extreme nose bleeds with no obvious cause.

Also as a young teen I often felt faint, especially if I was upside down. I passed out in the middle of a gymnastics vault, in the air, and was prohibited from continuing with gymnastics. My migraines are immediately exacerbated if a bend over and have my head below my waist.

I hope that this is sufficient to give context into my state of health. I will post what labs I have in my next entry.

I am truly grateful to be here with all of you, finally empowered to save my own life!

Blessings,
Anneh

# Thread: Journal Update - incl BP coffee, metamatrix, tim jackson..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-update-incl-bp-coffee-metamatrix-tim-jackson.11791/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-09-28T13:56:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-update-incl-bp-coffee-metamatrix-tim-jackson.11791/post-142081>

Hi hi all,

Its been ages i know! What a journey this life of mine is! Heres an update:

1) WEIGHT LOSS! i put it all down to bulletproof coffee! Well, it certainly helped going from BAB to BPC.. i do still add protein to my coffee / consume some protein, but BPC has also been a life changer, because, i can have coffee!! I have found, im better not trying to push through to 2pm either. some days it happens, but its not a conscious effort. I think thats adrenal trash thats put paid to that. If i ever feel out of sorts ie travel etc, then i SMASH bpc - load up on butter, have maybe two in a morning.. it sorts me out so quickly

2) TRAVEL - iv just come back from Japan. I was there for a weeks work (bike race). We went to the Tour of Hokkaido. Its an 8 hour flight to get to. Pretty shit sleep over there, but WOW! the hotel buffets were amazing - every single meal. So i was loading up on raw fish, miso, seaweed, cooked fish, duck.. and really nice deep baths in the hotel rooms, with massive ice dispensing machines on certain floors, hello ice baths! and daily earthing / looking at the sun.. but something was amiss.. i was experimenting with a pharma sleeper, but taking like a quarter of a tab (im trying to hard to get off melatonin) and it proved to me again, how much i cannot handle pharmas.. anyway, i was uber drowsy by day, so was it that that made me a bit ratty? which i havent had for a long time.. plus my feet got a bit puffy on the flights. Flying, especially over nite, throws me out. its pretty much a pick at an airplane meal and thats me, done, toasted  but the time difference was only one hour... ticket to wellness, right? hmmm

3) came back to aus from japan, straight to another race... wow, tired. definitely felt a bit flat that week. did all i can (earthing, high fat etc) finished that race, had four days off and was pre menstrual - wow, i was like a lactating hippo, i thought id never stop eating :-o i cant remember ever being that bad! dr TJ reckons the circadian disruption can do that... hmm i thought id nailed that.. anyway, got a period and back to not needing much food / obsessing about food.. lol

4) FISH OIL - well, i decided to self medicate with fish oil. i decided im not getting enough seafood, so il supplement. First time in MONTHS that i stopped getting the debilitating hormonal headaches that can last up to ten days post period.. that was a BREAK THROUGH! they were so bad not even my super strong migraine suppositories could move them - and thats saying something... iv noticed this month, some headache-ines coming into the picture.. hmm interesting to note, considering hte shocking build up and feeling so out of whack..

5) DR Tim.. well, i had another consult this week, then we found out he didnt have much info from metamatrix, cos htey hadnt sent him the full results. thats a very expensive mishap for me, but ... i cant change it now. I am happy to be working with him though.. im now using a curcumin skin cream to help lower inflammation (im going to start adding a bit to my BPC i think), and starting on homeopathic treatment for parasites / viruses. he also wants me to get other viruses tested for - i do think he doesnt realise that he needs to be specific! I could have had all the tests he wants me to have, in thailand. and im back in australia now.. grrr im really reluctant to fork out $60 to some GP that will tell me to go home, exercise more and eat less - as past history has shown.. anyway. il also do another hormone panel an ASI.

6) EXERCISE - well, im now ABLE to go out walking! even up hill! i used to have crippling lower back pain... was it the extra weight? (oh, so iv lost around 6kg i think? ) i enjoy walking, some days i try a more of a HITT style work out (but i do feel uncomfy still trying to 'sprint', better if i have a hill i can do that up or a stationary bike, but even to get out and walk, i love it! yoga, im doing daily again, and qi kung. i like to do my qi kung on wet grass !  id love to be weight training again one day, but im still also aware that im not 100%...

7) BLUE LIGHT: well, one aspect of my job thats awesome is i spend a lot of time outside! so i always try to make the most of barefoot when i can. and expose my eyes to daylight. Iv also made a conscious effort ot use my blue blocking shades lately and im sure they actually make a pronounced difference! I was having a shocking time with sleep pre japan, it seems to come in waves, which is why i think its possibly virally / parasitic related..??! as opposed to just cortisol..

8) THAILAND - well, i was in thailand for june and july.. for the last month or so there, i was very depressed, sad, crying, helpless, hopeless... it was awful! i was eating a lot of oysters, doing BPC, coconut oil etc. .all that good stuff.. but i was also staying in a room that had around 12 - 18 wifi signals. this rang alarm bells and has for sure, put me off going back to that same place. i also wonder about the powerlines - in asia they tend to be very low, with loads and loads of wires.. the difference in me was hideous! then i flew back to australia, pretty much straight to another race, and i INSTANTLY felt better. :-o (wow, JK really knows what hes talking about LOL!! no, seriously, its quite astounding to read his posts and then have it hit you square on... )

well, i m sure il think of sommit else, but thats where im at for now! I have another trip for work, to china, but its for two weeks. so that will hopefully be ok, but it could have a 3 week hong kong prelude. Which would be a holiday. Im just a bit nervous now that il have a shocker of a time there.. eek. keep on ticking along.. sometimes i still get frustrated but, aside from the last two weeks, i dont feel like an elephant, and thast a really nice feeling!

### caroline — 2014-09-29T00:48:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-update-incl-bp-coffee-metamatrix-tim-jackson.11791/post-142101>

# Thread: Lawrence Back on Track and beyond!

### lawrencep93 — 2014-10-06T10:20:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lawrence-back-on-track-and-beyond.11845/post-142965>

So I initially lost over 30kg in 5 months going low carb high fat. 110kg to 77kg.

I then put on weight due to a few reasons:
Ate carbs
Stress
"Bulking"
Friend died

So now I need to regain my health back and get healthy!

Today was Bio-Impedance body comp analysis
\====================================
Total Weight: 92.4kg
BF% 29.2%
Muscle Mass: 62.2kg
Bone mass: 3.2kg

### lawrencep93 — 2014-10-14T11:43:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lawrence-back-on-track-and-beyond.11845/post-143785>

Today did a bit of CT went for a walk in about 55 degree weather which increased to about 70 degrees at the end, I was just in a single and shorts. Was easy after drinking my bulletproof coffee and wearing gloves for my hands, made sure my body got cold air on it.
Plugging away at Jacks book, might switch to eggs and bacon in the morning to get protein in the morning.
Decided to try and eat before or at sunset then go in my room and turn my LED lights to red, and F.LUX on my PC to dim the blue light.

Changing my fish oil which is currently 250DHA/500EPA to the 500DHA/250EPA one, will take 4g per day of this along with a kelp tablet to help get iodine in.
Will try and get some fish in my diet where possible need to source some local fresh fish I can eat like sushimi safely.
Been trying to get to bed early but the weird part is I have been having naps for 2 hours in the afternoon before dinner, then sleeping for 6-7 hours.

### lawrencep93 — 2015-03-07T02:14:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lawrence-back-on-track-and-beyond.11845/post-157676>

I have not posted here for a bit.

Little update, weight is stable not really changing much.
Was on holidays over xmas NYE lived at sealevel ate fresh fish I caught and stuff, lost a little weight, but it seems all in a flux of +-2kg so hence why I said weight has been stable.

Back home, at work and uni now.

Ate 1kg of fish yesterday 500g salmon and 500g shrimp.

Going to try oysters again this time I will swallow raw oysters whole rather than chewing them.

Getting more on top of sleep and light cycles, remembering to set my lights to deep orange or full blown red when the sun sets.
This morning I ate 3 eggs, 150g bacon, 150g cheese for bfast (40g protein hit), got a 1.5kg grassfed tomahawk steak for dinner tonight, will see what I can eat of it and leave the rest of it for bfast tomorrow morning to get my protein in.

Going to try having bfast when I have time and dinner, some days I have early starts so it's easier for me to just eat when I get home so I have time to cook food fresh.

Also aiming to consume 1kg or more of fish per week, currently eating fish every Friday for lent, and if I don't eat it all Friday I will continue eating what I have the next few days.

Looking into buying glass bottled water but $ is a bit of an issue if I drink 2L per day and want glass bottles from Italy.

# Thread: Self Actualize's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-actualizes-journal.11918/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Self Actualize — 2014-10-16T23:43:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-actualizes-journal.11918/post-144042>

Hello everyone, my name is Dave and I'm 20 years old. I'm Canadian, originally from Winnipeg, Manitoba but I've recently moved to Toronto, Ontario a few months ago(yay, more EMF).

I'm 5'11" and just under 160lbs, I've always been pretty skinny.

So, I'll start with recounting my life and what led me to have the current health issues I can, to the best of my ability.

I'm the youngest of four children and my mother had me when she was 36 years old (I think specifying age matters), she's been a smoker non-stop to varying degrees since she was 13.

When I was young, I was a child who was really hyper and had high amounts of energy.

My dad got sick with brain cancer when I was 5 and eventually passed away when I was 8. I was sad for a little bit, but I couldn't quite grasp the permanence of death at that young of an age. I now think this hampered my development as a man at least to some degree. This may or may not be useful, but I decided to add it in just to give as much insight as possible into my health.

With my dad out of the picture, my mother was left with working full-time and attempting to raise four kids on her own while coping with the loss of her spouse.

Because of all the things on my mom's plate, she didn't have time to cook home meals and a lot of the time they'd be frozen ready made things. She also couldn't say no so I could pretty much eat whatever I wanted in between meals which would usually be sweets or baked goods of some kind, and as much as I wanted.

Fast forward to junior high and highschool. I was always well liked and popular, but I got more and more shy, nervous, anxious, depressed as time went on. Leading me to isolate my self more and more. Most of my time I preferred to play video games and surf the internet. A habit that I'm still trying to break.

Since I was obsessed with the way people thought of me and how I was perceived, I started to care about my nutrition in a way that was motivated by vanity. In grade 10 to the middle of grade 12 I started following a high-carb, low fat diet that included processed grains, dairy, factory meats etc. In attempts to achieve a ripped body. I mostly did cardio with a bit of weights but I wasn't motivated enough to achieve much. I was basically treading water in highschool trying to stay afloat. At the same time, I started developing either psoriasis or eczema all over my body that kept getting worse and worse. Bags under my eyes were always there too and caused me a lot of grief. I used to eat a lot of sandwiches, I'm talking one ever two hours throughout the day with smoked chicken breast deli meat and miracle whip mayonnaise. Gross.

I also started reading up on conspiracy theories etc which made my depression and apathy worse. I would tell these things to my mom which made her want me to go on medication. I refused, as it never felt right to me. I'm glad I made that decision.

One thing led to another and I eventually started seeing a Naturopath. During the last two years, I received an allergy test, a male hormone panel and a blood test for thyroid hormones. I still have the papers for the allergy test and the male hormone panel, but I can't find the thyroid test. I can say that she did diagnose me with borderline hyperthyroidism (I remember reading about the inaccuracy of the tests and how it could be either hypo or hyper and I personally thought it was hypo as I could barely walk up the stairs at my workplace and generally felt like death. It improved a bit when I went on a supplement called Thyrosmart.) She advised that I follow a moderate diet that consisted of lots of mostly green vegetables, lean protein from non read meat, fruits and gluten free grains, with no processed sugar, alcohol, gluten, dairy, caffeine, corn, nightshades. This probably would've been pretty effective but I couldn't stick with it for over a week and would end up binging on baked goods and breads.

My allergy test was too big of a file and I'm not savvy enough with computers to figure out how to shrink it. The jist of it was that everything came back negative except for a moderate reaction to cows milk and whey and a very low reaction to egg yolk. It was performed in January of 2012.

My male hormone panel is attached below.

I've started to eat more seafood, no shellfish yet, I'm trying to eat up all my foods I bought before I found this site.

From reading this site, I get the importance of EMF to our functioning. I'm trying to cut down my use of the computer and am attempting to completely quit video games. I do live with two roommates who don't follow any of these rules so completely cutting it out isn't really possible. Plus, I work until 10:30 at night at a grocery store sometimes and don't get home until 11:30, whilst walking in artificial light from the street lamps and cars all the way home. What are some steps I can take to limit it as best as I can?

In closing, I've been trying to solve all these problems for years but I've been unable to stick to anything for very long due to a compromised mental state.

My problems in a easy to read list
\-brain fog (can't think clearly, focus for long periods and retain information very well)
\-anxiety
\-apathetic depression
\-trouble falling asleep most nights
\-frequent urination
\-small, not well formed and frequent bowel movements
\-skin problems (eczema all over my body and really purple bags under my eyes)

Also, is there anyone who lives in canada or ontario that could point me to a good list of seasonal fruits and vegetables. I've found some by googling but I'm not sure if they're technically the right ranges as there is probably some artificial things being done to extend certain seasons of fruits and vegetables.

I don't have a whole lot of money and resources to sink into a whole bunch of tests. If there's some that are absolutely needed, I'll do what I got to do, as health is the most important thing, but I'd prefer it if I could just spend that money on good quality foods.

I hope this is enough information and I hope you can navigate through my verbal diarrhea. If there's anything I should be doing specific to my case just let me know.

Thanks for reading,

David

### caroline — 2014-10-17T00:35:38+0300

Good morning David from Oz! I was born in Toronto and now live in Australia.

Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi-Paleo EX .... it is a great way to get the first two years of the blog. There is lots of info on labs etc.

Have you listened to Jack's podcasts? there is a new one a couple of days ago that you must hear!

Do you have blue blocker glasses? that is imperative .....wear them when you are walking home in the evening.

You need to avoid fake lite as much as possible!

Dr. K's holy grail ..... the three legged stool ...... water, photo electric effect and magnetism.

so this should be what your day looks like...

upon waking .... go outside and look at the sun ....barefoot in the grass if you can and little clothing

have a BAB .... leaving four hours between meals and before bed..... leptin reset - on home page

you need to reset the Rolex in your brain!

drink R/O water

get as much grounding everyday as you can .... can you walk/swim in lake Ontario

C/T ...there is an easy start guide on the home page

what is your non native EMF exposure? work? home?

Please keep reading and asking questions!

From my research I would estimate nearly everyone has some biotoxin infection - mold, algae, fungus ect.... Allergies, MS, CFS, Fibro, Lyme ect are all mold/biotoxin based diseases.

Currently there are only 5 docs in the US that are certified to treat mold/biotoxin patients. I don't believe there are any docs in Canada qualified to treat biotoxins? Biotoxins such as mold are a huge factor in hidden inflammation. Which leads to a whole bunch of problems. Biotoxins require very specialized medical care with real drugs and treatment. Food, CT ect won't work.

Well, there's Wifi in my apartment that I can't get rid of, my laptop, my roommates stay up watching TV which faces the door to my bedroom, they keep the lights on until right before they go to bed, I have to walk home at 11pm under street lamps and car head lights. Again, I work in a grocery store some nights until 11pm so I'm under artificial lights there too.

I've been pretty sedentary for a while mainly playing video games and surfing the internet so I'll have to figure out some outdoor hobbies. Winter's coming as well so it'll be even easier for me to stay inside when I shouldn't.

# Thread: Erich's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erichs-journal.12044/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### erich53 — 2014-11-01T20:54:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erichs-journal.12044/post-145880>

Hi All,

I am a 36 year old male living in Brooklyn who is constantly trying to improve and optimize his life.

I was morbitly obese most my life e.g. 350lbs when I was 19. For the last 5 years I have been around 200lbs and active thanks to what I learned from Mark Sisson and later Dave Asprey & the bulletproof movement.

I work until 4am three nights a week, but I can sleep in as long as I want to. What is the ideal sleeping schedule with a job like mine? Should I go to bed at 4am every day and sleep until I wake up by myself. Or should I always get up at lets ay 8am and go to bed by midnight except the days I work?

I feel like I sleep well, and the sleep tracking software I used told the same. I usually sleep 8 hours.

My bedroom is pitch black. Upon rising I turn on the red LED panel over my bed, take a cold shower, open my window, do a 100 breath meditation, take my supplements and do some mobility work followed by a bulletproof coffee. In the warmer month here in New York I try to get to my garden within 30 min of rising and lay in the sun on the ground. In the winter that is obviously not as easy/pleasant. For the colder month I use a Philips goLITE/blue light during the day.

I try to work out two times a week. More has me too sore and I lose motivation. It's usually Basketball or heavy lifting (I tried both 5x5 and body by science) I also foam roll at least twice a week and do some stretching.

I follow the bulletproof diet, but I am gradually implementing what I learned here as well. Now I usually have my first meal two or three hours after my bulletproof coffee. It's either eggs or seafood. In the winter a warm cup of bone broth. I do not drink alcohol, am gluten free and usually low carb
Once a week or two late at night I feel decision fatigue and I make bad food choices: french fries and/or ice cream

I drink at least three litres of water a day. usually from my berkey filter or german mineral water from a glass bottle.

I eliminated plastic containers from my household.

My computer and phone turn off when I go to sleep, I also have two greenwave EMF filter in my bedroom.

My libido is good, actually great if I manage to stay away from internet porn. it's just so convinient :/

I own a grounding bracelet and the EmWave 2,but havn't gotten into using them regularly.

If there is any more information you will deem useful, please let me know.

### Josh — 2014-11-01T21:35:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erichs-journal.12044/post-145882>

Forgive me, I could not resist....welcome

### caroline — 2014-11-01T21:54:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erichs-journal.12044/post-145884>

Hi and welcome Erich. My understanding is that a berkey filter doesn't do a very good job.....

You are doing lots of good things for yourself ....but your work schedule sucks.......

### Inger — 2014-11-02T09:32:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erichs-journal.12044/post-145909>

get you a real women instead and get lots of oxy
it sure is going to help you health

# Thread: What the F an all that..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-f-an-all-that.12105/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-11-09T08:16:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-f-an-all-that.12105/post-146635>

Hi guys,

Here's the latest!

Remember earlier in the year when i stayed in north queensland at the organicy place? Im back there. hey presto - sleeps just divine! FINALLY off melatonin! What ive wanted for so long now! Its ridic how easy it is to sleep! I hang sheets / blankets around my bed, for that bit more darkness, and wake with the sun! yeeha!

Had some really crappy times the last few months - feeling enormous before my period, ratty, shitty, depressed..  its quite remarkable how much 'bigger' i can get at times. yuck  my last period i felt HIDEOUS before it, so large, moody and generally crappy..

now, Im into a nice cruisy day time routine here at the organic place. Limiting blue light, no wifi, all organic and a paleo butcher near by! OH, and of course im eating seafood as well!

Its been awesome to be able to 'stop' to - and finally connect to the inet, and start catching up on things,like labs for starters! I went to the local doctor last week and she was amazing! she didnt tell me to leave, that i was imagining things etc, she ticked all the boxes to the tests i wanted, and gave me a script for dessicated thyroid - which Tim Jackson wants me to go on to. (i self medicated with it a year ago and felt crap, but feel fine about starting it again, with Tims guidance!)

Amazing, got the tests back - they include low vitamin D, VERY high reverse T3 (the last time i had that test, it was 'normal') , so thats really cool to see - why? cos its a clue! waiting on ASI to come back, and HHV-6. Were starting to look into viral titrates to - to see what other stressors are going on, but so far, nothings showing up. it was a bit the same with the Metamatrix test (2200) - its not full of really bad stuff! Obviously, il go over it with Tim, but ... I have the results here in front of me. im hoping the dessicated thyroid will help.

So, I have days of up and down, as per usual. Its hot here, but the cold water is from tanks underground - it comes out SO cold, i love my cold showers! its all i can do right now, so i do it! Thats my CT! And i usuallly do it twice a day. OH plus 300 chooks - nice eggs to! We have raw milk here also, but im not drinking it, for now.

Im also on a mycoplasmic cleanse! Using a herbal formula that tim recommended -this along with ionic silver. I wonder if its that thats making me feel so darned tired a lot of the time! I hope so, it would be nice to have SOME thing happening, from something Im doing.

My next stint of work is not til december, so in the meantime, Il keep on sleeping, enjoying paleo, grass fed meat, adding duck fat to my bulletproof decaf, earthing, getting sunlight.... and I cant wait to talk to Tim Jackson again! oh, and heres hoping for an easier cycle this month - Iv self prescribed myself back onto st johns wort - I didnt like where my head space was going so chose not to have to live with it! Iv used SJW before and liked it.

Im too fatigued to be doing much 'exercise' at the moment, which can be a stressor, but it is what it is! still get in some qi kung and yoga

thanks Jack, again, and thanks guys ! xx

### caroline — 2014-11-09T09:25:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-f-an-all-that.12105/post-146636>

Hi Margo! awesome to see your update! seems like you are doing lots of positive things for yourself.

Where are you exactly? can't remember....

I am going to be around Byron Bay next week .... more inland - Dorrigo. Do you know it?
We have an eco cottage in a rain forest .... I can't wait to see the stars at nite and the waterfalls and rockpools.
Hoping to get an allover sun tan!

Keep well! xo

### margor_j — 2014-11-09T09:31:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-f-an-all-that.12105/post-146637>

hey caz!
yah, been a long time aye! but im plodding along! im near eumundi! whats the eco cottage - your own? do tell!
theres a little 'cob cottage' here, no electricity, earthen floor etc, but i know theres 8 legged critters inside so... feck that! lol

xx

### nicld — 2014-11-09T20:25:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-f-an-all-that.12105/post-146650>

WHOOOHOOOOOO, glad you stuck with it and are getting better.

### caroline — 2014-11-09T22:03:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-f-an-all-that.12105/post-146655>

# Thread: Sandy's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 88
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Da-mo — 2014-11-18T13:29:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-147705>

Been here a little while, thought I'd better start one of these and have a place to gather updates.

Gender: Male
Location: Waikato, New Zealand
Occupation: Power Station Operator/Maintainer
Interests: Health, Nutrition, Internal Martial Arts, Horsemanship

Born in NZ of a young Fijian mother and middle aged Scottish father who had thought he was infertile as a result of contracting spinal malaria in Africa - until I arrived along with my siblings.

I was born with a club foot which was operated on while I was still a baby and although the right ankle has limited range of movement and calf muscle is half the size of the left one - I was still able to play soccer, tennis, swim, run and be very athletic at a fairly high level (regional to national).

Gymnast through teen years, Martial artist through 20s and 30s, Very sedentary unhealthy lifestyle in early 40s and getting back to being healthy again from mid 40s on.

The martial arts part of my life was derived from Chinese internal or meditative martial arts using chi (vital energy) and yi (will/intent). It was a journey that started out being about self-defence, morphed into being about health and then finally was about philosophy and spirituality.

Married 24 years (but together 31, high school sweethearts) with 3 offspring ranging from 18 to 24.

Currently declining the lure of higher paying employment in favour of health and quality of life. We live on 5 acres by a river in beautiful surroundings. Smart meters are halfway up the driveway (about 75 meters away) and neighbourhood 415V power lines out on the road about 150 meters away. Nearest cell phone tower is 1200metres away as the crow flies, next nearest about 2km and next nearest about 15km.

Eating an Epi-paleo template and engaging in CT with good results including elimination of pain and inflammation from osteoarthrosis in knees. . . . grounding most days and enjoying the sunlight as much as possible.

On going medications - Flixotide and Ventolin for asthma.

Still struggling to give up cigarettes (mixed success) so looking for some sort of dopamine reset to help with that.

As an operator in a power station I have the perfect storm of shift work combined with high nnEMF. I'm currently manoeuvring into a position where I should be able to reduce the shift work to only 50% within the year and hopefully eliminate it not long after that.

Updates copied and pasted from this thread: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nightshifts-and-ct-timing-use.12109/page-2#post-147703](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nightshifts-and-ct-timing-use.12109/page-2#post-147703)

**10 Nov 14**
Got it - biggest thing I can do for myself is stop making myself hypoxic and destroying my magnetism. Smoking stops now.

My habit replacement strategies - any time I think of smoking . .
Meditation
Read blogs, watch podcasts and webinars
CT (while reading blogs, watching podcasts and webinars)
Exercise
Sex (who needs a reason)
Sexercise

**12 Nov 14**
Have had 2 cigarettes in the last 48 hours - effects so far . . .
Reduced ability to concentrate/think - tired brain.
Anything intruding or interrupting my thought is perceived as very annoying.
Carb cravings - ate 4 large cookies and several small ones
Restless leg syndrome and aches in rotator cuffs of shoulders
Knees surprisingly feeling better and feels as if better circulation in legs
Retaining water - not drinking coffee as it was always accompanied by a smoke.
Feel as if digestion has slowed down - bowels not evacuating as well.
Abdominal muscles feel more activated - toned
Increased appetite - I have read that this can be the hand to mouth habit but also the craving for nicotine feels like hunger but one cannot satisfy the other.
I have gained 2-3kgs - I hope this is water but it feels more like a full tummy

At least the nightshifts are over for another week and I can be more active in the daylight and CT in the evening with boatloads of water and seafood and good sleep.

Still digesting Jack's posts as much as low dopamine will allow for - its a good distracting when the cravings kick in.

**13 Nov 14**
Felt much better this morning - hammering the nicotine spray.
Sleep was a little restless but feel still feel rested this morning.
Libido lagging a little compared to normal.
Morning bowel movement better after dropping some magnesium last night following 20 minutes CT in 12degC water.
Ate 6 spicy sausages for breakfast - lunch today will be a tin of salmon and dinner will be a pot of oysters - then CT again before bed.
Had Mrs help me with getting a dopamine hit this morning before work - with some oxytocin and vasopressin for good measure
Wife and daughter also doing CT - they can go longer than me - non-smokers and more body fat.

**14 Nov 14**
Weight gain has been arrested since hitting the nicotine spray harder.

CT good again last night - listening to podcasts gets me through it - lighting several candles in the bathroom helped too, especially when getting out and the air has been warmed up by the candles.

Had to miss out on dopamine/oxytocin/vasopressin before work this morning - time was taken up cooking and eating BAB - corned beef and eggs. Eating it outside with bare feet on the ground and watching the sunrise.

The roses have bloomed so I'll pick one for my love before the horses eat them all. Red or apricot? . . . . decisions

**19 Nov 14**
Day 8 - have had 4 cigarettes over the past 8 days. Most recent was 3 days ago. Did not get the hit off it that I would have expected but then I have been using the nicotine spray frequently.
Health decisions have been good lately so hopefully dopamine levels are maintaining - nightshift does produce some carb cravings although I haven't' succumbed yet.
CT last night was very different - a lot less shivering than before, onset of shivering was much later and intensity was much less, barely perceptible. Skin did not get as red as it usually does even though temp and duration was the same. Could be time to increase duration.
Trying to work out how to implement some of the Jetlag Rx for when I'm on nightshifts. Its like flying to the opposite side of the world and back again within a 36 hour window. To make matters worse - its a morning arrival time (7am).

So . . . when my dopamine goes low my lovely wife can seem extremely annoying - managing to laugh at myself as I reach for "anti-bitch spray" (nicotine spray).

I better stay on the right side of her because from past experience stopping smoking, at around the 2 week mark, I start getting very . . . . errrrm . . . . affectionate

Oh . . . . the need for the asthma medications has fallen to virtually nothing.

### fitness@home — 2014-11-18T15:08:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-147708>

> The roses have bloomed so I'll pick one for my love before the horses eat them all. Red or apricot? . . . . decisions

Apricot!

Great start on your journal. Hang in there with the non-smoking. Well worth it in the end! I only smoked for one short 3-month period at age 18. So glad it didn't get me.

go on! Do not let your old bloody habit to prevent your great future! I am exsmoker, i feel for you! Everytime i had a cigarette ever since i stopped smoking I realized how bloody poisonous that stuff is!

### Da-mo — 2014-11-18T16:05:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-147714>

> [kovita said:](/goto/post?id=147709)
>
> go on! Do not let your old bloody habit to prevent your great future! I am exsmoker, i feel for you! Everytime i had a cigarette ever since i stopped smoking I realized how bloody poisonous that stuff is!

I'm on nightshift right now and I'm actually having much stronger carb cravings than cigarette cravings

> [kovita said:](/goto/post?id=147709)
>
> It would be interesting if you can share with us sometimes what have you learnt and heard from your mother. She must still be the generation which grew up when Fiji was relatively untouched. there must be a lot of interesting native wisdom you were raised up with!

Yes, my mother grew up in a mountain village in the Sigatoka valley. The river was their highway until my dad, a civil engineer, arrived with 200 men, bulldozers and dynamite to build a road.

Apart from the way they live so close to nature, the wisest thing I think is their laid back, low stress outlook. At any tourist resort in Fiji you will see the guests on holiday walking faster than the staff at work. This song I think expresses some of that:
_[www.**you**tube.com/watch?v=XxqYigFwUTQ](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XxqYigFwUTQ)_

### Da-mo — 2014-11-18T16:47:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-147715>

I'd better mention it since I think I saw it was a good sign somewhere on here - the other night I had the most vivid dreams - even more it was a dream within a dream within a dream. What I mean is I woke from one dream into another dream, then into another dream before finally being awake for real. Its kind of weird when you think you are awake but then realise you were dreaming of being awake

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=140127)
>
> Dreaming is a function of electrons within the DHA network and how the neocortex and thalamocortical relays are interacting because of the relationship of light to magnetism and the photoelectric effect. It is all controlled by water flows in the capacitor plates of our white matter.......under the control of the electrostatic fields and magnetic fields from our mitochondria. Magnetism is the father not the child to electricity in biology. The MS blog is going to open your eyes to this.......so will my September webinar.

> _Guess what dreams really are?_ They are the “firing up” of these newly made neural networks, so they become operational. [http://jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-9-quantum-sleep/](http://jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-9-quantum-sleep/)

### Da-mo — 2014-11-19T09:17:38+0200

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=147780)
>
> Dr Kruse only posted it on facebook -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/907231755956386/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/907231755956386/)
> I put up a reply too -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...\_id=907382562607972&offset=0&total\_comments=4](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/907231755956386/?comment_id=907382562607972&offset=0&total_comments=4)
>
> Post by [Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse) on his forum about DHA, RXR, and PPAR - [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/bio-hacking-eye-color.12033/page-3#post-146120](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads%2Fbio-hacking-eye-color.12033%2Fpage-3#post-146120)
> I wrote some stuff about PPAR-gamma and DHA here -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/50-grams-or-protein-no-can-do.11940/#post-144495](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads%2F50-grams-or-protein-no-can-do.11940%2F#post-144495)
> I am not skilled enough to understand the differences between mouse and human liver metabolism, but as I see it now, the paper linked above seems to show me this cascade (in humans) which must occur in order, and which must begin at night time:
> DHA -> RXR -> PPAR-gamma -> PGC1-alpha -> PPAR-alpha
> In layman terms, that basically means that you need to release DHA from cells at night in order to burn fat during the day, where PPAR-alpha should continue to rise as the day progresses (PPAR-alpha does lots of things to help mobilise and utilise fatty acids)
> The paper went on to show that feeding the rats outside their active waking hours (night time) blunted PPAR-alpha activity the following day. ie: They could burn less fat
> I would think that this result is pretty relevant to humans, since we rely on all the same signalling molecules. The only difference is the DHA -> RXR step above, whereby the rats probably use different RXRs, but I don't know enough about that.​

Boom!!
This explains my experience last nightshift. I don't usually eat on a nightshift - but last night I did - and today I am 3kg heavier than usual. I usually treat the nightshifts as an intermittent fast just like any normal night. Tonight I will not eat on the nightshift and sleep at home before I eat so the IF will be something like 17-18 hours.
Treating the nightshifts as if for jetlag and fasting during as per Jetlag Rx.

Bleh, nightshifts are always tough to deal with

If you're interested I've made some notes from John Kiefer's "Nicotine Declassified", which talks about the use of nicotine in the context of body composition and fitness. This also comes from a relatively conservative perspective, meaning that it is just a protocol made based on reviews of the current research. No Quantum magic here --- [http://tanyewwei.com/notes/kiefer-nicotine](http://tanyewwei.com/notes/kiefer-nicotine)

I've never had a cigarette ever, and only experimented with low dose nicotine via gum. The dose was as Kiefer recommends, 4mg via a gum that takes about 30 mins to deplete. I also made sure to get gum that uses sugar alcohols or stevia instead of phenylalanine-containing compounds as a sweetener, since stuff like aspartame can screw with neurochemistry. As a comparison, an average cigarette is thought to have 1mg of nicotine, so 4mg is actually a significant dose.

I definitely experience an appetite blunting effect, along with some of the thermogenic effects due to upregulation of uncoupling protein activity . Maybe this is something to experiment with?

.

Thanks for the link - I saw that referred to in the Fasted State Training thread

I'm currently using a nicotine mouth spray at 1mg/spray. Max dosage is 64 sprays per 24 hours which is a huge amount of nicotine.

You are probably just the guy to evaluate this, would love to get your opinion on the implications if it is valid . . . .

[http://www.sott.net/article/270514-Nicotine-vs-nicotinic-acid-niacin](http://www.sott.net/article/270514-Nicotine-vs-nicotinic-acid-niacin)

> Can nicotine be turned into niacin? There are those who declare there is no relationship between the two, that it is a mere accident of nomenclature. Well, let's see.
>
> Here is the molecular structure of nicotine.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

> For those unfamiliar with the shorthand in these diagrams, every unlabelled 'corner' is a carbon atom and the single-hydrogen-atoms poking out all over the place are routinely omitted for clarity. Lines represent bonds, double lines represent double-bonds. Carbon makes four bonds so any corner with three bonds showing has a hydrogen tacked on the outside of the ring. Nitrogen makes three bonds, oxygen two, hydrogen one... oh hell, I'm not getting into lecture mode at this time of night!
> Now, here is the molecular structure of nicotinic acid - niacin - vitamin B3:

> Take the five-atom ring off nicotine by breaking just one bond and replace it with a COOH group and you're there. In biochemical terms, this is known as 'a piece of piss'.
> The two molecules are not unrelated. A simple oxidation reaction is all you need to turn nicotine into nicotinic acid. It's not an accident of nomenclature at all.
> Much as the Righteous would like to have us believe.

### Da-mo — 2014-11-19T10:41:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-147796>

> Vitamin B3 (Niacin) is used commonly to refer to two different compounds, nicotinic acid and niacinamide. **B3 was first isolated during oxidation of nicotine from tobacco** and was thus given the name nicotinic acid vitamin, shortened to niacin. It is not, however, the same as or even closely related to the molecule nicotine. Niacin, as nicotinic acid or niacinamide, is converted in the body to the active forms, nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NAD) and a phosphorylated form (NADP). [http://orthomolecular.org/nutrients/b3.html](http://orthomolecular.org/nutrients/b3.html)
