### Pebbles — 2014-12-02T12:01:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pebbles-journal.12264/post-149007>

Hi Caroline!
Read Leptin RX)
Work place depends... I am a freelancer consultant, so everywhere where I get a contract, sometimes this means sleeping in a hotel during weekdays and returning home only for weekend but sometimes working from home as well.
We live in a house, classical brick construction, mineral isolation, ceramic tiles ( so walking barefoot in house is grounding as well), no smart meters, a little garden with fruit trees ( apricot, fig, sawer cherries, cherries, grapes, mint, lemon balm, parsley and colourful roses all around the fence) and a big 30-40 yrs walnut tree - grounding in the garden)
Summertime we often go to a lake or to a forest nearby. Laying in the sun in the dirt, soaking my legs in the water - and this is an instinct habit, Swimming tog w my GSD.
Water - we have a spring also close to us, we have carbon water filter and buying the cleanest mineral water (won several european eau-oscars). Unfortunately the bathroom water is not filtered, so still getting chlorine while showering.
No, have no blue blocker glasses yet. Living in the suburbs of capital Budapest, the place has a silent village like lights - so almost nothing.....is not a light polluted place.

About my maternal lineage:
My grand-grandma lived 91 yrs, in full physical and mental health - never ever been to a doc, never took meds - she just used a few med plants collected by her own and she had always cold water showers- her own CT). She was small - about 150cm and thin.
My grandma- unfortunately died at age 26- due to a mistake - doc cut an artery during an op, the op was not needed, she had only an uterus/ovaries inflammation due to a cold.
My mom - she is now 71, she had cyst on her kidneys ( last 7 yrs) -PCKD. Urol, said cyst could be due to flu vaccine or pneum vaccines she got a few years ago ( 1x and never ever again). Cyst fluid extracted- cytology neg-, sclerot fluid filled instead. Parallel with the kidney cyst her BP changed from whole life low 90/60 to 130/90, so she takes ACE inhib. Also we do not know when, her blood 0, RHneg changed to 0, RHpos - no blood transfusion, or similar
She has nodules on her thyroid, but all labs are OK and she does not feel anything neg hormonally. She had an uterus myoma and unfortunately ( her erroneous decision) had a full hysterectomy at the age 43, so can not trace back at what age I should expect entering meno..... Due to lack of hormones, she had a starting osteosp- but now is ok with paleo and Vit D3 and K2.
According to blood group, I've inherited my fathers one - A, RH pos and the 'outlook' as well - most probably with some armenian or mediteranian roots: almost black hair, olive/copper skin and green eyes - will turn out soon with my 23and me. And hopefully will not inherit the stroke - my father, all my fathers brothers and sisters and my fathers mom and dad as well are gone due to stroke - this sounds very bad and scary, I know.

Eating: can fast easily, so been eating only 1xday at 6pm, and had a morning coffee w lots of cream. Changed since 3-4 weeks according to Leptin RX, have a piece of protein at 6am - min 50gr, have something at 12:00 and at 6pm. Since 2009 am on paleo, so no grains, no legumes, no sugar. Have vegetables as CH source and some fruits, latest not every day.

### Pebbles — 2016-04-18T17:44:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pebbles-journal.12264/post-192975>

2016 March- April - tropical vacation - followed Jet Lag RX for travel- the EMF-edema gone in 1-2 days.
tanking high UV and IR.
Some days I was out on the open sea, with high adrenaline and with ZERO EMF - had no stuffed ears and no myoclonus, only the ear sizzling stays (its permanent and much weaker than a ringing). Its was such a refreshing feeling! I felt almost like in pre-floxxed times!
Hormonally felt OK as well!
The way back ( E to W), followed Jet Lag RX - While landing - pressure increase - the stuffing of the ear and myoclonus lessens ( lack of NO is compensated by other gases - NOS, H2S, CO, etc). 1 day passed and still some edema in my ankles.
My ears are stuffed, myoclonus again, ..... I wish I could return ASAP to the healing sun and open sea - with no EMF!
Still hope on some longer lasting positive effects......

### Pebbles — 2017-06-29T23:01:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pebbles-journal.12264/post-217519>

After 6 month in the tropics, 3 weeks home and starts again....
In January due to a scratched infected mosquito bite that did not want to heal in the tropics, I had to take Augmentin, which activated EBV - my childhood mumps-, ending with some Frey syndrome. - interrupted ABs after 3 days, apparently the Frey syndrome diminished with time.
Now back home (EU, 49 L) and due to inflamed lymph under chin and flu symptoms ( scratching and painful throat) lasting since a few weeks and fever fluctuating during the day between 36,5-37,5C - not progressive, but chaotic.
I made ultrasound, which showed activated chin lymph node and a 7mmx3mm "lymph node' intraparenchial in parotid.
The fever might be related or unrelated to above - never had any allergy and now turned out that I have dust mite allergy (after my return cleaned a room not touched for 6 month). Endotoxins blocked partially my bile as well, menses stopped. At 51,5yo could be meno, however I doubt stopes so drastically right after my return.
Now I was sent to cytology for the parotid nodule.
1- What labs other than C3 and C4 should I do for toxins? ( I do not have HLA snps, no HLA-DR snps)
2- Cytology for parotid - I have doubt doing it, since heard from you so many times the PSA test/testis situation - what is your opinion about punching parotid?
3- if no punching, blood work can be reliable to exclude cancer?

# Thread: Q'Rad Randy's Journal--Journey for a 1 year bio-hack!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/qrad-randys-journal-journey-for-a-1-year-bio-hack.12438/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QuantumRadical — 2014-12-22T05:18:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/qrad-randys-journal-journey-for-a-1-year-bio-hack.12438/post-150774>

Hi, gang! Well, it is about time I did a little bio-hacking myself. I have lived the SAD life over the last couple of months to intentionally get near to a point in health I was a few years ago--before I discovered Jack's Blogs. What do I want to get out of this? With a disciplined but fun approach to health and wellness, and following Dr. Kruse's protocols to the best of my ability, what types of changes and how much change can I, in my own N=1 bio-hack, experience over the space of a year. I am not trying to isolate any single parameter--analogously living the life of the monk without the Ferrari, but I will still drive my A-6!

I will be taking a robust/comprehensive blood analysis on December 26th this year (2014) and then again at 6 month intervals. If Nature wills, I will have nearly 6 months of results on Jack's Kruse \[sic\] . I will post my initial conditions here as soon as I get the results back. If Jack let's me have 15 minutes of fame on the cruise, I can do a summary debriefing on my first 6 months of the bio-hack. I will be journaling everything I can think of without making that activity too stressful--maybe just weekly entries here. I will also take photos of myself before, during, and after at regular intervals to see any visible physique/body composition changes (not necessarily for publication here but,... I might think about it). I will post some initial conditions on the day after they take my blood (26th), but for starters, I am 61.5 years old.

I think I will also go get a telomere test sometime in January, with a final measure next December. Not sure if I should get results midway. I don't know if the tests are accurate enough to make a difference after 6 months--each result may be inside the error bar of the other. I could use some advice here.

I do have 23andMe results from three years ago which I may post here along the way. I also have previous blood results which I may also post just for information and possible comparison to this bio-hack.

Some highlights of what I will track are:
CT events
Alcohol consumption
Coffee consumption
Bowel movements
OMs (or oxy moments, but no details! ), maybe!
Exercise
Food--seafood, oysters, junk, etc. Type and quality of food.
nnEMF exposure, or not.
Exposure to the sun
Sleep quality/hours per day (start time, finish time)
BAB or not
Water consumption
Weight/weight loss
Use of blue-light blockers
Use of IR nasal treatment. I have one of those VieLight 810s and will keep track of usage.

I am sure I will have a few more items. Just thought of one: focusing on posture--no slouching. If you can think of additional parameters to track or think about, please let me know.

Anyway, I am looking forward to journaling my journey--maybe even write a book about it. I expect this group to keep my spirits up as I go hardcore. Just kidding! I know this will make me feel better and healthier with blood testing results to prove it. Just thinking about doing this already lifts my spirits, and I know you hardcore Krusers will keep me in the game!

I welcome any and all constructive comments and suggestions. Let the games begin!!

Merry Christmas and a healthy and happy New Year.

Q'Rad Randy

### fitness@home — 2014-12-22T12:39:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/qrad-randys-journal-journey-for-a-1-year-bio-hack.12438/post-150788>

> I will also go get a telomere test sometime in January, with a final measure next December. Not sure if I should get results midway. I don't know if the tests are accurate enough to make a difference after 6 months--each result may be inside the error bar of the other. I could use some advice here.

There is a company based in Spain that offers a newer technology Telomere testing. My doctor told me about it last week, when I emailed them about getting a test done (have always been curious). Supposed to better than Spectra cell's test which is just the average telomere length.

I have the info at work on availability in US and will post it later, in case you are interested.

Edited to post this:

[http://www.lifelength.com/index-eng2.html](http://www.lifelength.com/index-eng2.html)

Hello Ms. Clark,

Thank you for your interest in Life Length’s Telomere Analysis Technology. Your email inquiry was forwarded to me as I cover Texas.

I would like to answer your questions as best I can.

1.  The physician cost of a TAT test is typically $589. This does not include ancillary fees such as blood draw and your doctor’s time.

2.  If your doctor’s office draws blood, they can certainly draw a TAT test kit. If they do not, perhaps see if they can recommend an outside draw station.

If your doctor is not already in our database, I can assist with opening an account so a kit can be sent to you. Please let me know how I can be of assistance.

In health,
Micah

**Micah Weathers**
Western Regional Sales Manager
[mweathers@lifelength.com](mailto:mweathers@lifelength.com)

_**Quite pricey compared to the Spectracell test I can get at my doctor's for $200, but it is a more detailed test and report.**_

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/qrad-randys-journal-journey-for-a-1-year-bio-hack.12438/post-151068>

> [Valerie said:](/goto/post?id=151065)
>
> What is your experience with the vialight 810 to date?

I have used it a few times. Nothing negative. I am sure it works over the long term, but things change at biological rates--weeks to months, so I have no expectation of noticing anything immediate. In fact, it is very likely that any changes will be subtle (in the big mix of things) and unnoticeable, except in the bigger picture of lab results over time.

The via light is easy to use. I am trying to make it a routine at night. Read a little before going to sleep with the thing stuck in my nose.  My family thinks I am crazy, but I intend to prove it via the bio-hack and real lab measurements. Vie light is just one of the many things that I will be using to get more toward optimal.

Valerie, does this help? I am buying into the electrons, light, magnetism, and water stuff that Jack talks about. Certainly doesn't hurt my head--actually makes sense if you have any understanding of quantum physics. The vie light at the correct light frequency makes sense. We shall see.

Happy New Year!

### QuantumRadical — 2014-12-28T19:34:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/qrad-randys-journal-journey-for-a-1-year-bio-hack.12438/post-151094>

I have been thinking more and more about this bio-hack. "Full court press" is the way I want to describe it. Heavy focus on CT, seafood, and good sleep. Get some sun when I can. No artificial foods or sweeteners. Minimal to no alcohol or coffee. Plenty of good water. Use blue-light-blockers as much as I can. I will be talking to my optometrist about getting blu-light blocker glasses for anytime and anywhere. My eyesight has actually improved over the last couple of years so it may be an interesting discussion with her. I think I will also go ahead with the telomere testing but I won't get to that until late January at the earliest. I should probably also get a general physical from my doctor to set a baseline. Again, that won't happen until the last part of January--working on a big proposal for the next two or three weeks in Chicago with plenty of exposure to nnEMF and plenty of opportunities for stress.  Shijin13 would understand.

# Thread: Daytime napping & circadian signalling (?)

**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-22T04:45:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daytime-napping-circadian-signalling.13014/post-156259>

I'm curious if anyone knows how much a daytime nap, like say an hour after work (4 PM-5 PM), if it would cause significant impairment of our natural cellular signalling.

When I come home from work, I feel like I absolutely need a nap and I will usually drift into a pretty deep sleep pretty fast - for about an hour and a half.

I've heard that it is not good practice but in other parts of the world, people take "siestas".
Also, a lot of animals will nap during the day as well.

If I skip that nap - boy I sure notice as I usually am quite fatigued the following day.
Has Jack discussed napping anywhere on this site?

### caroline — 2015-02-22T06:15:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daytime-napping-circadian-signalling.13014/post-156264>

good question Mike ....

### Inger — 2015-02-22T10:10:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daytime-napping-circadian-signalling.13014/post-156273>

Jack has said... sleep is good anytime !!!

My parents use to take naps at daytime often! And they are both pretty healthy.......

I know if I feel like taking a nap in the daytime.. I always do.. and it makes me feel awesome. It happens very seldom tho, but when, I know my body needs it.
I think we should always sleep when we feel like it! It is a clear signal from our body, it needs rest and regeneration
Maybe it is like... The Great Medicine. And it is all for free

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-22T21:08:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daytime-napping-circadian-signalling.13014/post-156306>

I know some very accomplished figures throughout history have been known to nap.
The only person I can remember right now is Winston Churchill (but he was also a drinker!).

..then you have the "Iron Lady" who days "napping is for wimps!"

A lot of people advise against napping saying that it will impair your nighttime sleep.
I'm really starting to trust Jack's approach which, like he says, is really not his approach at all.
..it's Mother Nature's approach.

I used to trust the "conventional wisdom" but that just made me sick!
I'm kind of bitter about it.

"Whole grains and dairy is good for you!"
(yeah right. Interesting that these are agriculture industry recommendations!)

"Eat several times a day to keep your blood sugar stable" -
(A great contributor to leptin resistance! I totally fell for this one!)

"Ketosis is dangerous when you are cutting down! "
(I learned a lot of garbage "rules" when I was weight training a lot in the '90's)

Now I ask myself instead "What would happen if this was say 100,00 years ago. and how does that answer match with 1,000,000,000 years ago?" and so on..

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-23T01:04:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shellfish-and-food-allergies.13017/post-156320>

If shellfish are so healthy for us, why do so many people have terrible allergies to them?
When I told my naturopath that Dr Kruse recommends seafood, especially shellfish, she was surprised.

"When I do food sensitivity testing on people, shellfish is a really common reaction." she said.

Is it because they are "bottom feeders" and end up with a lot of toxins in them?

I barbequed a bunch of oysters this morning and honestly, I don't feel that great.
Not bad, but not good either.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shellfish-and-food-allergies.13017/post-156322>

I actually started catching a cold again whereas the day before I felt great afterwards!
I ate oysters for breakfast on the day I didn't feel good.
(?)

### Inger — 2015-02-23T13:45:04+0200

I do not believe shellfish has a lot of toxins in them... at least not if they lived in somewhat clean waters

The Baltic Sea is very polluted BTW.. still.. the health benefits of eating the fish from there is 40+ times bigger than the issues any pollution will do...
This I read on the site of the Finnish government
and I believe it, because the statistics show the fishermen (that eat a lot of Baltic Sea fish all the time) are among the healthiest in the population.....

Thanks for the clarification. I did escalate the CT pretty quickly - The first day I just dipped myself for about 20 seconds at a time, four times, but I kept my feet in the water the entire time - about 30 minutes.
The second time I managed to keep myself under (up to my chin) for a whole half hour!
I was so proud of myself but I didn't get the energy boost like the day before.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-25T05:58:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shellfish-and-food-allergies.13017/post-156550>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=156549)
>
> in the face of a poor redox people can develop intolerances to many different proteins. it is the context that matters, not the "offending" protein, but rather they way the system handles it.

This is a Random Clinical Trial using fish oil, but not fish, but what the hell  -- [http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0091674903022711](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0091674903022711)

A more important paper **'Chitins and Chitosans as Immunoadjuvants and Non-Allergenic Drug Carriers**', which I just briefly skimmed  (but will give a full summary of eventually) -- [http://www.mdpi.com/1660-3397/8/2/292/htm](http://www.mdpi.com/1660-3397/8/2/292/htm)

This paper seems to indicate that the main allergen in shrimp is tropomyosin -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tropomyosin](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tropomyosin)

Tropomyosin is abundant in actin filaments. Actin and Integrin are critical components of the collagen super-information highway of the body.

Fundamentally, a "seafood allergy" is due to malfunctioning T-helper cells (usually Th2). We should be asking why these cells are malfunctioning, instead of avoiding shellfish forever (only avoid them until immune system is fixed).

What then likely happens with shellfish allergies, is that you get lots of tropomyosin, and create antibodies against this tropomyosin, which end up destroying your own collagen networks.

The issue here is T helper cells not having enough energy (electrons), and being unable to distinguish dietary tropomyosin, and over-reacting to cause unwanted antibody production.

\----

**Why I don't like allergy tests**

Allergy testing nowadays usually looks for the presence of particular antibodies (immunoglobulins). Typically you will have antibodies floating around in the body for quite some time, but the fundamental issue here is T-helper cell dysfunction.

The job of antibodies is to mark "invaders", and also to activate "defenders" (eg: phagocytes). Both of these processes depend on enzymatic function, which we know is definitely reliant on proton tunnelling and electric resonant effects.

So what happens when the redox potential of your body's own collagen networks improve, and the actin within them no longer resonate with the antibodies you produced when you were sick? The antibodies are no longer harmful , and you also stop producing extra antibodies because your T-helper cells can now recognise "invaders" appropriately.

\----

Sidenote: Chitin in shellfish is awesome animal fibre! ​

...

# Thread: Figuring out thyroid labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/figuring-out-thyroid-labs.13060/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Galena — 2015-02-25T19:36:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/figuring-out-thyroid-labs.13060/post-156593>

Hi Everyone:
I am new to this forum, have been following Dr. Kruse's teachings for some time. Was eating Paleo, low-carb before starting to read Dr.Kruse, and now transitioning to Epi-Paleo ketogenic over last few months.
Just got my Magnetico pad set up yesterday (10 gauss), and excited to see the effects on my sleep.
I work in an office setting where non-native EMF is high (computers, cell phones, Wi-Fi), at home we have done what we can for now to minimize exposure, until someday hopefully can move to better location.

Brief history:
Suffered from recurring depression and binge-eating and food sensitivities most of my adult life. Managed to keep normal weight through "diet and exercise". Currently I weigh 135 lbs and height of 5' 2 inches. Past 10 years, binge eating has become more manageable by using low carb diet and basically avoiding trigger foods (abstinence). I do not take any antidepressant medication.

For last 5 years, I noticed my sleep was getting progressively worse, until I started wearing blue-blocking glasses in the evening, CT in the evening, and lying on a sleep-induction mat (from Dave Asprey) before bed. These things have greatly improved my quality of sleep.

First went to anti-aging doctor in 2011 mainly to check on my hormones ( i am currently peri-menopausal), and was surprised to find out that my Free T3 is rather low. Surprised, because I don't really feel strong symptoms of hypothyroid, only that my feet are very sensitive to the cold, and sometimes feel some fatigue, but not extreme. The thyroid panel has been re-checked a few times since 2011, and always stays about the same.

Have had many other tests (including ASI, GI-effects, heavy metals (urine), 23-and-me SNPs), the main findings that stand out are:
\*all day long my cortisol is at the low end of the normal range on ASI
\*I have low diversity of gut microbes, high fecal secretory IgA (indicating some food sensitivity that is not yet identified)
\*high levels of Lead and Mercury
\*homozygous for MTHFR A1298C (23andme)
\*low HS-CRP (0.4 mg/L)
\*DHEA-S =4.5 umol/L (convert to U.S. = 166ug/dL; Dr. Kruse advises >250 is optimal)
\*Bun:Creatine ratio = don't know yet, waiting for next set of labs to do the measurements.

Last thyroid panel Sept.2014:
\*TSH = 1.67 mIU/L (range 0.2 - 6.0)
\*Free T3 = 2.6 pmol/L (range 3.5 - 6.5) --> in U.S. units 2.6 pmol/L translates to 169 pg/dL
\*Free T4 = 14.0 pmol/L (range 10 - 25)
\*Anti-TPO = 7.0 kIU/L (range 0 - 34) ---> considered negative for Hashimoto's

I started doing CT, worked my way up to 25 min per day in water about 48-50Degrees (cold straight from the tap in tub, waist -deep). Also, it is winter here in Calgary, so I walk outside and get cold often.
Have been doing CT almost daily now for 4 months. Just got my thyroid panel re-tested, and waiting for results to see if CT alone has any effect on thyroid. I certainly notice that I sleep more soundly with doing CT.

My anti-aging doctor is recommending the next step may need to be heavy metal chelation, but I am sceptical about this. For now I am working on gut issues, and using methods to increase Glutathione to support detoxification of heavy metals. Her interpretation of the Low Free T3 is that there is a problem with conversion of T4-> T3, and heavy metals may play a role. I need help trying to understand what limits this conversion.

I appreciate any feedback from other members !

### Hope — 2015-02-25T20:00:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/figuring-out-thyroid-labs.13060/post-156595>

Many causes re T4>T3 conversion......Heavy metals just one; it's all over the blogs. I wish I remembered which one specifically now.

I have no doubt folks will tell you the five top reasons. Sounds like you have Brain Gut issues too....all too common with nnEMF around. So glad you got a Magnetico.

Thanks for sharing your story!

### Josh — 2015-02-25T20:58:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/figuring-out-thyroid-labs.13060/post-156597>

Consider that T4 conversion to T3 requires good sulfur handling to make the enzymes. This may be a CBS pathway issue. If you have CBS methylation issues based on SNP's and or poor redox, conversion and detxification of heavy metals will both be unreliable. Conversely, if these are resolved, the metals may be detoxified by your body without intervention. The MTHFR SNP will predispose you to acumulate metals when it expresses. Detoxifying you under these circumstances may actually do more harm than good. Also where is the reverse T3? You may be converting, but deactivating it to minimize inflammation?

### Josh — 2015-02-25T23:35:08+0200

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-02-26T00:23:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/figuring-out-thyroid-labs.13060/post-156622>

I've been wondering what Jack Kruse thinks about these things, but, myxedema at the Achilles heel tendon and/or bicep have proven to be equally, or many times more accurate than labs for diagnosing hypothyroidism; then there is the Barnes Temperature test. He treated thousands of patients with thyroid hormones and a high saturated fat, low carb diet, and autopsied many humans and animals in Europe after they died to analyze what thyroid hormone did systemically. He came to the conclusion that the best test for thyroid status, outside of myxedema, was body temperature first thing upon waking in the morning, taken under your arm. He even ran mini-trials on his patients utilizing multiple batches of the first blood tests, and found them all to be unreliable.

[http://www.turner-white.com/memberfile.php?PubCode=hp\_jan04\_thyroid.pdf](http://www.turner-white.com/memberfile.php?PubCode=hp_jan04_thyroid.pdf) **Woltman's Sign of Hypothyroidism - Mark A. Marinella, MD, FACP, CNSP**

[http://www.cmaj.ca/content/179/4/387](http://www.cmaj.ca/content/179/4/387) **A classic sign of hypothyroidism: a video demonstration**
[http://press.endocrine.org/doi/abs/...id=ori:rid:crossref.org&rfr\_dat=cr\_pub=pubmed](http://press.endocrine.org/doi/abs/10.1210/jcem.82.3.3810?url_ver=Z39.88-2003&rfr_id=ori:rid:crossref.org&rfr_dat=cr_pub%3dpubmed) **Estimation of Tissue Hypothyroidism by a New Clinical Score: Evaluation of Patients with Various Grades of Hypothyroidism and Controls**
This is just a snippet of the literature. Other studies found absolutely no association between T4 and TSH with thyroid hormone reactions, levels, etc. but strong associations with myxedema at the Achilles heel in+total cholesterol. This makes even more sense in regards to what Dr. Kruse has stated about T3 combining with LDLs and Vit.A to form pregnenolone in the mitochondria.

### Galena — 2015-02-26T00:40:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/figuring-out-thyroid-labs.13060/post-156626>

Thanks for your remarks, Hope and Josh.

Josh: Reverse T3 was included in my last set of blood tests (this week), and I don't have results yet.

Magnetico update:
Disrupted sleep continued for about 5 weeks after getting the Magnetico. Then in about the 6th week, I began having some nights where I was sleeping solidly for 5-6 hours straight, uninterrupted, and now that is pretty normal to sleep that many hours continuously (have had the mattress now for close to 3 months). On the occasions where I do wake up in the middle of the night, it is easy now to fall back asleep.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-04-18T08:24:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/figuring-out-thyroid-labs.13060/post-161611>

We just got our 20 gauss on Tuesday. I had one night where I was waking up a fair amount.
Last night my stomach was churning not long after I went to bed but I think that was more due to the coconut oil I took just before I went to bed.
Coconut oil is an antifungal, so it probably wiped out some candida, which in turn gave me the runs.
Other than that, no problems so far.

# Thread: Kelly's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kellys-journal.13179/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ktrosper — 2015-03-05T00:07:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kellys-journal.13179/post-157457>

**"Know thyself."** \-Socrates
Many of these questions we must ask ourselves on the road to optimal are impossible to answer "correctly, in context" without this knowledge of ourselves.. It's the first and quite possibly the most critical step toward optimum, in my opinion..
**About my journal:**
I have and will continue to put a lot of thought into this.. I'll edit this as time goes, as I continue to learn and build understanding, as those I trust and respect come to influence me and as my priorities change..

**My Guiding principles**:

-   Context matters.. Always try to put everything in context.
-   Value truth above all else.. Seek it and embrace it, even if it means the destruction of a premise, belief or value..
-   Ask the right questions/Ask better questions.. Useful answers come from asking the right questions.
-   When you know better you do better/To believe in it, I must understand it.. Change is easy when I understand why the change matters.
-   This is a marathon, not a sprint.. The road to optimal is long, there's a lot of learning to be done and there are only so many hours in a day..
-   Search for conflict.. conflict reveals a possible flaw in the premise, a possible gap in the knowledge or a messed up value-system.

**My Goal**: I want to achieve optimal **HEALTH** while promoting **LONGEVITY** while **ENJOYING** my life...

Is this the same as wanting to Enjoy life, while promoting optimal Health while promoting Longevity or
wanting to achieve Longevity, while promoting optimal Health while Enjoying life? Close, but I don't think so.. The differences are subtle, but they are worth thinking about..

Defining what I want begs more questions..
Some of these obviously seem redundant, and maybe they are.. It's my way of ensuring that I've arrived at the right answer and I've set the right priority within the context of my life.

Can I enjoy life without health? Probably, but to a lesser extent..
Can I enjoy life without longevity? Of course, but I'd prefer to enjoy it for as long as possible.. Some things I enjoy come with risk.. Life is full of tradeoffs.
Can I live a long time without health? Chances are reduced, for sure.. I suppose it's possible to live a long time with a feeding tube stuffed down my throat while on a ventilator..
Would I enjoy living long without health? Certainly less enjoyable than living long with health..
Would I want longevity without enjoyment of life? No, Probably not.
Can I achieve optimal health yet lose out on longevity? Of course, accidents happen..
Can I achieve optimal health yet lose out on enjoyment of life? Yes, and that's not what I want.

These questions, in my opinion, are worth contemplating AND the answers worth documenting.. The answers help to define my focus and my priorities. They tell me where I'm at and they shine the light on the path forward.. The documentation makes them "real", allows me to scrutinize them and search for conflict, revisit them as needed..

Before I could answer any of these questions I had to **define** these objectives..
Before I could really define any objectives, I had to understand what it is that I truly value.
Know thyself..

**My current/best definitions**:

**"Enjoying life" is..**
Spending my time and energy on the people and things I love and value. (Family stuff, CheapEagle (my Powered Paraglider), Gladys (our RV), General (my truck), Hunting, Working on my Health, Learning/Self-educating, Becoming a better man, Running, Bucket List). Risk/reward value judgments are obviously a factor. Would I enjoy snorting a line of coke from here to Memphis? Probably, but I'm not willing to risk my health or longevity over it.. Where is the sweet spot?

**"Longevity" is..**
Duh! Living a LONG time.. Centurion? Sure, I'll take it.. but longevity's value is relative to health and enjoyment. Would I want to live to be 200 if I had to spend that time in a bubble in the basement? Of course not. Quality of life matters. Life is full of tradeoffs. Where is the sweet spot?

**"optimal Health" is..**
Physical, mental and spiritual functionality as close to peak as possible (optimal)- but not at any cost.. Value judgments are a factor here, too. Could I peak physically on HGH or steroid injections? Yes, but the cost to longevity is not something I'm willing to pay. Would I peak mentally if I spent 10 hours a day doing brain sharpening skills? Maybe, but that kind of investment would be too steep for the return.. Could I peak spiritually if I were to meditate 20 out of 24 hours? Maybe, but at what cost? Where is the sweet spot?

This "sweet spot" is most probably in a different place for all of us.
It all comes down to value.

Know thyself..
(to be continued...)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### Марио Кинтанилья — 2015-03-06T01:51:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mr-qs-journal.13197/post-157593>

I am walking living proof of the applied practices from the LeptinRx and . From birth to half of my life I came from Central America and grew up with 80% of my nutrition being white rice, red beans and corn tortillas and from the trees fruit, and with raw milk cheese and lean meats as the remaining 18% of proteins and fat. I enjoyed excellent health and continued in excellent health after moving to North America. Once here, I adopted the SAD and began to become a bear in the fall of the 3rd quarter of my life, but without the power of a bear. At that point I tried to "reset" my nutrition to that one from the first half of my life, but it turned to be worse than the SAD. Did paleo too before it was marketed with a name, no improvement. It was until learning through my sons conditions that I learned about ketosis and the impact to the brain. Yes, I was hooked 18 years ago but it took me those 18 years to wait and learn how to apply it to myself and not just my son. This part 4 quilt sums up complete truth. I can't return to the nutrition of the first half of my life, but I don't need to, or should. A ketotic state and regulating my connection to the nature's way with a reduction in man made continues to make improvements in all aspects of my life. The knowledge shared by Dr. Kruse it's the grassfed parsley butter that melts on my ribeye. The analogy it's because I don't do ice cream with cherry on top.

I've come across a few neurosurgeons in my life and god bless them all for what they do, but Dr. Kruse has gone above and beyond to share his passion for knowledge and truth seeking, that it's palpably inspiring.

What ketosis began to fix for me:
\- Being Overweight
\- Arthritis
\- Knee Joints
\- Gout
\- Insomnia
\- Chronic Fatigue
\- L4 and L5 injury plus inflammation and sciatica pain, this being the fix that I was told by modern medicine that I had lo live with, taking steroids, injections and pain killers the rest of my life. GONE!

I formed a group with friends and family members to spread the ketosis gospel and got as far as getting great results for some of my family members under this protocol, then one day a total stranger came to my group and began sharing his own experiences, knowledge and books written, there he mentioned cold thermogenesis and Jack Kruse, that was the start then, until today. I have become a sponge of reading and understanding how all of it comes together and actually applying it with optimal results. That is my keto-clarity journey and I will be sticking to it.

I have applied the writings of Dr. Kruse and obtained optimal health, from a metabolic state of derrangement.

Fixed issues after reading Dr. Kruse:

\- Insomnia
\- Brain Fog
\- High Blood Pressure
\- Low T
\- Gray Hair!
\- Inconsistent energy fluctuations throughout the day
\- Emotional stability and increase in empathy.
\- Truly loving myself.

I will keep reading, seeking understanding, applying and living optimally.

That's it.

### alaskatt — 2015-03-20T02:10:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/traceys-journal.13385/post-158994>

Hi! I am not sure if this is where I am supposed to post this but I would love some guidance from all of you very smart folks! I have Hashimoto's, Melasma, and Kallman's Syndrome (a genetic issue that causes a failure of your Hypothalamus to create GNRH). I also have a history of candida, UTI's and constipation. My 38 year old sister was diagnosed with Leukemia on January 9th and has a Stem Cell transplant tomorrow. So, that has sort of light a fire under me that life is short and you need to protect it. My mother has very severe RA, and my sister has Type 1 Diabetes. So obviously we have something going on with AI in our family. I have been gluten free for a long time which helped me loose 35 pounds, however I have tried other diets, and nothing has worked. I look sort of puffy all the time, I don't heal easily, and bruise easily. I have had issues with depression, SAD, mood swings from fertility treatments, etc. I really want to know more about what would be a safe treatment for Hashi's and my Kallman's Syndrome. I am currently on Armour and was on BCP for my lack of estrogen.

I have recently gotten on the keto diet as I would like to lose this last 15 pounds or so and I am doing HIIT workouts and playing soccer. The scale hasn't budged. It's very frustrating... The good news is my energy is way better and I'm sleeping better. I am excited to start learning more and hopefully become a success that can give back to this great community in the future.

# Thread: A Journal of Changes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-journal-of-changes.13506/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AJC — 2015-03-28T00:42:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-journal-of-changes.13506/post-159583>

Hello everyone,

I'm not really the type of person to make a public journal like this, but seeing all the support and enthusiasm of the members here has inspired me to join in. Although my journey back to health is not starting here, I feel that with this I am starting a new and significant chapter.

About me: I am 21 years old, a college student at a large campus finishing up my last year of undergraduate. In the fall I will be moving from Michigan to Tempe, Arizona (right outside of Phoenix) to begin schooling to become a Naturopathic Doctor. My passions are learning and discovery in all branches of natural and social science, and in particular their application to the fostering of well-being in people. I also love finding the overlap of the various branches where it is clear that all knowledge is One. Needless to say, coming across this website has been an unexpected joy.

Now about my health: This is called a Journal of Changes because, fortunately, my life has been one of Changes. (I am feeling energized from just writing this) I have been healthy by all "conventional" standards my life-no major illnesses or ailments, other than a few herniated discs in my lower back and peripheral neurasthenia in my leg as of late, which I have been working on correcting through exercises.

I did, however, eat the SAD--one of the worst versions, including lots of pop energy drinks as a teenager-- from birth up until a few years ago. I also smoked cigarettes for a few years, but have not smoked in nearly two years now. I was also pretty overweight during this time, beginning in about 6th grade through 11th grade. I weighted 233 pounds as a 14 year old at under 6 feet. I also had a very melancholy mood and extremely low energy, feeling exhausted nearly every day. I have gradually lost this excess weight as a result of changing my diet. I now weigh around 160 and am just over 6 feet tall. My energy levels are generally much better and my mood is a million times better.

Despite this, I still feel there are some underlying health matters to address. One: I have very poor circulation, and things like drinking water will sometimes make me extremely cold on the inside. Two: I do get seasonal affective disorder, and come to rely on coffee to motivate me to get my work done. Three: the quality of my sleep is pretty poor, varying from nights of near insomnia to nights where I sleep all the way through. Usually I get to sleep perfectly fine, but will wake up multiple times or wake up at around 2 am wide awake and ready to start the day. 4: My digestion is very poor. I am talking constipation over the course of 3-5 days. This is one of my biggest issues. 5: Despite improved energy levels, I know they are still not where they can and should be, and I am trying to take steps to improve them. 6: No matter how much sleep I get, I have had dark circles under my eyes for as long as I can remember.

I am only recently embarking on a pescatarian version of the epi-paleo diet. I actually do not eat red meat, pork, or fowl, but do enjoy seafood, eggs, and good quality dairy. I eat a lot of vegetables, cooked and raw, some grains and beans as well as nuts (although I have cut them out since coming across this). I am in the process of converting from a sugar burner to a fat burner. I should add that I'm also in the process of optimizing my immediate environment as much as possible.

I'm waiting to go home to see my PCP to get some labs ordered so I can know where to begin on my Journey to optimal.

_One thing I am trying to solve right now:_ I'm not really sure if I'm LR or not, as I don't really think I fail the mirror test and I have no labs to analyze. I'm trying to figure out how to "get my Rolex working" and get into fat-burning mode. I started on the Leptin Rx and have been feeling pretty nauseous after the BAB (especially the day I ate 5 raw eggs + coconut manna). I was planning on pushing through, but since re-reading am not sure if I should continue, because if I'm LS I'm worried that it may be a step in the wrong direction to retrain my brain to use a different circuit than the more evolutionarily recent one. I'm really wondering what I should do in the meantime, the best way to reset my rolex. Any insight into this this would be very much appreciated.

I look forward to joining you all on our continued journey of changes. I look forward to diving into CT and the information here. I'm reading the blog more or less from the beginning (using the Study Guides) and am a couple years behind at the moment, but aspire to become a Silver or Gold member and really get rockin'. I also signed up for the Biohacking 101 e-course so am excited to begin to receive instructions there.

I understand this is a place of sharing, support, and guidance. If anyone would like more information about me, my health, or my history for this purpose, please feel free to ask.

Wishing you all the best,
\--Andrew

### Josh — 2015-03-28T01:21:20+0200

You need to learn to think at the atomic and subatomic level about health, period. Otherwise school will be a series of partial truths at best leading to mediocrity as a practitioner. Thanks Josh, I'll be sure to listen to those. I know that ND school won't be perfect, but as far as I can see it's the best option open. I'll just have to make of it what I will--but it'd be like that in any field I enter. This type of integration isn't really taught outright anywhere, it's up to each of us to connect the dots and innovate for ourselves.

Fitness@home: Love your CS Lewis quote. I imagine the SAD will go away when I move to AZ. The choice of schools for me was between this one and one right outside of Seattle--the sunshine of Arizona was a big draw.

Thank you again for the warm welcome Caroline and Nonchalant.

Looking forward to walking this path with all of you.

### Kathy1967 — 2015-04-04T05:19:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-omega-6-3-ratio-results-ok-for-ct.13634/post-160271>

I just got my results for my O6 O3 ratio. I think it's good, but the results say I'm at high risk because of my 6 level but it appears that my ratio is 5.69 to 1 (06 to o3). I don't take fish oil other than fermented cod liver oil maybe 2 - 4 times monthly. I eat LOTS of bacon (I was a vegetarian until about 12 years ago and didn't understand the loveliness of bacon so I'm making up for lost time!). Doc just said my progesterone was low but I didn't SEE the results yet. When I get those results I'll post them all:

-   progesterone - 0.4
-   cortisol AM - 24 (she said this was high)
-   crp - 0.43
-   vit D - 47.3
-   B12 - 463

So any comments would be appreciated. Does it look like I'm good to go for the CT? I've been doing it but holding off on adding ice to tub (although water has been around 40ish Fahrenheit without ice! - Oh! I have city water and it is fluoridated - it's all I have (boyfriend has well water so I love showering over there), is it still horrible to bath in it when it's cold?

# Thread: It's not about the destination....but, the journey

### mamadell — 2015-04-10T00:04:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-about-the-destination-but-the-journey.13746/post-160757>

so...here we go
my first entry into my journal and another step along this journey...we call life

A New Yorker who found herself in Sedona AZ , I've been living in AZ for almost 10 years now
and, have noticed over the past few years of living here, that i just don't feel 100%

Have been following the Epi Paleo rx for about a year now as far as the diet part which feels quite natural to me...previously, i was following the raw food movement, then vegetarian,vegan and, then one day, i happen to pass the meat counter and started to drool LOL !! so, bought the steak and felt better than i had in years !!

Been concentrating on increasing my DHA intake ...eating quite a bit of fish ( sardines and salmon ) and frozen shrimp.. when i can, i have oysters shipped to me
What i have noticed over the past several years, is that the amount of inflammation in my body is increasing
and , my energy is decreasing
I have Very high oxidized LDL levels , low free T3 levels , very high SHBG levels and all sex hormone levels are very low
Genetic hyperlipidemia , chronic bouts of systemic candida and chronic mucus and phlegm

Have tried CT once and my face turned purple .....finger tips turn white...all my blood pulls away from my extremities...

Have just started listening to all the older webinars and in the meantime, would love to be able to know what specific blood tests i should be asking for other than VAP, complete thyroid etc
Would like to be able to get to the cause of my chronic inflammation ....feels like something has been brewing for 20 years and finally started to show itself as symptoms
The journey continues.....

Have you ever had a really extensive hormone test done? Genova Diagnostics Complete Hormone give a lot of information. It is a 24-hour urine collection. My BHRT doc has me do this annually.

I think @[SeaHorse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/562/) had issues at first with Raynaud's type symptoms when doing CT, but is now able to. Don't give up on it. Maybe use water that is not a cold and ease into it?

Good luck on your journey

been following the leptin RX, CT protocol and mitigating EMF exposure as much as possible

and oxidation has increased

# Thread: A journal of personal alchemy. From something not gold, into gold.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-journal-of-personal-alchemy-from-something-not-gold-into-gold.14529/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### deallen7 — 2015-05-15T20:09:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-journal-of-personal-alchemy-from-something-not-gold-into-gold.14529/post-164378>

Hello Everyone,

For these first 6 journal posts, I aim to provide a complete history and context for my current health struggles. It's just as much an exercise in analysis of my own health history, as it is in providing you all context for helping me probe deeper. Mentally tough for me to let go of this information and allow others such an intimate view of my life, but I am trying to give less f cks in my journey towards optimal health. Who cares about the baggage as long as you can experience a vibrant and healthful life! Thanks in advance for reading.

### deallen7 — 2015-05-15T20:11:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-journal-of-personal-alchemy-from-something-not-gold-into-gold.14529/post-164379>

**Birth (1 of 6):**
September 26, 1988. Normal birth. No C-section and I was breast-fed. Thanks for the bacteria and fat, mom! I was a chubby baby. My mom was the last born of 9 (7 females, 2 males) and grew up on a farm near the beach. My dad was the last born of 3 men. He grew up in the suburbs of a growing city.

History of alcohol abuse on both sides of the family. Makes sense, considering my heritage and mutations.

My parents had 4 males. 3 had red hair, except for me. I was born with blonde hair, and turned out to be the tallest of the bunch. Each of us was born during the range of September 6 - October 1st. There has got to be some interesting reason for why my parents were most fertile during January. Any ideas? Maybe this is an indication of poor redox potential on my Dad's side, and he required the cold to compress his mitochondria so that his redox potential rose to the point where he could spare some of his zinc and create life! Still wrapping my head around some of these concepts...

DNA Mutations (measured in 2015 with 23andme):

METHYLATION

(Homo)
VDR Taq

(Hetero)
MTHFR A1298C
COMT V158M
COMT H62H
MTRR K350A
MTRR A664A
BHMT-02
BHMT-08
CBS A360A

DETOX

(Homo)
GSTP1 I105V
SOD2 A16V

(Hetero)
CYP1B1 L432V
CYP1B1 N453S
CYP1B1 R48G
CYP2A6\*2 1799T>A
CYP2C9\*2 C430T
CYP2E1\*1B 9896C>G
NAT1 R187Q
NAT2 R197Q

**2 of 6 coming soon...**

### fitness@home — 2015-05-15T20:32:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-journal-of-personal-alchemy-from-something-not-gold-into-gold.14529/post-164380>

Welcome deallen 7. Looking forward to reading more.

### caroline — 2015-05-16T05:10:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-journal-of-personal-alchemy-from-something-not-gold-into-gold.14529/post-164405>

Welcome deallen........

### deallen7 — 2015-05-16T16:28:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-journal-of-personal-alchemy-from-something-not-gold-into-gold.14529/post-164435>

**Childhood (2 of 6):**
Relatively clean diet when my mom was cooking. She was too heavy on the grains and light on the saturated fat by today's paleo standards, but she was always very wary of processed foods, artificial dyes and vegetables oils. Diet was heavy on carbs and animal protein. Heavy on bread and other grains. Moderate vegetables. Low on animal fat. I supplemented heavily with candy, popcorn, soda and ice cream whenever there was an opportunity. Never ate butter. Ever. I didn't like it for some reason. I always avoided tomato sauce on my pasta, opting instead for an olive oil, herb and balsamic vinaigrette dressing.

I had a couple of brain traumas as a kid: (1) falling on my bike while riding down a hill and (2) busting open my head on the side of a brick house while playing hide-and-go-seek.

Spent many weekends in a chlorinated pool during the Spring and Summer. Also spent time swimming in most-likely-polluted freshwater lakes and rivers. Didn't know any better at the time...

I spent a lot of time fishing as a kid. We pulled striped bass and catfish out of the Congaree and Wateree Rivers that run around Columbia, SC. Maybe they didn't have the mercury warnings 12 years ago, but these rivers _definitely_ do now. I remember one specific event at a pond near my house. I pulled a HUGE large mouth bass out of the pond. The monster was probably around 8-9 lbs. I took it home and ate it. Can't imagine the heavy metal load I put on myself as a young fisherman through these types of excursions.

Allergies started to develop as a kid along with the brain fog, lethargy and inflammation that occurs with chronic allergies.

Sinus infections that were treated by a total of about 4-5 rounds of antibiotics over the years.

House that I grew up in usually had a fire going during the winter and plenty of soot/smoke would spill back into the house. We always had dogs. I suspect some mold growth as well. I developed asthma.

Socially, athletically, and educationally, I didn’t “thrive” during these younger years. I was not very loud or confident in social situations, was pretty timid while playing basketball and soccer, and I couldn’t focus during school. I had absolutely no idea how to reciprocate attention I began receiving from the opposite sex.

Despite being a very chubby baby, I struggled to gain weight and remained thin with very low body-fat as I grew into adolescence.

### deallen7 — 2015-05-18T21:40:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-journal-of-personal-alchemy-from-something-not-gold-into-gold.14529/post-164634>

**Teen (3 of 6):**
Even while eating poorly for my health requirements, I eventually developed into an excellent athlete and student. I loved football, but I struggled to stand out because of my low weight, so I steered towards soccer. I also ran cross country and was usually playing two sports during any given season.

Grades were very good, despite issues concentrating in class, doing homework and studying.

Developed poor eyesight, really bad acne, chronic stomach pains that always occurred after high-endurance activity. Started to become very sensitive to cold, especially in my extremities. Started to develop slight gynecomastia. I discovered porn.

My hormones were never where I thought they should be. I didn't have the same aggressiveness that I noticed in my friends and I didn't start growing facial hair until late in my teens.

I think I may have had an instance of _Angina Pectoris_ one day on the way to a soccer game, driving on the highway while eating my normal pre-game snack: slices of peanut butter toast. I felt an incredible shocking and gripping pain in my chest that left me feeling very cold and shaky. This was when I was 17 or 18. I drove to my soccer game and played as if everything was normal.

Started to experiment with marijuana and alcohol over the weekends.

Lot's of social anxiety. Terrible conversationalist. Trouble remembering names and faces. Really difficult for me to meet new people. I tended to latch on to good friends, rather than explore new relationships. I continued to struggle with reciprocating positive attention I was receiving from girls.

Really bad sugar handling. Relied on adrenaline or other excitatory hormones to get me through soccer matches. I always started off matches feeling slightly hypoglycemic. It took a few minutes of excitement for me to get into the game.

### deallen7 — 2015-05-20T20:23:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-journal-of-personal-alchemy-from-something-not-gold-into-gold.14529/post-164824>

**College (4 of 6):**
Enrolled at a 4-year University in the Pre-Med program. Joined a fraternity.

Poor grades the first year. Drank too much, stayed up too late, ate horribly, experienced chronic stress that entire first year.

Dropped out of Pre-Med program and started pursuing a BS in Economics.

Grades improved as I devoted more time to studying but cognitive function remained an issue.

Social anxiety remained an issue that I treated with semi-successfully with alcohol. I’m guessing I was self-medicating with GABA to mop up all the excitatory neurotransmitters that were causing the social anxiety.

Continued trouble with names and faces. Memory was pretty poor.

Never seemed to understand why people liked going to football games. Didn’t quite understand the healthy person’s thirst for that type of atmosphere and energy. It’s like my adrenals couldn’t pick up on the energy fields that seemed to be contagious to others.

Seemed to have a bi-polar personality. Some days, I would have phenomenal social days. Really happy, witty and friendly. Other days, I would struggle to get out of bed. Struggle to smile and speak to people.

Asthma got worse.

Arrhythmia developed.

I started dipping smokeless tobacco. Felt terrible doing so but because of my poor neurotransmitter health I easily feel into patterns of addiction.

I remember two distinct nights where I blacked-out and fainted after smoking marijuana. Both instances were after sushi meals heavy in soy sauce.

Turned a corner my Senior year after meeting an absolutely incredible girl. Her mom was big into alternative health and helped shift my health perspectives in a very positive way.

My final year in college I had a major "light-bulb" moment after watching a food documentary. Really sunk in that "Food Matters." I then decided to go on a mediterranean/raw diet. The mediterranean diet was a poor choice, looking back, but the experience was very impactful as it started me on the journey down the path of searching for my perfect diet.

I started experimenting with supplements.

Continued drinking too much and habitually smoked MJ. Quit all tobacco products.

### deallen7 — 2015-05-22T23:45:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-journal-of-personal-alchemy-from-something-not-gold-into-gold.14529/post-165074>

**Past 2 Years (5 of 6):**
Have been consistently eating a paleo diet. High animal protein and fat, moderate carbs from roots and tubers, moderate to low cooked vegetables, occasional raw greens. Was still eating grains but low on gluten. Was eating night shades.

Completely annihilated allergies by cutting out all processed white sugars, adopting a paleo diet and supplementing my diet with psyllium husk fiber and bentonite clay. I think my allergies had a lot to do with intestinal dysbiosis from all the sugar and grains, but even more to do with reliance on ATP-PC system that lead to poor methylation, which lead to a high ammonia load, which lead to high histamine levels. The extra fiber and clay I consistently added to my diet helped mop up the ammonia load and quench some of the histamine release.

Huge improvements in cognitive function, energy and libido, but still not what I felt was "100%".

Massive improvements in social anxiety, confidence and speaking ability. Continued to exhibit a bi-polar social nature. Some days very on, some days off.

Started to consistently have “best day’s ever”. I hated days when I was “off”. I developed the constant desire to hack my diet and supplements to be “on” at the highest possible level.

I began to notice correlations between sex, alcohol, smoking MJ and my mood. It seemed to take me a week to recover from either of these three “demons”.

Major reductions in alcohol consumption. 1-2 drinks every couple of weeks. Absolutely no tobacco.

Completely got rid of porn addiction. Less frequent, er, zinc emissions and only with a partner. Holding on to my zinc has probably been the most impactful thing I have ever done in terms of supporting mood, energy, cognitive function, confidence, hormones, etc. I think that losing the zinc takes away from my redox potential and reduces my methylation status. The zinc seems to be one of the plugs at the bottom of the electron reservoir that, when I am deficient, creates a big gaping hole.

Worked in a very high stress job in Marketing.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Matt's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matts-journal.15784/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mnloeb — 2015-06-24T07:15:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matts-journal.15784/post-169441>

Hi Caroline,

Thanks for the response! Oz is great...spent a semester at University of Melbourne. The Aussies were so kind and gregarious. Anyway, I am going to try my best to be as detailed as possible...hopefully the note isn't too disjointed. Desperately want to regain energy--was a writer before feeling out-of-it/emotionally detached. This is a little scary--having difficulty functioning/communicating. My cognitive processing is limited...just perplexed at how scattered/unreliable my mind has become. Before launching in, I apologize for the self-absorbed nature of this email.

Mom's health/childhood: Mom was the family matriarch. Just a vibrant, big-hearted personality. Unfortunately, she passed away a couple of years ago from pancreatic cancer. Her resolve was something else. Grandma Shirley passed away from Alzheimer's disease; she was incredibly gifted--graduated from college at 19. I have two younger brothers. Neither have had mental health issues. One is working in Chicago and the other is living/looking for a job in Austin. They can sense a transformation in my personality over the past couple months.

I believe I had a normal birth--can't recall if I was breastfed. Childhood was great--I excelled in school and was active in high school activities. I had some anxiety/nervousness at university but nothing like this...performed well and led a couple organizations in undergraduate.

I am solely drinking tap water...just added some kosher salt. For breakfast, I typically have a banana and/or oatmeal. Yogurt has become a diet staple. Granola bars as well. For dinner, I used to make pasta but my digestive system has become sensitive. I may have a burrito and complement it with some type of vegetable--namely carrots. Cooking ability is limited so have been going out to dinner...probably too frequently. I started downing protein shakes over the last couple days...but the inertia/apathy has remained. It is taking all my power to compose this email...know this isn't a Pulitzer Prize effort.

Sleep can be fitful. Because of bouts with insomnia, I have been taking Ambien to go to sleep after trying Melatonin. Unfortunately, the apartment isn't totally dark. Libido has been good. It is the one thing I am not worried about

nnEMF: I am not sure what this is--just tried Googling it but didn't spot any pertinent articles.

Sunlight: The weather has been cooperative in Seattle. I believe I get a sufficient amount of sunlight each day. Not sure how feasible it is to get to the ocean...sitting at the beach and unwinding sounds great.

C/T: I haven't had a C/T scan. With my insurance, I am not sure how feasible that is.

Exercise: Energy level has been too low for regular exercise. I have been going on lengthy walks, although balance has become an issue. Throughout my childhood and early 20s, I was a big athlete--from tennis to basketball to weight-lifting. When I try to exercise on the elliptical for five minutes, I become absolutely exhausted--bordering on collapse. Thinking consumes all my energy...believe I am just completely drained.

Books/podcasts/membership: I don't have Dr. K's book...but thanks for the recommendation. I am having difficulty concentrating and reading but will do my best to pick up his book. I do listen to podcasts--although concentration wanders.

Thanks again for the gracious response. I have never experienced anything like this...just got off the phone after a quick, five-minute conversation and realized how out of it/anxious I sounded. I have withdrawn from people because of my lack of mental acuity and pangs of anxiety. Would love to get a little bit of my mind back. Hoping for the best for the countless other people on this site. Thanks again.

Matt

### mnloeb — 2015-06-24T07:33:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matts-journal.15784/post-169442>

It has been inspiring to read other posts. Kudos to all the success stories! Sorry about the constant use of "I" in my post--typically not this needy but know this isn't myself.

### Lahelada — 2015-06-24T14:11:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matts-journal.15784/post-169461>

Good morning Matt and welcome. You just made me realise how used we are to talking in our own jargon so let me clear that up for you first.

nnEMF = non native Electromagnetic Frequencies (without the nn you would probably have found it )
CT = Cold Thermogenesis

Reading your journal your reliance on carbohydrates jumps out at me. I wouod uggets that you get Dr Ks book .It is probably the easiest read o isten to some of his podcasts .You can find them on the homepage.
There are some immediate changes I would make
1\. include fish in your diet,a lot of it . Oyters if you can swing them would be great,sardines in cans are absolutely fine as a fall back. Throw out oats ,any grain and bread products,no rice beans or pasta . Pork and bacon are your friends . grassfed butter and cheese maybe your friend. You will need to experiment with the latter.
You are in summer so technically would not need to live a ketogenic diet but you might decide to do so to give yourself a kickstart.

2.Water : How much do you drink ? Tapwater is not th best for rehydration.Try a mineral water or reverse osmosis water. Dr K swears by reverse osmosis .

3\. invest in blackout blinds if you can,failing that a sleep mask .Keep the bedroom cool .
Keep all electrical gadgets out of the bedroom. Consider switching apartment lighting to amber lights. Invest in blueblocker glasses or if you cannot swing that yellow welders safety glasses.Think Elvex or Uvex. Do not expose yourself to wifi or blue light after sundown if you can avoid it. If you use the PC switch it off at least one hour before bedtime . Try to make bedtime no later than 10 pm .If you cannot sleep that early you can read with a red light.

I am out of ideas.I may have forgotten something but it is a good start for clearing your head and working out what your longterm strategy will be .

Say "I" a lot . Without an "I" there would be no "he" or "she".

### mnloeb — 2015-06-25T03:33:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matts-journal.15784/post-169507>

Thank you for the detailed, helpful response. Just a wealth of information...all of your tips are sensible. Have these recommendations changed your life? CT is fascinating. If you don't own a bathtub, can sitting down during a cold shower be an adequate substitute?

I solely drink tap water but find myself dehydrated. Is there a specific type of mineral water you recommend? It sounds like ingesting salt is a negative--even if you perspire right?

Light therapy jogs my brain but difficult to tote the device outside the apartment. Will practice deep breathing and mindfulness--trying to induce more calmness.

Thanks again.

Matt

### caroline — 2015-06-25T04:18:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matts-journal.15784/post-169515>

Hi Matt ...good job on the journal ....

Have you had a look at the easy start guides on the home page?
Take a look at the leptin reset ....your day needs to look like this.....

first thing when you get up .... go outside with as little clothes as possible. You need the early morning sunlight on your body.

Have a BAB within 30 minutes of rising ...... big ass breakfast of protein and good fats

You need tons and tons of seafood \[ your diet sucks! btw\] ditch the sugar and lousy carbs
you can buys tins of sardines and oysters and salmon and mackerel etc. etc. have these for breakfast with a couple of eggs and bacon
Do you have a crockpot? you could throw a leg of pork in at nite before you go to bed and wake up to breakfast!

lots of bottled spring water

you absolutely need blue blocker glasses - very reasonable on amazon

Get all electronics out of your bedroom .....TV, iPad, iPhone, clock radio etc etc.

keep reading and asking questions! Dr. K. always tells us there are no stupid questions ...if we don't know we must ask.

There is a great thread on the importance of newbies.

### fitness@home — 2015-06-25T20:16:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matts-journal.15784/post-169581>

> Sleep can be fitful. Because of bouts with insomnia, I have been taking Ambien to go to sleep after trying Melatonin.

Hi Matt,

Ambian = bad for me. In 2008 I had a period on very poor sleep. Doctor prescribed this and at first I thought," This is wonderful. How nice to get some sleep!" By the 4th or 5th month it really started to have a negative affect on me. Severe brain fog, trouble waking up, forgetfulness (had to make notes to myself at work)...

One morning I was even smacking myself on the face while driving to keep myself awake/aware. Never again! In my case, the negatives outweighed the positives.

Lah & Caroline have given you some good advice. Dig in and started reading here and ask questions.

### eddy meadows — 2015-07-18T09:18:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.16484/post-172228>

Hi all,before I get started I would just like to say I'm new to all this and finding it all a bit foreign,so please stick with me!!
I'm a 36yr old male from England,and I'm looking for a way to improve mine and my families life
I am 6ft 1 inch tall and weigh a little over 13 stones(pounds and kilos???) i am and always have been lean and live a very active lifestyle
I have been trying to change to the paleo diet recently but I have so many questions,the biggest being am I suited to it?? I had my gallbladder removed when I was 27,due to bad diet etc and I've seen quite a few articles telling me paleo would be a no no,I've also spoken to people that say I would be fine, it all leaves me slightly worried though,as I'm not 100percent which way to turn.....
I do know that something in my diet must change though as recently I've started to notice changes in my gut,if anyone can help or offer advice please do
Thanks for reading

# Thread: Two steps forward, one back, repeat!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-back-repeat.16752/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RobH — 2015-08-21T15:03:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-back-repeat.16752/post-175285>

I guess I ought to have an optimal journal also. Should keep me accountable I think, and that is necessary when life seems overbusy and I get distracted with the details.

Here was my first try. When I read through it somehow feels like I need to start it over.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/)

A lot has transpired since then, and I probably don't have time to go into all of it right now, although bits and pieces are scattered throughout various places in the forum. Suffice to say I've had a couple steps back but I'm moving forward slowly but steadily again.

Suffice to say the Kruse Cruise was a defining moment. Still working off that momentum.

Not feeling too optimal right now. Working night shift. Luckily not too busy so I have a little time to catch up on reading, Wim Hof Video, Quantlet videos, etc. Organizing, etc. Drinking a LOT of water, using FLUX on computers and trying to minimize the damage with blue blockers. Nicotine gum not coffee tonight.

Being here at work is a window into the cognitive and physical devolution of the human race. Sad. I'm don't mind taking care of acutely ill and injured folks, but at night like we see a lot of "frequent flyers" who continue to abuse themselves and are back over and over again for the same self-induced ailments at all hours because it's convenient for them and we are here to serve "with a smile" as mandated by our hospital system and the all important patient satisfaction score. Ultimately it makes me feel abused, frustrated and angry that I am trashing my circadian biology being here to take care of them when I should have been asleep when the sun went down. I know that may shock and horrify some of you, and I may seem uncompassionate (I'm not) or judgmental, but it is hard to give infinitely of yourself (when you know the consequences) to people who don't respect themselves enough to try to do even a little bit better. I spend a lot of time trying to educate and inform, but many see the same ones back again with the same problems. Some people are incorrigible and don't care to get better. Vampires of sorts. There are some (about 20-30%) who need to be here tonight and I am happy to give them everything I can. I guess I need to focus on those and the reason I am here.

My career evolution plan continues to fall nicely into place...

I look forward to the day when I know I'll sleep in my own bed (on my magnetico if I can kick the dogs off -- they love it) every night.

Found a great sale locally on "BBQ" oysters (the big ones) from Willapa Bay, WA today. 50 cents each! Tomorrow's recuperation plan after tonight's circadian insult is a dozen oysters for lunch, a nap in the sun and some CT in the tub if the kids haven't used all the ice by then (now they want ice baths!). Maybe a little targeted red light or time in IR sauna with red lights on. Will start looking into aquarium cooler ala The Quantum Radical.

Watched Mobilize. No surprises other than the focus on tumors not much on the metabolic and other insidious effects of EMF (although they did mention the mouse experiments and behavioral problems). No phones for our kids despite enormous peer pressure. I wish it would have been surprising but from what I've learned now it is all to be expected. I have diagnosed 3 brain tumors (right sided, right hand dominant folks) in the last 3 months, and I don't work THAT many shifts.

Thrive next on the list.

Big stack of books on my desk! Nick Lane's books, Healing Sunlight, etc. Great stuff.

Fires burning west of us and air is awful this week. Yes, California is a lousy place to be and we won't be here forever, but don't have a solid plan or timeframe yet and pretty heavily invested in a variety of ways so it will take time to extricate. Supposed to be a big El Nino year (RAIN! SNOW!) and we live in a pretty darn nice rural spot (way north) where water isn't such a huge issue ... yet. There are some great water falls, rivers and natural CT spots around us and some amazing wilderness areas. Will have to look hard to find a better spot in many ways. Of course the coming big earthquakes as the central valley sinks (historically not much in our locale, thankfully) could accelerate that process....

Going to cut way back on coffee... I like it but don't (think) I need it and it seems (thanks Gretchen!) that will be a bit of a biohack for the next few months until my next lab draw. I can live without it.

I can't wait for winter and our lake to get cold again. Not quite ocean CT but is it pretty nice.

Got some labs recently. CRP still high (7 -- arrrrggghhh!)(likewise homocysteine) despite DHA every day and sun since the cruise (vit D 50+ no supplements) since the cruise so I need to dig deeper into the EMF in my my work / home environment, maybe look at a few more labs, and needless to say, CTCTCT. I know I'm missing something.

Oops they're triaging another and I've got to dictate so I can get out of here when relief arrives.

Sorry about the supposed randomness of these thoughts... gotta (re)start somewhere.

Thanks to all of you. I appreciate being part of this community.

RobH

### nicld — 2015-08-21T18:45:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-back-repeat.16752/post-175299>

I just read The Healing Sunlight and it is an awesome book. Makes me wonder how we can get back to using the sunlight to heal since we have seem to gone away from it. Hospitals especially need this.

I now know that when I build my next house, it will be built to maximize the sun.

### caroline — 2015-08-22T04:18:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-back-repeat.16752/post-175358>

So glad you are here Rob ... you are such a bright light - Thanks for all you give...... and what you share.

Get those Dogs outa your bed for crying out loud!

### Joe Gavin — 2015-08-24T22:51:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-back-repeat.16752/post-175575>

Cool to read Rob! I quit the coffee a couple of weeks ago based on a post by Jason L on the FB page and so far pretty painless. Work in EMF hell and well, in NJ too. Hear you on the extrication plans. Thanks for sharing!
