# Thread: Unfortunate recent reaction to oysters -
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unfortunate-recent-reaction-to-oysters.16792/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JodyT2 — 2015-08-29T09:05:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unfortunate-recent-reaction-to-oysters.16792/post-176013>

I've been buying oysters for a while now. Very reliable source, I eat them over the days from Sunday to Wednesday. Zero problems.

Then 2 weeks ago something changed. We were having a heat wave and our refridge was working overtime to try to keep things cold. I knew the fridge had zones that were colder than others and it was particularly challenging that day because I had come home with 5 doz and I put them in separate bowls in various places in the fridge (I have 2 roommates so I can't hog every space). I have a refridge thermometer that I bought for CT but I keep it in the fridge, so I was able to verify temps and could see the struggle the fridge was having, hitting almost 55˚ even though it's set at 40˚. Lower sections of the fridge were colder so I was trying to put the oysters lower. The house has essentially no insulation so we tend to not use A/C much. But, we eventually put it on. This ended up over-cooling the fridge in places and I was pretty certain that some of the oysters froze... but it was such a gentle freeze that it wasn't like there was any ice on anything. However, the wet towels I put over the oysters to keep them from drying out... those had frozen in the way that wet fabric freezes into a shape. I didn't know if this would have insulated the oysters? Or if this was proof they froze. My roommate and I thought it over and decided to just eat a bunch that evening because we didn't want them to go to waste. Mentally, I found that I was having a bit of a hard time eating them though. I didn't want to be eating them, and they just weren't going down great. Neither of us got sick and my roommate was thrilled to have them. He seemed fine. But I couldn't say why exactly, but I just had this mental thing. Something was turning me off.

I still had some unfrozen oysters left over that seemed to be ok, and I ate those later in the week. If I'm remembering correctly, those did not sit well in my stomach but rather, made my stomach hurt. I did not get sick, it just made my stomach hurt and it took the better part of the day for me to feel better. I had made a protein smoothie thing a while after eating the oysters. It's a product my doctor has me taking. I thought maybe the combo of those just didn't work or something but I've been doing the protein thing for a while too. None of this stuff is new, and I'm pretty sure I've eaten them in close proximity before.

So, fast forward to this week. I bought oysters again at the Farmers Market on Sunday, brought them home and I ate some on Monday. Stomach ache again. I made sure to not have the protein smoothie thing. This time I had bought some different, larger oysters though. I thought maybe it was the 'new' oysters that weren't working for me. I had some others types remaining yet that were smaller which I have had many times before, so I decided to eat those later in the week, on Thursday (I didn't eat any oysters in between Mon & Thur). I was very careful to not eat anything around that time so as to not upset my stomach with other things. Still ended up with a stomach ache. I've since eaten shrimp and salmon with no reaction. Have not yet tried mussels, but seafood seems fine.

As a weird aside, I noticed recently... starting around the same timing as the oyster problem... that my nose is boogering up more. This is totally different than what I feel I have been experiencing for the last year (which was a low booger rate), so it's a noticeable change. It's not loose snot, it's the crusting up you get in the nose either when you wake up, or throughout the day. I have a nose ring so I'm perhaps a bit more aware of this than others.

I am not sure what all this means, but it is a noticeable difference that I can't attribute to anything. Maybe they aren't related but... I'm thinking they are. I'm not having trouble eating anything else... seems to just be the oysters. That is super unfortunate because I've been noticing improvements with my skin in general so I KNOW I was having a positive reaction to them.

I am supposed to be having blood drawn again - I have the paperwork and just need to go in for the draw so I will have something to reference, but I'm not sure it will point to anything. What would change with oysters if they are slightly frozen? They were eaten within just hours of potentially freezing and kept very cold the whole time... just don't know what might have changed??? Or, any ideas what tests could be done?

Any help appreciated -

Jody

### fitness@home — 2015-08-29T15:53:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unfortunate-recent-reaction-to-oysters.16792/post-176017>

Jody, can't really comment on the oysters but wanted to say that sometimes my body seems to need a break from certain foods. After binging heavily on Gulf shrimp for the last couple of months, I noted my body doesn't seem to like it as much, nor do i desire it.

When this happens I just take a break from that particular item (has happened with salmon and sardines before). In a few weeks I'm craving it again.

How about I eat your share of oysters for dinner today and you go for the shrimp I am no longer craving?

### JodyT2 — 2015-09-05T22:41:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unfortunate-recent-reaction-to-oysters.16792/post-176567>

Hi Fitness@home-
Thanks for your comments. I've seen a few people mention needing to take a break from things. I'm hoping that is what is happening at the moment. I see how it seems to be tied to that event of them freezing but I don't really know what to think about it. I'll just do that - - take a break - - - and gladly eat your shrimp!
Thanks

### cinnamon — 2015-09-06T03:12:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unfortunate-recent-reaction-to-oysters.16792/post-176587>

Hi Jody!

I'm wondering if your oysters were already shucked. I don't think they last too long in the fridge (maybe a couple of days?) once they have been shucked. And if the fridge wasn't working too well, they might have been a bit off.

On the other hand, if you were shucking them yourself and they were still firmly closed before you used your shucking knife, they were probably okay.

Even if they were okay, I have found that if you have a bad experience, it's best to back off for a few days. It's kind of like your gut reacts and says "Oh no, remember what happened last time!!"


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Celeste's journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/celestes-journal.16820/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Celeste — 2015-09-04T17:23:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/celestes-journal.16820/post-176477>

Relatively new back on the forum. I am eating fairly consistently epipaleo. I've been doing two to three meals a day. One is usually seafood (shrimp, scallops, oysters or fish). A lot of times I make the seafood into a ceviche, with a little cucumber, avocado, onion or garlic, and of course lemon. The other meal is usually grass fed bison organs, meat and marrow, with a little fermented veggies. For fats at the moment I'm using coconut oil, raw butter, the marrow, avocado, occasionally olive oil and cacao butter. I try as much as possible to eat everything raw, which I've been doing over 13 years.

I've fairly consistently been wearing my blue-b lockers after dark. Also I feel it now when I eat after dark, and the next day get back on track. I need to go and buy the blackout shades for our bedroom. When I was first reading on the forum before, I had the house assessed for EMF. We corrected just about everything focusing first on the worst problems. I do have a amber bulb in each room, including the bathroom. I love the cozy feel.

Not sure why I am having new symptoms. I noticed recently my lower jaw is inflamed along the root line. Usually never have dental issues. But it is moving a long the jaw. One area becomes inflamed and then another area gets inflamed and the prior area heals. My thoughts on what it might be:
● a reaction to having the plate in my wrist removed. Body detoxing the metal?
● A reaction to supplements. Just started taking more in the last week or so, just when this reaction got intense. Starting today will cut everything but the DHA and a raw chorella I've been using to detox the titanium.
● Or it might be the alkaline water (coral source of minerals) that I buy at the water store. If the other things don't help will switch to getting a spring water delivered. I've been using that water about a month.
● Oh, and I also recently started doing tea. Usually organic green or puerh with a spoon of coconut oil and raw butter. Usually have not done caffeine, so new to me. I only do it right after my morning or lunch meals, not in between.

Okay, out to start my day. Just wanted to get this out.

I guess the good part is the value of our house going up. Maybe eventually we can relocate after I retire. There were areas along the central coast with no color. Eventually do you think we'll develop technology to undo the damage?

Last night I was upset as I will often ask my husband is the WiFi off, and he says yes. I took out my meter and checked and it was relatively high on the lower range (0-3 milligauss). Still on, even though he thought it was off.

Not his fault, as I'm the one who cares about this stuff. He thinks we still need it for Roku. He helped me set up my tablet to run without it.

At the end of the month I start a training in Ojai, a bit away from LA. Have to check out the levels there and see what difference it makes.

# Thread: Yec'hed mat

**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 34

### thomas — 2015-09-14T14:49:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-177095>

Continuing my journal that started in meet and greet there:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/yeched-mat.15266/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/yeched-mat.15266/)

I have spent the past week really awfully tired and depressed (my thought go crazy when I am tired, can barely function, Life really really sucks then).
Dunno what triggered it. Might be nnemf, might be bacteria/virus/parasite, or just decreasing light levels (lower and lower uv in france.)
I slept well tonight and today I am ok, back to a happier more relaxed state. To do that I went to bed around 8 pm solar time, which is as early as I 've ever gone to bed in my adult life as I remember.
Pretty sure going to bed at the same time everyday is bad advice in yearly cycles. As we approach september 21, we get to the maximum declining levels of light per day. Seems to me I need to go to bed earlier and earlier till december.

Concerning that topic I am researching:
\-How to never sleep like shit in the first place. Have consistent good sleep even if I fuck up.
\-When I sleep like shit, how to restore brain function.

\---

One thing I've found in the past month, I try to spend the first 2-3 hour of the day outdoors (eating, walking...). It really does improve my mood a lot.

\---

Seems there's a reason may-june-july were and are still my favorite months of the year.. Little clothing and uv.. feels good.

### Inger — 2015-09-14T18:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-177102>

I go to bed at 8.30 or 9 PM once in a while lately..... but mostly 9 to 9.30 PM... I LOVE to go to bed as soon as it gets dark/as it starts to get dark. It just feels so right.

I usually know why my sleep is disturbed.....
for me it is
too late eating...... like 7 PM is too late. I prefer nothing after 5 PM
too much nicotine/caffeine/alcohol (shame on me )
Too late work/too much stress = high cortisol I think

if I fuck up I usually do not sleep too well and that is fine when I know that. A few days or even a day of great living restores me fast tho, thankfully!
But to be honest I have had way too many sins this summer..oh man. What to do..lol

To restore crazy fast I start with morning light outside grounding.... ditching computer.... walking in the woods at sunset.... lots of water... raw ball smoothies / fish heads / wild herbs / oysters / raw heart..... lots of good stuff and raw! Always gives me energy.. especially high hearts. They are awesome! And then I eat a early dinner and go early to bed and I am new again

I think we can still enjoy the natural light, a lot  even if it gets darker.... we just need to spend time outside.. like you do.
It is funny... we all can tolerate different amounts of "cheating" and we do feel it when it is too much, no?
well.. then we have to stop..lol take a break and suck us full of energy again
I cant tolerate too much and I start to feel so awful, uh. But I do restore very fast, which is awesome
My bad is, I close to never have a day off from work, most weeks I work 7 days. So I need to be pretty strict as my life is so busy already. There is not too much time to restore, because you need time, to be outside in the wild. That is why I need to be careful not to do too much wrong.
Right now it is raining here.... but I lay on my floor grounding at my open terrace door so get natural light on my skin/eyes as I am on the computer
and tonight I will go to bed latest at 8.30 PM for sure... cant wait to go out to that silent dark horse barn.. man I love to sleep there

I am doing pretty well today.

I think in the beginning of the week, Got some of the best sleep and alertness of my life. Then seen some family, started to eat Raclette at 10 pm, in tons of powerfull leds/fluo lights.
That fucked me up. Took me a good 2/3 days to recover. Today fine again.
Eating after 5-6 pm = Hell.
Artificial light I just can't stand it.
I think I transformed into a morning type of person. Typical social activities in the evening are so fun, but so not worth it.
Now That I know what good sleep is like, I just search consistency.

Being outdoors most of the morning is a pretty big transformation. More and more outdoors = happier.
On that point pretty confused about living indoor/outdoors/shelter.
I know I want to be outdoors most if not all the time. (at least during daylight, but I love the stars too)
But we grow up and live in an indoors society.
I am so used to living in an house, but envisioning a lifestyle without..
Oh well..

I know I can handle fall/winter much better than a year ago. but still creating my winter Rx. still thinking about moving in between tropics when the darkest monthes come.

Albert Camus

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-06T21:00:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-178753>

You see, Thomas. I quote a British rock song and B612 quotes a great French writer. No contest.

### b612 — 2015-10-06T21:22:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-178754>

But all we want is to make you feel better

### thomas — 2015-10-06T21:58:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-178757>

thanks guys^ it's fun to make catchy sentances isn't it.

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-07T15:31:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-178807>

> [thomas said:](/goto/post?id=178757)
>
> thanks guys^ it's fun to make catchy sentances isn't it.

Well, I was soundly beaten, Thomas, as mine was only from a rock song.

It was the first line of Cream's _Tales of Brave Ulysses_. I think _he_ was in my mind because I've been re-reading the _Odyssey_ - or rather listening to it as an audio book this time. I'd just got up to the encounter with the cyclops. I found it quite interesting to listen to, because I'd forgotten that the cyclopes were said not to till barley, which just grows wild around them, and to "have no civility". They're "wild men" of a sort - though they keep sheep.

What I thought was interesting was that they're solitary. It made me think of the 17th and 18th century contract thinkers, who envisaged the "original state" of man, whether positively or negatively, as a solitary one. Nothing could be further from the truth, of course. And a human being in a truly solitary state wouldn't even be recognisably human - as your countryman Jean Marc Gaspard Itard found with Victor of Aveyron (_L'Enfant Sauvage_).

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jean\_Marc\_Gaspard\_Itard](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jean_Marc_Gaspard_Itard)

### thomas — 2015-11-15T15:59:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-182029>

Slept like shit the all week. Have not been so low in a long time.
Guess what? Yersterday I found I had a tablet computer "on" 1 meter from my brain at night (I used it few days ago). Shut it down, put it in another room. Slept just fine, feel much better.

Is it just a coincidence?
Well for me that certainly works to transform the nnemf issue from a potential possibility into a personal truth. I am gonna take it much more seriously.

### thomas — 2015-11-15T16:03:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-182030>

Apart from that what I've spent the vast majority of my time doing lately is thinking/researching where to move at least till march/april.
I will probably write about that later, but that might take a lot of pages.

### thomas — 2015-11-17T14:11:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-182165>

I am a weirdo, 1/4 Vietnamese - 3/4 Celt.
I have deep blue eyes, white skin, reddish beard, but pretty dark hair, and a skinny southeast-asian figure(think bruce lee) And I tan very easily, and very deeply. Does that mean my ability to get sun is fucked, or the opposite enhanced?
Maybe I can never get enough sun at high lattitudes. Maybe I always get too much sun at low lattitudes. Maybe I got the best of both worlds, adaptation to both little sun and lot of sun. Who knows?? Only I can...

### thomas — 2016-01-19T13:54:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-186728>

It's now clear to me, the time of the year I feel best is late August/ September. There I can think the best, feel happiest, get the most motivation.
I think it's also the time when Vitamin D is the highest(in mid-latittude-north-hemishpere), and when I got the most accumululated sun. Coincidence? I think not.

If we consider that 40-50deg solar angle is the minimum to make significant amount of vitamin D, then we need to go south of 16-26deg lattitude to make D all year long.(north hemisphere).

### thomas — 2016-01-29T15:19:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-187611>

This morning I got a few ideas to cure some of the mental clutter that plague me from time to time...
Comparing yourself to others, or to most people = Conformity = The most dangerous force = Fastlane to mediocrity = Guaranteed mental clutter
You have your own path among an infinite number, Every one has his own unique path. Some people achieve in a week what others won't even do in their lifetime. the only difference is what's in between their ears and how they use it.
Don't compare yourself to others, instead take mindsets, ideas and behavioral cues from people that inspire you.

### thomas — 2016-02-28T16:02:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-189917>

Brain is booting... NICE The 120 days around the winter solstice absolutely sucks tho.
Largest mistake of the year, not getting out of europe before the light gets too low and I am too lethargic to move my ass.
My most likely destination next october is SouthEast Asia unless I change my mind. Right now probably gonna spend the next month in morocco. Then April-september traveling around europe. Not fixed plans yet, but that's just the idea.
BTW traveling to the right countries often cost less money than living at home in the first world.

### Jenelle — 2016-02-28T19:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-189923>

thomas ~ **how cool** that you are able to travel the world. So many of us are "stuck" (or so it seems).

I read a post from you about the 4-hour Workweek book, and I've got it in my Amazon cart to read someday. I am intrigued. Thank you.

### thomas — 2016-02-28T20:33:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-189930>

You know it's just about removing the bullshit beliefs in the head.. Seriously you can live for 3 months in se-asia with 1 month of minimal income from france, that's something anyone can do. I realised from the people that think they can't do what I do, that it's also true for the things I think I can't do, but other people do. the truth is more like if someone can do something, there's a high probability you/I can do it too.
Check: it's pretty accurate on the cost of living by country.
[http://www.numbeo.com/cost-of-living/rankings\_by\_country.jsp](http://www.numbeo.com/cost-of-living/rankings_by_country.jsp)
also check "life nomadic" on tynan.com. It's good principles on travel.
But I cheat, I am a single guy. That's why I can't really give advice to families with fixed income and jobs and schools. Tho I have seen a lot of families traveling too(with kids homeschooled as well).. There are a lot of ways to live and spend your days and get people to give you money for sure!

4 hour work week will definitely give you good principles. Maybe the most packed powerful book I've ever read.

### thomas — 2016-05-25T19:15:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-195330>

Just remembered my formula for exteme sexual drive that I discovered by accident at christmas.
it's simple.. duck liver+oysters in large enough quantities. Guys that will make your balls want to explode, ladies I dunno LOL.

Btw these days i think a lot of health as a point based system.(thanks to scompy I think) exemple..
Get some sun you get some credit.
Go to bed to late with artifical light you get some(big) debit.
Based on it a lot of people are in the negative of course.

### Inger — 2016-05-25T21:52:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-195347>

love your sex drive formula...LOL add nude sunbathing and your balls _will_ explode....
My fiance decided to stop eating oysters in Nice after our first oyster dinner as it was too powerful together with the mediterranean sun he just could not even sleep anymore his hormones were boiling too much... lol i will never forget that   I thought it was so cute and I would not have minded him have even more oysters....

I eat liver and oysters frequently... love them both... makes me feel just awesome

I like your point system / health  that is how I think too... like.. I need to recharge my battery and I can consciously de-charge it with... doing things like eating late.. staying inside etc. and then I have to recharge again...because I do not like how I feel..lol

### thomas — 2017-12-14T21:44:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.16857/post-230321>

So I just returned from 3 weeks in fuerteventura. This island got a great energy. Lots of volcanoes, lots of sun, lots of ocean and waves. Cool people. Not too many wifi&cell antenas, I barely used internet there. hell lots of areas without signal even. I can definitely recommand the canaries for the european people, easy and cheap to go, rather inexpensive too.
Other than that I have peroids where I follow the health principles of this community better and others where I just fall off (to the point of living late at night, sometimes).

On another topic.
So I tried working out again. Not much just some calisthenics like chins, dips, one leg squats in nature going to maybe 80% of muscular failure.(leaving 2 reps in the bank). 3x a week.
A bit on this model:

(some inspiration)
Unfortunately got a bit too tired after a week. Like hell kind of tired, no motivation, no happy energy, not worth it. Really sucks actually. So I stop for now. It's ok, it's a symptom and a health clue, no need to get angry or depressed. Now just need to find the roots of this problem and play health detective top hopefully fix it. So I am asking the members here if they have clues where I should direct my search and solutions maybe? I don't even know if it has a name? fatigue?

peace(of mind). thomas.

# Thread: Jumping In - Gut issues, Hyperthyroidism, Anxiety, Sleep Issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jumping-in-gut-issues-hyperthyroidism-anxiety-sleep-issues.17501/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Coreena — 2016-02-07T00:53:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jumping-in-gut-issues-hyperthyroidism-anxiety-sleep-issues.17501/post-188447>

Hello to All,

I'm SO new to JK Forums, but decided to go ahead and start my journal. I'm not even done reading the book...

My stats & history:
Age: 34
Ht/Wt: 5'3", 120 lbs

Current Health Issues: Sensitive to Gluten, possible leaky gut (need to get some labs done), Slight hyperthyroidism with nodules. This is at the forefront of my mind right now because I saw the doc last week and the nodules have gotten bigger. Albeit no one could look at my thyroid and notice the difference, but it's never good when nodules on your thyroid get bigger. Am getting labs done this month to see the blood levels. I've never had to take medicine for it and I'm determined to FIX THIS NOW. Especially since AI stuff seems to run in my family...
I can get stressed/overwhelmed easily and I'm a perfectionist, so I can really make my own head explode. Which can cause sleep issues. Sleep issues aren't very bad at all, but really, none of my "issues" are. My goal is to stop the minor problems from EVER becoming major problems. I want to feel great for as many years as I possibly can.

Diet: Strict paleo for the last 10 months. Did GAPs for 5 months before cutting out dairy (except for ghee and a bit of heavy cream). Had a month (Dec) where I basically binged on peanut butter cups...don't ask me why... and I quickly saw the ill effects. So that is all out of my system. Getting back to what works. Strict paleo, and this time around, more seafood, cutting out nut butter, heavy cream and the little bit of honey in my coffee every morning. Just keeping ghee and seeing how I feel. Might even cut out the coffee altogether during leptin RX, and replace with KeVita. Again....have to finish the book! Have been eating big breakfast, no snacking after dinner for past week.

Have starting hacking environment too - wired ethernet, landline, unplugging things, blue blockers, amber bulbs, more sunshine, more water, etc.

Glad to be here....

### nonchalant — 2016-02-07T01:46:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jumping-in-gut-issues-hyperthyroidism-anxiety-sleep-issues.17501/post-188449>

Welcome Coreena. I had an enlarged thyroid a couple years ago. I took Armour thyroid for about a year, but now I'm off of it and my thyroid is good (both in the way it feels and in my labs). I think sunshine was what turned it around, but it could be a combination of Jack's protocols.

Your stress might also ease up, with improved health. Mine sure did. I used to overreact to minor problems. In fact, that is one of the symptoms that I watch for, because it is an early warning of environmental issues for me. My ability to tolerate temperature swings is another. Next time you want to binge on p.b. cups, you need to see what is wrong around you. December wasn't because you were weak or bad.

### caroline — 2016-02-07T12:16:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jumping-in-gut-issues-hyperthyroidism-anxiety-sleep-issues.17501/post-188469>

Welcome Coreena .....

### marlowe — 2016-02-29T23:56:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jumping-in-gut-issues-hyperthyroidism-anxiety-sleep-issues.17501/post-190003>

I am also pretty new here with a lot of the same issues you have. It seems like improving the negative environmental effects is the best way to overcome the hyperthyroid stuff, food has less of an impact than I always thought.

# Thread: On the grueling path to Optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-grueling-path-to-optimal.17535/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PathFinder — 2016-02-11T08:59:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-grueling-path-to-optimal.17535/post-188751>

I hope to write journal as my ability to communicate allows. Communication, thinking, decision making and focus are uphill tasks and the key issues along with sleep. Sometime it is even impossible to write a single line

I have great hope from this forum (in fact this is the only forum which discusses and have possible explanation for my illness). Reading the forum/website gives me strong clues about my problem, Problem is lack of roadmap My idea is to put as much info as possible

Background:

53 yrs old male. Since early age worked in chemical industry and shift work that triggered this disease. Problem persists since teens and early 20’s. Slender built weight 130lbs. Gallbladder taken out 12 yrs ago.

Current physical and mental health, status

Insomnia: Sleep fall Ok, not too much effort-However In a typical night when sleep starts at 10:30p then wake up at 1:50a-3:30a and 4:30am to empty bladder. So I sleep 3,4 and 5 hrs depending upon number of times I wake up. It is difficult to go back to sleep at 4:30a, wakeup feeling is like a switch is flipped on. And sleep is shallow for most part. Hands are always cold even before since when this insomnia and adrenal burned out didn’t set in. Muscle injury doesn’t heal at all. I have damaged muscle from weight training for more than year even when I have abandoned it completely. Food is like an 800 lb gorilla to deal with, Current food: I am on low grains diet. Take small amount of rice or wheat twice a week. My staple is black chickpeas with protein (eggs, mutton, beef, chicken and fish). Take some butter fats and food is cooked in industrial seed oil. No processed food and minimum to moderate sugar intake

Past EMF status, and professions

Worked in chemical industry few years and then in heavy duty IT job for many years. Not currently in IT or blue light dominated field except wifi and white light at home. So EMF and lack of sunlight and abundance might be contributor to my illness but not a causative one. Infact it has a strong genetic component. Some at atleast couple of close members in family arrived at same destination (same ballpark) by taking totally different route.

Past meds

Took SSRI 16-20 yrs ago that help in sleep and mood. Because of side effects strongly dislike ‘em. Taking bromazepam 1.5mg almost daily. But I believe bromazepam and other Benzos are mentally paralyzing.

Past labs

4 years back Candida IgG(29.9) Ref +ve >11, Vit D(37) Ref, DHEA-S(328 ug/dl) Ref 80-560: B-12 serum (479.6 pg/ml) Ref 189-883

**What fails sleep**

Wheat and rice both help and disturb sleep. Carbs help in sleep onset but play a key role in disturbing continuous and restoring sleep. It seems food opens the Pandora box as soon as it is ingested.

GFCF diet helps in clearing burden off the mind. Grain free diet is even better for mind. However it fails to improve the sleep and severe weight loss is show stopper.

Fermented food specially sour disturb sleep.

**Whats helps in Sleep**

These things help in sleep not in any particular order. It is not possible to sustain and maintain good sleep.

Herbals:

Herbal concoctions of Ayurvedic help in sleep and mood. However it is very difficult to sustain the benefits for long or attain substantial improvement.

Antifugals help in insomnia. I had a course of difflucan for three months four years back along with power doze of multi Vitamin and my sleep noticeably improved.

saccharomyces boulardii also help in calming mood down and slightly improves sleep.

Probiotics (mixed 12 strains of Bifido and Lacto bacteria) also help in sleep.

Vitamin D/C

4000 iu daily helps in sleep before effects tapering off completely in 10-14 days.

Intense Fasting: 15 hr fasts for a month or longer improved sleep few years back. That improvement quickly tapered off(15-30 days) when switched to regular 3 meals or 2 meals with eating fruits, tea etc in between, These were conditions for fast to be helpful for sleep.

_Must feel ravenously hungry at middle/tail end of the fast. Ie; Not to overeat before start of fast._

_Eat lots, of sweets when breaking fast and ideally workout after so that dinner doesn’t becomes detrimental to sleep. Min 3hrs between dinner and sleep._

_Eating grains/legumes/lentil at dinner also helps. But it doesn’t matter whether you eat meat, rice, chickpeas, wheat bread and fruits. Sleep comes naturally._

Regular fasting also helps. Feel better at the tail end of 8-9hr fasts. Must eat sweet sugary food at the end of dinner. Problem with Fasting and no-grain diet it acts as two edged sword-results in T3 demise and subsequent destruction of testosterone.

HIIT and wheat

Interestingly wheat (sourdough bread) helps in sleep in combination with HIIT or weight resistance exercise and some probiotics etc. However effect of opiode, peptide from gluten and casein adversely affects focus n mood.

**Vitamins**

Multi B+ vitamins trash sleep- B-12 injections/B-12 tabs initially help but soon degrade sleep. However endogenous B-12 produced by e-coli/salmonella bacteria when stomach is upset improves focus, cognition and mood tremendously. B-12 link is my hunch nonetheless very strong one and repeatable.

Problem is of roadmap. As noted there are dozens of routes and interventions which help in sleep once in a while. This also raises hope that “sleep inducing pathways” might be damaged but not severed. But for last few decades I am lost and running in circles. I am positive it is circadian cycle dysfunction and leaky gut with adrenal fatigue but mounting a coordinated defense against it out of my hands. Without knowing the gist of each route and underlying chemistry and holistically addressing issues, this is an exercise in futility.

### caroline — 2016-02-12T01:57:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-grueling-path-to-optimal.17535/post-188813>

Welcome.....

Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi- Paleo RX ......It is the first couple of years of his blog ....and much easier to get they!

Do you listen to JK's podcasts ? These are a great way to get fast info and mostly in terms we understand!

If you can manage it .....join as a "gold" member. There is a monthly webinar and a follow up live Q&A where you can ask Jack anything.

### PathFinder — 2016-02-14T05:55:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-grueling-path-to-optimal.17535/post-189001>

> [Cpt.Tired said:](/goto/post?id=188815)
>
> You have come to the right place. The info here is light years ahead of our time. Very interesting too.

Yes this stuff is mind boggling but starts to make sense if you relate life experiences and observations.

We have witnessed the effects of light on chicks breading, Poultry industry uses high powered blue light to quickly fatten chicks and bring it to a market.( High powered light bulbs changes their hormone profile and they stop sleeping and start eating continuously). Aren’t we humans are doing the same thing by using blue light day and night?

There are so many questions that I am sure I ‘ll find answer here. The intricate dance of cortisol, Leptin , Prolactin and Melatonin. Specially I would like to know why some days are different and what triggers that chain of reactions that ties them all together and good sleep fall occurs.

Where fasting stands in the grand scheme of things, long before I came to this site, I was asking why fasting helps in sleep to practitioners but nobody could answer that

# Thread: Toby's Journal to Optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tobys-journal-to-optimal.17593/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Toby King — 2016-02-22T14:36:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tobys-journal-to-optimal.17593/post-189565>

Hey everyone, so I decided to just get started on my optimal journal, here is some background from an earlier post:

\----------------------------------------------------------------------

I am 16, and have had many issues with health for quite a while. After living in Southeast Asia for four years, I developed many problems with adrenal fatigue, nausea and headaches every day, and took around a year off of school. I started working with a naturopath and changing my diet/lifestyle, I have been strict paleo for 12 months, but even though I have had some success I still am struggling with quite a few things:

-   Adrenal fatigue - Cortisol levels of Morning 6.0, Midday 4.2, Afternoon 1.4, Night 0.3
-   Low Testosterone - 284 pmol/L
-   Estradiol at 72 pmol/L
-   Estrone 6.0 pg/ml
-   Lack of energy
-   Still a higher body fat than I would like

After scouring many of Dr. Kruse's blogs (Thank you so much by the way!) I have been working intently on changing my environment. I have been avoiding all blue light after sunset for the past few months. E.g. Blublockers/F.lux/Red LED strips throughout the house. Have been on the Leptin reset starting my day with the BAB for around a week now, quite a change from the Bulletproof coffee/intermittent fasting of the past year.

I have been experimenting with CT for more than 6 months, but am starting to have issues with worrying parents and my naturopath is very concerned that it is damaging to my adrenals. I love CT, but my parents seem to think that it has attributed to my low cortisol. I would love some advice for convincing them that it is a power for healing.

I have been following the Epi-paleo diet for a while now, unfortunately I can't stand the oysters... Any time I try to eat them I retch  Luckily here in Australia we have found a source of Canadian wild caught salmon that I consume as much of as possible.

After reading the Adrenal Rx I am starting my day with 5-20 minutes of sunshine for my eye-clock, and try to get as much sun throughout the day as I can.

\----------------------------------------------------------------------

Now onto current day:

I am in week four of my final year of school, and I think one of my biggest challenges is dealing with the large amounts of emf that come from every single class room having its own wifi unit, and every single teenager with their own phone on, combined with the horrible fluorescent lighting that fills every room. After reading Jack's post about back to school headaches as an epidemic I think I am starting to see why my cortisol and testosterone are tanked.

To try to combat all mentioned above:

\- I have hard wired my entire house with ethernet, so that we have no wifi on at any time, when I am not at school.

\- Every BAB I have 5-6 pastured organic DHA rich eggs I source from our friends with chickens

\- I am consuming as much of my electrons as seafood as possible, we have access luckily to both organic farmed salmon, and wild caught canadian salmon frozen and shipped here to Aus. Unfortunatly oysters make me retch, and sardines aren't much better, and I am doing mainly salmon, and tuna at the moment.

\- I have been working on light as much as possible right now, exposing my retina and skin to it every morning, and afternoon, before and after school, and on the weekends I try to get as much sun as I can without any intense burns. In this respect I can feel that my body is starting to deal with sunlight better, as it takes a lot more time than it used to for me to get burnt, even in this strong UV Aussie sun.

\- We have a cafe which I can utilise to purchase san pellagrino wholesale, and I try to drink at least two 1.5L bottles of san pellagrino a day

\- I just finally made the decision to buy a freezer to get more serious with CT, I think it has helped me, even with my low adrenal status.

\- My light environment at night is as red as I can make it, I have about three physical filters on my computer screen, plus flux, so there is nothing getting out of that.

\- Much to the chagrin of my classmates and teachers I have taken to wearing either gunnars, or sunglasses, in class to try to combat against the fluorescent lighting.

\- I guess it just took a little bit of time but my hunger cravings have easily passed, and I am finding it much easier to have an early fat/protein dinner at least 4 hours before bed.

\- I have stopped pretty much all supplementation, although I am considering getting another IV vitamin C 15grams, as I had it a couple of months ago, and felt a huge burst of energy. I would love to see the comments and opinions on this from all from the forum.

\- I signed up as a gold member, and have been making my way through the webinars, attempting to use as much of the information as I can

\- I have a SIBO test ordered, and should arrive soon, as I would like to see if that has cleared up yet, as I had a positive result about 10 months ago, and dealt with it herbally and with oregano oil and the like.

\- One of the biggest wins for me is if I can get into nature. I am blessed to live on a 65 acre property with waterfalls, creeks, big beautiful trees, and lots of area to explore with my dog. Any time I spend there is time I cherish.

Another issue that I am still dealing with is higher than preferable body fat, and I am hoping that the leptin rx with ct protocol combination and some biweekly 5-10minute HIIT weight training will work to better that part.

Thank you awesome people!

Toby

walking (while wearing speedo's or very short pants and leather sole shoes only) one hr/day/365/yr within 10AM-2PM

2hrs/day better

goal=

### JanSz — 2016-05-18T17:40:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tobys-journal-to-optimal.17593/post-195026>

Get blood test.
Look up the list (58 items long) here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/#post-81508](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/#post-81508)
replace appropriate items on that list with this combination test:
**Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) Pediatric Profile Six—Comprehensive Screen (Endocrine Sciences)**
**Test Number: 500175** CPT: 82157; 82533; 82626; 82633; 82634; 83498; 84143; 84144; 84403
**Test Includes**
17-α-hydroxyprogesterone;
androstenedione, serum;
cortisol, serum, LC/MS;
dehydroepiandrosterone, serum;
deoxycorticosterone (DOC), serum;
11-desoxycortisol;
17-OH pregnenolone, serum, MS;
progesterone, serum;
specific compound S;
testosterone, serum (total)

\----------------
Above list is made using:
[https://www.labcorp.com/wps/portal](https://www.labcorp.com/wps/portal)
[https://www.labcorp.com/wps/portal/provider/testmenu](https://www.labcorp.com/wps/portal/provider/testmenu)

# Thread: Rise and shine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rise-and-shine.17653/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Blooming Flower — 2016-03-09T07:31:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rise-and-shine.17653/post-190504>

**Introduction**
Hi Everyone, I am 46 female, married with 3 boys (21,19,12) currently living in Australia - Latitude: 21°09′39″ S, Longitude: 149°03′29″ E, Elevation above sea level: 26 m = 85 ft. Have always lived within a 300km radius of this place. The average winter temp is 14C (57F) and summer is a steamy hot 35c (95F) so not much variance.

I am starting my diary today to look back upon and see my growth and as a way to start opening up. I also look forward to receiving help and recommendations to improve my learning from others.

I am a bit of an upper class Bogan, it has been pointed out to me on several occasions when people first met me they are reminded of Kath and Kim (Aussies will relate).

Have never much liked or eaten seafood; have avoided the sun most of my life…..oh how times are a changing…

GIHA (gee I hate acronyms) and this site is full of them…but I ain’t going anywhere!

I don’t like writing long post (or maybe more reading them…) as have always been a dot point girl…. Sucked in me!

Chose blooming flower as a name cause up till now I have been a shrinking violet… and that ain’t a life worth living!

Thanks to other members for the diary format… I liked it so used it.

**Goals**
Get from current level of understanding (conception) to at least the grade 3 level I have heard Jack refer to that I still don’t understand, then beyond.

Authentic wellness – my idea of optimal - no bullshit lies to self, an open/loving heart, be better every day, stay out of hospitals and medication free, an attractive oozing of confidence, an inner sense of wellness with my inside shining brightly to the outside. From shrinking violet to blooming flower.

A role model for my kids, family, friends…. Live inspired and be inspiring.

Get outdoors, train outdoors, love seafood, move injury and pain free.

**Parents**
Biological Father – met him briefly when I was 26 and didn’t stay in contact. From the little I know and remember he is still alive at 68ish, still works in television for world news, wears glasses and drinks lots of red wine…that’s all I have!

Birth certificate father – from age 8 - 18, violent alcoholic, smoked in house. No contact since then.

Mother – Still alive 68, didn’t believe in breast feeding, suffers from severe asthma and eczema since early childhood. Her mother died at 84 and father died at 34 from a heart condition on the operating table. Heavy smoker from teenager until probably early 40’s. Poor walking mobility, lives only inside on level 3 apartment in Sydney, on the pension with limited money, has no friends she sees or talks to, claims to be the happiest most energetic person alive, and to her credit she is always up and focuses on the positive and is encouraging to take action. Has been working on herself for over 30 years (as we are told constantly) and tells the same story over and over and is now writing her memoir to inspire others…. Has no relationship with any of her grandchildren (or anyone for that matter, only psychics that apparently understand her and support her). One daughter is no longer talking to her. I didn’t talk with her for over 10 years (have been back in contact for over 8 years now) and could easily not again, however I know life is so short and I feel for her and basically bite my tongue as to have a different opinion then her in an argument / loss of contact not worth having, my biggest fear (a part from frogs) is becoming her!

Reason for all of this…do I have mother “ issues”…mmmmm..(think so..). I pull myself up if I begin to judge and change it around to be more grateful but wholly f… it is a challenge.

**Body Composition**
Was always slim and active as a child, started to pile on around 14. Have been on a diet ever since then. Early adult years I swung between size 8 to size 18. Before my 40th birthday I started weigh training in a gym and got in shape, maintained for a bit then went back to skinny fat when I stopped the training. Started back in the gym with heavy weights about 4 years ago and was a strong skinny fat until 3 years ago I started fasting every day and within a month the weight just stripped off me. I have maintained approximately 17% body fat for the past 3 years. It has been the easiest most manageable lifestyle I have ever done…..but I still don’t have that healthy feeling…something is not right…which I think ultimately has led me here!!!! YAY

**Diet**
Can’t really remember what I ate up until the age of 8, but I lived with my grandmother and she was a good cook. After that all I remember eating 80% of it came out of a tin served with bread, milk, cereal and rump steak with plates of lollies once a week(yep I often heard we were lucky with the rump and the lollies were an apology for getting hit around a bit too much).

Basically done them all (high carb, low carb, high protein, no protein, low fat…never high fat except when I wasn’t on a ‘diet’…then it was whatever.. which would have been high bad fat).

I remember at 18 eating nothing but fruit and vegetables (and I am talking mountains of it a day) for 6 weeks, was skinny as then I turned yellow and thought this can’t be all that good.

I just started eating breakfast again after 3 years!!!! and my goal is to have seafood as often as possible, every meal if I have available…I still don’t like it but I do it! I appreciate something has to change in order to get the change I seek…. The more I read, the more it will surely start to make sense why…

**Alcohol**
Can count on one hand the amount of times I have been drunk, never got it! Now would have maybe a mouthful once or twice a year just to see if I like more...

**Water**
Have only drunk tap water until about 8 years ago when we purchased a reverse osmosis filter, only because the tape water was brown. Never really drank enough of the stuff…..ding ding.
And apparently living in Australia the water isn’t worth bottling…don’t understand that one yet, but I have started to purchase Pellegrino at $2 a bottle, can come out of the alcohol budget….but don’t get that it is ok as contains fluoride? Have increased my intake to 3-4 litres a day…mostly from the filter, not the bottle else I would be living on the street.

**Environment**
So always lived around this general area, but not sure of the relevance, we did live in a coal mining town for 20 years and apparently there are black coal deposits under the town. We really don’t get much of a winter (maybe cold for 2-4 weeks), but I have always felt the cold, I am looking for a jumper if it is under 24C /75F…has to mean something, but I don’t know what yet.

**nnEMF**
After the age of about 10, I have always been an indoor creature, was made to not wanted to. At 17 I got a job at the local black coal mine in the office and worked in an office environment for another 28years. Always been surrounded by artificial light, computer or television and microwaved food. Loved it when mobiles come around, but never liked talking on them other than speaker cause the heat burns my ear…I have started to turn wi-fi off at night and not take my phone with me everywhere I go. Have disconnected the clock in the bedroom. We sleep in air-conditioning and a fan as it is soooo hot, and have been known to in winter too for a noise blocker. I purchased an extech electromagnetic field meter…but need to know how to use properly to know what a good and bad reading really is…basically it only registers a reading if you are up close to the object (eg jug or computer) else zero register….more to learn.

Have disconnected our microwave and moved out of the kitchen, haven’t even missed it!

**Exercise**
I am a sitter unless I am at the gym or on my daily walk…this has to change and I am making progress towards…as in get outside more even if to pull weeds, mow the grass. I have given up my gym membership and taking my training outside!!! …. So I am now looking at trees differently…as in could I climb you… and doing my walk in the sun barefoot if not too hot..…. I will take my weights to the park!

**Light**
Have mostly always avoided the sun (until now). For reasons I still don’t get or could articulate to another why I do, I am now sunbaking most days for about 20-30 minutes in the morning fully naked in my back yard. I have purchased some blue light blocking glasses and only have 2 Himalayan salt lamps on at night. I shower at night using an oil lamp for light.

**Circadian Cycle/Sleep**
Was crap for years, waking up many times a night to pee, sometimes unable to get back to sleep. Was sleep deprived for years (21 years ago) with young kids and work. My next aim is to wake feeling refreshed, hence why I stopped fasting and started the Leptin reset.

**Libido**
Sucks, non-existent for most of my life. It could be the fact I still feel like a kid, not a woman at all. I feel so suppressed and always have, I will hide and say nothing, never want to be noticed, never want to be questioned as I wouldn’t know the answer or be able to articulate what I feel. I am on a treadmill going nowhere. Married young to get out of a situation and don’t think I have ever been in love or even know what that means. My husband is a beautiful soul with ugly parts as we all are. What you don’t appreciate depreciates and I haven’t been appreciating him for a long time. I am doing the motion out of obligation nothing else. Sad but true, I so want to feel horny, I have had fleeting moments over the years and it was amazing, but all so brief!

Funny thing is, he has been telling me for years, get outside with nature, be in the sun, eat seafood, go masturbate regularly, sleep naked. I have been the crazy one while he has remained the constant all these years, but I just ain’t attracted to him or anyone…but I really want to be… why live your life missing this experience!

**Stress/Anxiety/Emotion**
Nothing other than the usual stress of being a mother and life’s demands for a working class family. I am sure the suppressed emotions take their toll though, as I struggle to feel.

**Supplements**
Nothing

**Dental**
Lots of teeth removed as a kid due to overcrowding. A mouth full of silver fillings.

**My Current symptoms**
Don’t retain what I read or hear. Literally if I see a word that I don’t know the meaning , I look it up and then I could read the same passage the next day come across that same word and think…I have no idea what that means! I couldn’t explain something to someone that I have read and kinda understand on the blog…makes learning very frustrating!

No drive – as discussed above nor with much else at the moment….

Lump in my lymph node under my left arm. Have had checked and they say all ok nothing to do or worry about as that happens….mmmmm Isn’t left side the feminine side….

Have had gut issues over the years, stomach ulcers, and ongoing constipation.

Also read somewhere that when you have low dopamine or something???, people don’t see you…that is very much me! I am often not seen or heard even if I am not hiding…this then makes me want to hide..…you know talk to someone and they don’t answer, post something and no one replies, the one that doesn’t get invited to the party.. you get the point …I suspect with my symptoms it is still low, but that is ok, cause I am working on getting me some of that! I don’t’ think it is an ego thing, cause there is nothing more sexy than an authentic self-confident person… Plus changes are happening cause I am writing this…

And the last few days I have really noticed I pee all the f’ing time….I counted 5 times to my friends 0 times when we were together…. Can someone point me to where I need to go on this one…electrons, uv, water… I don’t know?

**Changes I have implemented in the last month or so** (even if I don’t know why at this stage!)

Sunbake daily 10-30 min in the morning nude where possible. Look towards the sun

Daily walk 30 minutes barefoot where possible (easy when has been raining and ground not hot)

Started to eat breakfast after 3 years, consisting of 50+ grams of protein – no snacking – didn’t snack anyways. (good for the adrenals??)

Eating seafood (prawns, white fish, salmon, tin tuna, tin oysters, fresh oysters (not many yet), tin sardines) – trying to add some at each meal

Drinking more water, a bottle of two of Pellegrino plus filtered water

Wear blue blockers at night, have ventured into shops with on…this will get better

Lights off at night and use oil lamps

Disconnected and taken microwave away.

Installed blue light filter on computer, phone and table

Training at home or outside – no lights

Started initial stage of CT…dunking face in water

Started to let myself get cold – cold showers, aircon on in car (we are still in summer and hot here!)

Reading the blog outside – is there still benefit to be outside but in shade or under a roof while I do this? cause the sun will burn me to a crisp otherwise.

Optimal Education

Keep reading, listening, watching and witnessing. Implementing what I learn and start a journal…..

### caroline — 2016-03-12T02:46:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rise-and-shine.17653/post-190688>

Hi Blooming Flower! What a great start you have made!

I have such a similar story ....my hormones were crap. Dr. K. recommends oxy twice a day to access the pharmacy in your brain.
I said "I don't feel like it - no desire at all". JK said " I don't care wether you feel like it or not ....just DO IT"

So - I did! And it works ....along with the leptin reset and all the other stuff you are already doing!

Are you in menopause? You can get a saliva test done thru ZRT labs in the U.S. And see what your hormones are up to.

You may need BHRT.....

Do you know Suzanne Somer's' books? She does a great job of explaining hormones. My favorite is Breakthrough.

I am just south of Sydney.

Btw ....you can get a good imported water from Aldi. It is Italian - in a blue bottle and very reasonable!

### Blooming Flower — 2016-03-14T07:02:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rise-and-shine.17653/post-190800>

Thanks @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) ... I can assume what you mean by oxy from your next comment....but can you please spell it out to me so I am not assuming and understand fully the lingo... I did a search and couldn't find anything other than painkiller...lol
Have you done the ZRT labs since you are in Aust...imagine would be expensive? My sister in law apparently has a great hormone doc that she is going to hook me up with. Cheers for the other info will look into it...unfortunately with the water I am about 1000km from the closest Aldi...so pellegrino it is until I source something else.

On another note, last night slept straight from 9.30pm till 5.30am....and woke up feeling half decent! Still catching the sun daily and have been eating breakfast for 2 weeks now with no regrets.

My 12 year old son has been doing the leptin reset rules and has lost 7 kg in 5 weeks....asked him yesterday - have you been hungry since starting? No. Do you feel like you have more energy? Yes. Are you sleeping better? Yes. Any regrets? No. He has followed the rules every day even when he has been to birthday parties and sleep overs. I am so proud of his commitment to himself (he was getting very chubby and constantly seeking out crap..supplied by neighbours, friends and the good intentions...mmm of family as I don't have in house... and always starving....as you can imagine). Hopefully he will be able to maintain his new habits and lifestyle when he is constantly faced with others doing the opposite.

### caroline — 2016-03-14T08:16:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rise-and-shine.17653/post-190801>

You can get oxy in lots of ways ....but I was referring to sex. There is a great oxy thread here somewhere - likely in the cave.

Hugging, kissing, touching, pets, laughing, dancing, singing etc etc.

Your son sounds like quite a remarkable young man! Does he follow along here at all? Would he start a journal of his own? Do you have the book by John Ott? Health and light. It would be a great read for both of you! Does he have blue blockers?

I have done ZRT labs a couple of times. I can't remember exactly the cost - but it was much the same as the labs here. Of course our $$$ sucks now! The good thing about ZRT labs is it is very comprehensive and the results are sent to us.

My Doc was most impressed when he saw their reports.

### Blooming Flower — 2016-03-23T02:10:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rise-and-shine.17653/post-191327>

far from it caroline...don't know how to be authentic......never have been.... so this is me just writing/spewing a capture of where I am atm
ok I got nothing...still feel like I am on a treadmill to nowhere...have for years.... not passionate about much at all, no drive, I read I mostly don't understand
I am full of shit literally...constipated or more correctly no desire to even take a shit...or give a shit...or a holding on to whatever so tightly...a tight arse
I feel I have a knowing but don't know what that is...it gets confused with all the other bs in there..., I feel smart (certainly not academically..nor street..I would be eaten alive.), I feel dumb and boring as bat shit (more shit...), childish, full of fear, skill-less having lived the same year over and over and over and over again...digging 10 000 1 meter holes...no depth, no substance..... a grateful ungrateful most negative positive person.... I judge someone who would die for me as an aimless dart...when that is really me...take action...what fucking action when you got nothing and don't have a burning desire

...lack of sun to the point of avoiding wherever possible, lack of grounding, living inside watching tv and computers, hating on seafood..always having lights on no matter what time of the day...avoiding discipline...sitting...always taking the easy way out...living in my head, going around and round and round and round with the same bs...selfish, a sense of entitlement, a problem with hierarchy and authority, thinking I am better than this person and not worthy of the next, don't ever give me a compliment i wont buy it...would prefer you tell me I am shit (ffs!!!)...life of bad decisions or more lack of decision making...

For the first time it makes the most sense ever why, even if that is someone else's word and knowledge...at the end of the day that is all I have or will even have to base my thinking and decisions on, so I will continue to get outside and seek the sun, I walk barefoot, I force down seafood, I read to start to fill the process of filling the gaping hole , I do shit I have no desire to do, I sit in darkness or wear orange glasses, and from today I drink more water cause I have no where near been having enough..it so is fucking about environment...i am a product of my environment...it was shit so I get shit... I do this hoping to see and feel a difference... so thirsty, it is only mid morning and I have already downed 2L, going now to drink and walk barefoot in the rain

### caroline — 2016-03-23T02:50:20+0200

### Blooming Flower — 2016-04-29T01:55:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rise-and-shine.17653/post-193802>

Doing a quick check in. Have been continuing with my optimal journey cause once you hear this info there is no not doing. Starting to notice changes heading in the right direction (next step is to figure out the lab details to confirm and have more of a knowing...for me and below as you will read).

My 12 year old has reached his goal weight (a loss of 8kg and 8cm around waist) with minimal effort and is maintaining nicely, although he thinks the dark nights is just not "normal" he is slowing coming around.

My 20 year old was back from uni for a weeks break and called me a freak....kinda hurt (for a millisecond), but I know he doesn't know nor wants to a that age, but he will be watching....his girlfriend is half way studying medicine and her father is a doc...so they know everything....hahaha

My 22 year old is in need of serious help (he has been to doc but doesn't share too much of the situation).. he lives 3000km away (Melbourne), sleep is screwed up and has been for years, depressed, uni drop out 3 times, drugs, unable to hold down a job for any period of time, bombarded with blue light and nnEMF, lives on processed food or not much food, claims he has ADHD and chronic fatigue and is taking someone else's medication that is like magic... so he just told me (geez...keep breathing mum).... claims he is coming home in 2 weeks for a few weeks as he is sick of being sick and tired and is wanting our help (just want to get him here...he can be under the thought he will be fixed in a few weeks...I really don't think he thinks that)... so I need to find someone fast....any suggestions for an Australian based person that can help? Initially I will introduce him to light, water and magnetism he hasn't seen them for a long time.... I would also like to mention that this kid is super intelligent (well was...hopefully not too late...) and his potential is mind blowing...if only he could see and feel it...well that is the goal of being optimal isn't it..we are all full of potential! Contact me if you have any recommendations - 0439 789 134

Hubby is on board and making changes too..... he surprises me as his job is far from ideal...works shift-work in an electric shovel for 27 plus years! Yet his sleep is great, he is medication and pain free.... he has always been one with nature and that must be his saving grace!

Stumbled across a company in Aust that does home assessments for EMF, they have a consultant that lives 4 hours away... totally excited to get that check out.

Now out to get the sun and a bit of naked mobility work/flow!

Even without all the deep science ....this all feels so intuitively right ....so just keep doing and reading and learning and be an example of lite and love for your family.

No one likes to be preached at or to. - even if it is done with love.

Take care of you first .....and you will be helping to lead the way to an optimal life.

Don't compromise what you are doing ....even when others are dismissive or challenge you.

You have so much to give.....this is now your job ....be and set an example - and you can't even imagine what will start growing in your magical garden!

You will find peace and so much energy.....and you will share all of this in countless ways you can't even imagine.

I was always so strict about what I needed to do for myself. My dearest friend thought I was a little nuts but respected what i was doing for myself. One day, at my citizenship ceremony I decided to have a lammington ( an Aussie cake). My friend couldn't believe it and it really disappointed her. I will never forget the look on her face.

Of course - it was my choice ....but it was a bad one.

# Thread: Journey post concussion
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoshuaLP0 — 2016-04-18T14:09:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/post-192969>

**Goals**
In August 2016 I sustained a concussion (no loss of consciousness) after banging the top of my head in a van. Symptoms of extreme fatigue, dizziness, headaches and brain fog lasted approximately 2 weeks.

Around 2 weeks post, just as symptoms where beginning to clear my girlfriend at the time head butted me in the face accidentally (I hope). This re-triggered the symptoms. 2 weeks after this, just when symptoms were beginning to clear I woke up in the night by banging my head against the wall - since then the symptoms have persisted (although in different permutations). In addition to the above, I have developed anxiety around physical contact (especially with my head) through fear of another concussion.

My goals are to get back to where I was before the concussion (and hopefully even better!).

**Background**
Age -27
Single
154 lbs
6'3 tall

**Family**
Parents in mid 60s with no known health issues
Grandparents - granddad on my fathers side had dementia/grandma on same side passed away at 95 (no serious health issues)
Grandparents on my mothers side still both alive in mid 80s with reasonable health
Sister recently diagnosed with Chohns (likely to have genetic vulnerability due to Ashkenazi background)

**Personal health before concussion**
General health before the concussion was generally ok (although energy wasn't always great, and prone to get headaches). I suffered from mild IBS in my teens through to my early 20s which led to a number of issues with energy/brain fog in my early-mid 20s. Diet changes (no gluten,/dairy) and acupuncture helped alleviate many of the issues.

Previously fractured my eye socket (and concussion) from football injury but with no long lasting effects at the time.

Generally would drink alcohol fairly liberally in social settings before the concussion (but mainly confined to weekends).

**Treatments post concussion**
\-Acupuncture (effective in short term but not necessarily long term)
\-Osteopath (same as above)
\-Cognitive Behaviour Therapy (Effective for anxiety related issues)
\-Floatation tanks (useful for calming me down during anxious periods but not seemingly cumulative in benefit)
Low level light therapy (I started this treatment most recently where I would say I was back to about 70% but was still finding myself getting fatigue headaches after periods of mental exertion). Therefore decided to go ahead with the LLLT:

I've had 3 sessions in total, all fairly short in duration/low intensity - the first two to the frontal lobes and the third one to the brain stem region.

My response to the first was very in line with a typical hormetic curve; immediate boost for 4 hours or so, a 24 hour dip (a Serotonin hangover would be my best description) followed by a leveling out. The latter two treatments haven't followed such an obvious trajectory, triggering a return of headaches (vasodilatory in nature), fatigue, irritability and 'brain fog'. My thinking is that this is an essentially extended hormetic response, with the side effects being triggered by increased nitric oxide (and potential associated inflammation). Symptoms are currently worse than when I started the treatment.

**Supplements:**
Currently:
\-Curcumin (for inflammation)
\-CoQ10 and L-Theanine (for mitochondria and anxiety)
\-Magnesium Taurate (considering switching to Magnesium L Theorenate)
\-Vitamin D3 (400ui)
\-B complex
\-Krill Oil
\-Cordecyps/Reishi (occasional)
\-Chlorella (occasional)

**Diet:**
Generally I am on a more conventional paleo diet but am yet to fully transition to a Leptin reset diet. Try to eat 3 portions of seafood per week + bone brothers + meat offals

**Water:**
Recently made the transition to consciously only drinking un-fluoridated water

**Sun:**
Living in London very poor exposure to natural sunlight (previous Vitamin D tests were deficient, but normalised after supplementation)

**Work/Home Environment:**
Have lived in London my whole life (except 3 years in Manchester)
Work in technology so high exposure to EMF/Blue Light (use flux on an all day basis)
Live in apartment on 5th floor

**Grounding:**
Not yet experimented with it yet

**Sleep**
Generally have always slept well, usually more than 8 hours per night - although was never very disciplined about consistent times (circadian rhythms). Immediately post concussion sleep was between 12-14 hours. This has dropped to 9-10 hours but not waking up feeling particularly refreshed.

### Just Breathe — 2016-04-18T22:41:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/post-192988>

Welcome!
It's ok to be deeply concerned about a concussion. You can heal from it! Have faith!

The best tip I have for you is to get sunlight in your eyes in the morning, and in the evening. Catch the sunrise and the sunset every day. Completely avoid bluelight and reading. The faster you do these as strictly as possible, the faster you can heal up! My concussion took 6 weeks to turn around and it was an accidental head-butt from someone else. You can do it!

### Dasha — 2018-03-07T18:41:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/post-236590>

Josh - read your symptoms and they are extremely similar to mine. (I fell in October and have been having fatigue headaches just on the side of where I fell since then).
Just wanted to check to how you are doing / if anything has helped alleviate your symptoms since 2016.

Thanks in advance.

Cheers

### JanSz — 2018-03-07T18:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/post-236593>

@JoshuaLPO
Banging messed up your pituitary gland.
It was not that great to begin with.

Get pituitary MRI.
If MRI is clear, you may have to find Endocrinologist that will test and adjust all your pituitary hormones (that are out of place).
(Good luck finding that endo).

If MRI have found something,
you need twice the luck.

//////////////
It newer hurts to spend lots of time on sunny beach.

.....

### Dasha — 2018-03-09T16:06:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/post-236763>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) -- what do you mean to adjust pituitary hormones? Is there something specific that can be taken to readjust them back to normal?
Why does a concussion cause issues with pit gland?

(I had a concussion in Oct 2017 and had 95% of the same symptoms as Josh. I currently still have headaches and fatigue. Headaches my osteopath is telling me because I have TMJ, which I had before, but got worse after the fall.)

### JanSz — 2018-03-09T16:46:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/post-236768>

> [Dasha said:](/goto/post?id=236763)
>
> @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) -- what do you mean to adjust pituitary hormones? Is there something specific that can be taken to readjust them back to normal?
> Why does a concussion cause issues with pit gland?
>
> (I had a concussion in Oct 2017 and had 95% of the same symptoms as Josh. I currently still have headaches and fatigue. Headaches my osteopath is telling me because I have TMJ, which I had before, but got worse after the fall.)

Pituitary gland is responsible for majority of body functions.
Usually not all pituitary hormones are messed up due to concussion.
You have to first do the tests and see where you stand.
Problems with pituitary are not always due to concussion.
You can always hope that your pituitary gets back to normal,
but,
in the mean time you have to do your living.
Monitoring and adjusting is the only way I know about.
Monitoring also allows you to find part of the system that do not need any interventions.

Hormones of pituitary
[http://www.biology-pages.info/P/Pituitary.html](http://www.biology-pages.info/P/Pituitary.html)

# Thread: Action Time
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/action-time.17869/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JBS — 2016-04-27T07:14:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/action-time.17869/post-193669>

G’day Team Kruse

OK it’s time to bare all, seems naked truth is what this forum is built on. Don’t worry no photo’s

Age: 49
Divorced with one 15 y/o step-daughter
Live: Manly, Sydney Australia – top (3rd) floor apartment overlooking Sydney Harbour through pine trees, surf beach close by.
Work: Serial entrepreneur. Until very recently spent my working life in an 1st floor office in the CBD under fluro lights starring at a computer most of the day.
Quals: Bachelor and Master’s Degrees in Env Science (8 years at uni studying environmental health with sun/blue light, magnetism and nnEMF barely mentioned).

The problem – blue light disease, not enough DHA and connection to Big Mamma/Papa
Have had weight issues for 30 years all diet nor exercise regimes had no lasting effects. Bloods taken in mid Feb and confirmed what would happen to someone with my lifestyle i.e. pre-diabetic, low testosterone and metabolic syndrome. I have been blessed with a highly resilient body and amazed it has lasted this long given the abuse.

**Remedial Action**
Stay away from main stream medicine and fix my environment.
Started Leptin Rx late Feb 2016 (good results to date and will report after 12 weeks, 6-8 weeks won’t be enough for the damage I have done to my body).
Daily morning sun and no sunglasses ever.
Drink spring water or sparkling mineral water.
There is no way can I do CT as recommended but instead go the beach every morning and snorkel or body surf for 30-60 mins (sea temp is currently 20-22C but decreasing now we are going into winter to around 15-16C) and plan to continue fun CT during winter.
Reduced my nnEMF by working from home mostly with balcony doors open wide to natural light, purchased a Blushield device and cable connected my gadgets.
Added f.lux to screens and wear blue blockers at night if watching any screen. Lights are off.
Only grounding I am getting is time at the beach.

**Goals**
Optimal health (lose 30+kg, no metabolic syndrome, etc.)
Write a book on sun/light and nnEMF to help educate people
Start a new biophysics tech company

Cheers
Jason
