### JBS — 2016-08-03T05:52:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/action-time.17869/post-199242>

Update

Fell off the Leptin Rx wagon over June/July (winter downunder), nothing too serious but rice with Asian food, chips with fish, sandwiches, a few boozy boys nights out and some deserts with my daughter. Little or no ocean swimming but ate loads of seafood. Stayed true to blue light mitigation and had around a 30% morning sun hit rate. Sunglasses remained in the draw. Interestingly I have not gained any weight (3 notches off my belt)! But i did not lose anymore either. Energy levels and libido improved, blood pressure dropped a bit but remains very high and have not checked my bloods. So back on the wagon this month. My suspicion is that blue light is a major culprit in my n=1 journey. Been trailing blushield (photonic micro crystal tech) using plants for my experiments re nnEMF with surprising success.

My new book _In The Dark - new ways to avoid the harmful effects of living in a technologically connected world_ is with the publisher they are dumbing it down against my wishes but better to get the message out in a format mothers can understand. The only way we are going to educate the masses about the profound "back to nature" knowledge is by building an army of angry mothers! Pissed off mothers are the only population group politicians and the establishment are afraid of.

### Just Breathe — 2016-08-03T18:08:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/action-time.17869/post-199253>

Hi Jason!

I had to laugh, pissed off mothers are a powerful group!

I wanted to second Caroline's vote for Whole 30. If you look at any books for the whole 30 and some of their recommended cooking books, I've found a number of recipes to help me avoid food boredom when transition to full on paleo-life. I've used the Leptin Rx as my diet and the Whole 30 books for recipes & inspiration.

### JBS — 2016-10-30T03:49:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/action-time.17869/post-203441>

Been back on Leptin RX (with some CT) for 4 weeks again and the weight just falls off. A far way to go but down from 145kg to 129kg is pretty darn good! On top of that my knees have almost fully recovered from Grade IV cartilage damage - tennis court awaits!

When I got hit by the quantum health wave, especially circadian biology and Wallace’s work on mitochondria, my life changed. It brought me back into the environmental health game which I left in revulsion 25 years ago. Invigorated and inspired I have decided to go on the front foot and share my take in the most effective way I know how. My new book In The Dark is being launched this week and I am doing interviews and podcasts to help spread the message. Written for non-science degree mums and dads who want the best health and well-being for themselves and their children while living in a technologically addicted world. If Pollacks book is for 3rd graders this for kindergarten kids. Those 'ignorant idiots' that Jack refers to include my family and colleagues and they need to be fed like babies if they are going to take quantum health seriously.

# Thread: my journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.17870/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hello — 2016-04-27T14:53:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.17870/post-193688>

Hello all, Laney here. Thanks for posting such great stuff on this forum.

This is mostly a placeholder until I can get round to writing more, so I thought I'd start by writing up snippets or observations.

For a bit over a year I've had trouble breathing, an undiagnosable condition (at least undiagnosable by the doctors I saw). I found that sitting in the sun while grounding, or lacking sun just grounding helps my breathing basically immediately, and if I stay out in full blast sun long enough the effect will last a good couple of days.

I found this forum and after starting to do some reading on here and combined with my own personal experience it seems to me that there are more important interventions to make in terms of getting healthy than supplements (and I spent months taking \*bucketloads\* of them with little effect until I tried my sun experiment).

The only other things I've found that has had an effect on my breathing trouble has been magnesium put on transdermally.

I'm homozygous for MTHFR A1298c, and I see from some other posts here that one gets by this SNP by moving to the equator. I'm in Sydney and I know sunlight exposure here is perhaps a bad thing - ozone hole? - but, if so, I have to take a risk there because of my strong preference for being able to breathe.

I do have a reptile lamp from rayminder.org I've been thinking about using, but don't think I understand enough yet about the technology to start off.

Thanks for listening!

ps: I'm a Sydneysider, get in touch if yo want to compare notes re: sunbaking, water, etc.

### Jenelle — 2016-04-28T21:12:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.17870/post-193785>

"my strong preference for being able to breathe"

Yeah... Can't argue with that!

Welcome.

# Thread: Menopause sucks!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menopause-sucks.17915/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### pascale ahmad — 2016-05-08T09:11:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menopause-sucks.17915/post-194354>

I had a rough week, just had a period after several months, made me feel like hell. 10 days of PMS with breasts the size of water melons and totally unable to go to the loo even with double dose of my usual ayuvedic herbal laxative, had to give myself enemas. Then the floodgates opened, was really painful... Followed by a 3 day migraine. I took some painkillers, even tough I knew I shouldn't but I had to go to work (in my EMF infested office) and I needed something to get me through the day. Actually it didn't help much.
I only have a mild headache today, it Sunday I can take it easy.
I don't know what is worse, having the accasional period which makes me feel like hell but I somehow find it reassuring because I feel that I still have some hormones left in me vs not having anymore period but then I'll be aging so much faster....
I think BHRT would probably be right for me now but I can't seem to find a doctor who specialises in this here in India.
I'm not at all confident that I can work that out for myself at the moment.....

### caroline — 2016-05-08T09:31:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menopause-sucks.17915/post-194355>

What I did here in Oz ..... I went to a compounding pharmacy and asked what Docs are writing scripts for BHRT. They can't tell you much more than that ...but if they have feedback from their customers- they can tell you that.

_I got names of three Docs and made some phone calls ....and asked a lot of questions!

We all need to think outside the box!

I wound up with a wonderful Doc that is totally on board with a lot of things here..... I got very lucky!

He has no time to research all this stuff - he is way too busy ...but loves to discuss all of this ...and he tells me he learns so much from me._

### pascale ahmad — 2016-05-08T10:01:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menopause-sucks.17915/post-194358>

I couldn't find any compounding pharmacies, I already tried that. I guess they must be some but I think they are only attached to hospitals and not accessible to the public.
I was particularly interested in the Wiley protocol, which I had read a lot about but no luck here. I think I will have to wait until my next trip to Europe and find someone there are guess.
I don't think it would be wise for me to strat tempering with my hormones myself.

### fitness@home — 2016-05-08T14:50:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menopause-sucks.17915/post-194360>

Keep looking for a BHRT doctor that can help. Not sure we're you live.

I have been on BHRT for at least a decade. There was one 6 month period of time that I was off of them, but it wasn't pretty!

They do help keep you young. I get comments from friends and family when I go home to visit. I just plain feel better on hormones.

### pascale ahmad — 2016-05-09T14:41:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menopause-sucks.17915/post-194408>

You do indeed look young! Yes I think that's the way to go. I live in India, BHRT has not caught on here yet.

### pascale ahmad — 2016-05-20T17:44:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/set-back.17974/post-195132>

I had to take one for the team this week! At work our partners from China were in town to sign two very big contracts. This meant three days of solid meetings in blue light and freezing AC, obviously couldn't wear my blue blockers. Every day ending with a sit down dinner way past sunset, no control over the food and copious amounts of alcohol. The Chinese, who were on top form, kept cheering "bottoms up" throughout every dinner and I had to indulge in chugging a few, under the watchful eyes of my boss. Didn't get my regular dose of sun either. I am not bloated like crazy. Not quite got back to my routine yet, woke up a bit too late for sunset this morning and it was already to hot to sit out. Drinking lots of coconut water, it feels like the right thing. Tomorrow I have to make sure I don't miss sunset and get a good dose of sun gazing and grounding. Hopefully I can turn this around quickly.

I hope Im in my journal. Im feeling very uncomfortable in my legs today, hard to describe, sort of like I need to shake them! Horrible. I went to Tai Chi yesterday and we all did Tai chi walking. When I do this my legs almost feel normal. I can move them slowly and do proper heel toe and they dont feel like they will collapse which is wonderful. There is definitely something wrong in my home though, I walk in a woomf... collapsing feeling. Im so tired of it...sigh

On the positive front today I ordered blue blocking glasses and phone screen cover. No lights on last night and sat out in the cloudy, cold, misty Irish morning (is the light still beneficial?). Should I be watching the sunset too? Ive come across this thread. Is the evening light beneficial too? Sat out in wonderful sunshine on Tuesday and Im getting a tan... nice... vitamin D etc. Im rambling.. but then its my journal isn't it?

Do you people think I could really get my health back? Really? Walk normally again. I so wish wish wish...It makes me sad not to go places because I cant get around.

# Thread: My story of striving for health.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story-of-striving-for-health.18162/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dylan848 — 2016-07-14T03:41:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story-of-striving-for-health.18162/post-198332>

I'm a 19 year old male, currently living in Australia. The period from about November last year to now has been the toughest of my life and I feel like I'm doing it alone so I decided I'd post here where some people might actually understand and relate to what I'm doing.

It started with severe anxiety around my ex, and not knowing whether I wanted to be with her any more or not, this lead to a deep depression which I'm still slowly making my way through. I now see that my problem didn't start there. My problem was a built up accumulation of things over time. The first being diet; I was vegan for a bit over a year, tracking what I ate and thought I was doing well and that it was a viable option. Now I realize how bad this was for me with the little amount of fat I would have been getting, my hormones would have been destroyed especially my adrenals.

Secondly, I smoked marijuana occasionally which obviously wouldn't have helped and I had panic attacks the last two times I had it. I realized this was an issue and haven't smoked or drunk alcohol since.

Thirdly my lifestyle changed dramatically at the end of last year. I'd just finished school and basically moved in with my girlfriend at the time. Our day mainly consisted of staying inside and watching shows/movies on a laptop.

My circadian rhythm was messed up and I would have been taking in way too much EMF. After I broke up with my girlfriend I thought my problems would be solved but at the time I didn't realize the other factors at play. After more panic attacks (which I was never an anxious person previous to this) I sunk deeper into a depression, thinking I'd never be the same. I had pretty bad depersonalization also.

I had no libido, no motivation, extreme cognitive decline and brain fog. I felt a third of the person I used to be.
The only thing keeping me going was hope and pure willpower, knowing I had to figure out what was wrong because I wasn't taking the easy way out.

I'd been to my GP twice as well as seeing a psychologist but he just slumped all of my symptoms into anxiety, which I knew wasn't true and there had to be more going on.

I came across Dr Terry Wahls and how her diet had completely reversed her symptoms of MS, from being wheelchair bound to being able to walk and ride a bike again. This gave me hope and I figured if she could do that I could easily reverse my symptoms. It also made me realize that I probably had diabetes though, as I had neuropathy in the left side of my body and felt a lot better after cutting out all carbs except starchy vegetables. Also my vision was declining, fortunately it has recovered relatively quickly. My anxiety was almost completely gone after cutting out sugar and grains which was amazing.

One time after being on track for a while I had a binge on carbs and my neuropathy became much worse as well as headaches and brain fog. This lead me looking into autoimmune diseases and leaky gut which I realised I also had (am allergic to dairy and tried an elimination diet and have noticed extreme sensitvity to eggs, nightshades and nuts) After a few weeks of changing to a paleo esque diet I suffered a concussion training Brazilian jiu jitsu. For the next few days I felt so similar to how I'd felt when I was at the lowest point of my "depression" if not worse. I still get headaches in the area I was hit but the extreme brain fog and poor vocabulary recall have returned to normal I think.

Anyway, the last few weeks I have been doing keto which lead me to Jack kruse, this has given me a lot of hope, along with learning about neuroplasticity. I'm doing my best to reduce emf's and bluelight although it's hard as I still live at home and my parents wouldn't understand and getting sun almost everyday.

I feel as if I've lost my personality, partly because of the concussion and partly because of spending most of my time researching about this stuff. I just really hope I can fully recover and get back the love for life I used to have.

I also worry about my future, I want to study at university next year but am worried the toll it will take on my health; late nights, blue light light and emf exposure due to study. I guess I can only do my best to negate these factors, though it is very tempting to move closer to the equator and become a farmer or something.

Any encouragement and tips would be very much appreciated.

### Jenny S — 2016-07-15T00:53:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story-of-striving-for-health.18162/post-198368>

Hi Dylan, I'm in Oz too, on Bribie Island, quite a few of us around - where do you live, could be someone close by you could talk to or catch up with.
Sounds like you are doing really well, especially for your age, plenty of us on forum would like to have known years ago what you know now!
I also started with Terry Wahls, fantastic story but shame she doesn't know about artificial light. Think you will find a lot of your food intolerances will disappear as you get better, mine did.
Have you got some blue blockers yet? You can get some quite reasonable looking ones to wear if going out at night.
Won't be an easy path going to Uni, but you will be able to do it.
Good luck, Jenny

### Dylan848 — 2016-07-15T02:20:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story-of-striving-for-health.18162/post-198371>

Hi jenny, I live on the Central Coast in NSW. Yeah I have noticed a big difference, especially lately with focusing on my circadian rhythm.
I have heard that food intolerance can go away, I guess I just struggle with having the patience at times haha. How long did it take for you to notice changes in your intolerance roughly?
I want to get some blue blockers but am not sure where to look or if there's a specific type I should get, any advice? Also do you have an opinion on magnetico like products?

### Dylan848 — 2016-07-15T02:23:57+0300

I realise my first post probably seemed quite negative and was just a list of things that have gone wrong but I am optimistic about the future and have seen significant improvement already, I guess I was just at somewhat of a low point yesterday.

### Jenny S — 2016-07-15T07:41:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story-of-striving-for-health.18162/post-198382>

> [Dylan848 said:](/goto/post?id=198371)
>
> Hi jenny, I live on the Central Coast in NSW. Yeah I have noticed a big difference, especially lately with focusing on my circadian rhythm.
> I have heard that food intolerance can go away, I guess I just struggle with having the patience at times haha. How long did it take for you to notice changes in your intolerance roughly?
> I want to get some blue blockers but am not sure where to look or if there's a specific type I should get, any advice? Also do you have an opinion on magnetico like products?

Most on forum have these blue blockers which can be bought from Amazon, called something like Uvex Skyper Blue Blockers Safety glasses. They block all blue light but don't think too many wear them in public! There are a lot of yellow or brown ones on ebay etc which are better to be seen in public in, but don't block all blue light. Also Bunnings have red party light globes for about $1.60.
Caroline lives near Wollongong - she will probably post on here at some stage & has been on forum forever - well nearly!
My food tolerances took a while to change but I was 44kgs & am very tall - so very sick. Only food I could tolerate was meat, fish, eggs & a few vegies cooked in a bone broth (which I know Jack has regularly too) I was also eating very high fat - this was before I came across Terry Wahls then Jack. I still don't have dairy as don't think it is good for you unless raw which is illegal in Australia. _I_ don't touch anything with gluten but I'm fine with nightshade & nuts now.
The magnetico is fantastic & most who can afford them have them - I'm one of the lucky ones.
You weren't being negative before - just stating a few facts which are always good to look back on & see how much you have improved.
Have you had any blood tests yet? Josh (paleo osteo) on forum lives in Vic & he has been a great help in ordering blood tests & recommending supplements & lifestyle changes.
That's enough ranting for moment, but you are doing well, Jenny

### Dylan848 — 2016-07-15T11:30:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story-of-striving-for-health.18162/post-198388>

Cool, I think I'll start with those blue blockers, then consider getting some to wear out.
That's good to hear, I don't think I'd ever have dairy anyway too, mainly just the nuts, nightshades and eggs that I miss aha.
Unfortunately I never got full blood work done, my doctor only did tests related to my previous diet, being vegan, so B12, iron etc. and I think some thyroid markers, I wish I had asked for a full blood test though.
I can't really afford a magnetico right now but I've been looking at an Australian brand that seems promising, and has 3000 gauss, which seems closest to the magnetico and has positive reviews so I think I'm going to purchase one of them after seeing all the benefits people have noticed from them.
Thanks again for your help

### Jenny S — 2016-07-18T03:05:36+0300

# Thread: Severe brain fog/mental haze to the point of derealization
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-brain-fog-mental-haze-to-the-point-of-derealization.18200/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Constant_Growth — 2016-07-22T15:05:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-brain-fog-mental-haze-to-the-point-of-derealization.18200/post-198781>

Hi everyone,

I'm a 32-year-old male, and am experiencing some really bad brain issues, amongst other things. This has led me to a lot of research, and finding some great resources such as this website, but I'm still not there yet.

I'd love to hear your opinion. Here's my story. Ask any questions you like, I'll be happy to share what's going on as much as I can, maybe I'll get some clues... that's what I'm hoping for.

Since about a year and a half ago now I've been experiencing all sorts of nasty symptoms. It started with an excessive amount of stress, after several situations in different areas of life.

I then starting experiencing insomnia to the point where I'd be getting 2-4 hours sleep per night on average. I then started to experience a huge increase in my always to some degree present anxiety levels, and I had started to develop what I would call generalized anxiety disorder, though I never got diagnosed.

Long story short:

\*\* after 4 or so months of this, with my inability to feel I could do the normal stuff in my life, to pay the high rent and run a business, etc, I had to decide to move back to australia from scotland

\*\* got a good Dr on my return to australia

\*\* 2-3 months later we test and find out that I have:

\-blastocys hominis parasite in gut
\-overgrowth of streptococcus bacteria in gut
\-undergrowth of ecoli bacteria in gut
\- some pretty depleted mineral levels via HTMA testing, also now learning that it's more about the ratio (between Ca and Mg for example, and mine seems far apart according to what I know)

\- Bought an earthpulse, helped sleep during worst phase, but stopped using it completely after about 2 months based on Jack's and other feedback about it being bad long term..

\- then a few months later, it's confirmed I am somewhat Pyroluriac (19.5 or something)

Sleep gradually gets much better over the next year, but as I'm writing this, it's still not good sleep in my opinion.

The worst is that I feel this intense brain fog ALL THE TIME, it's virtually always present, and this is something that I probably first started experiencing on and off about April 2015, a couple of months into the insomnia. As in subject, it could even be "derealization" that's how weird I feel. Like REALLY spaced out, not present, inability to focus. Short term memory loss is a thing for me now too... today I actually put the tea pot in the cupboard (full of tea still, no intention of doing so) and had no recollection of doing so, and couldn't find the tea pot for a couple of hours later.

So some serious cognitive dysfunction is going on.

I am still not sure what it is. I'm sure that it could be one of, if not all the above factors, but I have a kind of theory as to what might have led to all of this. I have heard that overgrowth of strepp can cause OCD for example, and I've been experiencing a lot of OCD thoughts. Some reports say the parasite blasto hominis can cause serious brain dysfunction and anxiety too, so who knows.. the other theory is that it's the higher copper. I'm getting copper serum tested next week.

My hypothesis as to why the above is happening is that it's LARGELY due to the fact that I have lived in the UK for about 8 years up until last year (low levels of Uv light) also i have lived probably an overly sedentry lifestyle, and i have been working on a computer that whole time (my own online business stuff).

I believe EMFs are a large cause of my current brain function.

i am working on mitegating them, but it's currently very hard to spend less than 4-5 hours per day, as I need to create income to live, but mainly to earn enough to pay for the expenses of overcoming this health challenge that's so severely taxing my joy in life, and productivity.

So far all I have been really able to achieve is getting some Uvex sunnies, and wearing them all the time when indoors, and especially when looking at screens. Apart from that, just turning off the wifi in my mum's house and using ethernet cable.

i will get a magnetico, faraday cage setup, and full EMF consultant to look at the living space, WHEN i can afford it. That's why I have to work on building my business, but this could be MONTHS before i'm able to afford all of this stuff.

I'm convinced that my brain fog seems to be worsening over the past few months because I'm still getting too much EMF, and this is what actually somehow through off my neurotransmitters (which i think the brain fog/derealisation is related to).

What does Mr Kruse think about this?

What should I do?

I'm trying to get more grounding, sunlight and movement into my life, whilst reducing EMF, will get a RO water filter, and am cutting other meats to just stick with fish as of this week - but the brain fog is relentless and seemingly getting worse.

I started kundalini yoga to see if that will have any positive brain benefits. I'm going to start doing that like 3 hours a day, I'm just so desperate to get some relief from this, and move closer to overcoming it....

oh, and I got a new Dr this week who's trained by Walsh, so we're going to figure out my methylation status, etc

Please help if you have any ideas or thoughts of how i might improve this situation.

Cheers,

N

### Jenny S — 2016-08-08T11:14:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-brain-fog-mental-haze-to-the-point-of-derealization.18200/post-199419>

Welcome N, I'm an Aussie too - where are you living? Quite a few Aussies here.
Magneticos are great, tho expensive to get here - from what I've read here I'd be wary of faraday cage, but I'm no expert on any of this.
Trifield meters are great & fairly inexpensive over here - think I only paid about $120 dollars but can look up if you want & where I bought from.
As far as all of your other issues plenty of experts on forum to help. I know blood tests are a definite.
Good luck
Jenny

Nick

I've been following a ketogenic diet, and doing all of the lifestyle things (amber glasses on at night, trying to be in bed by 10pm, working out mid morning, walking barefoot, no caffeine). I wonder if I should consider bio-identical progesterone? I hoped to fix everything without exogenous hormones. The day after I did my saliva tests I started taking 2 tsp of Maca daily and plan to test hormones again after 3 months (should I do blood or saliva?). I'm 48, gained about 15lbs in the last couple years, waist getting bigger. I've attached a copy of my results from ZRT. Thanks for any help I receive!!!

After thought - I'm attaching some old labwork too. Thanks again!

Kathy,
last menses 6/29/2011
today 8/12/2016 Kathy is postmenapausal

Menopause. Menopause is the point when a woman no longer has menstrual periods.
Postmenopause. These are the years after menopause. During this stage, menopausal symptoms, such as hot flashes, can ease for most women.
\===========================================

ZRT, saliva & blood spot, August 2016
estradiol=just right
progesterone=very low
testosterone=lowish
cortisoll,saliva=low

get
Micronutrient Test (MNT)
post results
\================================================

Do not shower couple hours after sun exposure.
Do not use anything on your skin or eyes.
Post your latitude.
Possibly you are too far from equator.

Eat sulfur containing food, garlic, onions, MSM.
Sunlight contain UVa & UVb only 2hrs before and after solar noon.
UVa & UVb are need to make vit A and D.

### Kathy1967 — 2016-08-13T04:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-got-results-back-from-recent-saliva-tests.18267/post-199576>

Thanks so much for your time and consideration!!! I've given up bread and most starchy carbs years ago. I've never been much of a sugar eater. I"m still having regular periods - thank goodness! I don't want to stop for a VERY long time (I don't want to ever stop!). The last menses from 2011 was just on one of my old lab tests from 2011 that i posted - sorry for the confusion! I'll start doing the liver and onions - I make my own liver capsules ... but I don't always remember to actually take them! I have to pay attention to your sentence "Mind aggressively Circadian Rhythm" and focus on the AGGRESSIVELY portion of it! After I discovered Dr. Stephanie Seneff I've been adding more sulfur foods (broccoli and kale mostly) daily and MSM only sporadically. My latitude is 39.4523° N, 74.7277° W; the highest I've ever gotten my vitamin d is 47 (that I know of) and I don't supplement. I have been checking out the Sperti Lamp for years - it would probably be healthier than the tanning salon I use!

After I posted the tests in this forum I realized I should have posted them in the "Optimal Labs" section, so that forum has my labs organized in a better order! Here is what I posted there:

I'm hoping someone can suggest for me how to naturally raise my progesterone. My saliva results show that I'm low in progesterone but high in DHEA. I attached recent saliva test results as well as some old tests for reference. I've been following a ketogenic diet, and doing a lot of important lifestyle things:

-   amber glasses at night
-   trying to be in bed by 10pm
-   working out mid morning (& not lifting to the point of failure anymore)
-   walking barefoot
-   no caffeine
-   ketogenic diet (80% fat, 15% protein, 5% fibrous carbs) with TONS of fat and (grass fed beef, seafood, kerrygold butter, avocados, cruciferous veggies, herbal tea, spring water)
-   Sun on the eyes in the am every morning between sunrise and 10am (minimum of 5 minutes, max at 30 minutes.
-   In front of computer 4 days a week for 10 hours per day from 7:15am - 5:15pm but I go outside every hour for stretch and sunshine barefoot & sometimes walk down & put my feet in the lake (I work at a park)
-   Not sure if this is good or not, but I've been going to a tanning salon a few times a week for 10 minutes in the morning - I started this a few years ago when I found out my vitamin D was 16! Natural sun got it up to 32 but with work I couldn't stay outside long enough to raise it any higher so I started tanning - & my level now hovers around 40

I wonder if I should consider bio-identical progesterone? I hoped to fix everything without exogenous hormones. The day after I did my saliva tests I started taking 2 tsp of Maca daily and plan to test hormones again after 3 months (should I do blood or saliva?). A few health things:

-   I'm 48 year old female
-   Gained about 15lbs in the last couple years
-   Waist seems to keep getting bigger
-   Foot surgery in Feb of 2016 (still in pain from that - better BEFORE surgery!)
-   Raynaud's

Thanks for any help I receive!!! NOTE - files won't upload, I'll try again but here are some highlights:

-   8/3/16
    -   Estradiol (Saliva), 2.6 pg/ml, Range 1.3 - 3.3 Premenopausal (Luteal)
    -   Progesterone (Saliva), 40 pg/ml, Range 75-270 Premenopausal (Luteal)
    -   Ratio: Pg/E2 (Saliva), 15 Range - Optiaml 100-500 when E2 1.3-3.3
    -   Testosterone (Saliva) 27 pg/ml, Range 16-55 (Age Dependent)
    -   Cortisol, Morning (Saliva) 3.4 ng/ml, Range 3.7-9.5
-   2/5/16
    -   BUN/Creatinine Ratio 16
    -   Calcium, Serum, 9.1 mg/dl
    -   Cholesterol, Total 198 mg/dl
    -   Triglycerides, 41 mg/dl
    -   HDL Cholesterol, 73 mg/dl
    -   LDL Cholesterol Calc, 117 mg/dl
    -   VLDL Cholesterol Cal, 8 mg/dl
    -   Vitamin B12, 572 pg/ml
    -   Folate (Folic Acid), Serum, 9.3 ng/ml
    -   ANA Direct, Negative
    -   Antiscleroderma - 70 Antibodies, <0.2 AI
    -   T4, Free (Direct), 1.11 ng/dl
    -   TSH, 1.890 uIU/ml
    -   Smith Antibodies, <0.2 AI
    -   Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxy, 39.8 ng/ml
    -   Triiodothyronine, Free, Serum, 2.6 pg/ml
    -   Antinuclear Antibodies, IFA, Negative
-   3/25/15
    -   Progesterone (not saliva), .4
    -   Cortisol (not saliva), 24.0
-   4/8/14
    -   T3 Uptake, 36%
-   7/18/11

    -   IGF-1 (Blood Spot) 217 ng/ml, Range 100-300

JanSz thanks tons for your thorough response! Maybe someday I'll be able to help you with something!

### caroline — 2016-08-13T07:21:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-got-results-back-from-recent-saliva-tests.18267/post-199580>

Do you wear nail/ toenail polish? Makeup?

I was oiling some timber for our new deck. I was barefoot on the grass and I accidently got some oil on my two big toenails.

I have since lost the entire nail on the right toe - and will lose the other one as well.

Every inch of Our body is our solar panel......

### JanSz — 2016-08-13T16:06:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-got-results-back-from-recent-saliva-tests.18267/post-199585>

Ok, Kathy is menstruating.
In NJ, we are close, I am at 07054 zip
Looks like you have a little choice but using Red Sperti.
Must be red, you want UVa + UVb.
\-----------------------------------------------------------------
Post list of medications and supplements that you are taking now.
\-----------------------------------------------------------------
I would put progesterone at most as a second.
At first attempt, I do not want to use progesterone supplementation, but we will see about that after we make some progress on cortisol.
Your saliva cortisol is in the dumps, seriously.
And we still do not know how bad it is.
We do not know your 24hr cortisol variations.
You should get boost of progesterone in the process of correcting cortisol.
That bad cortisol makes you gain weight too.
You need cortisol to push freeT3 into your cells.
Also you do not have much freeT3 to push.
\-------------------------------------------------------
We can say that you have adrenal and thyroid insufficiency, plus other.
**I am not a doctor.**
Doctor would start guessing about what you need for the rest of your life and would newer guess right.
I do not know of better way than doing Spectracell test.
At first every six months, until stabilized.
Spectracell test is available in NJ.
this
[https://www.spectracell.com/patients/get-tested/](https://www.spectracell.com/patients/get-tested/)
will tell you who draws blood for the test.
Go there, ask for name of doctor who does this tests, talk to him/her.
Post (complete) pdf file after you get it.
We will take from there.
/
I do not know of anything close as good as Spectracell Micronuterient Analysis.
I have spend my own $$ for doing three times NutrEval from Genova.
You know only how good it is when you do it 3x within very short time.
/

/
I have a mix feelings about amber glasses.
They encourage people to have otherwise bad lighting.
/
Speaking of light
less emphasis on individual frequency range
more emphasis on complementary frequencies
(we need UV and IRa at the same time)
/
still not clear about heating and air-conditioning in this context (@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/))
/
Fraunhofer lines are the reason why we will never make ideal artificial light.
/
Sun on naked eyes the moment you open your eyes to get up off the bed.
If no sun look at
Exo Terra Solar Glow 160W
Amazon or ebay
/
There is something about Raynaud's in Spectracell,
but I can't recall what.
Overall, expect some improvement.
But if you want to know beter, fish here:
[https://www.spectracell.com/clinicians/clinical-education-center/education-center-webinar-library/](https://www.spectracell.com/clinicians/clinical-education-center/education-center-webinar-library/)
start with this one:
Saving the Mitochondria
Presented by Bernarda Zenker, MD
/
while on mitochondria, IF as explained by her in Q&A part sounds promising
/

### Kathy1967 — 2016-08-17T17:39:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-got-results-back-from-recent-saliva-tests.18267/post-199725>

Hey Caroline - no, normally I don't wear polish or makeup (other than mascara and some face powder maybe) BUT recently I did put on toe nail polish for an event I went to and its still on! I'll remove that. Caroline - I remember Dr. Kruse said something before about the use of crystals to help combat EMF to in turn help raise vitamin D levels. Do you know anything about that? I'll probably just go into the "Ask Jack" forum and ask him! Thanks for your continued caring!!!!

# Thread: Videha's Optimal Goal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 50
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Videha — 2016-08-21T16:40:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/post-199959>

Thought it best i submit a little history for Dr Kruse to peruse if he chooses to.

\*not in proper order, just flowing as i type. (focus & concentration issues also play a role- mind jumps from one topic to another).

Mother developed very severe asthma when i was born ( got told it was my fault apparently-shame & blame from the get-go), she was constantly getting ambulanced off to intensive care, very scary to a small baby, never knowing if mother was going to come back.
On one of her many stays in hospital, she was being given an inter-costal? injection, they ruptured her lung. Ended up in icu again.

She died at the too-young age of 58 from asthma attack leading to fatal heart failure...according to the autopsy report. I found her body stiff & cold, all her blood had pumped to her head & her face was totally black due to slumping over. Worst day of my life....lost my best mate, i was 28 at the time. Anyways enough of that.

Father left when i was only 3. He disowned his own flesh & blood (me) as i was born a girl & he only wanted a boy. He was a heavy smoker, mother didn't smoke but would have been getting 2nd hand smoke. Me also. Have one pic of me as a baby-father had cigarette about 5" from my face.
He died in his 60's from lung cancer.

Mother was slightly overweight,was on a lot of prescription medications, zero tolerance to the hot weather, constantly struggling for breath, had ovarian cysts,had a goitre & most probably a lot of health issues i don't remember. \*will update if,when any more memories surface.

Grandmother died aged 51-53 yrs - i was 2yrs old. Don't know much about her history apart from she was mainly healthy, but suffered from some depression. She was hospitilized for it & treated, with what i do not know. I can only imagine, perhaps drugs & shock treatment in those days?

They decided to do 'exploratory surgery' on her & she died on the table from golden staph.

Sorry i do not have more detail to share- childhood was very painful,hardwork & lonely,looking after a sick mother from the age of about 8yrs. No friends at school,no fun, such a serious life for a little girl.
My only solace was the backyard, i spent any spare time i had out in the elements, playing with earthworms etc & talking to the plants & birds...:
I say ~nature loves you back without a price tag. Thank you Mother Nature, without you i would not still be here.

Got rear-ended whilst waiting at a red light....sustained whiplash injuries x 3 separate car accidents.

Got flung through a windscreen at 18 yrs, sustained facial injuries,damaged teeth, got dumped on side of road to bleed out. Luckily someone found me,took me home,mum cleaned up all the blood,hence hosp did not realize the severity of my injuries. Hosp surgeon said another 2 minutes,would have been dead.
Hosp ordered radiation therapy for my chin, today you can still see the square where they did it. Painkiller medication & mogadon for sleep.

Had ears pinned back as a child, hosp nearly took out my gall bladder by mistake. Luckily i spoke up in time. Both wounds opened up under the bandage, i was in extreme pain, got told...don't worry....when bandages came off both incisions had separated & i now have thick scars which still hurt especially when i wear glasses to read.

My age is 60. Weight 45kg. Small frame, height 5' 3" approx, no excess fat on body, good muscular definition on arms & legs, flat belly.
Find it hard to keep weight on, always on the move, live alone, no male help so am kept buzy maintaining my home,garden & dog & helping others.
Usually have high energy, difficulty going to sleep, staying asleep. Old injuries make sleeping difficult.

Tinnitus 24/7 -365.
No tolerance to the heat nor the cold, noise & bright lights.
Libido....what libido? nope ..zip....no partner at present. Hormones...pretty much none...
Folk always used to think i was 20 yrs younger than my real age. In last few years...aging. Yep i need more sun & DHA & a host of other changes i have learnt from jk so far.

more info..........

\-mother had several miscarriages & stillbirths, drs implanted a hormone pellet made from real sheeps' ovaries into her buttock & as a result i came into the world. Is this why i have always felt like a black sheep in this world LOL \*pardon my odd sense of humor...tis part of my healing regime as i feel laughter is good medicine.

\-I was 3 weeks overdue, mum was very unwell, they induced her & used forceps to get me out. As a result of being overbaked in the womb-she told me i was so ugly, dried up & looked like an old man...that she didn't want me to be seen in public,she also used to strap my protruding ears back with elastoplast under my bonnet. Self esteem issues anyone? sheesh!

\-disturbed a mole whilst drying my back, taken to childrens hosp,admitted immediately,operated on-removed mole & a heap of roots,flesh. ribcage still tender if someone pokes that area. 8-10yrs?

\-broke my own nose with tyre lever trying to change a flat in the rain, whilst then boyfriend watched tv. 21yrs old approx

\-early menopause with very severe hot flushes & night sweats etc-(when my social life, tai chi & yoga all become too hard,couldn't bear being in hot car, still can't tolerate more than 5 mins..no air cond in car)

\-2003- work dr said i had emphysema-consulted my normal gp-ordered chest xray, hosp did xray-7 times same day.. trainee practicing ?

\-recently when i broke foot, hosp asked when last tetanus shot was, said not sure, told them i did not want one, they shoved it into my leg anyways.

\---

postcode 6112 about 48 kilometres from perth city, country style living,lots of greenery. Still too close to the masses for my liking. Very emf sensitive. I get the distinct feeling Jack will tell me to move

Love being outside in my garden soaking up all the wonders of nature, love to work in the rain, getting all dirty in the earth, am in my element when i can do this, makes me happy! Birds visit me & sing with me, especially when i play the flute. No lessons- i just let the flute & my heart flow..lovely,calming,bees buzzing around...such joy!

Summer-now that is a different story, have fair skin, green eyes & a lot of moles all over body, like the warmth but full on 40+ C temps makes me feel like i'm going to spontaneously human combust, i can feel the sun searing into skin, so unfortunately i am unable to function well in the intense heat. Used to have to wear sunglasses but stopped using them, after finding out they caused black mold, it only took about a week for my eyes not to hurt.
Had decent eyesight up until someone gave me a very old computer & since then eyesight has suffered for reading. Using magnifying glasses for comp work with blue light blockers. Very uncomfortable as they slide down my nose & affect my breathing,also hurts my scars on the ear surgery area. Stopped using them for those reasons but since discovering Jack's work- i have been diligent using them.

Concussion when young. \*don't recall what treatment, got taken to hospital- didn't even know my own name.
Severe nosebleeds when child.

Started periods very early, heavy bleeding & bad cramping, losing days from school, mum consulted hosp & doctors, their treatment was the contraceptive pill & also put me on aldactone (yellow pill)
\* suffice to say, i feel this is what most probably wrecked my hormones, given i was so young at the time.

Suffered from hayfever so badly i couldn't function/ was prescribed Polaramine (red pill)

Did parachute jump -wind shear dumped me back on earth-snapped my left foot nearly clean off, requiring orthopaedic surgery, had titanium plates & screws inside ankle for years. 2nd op to remove metal done. Still limp & painful driving manual car. Sometimes pain is so severe i overbalance & usually end up banging elbows on walls...both elbows have tendonitis/tennis elbow,visible bone sticking out.
Was given pain med pethadeine, made me vomit immediately, then stemetil to curb vomitting. Not sure what else i got given.

Just after i recovered from second surgery on ankle,got off crutches,walking normally-a drunk driver went through red light, ended up in hospital again.

A fully laden furniture removalist truck reversed unexpectedly-i got thrown to ground,pinned under back tyre - sustained broken ribs & other damage. One side of my chest is now higher than the other.

Sustained back injuries including soft tissue damage from work accident, had injections in my spine,forced to take anti-depressants,painkiller medication,etc.

Don't recall being sick much as a child,mainly had mishaps,accidents etc. \*My take on that is i felt so unwanted/ unloved that a part of me was trying to check out. Glad i wasn't successful....i want to live~

Recently i broke right foot, twice. Hosp wanted to operate, i said... i don't think so, my body will heal itself,given enough time...& it did. Walking a lot better.

Have always had problems with teeth, still have 3 mercury fillings . Anti-biotics & pain relief meds after having some teeth removed. Didn't want anti-life-biotics,dentist wouldn't work on me so i succumbed. Took pro-biotics after course finished.
Recently i broke a mercury thermometer.

Present day:
I rarely get sick & have managed to get to 60 yrs of age without taking any regular medications in my adult life... developed an aversion to medical drugs & interventions-most prescribed drugs i have had in my younger years my body has reacted badly to. Also i noted with much concern that a lot of folk on meds actually seemed to get worse then die. I also felt that it just not natural to swallow man-made chemicals.

\*No offense intended to anyone, just been my experience that our bodies are self-healing mechanisms which when given the right materials, body KNOWS what to do. Emergencies & broken bones aside.

Dietary-Only cos caroline asked....  Usually a main evening meal, either steamed & raw vegetables, greens, pumpkin, sweet potatoes, salads with either some grass-fed lamb (when budget permits) if not- chicken breast. Organic coconut oil by the spoonful. Went through phase of never eating brekkie (not hungry in morning), then tried to get into breakfast mode-plain oats raw in soaked in water overnight, to which i used to add a protein powder,moringa,spirulina, barley grass, chlorella & wheatgrass, bee-pollen, blueberries, banana & other assorted so-called superfoods. Got me into the good habit of eating breakfast. Then came across d.asprey & discovered BP C with brain octane if i needed to do heavy mental work, or his mct-xct oil, along with grass-fed butter from nz ...love the brew & i find i have no appetite til late afternoon/evening meal time. Been adding collagen to it also.

Nothing out of a package or tin, no bread, no rice, no pasta, no lollies, no sugar, no icecream,no cakes or biscuits, no chips, no takeaways, no sodas or fruit juices, usually only water, sometimes with apple cider vinegar. Green tea, white tea, used to make herbal teas then turn into what i call my medicine smoothie. Used to do a lot of juicing, fruit & vege with herbs & greens, lived on a liquid only diet briefly ( due to teeth loss-couldn't chew) - lost too much weight, looked & felt good though. Got into raw food recipes for awhile-nuts etc. Drink marine phytoplankton in water each morning.
Usually listen to my body & it's reactions & adjust food accordingly. Not that much into eating these days. Go to supermarket & come out with nothing....lol. People call me weird because i don't eat the way they do....lol...they're all sick/on meds & up until recently i have been feeling great,(aside from old injuries) &med-free.
\*\*Been making a lot of changes since finding Jack's blog. Will get this posted first, then later post my changes so far.

Have only visited the dr 3 times since 2008, for blood tests,mole check, breast check, pap. dr has always said my results were good. So merrily sailed along in life.
April 2015- a new friend who had just been in hospital -visited - i got whatever he got in hosp-some sort of bug/virus....unknown. All i know is i got really sick, worse than i have ever felt, thought i wasn't going to make it..nursed myself, got better,friend came back-i got re-infected 6 times in 3 months. I still feel i have something residual as my breathing has been difficult since then,so got a native american flute to assist my lungs/breathing

Recently started feeling bad-not my normal self-heart pounding,rapid pulse, breathless upon dressing, total lack of energy,not been able to do anything physical... i realized it was time to consult a doctor. Went to chemist to check blood pressure & pulse. Bp was normal. Pulse was rapid-was hot day & i had left my water home. Re-did pulse-was ok. I thought possibly adrenals, thyroid & hormones?? Or just over working myself.

August 2016

Visited dr, requested blood work & chest x-ray. He says i have fibromyalgia, pre-diabetes?
Have always had a lot of daily pain, but just put it down to all my previous injuries,so pushed past the pain & did what i needed to do,then suffer the bad days.
Have turned into a recluse, no children,rarely get visitors, no social life, judged as an outcast for not eating 'normal' food, have no tv etc....i don't mind as i love my own company, i have a soulmate big dog, i love to sing,play naf, dance, paint, garden, researching health (not anymore -since finding this fantastic place) though i do still like Daniel Vitalis & his ideas, re-wilding,getting back to nature, medicinal elixirs etc.

Never have felt like i belonged,always felt different but still remain optimistic,happy & love helping others. Just like my mother -who always put others before her own welfare, now i will be focussing on myself for a change.
Now is the time to help myself & start over, new disciplines, new thoughts, new actions= new results.

I am now a convert away from foodie gurus as jack calls them, my top priority now is to put into place what i have learnt so far,soooo much more to learn & act on. Not enough hours in the day, besides i now have seafood to locate, sun to soak up,light bulbs to research etc, stuff to buy, money to manifest etc....lol See, i have to get well so i can do all this amazing stuff. Any help gratefully accepted. Thanks everyone.

Now i am ready & determined to start phase two of my life where i can get to live not just exist, thrive not just survive.
This is my intention~ (been through the mill.........now to tackle the mountain.
I have the xray & bloodtest results... Q- which is best...Post on here or in blood test area or both? Please advise. Thank you

Gosh, been editing this to make this shorter & i must have deleted the paragraph apologizing for the length of this post, getting tired now....always wanted to write a book, so will call this chapter one of my old life....lol

\*I can feel good changes happening within already, even though my actions are baby steps. More in my next log.

### Videha — 2016-08-21T18:04:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/post-199961>

Already implemented some small but very important changes in my life:

After sizzling my brain up bigtime reading some of Jack's blogs,came to the forum to search for snippets that i could implement straight away! Was feeling a sense of urgency to get started, wasn't getting any replies to my 3 sent emails to 'contact us', thought i may have not made the cut...lol, so decided i would implement what i had learned so far.

My baby steps so far:

Resumed wearing blue light blocking glasses (bought a year or so ago)

Yanked myself off the forum & put myself to bed at 10PM (knew it would take me hours to get to sleep) next night 10.30pm,next night 9.30PM. Unheard of for me (usually have my best creative ideas & energy the later it gets) mother used to say i wouldn't sleep as a baby... poor mum. Yep- i now know all those all-nighters & sleepless nights have done damage.
I own that & am willing to change it, hence my earlier bedtimes.

Going out barefoot in the cold,rainy weather to get as much LIGHT as i can, making sure i go out in whatever sun there has been -not much lately-icky silver whited out skies..grrrr! Way too many planes etc!

Been eating wild caught, salt-free sardines in spring water , with avocado for breakfast instead of my usual BP coffee. That is big! Have never liked seafood-prawns, crays, crabs> yes, smelly fish> no. That is a big goal i am working to change, as Jack has explained the vitalness of DHA.

Went out in cold,rainy,overcast day looking for seafood , first at 3 supermarkets> bought 500gram salmon $13.00AU.Farmed i quess but better than nothing!
Took a totally different route home & just happened to glance at some shops>saw fish place, introduced myself to the owner with a smile & hand shake, told him the story about an amazing neurosurgeon,chatted to him about what does he like, how he does his oysters & fish, actually shocked myself & bought 4 oysters-($8.00 AU) Yikes! He gave me a free lemon too! Bonus~ He told me he eats raw salmon, but has never been brave enough for the raw oyster trip.

So, i overcame a longterm dislike of them, i got them down..Yay! Have only ever tried 1 in my life-raw-swallowed it & immediately it came up back into the ocean lol I quite like smoked oysters, but jack says raw so raw it shall be. Hope you are listening stomach>you have much to adjust to..hehee.

I rang to thank him for his time- told him i will give him all my business, as getting DHA is vital for my healing etc - i encouraged him to try the raw oysters too. I asked him...if i bought a quantity of oysters, could we do a deal pricewise as my budget is usually only about $20 a week for food. He said he would give me a discount...woot! Nothing ventured =nothing gained, glad i asked. So fish source nearly handled. Came home feeling proud that i had gotten some seafood & made what seems to be a very good contact.

That night i plucked up the courage to eat the salmon raw, thin slivers with grated ginger,some garlic, splash of Brain Octane Oil with a salad. Couldn't eat the whole piece of salmon (not used to cold slimy fishy stuff) lol (ate for brekkie next morning) I WILL learn to LOVE seafood! Yes, i am very determined, as i want my vitality & life back. The 2nd night i couldnt face cold raw salmon, so grilled top & bottom only few minutes-really enjoyed that meal. Yes- i realize heating destroys the DHA but better cooked salmon than no dinner. Summer will be easier to face cold raw salmon.

Another big change i have made is- instead of waking up & piling on layers of warm clothes (house is consistently 10-15 degrees celcius) down to 8 C some mornings & evenings... have been going outside, facing east, sun gazing, even when not sunny, eyes watering & wanting to close, started blinking rapidly, persevered each morning. Eyes are adjusting slowly, besides crying is a good release imho.. can't recall whose post it was on forum, but it made me laugh. Someone asked why they cry when sungazing..their reply was i like it.... lol
Low dopamine & the blinking cools the retina to enable more LIGHT in...right? Please correct me if i have it wrong.

Not been very nice weather lately (coming out of winter in Oz) so standing on mother earth with cold, wet feet, exposing myself as much as possible is the complete opposite of what i usually would do, shivering is my new start to the day. I then eat my sardines & avo, the dog gets the juice, scoots the tin all around the yard.Water, water & more water, then back outside whenever the sun shines. back out into sun then back into dark,gloomy cold house, back in sun, all day long. Am starting to get used to the white,cold numb feet, but still have concerns about exposing myself too much, as ido have neighbours who pop their heads over the fence with no warning. Oh well...may adopt the 'new who cares thought' as i know i need to do this.
Tan through bathers on wish-list, after red light & uv globe, Jack's book, seafood & the list goes on. Maybe the universe will shower me with some finances as a reward for my strong intent to do these things? So far i have managed to manifest a free lemon, a good seafood contact, a discount too! Can't hurt to be optimistic imho...

So even though my actions will seem small in the forum's eyes, to me they are all a big win in my new journey back to health.

\*Would like to hear any input on ways to make raw fish more palatable until my tastebuds change. Preferably thngs i can make from scratch-i don't use processed condiments.

So far, i have been feeling more happy ( more dopamine? yes? ) Either way, i feel great about facing new challenges/flipping my behaviours & routines around & having new goals.

Cheers to everyone & i look forward to learning more...........Let the new life begin........

### Lahelada — 2016-08-21T19:22:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/post-199969>

Hey, what a great start you have already made. The changes you made and the change you have seen is awesome. Keep going.

### Videha — 2016-08-23T13:16:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/post-200069>

Update: After posting list of blood tests & seeing it all messed up- my bodymind threw me a big curveball, i had intended on posting my thoughts on the blood tests & which tests were causing me concern....didn't get that far. Typing them up was very stressful, no numbers on my old keyboard, glasses were caning my nose & ears...took me all day, when i would've rather been outside.when i finally got to finish it, was very relieved it was done, the instant i saw how it posted, meltdown happened when i thought i would have to re-type it.

Started getting really severe chest pains,shaking etc- immediately realized i had to get offline straight away. Logged off shut down comp, used lavender eo, sat in dark & let myself process. Suddenly my awareness came flooding back to life & i can see very clearly where i have gone wrong & why.

The insights that i got:

The entire month of August has been very full-on for me, usually only leave my home once a week to fetch food, since health took a turn for the worse & i made the hard decision to seek help at gp, i have subjected my body to 2 long drives to consult him to get blood work done, another drive to hosp, 2 more drives to get blood tests, stressing out when i looked at my xrays (saw white spot) stressed out more when i searched white spot on chest xray (cancer) been overdoing it on net trying to figure it all out, not enough sleep, worrying about what blood tests would reveal, way more driving than my body is used to (increases pain), using more money on fuel than my budget allows, entering back into the medical merryground which i have successfully avoided for a long time (apart from getting blood tested), stressing out by having to be in all the environments full of sick patients, all the extra exposure of other mobiles, etc etc.
Then on top of that putting my face on the net has been freaking me out a lot, plus divulging so much personal information.....

All the excitement of finally finding Jack's site & what i KNOW is the information i need to heal, (big thankyou Jack for sharing such valuable information!) stressing when i got no responses to my emails to join (got that handled YAY!) , there is more..... but suffice to say...........when the crunch came last night it was a big wake up call.

I know i had to do all these things to get to the bottom of why my health has tanked, but have paid a heavy price> hence my reaction last night.

Also typing up the journal pulled me out of present time & back into the past, shocked myself at how much the body has endured over the years.
Amazing i am still here....reminds me how even tho the flesh can sometimes be weak, if we never quit, we can overcome a lot.

\*Decisions made:
I HAVE to SLOW DOWN, PACE myself & continue only working on the highest PRIORITY (3 legged stool) & studying Jack's material, figure out some relaxation strategies to lessen my stress levels, stop trying to change everything all at once, RE-FOCUS on what i can do NOW, stop worrying about all the things i have no control over, (i have always gotten distressed about what is happening in the world, be KINDER to myself >STOP pushing myself to the limit trying to absorb too much stuff at one time, schedule in some time where it involves giving to myself first for a change, lessen my energy & time output trying to help others all the time....not easy when one has been trained to serve others before oneself...but i know that is what is needed. i am the one who needs me now!

Progress report:
After allowing myself to re-balance last night,went to bed & finally fell asleep, had a funny dream about a 3 legged stool chasing me LOL..THANKS caroline! ..used to always have very vivid dreams, lately not been getting into rem, so no dreams, i take this as a sign my dopamine is improving.

Woke up to glorious warm sun, beautiful clear sky, (don't see that much here anymore ) it was much appreciated... been pushing myself to not pile on the clothes upon waking - going outside doing new routine in cold, was starting to feel absolutely icy in my core, which was tensing all my muscles up,increasing pain in old injuries etc, so the warmth was great. I felt so much better for it!

Decided to go get seafood & run some important errands, struggled lifting my roller door, car decided to throw a hissy fit... finally got going, was very breathless...went to put my bag on counter, next second i hear this swearing from behind me, rough looking tatooed lady started paying out on me, i said i am not pushing in, still she continued...........

next stop...aldi just opened last week, so went looking for spring water, found the same brand as Caroline uses......grabbed 2 bottles, bit concerned about water in plastic....BPA...... but better than nothing.

Went to new fish place, owner not there - will go back thurs. Last night i put out to the universe, that some financial help would be wonderful..... walking out of the car park, i looked down & found 10 cents....not much....but a start...lol thankyou universe!

Went to get food for dog, 3 new young guys there.....got some interesting reactions there.....am i getting my mojo back?? lol

Made a decision to start wearing my blueblockers in public.... recovering my health is way more important to me than what other's think...
Decided to not replace my bpc brain octane oil & mct xct oil.........will put that money into seafood.
Decided to spend some time pondering what i have that i may be able to trade for seafood, worth a try. Nothing ventured...nothing gained.
DHA top priority..
Last week i bought krill oil after reading somewhere on blog or forum that jk said ok to use it, i figured dha from that would be better than nothing, so spent $30 on some, then found another post saying not to use it, so today i took the unopened packet back, which enabled me to buy salmon. YAY

Lots more .... won't bore you with all the details... \*the meltdown's upside was that i felt my normal way of positivity & trust returning again, meaning i remembered how i used to function....love what & who i am, put out for what i need, take what comes with gratitude, trust that the universe always provides. Will share another story about that topic another time.

(still have lots of pain & worrying about the test results-no feedback so far)....
spent most of the afternoon in the sun
was GOOD day today > sardines,spring water & SUN....HAPPY happy. dopamine must be improving.....

So all in all progress is being made.
Oh & i need to be as loving & gentle,compassionate to MYSELF, as i am to others.....tis my time to shine my light upon myself.
Chapter 3 over & out lol

### fitness@home — 2016-08-23T15:56:24+0300

### Joeakim — 2016-08-23T18:31:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/post-200080>

Trying to absorb, especially if you are like me "johnny come lately" into the group, Jack Kruse's blogs and podcasts is nearly impossible and quite frankly exhausting and stressful. However, I go back to the BIG THINGS which are the 3 legged stool: Sunlight, water and magnetism.

I get the morning sun to reset and restart my bioclocks, drink lots of spring water (make them EZ water) and limit artificial lights whenever possible and blue lights at night. Get the seafood if you can but don't stress by raising the cortisol. Simply do the best you/we can. Exercise if you need to.

My goal is to create as much happy chemicals in my brain, make good decisions, keep away from the hospital unless you work there, and only to see my PCP to keep checking on my wellness by doing labs. Along the way, make impact to those who I love and care for. If you really want to be happy, go make some stranger's day by doing something extra special!
Best of luck and happy trails!

### Videha — 2016-08-23T20:50:18+0300
