@Joekim, yep i can relate to your 1st paragraph...(apart from the word 'impossible'- i try not to use that one )
i decided last night to focus on the 3 legged stool as best i can, finding it hard to focus on reading,as i'm getting more anxious daily, been trying to figure out my blood tests myself, dr says all are fine, well imho, he is 'treating the labs & not the patient'...the labs don't look fine to me!
Re: exercise-used to lift weights (had lovely big well-defined muscles on arms & was very strong) guys in the street used to comment on my arms, lol my exercise was hard yakka in yard, until recently when i suddenly started getting very breathless & severe chest pains, now have had to back off anything physical, getting dressed i feel like i am about to have a stroke...........which is worrying me silly.
Re: the doing something special for other's...have done that all my life, still do, i like to make a difference in people's lives...... You sound like you are well & truly on track...well done!

### Videha — 2016-08-23T21:14:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/post-200094>

update: salmon dinner, bed 10.30pm,still awake 1am, unable to get to sleep, heart rate racing,was feeling really tired -thought i would fall asleep, but didn't, decided to get up, check in here to see if there was any feedback on my blood tests, nothing so far..
Am still feeling very positive about all the new changes, goals & being here, enjoying the interaction with everyone, learning new valuable information- however underneath that surface level, is my deeper reality of> i am feeling really scared about what is happening to me.

\*The breathlessness & chest pains are happening to me even when i am sitting or laying down, not moving, i am not exerting myself at all......very scary! Am used to being pro-active in my health, but this time around feeling like i don't know enough to help myself....since i don't fully understand all the blood work results ..

Thank you to everyone who has interacted with me so far, i look forward to learning & sharing with you all in the future.
Back to bed now, will check tomorrow to see if any feedback. Goodnight all

### Videha — 2016-08-24T21:55:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/post-200148>

update: today i typed up a post,really laid myself bare,speaking my truth as is my way..it was very up & down, covering some questions, some insights, concerns etc,spent a lot of time going over & over it trying to make it shorter as i was feeling that i am being too long winded,which will put people off from reading my posts. People are buzy i know!
I always write from my heart & as my mind downloads to me, don't usually 2nd guess myself either. Or edit myself.

Got it typed up really quickly, felt i should edit it again, blew a lot of time to make sure it wasn't too long.It is still too long, wrote a special someone on here asked what she thought was best,reply pending..since i am so new here i don't know all the rules & regulations, yet!.. decided to hold back on posting it til i heard back, spent some time liking posts i came across & replying on forum, blue light blockers really aggravating my old nose injury - getting a large red painful lump/ bruise on my nose, forum is easier for me to read so spent time there (am finding it more & more difficult to read the blogs with the really long paragraphs & no linespaces,not complaining!) just explaining my truth.not used to having to explain myself either lol i know i should be reading the blog more than the forum, so much there i want to learn...my life depends on it!

felt good went i logged out, very tired after last nights sleepless night... tonight once again lay there from 11am-1.15am still wide awake,wasn't feeling bothered by anything,apart from the not knowing what is going on with my health..

Usually when i have trouble falling asleep it is on increased pain nights or when i'm worrying excessively about something...that i have been trying to resolve... tonight was totally different...sleep is now worse than ever, felt my dopamine has been building up again from my new routine, something is either depleting my dopamine as quickly as i am building it up..or something>my thoughts> blueblockers not blocking enough blue light... himalayan sea salt lamp decided to die on me, had to resort to lamp.
Or the HRT is doing what all pharmaceuticals have done to me in the past (why i have a very hard strict policy of never imbibing any chemical....not natural! causes more imbalance.. i chose to give hrt a trial against my better judgement to see if it would work...... now regretting it... i feel that i should stop using it ( explained that already in blood test results)
i could be wrong but i can feel my system is going up & down, moods too ......... this is not normally how i feel, despite my life being tough, am usually always good natured, not moody or irritable...today when i nearly fell over from being too stiff \*(in unposted post)..i yelled at the dog...i NEVER do that, he is my treasured, loyal companion....so that upsets me... all in all my whole being & disposition is changing...& not for the better...not good! i acknowledge i have had gains but now feel like i am going backwards...spinning out of control is not a feeling i am familiar with......

For a couple of days my sleep was improving (not getting up at 3am) YAY- now 2 nights in a row wide awake now 2.15am!! when i have difficulty getting to sleep, after about 10mins i usually will get up, have a drink of water,chamomile tea-that usually works, now because of my new knowledge re blue light, i've been just laying there which is frustrating.

decided i would get up & post the post after all, pondering maybe that is why i wasn't falling to sleep> so i am taking the risk once again of getting kicked out for yet another looooooooooong post. x2 not going to keep apologizing for my way of expressing myself...also not something i normally do... getting kicked out is the last thing i want...i'm a huge risk taker
Proof> jumping out of perfectly good aeroplane for one....walking across the nullabor alone..LOL so posting both long posts now (to clear my space...& who knows who may benefit from these crappy posts in the future....butt on line & bravery in full swing taking the risk now...if i happen to get bounced out..i will deal with it then. Tho i do feel Dr Kruse is a very kind,loving compassionate man, (can feel it in his energy on the podcasts)who wouldn't do that without a good reason- one never knows what will happen...that's why it's called risk.... LOL ok 2 posts coming up.... thanks everyone who has interacted with me so far,there are some wonderful folk here.... goodnight

### Videha — 2016-08-24T21:59:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/post-200149>

ok here is the one i was stressing about posting.....
yesterday-lifted 15kg bag dogfood into trolley,into car,out of car, partial collapse-getting it into house>severe back pain today..where are all the young strong helpful guys these days?
\-----------

Very rough night, up 6..15am totally debilitated/extremely weak/body stiff,icy hands & feet,barely able to walk, into warm sun, cold wind,dog ran in front of me, nearly fell down ramp,spilt hot drink all over him, sungazed, eyes back to watering again..burst out crying (too much built up pressure/worry etc for entire month ) let tears flow knowing they are a good release & healing..

Called out to mother earth...please help me-help came in the form of realizing i had clean forgotten to add in very important info to journal. Here to do that now..will post in blood tests section above..
Please know... i am not wanting sympathy,pity or any of that type of stuff, rarely talk about my health to anyone-only listed it all down as i was asked to provide context & details in order for Dr Kruse to direct me.

Actions i took to help myself BEFORE i found JK's site.. (when all of a sudden i was starting to not be able to do my daily chores)

\-made list of symptoms/activities that had suddenly become problematic, list of what i thought it could be....adrenals, thyroid,hormonal issues, methyalation issues,metabolic issues, heavy metal toxicity?? gp is ok with me being pro-active in my own health....which is good.
\-chose to seek help/blood tests-made list of tests i thought might give me much needed insight...got knocked back on hs crp, & other's ,been trying to analyze the results myself-getting more confused.

Not too phased about the fibro side of it... (am used to ongoing daily pain & bodily limitations)...

\*\*heart symptoms & pre-diabetes diagnosis concern me the most- no expertise or knowledge in either.

\*\*I do see that Dr K has the answers -am worried my heart is going to do me in before i get a chance to study more..i know ongoing stress & worry exacerbate my condition, feeling totally out of my depth & everything i have tried is not helping.

Gp didn't ask me any questions at all, just looked at blood test results, said they're all fine, your fine! ... said to him-am used to constant pain etc (from all the injuries) but if blood results & chest xray are 'normal' -why am i getting chest pains & breathlessness, his reply -you have Fibromyalgia! ...and are pre-diabetic! He didn't explain what that was,asked what my options were...reply> medication (anti-convulsives,pain meds & anti-depressants) none of which i wish to put into my body-given when i was forced to take anti-deps before,i went downhill fast...he knows i don't do medications & has never pushed me to take anything...(which i greatly appreciate)

Told him i must have had fibro a loooooooong time-but-have never had any heart symptoms, his reply-the heart is a muscle too...i'm still puzzled why heart symptoms are now present.

Dr said u could try HRT to see if u feel any better,putting aside my strong reluctance to take any medication, feeling so desperate to get better,surrendered & thought who knows, it could just be what the body needs...got chest xray ,saw white mass>on net to research> disturbing posts about lung cancer (dad died lung cancer) spent 3 days stressing, got myself together,remembered it could just be residual damage/infection from the 'whatever it was' i came down with from friend who had been in hosp.

Started researching Fibro,lung information-didn't come up with much apart from...no known cause,medication only treatment,blah blah.. decided would have to make own natural treatment plan for myself to deal with pain issues, etc.

Made list of what i thought may help, hawthorne herb for heart, anti-inflammatory items..turmeric etc. Bought hawthorn.
Came across book on healing hormones naturally -blew my food budget on that in the hope it would educate me further & hold the missing pieces i needed. Asked neighbour who has fibro what she does-lots of medications.

\*Found JK's site & felt i had hit the jackpot, intuitively knew the answers i needed were here..tried to join..had no pic,sent 3 emails-no reply, finally found an old usb with a pic,joined,got accepted .Yay!

Stopped all other research on other sites,started reading JK's blog & forum, scouring for things i could do immediately.
Started doing new routine in mornings-sun,water,seafood,researching uv,infra red globes,checking out where to get john ott's book & jk's book,added to my list of important stuff to save up for.
Stopped all my supps-chlorophyll,himalayan sea salt,vit d, selenium-maybe that was a bad call on my part??
Saw Jack say~most with fibro have so much to handle all at once...& a lot just won't do what it takes,that it can take up 2-3 years,i knew i was & still am willing to do whatever it takes to get better!
kept reading,a lot went over my head,continued to look for info i could understand,the importance of light,dha & magnetism started to sink in-been doing that best i can!

I feel i've done a lot to help myself so far,frustrated that i still don't get why the heart/major energy/stamina started overnight suddenly & still happening, given the so-called "normal" test results. Imho the labs parameters are skewed...(no offense intended Dr K
I feel i am falling thru the cracks, along with soooo many others.

\*I realize now that it all comes back to light,dha,magnetism, & that it will be a long hard road,however feeling very scared that if i don't get some guidance soon- i may be heading for a sudden heart attack (like mum)

\*\*Please know that i am very grateful & appreciative for all the very valuable info jk is so lovingly & generously providing, i do completely understand he is a very buzy man & am not expecting anything,nor complaining, would just like gain some insight into why & what i can do, to prevent myself from becoming another statistic.
I want to stay alive as i am fascinated by his teachings & want to really delve deep into studying & learning more.

Have intuited Dr K has the information i require & i am excited to get into his work,my understanding of what i have read so far is small i know-my desire to learn & implement it all is very strong. Have a passion to learn & gain more understanding....however the heart & diabetes worries are starting to override my ability to read,absorb the info.

I feel uncomfortable asking for help..but here goes.....

I need help/advice... please can anyone share any information about how i can deal with this? Anything..feeling very desperate right now, anything that anyone feels may help is greatly appreciated!

My learning here (so far) is very basic..(the importance of the three-legged-stool )still have many questions,things i am unsure about,
didn't want to bother anyone with newbie q's... as i know the info is somewhere on site...finding it & getting through it all with heart pain happening/affecting my focus ..doing the journal/btest post has eaten into my reading time somewhat. ..blueblockers starting to aggravate my nose injury too so able to read less as days go by...going to look to see if i can locate a smaller pair when $ allow.

Q- Maybe i have mistunderstood something important & am doing the morning routine wrong?
Is there any possibility i am doing harm to myself by doing too much too soon?
Giving the body too many changes too soon, especially the additional bareskin cold exposure.

In am-i know i have to gaze to east to re-set circadian clock, in afternoon-been looking at sun also...
do we get enough light via skin alone or do we always have need to be gazing? not getting anything done when outside, so need clarity on this. tried to do some weeding whilst gazing..didn't work too well lol

Still need to learn about all the different light spectrums,timing (should i be avoiding certain times)? Saw somewhere here 8-10am & 12-2pm, does this mean i am supposed to be avoiding the other times? Been going out whenever there is ANY sun.
Yes i have rushed into things a bit,before understanding everything fully...(don't have to speak chinese in order to enjoy chinese food  Thanks Jack- that's a useful one! ..thought i was doing the right thing..perhaps not...

\*\* ? Which blog would be the best to begin to understand this more please? Preferably one with layman's lanquage if possible
\*\* ? Are there any other folk's here who have similar issues> i can search their forum posts.

Re: cold issues..haven't read much about CT yet, what i do know>when i put clothes back on & get a little warmer>i instantly feel way better...hands & feet still icy,all this winter been unable to get bodycore temp up, even when out in sun..been going out in sun whenever possible(before finding this site)

\*usually eat warm evening meals in this cold weather, now thrown a curveball to body....raw cold seafood...chinese medicine>spleen & cold food?

\*\*Is it possible that there are some folk who cold is detrimental for, if they are already unwell?

\*\*\* Tomorrow am going to do an experiment N=1 (sorry-am not intentionally being disobedient)  just feeling way too much pain which is hindering my cognitive abilities & movement > being this cold constantly-even fully rugged up at night is exacerbating pain in all old injuries) & i normally always honor my body's signals- it is screaming at me for some warmth.... when i am too cold & shuddering that is when i tend to stiffen up & fall over......not wanting any more injuries...so owe it to myself to see what happens.

Same for summer-last year spent entire season wrapped in wet towels, the minute i went outside got dizzy,nearly passing out & had to come inside. Since menopause heat has been problematic. 5 mins in non-aircond car-have to pull over.
Thinking that CT may be really useful for me in the hot weather....willing to give it a go for sure....

Have to learn more about melanoma's & skin cancers, the dreaded doughnut hole, do & don'ts etc (have large moles-got told to keep them covered...old school thinking..now am exposing them, getting sore nippies (never seen the sun before) lol, face going red, concerned i could be making things worse (oz situation-melanoma rates etc) don't know much about all that..also had a surgeon tell me a small flat black one i have will kill me & to get it off immediately....i declined....i am still here! I listen to my gut....it said leave it be!

ps- re: the word 'fine'....whenever i hear someone use that term...i hear > F'kd up,insecure,neurotic & emotional........... LOL
Maybe that's was the gp really meant.....lol sorry for swearing....wanted to leave on a happy vibe

\*\*I humbly apologize for the length of my posts & i ask for your patience please.......just 1 tiny lady wanting to save her own life -reaching out for help.
I do feel a lot less stressed after typing this out~so am going to jot worries down in notebook as part of my healing journey so i don't subject you guys to my long ramblings, so sorry, will be more succinct & to the point in my posts.

I can hear you all cheering,mumbling tfft LOL Dog doesn't speak good english so no point talking to him...lol
Thank you everyone~ x

\----------------------
\*nb to me> i AM...WORTHY of the HEALING i DESERVE! Step aside & accept what comes.......Stay STRONG! ....tis always the darkest before the dawn... stay FOCUSSED on the LIGHT.......(within & without) LIGHT will overcome this current darkness you are in..........this too shall pass. fear NOT! Embrace totally my latest piece of furniture...a powerful 3-legged-stool ;

### Videha — 2016-08-25T11:19:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/post-200172>

N=1 \* a happy positive post for a change~ lol

Just to share some good stuff from today-was outside soaking up some sun/grounding etc, had the thought gosh i am getting so behind with everything here-(since recent change in health), if i don't get well soon, my home is going to be total chaos... all i have been doing lately is standing around doing these new practices... (still weak no strength etc ) am the type that likes to accomplish something every day..never been one to be lazy.. i like to be on the move all the time,sitting hurts. even though i have hardly slept in days.....

today my spirit SOARED....thankyou mother nature... (dopamine must be rising)
also clearing my space by posting my rants...lol really helped me too.

\*i realized i had been so pre-occupied with this site & doing all the right stuff etc to heal myself, that i had not been doing something that i normally would do (something that i am drawn to doing when i feel out of balance)came racing inside, grabbed my favorite bass naf .. back outside. Playing flute ALWAYS re-connects/ calms me, balances me & makes me FEEL so HAPPY! They are so much more than just a musical instrument.......

Being a very considerate neighbour i don't play outside much... today i thought hey...i get to listen to their chainsaws, cutting metal etc & 7 kids outside screaming & yelling at the top of their voices, 1 of them screams the same word over & over & over..& over......1 cries loudly constantly...& so on...all of which aggravates my tinnitus & peaceful space....yep  environment....lol

today i put myself & my health first...i played & played (out of practise & cold hands, still breathless).....kept at it....

I had the 'bestest' time ..yep> i make my own words up too LOL my back up singers arrived> (birds always join in with me, even when i play indoors) 2 butterflies were flying 1" apart from each other circling around & around in a gentle spiraling motion close to me..every time i see 'a pair' of any creature....my thoughts are.. an omen of 'couple' , my heart skips a beat then i think maybe it's 'my time' to for love with someone...forever optimistic...  He is out there somewhere!

Next was a very low flyover large black bird, then another. i burst out laughing... have some some amazing experiences when i was waiting for my flutes to arrive from usa. will share another time...then the bees starting coming closer to me, i adore bees & have a LOT in my yard, i sit about 3" away from the bush they frequent....never been stung...they know i love them!  one bee decided he wanted to get even closer to me & tried to fly into the end of the flute....LOL

A lot of other stuff happened too, suffice to say my time outside was glorious!!........still no physical work done, however i feel i accomplished something....which also makes me happy.... new rule> must make time to play EVERYDAY...no matter what ! ...like i used to~ in other words i have to get back into my usual routine of all the daily healing things i normally do.... & have stopped since i came across JK....that just shows how much i am drawn to jack's work....everything & i mean everything else has been forgotten....LOL
Yet another chore on the 'i will do' list...when time allows...bend time bigtime to fit everything in..............

not going to proof read 4 times....yay! i just made me some time.. bye all ~

### Joeakim — 2016-08-25T16:39:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/post-200189>

@[Videha](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19324/) as you know repetition is mother of all skills. Once you acquire the knowledge, practicing is the key to improvements. Think to improve your practice in some small way leading to mastering it. That is all we can ever do including JK. Work on big things first. What is the big thing? It could have changed but by listening to JK's podcasts, I've gathered the list and I practice them.

This is a copy n paste from my journal:

**"I'm writing down order of importance from JK's podcast at Selfhacked.
1\. Circadian Rhythm
2\. DHA
3\. Limit exposure to Blue light
4\. Water (higher PH if possible and cold to create higher EZ) and lots of it
5\. Sleep (is an outlier since better the circadian rhythm, better the sleep)
6\. Cold Thermogenesis
7\. Reduced physical/psychological stress
8\. Exercise

So how do I go about being in harmony with this list?
1\. Morning sun daily
2\. Eat more fish & oysters...seafood
3\. Turn off all artificial lights if possible, disconnect WiFi and use ethernet connection, use f.lux, wear UVEX (consumer report test shows most block of blue light, wear more clothes to cover the skin when inside, use different lightblubs (red & purple)
4\. Drink spring water preferably higher PH and cold
5\. Get a daytime shift work!!!! So I can sleep deeper and better
6\. Keep doing CT from Face Dunk in ice water + ice water post shower until get the metal tube to submerge full body
7\. Yoga & meditate
8\. Yoga, HIT, or Sprint: Do it outside in the sun or severely limit working out in the gym under blue light

When it comes to food, I need to be thinking; how can I produce more electrons and make them efficient flow to energize my body"

Best wishes!**

N=1 (slightly modified routine,tweaking to see if body coped better).. a kinder,gentler approach than when i first began! know better=do better
am>warmish sun/chilly wind/cold wet soil..left more skin covered to maintain some warmth whilst gazing/grounding/waited til less chilly wind/more skin exposed/sardines/water/inside when body started tensing up from cold wind (when too cold>body tenses up=more pain/stiffness>affects my mobility=increases risk of more injuries. played flute/gazing/spring water= glorious day with creatures/nature, sun>HAPPY!
good results all around imho...body certainly felt much better for not getting too cold..CT not appropriate for me at this point>summer time it may just be what i need to get me through the heat.. need to learn about it FIRST...no more headlong rushing into things i don't fully understand...lol
Dinner salmon
Started donut hole thread-lost focus..moved on. Changed flux settings. NO artificial lights from sundown/multiple collisions with dog & furn=few minor bumps & scratchs (too risky to do this again until i can purchase uv/ir globes...need to learn more first...

edit..was using a lighter after my tealights died, burnt thumb & singed off half my thumb nail lol all not so good but way better than having artificial light on ...

### Videha — 2016-08-26T19:06:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/post-200279>

been doing the absolute best i can, given my current health/feeling of being on the verge of heart attack, have pushed myself to locate & learn for myself what i need to know, i remember reading a post whilst i was waiting for acceptance to forum that we need to research for ourselves, (already obvious to me) so i had a reticence about bothering anyone with questions... i always do my own research, i dont ever expect anyone to do anything for me..
i am trying to save my own health, my intuition lead me to this amazing site & jk...i had nor have any expectations of anyone here doing anything..

made so many big changes since i got here, learning a lot, giving my all, met some wonderful kind,supportive people..asked a few questions got great answers, more researching,reading,trying to fit in, get to know people, contribute back etc share my story as asked give context etc etc....
yes it has been hard for me, anything hard just shows to me it is worth pursuing for big benefits ensue... i want my health back..i admire Jack for his bravery & putting himself & his work out there... tis a big risk...for sure, risk is good for us....i took the risk of asking a question so i could fix my new 3 legged stool quicker...it still is missing half a leg...so i asked... bad choice it appears... i have been walking on eggshells here, since i was worried i would get kicked out if i broke the rules.. by doing long posts etc.... i have smashed a lot of patterns,behaviours & done everything in my power to fit in, do the practices...etc...all in my log...i feel bad now as i alienated someone by being a noooob beginner...

i have seen many folk ask many questions from many people...is this not what sharing our energies & wisdoms & experiences are for in this forum?
i surely am not whining about the person involved, all i know is i feel energy,where people are coming from etc.. my bodily reaction when i read the post is my problem, my responsibilty & i hold no bad feelings, nor am i wanting to cause any hassles... i do however feel that all my gains i have been striving for, have just gone up in smoke.... yes i have been on a roller coaster...it is very exciting to be learning...my health issues are what i came here to learn about, i didn't come here to be liked, i did want to be accepted & to learn from others etc.....

a beautiful supportive member welcomed me warmly & said if you have any questions...jusk ask...that made me feel it was safe to ask a question
well i did... i got a reply..... the instant i read it i started shaking all over...yes i am fragile right now..my log shows that.. i never wanted to share my life story but knew i had to...so i did...i also usually never discuss my health either...no one gives a damn about my life, i am used to that..
i let go & showed my darkest moments.... like i am sure everyone did at first knowing it would one day maybe help another..
i am very proud of all my gains ... so far.. jack posted no artificial light whatsover...i took that on immediately even though it is making it super hard typing.... posts are taking a long time...i persevered....tonight for eg...i am now way past my new bedtime....from having to light a lighter to see keyboard...would have been much easier to turn on a light... i didn't.... not a quitter... very upset now... for falling into another black hole...
my purpose in writing this is solely to clear my space before i go to bed....otherwise it will play on my mind....

when i am back in balance i will search out whatever questions i have posted & remove them.... midnight & i have broken another rule by staying up to write this........... i am only human............. rant over...... not going to proof read....i need sleep Good night ~

### Sue-UK — 2016-08-31T12:19:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-optimal-goal.18304/post-200560>

Hi Videha

I have very limited vision in one eye, and my night vision is especially compromised. I can't safely do "normal" things even if its not fully dark yet. Done various swaps to red and amber bulbs and covered a torch light with an amber filter etc which allows me to function ..... I also live with other people so when they are around I still get exposed to the more detrimental wavelengths of artificial light after sunset. So after sunset is the time I get most covered up, to stop as much of any artificial light as possible touching my skin surfaces. I use a thin black hat pulled down over my head, (with a cooled gel pack), and my blue blockers.

Great update by the way!

# Thread: Matthias's notes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matthias — 2016-08-30T18:49:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-200523>

Hello everyone! This is where I'm going to keep my ongoing collection of biohacks (of varying degrees of control and observation). I'll try and keep everything dated, and also be as comprehensive as I recall about any other environmental conditions.

**My background**

My father is an American from Boston, MA; he's mostly Irish with some German. My father has a handful of niggling health problems and only a few bad ones: he has been myopic for my entire life, needing glasses since he was in his mid/late 20s; he has psoriasis sores across his body, but typically on places covered by clothing (**clue**). He had chicken pox when he was younger and an episode of Shingles the mid 2010s. His father passed away in the early 1970s (age in his 50s) from pancreatic cancer; his mother passed away in the mid 1990s (age in her late 80s) from complications arising from arthritis.

My mother is from [Taiwan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taiwan) and came to the US in the 70s. She has bipolar disorder and is currently medicated. She was diagnosed with breast cancer in the early 2010s, had surgery, and has been in remission since ~2012. Apart from those two, her health hasn't had any major indications. Her father passed away in _his_ 50s also in the mid 1970s and _also_ from pancreatic cancer; her mother is still alive and kicking in her late 90s. (My grandma seemingly has undiagnosed mental illness, based on the anecdotes that come back from Taiwan).

My parents were both born in the late 1940s, and they had me when they were in their early 40s. I spent most of my formative life in suburbs outside of Boston, MA; about a decade ago I moved to Springfield, MA. (Population density about [4800 people/sq. mi](http://www.city-data.com/city/Springfield-Massachusetts.html), i.e. _not good_).

**My current state**

I still currently reside in Springfield, MA, because I have developed a personal and professional network in this area. (And there's a little bit that I have yet to finish).

I sleep on the second floor of my house in a room with the lowest electric and magnetic fields, according to my TriField. (No Magnetico yet, but I'm saving for it). I have a weak Wifi router in the basement, and frequently disable it. I chose a house with a large yard (and tall fence), so I can go out first thing in the morning and get almost naked sunlight daily.

Unfortunately, my professional life consists of working at a desk in front of computer screen (cf. profile picture). Additionally, _at work_ I'm exposed to at least a 5-6mG magnetic field and with frequent Wifi hotspots around the office and the shot. Worse yet--my personal interests involve some level of screen usage--ones which often require accurate color definition (I like movies and make short ones in my spare time). Both at home and at work, I wear my blueblockers as often as is feasible

Days when I eat breakfast (probably ~3 days of the week), I'll have a BP coffee, a half dozen eggs, and probably some beef. If I ate breakfast, I usually skip lunch, but both are almost every day devoted to seafood. Some amount of crab, oysters when they're fresh, lightly-cooked wild salmon, etc... Gotta get my DHA. Usually after dinner--and I try to get this done before dusk--I'll do my cold thermogenesis. In the winter, I don't heat my house, and I make a point of walking around outside almost naked on the non-windy days..

**My goals**

Concerning just my health, my goals are modest:

The things I _really_ want to fix are:

-   Cold hands/feet
-   Minor psoriasis/dandruff/skin indications (which I'll include pimples)
-   My myopia (slightly stronger than 20/40 vision--both eyes)

And the stretch goal is a meager:

-   Be biologically 25 for the next 3 decades

The biggest design constraint for them are: I have to spend a large part of those next decades in California--both for the desire of my heart, the movie business, and also because I need to accomplish certain things in California.

### Matthias — 2016-09-15T23:12:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-201493>

Biohacking notes

In the past few weeks, I’ve done some one-off trials of different things based on what I’d read and learned:

-   Wine consumption
-   Nicotine
-   Slight reappearance of pimples

**Wine consumption**

In Mid-late August, I stayed over with some friends in, and in the evening I had some red wine with dinner socially. It had been months since my last drink (which had been under similar conditions).

Over the evening, I drank 3 glasses within about an hour. While doing so I had made sure to drink plenty of Pellegrino water, and I kept my blueblockers aligned with the rest of the evening. I started drinking ~7:30 and finished at around ~8:30. By 10PM, I noticed that I felt totally sober.

Having explored this, I would later discover [Rub Sal’s talk at Nourish Vermont](https://youtu.be/wHDNW4qQI9I?t=3836) (timestamp included). Between both forms of information, I decided to try and reproduce this once I was at home. I bought a bottle of Malbec (100% Argentinian Mendoza) to have with dinner in the evening while I ate (almost) naked in the setting sunlight before dusk.

Didn’t feel a thing.

Through the week, I would try one or two glasses, all with the same effect. (**Note**: I made sure to abstain from CT during this time based on Jack’s FAQ: [https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-13-the-faqs/](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-13-the-faqs/) and also a stray post I’d read where Inger had also mentioned things going badly having done CT after drinking).

During the weekend on the subsequent week, I deliberately drank 3 glasses of wine within 30 minutes with my lunch; I did so while almost naked and around ~2:30PM, just after solar noon. Same thing: I didn’t feel a thing beyond a minor motor inhibition, which dissipated after an hour or two.

The idea for drinking mid-day with the sunlight on my skin comes from either Jack’s or Rub Sal’s talk at Nourish Vermont wherein they mention that resveratrol absorbs UV light, and another stray remark that one of them makes is “when you’re getting UV light and doing CT, it almost doesn’t matter what you eat or drink”.

**Nicotine**

Subjectively, I’ve noticed that I always find myself struggling with is being motivated for non-self-initiative projects (mostly at my workplace, but also just tasks for/with people in my personal life). This was partially alleviated whenever I would drink coffee (usually black, but I really enjoy bulletproof coffee).

I would later develop a thought that it might be related to my dopamine metabolism. In the recent period of time of the past ~2 months, I had noticed that this effect of unmotivation had gotten worse. What I hadn’t realized at the time was that I wasn’t doing cold thermogenesis (outside of having done it every day when I was staying on the 15th floor in downtown Philadelphia for a chess tournament).

Jack had remarked on nicotine has a worthy biohack and having regulating effects on dopamine. Both Jack and [Rub Sal](https://youtu.be/wHDNW4qQI9I?t=4049) (timestamp included) and [Luis](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/page-5#post-197918) and the had emphasized that it was to be used synergistically with UV light. (Jack had also mentioned that this is partially why [there’s a lower incidence of cancer from those who smoke outside than those who smoke indoors](<http:// https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nicotine-and-mb-and-high-uv-exposure.18173/#post-198460>))

At the time I decided to try the nicotine experiment, I had wound down the Malbec trial. (I abstained for two days before trying nicotine).

I bought the 21mg dosage of NicoDerm CQ from the Pharmacy. (It cost fifty dollars. Fifty Dollars!). I cut up a single 21mg patch into quarters and put on one patch in the evening with dinner while I ate almost naked in the backyard. I felt—at most—a mild effect, including both my heart and my focus.

The next day I tried the same thing in the early morning sunlight, and later got sunlight around ~8:30-10AM.

**Woah** it felt great. I got _so much_ done that day—and it was all without any serious indications that I’d get from an overdose of caffeine (e.g. heart palpitations, racing mind, inability to focus for long durations, etc…). My performance rating on the chess tactics server even went up, too. My response time _decreased_ for individual actions.

I tried it again the next day with the same sunlight conditions, and it felt the same. Nicotine is great.

However, this biohack told me an important lesson: **do cold thermogenesis**. I realized after digging more that the reason that my dopamine metabolism wasn’t performing as it should—and other things like my sleep hormone cycles leaving me feeling a little too groggy—was due to my not doing enough CT.

Nicotine is great, but it doesn’t replace changing the internal, cellular quantum yield.

**Reappearance of pimples**

Within the past ~six weeks, I had noticed I had been getting more pimples. Minor sebaceous ones that clear out in a couple days—nothing drastic. They had appeared, gone away for a time, and then recently reappeared.

I did notice, however, that I had almost none for a period where I was traveling to/from Philadelphia on a trip last week. On said trip, I was in a hotel at altitude and without a Quantlet. So I did heavy CT for the day I was there, and I exclusively ate seafood. Oysters, littlenecks, shrimp—everything.

No pimples.

Even when I'd been in a magnetically compromised environment without optimal levels of sunlight. When I had come back, I resumed my usual diet. It’s more land food than I would like—primarily for budget and availability reasons. (Turns out getting living oysters in the Massachusetts inland is not as convenient as being on one of the coasts).

But something I happen to really enjoy is _pork_. Specifically pork belly. Anyway, I suddenly came to my senses and realized that the pimples and sebaceous indications are due to my O6 being imbalanced.

\~~~~~~

Supensaa, great finds! I’ve got some stuff I’ve learned about my condition as it relates, and I’ll elaborate on all of the bulletpoints:

-   Critically control **all** of your the (blue) light environment
-   DHA intake must be very high; supplemental iodine food is essential
-   Fournier effect on extremities
-   Sunlight & Grounding heal ailing tissues
-   Improve the magnetic sense/fields/environment

Now, I know all of this sounds like straightforward, run-of-the-mill Jack Kruse Forums advice, but particular technique is why I’m elaborating.

**Blue Light Environment** – this one was the key for the biggest reduction of my symptoms _across the board_. In the winter, I had subjectively noticed that my conditions get worse (this should be a **big hint** to myself and anyone else). This meant my scalp was/is flakier, I’m more prone to get pimples, etc…

From some biohack notes, I had decided to start wearing blue blocker glasses indoors in the winter (keep in mind I’m in Massachusetts where UV light totally goes away). As you can see from my picture, I spend my whole day bathed in blue light and light from computer screens.

After as quickly as ~2 weeks, I had noticed that the amount of skin that I was shedding was _way down_, and I hadn’t had any skindications from either pink psoriasis-like discolorations or pimples.

Big takeaway: **it’s basically about a blue light/nnEMF imbalance in your body caused by the environment**

It’s that simple. Use the blueblockers to take away the excess blue light—both day/night and indoors—and you’ve already reduced the symptoms.

**DHA intake & Iodine** – this goes hand-in-hand with the previous point, and the previous point on the above recent biohacks. We get the pimples because that’s how we express inflammation from excess O6 in the diet. (I haven’t had any mass spectrometry done on any of the sebum from pimples, but I’d _guess_ that’s basically what it is—that and keratin).

Blue light destroys DHA; the blueblockers prevent the destruction of DHA. _Iodine also prevents the destruction of DHA_. And pastured eggs by themselves aren’t enough, you gotta get seaweed; [dulse](https://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb_sb_noss_2?url=search-alias%3Dgrocery&field-keywords=dulse+leaf) is great, and [arame](https://www.amazon.com/EDEN%C2%AE-ARAME-SEAWEED-2-1-OZ/dp/B001AS975U/ref=pd_sim_sbs_325_1?ie=UTF8&psc=1&refRID=Z843JKQ31WP7C6EZ0V8F) is the best.

If you’re blocking the destruction of DHA while also supplying plenty of it, then you’re going to see symptoms reduce. When I’m eating oysters, my skin is as full as it is with the way I want it. (The only problem is… for me, it’s difficult to practically have them for every meal).

**Fournier effect on Extremities** – I mentioned in my bio that I don’t heat my house in the winter and walk around the house without clothing. (I’m not sure how advisable that is if I’m around sub-optimal light on my skin, in retrospect).

However, in the winter time I wear a [Cool Fat Burner](https://coolfatburner.com/) basically all the times I’m at/around the house. (I can’t keep my tub filled with water because the water will freeze, and it will make it impossible to take a shower for days I have meetings).

For the cold hands/feet, I’ve found that filling my tub with **warm** water, and submerging both my lower body and my hands in the hot water. My head, torso, and arms are exposed to the cold bathroom while I’m wearing the ice vest. This is Fournier effect; essentially I’m gaming the electron flow to my hands (and lower body parts).

This acutely helps with my symptoms for a duration, and has eliminated most of the indications. I usually only get the cold hands/feet in the winter now, if I’m doing everything else (CT, DHA, etc…).

**Sunlight & Grounding** – This is where it starts to seem like “good advice”, but it’s really really high-impact. But it’s just cellular reality of how light moves across the body.

Getting sunlight directly on a portion of affected skin helps, but getting it helps everything. I’ve had my scalp clear up even when it was blocked from sunlight (i.e. thick hair and a hat) _because I was getting plenty of UV light on my skin elsewhere_.

This is where being almost naked as often as possible and grounded (read: go to the beach as often as you possibly can!) is really important. It doesn’t happen if you’re just wearing shortsleeve shirts without a hat. All of your skin can develop skindications; all of your skin needs sunlight.

**Magnetic sense** – This is the big one:

I get cold hands because my hands are on a keyboard & mouse for 9 hours of every day. When Jack mentioned in his Nourish Vermont video that he’s living on “borrowed time” because of his light/magnetic environment at work, my current state is a shade of that.

Any number of the niggling issues that I, you, or anyone else has is a direct result of modern living. (Much as we all love cell phones). It sounds like woo, but when I moved my computer from the room with the breaker box to the room _as far away from the breaker box_, I saw my skin clear, and my response to CT skyrocket.

_You can improve all you want, but it’s meaningless if your environment un-does it_.

There are things that I know would improve where I am: getting a Magnetico, moving away from Springfield, shutting off the breaker to my home at night, etc…

And there will come a day when I’m doing all of these things, and I’m writing about how they worked right here in this thread.

Blue light is just another _form_ of nnEMF, and it’s typically what’s the biggest cause of our skin problems. But, in general, it’s the whole magnetic environment you’re in that’s the problem.

\~~~~~

Anyway, the big takeaways for you are:

-   Always wear blue blocker sunglasses with artificial, non-incandescent light (and all the time at night)

-   Get lots of supplemental iodine from seaweed in addition to eating the oysters
-   Do cold thermogenesis, and use the Fournier effect for ALL that it’s worth
-   Expose your skin to sunlight more than you think you need to

### Matthias — 2016-09-27T18:18:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-201974>

**Sleeping Cold**

I deliberately don't use any form of air conditioning in the winter or summer. For the winter, the reason is to maximize my cold thermogenesis; for the summer, it's cumbersome to manage an air conditioner because I use push-in blackout shades in my windows.

This past summer, I noticed that the greenhouse effect in my home of the hot (still, humid) air can really disrupt my circadian rhythms. Throughout the summer, I was waking up groggy, missing getting awoken with sunlight, and usually not "waking up" until I'd gone outside and done something active to start the day. And this was true even though I was getting a _lot_ of sunlight on my skin, and using infrared at bedtime.

The temperatures are winding down (as is UV in the atmosphere--yikes!), and so in the evenings, it's colder. Such as I can with the season, I make sure to sleep with as little clothing on, and use as little blanket covering as well.

What's been happening to me in the past few weeks that crosslinks from my trip to Philadelphia; in Philly, I had deliberately set the AC in my room to be as cold as possible all the time (as you'd expect). I would do the same sleeping routine to be in the cold at night.

Anyway the short of what's happened with me is that my libido has skyrocketed. Like... crazy.

And I'm now convinced that it's a mixture of plenty of dietary zinc, but also prolonged cold exposure at nighttime.

### Matthias — 2016-11-02T21:33:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-203531>

**The bad kind of update: Got a new cell phone
**
The following might be one of the worst mistakes that I've ever made while improving my redox potential more and more: I got a new smartphone: a Motorola [Moto Z Play](http://www.gsmarena.com/motorola_moto_z_play-8310.php).

I got it yesterday. Ever since then, I have felt **_awful_**.

My previous phone was a LG [Google Nexus 4](http://www.gsmarena.com/lg_nexus_4_e960-5048.php). In my Trifield measurements and subjective with/without testing, it was really great on Airplane mode. Didn't much move the needle, was fine to have against my body (ONLY in airplane mode). And, in terms of design and usability: it was nice and small, good for single-handing, unobtrusive, and I loved the physical appearance of the phone's back.

The poor girl hit the floor on Sunday, and the screen became unusably cracked.

Somewhat foolishly, I had decided to just get a new phone without testing any from the market. I ended up choosing the Moto Z Play, but I imagine that if I were to live with the phones of any of my peers, I'd have the same feeling. All phones now have: LTE, excessively power Wifi antennas, and similarly-powerful Bluetooth and Location antennas.

Whenever I _hold_ the phone, I get a sensation in my hands. When I'm at home in my nice cold house, my hands get freezing after a few minutes.

My heart starts _racing_ after I've been using it for a few minutes off-airplane mode. And that's even when cell data/LTE is off! Like, it's insane! I'm getting symptoms that I _never_ got with my Nexus 4. And they all get worse when I turn LTE on.

Honestly, it even feels like it's still generating some EMFs when it's in airplane mode (and even with location off). I biohacked keeping it in airplane mode next to my bed (something that I'd also tested with my Nexus 4 to minimal effect on my sleep); it was terrible--I woke up at least twice in the middle of the night.

\*Edit: Also, across today, I've had to drink THREE times as much Pellegrino as I normally did/do over the course of the day just so I didn't feel awful. I had to go outside at least twice as much to be asymptomatic.

\~~~~

When I was first setting it up, I had to use it heavily just to make sure all of my contacts were updated, that my email was good, etc... My mind felt _numb_ by the end. I realized:

_This is exactly why some of my friends are the way they are; why it's hard to actually engage them, and their brains just don't work the right way_

I feel some resentment to this being the direction of the world is going; there are certain goals and network that I actively pursue (specifically, I want to run for local political office), and I need a phone like this to really make all of it work.

But this--the things it does?

It sucks.

### Matthias — 2016-12-04T04:06:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-204745>

**Early Winter Update**

Now that the bad season of no-UV, cold all the time, high-fat, high-grounding season is on us, I thought I'd share a little bit about the effects that I've measured in myself (relative to previous winters), and any data-driven anecdotes.

**Response to coffee
**
Something I've observed is that in in the summer with lots of UV, my coffee metabolism is very smooth: any jitters or side-effects I get are minimal, the upside to my cognitive performance is high, and I don't get much burnout or ill effects.

This winter, I haven't experienced that much of ill reactions, but my symptoms definitely change. I'm not as reponsive to it, for a start. (I suspect this has to do with the upside of caffeine relative to the amount of supplemental UV that I get). Additionally, where I could easily abstain from it in the summertime--as much as I _loooooove_ coffee; shoutout to my girl caroline--my cravings/impulse to chain consecutive days with coffee are much higher.

My tolerance for coffee has slightly diminished. I experience moderate compulsion (which I imagine--but can't empirically confirm--is dopamine imbalance) for a slight period about ~2-3 hours after first consumption. It doesn't last beyond several minutes.

Finally, I get more ache in my bones than I did during the summer. Typically, when I abstained from coffee for any period in excess of ~2 weeks, resuming consumption would leave me with some achy bones. Jack or someone more studied would have to shed some light on exactly what's happening--and why I can feel it--but it would only last a single day even through continued usage. Drinking coffee daily in the winter, however, gives me this feeling on a much lower level--however, it happens every day.

This leads me to believe that coffee in winter is a circadian mismatch. However, I've put myself in a place where I also feel an increased dependence on it. What I'm guessing this indicates is that I need to up my Cold Thermogenesis amount and/or further reduce my carbohydrate intake. I'll try biohacking both.

**Blue light without blueblockers
**
As you nice people can see, I wear my blueblockers indoors. I worked/work at a manufacturing facility, so it was easier for me to keep them on during the day because they're safety glasses. However, something I intend to do in the near-future is apply therapeutic tints to some cheap non-prescription lenses from Zenni Optical. ([hat tip from Jason Lauritzen](https://medium.com/@jasonlauritzen/blue-light-and-health-plus-how-to-make-your-own-blue-blocking-glasses-1a3d2694ad60#.rmqtomz20); that link will show you how you can do the same). However, I haven't made them yet. So in situations where I would use attractive frames, I just go without.

Today I had a workshop/seminar in which I was without my blueblockers but also kept in a windowless, fluorescent-lit room with a projector for about 6 hours. Over the course of the session, I could _feel_ my eyesight worsen: I'm normally just under 20/20 because of some astigmatism that would correct itself if I were in a location of the world with UV light all the time; at this seminar, I was losing focus on typical things I'd been able to focus on by the end. Additionally, I'd found that my eyes were giving off floaters--something I found more common as a kid, but that I rarely get since changing a lot of my quantum/magnetic environment.

Anyway: I can't make my special glasses and ersatz Carbonshades soon enough.

Matthias!

What's up man ~ I grew up in western Mass and spent the first 15 years of my life there...

I know springfield's not the most optimal place to live but there are plenty of great spots in the area... The Berkshire region is just a short drive away. Definitely worth it for even a day trip just to get out of the city and go camping or for a hike. Get out of the city dude!! Nature awaits!!! Even in western MA...

### Matthias — 2017-01-01T15:10:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-205983>

**Magnetico: Day 1
**
Shortly after posting last night, I went to bed, natch. I noticed I had some difficulty falling asleep--though I don't know if it was related to the pad, to my excitement, or to having been slightly stressed just beforehand after lugging and setting all that weight. Gretchen mentioned that there are possible detox symptoms, but also mentioned that her read on it was that if my methylation pathways were working right, I shouldn't have much of a problem.

Anyway, at around 1:30AM, I woke up in the middle of the night. I was aware disrupted sleep was a possible symptom, so I'd assumed that it might happen to me. But what I'd noticed was:

I was really, really warm

My house is cold--especially in the winter--because I deliberately don't heat it. But because it can get _very_ cold, I have a fleece blanket, and two comforters. (I'm not married and like the pressure on my body. What do you want from me?).

When I'd woken up, seen the time, and gone back to sleep I had trouble getting back _because_ I was so hot. It dawned on me:

_Oooooohhhhh_ **this** _is how humans stayed warm at nights in winter_

Seeing the effects of an unadulterated magnetic field--even after a single day--was pretty eye-opening. Looking forward to the next four week to see how it works.

I will say, though: the Pellegrino bottles of tap water I put under my bed seem to taste about the same. I'll leave them there, though.

### Matthias — 2017-01-02T16:20:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-206037>

**Magnetico: Day 2
**
Last night I slept similar to last night, everything considered. I went to bed at ~9PM because yesterday morning I had gotten up at just after 6AM. I had omitted that detail, but that was a large departure from getting up closer to ~7AM (with natural sunlight) as I had been. In the evening, I felt the orange glasses _really_ start working at around 7:30 all throughout going to bed. It felt so weird not to want to stay up and watch the football game.

_Key observation: I suspect that the Magnetico enhanced my response to the orange glasses again; they were having a strong effect similar to when I first started wearing them--much as the **red** glasses do/did when I would put them on_

In the middle of the night, I woke up at ~1AM again. Checked the time, and then went back to sleep. I was **not** very warm this time, however.

At around 5AM (5AM!) I woke up. I'd seen that it was still dark, and thought it might not be optimal for my circadian rhythms--given that there's no light--so I tried to fall back asleep. I couldn't easily fall back asleep, and was in a half-asleep, half-woken state until just after 6AM. I'd gotten up because I'd started to feel the need to urinate. It reminds me of when I first installed all of the pads on my bed: I mysteriously had to urinate after I'd finished setting them all. (I presume because of the effects of having the magnets that close to my body). I ended up urinating **way more** than I'd thought I had to--and probably more than I had across... the past few months.

_Key observation: My need to urinate feels very similar to my need to urinate after doing cold thermogenesis. It reminded me of a post I came across--that I can't find now, of course--in which Yew Wei compared thermogenesis and its magnetic effects to that of the Magnetico for the water chemistry in the cell_.

And finally, between yesterday and today, I've been keeping bottles of tap water in Pellegrino bottles both **under** and **on top of** my bed. I haven't been able to "taste" a difference between the two. However, after having kept the water there, I can notice a difference \*between Magnetico water and regular tap water.

While I can't measure the amount of exclusion zone that the water is constructing (it certainly isn't gel-like), I _have_ subjectively noticed that I don't have the desire to drink as much of it as I would with other, normal water from the tap. That effect will probably normalize over time--as will the effects of the Magnetico--as my psychological bias to the details subsides.

I know Jack mentions the copper pot for water **on top** of the Magnetico, but given that water is a dipole, can I still get the same effects as keeping it on the bottom of my bed? (honestly, it's just more convenient that way).

### Matthias — 2017-01-03T16:25:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-206083>

**Magnetico: Day 3
**
The previous night was similar to the first two, except I don't _think_ I woke up in the middle of the night as I did. (I can't actually recall, so I'm guessing I didn't). I went to bed at around 9:30--about 30 minutes later than the previous night--and I woke up at **4AM**. Four AM!

However, even at 4AM, I woke up feeling more refreshed/less groggy than I had in the two previous mornings. I had considered getting up because I was alert. However, I again thought "this can't be wise, given that I'd be up very early, and be around artificial light, etc..."

_Key observation/question: So far, the amount of actual sleep I "need" while using it has plummeted. Normally, I'd go to bed at around 10-10:30-11 and wake up around 7-7:30 with sunlight. Here, I've netted as much as 7.5 and as few as 6.5 hours. Is this how early humans could live? (Or at least get the most out of their day). If this is how it's going to be, this will be **awesome** to have more waking hours to get things done. It's just weird to think of it in this way._

Anyway, I decided to force myself back to sleep. I think this might be a mistake because it's a circadian mismatch. At the time I went back to sleep, I felt myself getting a slight chill around my shoulders (indicating, I think, that there was a changeover in my mitochondria to environmentally/activity-driven thermogenesis, instead of resting/sleeping thermogenesis). However, I only slept for ~45 minutes and re-woke at 4:45AM.

When I'd re-woken, I felt myself with some minor sweating. As-in, I think I had been generating heat after having gone back to bed. It still being early I **again** went back to sleep. I then slept until around 7:10AM. (Which I considered more reasonable--there was light out--and I got up).

Either on second pass or third, I _dreamt_. The previous two nights, I hadn't dreamt--or at least I hadn't had conscious recollection of dreaming. In retrospect, I _think_ I dreamt between 4 and 4:45, but I'm not sure. (The dream was great; I was part of a Presidential transition/reservation meet--that was **not** the current administration; I would have remembered The Donald--and I was networking with various people. I had my great pick-stitched blue pinstripe suit on, and there were attractive women who I was effortlessly approaching. I mean, it was like real life in that way, but it was at a ranch with meetings and business. It was a fun dream).

I think tomorrow I'm going to be more conscientious of the time, and just say "screw it" and get up wicked early. Sleeping the whole night--again, in my recollection--seems like a major watermark in my adjustment.

**Coffee with Ubiquinol**

In a follow-up to my remarks earlier about coffee in the winter, I'd done more biohacking (or at least... written observations about a biohack that I'd probably done earlier) with coffee in the wintertime.

For a business trip I took to SC two weeks ago, I purchased some Ubiquinol for the flight. And I had the balance of the bottle left over. While I had recalled that any non-whole food supplement should be accompanied by ubiquinol to offset the increase in protein turnover with any supplement. When I'd biohacked _not_ using ubiquinol in the summer with coffee, I'd turned out okay. (\*Which, in retrospect, was likely due to all the UV light I'd been getting. Recall: I'd been _drinking **serious** amounts of alcohol in a biohack and_ also _been turning out okay_)

So, I'd lapsed and _continued_ using coffee without any ubiquinol into the winter. Anyway, having resumed consumption of both (usually around 400mg Ubiquinol and 20mg PQQ for synergy), I can say that my caffeine symptoms such as loss of focus, compulsion, racing mind, etc... have all been strongly mitigated.

Just like GE (via Reuben) says:

_The tool you use to measure is more important than the improvement that you make_

### Matthias — 2017-01-11T03:40:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-206594>

**Started a new job, chipped my tooth, got sick**

On Monday, I started a new job at place that does lots of work lasers, powdered metal, and exposed hot metal. It's great--I really like everybody there, and what we do. However, it had been awhile since I was around very powerful non-native EMFs, as I'd been funemployed. At my new workplace, not only are we around the EMFs from major CNC machines (and it's _much_ more hands-on), but there are also _3kW induction heaters_ around at various parts of the shop to heat up workpieces. I'd had to spend some time around one yesterday for most of the day.

One of my coworkers was sick with a bad cold yesterday, and he ended up being out for the day today. However, I felt unvanquished and immune, given that my exposure around such people didn't lead to any illness. **But...** Today, while I felt fine and not ill, my voice became more and more hoarse as the day went on. If I had to guess, my body resisted whatever it was, except in my throat. I'll likely report back if it gets worse (or gets suddenly better).

However... The most interesting thing to me happened _this morning_. I'd decided to make some bulletproof coffee, as I'd been getting up before dawn in order to make it to work on time--something else I didn't have to worry about when I was waking up with light during my funemployment. Anyway, I happened to bring the pitcher too close to my tooth, and it hit against my left upper front incisor.

_My tooth chipped
_
Every other time that's happened, my tooth was fine. Totally intact. This time? A small part came off. (For which I'm very thankful, as I'm glad it won't require any kind of inordinate dental care. I might have it examined--I might not).

However, this forum has shown me that there aren't really such things as coincidences if you look deep enough for the proximate causes of everything that happens--both metabolically **and** in life.

_I don't think all three things happening at once are a coincidence, either_

# Thread: Jen's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-journal.18457/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jencole — 2016-09-22T06:45:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-journal.18457/post-201723>

Thanks to everyone who contributes on this site! It is truly a lifesaver.

I learned of Leptin from an internal medicine doc who I thought would “help”. He mentioned weight loss surgery as a solution to my problem, I asked “can we simulate weight loss surgery and get the same results?” I’ve not seen long term data on the surgery and health risks and after 3 family members had this procedure, I was not convinced the weight would stay off.

So he told me about leptin and suggested I research, then prescribed phentermine. I had success, then gained the weight back. I would exercise and 15 minutes in I would could have laid down on the floor and taken a nap. That’s when I knew something was wrong, something that I didn’t have control of and something that had plagued me since puberty.

Law school, two masters degrees, a bachelor’s degree and an outstanding career, I could accomplish anything in these arenas, but I could not for 25 years, conquer weight loss. Man this pissed me off.

I returned to the same doctor to seek help with the leptin, he said that I was exhausted because I was carrying so much extra weight. I walked out and spent hours researching experts in the area of leptin. I knew I needed direction and conventional medicine wouldn’t get me there.

I need help, I want to be better, and I want to fix this. I am committed.

I have spent 2016 changing every aspect of my environment I can think of. I work from home so that I can get outside throughout the day, stay out of the wireless world and be in the sun when I have the opportunity. I have changed to a less stressful job and I have made major modifications to every aspect of my life.
It’s been in stages, but I am ready for next steps and I believe that includes looking at my total health picture and creating a plan.

I have a biohack scheduled with Dr. Kruse in January, but am looking to get started and have direction.

**Family History**

-   I have the body of my maternal grandmother. I am the oldest daughter of her middle child (she had 9). I feel like I am her clone and that scares me. She unfortunately died of lung/live cancer, but prior to she had breast cancer and heart problems. I once tried on a coat she had custom made for herself, it fit me like a glove- this scared the shit out of me. She was a size 22 and obese most of her life. She tried every diet she could think of, religiously and was never successful in her quest to lose weight.
-   My mother doesn’t seem to have health issues, she is a little overweight in the stomach area, but no hx of cancer or anything else.
-   Dad is diabetic and has high BP, he is very very active, always outside.

**My History**

-   Currently 38, 275#, 5’3
-   Started gaining weight around age 10, was skinny until then. Started gaining weight at this time.
-   Grew up on a horse farm, didn’t have cable until college, always outside growing up. Was very very active.
-   Was diagnosed with hypothyroidism at age 20. TSH was 26, I have not seen it that high since then.
-   Menstrual cycle has never been regular.
-   I have had 1 pregnancy/1 delivery. In the beginning of the pregnancy, I felt great and even lost weight. At the end I was diagnosed with preeclampsia and delivered a month early.
-   In the past, I could do an extreme diet and lose around 20 pounds. I could hold that weight off for a month or two, then it would always come back.

**Current Health**

-   Menstrual cycle not regular
-   Fatigue often
-   Brain fog
-   Hursutism
-   Restless leg syndrome
-   Thyroid not optimal
-   Obesity (BMI 49, weigh 285)
-   Iron Deficiency

### Goosey — 2016-09-22T19:09:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gooseys-optimal-journal.18460/post-201750>

I've been thinking about doing this for over a month, and am tired of having all the thoughts clanging around in my head and not actually putting them out there. So this is me, kicking the first pebble down the hill.

### Goosey — 2016-09-22T19:56:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gooseys-optimal-journal.18460/post-201758>

WATER Fall 2016

So I live in AZ, and the first reply I got from the Doc when I did my introductory post was, "Don't drink the water in AZ because of the fluoride". So here's some context about my water.

I live in Chandler and the water here is super hard. Sometimes it reeks of chlorine. Having lived in AZ for 30+ years, I'm used to the water tasting like dirty stone, and avoid drinking it for the most part. However, Doc's warning has me avoiding the ice too, for the most part. I don't take showers, and when I bathe, the water is pretty tepid. I also mix a little ascorbic acid into the bath water to neutralize the chlorine.

As for my drinking water, I've been using a distiller for over a year. In years prior, I've tried getting gallon plastic jugs of distilled or purified water from the grocery store, but found them to have an astringent quality that dried my mouth out. Then I found a brand of bottled water that was slightly alkaline. It shipped from Nevada and had a somewhat more viscous quality that wetted my mouth better. If I drank that exclusively, I could probably kill two cases a week. After a while, I realized that having someone in Nevada, a drought state, bottle up their drought water, and have a trucker drive it to a warehouse in the drought desert I live in (wasting gas), then someone else drive it to the store (more gas), so I could guzzle the water down in an hour and then generate X number of empty plastic bottles that need to be recycled... It just seemed silly and wasteful, which is how I decided to try the distiller. I got it used on eBay.

I just recently heard a podcast featuring Jack being interviewed by a stylist/nutritionist from Los Angeles, who drives up into the mountains to collect his own spring water, and in that interview Jack mentioned running RO water through an aerator and over volcanic stones to help structure it (he said something about knocking electrons off as the water ran over the stones). So I bought some volcanic rocks and an aerator, along with a 1 gallon glass jug for storage. I was also concurrently searching for a better collection bottle for my distiller, because the one I was using was plastic, and had a tiny opening that was hard to clean. Ultimately, I settled on a glass Kitchenaid 5 qt bowl for a stand mixer. I started using the aerator, the rocks (in a steel funnel), the glass jug, and the mixing bowl all at the same time, and I noticed immediately that the water had a different texture. The aerator was kind of a pain in the process (I only have the two hands), so I dropped it out for a few cycles, and I realized it wasn't really contributing to the change in water texture. I assumed it was the rocks that were making the difference, but I started reading The Fourth Phase of Water and, after a few chapters, I realized that it was probably the hydrophilic surface of the glass collection bowl.

My distiller is an older Kenmore countertop model, and I positioned the drip spout of the distiller over the pour spout on the glass collection bowl. The result is that every drop of water that comes out of the distiller has to dribble across a minimum of an inch of glass, before landing in the body of distilled water at the bottom of the bowl. When the process is just starting, the water has to trickle over 6 inches of glass to hit the bottom.

The water that I get out of the distiller now has a viscous, syrupy texture that has some of the mouth feel of a sugared soda (which is one of the things I enjoy about soda). Another observation: I decided to purchase a bottle of Hawaiian spring water, partially because I like the water and partially because the empty bottle is useful. On this particular day, I had a rare-for-me dehydration headache, and I kept drinking the chichi Hawaiian spring water, because... Hawaiian spring water. But this particular bottle had that same astringent mouthfeel and it wasn't doing anything; I was still thirsty and my head still ached. I grabbed a glass bottle that had a little bit of my distilled water in it, and drank a couple of swallows. My thirst went away immediately and so did the headache.

I work at a golf course and my days are frequently 10-12 hours long. My options at work are filtered water and ice, Dasani, or standard F&B beverages (iced tea, fountain sodas, coffee). I now take a half-frozen plastic bottle of distilled water, along with one or two big glass Voss bottles of distilled water to work. I keep one out to drink, and bury the other two in ice. The bottle I keep out can get up to 100 degrees, but it stays syrupy and kills my thirst. That bottle can be glass or plastic - the quality of the water holds up, in my observation.

At home I store the water in glass. I have a trivet covered in magnets, and the 1 gallon glass jug lives there. My dogs' bowl sits on another magnetized trivet, and they get the same water. I have a glass jar of water in the refrigerator on a third trivet. I water my plants with the water from the distiller now.

I run the distiller overnight, and usually also in the morning when I get up, so that's 2 gallons a day.

I had a DEXA scan in the spring, I'll probably have another one this winter, to monitor my bone density and make sure that this water isn't causing leaching issues. All my water, including water run over stones, comes out at 3 ppm using a TDS meter.

I want to have a sediment and contaminant filter installed on the water line into the house. I would rather bathe in less garbage, and the stalagmites that develop in my distiller are appalling.
