# Thread: Elizabeth's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elizabeths-journal.18515/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Miss Lizzie — 2016-10-03T21:32:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elizabeths-journal.18515/post-202233>

Hello,

I'm a newbie and it was suggested I start a journal entry, so here I am. Bear with me. I will try to be as succinct as possible.

Background: Haplogroup H5. Blonde hair, pale skin and brown eyes. 99.9% Northern European. Pretty boring. :/ My mother is first gen born in Canada. Her family is Scottish, Irish and English. My father's side of the family is English/German. Father's side of family has severe history with Crohn's disease. My grandfather almost died from it. Also a tendency to develop type 2 diabetes. That side tends to be pretty overweight. Mother's side is a train wreck. Dairy allergies, chronic fatigue, hayfever, inability to deal with stress, sleep walking... you name it.

I work at a university and have a stressful desk job. Co-workers and boss are great, but it's a deadline driven, high stakes position. I do like the work though. I always enjoy a challenge.

I grew up in Vancouver, BC the land of rain.

\-Age 6-22 chronic bronchitis with antibiotic treatment.
\-Age 10 puberty
\-Age 13-19 erythromycin everyday for bad acne
\-Age 17-23 symptoms of hypothyroid, but no treatment. (Canadian drs wouldn't run the tests)
\-Age 18-22 university with screwed up circadian rhythms.
\- Age 19-28 IBS/crohn's symptoms.
**\-Age 23 moved to Santa Barbara, CA. No more bronchitis!**
\-Age 24 diagnosed with hypothryoid. Normal TSH, high T4, 0 T3. Put on synthroid and cytomel
\-Age 24 bad car accident. Sandwiched between two SUVs on the 101.
\-Age 26 surgery to L4,L5 microdiscectomy and laminectomy.
\-Age 28 severe crohn's flare up with intestinal bleeding. Also got a horrible case of shingles that year.
\-Age 31 did GAPS diet and protocol to heal gut. No more crohn's and also fixed my thyroid.
\-Age 31 no more thyroid meds needed.

At age 34, I had my first child. I made sure all my hormone levels, vitamin levels, omega levels were good before getting pregnant. Got pregnant first try. At 6 weeks, hyperemesis kicked in and I puked everyday until I gave birth. I gave birth to a super health 8lb 5oz baby boy. Only gained 20lbs. I breast fed for 18 months and was a milk cow. I would pump about 50oz/day! I felt pretty good during that time. Baby slept well and started sleeping through the night at 8 weeks. Lost all my pregnancy weight plus some.

That brings me to the present. I'm 36 and had my third health crash. About 3 months after I stopped breastfeeding, I had started suffering from severe exhaustion, brain fog, weight gain (30lbs) and insomnia. I'd be exhausted in the morning and wired at night. Any exercise made me want to sleep for a week. It was like my body just decided to crap out on me and give me a giant \*&%^ you! I gave it a couple of months, since I thought it might just be hormones adjusting. It didn't feel like a thyroid problem. I went to see my doctor. My tests came back and it pointed to screwed up adrenals. So I started doing tons of research. This ended up in going down the rabbit hole and now I'm posting here.

My test results from May showed:

CBC with differential- all normal except RBC
RBC count -5.59H range 3.8-5.10
Odd thing. My RBC count is always out of range high, unless I have intestinal bleeding... then it slips to normal.

Comp Metabolic Panel. -Everything normal.

Glucose- 94 range 75-99
CRP High sensitivity 0.4 range < 3.1

Lipid Panel
Triglycerides - 58 range <150mg/dL
Cholesterol - 174 range <200 mg/dL
HDL - 63 range >40mg/dL
LDL- 99 range <100mg/dL
VLDL- 12 range 7-35mg/dL
Chol/HDL Ratio 2.8

Thyroid
Free t3 3.2 range 2.3-4.2 pg/mL
Free t4 1.0 range 0.8-1.7 ng/dL
TSH 1.920 range 0.350-5.5 ulU/mL

Vitamin D 25-OH total - 37 range 30-100ng/mL I was also supplementing with 5000 iu of D3 per day at the time of test.

DHEA-sulfate 94 range 26-460 ug/dL

My doctor did not do a cortisol test, so I'm blind in that area. However, with my symptoms and my vitamin d and DHEA being low, it definitely seems to point to an HPA axis dysfunction.

Starting in September, I've been doing the Leptin Rx and modifying my environment at home and work.

At work, I have installed a stand up desk, turned off all the lights in my office, installed F.Lux on my computer and wear blue light blockers. I do have a window, but it's north facing and receives no direct sunlight. I make sure that I walk and get sunlight a minimum to 2x per day, using my D-minder app. My coworkers have started coming with me!

At home, we have a south facing house with tons of windows. They are open during the day and we never have to turn the lights on until it gets dark. We now turn on minimal lights, wear blue blockers and have amber lights and salt lamps to keep the blue light at a minimum. All electronics go into airplane mode and I have black out blinds in the room. My 2.5 year old also sleeps in a dark room with no night lights. He goes down at 8 and wakes up at 6 pretty much everyday. I go to bed at 10 and wake up at 6. I do cold therapy at night after dinner.

I can say that my circadian rhythms have switched back to normal! I'm tired at bed time, no insomnia and relatively awake in the morning (takes me a full hour to wake up). I lost 10.5lbs last month and the brain fog is clearing up.

I'm going to go back to my doctor this month for follow up tests to see where I'm at. I'm also going to have him test my omega levels this time too.

### Miss Lizzie — 2016-10-04T21:18:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elizabeths-journal.18515/post-202283>

I forgot to add my hemoglobin and hematocrit.

Hemoglobin 14.1 range 11-15.5 g/dL
Hematocrit 44.4 range 33.0-46.5%

### Miss Lizzie — 2016-10-20T20:34:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elizabeths-journal.18515/post-203124>

A little update. I still haven't made it back to my doctor for follow up tests, but I have been implementing more things around our home and my office.

I start work at 8 AM so morning sunbathing is a little hard. I do go out into my back yard, stand in the wet grass and look directly into the sun for about 5 min before leaving for work. I can now look directly into the morning sun without tearing up or blinking.

I have started riding my bike to work and on my ride to work, I face directly into the sun. I wear a tank top so more of my skin is exposed. It's cold in the morning now, so I get both the cold effect (although pretty minimal) and direct sun on my upper body, face and eyes for 15 min. On the way home, I'm also facing directly into the sun.

At work, I go outside and get sunshine for 15 min at 10 AM, 12 PM and 3 PM. No sunglasses and I have started wearing a tank top under my clothes, so that when I go out, I take off whatever long sleeve shirt/dress I'm wearing to get more skin exposure.

I've been getting amber bulbs and salt lamps and placing them all over the house so we can have really low light at night. Also wear my blue blockers and have ordered a pair for my two year old and my husband. I've been getting push back from hubby on this. He will grudgingly wear the blue blockers sometimes, but he thinks I'm going over board.

As for diet, I'm having my BAB within an hour of waking up (2 year old generally prevents the within 30 min of waking) and now I'm not eating lunch! I'm simply not that hungry. I might have a small snack of some raw nuts, but that's usually it. Dinner is pretty small too. Usually just a protein and some vegetables. Of course drinking tons of spring water during the day.

We have a whole house water filter, but it doesn't filter for fluoride. Our water in Santa Barbara has naturally occurring fluoride. Luckily they don't add any. They periodically try to get it passed, but everyone always votes against it. The naturally occurring fluoride is at about half of the government "recommended" amount. I've bought a water filter that will filter out the fluoride and will be installing that this weekend. It's a triple filter that will also get rid of other unwanted contaminants. I bought a device to help structure water and I'm going to do some experiments on plants to see if there is any merit to it. I have a science degree and am a bit skeptical by nature and a big nerd, so I like to test everything. I figure that if I see a difference in the plants watered with the "structured" water vs regular water, there is probably something happening.

**I've been looking into crystals, shungite and orgonite. I'd like to place some in my office at work and maybe around our house. I'm a bit confused as to what is best to use and where to place it. Can anyone weigh in on this? I was thinking of getting a shungite braclet to wear too.**

I'm saving up for a magnetico sleep pad. I think this is going to help a lot.

I'm reading the fourth phase of water and John Ott's book. I shouldn't be surprised, but I'm really angry that this information (particularly about the sun) has been out there for quite some time and most people/scientists/government have been ignoring it!!!

I'm slowly but surely changing our house and my work space. Our diet is spot on, but it's that three-legged stool that I'm focusing on. Diet isn't going to matter much if I don't get that set up correctly. My health is definitely better, but still needs a lot more improvement to be optimal. The big thing for me is not giving up because I get impatient. It's taken a lifetime to do the damage to my body, so it's going to take time to heal it. I'm trying to intervene early for my son, so he can continue to grow up healthy and strong.

### Miss Lizzie — 2016-11-01T23:21:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elizabeths-journal.18515/post-203495>

Hi Jan,

Thanks for the info. I am actually taking a DHEA supplement right now. At first it made my skin break out, but now it's settled down.

I'm a little confused about using the Sperti light. Jack has said that nothing replaces sunlight. I live in Southern California, so we have good light all year round. I use the D-minder app so that I don't burn. I'm very fair skinned and my husband calls me stain glass Lizzy for a reason. I get out 3 times per day during the week when I'm at work (I go down to the beach and lay in the sun on my lunch break at least twice a week, and I also lay out on the weekends.

I get up in the morning and go stand outside in the sun barefoot for about 15 min too.

As for the red light- well, I have hit a brick wall with my husband, so salt lamps and amber lights it is for now. I tried to use red bulbs and he completely balked at that. I go to bed at 10 PM every night and wake up at 6 am. On weekends, without an alarm, I wake up between 6 and 7 am.

I did have blood panels when I was pregnant and everything was fine. Progesterone and all steroids were good. As soon as I passed my placenta, all the nausea and vomiting stopped. I thought it would take a lot longer, and was very surprised that it just suddenly stopped and I immediately felt better.

Funny thing is, my son received sunlight treatment from the day he was born. The midwives told me to take him out, butt naked and put him in the sun for 10 mins front and back for the first week he was born so that he didn't get jaundiced. He still runs around naked in the back yard and has a beautiful, light tan all over his body. He's never had a sunburn and he is as fair as me.

Interesting thing... I just read the blog on Hashimoto's and Melasma. I had a pretty big melasma on my forehead. Since starting this protocol, it's been fading and disappearing even with all of the extra sun. I had no idea why, but I was happy about it! Now I know why it's getting better!! My eyes are also getting better and my prescription keeps dropping. Maybe I'll be back to no glasses one day.

Okay.

**Mother and grandmother:**

Both my great-grandmother and grandmother were born and lived most of their lives in rural area of Grodno, Belarus. Lived and worked on a small farm. No technology, no electricity until much later, food was local.

They did not have any serious diseases, although my great-grandmother developed osteoarthritis later in life. When my mother was born, they both moved to Riga, Latvia.

Mother was healthy before conceiving, no TV or any kind of technology usage until later, standard Soviet diet, exercised. Developed breast cancer at 30.

I will edit this post if I remember anything else... Please, tell me what is relevant information on this topic.

Hi Mikhail;

Starting with love is a very good first step.
Raise your chin when walking, look ahead not down.
Think good thoughts.

Couple items to work on.
Actually for starters only two.
Light and water.
Water is simple and short,
drink only the best spring water you can find.
Most of your daily water drink cold and not boiled.
Newer drink artificial sodas or any other concoctions.
Coffee and some teas may be exception, but newer add any type of sugar or sweetener.

Light.
We are creatures of light.
Without light we die.
Same as a grass, cover the grass with something so it will not see sunlight, and it will die.
Light is connected with everything, your sleep, weakness, depression, lack of motivation.
Light mostly falls on the ground.
You should spend lots of time being connected to ground, preferably wet.
Lots of skin should be exposed to light, that includes eyes.
Minimal clothes no glasses for maximum possible time (while exposed to sunlight).
Thinking more general, we are talking about
light and lack of light
night and day
both must come at the right time
every time, every day, at the same time day starts and latter night begins
say, if you work night shift, forget about ever getting better
if you do not see (enough) of natural sunlight, you cannot get better

when you say body composition, that sound like a talk of a gymnast or body builder
some of them look good and do great things
but they have one common problem
they spend lots of time indoor, in a gym, ballroom or office
they are missing sunlight
artificial light
is not the same as sunlight
artificial light hurts us
it comes in wrong time and have frequencies that our bodies do not recognize
it took millions of years for our bodies to develop
we cannot change in few years, it would take many many generations to change our bodies,
assuming that it can be done
you are already born, you have your body made expecting certain environment
it is up to you to deliver that environment that your body was build for

All this time it was about proper
Circadian Rhythm.

\-------
There is at least one additional highly important item related to sunlight.
Many people these days have very low vit D.

Supplemental vit d is of questionable help.
May need special UV (ultraviolet) lamp to benefit.
Actually there is two types of beneficial UV and two vitamins that we need.
UVa- makes vitamin A
UVb-makes vitamin D
Assuming that we get exposed to this UVa and UVb lights,
to actually make those vitamins we need food
garlic, onions, MSM to provide sulfur so we can make vid D(sulfate)
and
carrots and peppers to make vit A.

\-----------------------------------------
Some places on earth are missing minerals.
Common missing minerals are
selenium
and
iodine and (potassium iodide)
It is good idea to supplement them
they are need to produce thyroid hormones

You may get locally something similar to this:

iodide is halide
it is lots about halides ( good and bad)
the good=iodide
the bad=bromide, fluoride, chloride
watch out for items that have bad halides
flour, spa water have bromide
toothpaste have fluoride
tap water, pool water have chloride

you need excess of iodide to displace undesirable halides.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/from-russia-with-love.18618/#post-203071](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/from-russia-with-love.18618/#post-203071)
\-------------------------------------------

Think of animal in the cage in zoo that you have to take care of, to keep it healthy.
More specifically, think of lizard.
They need vit D like people do.

Artificial day = 24hr
day-- 14hrs
night --10hrs
\-----
sleep time-- 8hrs
down time before bed time-- 2hrs
\------
day time you want 10hrs of very bright light and 4hrs less light
\------
Our bodies (and animal bodies)
have two hormones
melatonin--night hormone-- produced by pituitary
cortisol-- day hormone -- produced by adrenals (on top of kidneys)

when we see blue light of frequency 460-480nm
that light goes to pineal gland
and that stop production of melatonin

when melatonin level drops, cortisol raise
see chart in previous post
\--------------------

Conclusion,
if you see that blue light in wrong time, you cannot get healthy
Make sure that you do not use melatonin (or cortisol) as supplements.
Watch what your doctor prescribes, they love to give you script for both.

\-------------------
Blue light in the wrong time hurts us.
We can tolerate blue light in day time (from wakeup till about 2 better 4 hours before bed time.
TV, computer screen, cellular phone screens emit blue light.
Do not use them from 2-4 hrs before bed time till wakeup.
It is ok to use red light to see around.

\--------------------
For animal in cage in ZOO
or for you in a small room
get 2 reptile lights
and one red light fixed and one hand held red light.

Assume sleeping from 10PM - 6AM (8hrs)
wakeup 6AM look for 2-3 minutes at the sun or one of you reptile lights
for 10hrs use two reptile lamps
from 4PM till 6PM use only one reptile lamp
finish last meal 4hrs before bed time 6PM, turn off reptile lamp
6PM start red light
10PM go to bed, switch off red light
use hand held red light on the way to bathroom.

Sleep in completely dark room.
If you cannot sleep, just lay down (in that completely dark room.
Preferably without any alarm clocks, get off the bed at 6AM,
regardless if you sleept enough or still would like to sleep.

////

\========================================================
**A cross-sectional survey of urinary iodine status in Latvia.
[Medicina (Kaunas).](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25172607#) 2014;50(2):124-9. doi: 10.1016/j.medici.2014.06.003. Epub 2014 Jun 27.**
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25172607](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25172607)

**RESULTS:**
The median creatinine-standardized urinary iodine concentration (UIC) in our study was 107.3μg/g Cr. UIC measurements indicative of mild iodine deficiency were present in 31.6%, moderate deficiency in 11.9% and severe deficiency in 2.8% of the participants. The prevalence of iodine deficiency was the highest in the southeastern region of Latgale and the northeastern region of Vidzeme. The prevalence of **TSH values >5mIU/L** followed a similar pattern. The self-reported prevalence of regular iodized salt consumption was 10.2%. Children from urban schools had a significantly lower UIC than children from rural schools.

**CONCLUSIONS:**
Our findings suggest that although the overall median UIC in Latvian schoolchildren falls within the lower normal range, almost **50% of the schoolchildren are iodine deficient,** especially in urban schools and in the eastern part of Latvia. The absence of a mandatory salt iodization program puts a significant number of children and pregnant women at risk.

\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[

### Penny — 2016-10-20T23:20:47+0300

Hi Mikhail
As there probably is no sun in Russia right now - we actually have no idea where you live at this point, I would start with face dunks in cold water - like your countrymen in Siberia:
[http://siberiantimes.com/healthandl...the-snow-keeping-kids-healthy-siberian-style/](http://siberiantimes.com/healthandlifestyle/others/news/like-ducks-to-water-in-the-snow-keeping-kids-healthy-siberian-style/)

This gets your energy flowing - I would also be eating coconut oil if I were you - but really, your environment is killing you - the first thing to do is to fix your environment - starting with your laptop - try connecting a normal keyboard to the USB port and mouse to a USB port - don't have the lap top near your body - cell phones in airplane mode - change the wireless router to a wired one with an RJ45 ethernet cable - if you sleep near a wall, move the bed - in fact, experiment where on the bed gives you the most restful sleep - it is critical that you sleep - this is how you repair - for that, you need a pitch black room with no electronics in it - get a batter operated alarm clock -

If you're in an apartment, everyone else's wifi will be contributing to your demise, so if it's possible to move, do so - if you have access to a desktop computer that would also be great -

As far as crappy digestion, you can eat bone broths (stick a chicken in a pot, cover with water and simmer for 2-3 hours) with coconut oil and saurkraut - would be a great move - bugs can't digest fat, so they will die - I would also lose the simple carbohydrates which feed pathogenic bacteria - cabbage is also great -

There is a getting started guide on this site:
[https://www.jackkruse.com/getting-started-guide/](https://www.jackkruse.com/getting-started-guide/)
Also, for EMF mitigation, try this site:
[http://electricsense.com/](http://electricsense.com/)

Eat seafood, raw oysters, see the food guide right here:
[https://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/)
So, it would be good to know where you live and what your occupation is and how much time you spend on a cell phone etc. etc. etc

### Mikhail — 2016-11-27T14:06:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mikhails-journal.18625/post-204359>

Hello,

I haven't been posting all this time due to my condition becoming much worse, but I'm re-starting the journal now, because I must figure out what the cause is.

For the past three days I've almost had no sleep at all and it really bothers me. Within about 30 minutes of laying down my heartbeat starts to slow down, to the point where it becomes so weird and irregular with buzzing and twitching muscles and god help me recall what else, it wakes me up terrified.
I can't eat anything, food isn't digesting at all. I've lost a tremendous amount of lean muscle, and there isn't much more to lose, really. I feel extremely dehydrated and can't think clearly, also suffocating a lot, especially from emf exposure. It's just a nightmare. Please, help me change that.

I will start describing my current issues in more detail now, and sorry if I jump back and forth.

Digestion:

Food is not digesting properly and sits in the stomach for ages, giving acid and belching. I don't feel satiated after a meal at all, and tend to overeat. Some days it progresses to binge eating with suffering afterwards. I'm stressed and anxious most of the time.
In 24 months I've had at least 10 attempts to adapt to eating ketogenic diet, but failed all of them. Right now I "survive" on white rice and rice powder in a lot of water, some sweet potato, pumpkin, cauliflower, and small quantities of protein: eggs, meats, offal and some fish, seaweed tea, vegetable and mushroom stock, bone broth.

Solid protein isn't digesting, so I have to blend it to even have a chance at it. Most of the food it the solid state is not digesting for that matter, so unless it's blended I can't eat it. That's the issue that messes up everything. I need to be able to just chew meat and be fine.

Things like mushrooms even when blended wouldn't digest, or perhaps I can tolerate ridiculously small amounts (think 50g), and even that is not a guarantee. I can't eat 95% of vegetables. It's not a big loss, as I kind of hate vegetables, but what is a problem is that I can't digest mushrooms and seaweeds. That needs to be changed.

I have tried to supplement with digestive enzymes and found them to be pretty worthless. Betaine Hcl does help a lot, but whenever I take even 100-200mg it gives me so much acid later on I almost want to die. Same with any acidic thing or supplement: ascorbic acid, malic acid, acetic acid, alcar, nac, coffee. When I take it I get muscle pain in certain areas, and pretty much it messes up my digestion for hours or even days. I have to supplement with sodium bicarbonate to get through, which I hate to do.

I don't believe at this point there's even a single supplement that won't give me some kind of a problem. Here are some of the worst ones.

Vitamin D: gives me anxiety and irregular heartbeat, even when applied topically.
K2: almost the same symptoms, but on a good day I can tolerate some.
Ubiquinol: not digesting at all, gives me acid, and I kind of reject it afterwards, also messes up digestion (tried many brands)
Curcumin: I get unbelievably dehydrated, and also it doesn't completely pass through
Niacin: Anxiety, irregular heartbeat.
Almost all B vitamins except for, maybe, folic acid and B12: Anxiety, fluid retention, issues with digestion.
Iodine: problematic, also.

Bear with me.

[http://www.mayoclinic.org/symptoms/low-potassium/basics/definition/SYM-20050632](http://www.mayoclinic.org/symptoms/low-potassium/basics/definition/SYM-20050632)
Low potassium (hypokalemia)
Causes
Low potassium (hypokalemia) has many causes. The most common cause is excessive potassium loss in urine due to prescription water or fluid pills (diuretics). Vomiting or diarrhea or both can result in excessive potassium loss from the digestive tract. Only rarely is low potassium caused by not getting enough potassium in your diet.

**Causes of potassium loss** leading to low potassium include:

Chronic kidney disease
Diabetic ketoacidosis
Diarrhea (causing anal irritation)
Excessive alcohol use
Excessive laxative use
Excessive sweating
Folic acid deficiency
Prescription water or fluid pills (diuretics) use
Primary aldosteronism
Vomiting
Some antibiotic use
\---------------------------===================================
[https://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/co2/tab/sample/](https://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/co2/tab/sample/)
What is being tested?

Bicarbonate is an electrolyte, a negatively charged ion that is used by the body to help maintain the body's acid-base (pH) balance. It also works with the other electrolytes (sodium, potassium, and chloride) to maintain electrical neutrality at the cellular level. This test measures the total amount of carbon dioxide (CO2) in the blood, which occurs mostly in the form of bicarbonate (HCO3-). The CO2 is mainly a by-product of various metabolic processes.

Measuring bicarbonate as part of an electrolyte or metabolic panel may help diagnose an electrolyte imbalance or acidosis or alkalosis. Acidosis and alkalosis describe the abnormal conditions that result from an imbalance in the pH of the blood caused by an excess of acid or alkali (base). This imbalance is typically caused by some underlying condition or disease.

The lungs and kidneys are the major organs involved in regulating blood pH through the removal of excess bicarbonate.

The lungs flush acid out of the body by exhaling CO2. Raising and lowering the respiratory rate alters the amount of CO2 that is breathed out, and this can affect blood pH within minutes.
The kidneys eliminate acids in the urine and they regulate the concentration of bicarbonate (HCO3-, a base) in blood. Acid-base changes due to increases or decreases in HCO3- concentration occur more slowly than changes in CO2, taking hours or days.

Any disease or condition that affects the lungs, kidneys, metabolism, or breathing has the potential to cause acidosis or alkalosis.

The bicarbonate test gives a healthcare practitioner a rough estimate of a patient's acid-base balance. This is usually sufficient, but measurements of gases dissolved in the blood (blood gases) may be done if more information is needed. Bicarbonate is typically measured along with sodium, potassium, and possibly chloride in an electrolyte panel as it is the balance of these molecules that gives the healthcare practitioner the most information.

Aldosterone & Potassium
One source of potassium regulation is the hormone aldosterone.
Aldosterone signals the kidneys to excrete potassium into the urine.

Aldosterone is produced by adrenal glands, that are sitting on kidneys.
The outer layer, adrenal cortex, produces steroid hormones from cholesterol.
Signals from the blood cause the kidneys to make more or less aldosterone.
\------
Looks to me that you may have too much aldosterone.

-   [Crohn's disease](http://www.rightdiagnosis.com/c/crohns_disease/intro.htm)
-   [Paralytic ileus](http://www.rightdiagnosis.com/p/paralytic_ileus/intro.htm)
-   [Celiac disease](http://www.rightdiagnosis.com/c/celiac_disease/intro.htm)

**Symptoms Related to Afflictions of: Ileum**
These symptoms are related to afflictions of the organ: Ileum:

-   [Abdominal pain](http://www.rightdiagnosis.com/sym/abdominal_pain.htm)
-   [Nausea](http://www.rightdiagnosis.com/sym/nausea.htm)
-   [Vomiting](http://www.rightdiagnosis.com/sym/vomiting.htm)
-   [Bloody stools](http://www.rightdiagnosis.com/sym/bloody_stool.htm)
-   [Diarrhea](http://www.rightdiagnosis.com/sym/diarrhea.htm)
-   [Bloating](http://www.rightdiagnosis.com/sym/flatulence.htm)
-   [Fever](http://www.rightdiagnosis.com/sym/fever.htm)
-   [Malnutrition](http://www.rightdiagnosis.com/sym/malnutrition.htm)

### JanSz — 2016-12-02T16:43:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mikhails-journal.18625/post-204631>

May want to review actual experience of Fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT)
note:
it actually works,
it helps in relieving acute problems
receiver may acquire problems that donor may have (it is deed more intimate than marrying someone)
good idea to stay away from fat donors
looks like it is good idea to have detailed information about fecal donor
the donation point was in small intestine right behind ileum
reached with colonoscopy type of scope
as far as I can tell, it was just one time donation

I assume
that multiple donation
by mouth (using gel caps)
by rectum (via enema)
should be tried first
since it may be easier to accomplish
and may work just as well without big technology being involved

**\==================================================**
**Weight Gain After Fecal Microbiota Transplantation**
[http://ofid.oxfordjournals.org/content/2/1/ofv004.full](http://ofid.oxfordjournals.org/content/2/1/ofv004.full)
_Clostridium difficile_ infection (CDI) is characterized by a high recurrence rate after treatment.

Fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT) is a promising treatment for recurrent _Clostridium difficile_ infection. We report a case of a woman successfully treated with FMT who developed new-onset obesity after receiving stool from a healthy but overweight donor.

the FMT was performed 2 weeks later via colonoscopy. A total of 600 cc of the suspension of donor stool in sterile water was infused through the colonoscope starting in the terminal ileum. The colon and the terminal ileum appeared normal at the time of the procedure. She improved and did not suffer a further CDI recurrence after FMT.

it is now our policy to use nonobese donors for FMT.

studies have shown that FMT using a frozen inoculum from unrelated donors is effective in treating relapsing CDI \[[3](http://ofid.oxfordjournals.org/content/2/1/ofv004.full#ref-3)\]. In addition, most “professional” stool donors for FMT are selected on the basis of good health, including a normal BMI.

### Anton Chigur — 2016-10-25T11:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antons-journal.18655/post-203289>

Hey Guys,

I am starting my own journal here, I have read Jack's book and key articles on the blog. The key takeaway is that my lifestyle is pretty fucked up in terms of light, EMFs. I am an office clerk so I stare all day at a blue computer screen. Hopefully, I drink water from my own well. I am 30 and do not have any particular health problems except chronic Epstein Barr Virus which leads to high level of chronc inflamation (I am ill at least 5 years). I took horse doses of valcyclovir for 2 months and the virus stepped back, now 2 months later it is in full play again. This bothers me a bit as EBV might lead to various unpleasant things and I would like to solve this. I also have chronic rhinitis due to EBV which causes allergic reaction. Suprisingly having cut out all carbs and fruits, my rhinitis got much better very quickly already and I can breath through my nose, that is a small win))

So my near term goal is to get rid of EBV, my long term goal - I believe new lifestyle will allow me to make better choices, think clearer and I will leave my dull jobs and start my own project, I have a couple of ideas already.

I will do Epi paleo (plus lots of protein for immunity), CT and will sort out mess with light and will let you know how the things are going on. Not a lot of seafood in Moscow but salmon roe is quite accesible so I will focus on it)

I have done CT already, I did it in May and I am doing it now, unsuprisingly it is much easier to do CT when it is October and is snowing outside, so I can do 40 minutes in 10 degress C and I do not even tremble afterwards ang get warm, but in May it was much tougher.
Up to the point CT has given me flare up of the virus as I have a sore throat but the sleep is increadible.

Soon I will also install floating chamber in my house and this will be very beneficial I hope.

### DebraGM — 2016-10-27T21:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.18669/post-203373>

I just turned 50 years old. I have a grad degree in OT and I am a board certified health coach and personal trainer and holistic cancer educator. I am a food guru! Or was, after reading Dr Kruse's information. I have had Hashimoto's for 5 years ( I take 35 mugs T3 2x/day) and low vitamin D and low ferritin. I have been very sick with low energy, low body temp, weight gain, hair falling out, generalized anxiety marked by claustrophobia and difficulty driving on highways. In other words, my world got very small.
I got some relief having my mercury fillings removed and by have IgG food testing done. I removed gluten, dairy, almonds, eggs, beans, peas, and shrimp, crab and lobster from my diet per the results.
I also took 5 mg of hydrocortisone 2x/day to let my adrenals rest for a few months.
I lost 20lbs in 3 weeks and lost another 15lbs over the next 2-3 months. My energy came back, I went back to the gym and looked great for the last 2 years. I am strict with limiting toxins, I don't use the microwave, all my food and products are organic etc

Then my parents both got cancer. With the stress I totally fell apart. Hashimoto's symptoms came back and I gained 15lbs in 2 months. I've gotten 5lbs off. But I feel fatigued and the last 10 won't budge! And I wasn't thin to begin with but my waist size was 27" at 5'6" so I was ok with my weight. I don't have the energy to workout hard, but I have been walking everyday. I lost a lot of my muscle that I worked hard for at the gym.

I have been getting as much sun as possible, putting my feet in the grass, and eating lots of high quality seafood. I have spring water delivered in glass bottles. I just started following
the Leptin Rx. I realized I snack more than I thought, I get nervous leaving the house without a snack in my purse, and I must eat at night!! Trying to change all that!
I am wearing orange glasses at night and when using iPhone but I am terribly addicted to my iPhone. I did get a case to block RFs (but that only works when it's closed and talking on the phone). My head buzzes from iPhone like I am plugged into an electrical outlet. I actually feel it through my whole body. I live in a townhouse development where we have smart meters! Luckily there is an open field across from me. I'm not in the middle of the development at least.

I desperately want my energy back, I'd like to lose 15-20lbs and I'd like my hair to be thicker!! I'm tired of taking thyroid hormones. I'm tired of my neurotransmitters feeling off!

I would like to know how to read this info in the safest matter to minimize EMFs.
I did buy a Coronet meter.
Thank you for reading!!

Rejoined the tribe. Unfortunately I have only gotten worse since 2016. A lot worse. I guess I wasn’t sick enough to make a commitment to myself. I am now. Coronavirus woke me up.

I am also a Director for a holistic cancer organization. I want to be a good example and a source of real truth (a Rooster). I gained 60lbs, and I have anxiety, claustrophobia, sleep issues, etc etc.
I started watching sunrises about 10 days ago, along with Leptin RX, elimination of ALAN, and sunning at solar noon or Sperti D light. It’s cold in Pa now.
I’ve been nervous about a possible shellfish allergy. I had shrimp last night for the first time and I woke up with reflux and my lungs are bothering me today . I’ve been eating wild salmon and tuna and grass fed beef and pastured pork and eggs.

I’m wondering if I need to relocate. Listing my genetic snips:

MTHFR 677++
MTHFR 1298 —
VDR Taq ++
VDR BSM+-
CBS C699T +-
CBS A360A +-
COMT V158M ++
COMT H62H ++

### nonchalant — 2016-11-16T18:04:52+0200

@[DebraGM](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19441/) , for your iPhone addiction, I suggest you do what I did to get rid of my computer addiction. Do not touch it after a certain time at night. Then gradually make that time earlier and earlier, until you are not touching it after dark and before sunrise. It is difficult at first, but then the addiction just goes away. Make a paper list of things you want to discuss or investigate on it the next day, if necessary.

### Scompy — 2016-11-16T19:01:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.18669/post-203998>

I'm recommending now the 88T Cornet because it has electrical EMF detection as well. The 78s model is great too, but just doesn't do the electrical "dirty electricity". So if you have a TriField meter that already does electrical, that may be the way to go. It's around $130ish, and the 78s is about the same for around $260. That is versus the 88T that does everything. The TriField does RF poorly...more like broad strokes and you won't be able to pindown any Radio-frequency sources with it. For instance, you'll see the TriField ping up when you connect your cellphone to a celltower, but that's about all. Some cell towers in close proximity will get the TriField to start registering, so you can see my point about sensitivities. Instead, the Cornets are best for RF. The acoustameters are really, really good, but most people don't need that amount of detail with the RF for about $350 later.

How you use the Cornet matters...it can also output sound to find RF sources that you may not have considered. Don't pass off magnetic and electrical EMF sources either. It's easy to get stuck in a space that exceeds 3 milliGauss without even knowing it. For instance, pushing back your monitors even 1 inch may take you out of the field. Recently I bought an old-school vinyl record player and the magnetic fields were fine on it. However, the electrical EMFs ('dirty electricity') has about a 2 foot falloff from it. Knowing that, I have it tucked back in a place away from humans and unplug it when not in use so it doesn't keep emitting a field (and I also save mula from the power company).

# Thread: Jaguar's Journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaguars-journey.18718/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jason Siegel — 2016-11-11T04:32:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaguars-journey.18718/post-203812>

I am excited and honored to introduce myself here in the forum. My name is Jason and I am in Monmouth County, New Jersey. The most important thing in this world to me is individual freedom- the right of every person that is born into this world to their own body and all of its parts, and all of our natural and maybe infinite potential to physically and emotionally feel, to connect with our bodies, each other, and the Earth, to critically think, and to explore and create. Every day I still feel surprised and hurt when I see that humanity is largely under a spell of cognitive programming to a accept and be complicit in beliefs and behaviors that hurt us and limit those capacities we all have to feel, connect, think, and do.

I present myself as a rebel and leader against any religious/cultural/pseudo-medical efforts to suppress in any way our capacity to physically feel, to critically think, to connect to each other and to the Earth, and to create. I like to explore how even though it hurts and we experience so many scary and hurtful feelings because of the controllers, it is also so exciting and such an honor to be here standing for freedom and health and critical thinking against these forces of domestication and control, and the way it feels exciting and beautiful and fun to be in it together- the resistance.

I graduated from school with a degree in a biology in 2012, and was wondering about going into a PhD program but decided to work on learning, experimenting, exploring, and building resources in my own ways outside of the system for now. My goal is to unlock the ability of full tissue regeneration in the human body, so that someone who has lost part of their body is able to grow it back with 100% correct structure and function.

I suspect that there is an information field body- a sort of holographic blueprint or map of the exquisite three-dimensional architecture of the body. Projected from the central nervous system, this system of storage and processing of morphological information imbues every cell with a certain resting membrane potential (e.g. 43.7 millivolts), and this voltage directs its differentiation into into cell type (e.g. nerve cell), and it's awareness of its position in the body (eg. "position 24" in this Meisner's corpuscle nerve in this part of the fourth finger on the left hand).

All of the information and abilities are there, and now we just have to unlock the permission/instructions for the body to dedifferentiate and mobilize stem cells to fill in their positions properly in the map just as they did the first time, in embryonic development. I have read Dr. Robert O. Becker's book and this approach is inspired by the work of Dr. Michael Levin and his lab at Tufts University ([https://ase.tufts.edu/biology/labs/levin/](https://ase.tufts.edu/biology/labs/levin/)). I have been in contact with him through e-mail, and he is following the work of Dr. Gerald Pollack. Although he works with optogenetics (eg. expressing/activating a blue-light activated cation channel and a green-light activated proton pump to prevent and normalize tumors in tadpole embryos), Dr. Levin may not have considered yet the effects of the presence of extensive nnEMF and fluorescent light bulbs and the absence of sunlight and Earth magnetism in his laboratory.

My two main personal health challenges now are to heal from my chronic injury/pain along with returning to my athletics and to break though a kind of anxiety/sadness/fog which involves me being controlled by intrusive/irrational thoughts and stops me from doing things that I really want to do in situations where I actually have everything to gain and nothing to lose.

I eat an Epi-Paleo style diet, experiment with cold thermogenesis in the ocean, walk around barefoot every day in the wet grass and look toward the sun in the morning, and pay attention to WiFi and nnEMF exposure. I have not been doing well yet, however, with going to sleep early enough and waking up early enough to get into the sun between sunrise and 9AM.

I am fortunate enough to have the freedom to go anywhere and do anything that I want, and I have been struggling with anger towards myself and shame for all that I am not doing- being held back by anxiety/sadness/lack of focus largely related to a healthy body body part being taken from me against my will as a baby, "approach/assessment anxiety" relates to approaching any girl or group of people that I am curious about, and by chronic physical injury at the left sacroiliac joint, thoracolumbar junction, right knee, and left ankle.

Since I am free to be where I want now, I am going to start by getting a one-way plane ticket to a more safe and healthy environment. I think I will first go back to Costa Rica, where I lived for 3 months last year. I would like to learn more with all of you about determining the most ideal environments for me to be in. In Costa Rica, my home base has been in a place called the Diamante Valley, in the more sparsely populated and visited Southern Zone of the country, in a house within an organic permaculture community with multiple cold water springs for the drinking water. The altitude is about 2500 feet, and there are old growth rainforest ridges rising up over 1000 feet around. There are and many cool water river and forest realms to explore and two big waterfalls nearby. It is about 7 miles away and a 30 minute drive away from the beaches of the Pacific Ocean. It is also about a 30 minute drive from the nearest city, San Isidro de el General, which is a fun place to explore and hang out with my friends who live there and go to the University and to make new friends. Last year I began to get to know some good new characters from all around the world who came to the area to help create new communities with permaculture and new kinds of school and ways of doing things. I would like to learn and work together more with my friend who is the farm manager in this community. There is an special "feria" or organic farmers market for the valley every Tuesday, which is a good place to come together and learn about what's going on, play with the little kids, meet new friends, and try new foods. I also got to meet new Costa Rican friends and begin immersing in Spanish. I met three girls exploring on the river near the farm. I started hanging out and exploring a lot with one of them and soon she invited me to come stay at her house and also to come to culture class with her at the University where the girls are in the Diplomacy and English program. I got to meet more new friends and started to come to class every Wednesday evening, helping the students, helping the professor, and getting to know my new friends, staying over at friends' houses that night before going to the San Isidro feria market on Thursday mornings.

There is a lot of potential in that area, but I am also wondering about exploring somewhere new soon, and concerned about some things- There are "WiMax" cell towers in Costa Rica that enable internet access throughout rural areas and do work at my home base there in the valley. People like to get wi-fi modems that pick up this signal and then transmit it to their computers, or just get the internet access and cell service on their phones. In many of the Costa Rican homes there are satellites to pickup television signals. I really like being in the rural valley area and having the cool night temperatures and peaceful more isolated feelings there, but I wonder if it would be most optimal for me to down at sea level close to the beach, where I could easily jump in the ocean any time. The beach area near there around Dominical and Uvita has much more population density and nnEMF going on, but I would consider staying in the Osa Peninsula, which is further south and is mostly protected by Corcovado National Park. It is a bit of a challenge not having a vehicle there or in the valley as well but easier in the Valley with ride sharing and hitchhiking being more accessible. Maybe I should go to the Yucatan Peninsula for awhile? I am curious about the area of Dzilam de Bravo, which is at the coast right near the edge of the Chicxclub Crater cenotes ring and the zone of incredible abundance of cenotes to the east and south, away from major population centers and tourism. I am also wondering about other places including higher altitude areas pretty close to the coast in Panama, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, French Guiana, and Brazil.

The day that I began to realize that something is very deeply wrong with our situation here was the day that I began to learn about the shocking and unethical practice of child genital cutting- the amputation of healthy functioning parts of the genitals of innocent babies or children, obviously without their consent or any medical emergency that could call for proxy consent. In males this brings about a loss/inversion of the internal private realm that every boy and girl is designed to have and the tissues that are removed are extensive and possess an incredible neurological complexity, with approximately 20,000 Meisner's corpuscles just at the mucocutaneous junction alone (also found in the fingertips and the lips of the mouth) discerning fine touch in a detailed high-resolution manner, stretch perception, and much more. ([http://www.doctorsopposingcircumcision.org/for-professionals/sexual-impact/](http://www.doctorsopposingcircumcision.org/for-professionals/sexual-impact/)) This must be performed on infants so that men will "never know the difference" and "won't complain".

Fortunately, every day, more people are joining the effort to help activism and educating about this issue and also the practice of "foreskin restoration," which involves non-surgical techniques to place tension on the tissues near the amputation scar that induces mitosis and slowly regrows skin tissue and inner mucous membrane tissue (on the other side of the scar), until enough tissue is grown to cover the glans and close at the tip again, recreating the inside space and finally allowing all the remaining mucosal tissues to be not be exposed to clothing and air and to shed the layers of keratin that formed as permanent emergency callous over the exposed surfaces.

On that day I was devastated and in shock that I was forbidden from experiencing the structure and functions of the parts that were taken from me and that my remaining interior parts were not actually supposed to be keratinized, scarred, and rubbing against clothes all day. I wanted and still want so badly just to be able to feel what it felt like to be intact and feel like I would trade anything just to have my parts back. Working together with my friends to support each other and try to be positive, doing activism, and most powerfully doing "foreskin restoration" and "retaining" the skin forward and closed with a silicone cone and medical adhesive, have helped me to get through it, but this problem affects me deeply emotionally every day, wanting so badly to have the right to my own body and to feel each of those tens of thousands of missing nerve endings and those parts that are part of the proprioceptive map of my body and connect to parts of my brain that are all off-limits right now. I know that it will be one of the issues I can fully address after unlocking complex tissue regeneration.

It is really important to me to be able to talk to any girl or group of people in any time or place, even though there is all this initial fear of embarrassment and rejection, and so much going on right now that makes people people like its "awkward" or abnormal to reach out to others and go outside of your comfort zone. I have this strong dread/anxiety feeling that makes me want to avoid approaching girls and being "assessed" by them and it is very important to me to overcome this irrational feeling so it doesn't bother me all the time and so that I can approach anyone I want at any time- so that with my curiosity and playfulness and passion I can open up infinite corridors of possibility. I like to be curious, adventurous, and playful, and create situations where I can encourage that in others and help move towards really connecting and feeling alive.

The problem is irrational anxiety about approaching and being "assessed" by a girl that I am attracted to and curious about. I want to be happy to go anywhere with lots of people around and able to, without much hesitation or worry, approach any girl or group of people in any place, and, coming from a place of genuine expression and curiosity, talk to them and open up the door to connecting with that person, learning about them, having fun, and all kinds of exciting situations that would otherwise not be possible. If they don't feel like talking to me right now, or "reject" me in some way, or if I don't feel like talking to them anymore, then I can politely say goodbye and walk away satisfied and content knowing that I checked what was currently behind that door of possibility. I can rationally fully understand this but I am still controlled by irrational dread and anxiety that makes me want to avoid situations altogether, because I will be so angry and ashamed with myself if I skip out on approaching someone and then have to wonder what would have happened. I need to be rational and know that I have everything to gain and nothing to lose any time I introduce myself to a new girl, and I need to regain control of this hijacked fear instinct, feel fully feel safe and loved, and love myself no matter what anyone thinks, so that I can be free, and not enslaved to this very social control and fear of rejection that I so adamantly speak out against. I now understand that in my poor environment I have been in all this time, I must have fundamental imbalances in my circadian biology, hormones, and neurotransmitters. I need to address this on physical/environmental level, not only through forcing myself into tough situations (which makes me feel much better temporarily), figuring out how to truly love myself, and meditation and working on gaining captainship of my thoughts.

My chronic pain started with a small muscle spasm in my left lower back in 2006 following a comeback from a minor hamstring strain. Since then, I have had constant dull pain in the left lumbar and sacroiliac joint area with some kind of nerve entrapment affecting down to the left foot, and I have been all over the place with extensive exercise and chiropractic and other modalities in my attempts to get better. In 2014, I had a minor ankle sprain, In 2015, I had a minor knee strain, and in August of this year I took a hit from a wave that I caught too late right around the thoracolumbar junction which hyperextended me a bit there. All of these injuries have seemingly stayed about the same without healing, and I more recently began to realize that maybe something was wrong on a deeper full-body level, rather than just investigating the biomechanics of the sacroiliac joint and lumbar spine, and muscle activation/development. I understand now again that, my poor environment has got me in a state of low net negative charge, low redox potential, and ongoing parasympathetic "fight-or-fight" type state that does not provide me with the setting and raw materials I need in order to heal.

I am ready to experiment and learn do whatever is necessary to begin to heal, so that I can best help others, and work towards my missions and dreams. Thank you so much to all of you for your compassion and hard work getting to the truth and understand the way things work so we can move towards being truly safe and free.

Thank you Jenny for your warm welcome. I am excited to be here with all of you and begin learning and experimenting with myself, and to move towards a position of being able to help everyone else too.

Thank you Caroline, I am actually about to acquire Jack's book in a few days.

I am really eager to work on my dreams including unlocking human tissue regeneration, finding my full uninhibited voice for creative expression and interaction with others, and illuminating/eliminating the threats to individual freedom such as ubiquitous nnEMF, unethical use of drugs and surgery on children, and the harmful cultural cognitive programing about the body and Nature that make minds susceptible to accepting these choices in the first place. I will work to promote critical thinking and expose corruption and indoctrination, in the realms of science, medicine, law, education, and everywhere.

I recognize, however, that I have some serious work I need to do with myself in order to feel better, start functioning optimally, and access my potential.

I love my family and friends here but I have to go away from here now. I wish that I could go to work as a quantum biology apprentice with Dr. Kruse, a volunteer to help with experimentation and true innovation in health. I am free to go work as a volunteer anywhere now in health/biology, activism/conservation, permaculture, alternative education, or any appropriate field. I am ready to get my one-way plane ticket and just start with reseting and my Dr. Kruse protocols in a more optimal/safe environment, such as the Diamante Valley in Costa Rica, where I already have some friends and leads for projects, adventures, and missions.

Knowing that I am able to go anywhere in the world and do anything right now, does anyone have any leads or suggestions for me?

### JoeT — 2016-12-17T04:03:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joes-journal.18865/post-205371>

Joe's Jounral:

Goals:
\- My energy level is OK, but I am drinking a pot of coffee on most days.
\- Try to hack my cholesterol. I'd like to eat more saturated fat, but my cholesterol goes north of 300. Have family history of Heart Disease, and especially low HDL. Am about to embark on Ruben's CT/UVB protocol, as he mentioned this helped him in this area.
\- Avoid future illness - I live a completely indoor life.....never outside, in office all day. Computer use is high, but cell/wifi use is fairly low. (Don't own an iphone or ipad, just PCs).
\- I'm a fairly anxious person......not sure if this is something that can be helped on here.

nnEMF: Not sure if I'm missing something, but based on my testing with a Cornet Trimeter, with the exception of my train ride, my EMF environment at work and at home seems pretty good, considering I'm only 20 miles outside NYC. I'm almost always getting readings between .5 - 2 mg, and similarly good levels of RF??

Here are some labs results from March 2016....updated labs to follow shortly. Note that this blood works reflects a diet high in monounsuated fat, beans, and vegetables.

ldl-p 2011(High)
Glucose 82
ldl-c 163(High)
BUN 13
hdl-c 34 (Low)
Creatine 1.07
Trigs 87 (High)
Potasium 4.7
Total cholesterol 214
Calcium 8.8
AST 21
LP-IR Score 53 (High)
ALT 17
WBC 5.7
TSH 2.5
RBC 4.74
T4 6.1
Homoglobin 13.9
Vit D 37.6 (supplemented)
CRP 1.19
\----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
After about two weeks of just doing some real basics, I can report that I see a noticable increase in my energy levels. Sleep quality is up, coffee consumption is down. All I've done thus far is wear Uvex at night, changed my sleep location to a room with optimal EMF readings, heavily increased seafood (sardines), watched the sun rise for 5 mintues every morning, and got 15-45 minutes of UVA between 11-1.

I have many hacks in progress related to EMF reduction (train), Blue Blockers for the office, and getting better lighting for at home between 4-7..... so I will continue to update.

DUTCH
If you know someone in other states use their help, use their address.
It is only about address, sample you can ship from everywhere (I hope).
Results they will e-mail
I have not done this test yet, but plan on doing so (so it will help me if you figure good way out).
\-------------
\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Spectracell,
yes, in NJ have to be ordered by doc.
I do not remember name, I had requisition ready, I been to:
blood drawn here:
BARNABAS AMBULATORY CARE CENTER (Livingston)
Address: 200 South Orange Avenue Livingston, New Jersey 07039 (Map)
Phone: **973-322-7194**
Operating hours:
Monday-Thursday 6:00am-2:00pm No Friday and Saturday Blood Draws
Specimen Collection kits are available at this location; Blood Draw
Will Not be taken the day before and on any major holidays;
Walk-ins welcome. Please bring completed test requisition provided by your
medical or health professional with a copy of your insurance card and
upfront payment. Laboratory will ship specimens via FedEx. No prescription pad requests.
\=================================================================
The Lab is on a first floor, from (first floor) of parking lot, enter short walkway, lab is on the left, visible sign there.
While there, I asked phlebotomist about doctor who is writing scripts for those tests.
There is a doctor in the same building.
I should have noted his name, but did not. (I had my requisition ready).
You may call the lab, ask for his name and make appointment.
Otherwise go there, find name and see him or make appointment.

Let me know how that worked out.

\--------------------------
By incident I am using Redmond unrefined salt for years,
but occasionally I would use other usually sea salt and would not push on a salt.
Now I started licking salt of a teaspoon.
Whatever sticks to my tongue, then I relish the taste (taste is pleasant), I wait, do not chase it down with water (making sure that I really like that taste).
My blood pressure was normal but usually real close to **140/90
Now I see 111/64**
Wonder how long it will last that way.
..................
Dr Brownsten is saying that on Metabolic panel sodium should be at least 141
In Feb/2017 my **sodium=143(134-144)**
Wonder what it is now.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

@[SatoriHeart](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19303/)
@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)
guys, I need opinion on laboratory ranges for sodium (developed based on population that eats contaminated "refined" salt).
Full of ferrocyanide, aluminum and what not.

# Thread: Patty's journal of growing younger
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-journal-of-growing-younger.18888/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PattyW — 2016-12-22T05:25:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-journal-of-growing-younger.18888/post-205643>

Hello to all! I am excited to be on this journey where I feel younger and younger. What a surprise at the age of 56!

I was diagnosed with "adrenal fatigue" 15 years ago with symptoms way before that. My cortisol tested low several times. And from July 2013 to Nov 2015, I took hydrocortisone tablets. I was diagnosed with leaky gut. I consulted specialists and researched a ton, but I kept saying "There is something everyone is missing here". Bingo! Light, magnetism and water is it! Anyway I started getting things right starting November 2015 after a particularly bad episode of "adrenal fatigue" that left me unable to work. I was off the hydrocortisone by January and symptom free by May. I have been increasing my efficiency at living naturally. And it blows me away at how well this works. The better I feel, the more energy I have to do a better job at this. My vision has improved and I swear my hair is getting less gray. I have had a life of asthma and allergies (foods and airborne) and some of those still persist. I have had issues of low estrogen and severe vaginal atrophy. Estriol vaginal cream cured that and I still use it for fear that will return. My libido is average, I guess, but higher than a lot of women my age. It has been low in the past, though.

I live at ~39 latitude.

I'm 56 yrs old, female, fair skinned with red hair. I weigh 110 lbs and am 5 foot 2 inches tall. My skin does tan fairly easily. I do have some melasma and some non-pigmented spots (vitiligo?) and freckles. My ancestry is Celtic with a dash of Irish. My eyes are green. My mother died of Scleroderma. My maternal grandmother had diabetes and I think she died of a stroke. My father died of cancer.

I live in a house in a suburban-type neighborhood. My wifi was only on a few hours a day during the past year and off completely since November 2016. But the neighbors have wifi, of course. I started turning off electricity to bedroom 1 week ago. I limit the use of my cell phone and keep it as far away from me as possible.

I have used red bulbs after sunset for a year and mostly I just do stuff in the dark. I don't turn on any bulbs during the day when I'm home except for Solar Glo during winter and IR for healing. If I have visitors, they complain of being unable to see anything so I get my glasses on while they turn on a light. Geez! I even have red bulbs in my refrigerator. And when I drive at night, I wear the glasses. One thing that irritates me is all the lighting at night! It takes a lot of work to block out the freaking street lights and yard lights from lighting up the inside of my home. And when there is snow on the ground, these lights are magnified and obnoxious.

In the Spring, Summer, and Fall I put my water outside for several hours. In winter I put it under a UV bulb. I will be getting a r/o system next week. We have fluoridated water and I have been using the PUR filter which doesn't get rid of all of it. I'm sure there's more I do to improve my water.

My biggest issue is my job as a pharmacist which for most of my life required 4-5 days of 10 hr shifts with no breaks. Lunch was eaten while working. The nnEMFs are high and the blue light intense in pharmacies. In the past year, thankfully, I'm not working as much and I now work where I can usually get 30 min for lunch and a couple of breaks. I wear blue blocker glasses, keep my skin covered (I'm about to begin wearing a hat at one location), and I take my solar glo bulb with me at 2 of my locations. I take lunch and breaks outside and go barefoot. I can definitely feel the effects when I work, yuck! If I work several days in a row, it takes me a lot of sunshine and earth to recover.

I currently sleep quite well and I wake up refreshed. I go outside barefoot or if it's cold I wear soleless moccasins and I wait for sunrise. When I am not working, I spend as much time as possible outside. I lay on the ground if the weather permits.

I use cool water at the end of my shower. I used to run from the cold and now I go outside in winter purposely to get cold.

I eat fish most days and animal flesh most days. I eat seaweed daily. My diet has consisted of mostly organic, whole food for 15 years. I have been low carb for a lot of years, but now am having carbs in season. It makes sense! In the past year I have transitioned to not eating after sunset, eating the bulk of my calories in the AM and at noon, eating first thing upon arising and only eating 3 meals instead of 5. I have been obsessive about diet for 20 years and couldn't understand why I was not healthy. Now I know!

I was taking tons of supplements, but have decreased those. I had a Spectracell analysis done last year showing I needed vitamin E and choline so I still take those. I'm sure my status is not anywhere the same now. I take fish oil by Nordic Naturals which is one of the best, but I know eating fish is best. I'm plan to stop the capsules soon as I have increased my fish. I also take minerals, vitamin A, vitamin K2, a small amount of vitamin C and B complex. I take 10mg DHEA in the AM. I use progesterone and bi-est cream at hs (10mg/.625mg). I take a curcumin and boswellia combo and colostrum prn asthma and allergies.

I know I have a lot more work to do and I am sucking up information on this stuff daily. It's truly fascinating, but there is so much!! I read the Epi-Paleo book a year ago. I am reading the blog, listening to podcasts, watching webinars, and reading Facebook posts. Thank you Jack for all your hard work and for making this available to everyone.

### caroline — 2016-12-22T06:44:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-journal-of-growing-younger.18888/post-205648>

welcome Patty...... you are doing great!

with your training/career ..... you must be able to inhale a lot of this stuff! I would guess that your head doesn't hurt nearly as much as mine!

where do you live?

### PattyW — 2016-12-22T18:13:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-journal-of-growing-younger.18888/post-205669>

Most of it is over my head, but I just keep at it. I've always had a passion for learning about nature and how it is all connected. And I love being blown away with it, so this stuff just makes me crave more. I live in Northwestern Missouri. Oh, I forgot to say, my dog with congestive heart failure has had a dramatic improvement! He was supposed to be dead by now.
