# Thread: Patty's Story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-story.18893/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shane O — 2016-12-23T00:22:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-story.18893/post-205685>

I'm starting this journal for my mom, Patty. Hopefully one day she will join the forum and participate, but for now, she has more pressing matters, like the attached image. Plus, she isn't yet aware of Dr. Kruse and his work. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

My brother and I took her in last week for a CT based on some concerns about abnormal behavior over a few months and what we thought might be a stroke or other neurodegeneration. After the scan was completed, we were immediately sent to the ER and found out that she has a golf-ball sized glioma in her right frontal area. The on-call neurologist was empathetic and also to the point telling us that this was a very severe condition. He conferred with a neurosurgeon and an appointment was made (for this afternoon) to sit down with the surgeon and get a lay of the land. Too bad it's not with @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/). I'll post additional details afterwards.

For now, here is a bit of context on my mother and her mother.
Age: 71
Haplotype: ? (mine is J2b1a, and I don't think she has found out what hers is)
Lifestyle:

-   Born and raised in Portland, OR
-   2 children, ages 35, 38 (me)
-   Divorced when I was 6, never remarried or seriously dated
-   Occupation: Secondary school teacher for 33 years
-   Not particularly active, chronically overweight
-   Never smokes, rarely drinks.
-   Although I have convinced her to to make some dietary changes over the past few years, she mostly eats a SAD template.
-   Lives in a townhome, surrounded by other townhomes
-   Sleeps on the second floor
-   Spends most of her time in a chair downstairs, using her phone and laptop

Diseases:

-   Prediabetic
-   Depression
-   HPV
-   Glaucoma
-   Currently taking: simvastatin, levothyroxine, sertraline
-   After discovery of tumor, prescribed: an anti-seizure, steroid, and sleeping aid.

Labs:

-   She had a recent blood draw, believe it was for hormone levels, I don't have this yet, but will post when I do.

Misc:

-   Here are some thoughts she provided me recently on herself and her mother (Helen):
-   Hi Mom,

    I'm doing some research on my genetics and how they relate to my health. Mitochondria are passed down on the maternal side, and they play a large role in overall health and disease resistance, so I'd like to learn a bit more about Grandma Helen and you, hoping you can tell me a bit more about some of the following:

    -   Where was she born, and where did she live throughout her life (city, state)

    she was born in Portland (parents planned ahead for an expected twin birth) along with her brother Kenneth

    -   Activity level throughout her life...did she exercise like a daily walk or....?

    she walked a lot. I don't think she had a bike.

    -   Vices like smoking, pills, and alcohol, when did she start and what were her consumption levels?

    She smoked because her girlfriends did in college (but likely didn't inhale)

    She did not like beer.
    She got comfortable drinking sweeter things like martinis, wine, manhattans and Old Fashions
    Helen's mom was a teetotaler.

    -   What ailments or diseases did she have?

    I don't think she had any diseases and she was not a risk taker.

    -   What was her diet like?

    She ate what was put on the table (as a child)
    When she was out of the house as an adult, she indulged in high fat, sweet items and always talked about being healthy. **_HA !_**

    -   For you, I'd be most interested in these items before I was born and while you carried me in utero.

    I always craved sweet foods. I didn't crave salt, etc.
    I liked scalloped potatoes as well as baked potatoes.
    My recollection is that if I didn't eat a starch like rice, or potatoes......I'd be awakened in the early morning - simply starving
    I walked a mile to school each way: ages 5-14.
    Sometimes I had too much sugar and got a bit "flat".
    I do have a food diary that I used with one of the two of you. Maybe we can find it next time you're over.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Thank you all for reading and considering her story. I would love to dig into any resources that come to mind for you on the subject of brain tumors . I have read the EMF, Brain Gut, epi-onco-genesis series, as well as a few other random ones that have caught my eye. I also listen to many of Dr. Kruse's podcasts on repeat and generally take notes. I feel like I have a very basic idea of the three-legged stool and how it relates to cancers.

### Shane O — 2016-12-24T00:44:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-story.18893/post-205724>

So we met with the neurosurgeon yesterday afternoon. Here are some of the highlights (not the most appropriate word, I know) of the meeting:

-   He is recommending left frontal craniostomy to remove as much of the tumor as possible. Appointment is made for 1/4/17.
    -   Radiation/Chemo and other non-surgical procedures are not being recommended until after removing as much as possible...won't be effective without a tissue analysis
-   He didn't want to discuss much about mid to long range outcomes until his oncologist is able to analyze some of the tissue during the procedure
-   This tumor is **brand new** since February of this year when my mom had a CT for concerns about hearing loss.
-   The doc said that this is in a relatively easy area to access, with lower risk of bleeding or permanent loss of function than other regions of the brain.
-   She won't be able to live alone for some time after the procedure, and is not driving now or for the foreseeable future.
    -   Would love to get recommendations from you all on how to best hack the ICU/Floor/Rehab environments that she will be spending time in post-op
        -   Was thinking to make sure she wears her Uvex, get her outside in the AM, tourmaline bracelet, check on what type of garbage they might intend to feed her orally or via IV and see if there are better options...etc...
    -   Although a burden, my wife and I are considering moving her in with us after the procedure. This will be a positive thing in that I can more effectively control her environment and diet.

My mom seemed pretty numb afterwards and displayed very little emotion. She has had a very flat affect lately. I asked her if she wanted to get a second opinion but she doesn't unless I hear of something "she would want to know about". She has always had a "passenger" mentality when it comes to her health, has never really taken an active role in improving it. Getting her to buy-in on JK-Optimization hacks is going to be a battle I'm sure.

To be honest, surgical removal seems wise asap given that this thing has such a head start and is very aggressive. I'm certainly going to continue reading and am open to other ideas and your inputs.

Going to begin the process of ensuring her will is in order, my brother and I have POA & are co-signers on her checking account, her end-of-life care form (POLST) is filled out etc...

Thanks for reading!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Mary G - Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mary-g-optimal-journal.18915/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mary G — 2017-01-02T06:00:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mary-g-optimal-journal.18915/post-206028>

Hello,

I hope I’m doing this correctly

Basic Info: 43 yo female, married, with 2 children with very significant health challenges.

Location: currently in suburban Chicago, IL area

From: Immigrated from Eastern Europe when 16 yo; was 14 yo when Chernobyl nuclear explosion took place in close proximity (less than 15-20 miles) and was definitely strongly affected by it. My ancestors were originally from Middle East.

Family history: Grandmother and mother both hypothyroid, both of my children hypothyroid. Grandmother: heart failure; mother: sleep apnea, severe insomnia (cannot stay asleep), extremely poor energy levels, left knee meniscus tear requiring surgery 3 years ago. History of cancer for many family members.

MTHFR:heterozygous C677T and A1298C mutations

Immediate surrounding info: Work in the office setting, on a laptop, under fluorescent light. Live in 2-story home; extensive mold remediation 2 years ago in the basement.

**A brief history**

\- Born with twisted neck, straight via almost daily massage therapy treatments over a year

\- Sickly child, many colds and ear infections; adenoids removed when was 2 yo

\- Hair loss since 14 yo

\- Scoliosis

\- Goiter-like neck (same shape in all females in the family)

\- Myopia since 16 yo

\- No problem getting pregnant and carrying full term; however, both children born with small weight - 5.5 lb and 5.7 lb with health issues

\- Significant weight gain during pregnancy (40 lb) with inability to lose weight and inability to maintain any weight loss after 1st pregnancy. HCG was successful x2 cycles (2011) but again very rapid weight gain even on clean diet.

\- Difficult 1st baby with colics and other health issues who was unable to sleep at night. Completely disrupted my sleep/wake cycle. Felt like a zombie during the 1st year.

\- Completely exhausted, unable to lose weight, deterioration of executive function

\- Autoimmune thyroiditis 6 months after 2nd child was born – Thyroglobulin 57 H (norm <20). Within normal since then and TPO have been always within normal limit (but not zero)

\- Diagnosed with scalp lichen planus (autoimmune) and long telogen shedding phase of hair growth. I refused oral treatment and was given topical melatonin 0.75% topical solution in the evening and minoxidil 5% topical twice a day. This definitely helped with some hair re-growth. After reading and listening to Dr. Kruse’s take on the damage of melatonin supplementation, I stopped and hair loss resumed despite minoxidil.

\- Left knee pain on/off since 2007

\- Energy levels plummeted and very difficult to get thru the day, mental fog, further deterioration of executive function

\- Spring 2016: extremely severe back pain in L4/L5/S5 region (pain 10/10) with complete loss of daily function/pain/stiffness. Was able to get over the crisis with integrative manual therapy (IMT) practitioner.

\- Spring 2016: lab results (blood) many abnormals, including thyroid panel, liver function tests, cholesterol panel, female hormone panel, high leptin, higher A1C; nodules on thyroid ultrasound

\- Was told that have leaky gut, small intestinal bacterial overgrowth among other things

\- Have been free of gluten/dairy/soy/corn/nightshades, very few occasional grains, seafood-centric diet

**Current health concerns:**

_Metabolism, hypothyroid/HPA axis/female hormones disbalance_

\- Inability to lose weight – 5’2’’, 163 lb

\- Prior shape – pear, currently also weight gain around the middle

\- Low energy level and mental fog

\- Noticed sagging skin, lots of new wrinkles

\- Started on Cytomel 5 mcg in the morning (very low dose) to help with increasing T3

\- Irregular menstruation (delayed some months, breakthrough bleeding others)

\- Estrogen dominance, low progesterone – using Topical progesterone 20 mg cream on days 14-28 and EstroDIM Orthomolecular

_Inflammation, Intermittent low back, knee pain (on/off)_

\- Continuing integrative manual therapy, some supplements

_Digestive concern:_

\- Indigestion, have to be very careful with foo. Can’t tolerate red meat, any fried food, etc. Indigestion leads to back pain

\- Increased seafood intake – mackerel, salmon, scallops, shrimp, seaweed instead of meat

\- Taking digestive enzymes

**Current supplements:**

\- T-150 Xymogen – thyroid support

\- HPA Adapt Integrative Therapeutics – adrenal support

\- Adrenal Essence Xymogen – adrenal support

\- Vitamin D Supreme (vit D/vit K) Designs for Health

\- Vitamin C -buffered and liposomal

\- Glutathione Recycler K-57 Apex

\- CoQ10 Orthomolecular 200 mg per day

\- D-Hist Orthomolecular

\- Opti-Mag Neuro Xymogen

\- Tracheal Bovine Cartilage by Dr. Ron

\- Tumero Apex

\- Saloxin Xymogen

\- Cod liver oil by Rosita

\- Vitamin butter by Green Pastures

\- Chlorella Manna

\- Viracid Orthomolecular

\- Olive leaf Thorne

\- Lauricidin

\- Grape seed extract Designs for Health

\- Berberine Complex

\- Vital-Zymes Klaire Labs

\- Betaine-pepsin Orthomolecular

\- PancreaForce Energetix

\- BileMin Apex

\- pHysio Protease ProSol

\- drainage remedies and support of bladder, small intestine, kidney, liver, gall bladder, thyroid

**Where I am In Dr. Kruse's world...**

\- Joined as Gold Member in July 2016

\- read EpiPaleoRx book, absorbing info by listening to interviews and monthly webinars, EMF bootcamp, Facebook posts, etc.

\- Installed 14 stage biocompatible filter from RadiantLife.com

\- Using Uvex glasses at night, F-lux and Iris software for computer screens (can’t install on work computer), turn off WIFI at night

\- In the process of obtaining glasses with BlueTech lenses

\- Trying to increase exposure to morning light

**Recent Labs**: will add in the next post

**My Current Game plan**

\- Unfortunately, I’m currently in ‘analysis-paralysis’ mode in terms of which protocols to use in my situation

\- Educational consult with Dr. Kruse on January 3rd to prioritize which steps should be taken next (protocols, customization, etc)

\- Continue learning and implementing changes

### JanSz — 2017-01-03T19:12:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mary-g-optimal-journal.18915/post-206090>

Mary G
43 yo female
Grandmother and mother both hypothyroid, both of my children hypothyroid
\- Hair loss since 14 yo
\- Scoliosis
\- Goiter-like neck (same shape in all females in the family)
\- Myopia since 16 yo
Autoimmune thyroiditis 6 months after 2nd child was born
– Thyroglobulin 57 H (norm <20).
\- Diagnosed with scalp lichen planus
Lichen planus (LP) is an uncommon disease of the skin with a prevalence of 0.1% in men and 0.3% in women.
topical melatonin 0.75% topical solution in the evening and minoxidil 5% topical twice a day.
After reading and listening to Dr. Kruse’s take on the damage of melatonin supplementation, I stopped and hair loss resumed despite minoxidil.
\- Spring 2016 severe back pain in L4/L5/S5 Was able to get over the crisis with integrative manual therapy (IMT) practitioner.
What is Integrative Manual Therapy?
[http://www.imtassociation.org/content/what-integrative-manual-therapy](http://www.imtassociation.org/content/what-integrative-manual-therapy)
\- Spring 2016: lab results (blood) many abnormals, including thyroid panel, liver function tests, cholesterol panel, female hormone panel, high leptin, higher A1C; nodules on thyroid ultrasound
\--------------------
Metabolism, hypothyroid/HPA axis/female hormones disbalance
\- Inability to lose weight – 5’2’’, 163 lb
\---------------------
\- Started on Cytomel 5 mcg in the morning
using Topical progesterone 20 mg cream on days 14-28 and EstroDIM Orthomolecular
\---------------------
\- Indigestion, have to be very careful with foo. Can’t tolerate red meat, any fried food, etc. Indigestion leads to back pain
\- Increased seafood intake – mackerel, salmon, scallops, shrimp, seaweed instead of meat
\- Taking digestive enzymes
\---------------------
\- Trying to increase exposure to morning light
\- Educational consult with Dr. Kruse on January 3rd
\---------------------
I'm on is Cytomel 5 mcg every morning for low free T3. It was started within the last month - 12/5/16.
\---------------------
low blood pressure - my typical reading 110/70
\-----------------------
TSH was over 12
Liver enzymes were very elevated too.
LDL and triglycerides are elevated.
Hemoglobin A1c 5.6 (high normal), insulin 6.6, fasting glucose 79, fructosamine 200, and leptin 35.6H
Progesterone was very low 1.5 and I began using topical progesterone cream 20 mg on days 14-28.
Due to imbalance of Estrogen/progesterone, began using EstroDIM
\------------------------
I'm also working with Zyto practitioner ([https://www.zyto.com/](https://www.zyto.com/)).
\------------------------
I have tried both Lugol's and Nascent iodine. Both were pushing too much too fast, despite salt flushes protocol, other supplement, etc. In addition, hair loss increased.
\--------------------------------------------------------------------

### JanSz — 2017-01-03T22:16:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mary-g-optimal-journal.18915/post-206094>

Hi Mary G;
**I would like you to know that I am not a doctor.**
I do not have any formal medical education,
and I newer worked in medical field.
If you would follow any of the doodles that I write,
you are doing it totally on your own.
\--------------------------------------------------------------
FreeT3(2.3-4.2)
We want to have it in upper 1/4 of laboratory range
slightly over is ok too
2.3+(4.2-2.3)\*0.75=3.725
so FreeT3 should be ok if it is within (3.7-5)
Preferably it should be supplemented after one is already on established
protocol with Lugol's and selenium.

When eating NTH, it is good idea to do it right at wakeup.
On a day that you do blood draw for FreeT3
draw blood early in the morning (7;30AM) BEFORE taking NTH.
Also around 6;00AM drink 16oz water.

Assuming that your pituitary is responding correctly,
another criteria you may want to use,
when supplementing with thyroid hormones,
attempt to get enough of it to get:
TSH~0.5
but not much more.
You should not feel overly hot all the time.
Body temperature, underarm should be 36.6C

For me one pill a day of selenium from LEF.org works fine
selenium and other items must be checked using

Spectracell Micronutrient analysis (twice/year)

after 3-4 weeks on selenium one can assume good selenium level
and start slowly, drop by drop on Lugols, must be Lugols nothing else.
Read the protocol in my earlier post, specially if you find this process difficult (at first).
Using 2% Lugol's solution one want to be on protocol of
50-300mg/day
50mg=20drops or one cc or one mL
one squeese of the rubber topper on the dropper is 0.8cc=40mg=16 drops
usually 50mg/day is adequate, but I think that you should go at least 2x of that

Using at least 50mg/day of Lugol's for many years continuously is major requirement,
think of it as part of detoxification project.

Best is to supplement with
NTH, natural Thyroid Hormone from pigs
(most Jews are allowed to do that, not sure about Muslims)
There is variety of NTH, any is ok, but make sure to chew it,
and when you start with new brand or change source, follow up with testing.
There is also Cytomel and similar T3 only (synthetic) products.
Less desirable.
Usually they work too, specially if one is (completely) not able to convert T4 into T3.
Selenium and Lugols are helpful in that conversion.

1Grain=(38-T4)+(9-T3)
T3=4(T4)
1 Grain=74(T4) or 18.5(T3)
3Grains=(222mcg-T4) or (55.5mcg-T3)

Dose of 3grains/day is relatively common.
so you may have perspective that your 5mcg of Cytomel can't go to far.
\========================================================================================
You have low DHEAs=124.3
LEF.org sels DHEA pills, but I buy them from amazon.com
try first 100mg/day
or if you are already taking some, increase by that much.
Only those pills, no creams.
goal
Women 275-400 μg/dL
when you are going to be near the upper range,
watch E1, E2 and testosterone (specially FreeT)
also we can talk here about
progesterone and cortisol.
and pregnenolone steal
From your tests I am getting impression that
progesterone is about just right
but cortisol is low. (and aldosterone is likely low--->low sodium)
We need to make double sure where you stand here.
because;
if you are low on cortisol, you will not be able
to process thyroid hormones.
Thyroid hormone must be inside the cells to work.
That may be one of the reasons why you energy is low and you are tired
and
you are loosing hairs
Eventually, you will have to do

DUTCH

test I mentioned previously.
Get ready to do this test few times.

If indeed we face situation that you have
progesterone high enough that additional increases are not justified
but
your cortisol, (specially the one at wakeup and noon) are low
then
you will have to supplement (HC or Medrol)
lets leave this open for now...................
except
make sure that you mind your light and darkness.
That is a major separate project.

Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-176178](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-176178)

\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Your vit D looks decent, specially considering that you are in Chicago.
Likely you are using supplemental vit D.
Eventually try to wean out of supplementation
and
you need much higher levels of vit D
this
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
or 1.5x of that
Use natural sun to the max,
and Artificial lighting as above.

\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Your sodium is low.
likely you have low aldosterone. ---->>lowish blood pressure

Aldosterone is a brother of cortisol

First, try to eat more salt.
Hope,
that when we get cortisol in better shape aldosterone will follow.
If it is tough to live like that,
use Florinef,
that will require also lots of salty water.
Start with really small dose of Florinef.
Treat that as temporary project, that you will eventually wean of off it.
\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Best for last.

Your Fatty Acids Profile is a sick joke.

next time do better test.
do this test at LabCorp or MayoClinic.
Lets hope that you will not have to do it at Kriegers.
this test, do about every three months, until you stabilize at good levels, then less often.

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

I hope that your bad fatty acids are due to your diet that is high in
fish/krill oil
seafood
if not, you may have actual problem that must be figured out.
so for now, do not eat nothing of the two above.
After 3 months or so do
Fatty Acids Profile
again.

Do not feel bad, you are not the first with super high EPA and DHA.
To find this out is impossible without testing.
Some tests are made (on purpose) in such a way that they will tell you only the truth,
but it is incomplete truth.
Well, they have to make a living somehow, right?
\----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your insulin is high (6.7)
until it is about 2-3
it will not allow your body fat to be used for energy
insulin can be raised by anything,
few drops of sweet juice in your plain water or Stevia can do it.
And then you stuck, fat and tired.
Make sure to not loose your morale at point like that, because this can happen to the best.

Make sure that you work your self up into about month long fast, or as long as it takes.
After the big one,
think of IF (Interminnet Fasting) and one/day meals

keep in mind what is ahead of you:

1.--3-4 days of hunger
2.--few days of bad body smell (burning old infections and other undesirable elements)
3.--it should be much easier from now on
4.--monitor your
blood pressure
temperature
weight
go little lower than your long term goal

Good luck.

Likely your consultation with
@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
is over now.

...............

\\

### Debbieinbend — 2017-01-03T01:13:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/debbies-journal.18916/post-206070>

It's official I am starting my optimal journal. I joined Dr Kruse site as a member Oct 2016.
I started a ketogenic diet in Feb 2016 it took 6 weeks to adapt. I am 65 yrs so that might explain why it took so long, I am grateful that I was able to adapt. Feel the best I ever have.
My sub-optimal story is that I am hypo-thyroid (diagnosed at 30), grew up in Seattle, with much SAD. Moved from Seattle 25 yrs ago and currently live near Bend Oregon.
Have been diagnosed with osteoporosis, and high cholesterol. Took Statins and Boniva for two years. Off Statins now for only a few months.
Family history is not so great. Mother died of breast cancer at 48, Father died of a heart attack at 38 (had rheumatic fever as a child and was injured in the war) my half sister died of colon cancer at 63. One Brother has had irregular heart beat and the other has had high blood pressure most of his adult life.
My Maternal Grandmother lived until 84, Maternal Grandfather died young in accident. My Paternal Grandmother lived until 90, Paternal Grandfather died young but I don't know why.

I have just gotten my 20 gauss sleep pad, ordered the Gauntlet, eat fish two times a week, and am following the supplementation recommeded for the osteoporosis Rx. I live and work around Wifi, cell phones, and computers. Have all off at sleep time. Wear blue-blockers regularly, and seek sun everyday. Live in a small city, no smart meter, get out in nature often.

My test results that I currently have are as follows: Oct/2016

Hemoglobin AIC 108
HGB AIC Beckman 5.4
Apolipoprotein B 149
BUN/Creatinine ration 19/1
CPK 160
HS-CRP 2.30
Homocysteine 11
Vit D 34
Glucose 100
CO2 24
TSH 3.32 Thyroid Antibodies TPO 8 iu/ml,
TgAb <1
Cholesterol 279 (off Statins),HDL 79, LDL 182 , Tri 86
Bone Density 3/2016,AP Spine Tscore -4.2,Neck -2.6, Hip -3.0
\*\*Heavy metals testing results just received. High in Alum, Lead, and Mercury, and cadmium. Am going to start oral chelation ordered by local integrative medicine Doc.

### JanSz — 2017-03-09T16:14:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-and-son-optimal-journal.18977/post-209953>

You posted
insulin 68 pmol/L and translated to
11.33μIU/ml

[http://endmemo.com/medical/unitconvert/Insulin.php](http://endmemo.com/medical/unitconvert/Insulin.php)
translated
68 pmol/L= 9.721µIU/mL

my lab, on my reports gives insulin in uU/mL sometime and uIU/mL other times,
but at the web site it shows uIU/mL
so I assume that
uIU/mL is the same as µIU/mL

so, if your insulin= 9.721µIU/mL
it is too high
aim at 2-3

to get there you should have
over 24hr day period
long times where you insulin have a chance to fall

Intermediate fasting 24-->16/8
is one attempt at that goal
one would eat only within 8hr window, and not eat during the orther 16 hrs

Over time the 8hrs may shrink to less with a goal of eating just once a day.

In the mean time, you may want on beginning of the 8hr period eating only fats.
Fats do not raise insulin (or glucose) significantly.

\----------------------
You posted
Hemoglobin=9.4mmol/L
and translated it to 605.90g/L
that cannot be right.

I have found:
Hemoglobin leveles (which are roughly 12 to 17 g/dl)
1 g/dl = 1000 mg/dl = 1000/18 mmol/l = 55.5555555 mmol/l that does not give me much help.

Work on it until you get hemoglobin to within (12-17).

\=======================================

### tola — 2017-01-24T23:12:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-207299>

59 y/o male with no complaints other than bowel movements x5 in a 7 day period. No surgical history, medical history has me on doxycycline for 10 days about 20 yrs ago. Also, idiopathic a-fib x2 in 2014. electro-converted back to sinus. lights out on etomidate was actually kinda cool, like a lens that closes in on itself and then you wake up. That's when I started magnesium 400mg daily. palpatations gone. Now down to 100mg day maintenance dose. expecting RBC mg labs. vision still pretty good, no glasses needed. sleep is good, though I do wake around 6am to pee.

Ok. Haven't got my labs back yet, but I just looked at Scompy's journal and read a few pages of EMF stuff. I may have semi-biohacked my smartmeter by putting an aluminum roaster pan around the smart meter. It's like a parabolic dish so that at least it's broadcasting outward. Better than nothing and cost nothing. Also doesn't piss off the electric company tech.
In anticipation of having a low DHEA level, I'm not sure what I'll do about it. I don't have a problem taking 10mg/ day but I can't find any long term studies on it, especially higher doses....
Organic Acids Testing sounds interesting, too. Just starting to research it.

### tola — 2017-01-27T21:07:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-207450>

Here I am on the continuum...so what is my optimal? Being high normal on lab results? Just feeling good and damn the doctors? Slamming Sudoku? My optimal will be integration of body-mind-spirit. Seeing the pieces come together is the fun part. Reading about the dc current along the spine and the collagen matrix and then thinking about the governor and conception channels and how they flow...grounding is another...the ancients had you use your mind to supercharge your electron vacuum when grounding. not only is it pleasurable but you get a wink from nature while you're drilling down.

anyway, got my Quest labs in-
dhea-s 259 mcg/dl (38-313 wnl) I supplement about 6mos out of the year 10mg day
RBC Mg 5.2 mg/dl (4-6.4 wnl) Daily supplementation 100-400mg depending. Makes me wonder if I have absorption issues. The rest of the labs may clarify?

Question for y'all- what is the guideline here for using medical shorthand? seems like a lot of you are up on it and i see it sprinkled it here and there. it does save a lot of time....

### tola — 2017-01-31T20:28:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-207672>

More labs…please comment, but keep it clean, there could be ancestral health lurkers about.

**The Good-**

HS-crp 0.51 mg/L

HDL- 59 mg/dl

Triglycerides 58mg/dl (total cholesterol 256, total/HDL ratio 4.3. meh...)

**The Bad-**

Vit D, 25 hydroxy 35 ng/dl. As an experiment, I stopped supplementing D last summer to see if 2+ hrs of sun daily would ride me through the winter. Historical levels were high 50’s with 3-5000iu daily. Sun exposure for the last 2 mos. has been about 30 min to 1 hr. shirtless 5x week.

Fasting BG- 101 mg/dl

A1c- 5.7%

Same fasting BG as when tested 5yrs. Ago...still on the pre-diabetes fence. Eating winter diet of hi-fat, low-ish carb, try to avoid sugar, but avg. 1 oz. dark chocolate daily. I may look into Berberine for supplementation. Krusian tx is getting more sunshine? I can do that.

**The Ugly-**

Serum Ferritin 412ng/ml incoming!!!

I gave blood yesterday. I’ll give blood x3 and retest next year. If still high, I’ll test for hemochromatosis? (serum iron 136 ug/dl, normal range 38-169)..or not, since the tx is the same either way. 500ml blood contains approx. 225mg iron. So it would take 8x donations to get to minimum value. At least the blood bank gives out movie tickets for frequent flyers.

**The Unknown-**(enlighten me!)

Homocysteine 9.9 umol/L
<8 would be better? Is this a folate/MTHFR thing?

BUN/creatinine ratio- 19 (14/0.72) cellular dehydration from nnEMF? Do I need a beer transfusion?

DHEA-s 259 mcg/dl (38-313 normal) sleep is good, gut motility could be better, but clears up when I eat flaxseed with every meal. I’m wary of taking a large dose of hormones until I find long term studies. I’m fine with continuing 10mg 5-7x wk for the time being... started a few months ago.

B12 454 pg/ml (211-946 range) I eat a lot of meat. Thought it would be higher. Absorption?

TSH- 1.65uIU/ml

Free T4- 1.24 ng/dl

Free T3 2.9 pg/ml

I do supplement with Lugol’s, 3mg twice a week, with 100mcg selenium daily. Use non-iodized pink/grey salt. Try and eat lots of salmon. Should I pulse my thyroid with red light for a kickstart? I don’t feel sluggish but I’m not gonna play Aussie rules football anytime soon, either.

**The others**\-

CBC wnl

WBC with diff is wnl, midrange-ish, no signs of infection

liver enzymes wnl

UA clean, ph 7, s.g. 1.008

RBC magnesium- 5.2 mg/dl. I really thought I’d be high-normal with daily mg intake, but I’m

lo-midrange. Absorption factors? Poor supplements? Mg test hacked by Putin?

**Supplement**s- daily unless otherwise noted

100mg ubiquinone

1gm C time-release

100mg alpha-lipoic acid

100mcg selenium

100-300mg magnesium, malate or citrate

90mcg K2 mk7

3mg Lugols iodine 2x week

5000iu vit d, 3x week (restarting)

5ml cod liver oil 5x week

10mg DHEA 5x week

digestive enzymes if I pig out (who, me?)

¼ tsp apple cider vinegar if I pig out on bbq ribs

85% dark chocolate for medicinal purposes only

**Lifestyle-**

Moved out of Oakland. Now I’m in (s)wine country. Short walk to the micro-brewery.

I don’t miss the hood, the crime, bad light, fluoridated water and nnEMF fog

Eat high-ish fat, low-ish carb 100g day +-50g

blue blockers with red light, blacklite at nite, skin covered

no wifi at home nearest neighbor 75 yds. away

lots of gardening, no job, no worries (maybe worried about the new administration!)

RO water, exposed to sunlight when not too lazy. Should I rest the stainless steel water dispenser on magnets?

I'm like you trying to figure all this out and get better, too.

Your high iron level is the reason your Mag RBC is low... and will not get optimal while your iron is so high. Plus the blood sugar and hypothyroidism.

I've watched this YouTube before, but took notes yesterday so I could focus on the details. So many dots connect...

Somehow when I moved 10/2012, I got off track with JK and later started following a Facebook group.... I've learned little bits through the years, but after getting side-tracked late 2015 due to B6 toxicity, I finally started paying attention to the iron toxicity posts... no wonder I couldn't improve my RBCMag! Well, I realize I left out a lot of details, but hoping you have the time & attention for this 1 hour, 37 min YouTube on Rethinking Iron.

By the way, my husband has 85% chocolate, like you, every day. I used to have 99%, but stopped some time ago and sometimes have the 85%, too.

I didn't realize ACV is good to remove iron, so starting it up again in RO water with minced ginger and a bit of stevia. Yum!

Oh! Forgot to mention--and it depends who you believe, but your storage D is fine. Please do not use synthetic D3. I did for awhile and for some reason, my active D (aka 1,25-D or calcitriol) was elevated and over lab range for two years. Not good. Hormone D puts calcium into soft tissue and all kinds of havoc. Anything over 21 has no clinical benefit---despite the rampant direction. Reminds me when 'they' did this with fat.... and calcium. (Actually, we need tons of mag, but looks like you know that.)

[https://www.youtube.com/shared?ci=I2NrNNVPCYI](https://www.youtube.com/shared?ci=I2NrNNVPCYI)

### LinD — 2017-05-20T04:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-214318>

> [tola said:](/goto/post?id=209571)
>
> This bloodbank has the ability to do double RBC removal, recycling your plasma, which I think I'll try. I've just read that at 20% hematocrit, a skin flap will actually perfuse/oxygenate better than at normal range, so I guess I won't worry about losing all those iron-laden RBC's.

Oooh, Tola. I just saw this. Double-Red & Whole Blood donation notes below:
\~~~
Whole blood donations contain red blood cells, platelets, plasma and white blood cells.

**Whole blood donations can be performed every 56 days.**

\- BUT -

**_Double red cell donation can only be done once every 112 days (16 weeks) or more._** Double red cell donation is ideal for donors who want to minimize their trips to the blood center and who are blood type O.

**How Is a Double Red Cell Donation Different?**

During your double red cell donation, blood is drawn from one arm and drawn through a sterile, single-use needle set to a machine. The machine separates and collects two units of red cells and then safely returns the remaining blood components, along with some saline, back to you through the same arm.

Comment about double red donation:
'...double red blood donation is hard on the body. You lose A LOT of nutrients at once.'
\~~~
I wouldn't do double red, but whole blood only and go more frequently, but that's just me.

### tola — 2017-08-06T04:44:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-221017>

my latest iron panel came back.
TIBC- 297 ug/dl
UIBC- 201 ug/dl
Serum iron 96 ug/dl. last lab 6 mos. ago was 136
Iron saturation- 32%
serum Ferritin- 67, last lab 412

I calculated it would take 8 blood donations to get my ferritin down to 30ng, but I'm calling it a day at 5 donations and 67ng. I'll still give blood once or twice a year depending on labs.
I'm assuming the apolactoferrin 285mg consumed daily for the first 3 weeks and then, only consumed during my weekly red meat fest made some of the difference, though in hindsight I could have done without it. Apparently the lactoferrin keeps one from absorbing the normal 1-2 mg of iron that gets past our sm. intestine after and iron-rich meal.
Interestingly, I read that in the intestinal epithelium lies d-cytochrome-b reductase which takes oxidized and insoluble ferric iron and reduces it to soluble ferrous iron. The b-cytochrome is a 1 electron transfer agent with a peak absorption spectra of around 520-560nm. The color green hangs out at 495-570nm. In the taoist system, green is the color of the liver, and there's lots of iron in the liver....hmmmmn.

> Organic Acids Testing sounds interesting, too. Just starting to research it.

_Welcome and I hope you get a sun light protocol going ASAP._

### tola — 2017-08-10T21:20:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-221376>

here's a pair of my birkenstocks repurposed for grounded gardening. Can't always go barefoot once the burrs dry out. Got these aluminum screen door bolts, a.k.a. sex bolts, at my local ACE hardware. 3/8" depth. Cost under $2 for the bolts. You don't need a drill to install these in footwear. An awl will do the job, maybe even a 16 penny nail....can't seem to get my 2mb jpeg to upload, but you get the picture (cymbal crash.)

### tola — 2017-09-11T21:07:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-223389>

I haven’t had a run of A-fib for 3 years, but last month, it bit me. My guess is that I’d been eating a lot of celtic grey salt in the previous weeks, trying to see if I could hold onto water better and if that would combat my orthostatic hypotension/dizziness when standing. The dizziness seems worse in hot weather, so I got the idea I was getting dehydrated too easily, maybe exacerbated by drinking RO water. Funny that I never get dizzy when getting up to go to the bathroom at 6am...

Also, I’ve gotten sloppy with electrolyte monitoring. I wasn’t compensating for all that extra sodium with potassium, and I’d been slacking off on magnesium, too, which really extinguished palpitations.

I took 225mg of flecainide to convert to NSR in a couple of hours, after trying everything but carotid massage to convert. First time I’ve used the “pill in a pocket” approach. Beats cardioversion with 200 joules any day! When I looked up the mechanism of action on this drug, it came up as a sodium channel blocker. I stopped the ad lib salt munching and started back on 300mg magnesium, 500mg taurine, and 1-400 mg potassium daily depending on my salt intake.

I recently saw an interview on Mercola.com with someone quoting a german Dr. Hamer (sp?) saying that “territorial loss” was at the core of heart attacks. Bold statement, gotta hand it to Mercola for putting that on his site. I found it interesting that he said because of a heart rythym controller in the brain next to the coronary artery relay, you may be able to convert to NSR by putting an ice pack above your right ear. Worth a try. On a similar limb to walk out on, I’m wondering if my previous high ferritin of 400 meant that iron was depositing in my heart, thereby increasing my sensitivity to EMF.

### tola — 2017-09-15T20:35:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-223745>

Hail Einkorn! Ever since I went paleo, etc. a few years ago, my GI has slowed considerably. I tried lots of fruit, psyllium, flax, fibre, etc. to minimal effect. After reading "Eat Wheat," I decided to give einkorn flour a try. It's molecularly very simple, and tastes the business, but I also have increased energy, and the GI issues have disappeared, I mean, textbook poops!
Do I have some gliadin guilt over this? Yea, maybe. I'm going to do more research on this, but right now the whole grain honeymoon is in full swing.

# Thread: TBI Healing, and Family Health Optimising- Me and my children
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tbi-healing-and-family-health-optimising-me-and-my-children.18985/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Imogen Skye — 2017-01-25T07:19:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tbi-healing-and-family-health-optimising-me-and-my-children.18985/post-207312>

Age: 39
Height 5'6", medium frame
Weight: around 135lbs; it has been the same since I was 13, except during pregnancies and nursing
Organic, whole foods since 1998
Strict paleo, ancestral since Oct 2010

Dec 26, 2016: Started CT adaptation with outdoor exposure for 20-30 minutes at -20C to -30C, in bikini-esque coverage (+boots, hat, gloves) and face dunking in same temp, both 3x/day
Jan 8, 2017, started every day ice bath, 30 minutes, 1C/34F, hat, pink, no issues- took 6 minutes to ease in. Reduced indoor temp. to 16C
Jan 15, 2017, ice bath, added 5 minutes, hat, added bikini top with wool inserts because weight is dropping fast and I nursed for 8 yrs already- I'd like to keep a _little_ something , every day still, I slip in within a few seconds and use a thermometer because I can't believe it is cold since my body is getting used to it and it feels only cool. My children now run their own baths because when I do it, even though it feels very warm to me, they say it is lukewarm or cold. I still put my hands under full hot in the kitchen, but I rely on the fact that the water is only hot to know that it's the right temp for dishes. My body's temp. regulation was terrible-to-non-existent before CT and now it's very good, but my temperature-recognition is apparently terrible!

I've had to substitute outdoor exposure for two days of ice bathing- can't wait to get back in tomorrow!

Starting CT, I've had only a few symptoms- just better sleep, and strange one-day of knee soreness that I've never had in my life, but it left suddenly in the afternoon, early onset of period which is unusual, skin clearing up, sudden mass-memory return- longterm and from throughout my previous life, better focus ability, faster and clearer thinking- that came after the first week of outdoor exposure and convinced me to continue, though I was already resolute. I was hoping to reset my hypothalamus and affect my circadian rhythm; I knew I was having mitochondrial difficulties from my TBI from research I'd already done. Jack Kruse came up when I'd googled "hypothalamus reset" for the umpteenth time, so I skimmed, and started that day, and only after the unexpected cognitive improvements did I start reading more here and the reasons why this is working for me. Ultimately, I know all of what I am doing is temporary maintenance until I can get my mito into full sun as a way of life. And I am so grateful for this protocol and all the work Dr Kruse has done and shared here. I have had no beneficial or effective treatment for my traumatic brain injury from conventional doctors whatsoever. I was using near infrared sauna for pain and healing for the past two years, and that with my diet weren't enough, and I have not stopped seeking since this happened to me because I mother 5 children and my health went from fit and strong to frail and weak overnight from that accident. I am a very determined woman, and my manner is quiet and unassuming, so I am often mistaken especially by power-hungry people (especially drs) for passive, but have never been so, and any look at my life would show this. In other words, I have a LOT of skin in this game. Right now, I have all of it in.

History: TBI from vehicular collision March 2015, and a slough of symptoms from it, ranging from seizures to loss of tone in whole right side of my body, massive memory loss, loss of self-identification, hormonal upheaval that caused me to start missing periods- never happened before, including losing 75% of my hair on my head, hearing loss, vision loss, balance issues and vertigo, etc.... Basically a major selection of the whole gamut of traumatic brain injury symptoms. Additionally, initially, my speech and walking were severely impaired, I didn't have any of my previous basic and advanced skills and have had to relearn the basic ones since, very similarly to stroke sufferers. I don't yet have many advanced skills- mostly put my effort into skillfully cooking and keeping a routine with my 5 children who are and have always been home-educated. I was an artist before also, and a homesteader, but I lost my art abilities from brain injury (technical and scientific illustrator and glass blower), and had to move into town, for now.

Pre-TBI, I had 5 "full-term" pregnancies. The first two were csections due to frank breach and these babies were obviously very premature, but drs lied to me about it at the time. Then my next 3 babies were born at home (freebirths) and I saw what a full-term baby is like.
My 1st pregnancy was dated by ultrasound and obviously wrong; I estimate it at about 34 weeks: 7lb4oz boy. Gained 65 lbs
2nd was 38.5 weeks; I estimate it at about that, 8lb 3oz boy. Gained 45lbs
3rd was 46 weeks, 10lb, 3oz boy. Gained 35lbs
4th was 45 weeks, 10lb, 8oz boy. Gained 35lbs
5th was 44 weeks, 9lb girl. Gained 25lbs

I lost all preg. weight by 4 months postpartum and then had to eat all the time due to sudden shaking and faintness while my babies were nursing. Once nursing was over, this didn't happen anymore. Once my first passed his 1st year, he was robust, and the others were from birth. They all held their own heads up from birth, all advanced physically and cognitively through milestones rapidly. They are 13, 12, 11, 9, and 6 now and none have any obvious health problems except for adrenal fatigue, which came on for the first time this winter.

Our winters have been 6-9 months long, we lose the sun completely in December and have only sun and no darkness in the summer. We left for one year to the south where my children's sudden and brilliant health was astonishing to me; I couldn't believe how easy it was to be healthy! We are sun-starved up here north of 60, and it is now beginning to take its toll. We are moving south this summer/autumn, for sun. I am always brewing some root/herb tonic to help our bodies cope, and manage our meals throughout the week according to what I see we need individually and together, but it is apparent that this is not enough and never was.

I have a detailed analysis of an IQ test I took last year post-TBI, so I will be able to track improvements somewhat empirically. There was an 80-point disparity between my highest scores to my memory and concentration scores. I think I would already have a higher score after starting CT; I can see the improvements in my daily life. But I am still far off of my previous capacity, and I am not necessarily expecting to recover it fully, but rather to improve my functional daily experience for myself and my children. My current level of function is disabled, but this is not an end point for me, as I have seen my improvements over the past nearly two years and strongly believe- and deeply, emphatically know- neuroplasticity, and I am doing this all on my own and with the help of generous people who share their knowledge freely. I have a lot to live for and lot more to give, and I want to live a long life to enjoy a legacy of a permaculture settlement where my children's children and on and on can grow from the very stuff of the soil there. It's a lot to do, and I'll need some time to get it going.

Our supplements are:
D3
B complex
Mg bis-glycinate and threonate, also Mg oil and Epsom salts in baths
C-complex (with cofactors)
sometimes D-ribose (started for fatigue post-TBI)
sometimes fish oil (I have a taste for it and know when I need it; I eat fish 5x+/wk)

I use food as much as possible for nutrients, not pills

That's all for now (Some is repeat from my first post in another section)

### Imogen Skye — 2017-01-25T07:33:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tbi-healing-and-family-health-optimising-me-and-my-children.18985/post-207313>

Oh! Today, I noticed that the right side of my face is more responsive (my whole right side is slower than my left now and has less feeling). Since TBI, it has been droopy and sometimes slow to catch up to the left side's expressions. It feels weird still, like the muscles are starting to work faster but they are weak. I was practicing smiling and lifting my cheek. This is the first change in my face I've had at all since the accident, in spite of exercises and infrared lamp treatment. I don't know if it is from CT or because I was subject to a bunch of fast, bright flashing that nearly gave me a seizure on Friday (it builds up and then it's like an explosion in my brain that becomes the seizure, but I got out in time), and that maybe did something with my circuitry, or a combination of both. It was a seizure that caused the first facial drooping. It maybe that CT has already provided an underlying condition of adequate wellness for the circuitry to be jolted a bit into its proper place.

Anyway, I'd be so thrilled for my face to work normally again!

### JanSz — 2017-01-25T15:54:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tbi-healing-and-family-health-optimising-me-and-my-children.18985/post-207330>

Hi Imogen,

TBI from vehicular collision March 2015

One of the problems you may have after TBI is with pituitary gland.

I suggest that you ask your doctor to check behavior of your pituitary.
Pituitary produces hormones that manage whole body.
Have him check all of them.
Do not forget Growth hormone, very important, docs like to stay away from it or give unreasonable explanations when marginal or low. Keep eye on it. Start with it.
When testing IGF-1 make sure that doc orders (good quality) test.
Not chemoluminescence
but
RIA(radioimmunoassay)
On that test you want to have
IGF-1(220-300)ug/L

**Anterior Lobe Hormones:**

-   **Adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH):** ACTH stimulates the adrenal glands to produce hormones.
-   **Follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH):** FSH works with LH to ensure normal functioning of the ovaries and testes.
-   **Growth hormone (GH):** GH is essential in early years to maintaining a healthy body composition and for growth in children. In adults, it aids healthy bone and muscle mass and affects fat distribution.
-   **Luteinizing hormone (LH):** LH works with FSH to ensure normal functioning of the ovaries and testes.
-   **Prolactin**: Prolactin stimulates breast milk production.
-   **Thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH):** TSH stimulates the thyroid gland to produce hormones.

**Posterior Lobe Hormones:**

-   **Anti-diuretic hormone (ADH):** This hormone prompts the kidneys to increase water absorption in the blood.
-   **Oxytocin:** Oxytocin is involved in a variety of processes, such as contracting the uterus during childbirth and stimulating breast milk production.

.
.

During accident it is prone to injury.

We need everything

nothing is only bad
we need everything in the right amount.

### Sean Waters — 2017-01-31T13:22:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/abis-journal.19013/post-207625>

Hi everyone,

I'm writing this journal for my girlfriend Abi, as i'm concerned about her health.

She currently has flu, blocked nose and a cough, but loads of mucous and it is really bloody.

She also has swelling in her left cheek/ jaw/ ear. It aches and feels like high pressure.

A few years ago she had something similar happen, and her whole face swelled up and the Doctors were clueless, she had her tooth pulled out by a dentist with no pain killer as they couldn't anaesthesize the huge swollen cheek. When the tooth was pulled out all of this Gunk and Mucous fell through and the swelling and pressure disappeared.

Obviously she doesn't want to have another tooth ripped out, so what is this cause of this swelling and clotting of mucous?

It has yet to swell up as bad as before, but i'm wary of it happening again.

Her background:
\- Borderline Celiac (leaky gut)
\- Vitamin D deficient
\- Has precancerous cells on her Ovaries
\- has a damaged nose from breaking it a few times as a child (dancing, falling over), could this be why the mucus is leaking into the cheek?
\- Constantly on Laptop and iPhone, but Airplone modes the phone at night and I've bought her blue blockers that arrive today
\- Gets little sunlight
\- Mother had bowel cancer
\- Mother also had a cyst removed recently on the back of her neck which is now removed
\- Both of them seem to have overgrowth and mutations very easily

If anyone has any experience and advice on what to do please let me know.

### JanSz — 2017-01-31T17:52:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/abis-journal.19013/post-207661>

I am not a doc but it looks like a bad sinus (closed ostia).

This days there are ways to clean this up and keep ostia open (without this being major surgery).
Go for it.

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paranasal\_sinuses#Clinical\_relevance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paranasal_sinuses#Clinical_relevance)
The paranasal sinuses are joined to the [nasal cavity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nasal_cavity) via small orifices called [ostia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sinus_ostia).

### Sean Waters — 2017-02-01T14:06:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/abis-journal.19013/post-207701>

JanSz,

Thank you for your reply.

She has Lugols already, which she used topically on her skin as she had some small cysts on her face. Lack of sunlight + OD of blue light I think. Either way, it reduced the lumps right down.

HCL - will get some of this as I need some too

Sperti - I have a UV reptile bulb, can we use this instead? The Sperti doesn't deliver to UK and its way out of my budget right now. Lamp is obviously not as big or effective, so maybe 5-10 minutes EACH on face, arms, neck and legs, within 10cm of the lamp? The Sperti looks epic though, that's something on my wish list now!

Selenium - I will get her this too, I can't get her to eat Sardines or Seafood, but she might eat brazil nuts too.

Iodine - got it already, will get her on it again!

DHEA - Will get her this. I've got her registered at a new surgery in London and arranged a blood test appointment for Vitamin D, DHEA and is there anything else should check for? CR-P maybe? Hormones?.... I will then get her to do a follow up in April/ May.

Dancing - its disco dancing. She did it as a kid, but she wants to learn Salsa now, just needs encouraging. Fuck it I might do it with her actually, I've just started up Mixed martial arts again, so may as well while I'm on a role!

Artificial lighting - thanks for the link, I'm gonna read through it on my break.

Mother - will get to her next!

**Questions
**
1\. How long does she need to be on these supplements for? Until we see results from bloods that things are better or n=1 how she feels?

2\. Again, is reptile lamp ok for Vit D as per the above?

3\. Just to clarify, should we monitor Pregesterone, estrogens and testosterone in the next blood test as well as Vit D and DHEA?

4\. What range do they need to be in for optimal levels?

**Comments
**
Forgot to mention...

\- She has been using Birth Control Pills for a few years, her breasts went huge on them when she started when she was 15, then she came off the pill after 6 or 7 years and they have shrunk a bit. She is now OFF birth control pills and we are doing the Fertility awareness plan
\- She had her first period in about 4 months as soon as she come off the pill, last month

Could the above affect hormone levels for the blood test? How long should we wait until they might 'normalize' or not be influenced by BCP?

Thank you very much for your help!!

Sean

She has pains in her Shin bones sometimes, usually after too much blue light in the morning...

I think she damaged them a few years ago, they still must be damaged. If I rub my hand on her shin it makes her shiver and cringe, its a really sensitive area for her.

### Sean Waters — 2017-02-01T16:51:38+0200

Inger, I totally agree... the thing is I've been desperate to heal myself and learned all of this stuff, but to someone who is a non-geek like her and who isn't desperate to learn, it all sounds counter-intuitive and difficult to do as the effects aren't visible and measurable to the untrained eye.

I find this to be the case when you talk about blue light and nnEMF to anyone really, I find it very difficult to explain in Layman's terms... people want to talk nutrition and vitamins and organic foods but can't comprehend light. It's right beneath their nose and they can't see it.

JanSz,

My shin bone can take all my strength by the looks of it lol... I'll try it on her later, but I know it's going to be very light because of how sensitive it is.

what could this mean?

### JanSz — 2017-02-01T16:54:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/abis-journal.19013/post-207714>

In London you get minimal sun.
Red Sperti is a must.
Must be Red not White.
Both of you 15 min/day a day at real close distance from the lamp. (2-4inch)
Take 3weeks to harden skin, you do not want to burn skin or even close to it.

Reptile lamps are meant to be real close to the animal.
At the distance that people would use it, you may want to discount any UV.
But get lots of them and make your daylight out of them.
I use Solar Glow + red + IR at day time and red + IR latter.
see this:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)
\--------------------------
In London you can get very good test:

[https://www.spectracell.com/order/](https://www.spectracell.com/order/)
Micronutrient Test (MNT)

REGENERUS LABORATORIES
24 Underhill Park Rd Reigate, United Kingdom RH2 9LX (Map)
Phone: 0333-9000-979
\----------------------------

Look up the script I use:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199717](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199717)
for girls skip #30 (PSA)

\----------------------------
There must be an easy way to incorporate marshal arts with salsa.

[http://www.salsabeatmachine.org/blog/2011/01/the-key-to-dancing-salsa-on-2/](http://www.salsabeatmachine.org/blog/2011/01/the-key-to-dancing-salsa-on-2/)

### caroline — 2017-02-02T01:14:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/abis-journal.19013/post-207752>

Can you take her somewhere and get some sun? Majorca?

Please get John Ott's book ...Health and light. It is a very easy and quick read.

Does your girlfriend want the same fate as her Mom? surely not! She needs to get her act together - unfortunately you can't do it for her.

She needs to get off her tech stuff and get outside naked.

What is she eating? drinking?

Do you have all electronics out of the bedroom?
How is her sleep?

It is a hard lesson - but none of us can do much for the ones we love unless they are willing to invest and have skin in the game.

Good Luck

### caroline — 2017-02-02T01:17:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/abis-journal.19013/post-207753>

BTW ....Jack said on FB this morning that he is seeing a lot more young people with Tumours......

### Inger — 2017-02-02T09:39:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/abis-journal.19013/post-207771>

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=207713)
>
> Inger, I totally agree... the thing is I've been desperate to heal myself and learned all of this stuff, but to someone who is a non-geek like her and who isn't desperate to learn, it all sounds counter-intuitive and difficult to do as the effects aren't visible and measurable to the untrained eye.
>
> I find this to be the case when you talk about blue light and nnEMF to anyone really, I find it very difficult to explain in Layman's terms... people want to talk nutrition and vitamins and organic foods but can't comprehend light. It's right beneath their nose and they can't see it.

I know.....
My fiance listen to me when I tell him about those things but he is not really busy to change stuff... like he has still not installed f.lux on his computer! lol
But when we are together he always take care to only have candles on so he respect my rules a lot. and he switches off the wifi too. And he barely is on any tech when we are together.
He wants to change those things for him too as he kinda gets it cant be good but he is so healthy and strong so I guess he does not see any urgent need.

That said, the other day we were talking at the phone (we do every evening for quite some time when we are apart). I have a landline and always use that, and I switch off the electricity too using it because it still works, but with way less electric/magnetic radiation. Very cool btw . But he was using his cell phone out of convenience...and after talking for maybe almost an hour he said.. OH we need to stop NOW my head started to hurt real bad! And he got so scared! Me too.
After that day he calls me from his landline when talking longer talks...

I have told him many times to use the landline, but he kind of ignored it... but now he got scared. Sometimes we need to really feel it to change it..

Maybe you need to take you girlfriend for a vacation without any technology and lots of sun and nature and she might feel a big difference and start to realize that the tech is hurting her?

When we are together we just cuddle a lot all evening and read in candle light and talk and go early to bed we both love it. Maybe if you start doing that every evening with your girlfriend she will start liking it way more than being on her tech stuff   and she forget it

**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal

### Dylan J — 2017-02-08T20:23:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dylans-journal.19054/post-208233>

Hello all! I am a 26 yr new to the whole Optimal and hoping for some guidance on where to jump in

I got tested a few months back before discovering the whole Dr. Kruse world. (Labs attached)

My understanding from looking at my body, cravings, etc. is I'm currently Leptin Sensitive but I'm not 100% sure. I have been eating a Bulletproof Diet for about 24 months before these labs (Attached). But that whole time included the "safe starch" theory on some nights.

A few notes from the labs/life:
\-The high LDL jumps out at me as a possible infection/leaky gut? (My functional med doc recommended Mag O7 and saccharomyces boulardii which has seemed to help a bit).
\-I still have pretty big reactions to eggs (acne/stomach aches)...autoimmune/leaky gut?
\-I felt terrible when I had 5 or so oysters for the first time last week...autoimmune/leaky gut?
\-These labs were taken about 4 months after moving from Maine(near a power line) to a Washington DC highrise so may have traded one poor EMF for another. Less ppl in maine though and cleaner water.
\-I still have acne
\-I am radically effected in a positive way if I supplement with Gaba and 5-HTP. Anxiety decreases and Mood increases...making me think maybe not as leptin sensitive as I think.

My question is what prescription to tackle first?

As of writing this, I am moving back to maine in a week or so and looking to take my first steps towards optimal.

Thank you!

\-Dylan

# Thread: Chad's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chads-optimal-journal.19062/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Primed for your Life Chad — 2017-02-09T13:00:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chads-optimal-journal.19062/post-208298>

I love everything about making my life revolve around light, water and magnetism. I am conscious to maximize all the good aspects and minimize the bad. I am grateful for Jack and all his staff and this group.

I am 48, fit and healthy. Australian adopted from New Zealand (Irish Mum) to Hong Kong, Singapaore, US, Canada and eventually Adelaide. Had a restaurant in Brisbane but now a Wellness Coach in the Philippines after being in the Hospitality industry.

5'9 / 64 kilos (141 pounds). Good skin, sleep, energy and body functioning.

Previously over-trained (4 Ironman events and many marathons) and fueled poorly in a bad environment.

Travelling towards optimal for last 3 years but especially since really getting addicted to learning from Dr. Jack mid-way last year, 2016.

I live in a bad quantum environment in Manila with high population density and wi-fri and cell towers. I use an inexpensive cell without connection to internet. I have an ipad.  Ipad will go and we will move eventually.

Current Labs show:

\- Vitamin D of 99 nmol/L ( I wear minimal clothing for maximum sun exposure on my northern European skin- it is Philippine winter here at the moment)

\- HDL of 136, Triglycerides of 44 so ratio of .32. Previous ratio of .37

\- Total cholesterol of 348 and LDL of 204.

\- HS-CRP is a high 3.92 but was .74 in Nov. 2016 and .83 in April 2016. I do not over-exercise any more!) Not sure why so high for this period? I had a 24 hour "flu" just starting so I think this might have affected this reading.

\- Bun / Creatinine ratio has gone from

15 in 2013
16 in 2014
13 in 2015
19 in 2016
22 in 2017

HbA1c

5.6 April 2016
5.0 Nov. 2016
5.7 Feb. 2017

I do not drink alcohol, maximize sunshine, eat a lot of seafood and quality fats and green veg. Love CT and minimize fake lite but not enough. I do run to all my client meetings so sometimes run 20km within a day but wear a quality pollution mask and never feel tired or even sore.

Thanks.

### Primed for your Life Chad — 2017-02-10T05:35:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chads-optimal-journal.19062/post-208348>

Hello Dr. Jack Kruse, Any feedback on the above would be greatly appreciated.

Have read your book twice, listened to the podcasts up to 5 times each and study everyday on how to get to optimal.

Do CT, do not eat after sun down, feel I am a lot smarter, stronger and happier. I am a success story like others here but want to get to OPTIMAL too.

Thanks.

Dear Rocky,

Most docs would write a prescription for statins already because the high HDL means high Total Cholesterol (I am at 348).
I feel energized, have good skin, good brain function (not forgetful anymore) and am getting YOUNGER (haha).
Thanks for the comment and happy OPTIMAL days for us all.
CT is on my agenda tonight.
Cheers.

Chad,

I was told by a doctor several years ago statins would help me. My total cholesterol is between 325 and 350, but my HDL is 65 mg/dl and likewise Tri's are 44. I'm somewhat older than you but hope to improve these numbers and have added as much water, light and magnetism as I am able to squeeze into a day. While we're alive we might as well get younger, getting older sucks!

Stay cold!

I'm somewhat older than you but hope to improve these numbers and have added as much water, light and magnetism as I am able to squeeze into a day. While we're alive we might as well get younger, getting older sucks!
>
> Stay cold!

Hey Rocky, Love the "stay cold". I quote "cool" a lot with the double meaning lost on most.

I am up to 45 minutes in the cold plunge (approximately 52 degrees) and love it. I still shiver even 10 minutes after in the sauna!

Definitely much cooler to get younger and happier.

I also had a doc want to give me a statin prescription after I ran a 3:30 marathon following a morning bike of 91km and with my HDL at 126 and Trigs at 26. Haha. I don't do those crazy events any more and prefer CT and running lightly with my daughter.

I even sent her a lot of information but the drug company holiday and cash is mightier than the facts!!

Two top docs here just told a conference here that statins are good but could not answer a simple question on how to improve liver health. Grrrrr.

So glad to be part of this community. Cheers,

Chad.

### Rocky — 2017-02-12T20:31:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chads-optimal-journal.19062/post-208476>

Chad,

How do you do your cold plunge, bathub and ice? What do you do for the 45 minutes you are in? What holds your attention? Does it feel good?

I'm doing cold showers for 1-1/2 - 2 minutes and hope to take ice baths someday soon.

### Primed for your Life Chad — 2017-02-13T02:01:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chads-optimal-journal.19062/post-208486>

> [Rocky said:](/goto/post?id=208476)
>
> Chad,
>
> How do you do your cold plunge, bathub and ice? What do you do for the 45 minutes you are in? What holds your attention? Does it feel good?
>
> I'm doing cold showers for 1-1/2 - 2 minutes and hope to take ice baths someday soon.

Dear Rocky,

I started with face then feet in an ice bucket with my children and we are now loving the ice baths. It gets a lot easier as you go along.

I live nearby a hotel that also has a sauna and a cold plunge (as cold as an ice bath) side by side.

With my ice bath at hoe I listen to Jack Kruse youtube videos with the lights off and the hotel one I listen to Jack Kruse podcasts.

You need to concentrate on your breathing and I really love it. You sleep like a champion afterwards.

I sometimes am still shaking 20 minutes after but also enjoy this process too because I know it is making me younger!

Cheers mate and have a great week ahead.

Chad.

That's awesome Chad...wonder if others experience is the same as yours with shaking afterwards? Can wait to give this a try...thanks and you had a great week too!

# Thread: Thoughts on Artificial Ways of Getting UV Exposure for People in High Latitudes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-artificial-ways-of-getting-uv-exposure-for-people-in-high-latitudes.19151/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1

### James Hawkins — 2017-02-27T17:52:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-artificial-ways-of-getting-uv-exposure-for-people-in-high-latitudes.19151/post-209387>

Hi all,

I've been thinking about how to get UV light exposure in a safe and effective way. I'm currently living in Northern England, and in particular in a very cloudy/misty region, so sun exposure is minimal, especially around this time of year. I'm curious what people's thoughts are about means of getting exposure to artificial UV light in way that does not cause skin or eye damage. My practice is to use a UV light bulb with about 30 minutes daily of low dose (i.e. bulb is about 1 metre away from body) exposure, with no clothes on my upper extremities. Do you think this would cause any problems in terms of skin damage or exposure to the eyes. I'm curious as to whether other people on the forum have experimented with this.

Cheers,

James
