# Thread: Severe CFS, Fibro, Lyme(?), Burnout, etc. Totally Bedbound
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 91
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-13T18:22:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210256>

Hello all,

I’m new to the message board and relatively new Dr. Kruse’s work, but already heavily invested in it. Today I want to ask for your help on my specific issues, to see if anyone notices anything major that I might be missing. I’ll keep it as short as possible, though it’s complex and long-standing. Please understand that I’m doing the best I can, but am often so exhausted it’s too much for me to try to read, much less comprehend complex information like on the doc’s blog, so if I’m missing anything obvious here, or if this type of massive post is somehow violating the general rules, be gentle please. J I’m happy to adjust to whatever I’m not currently understanding.

My main symptoms are as follows (I’m leaving some of the “lesser” ones out here for the sake of brevity):

\- Insomnia + severely exaggerated sympathetic nervous system response since a young age, worsening over time to the point that now I sometimes can’t sleep if I have so much as a phone call the next day. For a long time I did not sleep at all and was in a state of such sympathetic dominance and biorhythm imbalance that I was never fully awake but never asleep, either, like being in a hellish purgatory. I couldn’t think, and had no control of my emotions. Most of the time I'm kinda half here, and thank God my brain can kind of run on autopilot so I can keep the thread of conversations somehow (and essays like this). I’ve had one seizure in my life, and I can say it was sort of like being in a state of prolonged seizure. With the things I’m doing (below) I’m starting to get good sleep most nights, finally.

\- Exhuastion so severe that there are periods of a week or more where I can hardly bathe myself; I pee into a bottle most of the time because I can’t walk the 10 feet to the bathroom everytime I need to pee. On good days, I can go down the stairs to sit in the living room with my parents, but still can’t cook for myself. I can go outside every so often with great effort.

\- Difficulty thinking, much less concentrating

\- Generalized, whole-body inflammation, including joint, nerve, muscle, and spinal pain and discomfort constantly

\- Severe heaviness in all my muscles – legs and arms feel like they weigh 50 pounds each sometimes

\- Difficulty breathing that is not related to any kind of anxiety

\- “Adrenal and thyroid burnout” – which I have come to understand have more to do with PVN/hypothalamus than anything else

\- Chronic infection in the teeth (after an idiot oral surgeon did a terrible job pulling my wisdom teeth) that I keep at bay with oil of oregano; without that, within 24 hours my joints are on fire and I can’t hardly bathe myself.

\- I had one seizure a few years back that almost killed me, but none since.

\- I have been diagnosed with Lyme disease, but for reasons I won’t get into, I think the issue is either secondary to the exhaustion factors OR not really Lyme. However, if I don't take oil of oregano constantly, as I've said, I go from being able to at least bathe myself every other day and walk to the bathroom to go #2, to not being able to bathe myself and sometimes needing help to walk to the bathroom once a day (and peeing in a bottle in either case).

\- Severe depression, anxiety, panic. I took antidepressants for 8 years, not because I wanted to, but because I started young and ignorantly, and then when I tried to get off them, it was a life-and-death kind of situation with how severely depressed I got. I have finally managed to get off of them permanently, and I’m 16 months free of them.

\- Blood sugar regulation is not diabetic, but definitely not great.

\- Bladder has been very overactive since I was a kid, and water seems to go right through me.

\- Short-term discomfort in liver and/or kidneys, periodically

\- Note that before I got this sick was a period of severe stress—and I really do mean severe, in the worst sense of the world.

A few concepts I already understand: basics on sunlight, water, and magnetism my body as a battery/the photoelectric effect and the role of DHA, the periventricular nucleus as the source of adrenal problems, biorhythms, etc.

Here’s what I’m currently doing:

Sunlight in the eyes in the morning when I get up for 5-10 minutes and on warmer days when I can stand I get on the roof to take in some sun in underwear (roof faces the road, so need UV-allowing swimsuit); blue-blocking glasses after sunset + body covered indoors; minimum 64-80 oz. of purified, non-plastic water every day; 20 gauss Magnetico sleep pad; almost entirely blacked out bedroom; magnesium bicarbonate, oil of oregano, shilajit, Master Amino Pattern in the morning, and some various supplements for “general nutrition”, and about to start a high-quality DHA supplement produced from algae in the SN2 position (I was taking simple fish oil before); an increasingly paleo diet (I do still eat some fruit and a little bit of wild rice, but I do eat lots of seafood, cooked vegetables, and bee products). I’m too exhausted to get down the stairs and outside (and it’s far too cold right now) but on warmer days I lay on the roof right outside the window as long as I can to absorb sunlight; full-spectrum light box on my body several hours a day; near infrared heat lamps; meditation and breathing practices; positive visualization; psychotherapy to help manage the hell that this is.

I’m too weak for cold thermogenesis right now, and my core temperature can't handle cold, but want to start doing face in cold water for just a bit and see how it goes. I walk down the stairs once on the days I’m able, which is about 2/3 of the time at present, an improvement from a couple months ago. I hope I’m not forgetting anything there. EMFs neutralized as much as possible, and since I live in a rural area, ambient EMF is less than 0.4 milligauss, thankfully. I can do only a little bit of NIR therapy or sunlight at a time because it can severely overstimulate my NS and adrenals and leave me exhausted. Again, it's hard for me to even walk much of the time.

I do take melatonin, which I realize now is a big no-no, but I’ll have to figure out how to taper off it later, as I’m been taking it for years to have any hope of sleeping. CBDs have helped some, though I realize I want my body to eventually be able to do it without them. But you have to understand, my body is just barely coming out of a life-and-death scenario, and I can’t do it all perfectly at once.

MY TAKE ON WHAT’S GOING ON:

I have worked with some decent doctors, but they were all clueless about the issues Dr. Kruse shares with us. It’s been a little over a year now, but I did have a full run-down of lab testing, and the main things that showed up were signs of severe exhaustion: low B vitamins, low alpha-lipoic acid, some relatively minor food allergies, and stool sample showing moderate gut dys-biosis and difficulty digesting proteins.

Methylation status: I'm not sure on this one, but I think I might have one of the MTHFR variants that isn't the really bad one, but isn't in perfect shape either. If that makes sense. Haven't been tested in a little over a year, but things haven't gotten worse in that time. Not sure what I should be doing for that precisely.

So it seems to me that my body is most likely extremely exhausted from years of nervous system overstimulation, that my biorhythms are severely out of whack, and that my nervous system and brain are severely depleted. I don’t actually think it’s much more complicated than that. And despite all the difficult crap in this post, I’m actually VERY OPTIMISTIC about all this, and I am convinced I’m going to be well, even if it takes a year or five. I have the support of my family, thank God, and with that comes a small budget to work with (which is how I’m paying for the Magnetico pad on a six-month payment plan).

So a few areas I’m interested in learning more about: modulating the stress/sympathetic response, and anything that relates to the adrenal glands, hypothalamus, and PVN; rebuilding energy production; **which tests** are necessary to get to check for things that could be messing with my recovery (things like methylation status, but of a variety I’m not even aware of) and anything else that seems relevant.

Thanks for reading such a long post, if you got through it all without skipping to the end. If you're going to tell me I can't get well, don't bother posting, please. I already get enough of that kind of thinking, so I want to hear from anyone who believes I can do this, even if it takes a while. Have an amazing day!

Matthew

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-13T18:26:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210259>

A little health history: nothing major in the family comparatively speaking.

A few cases of cancer on mom's side. Some depression in the family, only one or two severe cases in a large extended family. Parents both fairly healthy. A couple cases of MS on dad's side. One diabetic cousin. Overall a fairly healthy family of mostly farmers. Their biggest problem seems to be they overwork themselves and don't sleep enough. I never did as much work as they did since I've been exhausted since fairly young.

I personally have received no diagnoses other than severe exhaustion, depression and anxiety (no shit!) and probable Lyme Disease, CFS, and Fibro.

The only thing I still need to do is disable WiFi. However, we measured the EMFs coming off the WiFi router, and it's very little. Almost negligible. Can someone explain why this is so bad?

### JanSz — 2017-03-13T22:33:58+0200

Hi matthewsb77,
Glad to see you here.

You said:
Overall a fairly healthy family of mostly farmers.
Minnesota is where I live now.

Lots of wheat farmers there.
Farmers, specially when wheat growing,
when near harvest time, to stop wheat grass from growing they spray it with Roundup.
Not sure if you guys call it Roundup there, but it is a chemical that kills vegetation.
You can't harvest green wheat, but you want it to grow to the last possible minute.
So Monsanto figured out how to do both.
Even if your family does not grow wheat and deal with roundup,
Roundup goes into air and falls on you during rainfall.
Roundup contains glyphosate.

This may be your #1 problem.
The rest is a downstream of it.

..

### Penny — 2017-03-13T23:23:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210284>

I would eat oysters for the zinc (to make P5P/serotonin) and use rhodiola, shisandra during the day to mitigate the cortisol and ashwaganda and lemon balm tea for the GABA at night - your eyes need morning light to make serotonin - in fact, you could probably help yourself greatly if you looked at the sunrise and stayed outside for a while - you need a small hit of blue light, which signals mitochondrial repair - it also seems to me that you are low in electrons - it seems to me that when cortisol spikes, it uses a lot of electrons and depletes your collagen of them - so, I would eat gelatin and bone broth for the collagen and - he's going to blow a gasket on this one - vitamin c if you can't ground... in fact, if you read a book called Forbidden Healing, he has a lovely energy drink which is 1 tsp of vit c and a smaller amount of baking soda - you can substitute potassium bicarbonate for the baking soda - this will increase your exclusion zone and enable you to hold onto more water - in fact, the baking soda alone (just a pinch in each glass of water) will help - I have tried it after coffee and it will even mitigate some of the diuretic effect - of the adaptogen list, I would try the rhodiola first - the stuff is amazing - I have also had great luck using glycine as a sweetener -
and if at all possible, move downstairs - zero wifi in the house as wifi makes bugs grow - and see if you have a smart meter - you can go to a site called [www.electricsense.com](http://www.electricsense.com) for a lot of EMF mitigation articles - as far as water goes, you should be drinking it constantly - a gallon and a half a day is not too much - coconut water is loaded with potassium - which you are losing in droves if you are losing water - and always start slow with any hack because you can't take the hit - I like Janz post about the glyphosate - but Dr. K on his facebook page had a nice link to how glyphosate replaces glycine - I bet you're loaded with it - another reason to do bone broths (they have proline/glycine/glutamine) which is great for your gut wall - I get 2 lbs of it from Bulk Supplements - cheap way to fly -

Two more hacks for you to try are methylene blue and IR light therapy:
[https://selfhacked.com/2013/08/25/methylene-blue-the-cheapest-cognitive-enhancer/](https://selfhacked.com/2013/08/25/methylene-blue-the-cheapest-cognitive-enhancer/)
[https://selfhacked.com/2013/11/14/my-review-of-lllt/](https://selfhacked.com/2013/11/14/my-review-of-lllt/)

Just get one of those 250 watt IR lights like to brood chicks and lay under it naked - very therapeutic - and apparently glyphosate screws up manganese also - here is a great article about that - you need manganese for SOD -welcome BTW

[http://www.gmoevidence.com/dr-samsel-glyphosate-chelation-of-manganese-is-a-serious-problem/](http://www.gmoevidence.com/dr-samsel-glyphosate-chelation-of-manganese-is-a-serious-problem/)

Great reply! Thanks so much. A few questions/comments about it:

1) Looked up methylene blue. I'm extremely resistant to taking medications because of a long track record of them making me worse. I'll look into this though. IF I TOOK THIS, HOW LONG WOULD I NEED TO TAKE IT? I would need to avoid a dependency, obviously.

2) This is all great stuff. It's hard to know which stuff to try and which to leave, frankly, in general. Some of this stuff contradicts -- or is simply too much to do in conjunction with everything else I'm doing. How would you prioritize these things in your opinion?

3) Regarding the potassium: as I understand it, the adrenal glands function according to a ratio of sodium to potassium (Na/K), at least in part. The lower the ratio, the worse they'll get. So I'm nervous about supplementing potassium with that in mind.

By the way, totally on board with the glyphosate stuff. Already doing NIR lights and have mitigated most EMFs, though still have to figure out the WiFi and the Smartmeter.

### Penny — 2017-03-14T01:55:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210293>

You are absolutely right - too much stream of consciousness

The absolute best thing to do would be to go camping somewhere south of you where it is already summertime - taking lots of water and seafood - but, failing that, you need to get and stay hydrated so you can capture sunlight - camping would also fix your screwed up clock and give you the electrons you need - failing that, you have to make do with what you have - which is the cold - if you can get to a hot tub, warm your bottom half in the tub and leave your top half exposed to the cold - I forget what he called it - oh some Fournier effect -
So, you need collagen (bone broth), electrons (seafood/earthing/grounding), water and sunlight - and I guess you have the magnetism thing already handled - start with sunlight - there is a newbie thread here that tells you the order to do this stuff in -here it is:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/)
Don't survive, thrive. Do life soaring. Our mitochondria in our cells read and react to our environment; it shapes us and we are shaped by its "perspectives".

1\. Sunlight
2\. Un-fluoridated water
3\. Magnetism/grounding to Earth/CT protocol
4\. seafood
5\. Self Confidence
6\. Friends who connect with you in life and not just on line.
That is all...........Carry On.
[http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029](http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029)

I can think of many recent members who went of the rails this way. The last 4 series pointed this point this out but few understand the implications. The closer you are to the equator means you have more push from your current (DC) and then you may not need as much........unless there is something peculiar about your environment, job, eyes, skin, lungs, gut.

### kris90 — 2017-03-14T13:20:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210318>

Some thoughts:

\-Would like to see labs, maybe copies of old bloodwork, or go get updated comprehensive panels
\-Would like to know your endocrine profile (sex hormones, cortisol, thyroid, etc)
\-You keep mentioning that you think you got into this situation from stress. Have you identified the stress? Known, or unknown? Any allergies, or intolerances? Hidden toxin exposure? EMF sensitivity?
\-Sympathetic Nervous system is your immediate fight or flight system. It's meant for short term activation when: you are hungry, you face an immediate threat to your wellbeing, cold exposure and exercise. When you have chronic stimulus of sympathetic nervous system, the HPA-axis is stimulated to release cortisol which is our more long-term stress hormone, because at this point, the body needs energy badly. Chronically elevated cortisol will waste your body away, wreak havoc on immunity, decrease your T4 -> T3 conversion slowing down your metabolic rate, and suppressing your sex hormones. Additionally, it will interfere with sleep, etc. Need to identify the chronic stress.
\-From the above point, if this describes you, you may need to skip the winter ketogenic diet and feed your body with carbs. Cortisol is a glucocorticoid, its goal is to make glucose in the body out of whatever it can (muscle, bone and other proteins). Feeding your body exogenous glucose will help your body rely less on cortisol to make glucose, which automatically reduces your stress.
\-Look into supplementing with adaptogens like ginseng, it might help balance your sympathetic nervous response
\-After checking all your hormones, if you have deficiencies (at the pituitary level) consider an MRI of your brain to rule out a tumor. Sometimes you can have an ACTH secreting tumor in your pituitary that causes chronic cortisol. Need to rule that out.

I think 4x Cortisol saliva test would be a better gauge, because you can detect both high cortisol, and a dysfunctional diurnal rhythm of cortisol.

Sometimes individuals have low cortisol in the AM, but high cortisol in the PM, representing an inverse diurnal rhythm.

I will work to get my labs from last year scanned up. I had pretty comprehensive work done at that point, and I think it'd still be a fair measure of what's going on now since not a lot has changed in that time.

Coritsol reversal has definitely been an issue for me for some time. It's shown up on tests. The last one showed a pretty classic pattern of low AM cortisol and higher cortisol at night. That was a couple years ago, and things have gotten a lot worse since then, so I have no idea what it looks like now.

IDENTIFYING STRESS - yes, I think you're right on the money with this one. Bravo, brother! I have had some severe trauma in my life, and a lot of it got dissociated and split off for survival, for reasons I won't get into here. But it did create some PTSD, HPA-axis dysfunction with chronic elevated stress, depression, anxiety, etc. etc. But I am doing EMDR therapy, which is a form of therapy unlike any other I've tried because it really bypasses the whole analyze-and-talk routine and just gets right into the neurology of it. I'm about two months in right now, and it does seem to be having some effect for sure, possibly quite a bit. Hard to say what does what when I do so much. I think it would have more if my body weren't so totally exhausted, but it is what it is. But yes, I've removed most of the sources of stress by this point. Sensitivities, allergies, EMFs are accounted for as well.

My only issue with the recommendation of a high-carb diet is that my blood sugar is not super stable. It's in the near-prediabetic or diabetic range, depending on the measure you want to use. I also have been taught that with adrenal/HPA-axis problems, you don't want to do lots of carbs. Everybody says ketogenic, ketogenic, ketogenic. Am I wrong about this?

THE ONE ISSUE I HAVE WITH TAKING TESTS, besides all the money it takes, is that often the approaches are the same regardless of what the tests show. I realize that sometimes there's a specific level you need to supplement, and that's where it's going to matter the most, but. . . I'll see what I can do.

### Penny — 2017-03-14T20:04:17+0200

So, here's the low hanging fruit thing...

Are you in a second story room using a lap top? And is that lap top using wifi? If so, you are chronically raising cortisol -I would really start with moving to the ground floor and trying to be outside as much as possible - even touching a tree branch for the electrons - and if cortisol is turned around, do the ol' leptin reset with \*zero\* blue light at night - at least unplug the router at night, cordless phones and put all devices in airplane mode - make sure the wifi is turned off on your computer - even opeing up a window to let some sunlight in would be beneficial - do you have any relatives who live in a sunny climate you might visit for a couple of weeks? Also, see if you have a smart meter and where it is - this is probably the worst of the lot -

as far as getting some peace in your body, thus increasing resonance, you might want to try different ways to stimulate your Vagus nerve :
[https://selfhacked.com/2015/07/30/2...agus-nerve-and-all-you-need-to-know-about-it/](https://selfhacked.com/2015/07/30/28-ways-to-stimulate-your-vagus-nerve-and-all-you-need-to-know-about-it/)

Lie on your back, watch your breath, inhale and utter things to yourself like "love" or "I'm good" - at the end of it, you should feel a sublime feeling of peace - you can also "OM" and listen to music at 432hz - there's a ton of it on youtube - do that as much as possible -

lavender also is amazing as it resets your hypothalamus - lavender oil, lavender tea with glycine - also lemon balm, ashwaghanda and anything else that's GABA inducing - dump a bunch of drops on your bed at night -

Also, isolating yourself is probably not the hottest idea either - let me know if any of these help you - it'll be summer soon, even in MN...

You also might look into EFT (Emotional Freedom Technique) - people have had good luck with it -

Good! Step 1 is identifying and eliminating the stressors.

> [matthewsb77 said:](/goto/post?id=210344)
>
> My only issue with the recommendation of a high-carb diet is that my blood sugar is not super stable. It's in the near-prediabetic or diabetic range, depending on the measure you want to use. I also have been taught that with adrenal/HPA-axis problems, you don't want to do lots of carbs. Everybody says ketogenic, ketogenic, ketogenic. Am I wrong about this?

I disagree. If Cortisol, our long-term stress hormone that's sole purpose is to provide glucose to the body anyway possible when we are deficient under chronic stress (excessive energy steal syndrome), then it means glucose is EVEN more critical. The more you can get in your diet, the less cortisol will have to steal from your proteins. Carbs in the diet will be anti-catabolic which is a good thing under chronic stress.

Regarding your high blood sugar, there is a lot of evidence that low fat/high carb diets can improve insulin sensitivity, and have been used successfully to treat type 2 diabetes. This is a good read: [https://deniseminger.com/2015/10/06...-a-call-for-some-evolution-of-thought-part-1/](https://deniseminger.com/2015/10/06/in-defense-of-low-fat-a-call-for-some-evolution-of-thought-part-1/)

### JanSz — 2017-03-14T21:51:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210357>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eye\_movement\_desensitization\_and\_reprocessing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eye_movement_desensitization_and_reprocessing)

**Eye movement desensitization and reprocessing** (**EMDR**) is a form of [psychotherapy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Psychotherapy) developed by [Francine Shapiro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Francine_Shapiro) that emphasizes the role of distressing memories in some mental health disorders, particularly [posttraumatic stress disorder](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Posttraumatic_stress_disorder) (PTSD).[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eye_movement_desensitization_and_reprocessing#cite_note-shapiro-1) It is an evidence-based therapy used to help with the symptoms of PTSD

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210371>

**LAB RESULTS (some mid-2015, most early-mid 2016)

LYME:** I did the iGenex tests maybe 5 or 6 years back. The test was considered indeterminate because I tested positive on one marker of one test, out of about 6-8 different things on the panel. The one I tested positive on was the Plasmid B-Burgdorfi, "Southern Dot Blot." I can scan the tests in at some point if it would help, but the tests say that <1:40 = negative, 1:40 = indeterminate, and = >1.80 is positive. I was the middle one. So based on this, I went ahead and did the Cowden (herbal) protocol, and felt not change whatsoever in any way. Same with long-term antibiotics targeted at it. NOTHING changed. Not better, not worse, no die-off, no nothing. So I left it alone. Anybody able to comment on this apparent contradiction?
**DHEA -** High, believe it or not. HOWEVER, this was in the early months of severe stress, and I don't know if this is one of those levels that drops after stress. Could anyone inform? This was a test done in 2015.
**Low B vitamins** \- but when I take them, it's too stimulating for my nervous system, so I don't supplement them hardly ever at present. . . I crash and feel worse
**B-12** is HIGH on some tests, LOW on others (for the same period) - WHY?
**MTHFR - HETEROZYGOUS, C677T**. I understand this means I have a single mutation, but what's the proper supplement to deal with this properly?
**Low alpha-lipoid acid
High cholesterol** (though I was eating shittier meat at that time, and now better meat)
**High gadolinium
Low gut bacteria diversity
Slightly elevated E.Coli count in gut
Slightly low manganese and molybdenum
Low Testosterone
Poor breakdown of proteins in stool sample (high levels of un-broken-down proteins)
Cortisol - reversal pattern (obviously) in 2015. . .** haven't tested since
**Glucose** - high, in the near-pre-diabetic or pre-diabetic range

(I'm doing great on DHA and EPA, etc., by the way, as well as Vitamin D and pretty much everything else. If anything I could use less according to these tests, and I'm actually supplementing that more now than before.)

Possibly relevant, although within range:
**URINE CREATININE: 42.23 out of a range of about 23-205**

Regarding cortisol, as [JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) mentions, keep room as dark as possible when sleeping. Additionally, make sure you get as MUCH light as possible during the day. Use sunlight as best you can for this, and supplement with bulbs if you must ([JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) can give you good advice for this).

I think some carbs with dinner will help lower your evening cortisol and sleep should improve without reliance on exogenous melatonin. Look into adaptogens to help balance cortisol as well.

> Selenium
> Multi when I can (mostly too stimulating) - need an alternative with low or no B vitamins
> Probiotic/enzyme/veggie nutrient supplement ("Endoveggies")
> A/C/Zinc Copper Manganese B6 ("Limcomin")
> TMG
> Bee pollen
> About to try a DHA supplement made from carefully grown algae that is in the SN2 position- made by Mitolife
> Likely will do some alpha lipoic acid soon
>
> Again, thank you very much. Next doctor's appoint in April, but will get what I can done before that.

I do not know if you need all that supplementation.
Personally I prefer to test and figure it out.
It is easy to miss something basic and eat lots of stuff that you do not need.

If you think that you need help with digestion consider adding
Betaine Hydrochloride with pepsin.
Start with one tab, add another one during next full meat.
Observe your reaction, watch for warmth in your tummy.
I am 77yo and use 10 tabs with meal.
this one, there are many other:
Country Life Betaine Hydrochloride with pepsin 600 mg - 250 Tablets
[https://www.amazon.com/dp/B00068IY0...1&keywords=betaine+hydrochloride+country&th=1](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B00068IY0O/ref=sr_ph_1_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1489957715&sr=sr-1&keywords=betaine%2Bhydrochloride%2Bcountry&th=1)
\==================================================

To figure out (most) of your needed supplementation I suggest that you do this two tests:

at LabCorp

and
[https://www.spectracell.com/order/](https://www.spectracell.com/order/)

### Remo — 2017-03-23T12:22:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210919>

Hi Matthew, how are you doing ?? Im currently very brain fogged but I will try to type correctly.

I came to post in here because I have allot in common with your symptoms. Luckily I have seen improvements over the years but there will always be periods of extreme fatigue , brain fog, depression and fatigue.

Last summer I started seeing massive gains in health for 1 simple reason: I was outside in the sun more then I was inside (pokemon go gave me something to do). After this period I was able to start minor HiiT weight training which gave me a massive boost in testosterone/energy/muscle mass.
I started climbing indoors and for some months I was in "relative" great shape.
When winter hit I got ill and bam... it was like I was send back 6 steps back. I think this recurrence when your immune system drops is very common of Lyme disease or other chronic bacterial infections.

A few weeks ago I tried getting back to HiiT but I noticed that I lost the ability to sweat normally, whenever my body wanted to sweat I would feel a thousand needles pinching me. However If I continued trough the pain my body would sweat normally. I think this problem with sweating/inflammation is a sign of leptin resistance and Lyme disease in general.

1 . How are your sweating patterns ? Do you even sweat ? Looking back it was the combination of direct sunlight, earthing with sweating that really resetted my ability to detoxify. When I lost one of those three (especially sunlight!) it corresponded with a lack of detoxification.
2\. Have you ever done HiiT weight training ? Just lifting something heavy while your outside can promote an incredible spike in -IGF1 hormone growth. From personal experience you can do this even IF your are extremely fatigued. Actually.. the more reason to! walking actually increases cortisol for me while HiiT balances it out positively.

3\. I see you are taking quite allot of supplements. The only ones I would recommend (that has helped me):
Sulphur MSM (Gluthatione Detox),
CBD oil, **Hemp Oil is not the same as CBD**, hemp oil is a great oil but it is falsely advertised as CBD oil. I learned this the hardway! True CBD helped me allot with anxiety and concentration/pain.

4\. DHA, you **cannot** supplement DHA! It has to come from fresh raw fish: herring (biggest DHA), mackerel, Sardines, Oysters.
Read Jack's post on Fish supplements and you will understand.
I am curious to hear about your reactions to eating some raw salter herring. When I eat that its like my brain gets a zap! it usually fades away quickly (sadly) but it makes me feel better in general.

5\. Raw Milk Kefir, Jack is against american soiled diary (rightfully so imo) but if you can find a trustworthy local farmer who makes his own kefir then I would go for it. This stuff is magic for the gut bacteria.

I get raw milk from my cousin's dairy that's about 40 minutes away. They don't do kefir yet, but I put in the suggestion. Their cows are bred for A2 genes so they're not inflammatory, and my uncle, a cow geneticist, gives me milk from the best cows. Lots of sauerkraut, too.

### Lucien Burke — 2017-03-27T21:05:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-211239>

Hey Matthew ~ I've gotta say that your situation sounds a lot like mine. I became ill after a string of infections, stress, bad diet and light environment. I also came down with CFS about a month after a bad wisdom tooth extraction... I had bad cavitations in that area. I'm now basically bed bound with severe neurological symptoms and fatigue. Maybe we can bounce some ideas back and forth and see what works. Here's what I've been focusing on:

\-Cold (When I can, I also have bad body temp issues. But cold helps A LOT when I can tolerate it. Especially for cognitive function. I can tolerate this ice helmet even at my worst: [http://iceyourhead.com/cryohelmet/](http://iceyourhead.com/cryohelmet/))

\-Sun makes me feel a ton better when I make the effort to get outside

\-Intermittent fasting/Ketosis makes me feel better

\-Low Dose Naltrexone (Rx to take before bed)

\-Vielight intranasal LLLT infrared device. Use before bed gives me more energy the next day.

\-Ozone therapy. I bought a generator for home sessions. Rectal and ear insufflations. making ozone water, oil. Could help a lot with lyme and jaw infections.

\-Sauna

Good luck man let me know how it's goin...

### Lucien Burke — 2017-03-27T21:07:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-211240>

One more thing...

try NT Factor in conjunction with eating a lot of DHA to heal cell membranes and replace your lipids. I've just started to use it recently.

[https://www.amazon.com/Nutricology-...UTF8&qid=1490638066&sr=1-7&keywords=NT+factor](https://www.amazon.com/Nutricology-Energylipids-Powder-150-Gram/dp/B00908XN20/ref=sr_1_7_s_it?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1490638066&sr=1-7&keywords=NT+factor)

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-28T18:22:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-211300>

> [Lucien Burke said:](/goto/post?id=211240)
>
> One more thing...
>
> try NT Factor in conjunction with eating a lot of DHA to heal cell membranes and replace your lipids. I've just started to use it recently.
>
> [https://www.amazon.com/Nutricology-Energylipids-Powder-150-Gram/dp/B00908XN20/ref=sr\_1\_7\_s\_it?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1490638066&sr=1-7&keywords=NT factor](https://www.amazon.com/Nutricology-Energylipids-Powder-150-Gram/dp/B00908XN20/ref=sr_1_7_s_it?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1490638066&sr=1-7&keywords=NT+factor)

Not cool that you're so sick, but cool that I'm not the only one. Yes, let's work together, man!

I'm seeing small improvements from managing my light environment, taking an SN-2 algae DHA supplement from MitoLife, CBD emulsified tonic from Omica Organics, eating ketogenically and with an eye to Leptin, taking loads of oil of oregano (without this I think I'd be dead), doing red-light sauna therapy, getting as much sun as possible (unfortunately I live in MN, but the next 5 months are a good time of year for sun here), and drinking lots of good water. I'm doing some other things, as you'll find in the thread here, but these are the things I know help me.

I've heard of people taking Low-Dose Naltrexone for this, but I was resistant to trying it. What do you feel it helps you with? I'd rather never take another long-term prescription for as long as I live, if I can help it, BUT I'm not stupid if something will help me a lot.

By ozone, do you just mean simply a box that makes O3? Because I have one in my room. It's a dual air-purifier/ionizer and ozonator, but I don't use the ozonator right now because I heard from a few sources that it increases free radicals or something. . . Now, full disclosure, with everything else I'm trying to do for myself, I haven't fully looked into that claim. Is it something you know helps you, or are you just doing it because you've been told it's a good idea?

I had not heard of NT Factor, but I was looking for something much like this, so thank you! Vielight I have heard of. . . can't purchase yet since I am still paying off the Magnetico. But I can tell you that the Magnetico has helped a lot. What seems to help me the most have been Kruse's big three (light, water, and magnetism, especially 1 and 3) + mind and emotions. (See next paragraph.)

Also maybe of use to you: brain retraining. I did the Lightning Process seminars (convinced one of them to come to my house because I was too sick to go anywhere) and although I didn't experience any improvement with it initially, it was part of a larger program of mind-body work I've been doing for over a year now. I don't necessarily agree with the New-Age community's somewhat naive belief that you can do anything just by the power of your mind, but I do think it's often overlooked as a factor in healing. I've also been doing EMDR therapy to neutralize triggers in my nervous system, lots of deep breathing, visualization, looking myself directly in the eyes in the mirror and saying "I love you" (and meaning it and feeling it). I even go so far as to teach myself to think like a healthy person. . . you know, planning what I'm going to do in the next few months and years with my newfound health, minimizing how much I speak about illness outside of contexts where I need to, teaching myself to make jokes about things that are hurting. I do what I need to do -- tests, supplements, rest, communicating what I need, whenever these things are practically required -- but I keep seeing wellness too. Don't get me wrong, I'm still spending a lot of my time and energy on healing, but with the right mindset, I like to think.

### Lucien Burke — 2017-03-29T20:39:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-211341>

LDN modulates your immune system, improves circadian rhythm, and reduces inflammation by boosting endorphins and enkephalins. It's been proven useful in cancer and various auto-immune conditions. A lot of times it's used for chronic pain but its also been observed to work with fatigue. It's definitely worth a shot, especially if you have a high serum sed. rate.

Ozone is an oxidative therapy that yes, does increase free radicals in the short term but is a hormetic therapy that increases the bodies production of endogenous anti-oxidants like glutathione peroxidase and SOD. It also kills or inactive any infectious issue you could possible have. It also increases NAD+ and improves oxygenation of tissues.

You can look into generators and accessories for home therapy here: [http://www.promolife.com](http://www.promolife.com)

As Jansz mentions often... I think and feel that a massive root of this disease is issues with fatty acid composition of tissues and dehydration... So yeah DHA and NT Factor are huge.
Along with Ketosis I'm also gonna try to implement more intermittent fasting to up regulate autophagy and mitophagy. (Cellular cleanup)

@[Lucien Burke](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19548/)

[http://ntfactor.com/#&panel1-2](http://ntfactor.com/#&panel1-2)
NTFactor® is:

-   **a proprietary formulation of phospholipids** (pronounced "fos-fo-lipids"), which are special fats that are the key building blocks of the membranes of cells. These membranes not only protect each cell from the outside world, but they also hold receptors that enable each cell to communicate with the rest of the human organism and function in a coordinated, healthy way.
-   Damaged membranes interfere with absorption of nutrients and energy production and lead to premature aging and disease.
-   Cell membranes are damaged by free radicals, harmful molecules that are a by-product of life and a result of exposure to polluted air and other toxins, stress, smoking, being overweight and eating poor quality, refined food. Antioxidants neutralize free radicals but cannot repair damage that has already been done, and neither can other supplements. NTFactor® is specially formulated to do the job.
    **\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[**

I wonder if you have some more information on NTFactor?
NTFactor is described in just about the way we would like to hear it.
The problem is: does it really delivers on that promise.
Details are missing (covered by the word "proprietary").

I am getting most of my information on this topic from what Patricia & Ed Kane post, mostly on their web sites.
[http://www.bodybio.com/](http://www.bodybio.com/)
[http://bodybio-wellness.com/](http://bodybio-wellness.com/)

Among other things, ED discusses imposters and how to figure them out.
Since NTFactor does not give much information, there is no way to figure it that way.

It is about eating XXX and figuring out a way to deliver XXX to cell membrane.

The word "Phospholipid" have been cannibalized and and turned in such a way that lecithin is called phospholipid.

The shorter version is that what BodyBio produces and call PC is a product that have phospholipids in form of liposomes. Those liposomes are not digested and are water friendly.

Lecithin, even if it was about same product, it really is not.
It is dissolved in fat and it is digestible.

What is NTFactor I do not know. Do you?

BodyBio PC (Phosphatidylcholine) 300 SoftGels (1300mg)
[http://www.bodybio.com/storeproduct357.aspx](http://www.bodybio.com/storeproduct357.aspx)

[http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-Phosphatidylcholine-NC.pdf](http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-Phosphatidylcholine-NC.pdf)

As far as I can tell (Allergy Research Group) and (Nutricology) is the same company.
Both names have excellent products, I am only talking about lack of information.
\----------
Since you have used nice sounding words:
"good quality marine and grass fed omega 3's"
I assume
that you are not familiar with work of Patricia Kane and her husband Ed Kane
I suggest that you read all or most of their (short)PDF's
[http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory129.aspx](http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory129.aspx)
or at least the one on PC.
[http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-Phosphatidylcholine-NC.pdf](http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-Phosphatidylcholine-NC.pdf)
and then buy their book ($35.70):
[http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory126.aspx](http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory126.aspx)
To make it short
there is nothing good or bad about Omega3 or Omega6 or any other fatty acids.
We need all of them, in right proportions.
The problem
that I have when I see emphasis made on Omega3
is that
excess of Omega3 coincides with lots of (popular) illnesses that people have this days.
And it happens
that when tested many of those problems coincide with high omega3.
So,
I think that it is good idea to test and find out where one stands.

....
(popular) illnesses
Lyme
Multiple Sclerosis
Fibromyalgia
Autism
Parkinsons
ALS
PDD, pervasive developmental disorder
Chronic Fatigue Immune Dysfunction (CFIDS) also known as Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (CFS) and Myalgic Encephalomyelitis (ME)

number or other

When you read one of the PDF's I mention in post above,
it is really about lecithin

one is ordinary cheap, natural, lecithin
the other is ordered into liposomes (and cost more)

The fact that both may originate from soy is not important,
Or rather not important for the ordered kind.

.............................
One big assumption of the liposomal one is that it will be delivered to cell membranes in tact and then used in repairing cell membranes.

The other lecithin (the natural type) will be digested, taken apart, will cease being phospholipid.
Young healthy body could still pick up the pieces and make phospholipid out of them, but in this discussions we are concerned with sick people.

Kane protocol = PKProtocol
It is described in the book I posted above.
That protocol runs to blood tests.
BodyBio Wellness Report
and
BodyBio Fatty Acid Report

[http://bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html](http://bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html)

Wellness can be done at Labcorp
Fatty Acids must be done at John Hopkins

Both tests are post-processed at BodyBio
Samples of those post-processed tests are here:
[http://bodybio-wellness.com/report-blood-test.html](http://bodybio-wellness.com/report-blood-test.html)
[http://bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html](http://bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html)
\------------------------------------------------

In this conversation it is assumed that the NTFactor would be used instead of PC (from BodyBio).

As far as I can tell, need for initial PC use and further dossing decisions are evaluated based on this test:

Creatine Kinase (CK), Total Plus Isoenzymes Test Number: 002154 CPT: 82550 (total); 82552 (isoenzymes)
I included that test here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4)

PC, liquid or in pills is oral.
In beginning, to speed up the process intravenous phosphatidylcholine is usually used.
That IV one, is phosphatidylcholine only, while PC contains also other phospholipids.

There is more to PKProtocol then just use of PC.
\----------------
As far as I can tell, most peoples on that protocol have already massive amounts of omega3.

Omega3 is strong, when present it suppresses Omega6.
It is not the same when Omega6 is in excess.

......

> [Lucien Burke said:](/goto/post?id=211400)
>
> I also thought that the emphasis was supposed to be on DHA, and that the ratio of 6 to 3 was supposed to be 4:1

Yes, this ratio or similar ratios are quoted.
Usually that ratio is not really defined.
Those who read it, assume, that it is ratio O6 to O3, and that is bad assumption.
Besides
omega3 and omeg6 is not one component in each class.
Each class consists of many different fatty acids.
If you take those that are analyzed you got four in O3 and 6 in O6 (see picture below).
Then you could have zero in all but one in each class and you still may be fine if the ratio is right. (I doubt that it is actually the case, just opposite).

But wait @[matthewsb77](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19899/) test shows that his ALA is = zero.
For the matthew's sake, I hope that it is not true.

The best that I was able to figure about (O6/O3) (4:1) ratio is that it is advisable to have a diet that have
four parts of (LA) for each one part of (ALA)
That is stated in one of those PDF's.
Yehuda figured it out.

I called the lab that did that test. Apparently, it's not the test that's a problem. It just means that it was below the limit detectable. ">=0.09 wt%." Doesn't mean it's zero, but it means that it's way too low. I'm not really sure what if anything can be done about that. I'm wondering if there's possibly a gene issue there, since I'm also mildly allergic to flax.

I checked in with the lab about the ratio, too, and the ratio on my tests is: **5.2-to-1 O6 to O3** which the lab claims is relatively healthy (I know they're using the rest of the "healthy" population as a guideline). The overall number (%) for Omega3 was 7.0 and the value for Omega6 was 36.1. According to what I'm reading online, Omega 6s are typically supposed to be higher, and a good ratio is 4:1 Omega 6 to Omega 3. Everything I'm reading is saying the Omega 6s are often a bit higher and that's fine as long as it's not too high.

[I'm pretty sure Kruse used something from this guy's blog in his book, and he's saying in this post that the ratio might not even matter much. Providing it's not from vegetable that the O6 is coming. http://www.marksdailyapple.com/why-the-omega-3omega-6-ratio-may-not-matter-after-all/](http://www.marksdailyapple.com/why-the-omega-3omega-6-ratio-may-not-matter-after-all/)

Can't the body use another Omega 3 -- such as DHA -- that could be overly supplemented to sort of do the job of low or nonexistent ALA?

Like does it just reattach to a different enzyme? Pardon my - gaping hole - ignorance This is also why giving someone zinc might be a really bad idea...

# Thread: Helen Hall's journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/helen-halls-journal.19274/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Helen Hall — 2017-03-25T06:33:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/helen-halls-journal.19274/post-211043>

I'm 45 yrs old female.
I have been diagnosed pre-diabetic and PCOS since at least 2009. I'm of Chinese ethnicity living in LA and have stopped eating rice all together except maybe 6 times a year for at least 4 years as an attempt to cut carb. I also eat virtually no bread or potatoes. But still pre-diabetic and didn't know what to do. Last March I hired a building biologist (Oram Miller) who told me to install a kill switch, which I did. I really thought that would help with my fasting glucose b/c I heard Dave Stetzer said many diabetic who use his filter find they don't need as much insulin, and Oram told me when you have no electricity running on your wires, you don't have dirty electricity either. But last month my fasting glucose was 98mg/DL. I do yoga 3-4 times a week and do mid day sun bath in my backyard as much as possible, usually 1-2 times a week. I do wear blue blockers. I've installed incandescent light bulbs and put in these yellow bulbs (called bug light I think) thinking it would filter out the blue light. But I just heard on a webinar where Dr. Kruse said it's not just any yellow color that filters out the blue light, so it's probably uselesss. My mom died of stomach cancer at 68 she also had an auto immune disease called Sjogren's, my grandma died of some obscure lung disease where she only had 10% use of her lungs at age 87. I have twin girls who are almost 7. One of them is very bright and seems normal, the other has been diagnosed with Pervasive Developmental Delay and she constantly turns her head side to side all day, but she's not diagnosed autistic b/c she's socialable. Both of my kids had sensory integration issue when they were 2, but the one with PDD still has a bit. I sleep well now, but prior to kids I had a lot of trouble falling asleep. My dad often suffers from insomnia. I work as a freelance interpreter. I almost never use a laptop, my iPad is on nightshift mode (yellow screen) all day, I don't have wifi in my house anymore and no cordless phone. My ipad is on wired connection. The neighbors wifi do come thru. I've put aluminum foil in my kids and my bedroom, but sad to report it didn't work as well as I had hoped. My accoustimeter measured .13/Vm prior to putting foil and after foiling is .07/Vm. I was hoping for even more dramatic decrease. Btw, I found cornet meter isn't very accurate or sensitive b/c it almost always measured .0005mW/m2. Michael Neuert suggested I get the Acoustimeter. During the last month I've had 2 episodes of dizziness which I've never experienced before. I went to urgent care and found out I had very low blood pressure 95/50 sometimes the lower number would be around 45. My normal blood pressure is 100/60. Dr tested me for vertigo and told me I don't have that. I had a treadmill stress test (EKG) last week and was told I did well. I have on and off irregular heart beat. Years ago, a very highly esteemed traditional Chinese medicine Dr told me that my irregular heart beat at that particular time it was caused by a virus or something. He gave me herbal medicine and it did go away, but it came back pretty quickly. I don't drink alcohol or have many vices, but I do consider eating and snacking after dinner around 9pm as a big bad vice that I'm having trouble getting rid of, my other vices is going to bed around 10:30-11pm. Btw, I like to offer an advice to all interested in keeping cell phone on airplane mode as much as possible. Get a google voice account (it's free). Set up your google voice so that it forwards all calls to both your home and cell. Stop giving out your cell phone and only give out your google voice number. This way when you are at home you can turn your cell off and all calls can ring on your home phone. This works well with Verizon and att, but I don't think it worked well with sprint 2 years ago. I think you can even port your current cell number to google voice so you don't have to tell everyone your new google voice number, but I haven't tried it.

### JanSz — 2017-03-25T15:48:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/helen-halls-journal.19274/post-211059>

Consider to look at my idea of:
**Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.**
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)
\--------
Day/night are distinguished by presence or absence of 460-480nm light.
Red light (rather than yellow) for the night time.
When getting off bed announce day to your pineal gland by looking in direction of sun or bright light (ExoTerra Solar Glow 160W).
Keep wakeup time constant.
Variations cause jet lag.
Easy on blue-blockers, make sure that you get enough UV.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-176178](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-176178)
\---------
Worry less about your fasting glucose level,
instead
work on your fasting insulin level, goal=~2-3uU/mL
if fasting insulin gets too high it is impossible to use body fat for energy and therefore weight gain becomes inevitable.
\----------
Eating seaweed as a source of iodine is good practice.
But;
(this days) we need large excess of iodine/potassium iodide
It is not only about thyroid hormones, Hashimoto and some other AI diseases
but also
detoxification of halides, fibrocystic breast, uterine fibroids, etc
iodine for thyroid--->50
iodine for the rest of the body--->1500
selenium+(iodine+potassium iodide)

1/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)

1 dropperfull/day=40mg/day=16drops

\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
I'm 45 yrs old female.
I have been diagnosed pre-diabetic and PCOS since at least 2009.

I have twin girls who are almost 7. One of them is very bright and seems normal, the other has been diagnosed with Pervasive Developmental Delay and she constantly turns her head side to side all day, but she's not diagnosed autistic b/c she's socialable. Both of my kids had sensory integration issue when they were 2, but the one with PDD still has a bit.
+++++++++
Omega3 and Omega6 should not be over or under-estimated
they must be JUST right (or else).
+++++++++
To support my health, I use tests to figure out my next move. Most posted here:

# Thread: unravelling pete
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unravelling-pete.19351/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### peter robinson — 2017-04-16T17:19:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unravelling-pete.19351/post-212401>

So i will start a little from childhood.

Severe ocd and depression and inability to process information from school...high achiever in sport. Lost interest though at 16 think i was very depressed cause of my environment.

Went to uni and smoked weed in first year every day...then started to have panic attacks and wouldn't leave me room. Parents came and got me and I was admitted to a pysch ward, polydrugged on 4 meds and give 12 rounds of ect and kept there for 6 months.

Got back to uni 6 months later worse than i left and on all the drugs which i came off most apart from the AD. This all had led to fatigue most of the time. Moved to London< UK - started working and then became depressed again wasn't able to complete tasks most of the time or meet deadlines. OCD came back with a furry ..went on medication rollcoaster for a decade- pure hell off this and that.....

About 12 years ago came off all my meds felt for the first time ME....then was playing football one saturday morning and when i came off the pitch i could feel my face burning was like acid...went home and my templed and cheek were burnt deep down..I started to panic and looked for a reason and it got worse, i stayed indoors for 2 years and looked for every reason....tried everything just made me sicker...then i started to go a little bit mad from being confined to a room and isolation with face burning all the time. i admitted myself to private hospital and they polydrugged me, this seemed to help the photosensivity as i could start to go out but my mental state was a mess...the photosensivity eventually got less and less as my mental state got worse. MY d was in the sink and i supplemented with that. I became agoraphobc and suicidal all the time especially when i tried to stop the lorazepam not knowing it needed to be tapered ....

Anyone i took the lorazpam off and on over the next decade as i was so fearful of going anywhere to the point i wouldnt leave my neighbourhood and i slowly started to wean off the lexapro...i was getting severe fatigue and muscle pain legs from this - i had become completely exercise intolerant. My sleep was ok.

3 Years ago after working for over a year without break longest i had worked for 10 yrs i started to get anxious etc. i started to take lorazepam every day to get to work. Then i started to get ocd symptoms and fear and terror and adrenaline surges fromt he drug .....stopping it wasn't going to happen as it had got a grip of me. I dosed 4 times a day as i felt i was in tolerance and i was having spasms in me head. My teeth which had always been fine started to hurt from clenching and my gums were receding...i was getting trig nerve pain...

Soon after this i was told i had to leave my house i was in for 10 yrs it was my only sanctuary...i started to get photosensivity on my face and eyes....this got worse and i couldn't use the computer even. I spent 3 months in a darkened room at my friends house till i found my own place as i had been burnt badly by the sun.

Roll on 2015 september a dentist made an adjustment to my splint and I woke up one morning and my neck had collapsed, i knew my jaw had dislocated. I had some adjustments done and got the dentist to try to fix the splint... my whole face started to atrophy ...and i started to get spasms in my gut like the vagus nerve had gone mad. I did some SE work but to no avail- within weeks i was allergic to most foods and had no motility at all...never before did i have gut issues..my breathing had become laboured and i couldnt breath through my nose. I developed acid reflux and mouth breathing - i started to get decay for the first time since a child and would wake up each night several times to drink water. I wasn't sleeping to 2am as i was not tired and the depression came in hard. I started to taper the meds and as my whole cns had been messed this became hellish...6 months ago i stopped tapering as i started to get ataxia, slurred speech , cervical dystonia ...never had any problems with my spine before but the jaw had knocked everything out...i was so malnourished and haven't had any sun for 2 yrs...

Currently my eyes burn in any light as does my face...i have hearing problems cause of jaw , breathing and swallowing and speech problems.. I started to get severe inflammation on my body a few weeks ago and nerve pain and now my legs are all cracking and joints my fingers feel like they have nerve damage... my neck and lumbar and thoriac all seem messed up. I have no bite with my teeth and my head is twisted , with occipotal neuralgia and trig neuralgia...chronic fatigue and depression are prevelant...i never feel full when i eat.

My diet consists of veggies and fish and water and nut butters..i use a lan line for my internet and keep mobile off. I obviously cant get out in the sun but i do put my back out the window every day....i have tried to go to the local park and ground when its dark....

My mother had cancer last year.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Coming Full Circle- Chris' Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-full-circle-chris-journal.19355/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chris James — 2017-04-17T16:44:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-full-circle-chris-journal.19355/post-212444>

Hey everyone! First off, let me tell you a little about myself. I live in the scenic hills and forests of Southern Indiana with my wife and 4 boys. We moved out of Indianapolis about 5 years ago to get away from the hustle and bustle of city life. This is my journey!

I wrote a SUPER long first entry but deleted most of it because it was becoming to much like an autobiography.

I was a fairly healthy kid growing up until I was about 14. At that time I started smoking and then experimenting with drugs. I spent the first few years of my adulthood inside a windowless office surrounded by computers, servers and routers that I'm sure were giving off way to much EMF. I realized within a few years that I wanted a change. I became an ironworker because of the adrenaline rush I felt from being hundreds of feet in the air and the sun on my face. The 15 years I spent in that trade really took a toll on my health. I was breathing in all of those toxic fumes from welding, smoking, drinking and didn't take care of myself AT ALL.

That all changed a few years ago when I was in a bad car wreck. It really was a blessing. Because of some tears in my spleen and a small fracture in the elbow I was off of work for a few months and spent that time reading and diving in to health and "biohacking" on the internet and reconnecting with my family.

I ended up leaving my job and now work from home as a blogger.

Even though I am much healthier now, I am still concerned about all those years of damaging my body and want to raise my children to be as healthy as possible.

My mother just had some cancerous cells removed from her breast in January and is starting to show early signs of dementia. My father also had cancer when I was younger.

I will hopefully be getting my labs done next month so I can see where I am at and come up with a plan to correct any issues that I don't know about.

I eat a pretty clean diet and try to get plenty of sun every day.

I have learned so much already from you guys on this forum and all the information Dr. Kruse has published. Excited to have the ability to learn all I can.

### Chris James — 2017-04-27T00:12:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-full-circle-chris-journal.19355/post-212983>

I have been "becoming the sphinx" every morning at sunrise but have a question. I live in the middle of the forest so am not able to see the sun until a few hours after sunrise. Sometimes I do go to a park close by and welcome the sun that way but it is not possible all the time.

I was wondering if I would get the same benefit a few hours after sunrise since by that time I start to be able to see the sun through the trees.

### Inger — 2017-04-27T10:20:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-full-circle-chris-journal.19355/post-213004>

Welcome Chris!

You will still get the light tho, even if the sun is behind the trees.. Sun would be even better but you do what you can  Maybe you can build a tree hut and climb into it and watch the sunrise from there?

I just was outside an hour sitting naked like a sphinx sucking in the morning sun rays..the birds was singing for me... pure magic.... I thought I was in heaven

### Chris James — 2017-04-28T17:00:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-full-circle-chris-journal.19355/post-213045>

Thanks Inger! Would love to build a platform in the trees!

I know what you're saying. I feel the same way. It's been overcast and rainy here the last few days.  Waiting for the sun to come back.

### caroline — 2017-04-29T04:45:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-full-circle-chris-journal.19355/post-213059>

welcome Chris .....maybe build a tree house for you and your boys? and your wife too!

Do you do movnat?

C/T?

The only other thing she has has issues with is a racing heart every once in a while. She had it checked out when she was around 70 or 75 and the doctor just told her she had a heart of someone half her age.

As for movnat, I was thinking about going to one of their courses this summer but I do some functional/primal movement exercise every week.

# Thread: My Journal - hormonal imbalances and low blood pressure
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sun-sybarite — 2017-04-29T10:15:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-213063>

I'm a 39 yr old woman living in south India ( Latitude: 12.9716° N, altitude:920 m). I am lucky for the good health I have , I love my body and try taking good care of it because it does its best for me.The things in this blog resonate with me and I also like some of the technical stuff bcos I'm a bit of a science geek. It has also helped my myopia.
At the moment I have two problems that I am grappling with:

**Hormonal Imbalance:
**
From childhood I have faced debilitating tonsilitis infections almost everymonth that took away a large part of my energy and I was a runt. Didn't have much energy for life or sports. I remember my teacher called me lazy, but I wasn't I did the best I could but at times couldn't help feeling apathetic.
At 16 I developed PCOS - fatigue, acanthosis nigricans,hair falling in clumps, weight gain, irregular cycles etc. Ayurveda controlled this for a while. My pregnancy was aided by fertility treatments but after that I stopped treating my PCOS as there seemed no permanent cure.
A few years back (maybe due to working in a night shift) my hormones began to go crazy. I was amenorrhic for 18 months, my thyroid was low, I ballooned in weight which won't go away - it seems like low DHEA and HGH from the symptoms I understand - hair falling in clumps till I am almost bald, extreme fatigue(like tired and bed ridden all day), terrible memory, confused thinking, sagging flabby skin, even if I get sun for a day the sunburn takes months vanish ,excessive sweating, thirst which just won't go away.

Worse when I exercise, my bones and joints(knee, lower back ) hurt, so beyond gentle walks I don't do much. my bones also feel weak and easily breakable. Maybe osteoporosis, which a lot of women in my family have.

With the help of Ayurveda Panchakarma - and changing to a job with proper timings - I was able to bring my thyroid and my cycle back to normal - this was over a period of 18-20 months. But my other hormones it is not easy to find a doctor for this here. Endocrinologists only look at diabetes or thyroid or serious diseases if anything else they send women patients to gynacologists. All my gyno said ( after a scan) that I still had PCOS and she told me to check my Vit D levels. She prescribed me D chriro and myo inositol. She wouldn't even take me seriously when I asked about DHEA and HGH.
At the gyno clinic they seem more focused towards fertility treatments as that is quite a lucrative business these days. I am looking for a place here where they check the complete panel of hormones for women, maybe I will have good news soon.

My lab test results:
Haemoglobin:12.8 (Normal Range(NR): 11-16)
T3:0.82 (NR:0.60-1.81)
T4:8.90 (NR:5.01-12.45)
TSH:3.86 (NR:0.35-5.50)
Serum calcium:9.00 (NR: 8-10.5)
Fasting blood sugar:87 mg/dL ( NR:70-100)
Cholestrol total:156 (NR:<200)
Triglycerides:96 (NR:<150)
HDL :46 (NR:>50)
LDL:90.80 (NR:<100)
VLDL :19.20 (< 30)
Ejection Fraction :60 %

**Low Blood Pressure**:

Another problem I have developed in past 2 yrs is low blood pressure/fast heart rate. I don't know from where this came. Most times my BP is 90/60 and the pulse was 85 yesterday.On the net some articles say this can be the beginning of autoimmune disorders which is a bit confusing and scary. I have tried detoxing with sodium thiosulphate, methylene blue, homeopathy, maca root. On these supplements/meds, my diastolic BP gets pushed up artificially - it goes upto 100-87 or 98-85 - and gives me a headache and nausea.

It is so strange that only the diastolic goes up but the systolic remains unchanged. Has anyone here faced this? Or even know why? I read here somewhere that the diastolic is the pressure the blood pushing against the walls of the vessels. But then what is the systolic?

Low blood pressure also interferes with other medications. Like I tried evening primrose oil for my horrible hairfall to help with the DHT( bcos of the PCOS) but I got palpitations, chest pain.sweatung. I checked it out at the cardiologist. They checked my calcium, troponin, did a heart scan, ECG and everything was okay. The doctor said that there were minor abnormalities in the ECG and to visit after few months but after 6 months it was fine at the next checkup. So i believe it is not my heart but something else.

Doctors here don't take low BP seriously. They just told me to have regular meals and lots of salt. But I want to know the reason for this, blood pressure seems a very mysterious subject. Also when I sit in the sun now, I feel worse, my BP gets even more low and I get a headache all day. So I can't be in anything except very early morning sun and not long.

The low BP makes the fatigue because of my hormones worse. I used to sleep for 9-12 hrs( love my sleep!!) but now only 7 hours and wake up with palpitations. I used to fast one day a month but now get palpitations even if I miss a meal and pain in the chest area. Chest muscles feel weak and not able to life heavy stuff, esp. with left arm. Confusion in thinking.
Only thing which makes me better is vitamin C. I take it as sodium ascorabate, The more I take the better I feel. I don't take any other medication.

I've stopped using my mobile phone, wifi is off at night. There is population and technological density here but I don't watch TV and I want to improve my eyesight and have( see my [post](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/) on this). I eat healthy- lots of steamed veggies, low quantity of carbs - though I can't seem to burn fat - maybe bcos of low DHEA, Progesterone due to the PCOS. I am a vegetarian, but after reading Dr.K's stuff do try to eat fish once a week, and eggs too. I am trying to quit vegetable oils and switch to coconut oil - its easily available here and fresh too - but get chest pain if I take too much coconut oil and put on even more weight. Again maybe problem burning fats?

So my next step is to find a good doctor to deal with the hormonal imbalance. The myo/D-chiro-inositol the gyno gave for PCOS caused me severe gastritis and burning sensation in my food pipe for days which was unbearable. And I need to get my other hormones tested as well.

As for the low BP, I am all out of ideas. Maybe once my hormones are in balance I can try for ketogenic diet. I have read of others trying it with some success and even having fun with it. It might help my poor confused brain.
If anyone else has faced unexplainable low blood pressure like this - or would like to share anything that might help or that is interesting - I would love to hear their thoughts/their journey/their story!

How densely populated are we talking here? A city of thousands, millions? Is there nature close by where you can't get any phone signal?

Do you use artificial light after sunset? And where is your bedroom located, on ground floor? It's smart that you changed your job to proper timings, shift work is always sub-optimal.

> I eat healthy- lots of steamed veggies, low quantity of carbs - though I can't seem to burn fat - maybe bcos of low DHEA, Progesterone due to the PCOS. Again maybe problem burning fats?

Coconut oil is a much better choice than vegetable oils. Especially in summer, for other foods the general rules are: it has to be local and in season. Curcumin is a good addition. Yes it would be wise to add fish and perhaps some other animal products if you're up for it.

What type of water do you drink? Do you CT in some way? Any grounding?

**Sybarite** definition, person devoted to luxury and pleasure.

@[Sun-sybarite](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20040/)
Be consistent with your name.
Use lots of sun.
Currently you may be out of touch with sun.
So, you have to start slowly.
It takes about a month of incremental exposure to skin to develop proper skin callus.
Post your location or at least latitude.
Anyhow, get sunlight within two hours before and after solar noon.
Make sure to expose max amounts of skin to sunlight, including breasts and genitals.
Eat food that contains sulfur and carotenes.
garlic, onions, leeks
carrots, peppers

Myopia is recent disease, due to technology, due to spending excessive time in front of TV or computer monitors or cell phones

Being vegetarian will be tough. Make sure that you eat eggs.

Get this set of minerals and testing set.
Using it you will be following your test buds to get to proper level.
MTK plus - Liquid Minerals Test Kit

selenium
Lugols

### Sun-sybarite — 2017-05-01T09:09:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-213143>

> [WalterNL said:](/goto/post?id=213086)

>
>
> How densely populated are we talking here? A city of thousands, millions? Is there nature close by where you can't get any phone signal?
