>
> Coconut oil is a much better choice than vegetable oils. Especially in summer, for other foods the general rules are: it has to be local and in season. Curcumin is a good addition. Yes it would be wise to add fish and perhaps some other animal products if you're up for it.
>
> What type of water do you drink? Do you CT in some way? Any grounding?

@[WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/) Thank you! This is a city of millions but residential parks do offer some breathing space. A few times in a week. I try to go larger parks which are not surrounded by establishments which have EMF. I will try and see if there are any completely signal free places close to nature that is easily accesible - something that you can go at least weekly. That is a good idea thanks!

I live in an apartment , its located on the third floor. I also have a roof top garden where I get sun everyday. I ground everyday especially after spending time on the computer(for the job)- I feel like I was a battery it just sucked the energy from. I ground for an hour and feel human again, I could do it all day and not eat anything! Even if I'm hungry if I ground the hunger goes away, its like the earth feeds my whole body. Btw, I lie down on the ground wearing minimal clothing - just keeping my feet on the ground does nothing for me. Since I can't be in the sun much( as it makes me dizzy and my BP lower) I do earthing more nowadays.

For some reason I never could bear white lights on my skin, even before I read Dr.Kruse's blog. So most of my lights are orange/yellow lights(LED, tubelights) and the remaining I plan to switch to yellow too. In my home after 7pm we use only ambient lights, so no problem. Only exception is my school going kid who needs strong white light for studying - I don't know what to do for her. She also spends so much time on the computer, I am getting her blue blocking glasses.

Unfortunately since I have prescription glasses with high power, I am not able to wear blue blockers.

Indians use turmeric all the time, every meal 3/4 times a day, so its all good. And yes, definitely I eat only the local/seasonal produce, its more accesible and fresher. I eat an egg everyday bcos its a good source of vitamins and someone here also said of glutathione. I like it now and even crave it(so weird!). I will think about other animal products maybe chicken. I was thinking of gelatin because I hear its good for the bones.

I also want to do something for my muscles , they are so weak, even sitting up straight for more than a half hour make my back muscles hurt something fierce. I am trying to do exercises with small weights and they help. But I wonder if this is normal to have such weak muscles...I can't lift anything too heavy as well. Looking back to my teens , when compared to my peers I always seem to have had less stamina, able to lift lesser weights.....wonder why.

As for water I boil it and put a small leaf of holy basil it it, let it cool down and drink it - I like it this way. Some Indians drink it this way in copper vessels, but I don't use copper. I was also thinking about heating the water in the sun....

For CT, I know Dr.K said that pathway is there for all mammals but IDK, I've never thought about doing it. If you have low hormones, like low progesterone, will CT work for you?

I went and checked out the CT page and maybe I could try the face dunking for 2 weeks, its hot enough here and it might help my sleep!

Thanks again for the welcome and your time @WalterNL

### WalterNL — 2017-05-01T11:33:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-213145>

Well you're looking in the right direction. That city will be a problem though, within an appartment building is usually impossible to get away from nnEMF, and cities have a lot of such buildings. If you can't get well even while following all protocols on this site, the problem is most likely the city. If you can afford it there are magnetic pads to improve sleep, your body has to experience a magnetic field at night for optimal sleep.

The water you use is tap water? If so, do you know if it's clean enough i.e. doesn't contain fluoride? It's important to drink water with the least amount of fluoride. The water should be cold too. You can install f.lux on your computer to limit blue light exposure. Face dunks are fine to start CT, getting cold helps me sleep much better. Yes gelatin is good stuff, you can make bone broths from animal or fish bones.

There are also blue bockers (from UVEX) you can wear over your normal glasses.

These pages have more general tips: [https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/) [https://www.jackkruse.com/paleo-rx-easy-start-guide/](https://www.jackkruse.com/paleo-rx-easy-start-guide/) [https://www.jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-6-mitochondrial-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-6-mitochondrial-rx/)

### Sun-sybarite — 2017-05-03T18:20:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-213301>

Monday was a good start to the week. I tried out a fish stew(mackerel) recipe and it came out well. I also did face dunking; it was refreshing in the heat. It was for 30 intervals of 30 secs each after dinner. When I woke the next day I had slept for 9 and a half hours! I was so happy bcos - me and my sleep, we're kind of attached to each other,lol. Due to my BP I hadn't been able to sleep for longer than 7 hrs for over a year and I had missed sleeping longer. Also my skin felt good - soft and firm.
I guess I felt good, so I went out midday, got a lot of hot Indian sun, for around 12noon to 3 pm. I was able to handle it. But after work was too tired to do the facedunking, earthed maybe 20 mins. Today I woke up with a debilitating headache, nausea- couldn't keep even a mouthful of water down. The pounding in my head was unbearable and seemed to echo my pulse so I checked my BP and it was high 130-90 and over the hour it rose to 140-97! I was surprised, I had never had high BP before, it was usually ranged around 90/60 to 100/70.
I think my blood pressure centre in my brain( or wherever) is going haywire. I went to my GP and she gave me something for high BP. I slept in a dark room after taking that and only now feel human. Have no idea what brought that on. But I still want to do face dunking today it feels soothing and refreshing for the mild headache I still have.

Hi @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/), thanks for all the links, I was poring over the steroid pathway pdf and trying to understand it, some of it made sense but a lot went over my head unfortunately.

Thanks also for all the useful advice, yes I will try to take lots of sun - already get morning sun, but noon sun?? Here its like 38 Celsius - 100 Fahrenheit - and it only gets hotter after noon. I can try and get some in say at 10 am. Yes, I do get sulphur and beta carotene foods. Right now I eat eggs with very orange yolks - so maybe thats beta carotene and cholestrol? Also I crave onions. People here also drink Aloe Vera juice -say 15 ml a - day for some months of the year. Its supposed to have lots of MSM(sulphur) I think. When I drink it my skin looks so good! Its available in plenty here and I also grow it. Maybe I will try that also.

It makes sense Myopia is a recent disease. Sometimes I wondered what early man did when he was shortsighted before glasses were invented, lol, but now I know this was a disease of lack of sunlight in the eye. When I developed myopia , it was more from less sunlight in eye in combination with reading lots of (paper) books with fine print. It was the lack of sun plus eyestrain that did me in, IMO.

JanSZ, I have posted the normal ranges( as well as my latitude) with my labs.I'm sorry I didn't post the normal ranges earlier. At least 3 doctors had a look at my thyroid tests and confirmed my thyroids were normal. Can you please have a look again, with the ranges and let me know if you still think they're below par? Also the same thing for cholestrol.

It is very difficult to get bio identical hormones here without a prescription. They're usually prescribed for menopausal women. I have been talking to a doctor attached to a compounding pharmacy here and she has asked me to send all my reports which I will have to do tomorrow. They have hormone panel tests and bioidentical hormones, so hopefully something will work out.

With the way I'm ballooning - I look obese though I weigh like 60 kg - it would be grossly unfair if my cholestrol was too low,lol! Just my luck - instead of using it for my good hormones, my body is using it to make visceral fat, or whatever aargh . I will ask my doctor about the fatty acids test and the mineral tests too @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/). Tests here are available and affordable, its only that all of them need a doctor to prescribe them for you. There are exceptions like [preventative](https://www.thyrocare.com/wellness/Emailer/emailer1.aspx?enc=bYH3u1NA92Gem1tKShNZqcZzVF7430XUwMbz5j0QMRX8LNcGxwhUG/fCPsE2IA4tM2VAvj0jXkCL7kiibqjXTQ====GxwhUG/fCPsE2IA4tM2V&enc2=Rxhjgsdhf%20%20&val=UFVCTElD) health checkups, but they're tests which are prepackaged together and I don't think you can add on to those.

Yes, I read that about living near the equator and not needing that much DHA.Thats good because I wasn't having much luck convincing my kid to try fish, she didn't seem that much enamored with the smell! So can I give her those codliver capsules then? My HDL is low( it appears that a lot of women with PCOS have low HDL) but the truth is we eat a lot of Omega 6 oils and practically no Omega3/9. Some people with heart conditions do try and include flaxseed but that is not DHA, I think.

Thanks again for all the detailed suggestions and your time; you've given me a lot to think about/work on!

### JanSz — 2017-05-04T15:02:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-213354>

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-2#post-113774](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-2#post-113774)

**Jack Kruse's way to optimize labs.**

[http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/)

**What Are The Optimizing Labs?**
**August 26,** **2011** By [Jack Kruse](http://jackkruse.com/author/JackKruse/) [Comments (100)](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/#idc-container)

-   [Facebook26](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/?share=facebook&nb=1)
-   [Twitter3](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/?share=twitter&nb=1)
-   [LinkedIn](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/?share=linkedin&nb=1)
-   [Google](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/?share=google-plus-1&nb=1)
-   [More](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/#)

  [](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/)

**Readers Summary**

1.  What labs might I consider with my healthcare provider?
2.  How do I start?
3.  Is the quantified self platform of testing a commitment?
4.  How long should it take before I become rockstarish?
5.  The secret sauce is not the lab data but in how they are interpreted!

When you finally decide to take total control of you life and optimize yourself I always suggest testing. Many of you have bombarded my email and my [twitter account](https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse) for a blog to a list of those labs. Well, today’s post is for you. I was resistant to do this because I felt having the list of labs is a waste of time if you don’t have a physician who can decipher what they all mean for you. 7 years ago, I could not tell you what this group of labs meant at all to your optimal health. I learned it by reading and going to classes to optimize myself. After much reflection I have decided to give you what you asked for. I am fortunate because many of the PCP’s I work with understand these tests well. I may also update it as I think it needs to be updated over time.

The first set of labs are what I call the core lab set for optimization. There are other panels I add to the core lab based upon the history, physical, and the food logs I will have patients give me. This blog is meant to be a resource for you to refer back to when the need arises. Discuss these with your doctor but don’t assume they will run out to order them because they may not know what all these tests will tell them. That part takes some time. I recommend starting the dialogue with your doctor to assess their willingness to help you. Most of my PCP doctors are awesome “helpers” to their patients with these issues. This requires a lot of work on the doctors part and the patients part. Do not be surprised if this is not covered by insurance. The time required to optimize someone is unreal. I know how long I spent optimizing myself 6 years ago. It was a tedious process but I was not going to give up because I believed my life depended upon it. For most people, 2-3 years you can expect the changes you want. If you are really in tough shape it may take longer but that should not deter you. I love patients who bring big challenges. They are the most appreciative patients I have had in the last 5 years. We can never settle for a C or D when an A is possible.

**Lab Panels**

1.  VO2 Max assessment (cardio pulmonary findings lead to a work up if needed.)
2.  Initial visit DEXA with body comp. Optimization a DEXA scan to include body composition scores.
    F/U visits I use BIA assessments to monitor progress and limit radiation exposure.
3.  Neuro cognitive battery of tests to assess processing speed, response speed, memory , learning, and attention repeated annually.
4.  Chemistry 20
5.  Liver function testing
6.  Serum Iron, TIBC, Serum Ferritin and Iron % Saturation
7.  Lipid panel: VAP and/or NMR analysis
8.  Homocysteine level

9.  Complete CBC with differential and platelet count (sometimes an Ivy bleeding time)
10.  Hormone panel includes (gender specific)……serum and salivary cortisol, Total testosterone, Free testosterone, % Free T, DHT, LH, FSH, Estradiol (E2) high sensitivity, DHEA-S, PSA, IGF-1, Consider based upon history testing levels of progesterone, pregnenolone, melatonin, serum and urine Osmolarity, Prolactin levels
11.  Complete thyroid panel with antibody screening (TPO etc)
12.  Complete Urinalysis (women get beta HCG if they menstruate)
13.  Highly Sensitive CRP (Not a regular CRP)
14.  Complete Vitamin D panel

15.  Omega 6/3 serum test (for severe findings consider tissue assay)
16.  Fasting serum insulin and HbA1c assessment
17.  No panel is complete without an ASI and salivary melatonin level. Read Brain Gut 11 to understand why.

The next set of labs are considered add ons if the patients’ history or physical require it being looked at.

**Cancer history of colon, breast, ovary**

1.  CEA for colon cancer
2.  CA 125 for ovarian cancer
3.  CA 27.29 for breast cancer
4.  AFP blood test
5.  CA125, 15-3, 27.29, 19.9,
6.  BRCA 1 and 2 screen

**Previous heart disease**

1.  Apo A-1 and B
2.  B Natriurietic Factor
3.  LpA status, Lp-PLA2
4.  CIMT
5.  Calcium Index Score

**Bone pathology**

1.  Osteocalcin frozen blood analysis
2.  Se blood test
3.  RBC blood testing for Mg
4.  Deoxypyridinoline (DPD) Cross Link Urine Test
5.  Parathyroid Hormone assay

**Inflammatory/autoimmune panels**

1.  EBV panel
2.  ANA panel
3.  Complete Cytokine Assay
4.  Candida Antibodies
5.  Helicobacter IgG panel
6.  Hep A, B and C titers
7.  Breakout of Cytokine panel
8.  ESR
9.  RA factor
10.  SLE screen
11.  T Lymphocyte helper suppressor assay
12.  IL 8, IL 1 B or TNF alpha analysis

I also think you need to have a thorough history and physical exam. You need to retest on a quarterly or biannual basis to get feedback on your lifestyle and regimen to get things right over time.

Once this is all correlated, you come up with a customized dietary plan, a medication/supplement plan, and exercise plan, basically a game plan for things you might consider altering for optimal health. It should be a collaboration between you and your doctor and your other health care providers.

This is a very labor intensive task for both patient and doctor. But if you remain persistent and consistent and your relationship with your physician congruent and always focusing in on your health you can reach any goal you have for yourself. It must be adaptable but yet have some core values to it to get you to the promised land. The secret sauce is not the lab list; it is found in the interpretation of those labs and how they correlate with your epigenetic switches. That is what determines how you partition calories and what diet and exercise program are best for your current biology. You should expect this to change over time as you become optimized. Nothing stays they same in biology. I learned this on my own journey and it has proven true in those I have helped re-engineer themselves back to optimal. Enjoy!

The membrane is the outside skin that surrounds every cell,
however, there is much more membrane on the inside of
the cell, actually ten times more, surrounding all the tiny
organelles inside the cell.

**Cholesterol and Arachidonic Acid**
Two Vital
FATS
That Get No Respect

Cholesterol is not only a vital cellular molecule, it is also a large part of us, as it occupies 30-40% of our membrane (Gurr 2002 6). It is the precursor for vital hormones such as the adrenals, our fight or flight hormones, and the gonads, hormones that drive our reproductivemachinery. Cholesterol is important for the metabolism of the fat soluble vitamins, A, D, E and K, and is a precursor for bile acids which manage our fatty acid intake from the gut to the cells. It is intimately involved in regulating membrane fluidity over a wide range of temperatures,as well as creating a strong membrane structure, which,incidentally, equates to a strong overall metabolism. Most individuals with high cholesterol have a strong metabolism--- and they know it.
Very little appears to impact one with **high cholesterol**, like airborne disturbances such as pollen with its potential for allergy, etc. “When present at high concentrations, cholesterol enhances the mechanical strength of the membrane, reduces its permeability, and suppresses the main-phase
transition of the lipid bilayer” (McMullen 19967).

autistic spectrum disorder
cognitive abilities
Cholesterol is an essential element of myelin, the insulating material crucial for nerve function in the brain and central nervous system.
hypo-cholesterolemia is common among tuberculous patients

**How to raise Total Cholesterol?**

****How to raise Total Cholesterol?****

easily follow, and often does.
Similarly, patients with environmental illness almost always have low cholesterol including those with sensitivity to foods, chemicals, or even frequencies, like Wi-fi , which are commonly due to
the instability of the cell membrane. One patient complained that he had developed such a severe sensitivity to Wi-fi frequency that he could not travel or go into many business settings. His lab analyses revealed that he had a very low cholesterol level with a low Total Lipid Content
within his red cells. The patient found relief from his symptoms **after receiving high dosing with**
**IV phosphatidylcholine and a diet high in eggs, butter, wild salmon and balanced essential fatty acid supplementation.”**
**\----**

Wow, JanSz, thank you from the bottom of my heart for caring and taking your time to help me ( and I'm sure many others as well). I am meeting my doctor this week and was feeling a bit down as the consultation costs were high (higher than even the cardiologist) and I had already spent so much on doctors and endoscopies, scans and alternative treatments with no clear answers or solution. But seeing all this and Dr.K's pages I feel totally energised and hopeful that with all the information and guidance here I will be able to make sense and solve the problem.

I am taking down notes so that I can ask the doctor some of these things. Hope the doctor will prove a bit open minded!

Oh and also I paint myself with Iodine tincture everyday( alternating abdomen or chest). It gakes a while to absorb so I don't think I am completely deficient. There is Chlorine in our bathing water plus Iodine is good for PCOS so I do that. i don't think this Lugol's solution is available in India. I will ask in the pharmacy and see. Selenium is available as a part of a multimineral capsule, I also take Shilajit(fulvic acid) and have some orange sea salt in my cucumber juice and have lots of garlic.

### Sun-sybarite — 2017-05-09T00:07:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-213692>

> [WalterNL said:](/goto/post?id=213145)
>
> Well you're looking in the right direction. That city will be a problem though, within an appartment building is usually impossible to get away from nnEMF, and cities have a lot of such buildings. If you can't get well even while following all protocols on this site, the problem is most likely the city. If you can afford it there are magnetic pads to improve sleep, your body has to experience a magnetic field at night for optimal sleep.
>
> The water you use is tap water? If so, do you know if it's clean enough i.e. doesn't contain fluoride? It's important to drink water with the least amount of fluoride. The water should be cold too. You can install f.lux on your computer to limit blue light exposure. Face dunks are fine to start CT, getting cold helps me sleep much better. Yes gelatin is good stuff, you can make bone broths from animal or fish bones.
>
> There are also blue bockers (from UVEX) you can wear over your normal glasses.
>
> These pages have more general tips: [https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/) [https://www.jackkruse.com/paleo-rx-easy-start-guide/](https://www.jackkruse.com/paleo-rx-easy-start-guide/) [https://www.jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-6-mitochondrial-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-6-mitochondrial-rx/)

You are right there are lots of such buildings in the city. Before this I live in detached houses on the ground level but still had many wi-fi around. In this building now for the last few months I've noticed at night all of them switch off their routers so no wifi signal. I don't know why. Its a small apartment building not too overcrowded maybe 20 tenants.

That still leaves cell tower signals though. I have bad cell reception in my room which I always used to get irritated about as I had to crack open the window to make or receive a call. But maybe thats a blessing,lol. I have asked a couple of companies for quotes on anti-EMF window curtains as it seems to me most of the signals comes in through them. Apparently they also sell anti-Radiation bed canopies - suggested for pregnant women - which maybe can consider down the road.

@[WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/), thank you for bringing this to my attention about the water! I think I am what you call late to the party. I researched the water quality here and it appears we have some excess fluoride, nitrates and arsenic in drinking water. Totally shocked, and feel a bit like an ignoramus. I was only worried about the chlorine( as we can smell it) but had no awareness of the other pollutants. It appears clean drinking water is a bit of a myth Reverse Osmosis systems are not cheap, but I am saving up for it, also I have to convince my family, they are not ones for change especially when it doesn't seem urgent, but I care too much for my health. I am also painting myself regularly with iodine which in retrospect is a good thing.

I had bone broth twice this week, tasted so good.
Have installed flux on the desktop, thanks.

I have ordered the amber glasses to wear over my prescription ones and am excited to try it. Hope no one looks at me weird though, not too many people wear 2 glasses at the same time,lol.

I am doing facedunking, maybe on alternate days( somedays this week I was so exhausted I fell asleep abruptly) before bedtime and getting good sleep. Doing regular earthing. Doing exercise with small weights for posture - I have that forward neck posture going on and weak back muscles.

The magnetico sleep pad sounds interesting but maybe too prohibitive for me given the exchange rates and shipping charges. There are magnetic pads available here too but I don't think they're the same as mentioned by Dr.K.

Its also very hot here, I'm craving lots of fruits. I have maybe one meal a day, eggs, and some fruit and juices - mangos, water melon - the rest of the time. Having butter as it will help my cholestrol.

My blood pressure was weird for a couple of days -around 108-87 - had mild headache.

### Beristere — 2022-06-17T14:34:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-313628>

Hormonal problems are often caused by the thyroid gland, which secretes varying amounts of hormones. This problem is more and more common among women, so it is recommended that they give tests at least once a year to check the condition of the thyroid. Low blood pressure is closely related to lifestyle, and of course, it is related to your hormonal instability. Your body cannot interpret these changes, and your heart rate decreases. Patients also face skin problems, which is why [ipl skin rejuvenation](https://aestheticbureau.com.au/ipl-machines) is necessary.

# Thread: Thaddeus Journal Looking to Optimize
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thaddeus-journal-looking-to-optimize.19413/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thaddeus Owen — 2017-05-02T18:11:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thaddeus-journal-looking-to-optimize.19413/post-213238>

Hello Everyone. It's good to be here.
I've been following Jack for many years and finally at the point where I'm ready to engage and get more involved.

I have been biohacking for years, but took the route of getting too technical and moving away from nature. I recently have come back to the concept of getting better by removing things rather than adding.

I engineered a work from home assignment to be able to better manage a healthy workplace. I spent 10 years in Pharmaceutical R&D and now have spent 10 years keeping toxic chemicals out of furniture as a full time job. By removing myself from the plant and R&D lab, I am able to work remotely and hack my lifestyle and environment for health (but still am missing the tribal component of working alone all day).

I run a company at night with a business partner (ErisFit and PrimalHacker) where we blog about biohacking as we learn our way through this stuff. We intend to make it more accessible to others and ensure people understand the basics and the science, which is one huge reason why we're here!!

I have two boys in elementary school, and I'm currently going through a divorce.

I am looking to clean up as much of my environment for health as possible and use this to teach others how to do the same as well as keep my boys healthy in this crazy environment.

I recently ran a bunch of labs and I think my BUN/creatinin is too high and need some help on how to hack this one. I'm 41 years old.

I live in the suburbs in Minnesota. I turn off the wifi and cell phones at night, have wrapped aluminum around the smart meters on my water and natural gas meters.

I get outside each morning for 5-20 minutes and spend another 20 minutes walking at lunch.

I ground myself in bed at night with an earthing mat plugged into the outlet ground.

I eat higher fat and lower carb but not low carb. 80-190g carbs per day from sweet potatoes. Sometimes I eat soaked oatmeal. I do a BP coffee for breakfast with some protein and sometimes plantain pancakes with butter, no sweetener. Seafood 5 times a week from pacific salmon. Oysters once a week.

Here are some of my numbers I ran last month (APril 2017)
LDL-P 1168
LDL-C 112
HDL-C 49
Triglycerides 74
total cholesterol 176
HDL-p 29.2
Small LDL-P 156
LDL Size 21.1
Large VLDL-P 1.2 nmol/L
Small VLDL-P 156
Large HDL-P 4.5
VLDL size 44.6
LDL size 21.1
HDL Size 8.8
LP-IR score 39

sodium 142
potassium 4.0
chloride 104
co2 27
GLucose 97
Calcium 9.9
Bun 19
creatine 1.08
BUN/creat 18
Free T 10.9
T total 775
LpA 7.71
Homocysteine 8.6
HS CRP 0.35
T4 6.5
T3 total 95
Reverse T3 25
Free T4 1.06
Free T3 2.62
Vit D 65.4
cortisol 15.3
DHEA sulfate 178.3
Insulin 4.1
sex hormone binding 64
estradiol 20.0
ferritin 70.0
TSH 2.6

**Health History** -
Anxiety. I have struggled with this since I was 5. I'm now 41. Mostly have hacked this through EFT, coaching and deep work over the last 4-5 years. About 90% better.

High Blood pressure - sometimes 115/74 and sometimes 135/90 and sometimes as high as 145/94... comes down quickly after deep breathing. This has been coming down lately as well per the anxiety work I have done in the last few years.

**Reynauds** \- this is a big one for me. Getting worse the past year. My fingers and toes turn white and go numb when exposed to cold and take hours, literally hours to get blood flow back. C/T makes this a real issue for me

**No surgeries** ever. No medications. Was on Lexapro (anti anxiety, 10mg) for 2 years (10 years ago). No history of disease.

**Mother -** Anxiety and on anti anxiety meds for last 20 years. Reynauds. No other issues or disease. Perfect health otherwise. Low blood pressure. Very active 67 year old woman.

**Grandmother** - died at 90. Had dementia in her late 80s. Died because she did not want to be alive, no joy in life and wanted to die. She had no history of disease or medical issues until dementia

I **sleep** great. Usually 6-7.5 hours a night. 7.5 is my ideal. I fall asleep quickly and sleep well.

Wifi turned off at night. Phone in airplane mode. Blue light blocking glasses used nightly.

I **travel** for work for 5 days a month (sleep in hotels, fly on an airplane for 1 hour there and back). Sometimes add 1 additional trip to CA with 3.5 hours flying time.

**Physical**
I'm in good physical shape, 8% bodyfat, weight lift 2 times a week using strong first techniques (Squat, bench, deadlift, pull ups), practice Qigong, use kettlebells 5 times a week for a 10 minute warm up each day.

I do not do cardio.

I get out in the sun 5-20 minutes at sunrise daily.

I ground outside from May - October (I live in MN and it's cold outside of those months).

I have a Sunlighten Infrared Sauna acquired a few months ago, use it daily in the winter months.

I cannot currently move zip codes, as my ex-wife is not going to leave this place. So I need hacks for the long MN winter to stay optimal up here (perhaps it's heading down to Mexico and FL a couple times in the winter, using my sauna, using the Redjuvenator light at night and C/T by just stepping outdoors for 5 minutes in the winter..Looking for ideas)

**Libido** is decent, but not great. Although I'm going through a divorce right now, so could be related.

**Water**
Reverse osmosis. Been using this for 10 years. Add oceanic ionic trace minerals to water before drinking.
I also drink Mountain Spring water, carbonated, from Whole Foods - about 1 bottle a day.
WHole house water filter was just installed to remove chlorine from shower and bath.
I shower and do not take baths (Perhaps I'm missing out on something not putting myself in water once a day?)

\================================================================
Grounding to ground in power outlet is always questionable.
You may be better off not using that ground or good checking of it.
\----------

When posting any laboratory tests results it is better to scan each page of report, wrap it into PDF file and attach to post.
Retyping, post name, value, units, laboratory range, name of laboratory
I assume that your have
Vit D 65.4ng/mL
That would be not bad, but goal is to have:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
or more.
The problem is that you want that to be made by your own body, using sunlight or UV lamps.
When you see sun in Minnesota for 5 minutes or 20 min/day, that is not going to help.
Sunlight creates vit D,sulfate.
Pills give vit D
two different items.

Your
T total 775 is decent
but
sex hormone binding 64
is too high
that is why your
Free T 10.9
is low
and
Libido is decent, but not great.
Lets assume that it would help when you

get better vit D
homocysteine
and
your temperature under your tongue will be at 98.6F= 37.00C or real close to it --->>(thyroid + cortisol)

Your early morning cortisol =15.3
is not bad, but could be higher.
Better observing of Circadian Rhythm should improve on this.

Your
Free T3 2.62 too low
Reverse T3 25 too high
that
may be due to insufficient
selenium, iodine, potassium iodide
or
you just can't make better thyroid hormones and need to supplement with Natural Thyroid hormones from pigs.

I suggest this first:
1/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)
two weeks latter add
1 dropperfull/day=40mg/day=16drops
Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?psc=1&redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o04\_s00](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o04_s00)

Do that practically forever.
\---------------------------------------
Look over my posts on this thread:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/)

It would help if you could get this two tests:

From LabCorp (script required)

and
[https://www.spectracell.com/order/](https://www.spectracell.com/order/)
Micronutrient Test (MNT)
**$** 390.00

Post complete results here.

# Thread: Leo's journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leos-journey.19659/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Leo cosen — 2017-06-17T16:32:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leos-journey.19659/post-216430>

Hi everyone!

I am so pleased to have joined this forum. I started out with bulletproof diet 6 months ago and soon left it aside getting into Jack Kruse's amazing wealth of experience and understanding.

I am Swiss French born by lake Geneva , I am 27, cadet of a family of 4 children, my grandmother died in her mid'80s and my mother is in her late 50s. Both never had health issues but did and do have thin hair and a lot of skin tags and wrinkles.

I moved to London when I was 19 and have been here for over 8 years now.

**Brief background:**
I was very anxious as a teenager and played computer games (semi-pro) from age 11 till 18.
Drank 2L of ice tea a day
Ate 2x packs of biscuits a day
Smoke 5-10 rollies a day

**Life in the present tense, after having read some of Jack kruse's literature over the past few months, looks like this:**

Energy levels are great along with creativity and motivation to do things.. but also be still and quiet!

Very athletic and in shape (185cm / 67 kgs / 9% body fat (doing 15mn HIIT in park barefoot 3x a week- no more gym).

I work as a yoga, gong sound bath facilitator, meditation teacher and hold retreats,etc so lucky to be able to spent lots of time without artificial light. If I do it's on my iPhone and I make sure I'm outside.

\-I go outside everyday to catch morning sun for up to 2 hours
\-I'm doing cold baths for 10-20mns every night before bed (sleep about 7.5hrs)
\-got a reptile red infrared lamp (250W) which I sometimes use at night but I'm not sure whether it's the right one to use. I also have an LED laser that usually comes with security cameras.
\-I eat full keto during winter and have started adding baked sweet potatoes every other day at night accompanied by grass fed organic raw butter or single cream
\-I eat lots of sardines and mackerel and anchovies and rock oysters
\-2L of still spring water in glass bottles (Highland)
\-I take all the mito supplements advised including shilajit (just started)
\-I eat two Meals a day now (early am usually 7 or 8) and then round 5pm
\-I have earthing sandals / walk barefoot a lot and get about 4-5 hours outside daily. This sadly is made more difficult when it rains in London and it rains a hell of a lot. So summer is usually a relief for me.

\-I also have blue light blocking glasses and a filter on my screen in addition to flux (does it really generate a flicker?)

**The things that I'd like to work on are:
**
Thin coating on my tongue which isn't always there now that ive changed my way of living.
\-Get rid of floaters which I have in both eyes
\-increase my ability to absorb nutrients and water.
\-Regrow my hair in places (I seem to have come "scalp to scalp" with male pattern baldness)

Many thanks for reading and for any tips.

P.S: I'm thinking of moving back to Switzerland to live in the mountains. I also really like the south of Spain where I teach a lot of yoga retreats.

Blessings and mito hugs to all
Leo

### drezy — 2017-06-18T04:15:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leos-journey.19659/post-216463>

Welcome. You're off to a damn good start.

>
> \-I go outside everyday to catch morning sun for up to 2 hours
> \-I'm doing cold baths for 10-20mns every night before bed (sleep about 7.5hrs)
> \-got a reptile red infrared lamp (250W) which I sometimes use at night but I'm not sure whether it's the right one to use. I also have an LED laser that usually comes with security cameras.
> \-I eat full keto during winter and have started adding baked sweet potatoes every other day at night accompanied by grass fed organic raw butter or single cream
> \-I eat lots of sardines and mackerel and anchovies and rock oysters
> \-2L of still spring water in glass bottles (Highland)
> \-I take all the mito supplements advised including shilajit (just started)
> \-I eat two Meals a day now (early am usually 7 or 8) and then round 5pm
> \-I have earthing sandals / walk barefoot a lot and get about 4-5 hours outside daily. This sadly is made more difficult when it rains in London and it rains a hell of a lot. So summer is usually a relief for me.
>
> \-I also have blue light blocking glasses and a filter on my screen in addition to flux (does it really generate a flicker?)
>
> **The things that I'd like to work on are:
> **
> Thin coating on my tongue which isn't always there now that ive changed my way of living.
> \-Get rid of floaters which I have in both eyes
> \-increase my ability to absorb nutrients and water.
> \-Regrow my hair in places (I seem to have come "scalp to scalp" with male pattern baldness)
>
> Many thanks for reading and for any tips.
>
> P.S: I'm thinking of moving back to Switzerland to live in the mountains. I also really like the south of Spain where I teach a lot of yoga retreats.
>
> Blessings and mito hugs to all
> Leo

I have floaters in my eyes (also myopic). What are you doing to target these specifically?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leos-journey.19659/post-216550>

### Mito1 — 2017-06-19T21:34:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leos-journey.19659/post-216562>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=216549)
>
> I have floaters in my eyes (also myopic). What are you doing to target these specifically?

Retinal dopaminergic amacrine (DA) cells synthesize both dopamine and GABA.

If a retinal dopamine neuron can not clear GABA all the time under light, you will see an eye floater all the time when you expose your eyes to light.

The eye floater size depends on how many DA cells get involved.

.

I basically see my floaters every morning when I'm out in AM daylight (not sunlight - Scotland!)

I remember seeing them all the time as a kid growing up... I thought they were kinda cool, just gunk in your eye. I think that's what I was told.

# Thread: Andrew's Optimal Journey Begins
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T16:49:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219628>

Hello and thank you to all for welcoming me into the community. It has come at just the right time in my life where I think I need some help, and intuition has led me here... so I know I'm on the right path now, thank you.

I was advised to start a journal; this is good advice, as I think it will be very beneficial.

A bit of background on myself as requested, you might even be able to offer me some help right off the bat, as there is so much dense information available it's a little overwhelming to know where and how to start.

I'm not currently diagnosed with any specific medical illness or condition, however, I feel uneasy and dissatisfied with my physical and mental health. I'm also at odds emotionally and spiritually with my lifestyle.

I have 3 kids, recently separated from a long term marriage and am in a new relationship which is very supportive, and have inherited a step son, so 4 children - which is great as I love kids and a stable family home life.

I live and work from Manchester in the UK, which is typically quite a built up, densely populated city well known for being quite cold, grey and wet. I have my own business which is a digital agency, so I find the majority of my time is spent online with the computer or using the phone. Although I tend to avoid phone calls off the mobile device, I am inundated with SMS, emails and social media demands. My phone is often in my trouser pocket... Although I enjoy the creative aspects of what I do, overall, I have a lot of anxiety and stress that seems to overwhelm me mentally, and often it feels like I'm trapped on the typical hamster wheel, which I do try to juggle with an active family life with defined boundaries.

My parents are relatively healthy physically, with no known medical issues, though I think I've inherited their self-employed anxiety and stress related tendencies, being brought up in a home where everything was lived for future attainment and goal achievement and not being present or enjoying the NOW, or gratitude for what we already have. My dad's parents died of heart attacks, and on my mum's side, my grandad died of a heart attack but suffered much ill health from a genetic disorder which included muscle atrophy and diabetes. My mum's mum, my grandma, seems to be in good health at 87 and is only slightly over weight. My aunt passed away young from the same genetic disorder as my grandad, was very poorly and had diabetes. My uncle, my mum's brother, has the same genetic disorder and has diabetes and cancer and is obese. I've been tested for the genetic disorder and seem to be clear.

Overall, I'm slim although have battled and struggled with my diet and weight in the past historically. Being teased as a kid for being overweight led me to the gym, and weight training, which had positive and negative mental/body/confidence related associations. However, this path did lead me to become more educated about food and has been very insightful on my journey. I stumbled across Dr Mercola, Tim Ferriss, Dave Asprey and Mark Sissons over the years, and my intuition has strongly pulled me towards a low carbohydrate diet (ever since effortlessly losing weight on the Atkins diet many, many years ago). Something didn't sit well with me on the Atkins diet eating bacon and sausages, however, and I slowly gravitated more towards Tim Ferriss's slow carb or Dave Asprey's Bullet Proof suggestions. Something still didn't sit well with me at a gut feel level.

After my marriage broke down, I found I used food as an environmental, emotional and mental control factor, and became a big fan of intermittent fasting and fasting. So I was eating only one or two meals a day, and wouldn't eat anything that wasn't strictly controllable. This caused conflicts with my family life and being surrounded by 4 kids, who have different parents on the other side, and being brought up with what you would probably term the SAD in general.

Over the past 6 months I've gravitated more to a no grains, or limited grains diet, and I eat a lot of smoked salmon, grass fed meats, grass fed butter, coconut oil, MCT oil and seafood, all of which sits well with my on an intuitive stand point. I do still occasionally consume dairy (pasteurised cheese & cream, not milk) which I know I should either eliminate or switch to raw versions. For convenience, I eat a lot of nut butters, usually trying to stick to things like almond or walnut, but I'm a sucker for the blends and the legumes like peanut and cashew nut. I know I need to eliminate these. I do take around 40gml of Carlsons fish oil a day, supplement with things like Vitamin D, NAC, Magnesium, Iodine and others.

HOWEVER, I started to note things about myself and my life that I would class as _red flag_ signals:

**1)** Whenever I exercised with resistance training I feel completely exhausted and drained for about 72 hours, and ache so much, I started to question the benefits and have eventually ceased all forms of physical exercise. I've been resistance training and bodybuilding since I was 17, and had a stint of steroid use in my early 20s, so I'm no stranger to motivation or the mental drive to exercise. I'm not lazy. But I started to lose faith in the benefits from the negative physical impact. To me this was counter intuitive as I figured we should be able to do physically demanding exercise as humans without feeling like we've been steam rollered for 72 hours and unable to lift our limbs without wincing.

**2)** On top of heavy caffeine abuse (10 - 12 strong cups a day), I found myself daily using Modafinil, or Ephedrine (or equivalent CNS stimulants), even down to seeking out and buying numerous legal research chemicals such as Isopropylphenidate, 4F-MPH & 3-FPM. For years I have been convinced I have some form of mild ADD or ADHD or bi-polar after reading so many other peoples descriptions. I felt these drugs helped, but over the past 6 months, I realised, I was addicted to how they made me feel, and was seeking a 'hit' from them constantly.

**3)** About a year ago, after seeking out more chemical 'hits' I came across Kratom. For fear of the downsides of chemical side effects, I found the idea of a natural 'herbal' solution very appealing. I realised I was just seeking something to change my chemistry and distract me from day to day feelings in life. I quickly have gone from using 3 - 5g of Kratom a day, easily to 20 - 25g a day throughout every day without fail.

**4)** 12 months ago, after never being a drug user for 35 years of my life until these recent times I even picked up some cannabis and LSD after reading about micro dosing benefits. Very quickly I settled into a routine of happily vaping weed and swapping between regular LSD micro doses to full, high doses.

**5)** My new relationship after separating from my ex wife is with a very understanding, supportive woman who I love. She was a none smoker when we met, and she's battled smoking cigarettes for her entire life. Due to some personal family related stresses, we both smoked some cannabis, and then it was like a trigger for her to start smoking again. The bizarre thing that threw up a red flag for me, is that being a lifelong none smoker, and only ever vaping weed for a year, I decided to try smoking. I liked the way it made me feel, and quickly progressed in about a month to a 4 - 6 cigarettes a day routine. I don't know if I'm addicted, as I haven't stopped but I have noted I craze the buzz and resulting relaxation.

**6)** When I'm not working late at night, I try to avoid checking my phone or computer, however, I still go to bed exhausted, sometimes unable to keep my eyes open, and in the morning I can't wake up. It's a mission and chore to get out of bed with any energy, and it takes me an hour to an hour and a half to mentally lift the fog from my mind - usually helped by kratom, cigarette and caffeine. I know with every fibre of my being, that this is not life and certainly not what life can be for someone like me of my age.

Overall I'm aware of what I am doing and am intelligent enough to know that factors in my mind and life circumstances are throwing me off balance and I'm seeking/craving externally what is not being produced internally as it should be. As I'm sat at the computer staring at the dark grey clouds and rain outside, I feel a deep imbalance and dissatisfaction with where my life is circumstantially and feel uncomfortable that I cannot be at ease with how I am living. So I know I need a plan to optimise my health, more for my mental and emotional well-being.

Now, my parents live at the coast, beach side, in Southern England and get a lot of sun exposure, bare foot walking, fresh air and so on. My incongruence in my life which hits me hard is that I yearn for a life based on principles which I'm educated to know are healthy for me and my children, however I feel trapped in my high stress 'digital' work life, and with my separation from my children's mum, feel I cannot relocate for fear of losing the healthy contact I have with my children.

Every day I face this dissatisfaction and dilemma. Every day I do a little more research and try not to be overwhelmed, to make positive changes somehow, some way to a better life. An optimised life.

When I stumbled upon an article citing Jack Kruse about magnetism, sunlight and other elements, it was like somebody had finally shown me the box with the picture on for the jigsaw puzzle. Like I always had all the scattered pieces scattered chaotically around, but no idea on the bigger picture. I now feel I'm scratching the surface to see the bigger picture and actually see the map & plan on how, maybe, to finally link all of the pieces together in my own life. There is just so much to learn, and there is a fear of doing the wrong thing, when you think you're doing the right thing, that I'm hoping the interaction on this forum will be insightful and helpful.

Thank you for listening and God bless you all for being here supporting each other.

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T17:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219631>

**Current Diet**
Most days consist of smoked salmon, boiled free range eggs, avocados, grass fed steak, green vegetables and green salad, nut butters (almond, peanut, cashew, walnut, macadamia), grass fed butter, coconut oil, organic ghee, 100% dark chocolate.

Occasionally white potatoes, lentils, rice, pasteurised cream, pasteurised cheese, and once a week I'll probably eat something more typical of a SAD with the kids or social gatherings and/or meal with other people at a restaurant.

I avoid alcohol, refined sugars as much as possible, and moderate with a view to attempting to avoid grains & processed foods.

**Current Supplementation**
40ml of Carlson's Fish Oil
Melatonin 3mg
DHEA 75mg
Greens & reds powder with added probiotics
Vit D3 1000iu
Vitamin K2 45mcg
Vitamin B3 40mg
Vitamin B6 20mg
Calcium 50mg
Magnesium Biglycinate Chelate 50mg
Zinc chelate 10mg
Olive leaf extract 500mg
Curcumin C3 400mg
Hawthorn Berry 300mg
Guggul Lipid 300mg
Hibiscus flower 100mg
Ubiquinol 100mg
Milk Thistle 750mg
NAC 750mg
Tumeric Root 150mg
Bupleurum Root 75mg
Dandelion Root 75mg
Artichoke Leaf Extract 30mg
Schizandra Berry powder 35mg
White Peony Root powder 35mg
Biotin 500mcg
Copper 2mg
Chromium Picolinate 300mcg
NA-R-Alpha Lipoic Acid, Gymnema Sylvestre, Berberine (undisclosed blend)

**Current Vices**
Nut butters (almond, peanut, cashew, walnut, macadamia) - daily
20g of Kratom - daily
High caffeine consumption - daily
1 - 6 cigarettes - daily
Lack of physical exercise

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T17:06:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219632>

Today I shall be completing this recommended survey: [https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/](https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/) and will update the results on my journal thread.

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T18:04:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219636>

**Oh dear! I scored 10 on the Scompy Quantum Quiz.... the lowest score: "_10 points and below : You may look great on the outside, but your mitochondria may disagree completely. Start with some simple changes first._"**

Although, one or two questions I felt were a little ambiguous such as "_Do you expose at least half of your skin surfaces to sunlight for more than 30 minutes a day at least 3+ times per week?_" ... I would suggest in the better sunlight days, the answer would be yes, but when it's grey, wet and miserable I would say no. It depends on the time of year.

"_Do you get out into midday UVB sunlight several times per week to build skin callus and produce Vitamin D_" ... again I would say, yes in the warmer, brighter days, but definitely not in the colder wet days, which we get plenty of here in Manchester.

"_Do you eat oysters or seafood on a regular basis?_" I eat a lot of salmon, so answered yes, but wasn't sure if this related to just crustaceous type seafood like prawns, in which case it would be a no.

"_Do you walk barefoot outside or touch earth, grass, trees, sand, stones etc at least 3 x a week for more than 15 minutes a day?_" - again it depends on the week and time of year, it really fluctuates. When the weather is good, yes absolutely, and otherwise no.

I'm partly disheartened by the outcome, but also encouraged that there is a lot I can do to make small, rapid and easy changes to my lifestyle.

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T18:09:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219638>

After watching my first Youtube video of Jack Kruse, this week I removed my monthly contact lenses (the type you keep in for a month without removing them at all) and threw them in the bin. I switched to wearing my prescription glasses, only when I need to read or look at the screen and taking them off as often as possible. I also started to stand barefoot in the garden on the grass on any day when it's not been raining with a view to build up my natural sun exposure in the outdoors. I'm yet to achieve this on dull and wet days, of which there are plenty.

I also read this post this morning: [https://www.facebook.com/drjackkrus...LFz70joGzwfYpeYdbDXq0JNwlJp81G\_NfyGuk&fref=nf](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/?hc_ref=ARQuIr3k8CQMcgA_d12d7eK2KZqqUVLFz70joGzwfYpeYdbDXq0JNwlJp81G_NfyGuk&fref=nf)

"_Sunglasses, tattoo's, nnEMF from your job, and chronic work at night under man made light. A history of abuse looking to replace that dopamine and beta-endorphin and your best friend was another rocker I wrote about with this very same story..._" and I thought about my own substance abuse, and terrible lack of morning energy, although mild in comparison, perhaps it is my own inner demons chasing dopamine desperately....

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T18:19:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219640>

Today, this speaks volumes to me: "_I could have looked at the move as a stressful-one. Instead, I rolled with the punches, took each step at a time and ran the marathon. Is your cup half-empty or half-full? It’s true that technology’s intersection with our biology is shaping up to be our greatest hurdle to complicate our health as we move into the future. There are many elements we can control in our local and micro-environments. But if you lack a great lack of control where you live, it may require you to change your location to find a more optimal fishbowl._" ~ Scompy, [https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/](https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/)

Where for a long time I would have had a negative spin on my existing situation, I can currently accept where I am as it being what it is, and look forward to making small but effective changes over the days, weeks and months to come.

### caroline — 2017-07-22T02:27:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219698>

keep going ......

If you can manage it - become a gold member and you will have access to all jack's monthly webinars and the follow up Q&A when you can ask Jack anything. This is worth it's weight in Gold!

I would suggest in the better sunlight days, the answer would be yes, but when it's grey, wet and miserable I would say no. It depends on the time of year.
>
> "_Do you get out into midday UVB sunlight several times per week to build skin callus and produce Vitamin D_" ... again I would say, yes in the warmer, brighter days, but definitely not in the colder wet days, which we get plenty of here in Manchester.

>
> I'm partly disheartened by the outcome, but also encouraged that there is a lot I can do to make small, rapid and easy changes to my lifestyle.

Go for it, whatever the weather. I don't do storms but everything else is up for grabs. Challenge though, not misery or suffering.  I have an outside CT tub, trust me getting in during the winter when you've had to break the ice with a hammer can be a challenge ... but a few minutes of that and the dopamine lift is amazing. Grounding whilst sunbathing under cloudy skies or in the rain, there's still sunlight to be harvested, and circadian rhythms reset or reinforced .... I was sat in the CT tub once looking at a glorious blue sky to the east, whilst the black cloud right above me dropped buckets of water on my head. I just laughed with Mother Nature .... Dopamine isn't just about blazing sunshine, which is just as well considering our weather ...

I love being grounded on wet stone or grass. It's epic!

Well done on the contacts. Not sure your prescription. I am -4.75 in both eyes and like you I only wear my glasses when I absolutely need to (driving) and I'm on a mission to improve my eyesight through as much natural light exposure as possible.

You're doing fab!

# Thread: Matthew H's Health Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthew-hs-health-journal.19868/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mbhemming — 2017-07-22T23:30:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthew-hs-health-journal.19868/post-219777>

Optimum health journal

Hello. I welcome suggestions to help me move toward being optimal

\-main goal: I want to have the energy and dopamine status to allow me to work a full time job as a computer scientist.

\-about me

sex: M

age: 32

height: 6’3

weight between 170 -180(hard to gain weight even when eating a lot)

location: Halifax, Nova Scotia(I’m taking Jack’s advice and next month I’m moving outside the city to the ocean in a remote area)

ancestry: 95% north west Europe(especially english and irish)

education: undergrad in Math/Physics. Currently going back to school to do a masters degree in Computer Science

\-Main issue is periodic fatigue

\-description of fatigue:

\-I lay in bed not feeling like I want to move.

\-low cognition

\-low/no drive/motivation

\-I do nothing but I don’t feel very bored because there is so little actively in my mind

\-approximately each month I have a week where I can’t get out of bed(but its not allways episodic, like this. Sometime I just feel more fatigued for a day)

\-causes that trigger periods of fatigue: alcohol, stress, and exercise can all bring on one of these episodes

\-exercise: If I over-exercise, then I can be wiped out(I went and played basketball for almost 2 hours and I was in bed for 1.5 weeks afterward)

\-alcohol:I can pretty much guarantee that having 4 drinks in a night will cause me to be in bed for the better part of a week afterwards

\-stress:If I have a project due, sometimes I can ‘kick it into high gear’(**not** using stimulants). But then the next day, I crash even harder

\-extremely large meal: Overeating can stimulate a fatigue episode. If I eat really big, then I will feel full for days. This really throws me off. It does take a huge meal to do this. I am fine with even eating quite large meals.

\-sometimes I can feel great for about a week too. And sometimes I feel more or less normal

\-A psychiatrist says its not Bipolar

\-when I come back to feeling good, I will easily get work done. But when I’m feeling poor, its a total struggle

\-additional symptoms during fatigue periods

\-unusual urinary PH rhythm. Instead of the normal PH rhythm of most people who swing acidic in the day and alkaline at night. I stay at at PH of 6. In the past, it has gone up to 7 or 7.5 for a few consecutive days

\-lower cognition

\-low drive

\-social anxiety(present even when not fatigued). Sometimes the anxiety symptoms seem less, because of decreased activity in my mind. But other times, it can cause more anxiety

\-no verbal thoughts present in my mind. When I start feeling better, I notice verbal thoughts coming back in the morning

\-sliding into an OCD state sometimes(defiantly not all the time) during crashes where I will obsessively research something

\-cold hands and feet(during winter). But I notice I get warm palms after I eat

sleep:

\-when I’m feeling good, I sleep about 7 hours and feel rested. When I’m feeling fatigued, I sleep 9 or 9.5 hours and sometimes have a nap in the day too.

\-I go through periods where I naturally get tired at night but also have periods where I am vigilant at night. The sleep disturbances are fairly rare and don’t seem to correlate with the other symptoms listed. Since I started taking Jack’s recommendations about circadian biology(over past 1.5 months), I have found improved stability in sleep patterns. But still not perfect. I will have a number of nights where I fall asleep and wake up at the same time and then I will randomly get a sequence of nights where I have vigilance at that time. This happens despite being certain that I get no blue light after 8pm and finish eating at least 4 hours before bed(usually longer).

\-other observations

\-drinking 3 or 4 glasses of water(I get it out of a natural spring) can temporarily take away fatigue/sleepiness

\-vitamin D is currently at 53 ng/ml(see 2017 blood work attachment)

\-ferritin is at 231ng/ml

\-thyroid pryoxidase antibodies is ‘less than 3 IU/ml’ in 2015. They have the normal reference range at 0 to 5. But I understand you want this to be as close to 0 as possible. Soon,I will have new numbers soon and will have Thyroid Globulin Antibody too.

\-I was told by a naturopath that my morning cortisol was low but still in the mortal range(am coritisol=312 mol/L in 2016). See labs attachment

\-maternal haplotype = H

\-genetic weaknesses from 23 and Me: The only one is increased risk of alzheimer's- GSTP1 I105V(2 mutations), GSTP1 A114V(1 mutations). See detox attachment. I’m not sure what to conclude about this but I heard Jack talking about mutations on the genes associated with Glutathione. So I thought I should put it in.

\-besides the ‘fatigue weeks’ I can often be fatigued for a day or two at a time

\-I shiver easily after a few minutes in the ocean. I also shiver early and chatter my teeth really early in the winter. I’m very sensitive to the cold in the winter

\-symptoms where present when I was living over 2Km from cell towers, relatively low cell phone use, 3 WiFi routers in proximity.

\-what has helped periods of fatigue?

\-creatine _occasionally(not usually)_ pulls me out of the crashes

\-eating beef _occasionally(not usually)_ pulls me out too

\-Pepsi/Coke makes both me and my dad feel much better. My Dad has a lot of fatigue problems too and Cola helps him feel good too.

\-timeline of lifestyle

\-until I was 29 years old

\-had mononucleosis at age 19

\-symptoms slowly worsened during this time. But I was living really unhealthily during that time too.

\-more or less a ‘standard american lifestyle”

\-was a chronic over-eater but I still wouldn’t get fat. Addictive relationship to food

\-partied a lot on weekends. Binge drinking until 3am

\-In my early to mid 20’s I had extreme anxiety and panic attacks. These symptoms actually got better as my fatigue got worse

\-29 to 31 years old

\-drastic reduction in alcohol: some months I wouldn’t drink at all, other months I would have a couple night with around 4 drinks

\-began using methylphenidate. Worked at first but eventually didn’t work. Caused lots of anxiety

\-31 onward

\-lived on the 27th floor of a building with towers at 300m away.

\-fully stopped all alcohol

\-fully stopped all stimulants

\*noticed a definite improvement(but the problem is still present and bad)

\-past 3 months

\-went keto-paleo

\-past month

\-diet:I switched to eating at least half of my meals as seafood the other half paleo. Taking 3g of krill oil with non-seafood meals. Keto during the week. Moderate carbs on weekend. When I move to the ocean next within the next few weeks I will move to eating at least 3/4th of it as seafood. There is a dock just down the road where its easy to get mackrel, herring, sole, and calamari. And I will network with local fisherman for wholesale prices. I met a guy last year who’s father farms oysters and he said he could get me them for 25 or 50 cents. Often the docks have small buildings next to them where people are processing the seafood from their boat. It should be easy to network with them.

I am cooking the food to a lesser degree

I was doing IF before but I stopped it a couple weeks ago. I eat one meal 30 minutes after waking up (6:30am) and then another one at 3:45 pm(I did this because I heard jack say that 4-5pm is an optimal time to generate dopamine.

\-CT: Did the face dunking and now I go in the ocean(57 degrees). Some days I do the block of ice on chest. Adapting slowly but surely. I’m doing it 3 times per week and working up. My goal is to do it overnight before bed for an hour.

\-sunlight

\-getting AM sunlight

\-spending 3 or 4 days/week at the beach

\-I’m getting to a tonne of sunlight, generally

\-blue blocker Uvex at 8pm, sharp

\-when I move to the ocean I will have 4 beaches within a 10 minute drive. You get a cool breeze to so I find I can be in the sun all day there and not feel ‘drained’.

\-non native EMF

\-took Jack’s advice and threw out my microwave oven

\-cell tower close by. Currently moving outside the city. Although the problem began back when I was in a pretty low EMF environment. The place I’m moving has its closest tower at over 1km away.

\-reduced my personal wifi but I’m in a building so I’m surrounded by a dozen routers. This will change in the next month. I may have 1 or 2 routers in 60ft radius.

\-reduced my magnetic field expose as much as possible. Unplugging fridge when I’m in the kitchen, etc.

\-blue light reduction: Blue blocking glasses worn in almost all article light situations. Computers are at colour temp of 1900K and as dim as possible

\-cell phone. I now keep my phone 20ft away and route my texts and calls through my computer (ethernet). After I’m moved in

\-water. I get water out of a fresh spring and drink over a gallon per day.

Hey, Jack. Any changes I should make to the protocol above?

\-family history

Dad: Mental health issues(OCD, Depression, Anxiety, unusually obscure interpretations of events). COPD. Chronic fatigue.

Mom: breast cancer before she was 40. She is 60 and has been healthy since besides high blood pressure

grand dad: perfectly healthy until he died of hear attack at age 89. Still had perfect cognition.

nanny: she is 92 and perfect cognition. Celiac

grampy: 88, has alzheimer's(onset at mid 80’s), heart attack(age 80ish), skin cancer(not a serious type), bowel cancer, diabetes(onset in middle age)

grammy: rheumatoid arthritis, high blood pressure, was on blood thinner, died of stroke at age 72. She had a lot of relatives that had Dementia

Dad’s side: lots of food sensitive(celiac, dairy, soy), most women on my Dad’s side have Celiac.

Welcome!! I'm still a newbie to the forum and learning from the experienced ones here, but two things stand out to me...

First, the brain fog. The "not doing anything but not bored." I had that for years because of inflammation. Your description of fatigue I can totally relate to. You can make it go away.

Second, you have relatives with celiac disease. You didn't say anything about gluten in your diet so I'm assuming you had exposure like everyone else. Was the alcohol that made you feel bad beer? I read a paper where close relatives to celiac often have a mucosal reaction to gluten even when they test negative for celiac and don't have the so-called "genes" for it. You could have been reacting to it in the past without knowing it. Paleo should eliminate gluten anyway, but that could have been a problem. Celiac and thyroid disease go together as a pair. Both will absolutely give you brain fog and anxiety.

Also, sub clinical thyroid malfunction can masquerade as bipolar symptoms. I often had days where I was amped up, couldn't sleep, sociable and talkative, did all my chores in a couple hours, and they were always followed by a crash where I couldn't get out of bed. Hashi runs in my family - I had a low antibody count and so-called "normal" hormone labs, vitamin D at 53, but all the symptoms of Hashimoto's. It was horrible. No one would give me thyroid replacement, but I'm glad now, because I found better ways to address it!

I'll let others tell you where the best place to start is with labs and protocols, because I'm not there yet. But I want you to know that I can relate to what you are going through, and you're in the right place. You deserve to feel great, instead of what you feel now.

### drezy — 2017-07-30T04:40:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthew-hs-health-journal.19868/post-220403>

The biggest factors on the health of me, my wife, and son after sun and CT:

Me:
\-31 lbs
BP drop -15ish both numbers
libido up
warm hands (never since I was 15)

Wife:
Body fat more stubborn but finally coming off now from her March CT beginning
(Frankly like doc said the fat fit redistributed to ass and hips first and I kinda like it)
Sleeps best in the 15 or so years I've known her even though she currently is dealing with multiple product designs Still she has great sleep compared to even low pressure times. That factor alone makes this marriage 10x easier for me and her life easier too!

Son(12):
\-15 lbs (baby fat) over the last 3 months. Potential future man boobs BYE BYE. This guy is getting ripped looking and his momma complains about it now because she knows the girls will notice.
