# Thread: Sheddie's Optimizing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sheddie — 2017-07-25T14:17:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/post-220027>

Greetings all. Sheddie begins her 'Optimize -- or Bust!'

**Age:** 67 in 2017 **Sex:** F **Haplogoup:** H4a1 **Joined JackKruse.com:** July 2017
**Location:Lat.~33N
Dx:** Osteoarthritis (OA), Degenerative Disk Disease (DDD), Fibromyalgia, CFS, severe Dust & Mold Allergies, Asthma & Chemical Sensitivity Syndrome; Hashimoto's (1999); post-antibiotic SIBO, COPD (2017), MTHFR issues.
**Surgeries:** Cataracts (2017), Melanoma stage 1 (2017), Lumbar disk repair (1995), Arthroscopic (knee) repair of a 24 yo torn medial meniscus (1987).
**Current Rx:** Albuterol PRN; SIBO-related supplements; Lugol's...// 2018 Rx: 25mg compounded dessicated T3/T4
**Current Therapies / Protocols:** Leptin Rx; Cold Thermogenesis (face); Magnetico sleeping; regular sunlight exposure
**Extra Protocols: (\*=discontinued)** Continue Pain & AI management modalities: including; Neuro-muscular massage, Chiropractic, FIR/NIR Sauna, Lumen Photon (IR+red LEDs), \*Alpha-Stim CES; \*LCHFHP diet with an eye to FODMAPs; Sperti Lamps (winters); Ellis Water; \*Sporebiotics; DDW-25, 1 pint/day, 30 days on 30 days off (began 12/2018)
**Health WINS:** Tolerating cold showers; 12/2017-Weight down 11.6 lb; inflammation improved; D3 (2018) from 36 to 45 from skin Sun mainly, and what my IOLs will allow through; Summer 2018, no Fibro symptoms
**Health Goals:** Pain & Fatigue to zero; Light-Not Food lifestyle; 30-lb weight loss; reduce obscene number of supplements; 'medically stable' since ~2000 BUT hoping to bust that status for actual (true blue) recovery, in a race against actual aging!

Related Reading: Kruse website--Podcasts, YouTube clips, Forums; Hof-The Iceman; Epi-Paleo Rx; Pollack's Fourth Phase of Water, DDW publications.

My Haplogroup H4a1, while 100% European, broke that down to 10.5% Scandinavian (0 Finnish) with French & German beating that at 14.4%. The rest is Broadly Northwestern European.

**Journey Start-up:**

LIGHT: Managing 3-5 sun exposures daily; _still unclear if the 'pre-dawn UV blast,' or at the moment of dawn, or sometime past dawn should be the first UV exposure of the day?_; Have IOLs after cataracts so intent on skin as next best route of sunlight absorption; if sitting up sometimes allow sclera more exposure by sitting sideways (?); use a tan-thru' tube dress; wearing UVEX blockers when the house lights go on & f.lux all the time for wired desktop computer; unable to have a 'smart' or cell phone due to arthritic hands (not interested in 'hands-free' or wireless non-necessities!); planning more frequent _IR-sauna sessions in evenings._ Turn on a UV 'black light' bulb first thing on waking to take my time to safely get out of bed, and then (~5 minutes) make my way to grab some water and go outside. Every day is a sun-day. 12/2017 not cold adapted enough, so trying Sperti Lamps. 8/2018: after months of facing daily sunrise & sunset along with minimum 40 minutes full skin UVA & UVB, often more, Fibro brain fog & fatigue are gone, 2018. Installed a privacy fence and quit the kiniki when no one's visiting.

WATER: Intent on "a glass of water every hour" (Thanx to the recent video tipster!); Experimenting with spring waters and intent on moving away from distilled with trace minerals or Sole salt added, sometimes vortexed (magnetic funnel) or sun-exposed for 'structure;' structuring water with glass container set on a Lumen red-LED & IR mat as carrying glass out to the sunny patio is risky with thumbs that don't grip... 12/2017 decided on Icelandic Glacial bottled water, and, an Ellis distilling machine for tap water purification.

MAGNETISM: Grounding on un-treated cement patio when sunning; stopped use of earthing products hooked into the house electric socket ground until I have an outdoor spike set up; Have Orgonite and Q-link items in the car, house, and around the property perimeter outside; use a metal-free bed frame; use an Alan Maher mat for feet under the desk; sleep on a Magnetico.

Diet: Never was fond of sweets/sugar (mad for crunchy salty snacks); Low carb and 'as clean as possible' foods for decades (chemically sensitive after several years of workplace n-hexane exposure in the 1980s); currently on FODMAPs food choices for SIBO (post-antibiotics 4 yrs ago), with some temporary food allergy restrictions; Told No Wheat, forever; Paleo mostly the last few years; New action; advance to Epi-Paleo/pescetarian, mostly seafood for protein. _Getting the hang of that first meal/smoothie of the day! Thanks for the recipe ideas!
_

### Sheddie — 2017-07-26T05:11:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/post-220095>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=220094)
>
> sardines!

You bet, and I like 'em, too. Up to a point. Every so often in the past, my dog and I would share a can of sardines. I thought it would be easy.

However, a whole can is only 16g of protein and I think I need at least 25g of protein for breakfast then and there? Two cans might make my stomach rebel... What else on the plate? Not enough time to cook a fresh fish, and I wouldn't dare use a nuke-unit, if I want to eat 30 minutes after rising... Leftovers?

I just know many before me have conquered this bottom rung detail! I'm not sure but I doubt I can get out of, or find some hack around Leptin Reset Breakfast (new thread idea (?) for contributions??) Okay, just tell me, get it over with, if TWO cans of sardines are 'usual & customary?'

And Thanks, Caroline!! (Occam's razor rules!)

### caroline — 2017-07-26T05:43:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/post-220100>

There is someone here - darned if I can remember who ....they used to mix sardines with eggs and a little cheese and stick it in the oven. I think they broke the sardines up in an ovenproof bowl topped with two eggs and grated cheese. I would probably throw in celery and onions and bacon to mask the flavour of the sardines - lol

BTW - think outside the box ........dinner for breakfast. Before you go to bed stick a leg of lamb in your slow cooker and then wake up to breakfast - plus some nice broth.

When I cook I make a big batch of whatever so we have leftovers. We will lightly grill 4 salmon fillets and then, the next morning, mix it with greens and cucumbers and onions etc and homemade mayo and top with poached eggs. This is definitely our favourite.

Do you know what a western omelet is? chopped bacon and eggs and onions and mushrooms sometimes. I add in minced garlic and minced tumeric too.

There is an epi-paleo recipe section - have you found this yet?

### Sheddie — 2017-07-26T14:48:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/post-220121>

Found it! Will let its name have another curtain call: Moqueca – Brazilian Fish Stew Recipe.

For sure, 25+ grams of seafood protein would go down better with flavorful liquids. Can taste it already! Of course. It's all coming back to me now.

After being ovo-lacto Vegetarian for many years I took up with meat stews and soups to learn how to use meat. I was raised on primarily Scandinavian and French grandmothers' cooking, but, went vegetarian right when I moved out on my own. Went on a paella kick some years ago which I bet could stand the substitution of paleo cauliflower rice. Thai cuisine does wonderful seafood soup using coconut milk... I'm on a roll now!! And, ever cognizant that I'm in the age cohort of those who need to switch from Food First to LIGHT First! Being retired, I confess to being a Foodie for something to do. (More than 'enough to do' now with sun-seeking and CT, etc.) Strangely, I didn't gravitate to seafood recipes before. (Those darn beef-meat 'n potatoes guys in the house.) That's changed! It'll be a lot easier when my chicken & eggs restriction is lifted. I just got the OK from my functional medicine doc for dairy again...

Thanks for the wake-up! Now, where did I put those crock-pots?

### Sheddie — 2018-11-27T03:55:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/post-256673>

Update: Went all summer with no Fibromyalgia symptoms or flares -- first time in two decades! Also, thyroid test values came in at normal range for the first time in 20+ years. Kept off 10 of 13 lbs lost; still would like to drop 20 lbs. Still have The BAB to conquer, and while I apparently don't have SIBO anymore, I would like to see the belly shrink. All in all, energy stays up and I even went on two long trips this year. One place, I met a Black Swan! He didn't look at me but one day perhaps he will? { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### JanSz — 2018-11-27T15:51:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/post-256697>

> [Sheddie said:](/goto/post?id=256673)

# Thread: Sun Eater Journeyal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-eater-journeyal.19960/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigSkinny — 2017-08-05T18:50:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-eater-journeyal.19960/post-220979>

Hello all. I have been studying Jack's observations for a month through YouTube. Hint, If you watch them all even the deepest concepts eventually hit home through sheer repetition! I joined because there are still details to absorb and apply.

I came upon, optimal theory? Through my obsession with changing my health mid life. I will optimize the second half of my life. I feel, and percieve through studying, that being a mitochondriac is an actual path to that end result. Heart disease and diabetes are common on my Puerto Rican side. Obesity on the Irish/French side. My Grandparents on Mom's side made it to thier late eighties without dementia or heart disease but were still dependent on a slew of medications. In fact, a misplaced pill in the wrong container recently took my Nanny's life. She would have made it to her late 90's otherwise, but I digress. My father is a darker skinned "Hispanic" in the summer and a standard olive complected guy in winter. Mom is white enough to see through. I tan easily and rarely burn but have had some bad ones in my time, not lately though, d-minder!

3 months ago I weighed 305, I now weigh 260 due to:

ketogenic eating

sleep management

moderate but intense exercise in the early sun and earthed whenever possible

Over the last month I have integrated:

Intermittent fasting, 1 meal a day after work (usually) this was a natural progression of keto and kicking carb addiction/ food is becoming less important to me, yessss!

Sun/earthing, thus the workout methodology

Blue light mitigation EVERYBODY clowns my three pairs of always accessible uvex glasses

Spring water whenever possible

EMF avoidance, almost impossible but little bites

Increasing my seafood

I feel like these optimal protocols while not implemented perfectly have kick-started my second phase "the second 50 lbs" and I look to apply them more often. Even if optimal living was pure quackery, it isn't, there is nothing like a Sphinx workout- running, sort of, up the hills barefoot and swinging my kettlebell while earthed as UV starts to hit around 0830. I will always do this. Always.

That is my journey thus far. Would love some input/pointers on how I can further optimize. I will start logging diet here as I have stopped tracking macros after three months straight on myfitnesspal. IMF and experience have made it easier to mentally track macros. Imagine if I lost 100 lbs in 6 months?! I believe only mitochondriacity will make that possible.

Where father came from usually does not count much.
We inherit most from mothers.

Get your haplotype, usualy from 23andme.com
find out where your grandma was 10000 years ago.
Try to reside at that latitude or slightly closer to equator.

Generally,
closer to poles more seafood
closer to equator less but more local carbs.
In particular,
I found that it rarely works as planned, too many variable??
I can figure that one out only by (properly) testing my fatty acids.

We are super efficient machines,
working hard is not going to help you loose weight,
but will shorten your life.

Track your 4xsaliva cortisol
to see if you are doing good job when trying your best in following Circadian Rhythm.

/////////////
Puerto Rican father,
Irish, French mother
nice mix overall
but from mitochondria pov you are French/Irish

..............

### Frederica — 2017-08-23T02:12:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-222060>

Where to start.
Female: 58 yo, 6', 220 lbs
Have struggled with sleep for 6 years. Restless leg, tinnitus, wired and tired, now just tired all the time. When this started I weighed 174 lbs. I did not sleep for more that 4-5 hrs per night. Sometimes I would not be able to get to sleep until 1-2 because of RL, other times I would fall asleep only to awaken at 2 -3 and not be able to resume sleep. I thought I would go crazy. You can have some pretty dark thoughts when its dark outside and you're so tired but you can't fall asleep. Gained weight and started having all kinds of pains and muscle trouble. Did hair analysis. Went to many naturopathic Drs. Hair analysis said I had high copper toxicity. My hormone labs basically said I had no hormones ( well, everything was might low). BIHR didn't seem to help anything. Many of the supplements they gave me just made me more wired. I had a 23 and me done and some of my snps show I have a problem with things building up in my system (CBS, MAO, COMT, MTRR). What do I do with all this information?
I lurked around the site 2 years ago and was starting the leptin reset when I was diagnosed with triple negative breast cancer (1/16). Kinda got sidetracked. Did a boatload of research on TNBC and decided to have a lumpectomy, no chemo, no radiation. Started Reams Biological Theory of Ionization (you test your urine and saliva ph, urine conductivity and ureas and supplement accordingly) - 18 months and no reoccurring cancer. Slipped on my stairs last summer and broke my ankle and tore off a rotator cuff muscle. Another surgery. Gained more weight ( I never had a weight problem till after my 4th child at 44 yo), sleep tanked further. Pain in just about every joint. Frozen shoulder after surgery. Funny skin tags in skin folds. Hearing, vision all going down hill. I'm a 58 year old geriatric patient!!
I got desperate and went to a DO and he gave me .025 mg. lorazepam to take nightly. It helps but I don't want to be on it for long. However, I have some brain and energy back and am ready to do any and everything to get back my health and beat this toxic world we have created for ourselves.

Things I've done:
Gotten rid of most of my wifi toys (from 20 gadgets to 3), turn off all wifi in house and power to bedroom
nightly.
Get sun in my eyes first thing in the morning,
Use D minder app to get minimum 4000 iu Vit D daily ( I live in Eastern Washington and it's hard to get a lot of D).
Ground outside while getting Vit D (30 - 45 mins).
I drink lots of distilled water with fresh lemon juice.
I'm planning on starting the leptin reset next week after finishing Epi Paleo Rx book and getting my 14 yo settled in school.
I had some labs done this week and should get the results back soon.

So, this is my start to discovering what my body is trying to tell me about my environment. I've been interested in the terrain of my cells since reading about germ theory vs soil theory while homeschooling my kids. One of those seeds that gets planted, just waiting for water and light to make it grow!!

### Frederica — 2017-08-23T22:04:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-222108>

Fitness@home and Caroline - how exciting to have someone respond! Thank you.
Um, how to I give Dr. K this information about my mom and mom? Just post it here?

I do wear blue blocker glasses when I watch TV (I don't watch often but sometimes in the evening I sit with family and watch a show - I do strive to be off before 8pm). When I'm on my computer - I use the IRIS app set at the reading or health mode. I have led strip lights under my cabinets in the kitchen that
I turn to red in the evening. A 25 watt incandescent by my bed -not for reading but to see whats around me!!! Same in my bathroom. I'm working on the rest of the house. Most of them are LEDs but I don't turn them on much right now as where we live the sun doesn't set until 8. Winter will be a different story. I know that I need to change these lights soon. I have been sitting outside between 8 and 9 and listening to podcasts and other audios or just chat with anyone who will join me to avoid light in my eyes. Then at 9-9:30 I get ready for bed. It doesn't always happen but it is lovely when I can.

I haven't read John Otts book yet - It's on my list though. Would you say to put it at the top? I'm focusing on blog, forum, Epi Paleo now.

I have listened to a few podcasts - I think I have listened to the Vermont one - is there more than one of those though?

I live in a smallish town, 70,000 folks, but am near 2 other towns which ups the total population to about 200,000. We live on a half acre lot. Surrounded by other half acre to one acre lots so not too dense. But my phone registers many (4-6) wifi signals to choose from though they are mostly weak ones.

I am a stay at home mom.

I don't think it is a smart meter although it is digital, the guy still has to have access to the meter to read it.

I am going to order the meters to help me figure out what I am exposed to. On the list. So much stuff on the list.

In case this is where I post the information for Dr. K:

My mom had colon cancer at age 36 that metastasized into lung cancer - she died at 41. She didn't smoke, though my dad did. I believe she had a lot of stress in her life. My dad was a pilot in the Air Force working his way up the command path. We moved quite a bit, and there were other issues.
Her mother lived until she was in her 90's, she had dementia and strokes.

Thank you all for making this road a bit straighter for those of us following later.

### Frederica — 2017-08-27T02:56:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-222322>

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/). exactly!! I download the video and then put my iPad on the ground full blast so I can hear but not see. It is a bit frustrating when he says to look at this slide to understand why such and such is true. It makes for good conversation with my neighbors though. I've already had to explain to a few why I'm out searching for early morning sunlight in my jammies - sometimes the edge of my front lawn by the street is the only place it exists. Now they see me sitting in my grass in the back yard grounding and listening to audios full blast. Got to get those fences up soon.

### Frederica — 2017-08-27T03:21:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-222323>

Caroline: thank you. I have ordered John Ott's book. Should get here soon.
I have dunked my face in ice water for 2 nights now. Wow, I can barely hold my breath for 10 seconds the first time - it really takes your breath away. But I keep doing it and coming up for air until my face is really cold and pink. I am not sure what is making a difference but last night I slept without taking any lorazepam ! I am excited. I decided to not take it because I started to fall asleep sitting on my porch after dinner and that never happens. I'm not even totally on the diet part - only the Big Breakfast and reducing carbs But I know Dr. K has started to say its all about the light. . .
I'll do a search for the easy start guide, I don't think I've run across it yet.
I thought about joining as Gold but I really haven't read much on the site and figured I'd spend some time assimilating because all the questions I have now have probably been answered somewhere. I haven't even gotten through the top 5 starting blogs. Leptin Rx and CT Protocol so far. Vermont Talks 2016 and halfway through 2017. Some of his other podcasts out on the web, and read most of the Epi Paleo book (skimmed some chapters). Wish I could do some CT on Father Time and slow it down, or would that just shrink it like fat, or would it expand the spaces between moments. Ha ha, I'm lost.

### caroline — 2017-08-27T05:46:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-222325>

I love your attitude! face dunking is great ....and hopefully will keep the wrinkles away!

The BAB is important first think in the morning - you are resetting your circadian rhythm ....it is a light story!

### Frederica — 2017-09-07T03:45:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-223039>

I keep telling myself that it took awhile to get here, it will take awhile to get somewhere else.
I've started the BAB. I have no problem eating that much protein, just getting it together in time is a challenge.
I do try to save some from dinner the night before. Most of the time I am not hungry till 2 pm. I can tell which proteins keep me feeling full longer.
Eggs, fish, steak - yes. Chicken not so much. And the more fat the better. I gotta find a good hollandaise recipe.
We have had the smoke from the Canadian fires all week. I still go outside and look toward the sun in the am but its pretty overcast.
I'm not getting much D in the afternoon because of it. I have even started going topless in my backyard. The sunsets are beautiful though - very orange.
I ordered some red light night lights to put around the house and use in the evening. I tried to read a book by candlelight
the other evening but all the flickering drove me nuts. Too much draft from the AC I guess.
My sleep has been off and on. Some nights I feel tired and get to sleep on my own, though not great sleep. I seem to have dreams where I think
someone is in the room standing over me and I holler like crazy. My poor husband. . . He doesn't know what to think. He is very supportive and
tries some stuff but he doesn't suffer from any chronic ailments so sees no need. He did watch me CT my face the other night and said that he
didn't know waterboarding was part of the protocol ;

I finally finished the Vermont talks. Dr. K. mentioned if you put your haplotype and some snps from 23andme he would let us know where our best environment
was.
Haplotype: I3
C677T: no mutation
A1298C: no mutation
VDR : both are mutated on both alleles
CBS: There are four on my report, 2 have no mutations and 2 have mutations on only 1 allele

times up for my computer today.

### Frederica — 2017-09-14T03:35:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-223588>

So, I've been busy reading. And not understanding much but seeing the importance of sunlight and that my life really needs to change. It's a bit discouraging that we are approaching the no UVB season here in Washington. I've been doing pretty well on the Leptin Reset diet, I had a glitch last weekend when I ended up in the Urgent Care with a kidney infection and put on antibiotics. I have added yogurt to my morning meal - no sugar added. And lots of sauerkraut to help my gut. I did have some constipation but am not sure if it was caused by the antibiotics or if I have a leaky gut issue that I need to resolve first. So, I've printed out the Leaky Gut blogs and will be reading them too. I get out every morning for 20 minutes as soon as the sun comes up. Lately I have been waking at 6:30 - the sun doesn't come up to 7:00 - so I make my BAB and sit outside and eat it. Then come in to get my daughter off for her day and come back outside and read in the sun for about an hour. UVB doesn't show up until 9ish here so I might get about 15 minutes in the morning. I have started wearing my glasses and not contacts until evening. I have amber glasses that don't fit over glasses so I have to change to my contacts in the evening. I go outside sometime around 1200 -1:00 for 20-30 minutes to get more UVB. I am trying not to use my iPad except in airplane mode. I have hardwired my laptop. We have everything to hardwire all the other stuff but haven't gotten there yet. My sleep is my main issue right now. I have some nights that are better than others but I still take a lorazepam nightly. Even then most nights are very restless. I am strongly considering a magnetico pad. However, our mattress is an innerspring and I have read somewhere you shouldn't mix the two. So a new mattress and magnetico is going to be a big bill! I am going to rent some meters to help me see what I have in my house and car and take them with me when we go buy a new car. So glad we hadn't done that yet. I'm still just face dunking. I haven't read any on CT yet so am just doing what I know now. Wish our gym had one of those 50 degree pools.

### Frederica — 2017-10-19T21:03:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-226232>

I need to update more but its a funny thing when you know others are reading - I probably need a daily journal just for me where I can put down all my hacks and then update to this journal weekly. I know Gretchen and Misty mentioned doing a journal in the recent video that was sent to my email. Am trying to come up with all the things I would list: Intakes (supps, food, water), Environment (sun, nnemfs, stress, CT), symptoms (sleep, hunger, mood, emotions). My husband, ever the engineer, said I should create a spreadsheet. Then I thought there is probably an app out there. But I am going the paper route because I want simple and less damaging tech.
I have been diligent on my morning sun. Eating my breakfast out on my back porch with my feet in the grass and reading print material or listening to audio for at lease an hour, topless, totally freaked that a neighbor might just drop by. I have friendly neighbors. I need a sign that warns them - beware of . . . . uncovered breasts? Seems a bit off putting.
Anyway, I have lost 16 lbs since the end of August. Pretty happy with that. My favorite find so far is bone broth (chicken or beef) heated with shrimp and scallops or mussels. I can get a whopping 40g of protein without making myself miserable. I then add some leftover dinner to round things up. My treat at lunch is a Tblsp of coconut butter with some macadamia nuts ground up and sprinkled on top. Makes my day and keeps me loose! I find I am less hungry for lunch and sometimes just have a protein shake. No fruit, maybe a handful of spinach. I like the vanilla Paleo Protein.
I do my lunch outside and spend another 30 mins - hour listening to my holosync. I am a big fail with meditation but I can listen to holosync and it settles my mind. I'm not sure that it does all that it says but it is a good relaxation and I had it.
We are losing UVB. I am not going to be moving soon so I need to figure out what will help me get through this winter. Eastern WA state at a latitude of 46 is NOT optimal, I know. I am not a tree, I know. But I am not going to leave my family and live alone in the tropics. This will be a slow and steady process. Well, I am trying for a trip South in January for a few weeks but with a 14 yo in school . . .
My sleep is such a hit and miss. For a few nights it is not too bad and then bam I have a night where I feel electrified all night, my legs won't stop moving and I feel buzzy and my feet burn. All power is off to our bedroom, Wifi off, no cordless phones. The Meters showed my bedroom pretty clean when we turn off power. This is where the daily journal needs to come in so I can see all the variables.
I am waiting to go to a doctor in town that I feel is very good at reading labs but she is so booked - until December. So I went to my standard Dr. and got a few labs. She wants to put me on T4. I need to do some research.
Labs:
D, 25 hydroxy: 44ng/ml. (highest its been since I started testing it in 2011)
ESR: 8mm/Hr (I have no idea what this is)
CRP: <.3 mg/dl (not hs crp)
CO2: 27 mmol/L
glucose: 92 mg/dl
BUN: 14 mg/dl
Creat: .8 mg/dl
Bun/CRE: 18
Free T4: .9 ng/dl
Free T3: 3.2 pg/ml
TSH: 5.09ulU/mL

there are more numbers but I need to get off and get outside.

### Frederica — 2017-10-20T01:48:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-226245>

I wanted to be sure and post my successes after that bit of a rant above.

I had my yearly eye appt last week and he was surprised to see the cloudiness in my lenses all cleared up!! I told him that I've been looking at the sun in the morning and he just cocked his head and said - "not right at it I hope". The thing is I can look right at it at my latitude and as soon as it rises. Anyway, woot woot. I also ordered new glasses (with no uv protection) so I can look over them toward the sun without having to keep taking out my contacts!! I have not worn glasses for 35 years, this is a biggy for me. But if I've gotten any message from this site its how important sun in your eyes in the am is.

I've lost 16 lbs from eating epi-paleo. Not hard and no extra workouts. I've actually taken a break from the gym because I think I'm dealing with Adrenal Fatigue and probably was making it worse with all my pushing. Not to mention all the blue lights and wifi.

Most nights, when I put my blue blocking glasses on at 7:00 pm, I am fighting to stay awake at 8:30. It doesn't mean my sleep is good but at least I'm tired and that is a change.

About half the time I face dunk at night I sleep better but not every time. Sometimes I can become anxious while holding my breath. I'm so afraid of suffocation - something in my childhood and having 5 older brothers. It didn't help when my husband compared it to waterboarding either. something to work through. I

I've started reading through the blogs by date. It keeps me from going down too many trails and not finishing what I set out to learn which creates procrastination. I need to learn something to the point where I can take "re"educated action.

Sometimes my family will let me share what I've read and not roll their eyes. Sometimes they'll come out in the morning and sit with me. But, it's getting cooler now so not so much. I am excited about the cold. It is a call to adapt, to change with the earth.

I have a nice tan. Everywhere.

I'm not as stressed because I have a plan. I can take little steps and a mistake isn't going to set me back too much because I'm not doing anything poisonous to my body. I get very confused at what I can do with where I live and can start to feel like my only hope is to move to the equator, but I see other folks in the group living above 35 degrees lat. and they are working it. You just have to be more diligent in the other areas. I have to keep learning how to listen to this body God gave me. Wish I had started sooner.

There is a ton of information out there on most of what Dr. Kruse talks about and you can usually find a way to understand it at your own level. It's just that you have to be on the internet a lot. So much of this is already known but no one applies it to people like Dr. Kruse does.

My timer is going off - that's my other success. I time my computer use and then make myself go outside for a walk, or pull some weeds, or chat with a neighbor. It's been great.

### Frederica — 2017-11-01T21:10:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-227124>

So much to read - i love it. I have always loved to research but am a flop at application. However, I believe I have leptin resistance and adrenal burnout. Now, this has been after reading jacks blogs sequentially so I haven't even gotten to the newer stuff. I do get the light, grounding and water stuff ( well "get" is probably to strong a term) . I understand it is of utmost importance. But, based on my flatlined low cortisol I am pretty sure of adrenal burnout. I went to my doctor yesterday and took 2 printouts of Jacks - the leptin part one, and the adrenal rx. She actually read them and agreed with a lot of it!!! Woot. She even used the M word for my condition!! You have a mitochondial issue. I left her office feeling so encouraged. She is still not a believer in nnemf (she just bought a car with all the techy rf, bluetooth stuff and loves it) but baby steps.
So now, along with the leptin reset, I am starting the adrenal rx. We have to start slowly because my detox pathways are poor due to snps but she put me on Progesterone at night and ordered some labs.
We were talking about all kinds of stuff, like maternal mtDNA research and Doug Wallace. Her poor nurse kept coming in saying the next patient was ready, but she said she needed a breather and this was doing her good. She used to be a biochemist before going into Natureopathy. Hoping for a long and profitable relationship with this gal. And, she's on my insurance (at least for now). She really worked the codes to try and get most of the labs paid for. I feel so sorry for doctors nowdays and all the hoops they have to jump through. She mentioned something about having to bury some of this "woo woo" stuff deep in my charts in case of a chart audit. What the heck is a chart audit? All the things to watch out for . . .

### mike_g123 — 2017-09-30T01:12:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-gs-health-journal.20057/post-224496>

JanSz light tree. I turn this on when I wake up in the morning. I am exposed to it for a few minutes before I shower. Then I do 7 minutes a side with my red sperti. Then I make breakfast in the buff with the JanSz tree on, this is around a half hour of exposure. I have noticed better energy levels, better dopamine (mode) levels, and better immune function. It has been colder the last few weeks in the morning so in addition to morning sunlight exposure I have been able to do CT on the way to work. Next week the morning temperature should be in the fifties so it will be even deeper CT.

# Thread: Meridian's health journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meridians-health-journey.20109/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeridianGrace — 2017-09-06T23:06:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meridians-health-journey.20109/post-223009>

Taking a deep breath....ahhhhhhhhhh filled with gratitude for this glorious sunny day in Austin and all the help that I have received from the Jack Kruse community thus far. Through some magical bit of glittering sound, I found Mitch, who through his great kindness has taken some of the precious minutes that life has offered him to help me.... and help me greatly he did . (...I highly recommend that you offer new members a hand holding session so they can learn how to walk their way through all the well thought out and deep opportunities that this website offers.) Aheem////but back to it.

I am a naturopath/homeopath and forever student of natures secrets and full tilt boogy screaming healing answers. Listening to Jack has been like being hit upside the head and told...all these things that you have seen work in your life like eating what nature provides,greeting the sun and the moon.Tuning into the unique phases of the moons lessons, walking in the morning dew, sleeping on the earth, breathing fresh air, bathing in the cold streams and waterfalls., fishing and taking a deer once a month....that was my life for a year or 2. We would come back into town to get supplies every 3 months, hitch hike back to the beginning of a well studied trail head and back pack the rest of the time....with mother earth as our teacher and book or 2, for our entertainment. How did a 17 year old Jewish, honors student find her way to this life as a squaw with her own personal brave to learn together about how to live in harmony with nature. Well, I didn't just drop from the sky as a 17 year old.....so now to share some of the earlier....scarier parts of my life.

I was born in Youngstown, Ohio in 1952. My mother of Jewish Polish/Lithuanian descent, and my father of Jewish Russian/Ashkenazi decent. My mother had a first miss carriage at 31 and told that she could not get pregnant. She did not take her doctor for his word and soon after began having her string of 3 daughters. I was the oldest....and was quite sickly. That didn't stop me from taking over many mother responsibilities. Our home was coal burning. lead piped and lead painted, my mother smoked and had a mouth full of mercury fillings. She had many operations and was ill often. The basement and attic were moldy (I liked playing in those rooms) I was quite sickly, had psoriasis and sleep walked.

I had a string of kidney infections as well as strep throat. I spent more time sick than in school and by the time I was 10 had tonsils and adenoids removed, cystoscoptomy and was obese. I received my first lessons about light for healing at 9 and 10. Light treatment were my solace with the psoriasis. I hated the tar baths. My uncle was a highly respected pediatrician who made sure that we were well stocked with little bottles filled with think pink antibiotics. I used so many that I was told where they were and to help myself if I felt poorly in the morning.

2nd Light lesson, My father was told that he needed to take me to Florida every winter or move to California. There were openings for school teachers in Southern California...so off we went. My skin cleared up and I never had another infection.

I was quite precocious in many ways, menses began shortly after moving to California while still 10. Looked like a fully developed woman by 12. And by 14, found mediation, yoga, Hermann Hesse, vegetarianism and health food stores. I found the work of Rudolf Steiner and Anthroposphical medicine at 17 as well as my backpacking buddy.

Next amazing lesson..this one about electricity and our body electric. After coming back to "so called civilization" after a few years of back packing, I found that I just could not sleep in the house. In the early 70's all we had in the house was electricity, no cell phones, possibly not even microwaves but my whole body would jangle from the electricity in the house. I had to sleep outside on the lawn. Every time we would come in to town for supplies I would be shocked as I looked in the mirror and to my great surprise was a slender young woman. I literally had my pants tied on with a piece of sinew from one of the deer.

With out going into all my wild stories of living in spiritual communities and monasteries...next phase was leaving the mountains of northern California and off to Hawaii where I gave birth to a beautiful son under a banana tree in Waimanolo HI. He was on my body 24/7 and I did not cut his cord for about 3 days (lotus Birth) Most people have know idea how blissful a birth one can have. I was pretty healthy. I left my tipi on Mt. Shasta in October 1975 and spent my days working on a little Philipino farm's garden where I was invited to stay or floating in the ocean. No back pain for me...although Shiloh was almost 9 lbs.

My son has been remarkably healthy. He is 43 and to the best of my knowledge he has not had an antibiotic til at least 40. Possibly never. No vaccinations or meds. When I came back and embraced the modern world my weight came on and just would not come off. My spirituality at the time was such that I ate no meat. Went through a 7 year period of time as a raw food vegan including 2 years as a fruitarian.

That ended when I was diagnosed with colon cancer and found my way to the Kelly Program and Bill Wolcott. My program included far more balanced eating, more pancreatic enzymes than one could ever imagine 2 coffee enemas plus daily numerous different cleanses and Sun/Earth time. This was not the first time that Sun and Earth time was brought to my attention as an important part of healing.....but my focus was on the diet and cleanses. (I really needed Jack to help me get it) When a teenager, I studied the Essenes (Raw food vegans) but important training on being on the earth and looking in the direction of the sun and using prescriptions for sunning as an important part of almost any healing recommendation. I also was part of a community for a short time in Santa Barbara called The Brotherhood of the Sun where sunrise and set were a very important part of our meditation including specific initiation for breathing the sun into different body parts for next

### MeridianGrace — 2017-09-07T00:56:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meridians-health-journey.20109/post-223021>

More to share. Sorry guys, I have been waiting for more than 2 months to figure out how to do this. I must have expressed the cancer really early because I have never had problems since. I did the program for more than 2 years....and many might say until today since detoxifying has been a part of my life ever since.

My passion for all things that might be healing has been with me since 14, leading me to European alternative doctors. Between my husband an alternative doctor who lost his permit, previous board certified anesthesiologist, who went through pharmacy school before Med school....we have just about every treatment and testing device that you would find in European or Russian alternative clinics. I loved Dr. Dinshaws light therapy. I discovered Dr. Khlinghardts work early on and kept up with all the paradigm expanding things that he taught with lasers, LED's etc. At some point I would like to see if you all have heard about something called regulation thermometry. I would rather call it a thermal bio-scan. It shows us comparable thermal scan on the surface of the skin before and after a cold challenge to see how capable the autonomic nervous system is through different systems in the body. Also used as non invasive test to look for early signs of breast, ovarian or prostate cancer, as well as seeing if we can see an up-regulation of the autonomic nervous system with the treatment chosen for the patient. Through the many years in this field I have met some people with profound brilliance and major contributions to light and energy healing and testing. One was a pHd. student of Dr. Fritz Poff who developed frequency equipment. I have one of his pieces. Dr. Becker conducted his experiment to grow bone on one of my mentors leg that had been afflicted with osteomyelitis and osteo sarcoma. To say the least...I was on a quest for devices that could do that. Would so love to speak to people who are interested in light for healing.

Now for updates. I have been symptom free for many years....but up until this last couple of years where I focused on ketosis, and then Jacks work, I just couldn't get the last 20 lbs. off. Still have 10 lbs to go....but honestly...no one would say I was overweight for the last 5 years. I am 5'5" and my weight fluctuates every month dependent on the moon phase from 123 to 131. Before Jacks work 127 to 137. Just had a goal number all those years (when 200 and couldn't seem to break 150).. The number 117. We shall see if it happens.

Here is what I am doing. Getting up before the sun and putting up some herbal tea with medicinal mushrooms and sea weed, heat up the coffee for enema and head outside in my bikini to meet the sun. Rich joins me now...it is a lovely time a day for us. I have been working really hard at eating something in the morning. I have never been hungry till early afternoon....usually about 2 or 3. It is hard for me to eat then but I usually get down a can of sardines nibbling them over an hour and then slice a half an avocado, sometimes a drink with fresh green juice (only vegies) 1 - 2 Tablespoon of fresh raw liver, 2 raw eggs and 2 Tablespoons of grass fed cream fraische. I add some green leaf stevia and blend it. Add cold water to taste and it is surprisingly delicious. (especially using a large proportion of raw fennel bulb) in the juice. Other times will have a couple of eggs cooked in home made ghee with yolks left runny. Our back yard ends at a 10-15 foot deep spring fed creek. We swim most every day. Some times numerous times a day. The temp. is great now for a non mitochondriac....but too warm for doing CT. It gets quite cold in the winter but not sure if cold enough. It is so gorgeous in the water, lots of heron, egret, turtles and red-shouldered hawks. ...but I am looking at getting a 5 Cu' freezer to put outside and take down to 55 degrees. My body type is very trim from the waist up but fuller from the waist to the knees. I would like to sit in the water. I have been spending 10 minutes every day with a snorkle with my face in ice water.

I do the face CT because of my biggest challenge I have yet to tell you. Today I will start with the short story. Appearing perfectly healthy i attracted calcium to my aortic valve. My cardiologist and surgeon felt that I had the most stenosed aortic valve that they had every seen. I had no symptoms other than huffing and puffing at the top end of a steep hike and my oxygen saturation would go down jumping on our rebounder. Rich could hear my murmur with out a stethoscope from the other side of the bed. I did have the surgery by a truly brilliant cardiac surgeon. The surgery went well, I was walking steps 2nd day after the surgery and sent home. The side effect was a pump head syndrome. I have lost a lot of nouns. I started researching everything I could find on brain and mitochondria. I learned about mitochondria/ketosis and it helped.But I am at a whole new level since I started following your work. After a while Jack my blood sugar went up and insulin went down.) My only carbs were lowcarb vegies and what ever carbs were in my supplements. Occasionally I would eat a few black berries or blue berries. Because of the type 1 and 2 diabetes in my family I was concerned and confused about how this could be. The numbers always look better in a blood work up from the clinic than from my home glucometer....but lets just say that they have gotten to over 145 with out so much as a blue berry or tortilla chip in a year, while my insulin numbers went down. Wondering if there is something that I need to do for my beta cells. Looking at treating my self with homeopathic beta cells and pancreas.

The interesting thing is that my blood sugar has been consistently going down since sun and CT. My glucometer read 112 this morning and my blood work from the doc last week said 91 even though at home before I went to the office it was 116. I did a 4 spot insulin test with blood glucose challenge and it looks like I may be a bit in the type 1 range,

The other life long thing I have not been successful with is my stool. I have had loose yellow stool as long as I can remember. I'm doing some stool testing and a GI map soon. My sister has Crones disease and her biopsy showed celiac. Our family all has it. Will check my 23 and me...to see if I have 2 or 3 copies. Haven't eaten any grain in a number of years. I will do another ASI and HS CRP and vitamin D test soon. Can send you a copy of my most recent blood work, waiting for my reverse t3 to come back. My numbers keep getting better, so would rather not take any cytamel if I can function great with out it. I have been taking morning pregnenolone and licorice. My sleep has always been good..but not remembering dreams. I have full color dreams but don't remember details shortly after I wake. I have been doing WileyProtocol hormones for about 5 years. If you don't know about her work will fill you in. I have reduced myself a wee bit and wondering if I can get away with continuing to reduce if I keep up morning sun and CT and still have my youthful, juicy energy and sex drive. We enjoy it. Rich and I have been in quandary over weight and this body. I can water fast and gain weight and a few days later when I stop and am really hungry eat more than usual and loose weight. In my early Essene days I would do 21 day water fasts at the change of each season....sometimes not loosing any more than 1/2 lb over the whole expanse of the time. I am no longer fat.....far from it....but with my history it is a little scary to think that an effort would not take off a few lbs. I use it as an opportunity to be grateful for this miraculous body no matter the size. Just would like to feel that my efforts are rewarded too. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

# Thread: Bohdan's down under optimal journal

### Bohdan — 2017-09-12T13:55:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bohdans-down-under-optimal-journal.20138/post-223436>

Great chance for me to start off the journal not long after I've had a range of diagnostics done:

1.  Whole Blood toxic and essential elements (Doctor's Data)
2.  DUTCH (dried urine test for comprehensice hormones), which also included tests for Melatonin and Oxidative Stress (8-OHdG)
3.  Other "pathology" tests - thyroid function, CRP, Vit D etc etc

Includng the diagnostic files as pat of my record.

Some really interesting observations/red flags for me (had discussion with my practitioner):

1.  There's a whole suite of minerals (lithium, selenium, magnesium, manganese, etc) that is falling on lower quartile. To me suggestive of maybe a genetic/SNP issue; plan to do 123andMe sequencing.
2.  Some v high hormone levels - cortisol and estrogen
3.  V High oxidative stress

Have been trying to practice (though not consistently) some of the protocols Javk has mentioned, such as morning sunlight and blueblockers at night, but it seems i need to double down on the advice. More morning light exposure seems to be a good start for this.

# Thread: Build Better Hormones Naturally!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/build-better-hormones-naturally.20158/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kathy1967 — 2017-09-15T19:14:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/build-better-hormones-naturally.20158/post-223737>

Hi All, I would love your opinion on getting great hormones without supplementing! I just had labs done through Life Extension & was advised by them that I could supplement with 15mg Progesterone & 50mg Pregnenolone to optimize my hormones. I'm not completely opposed to supplementing but first I want to give my body about 3 -4 months going the all natural route. My idea is to get back into CT consistently with cold water baths (around 65 Fahrenheit for about 30 minutes) and ice baths (50ish Fahrenheit for about 15-20 minutes) 3 - 5 times weekly. I get at least 10 minutes of am sun every morning. I'm adding 1 tsp of Maca to my morning hot cocoa, starting to take 1 tsp of celery seed powder 3x daily to help with my adrenals, and continuing with my ketogenic diet. I have grass fed dairy frequently (sour cream, heavy cream, butter). My waist has expanded about 2 -3 inches in the past 3 years. I'm almost 50, my cycle is perfectly 28 days still. Here are my most recent labs. Any and all advice will be so appreciated!!!!

Day 21 of my cycle
Testosterone, serum - 12 ng/dL
Testosterone, Free - .06 pg/mL
DHEA sulfate - 137.6 ug/dL
Estradiol - 101.7 pg/mL
Progesterone - 8.2 ng/mL

Day 3 of my cycle
DHEA (not sulfate) - 173 ng/dL
progesterone - .5 - ng/mL
Estrogens, Total - 81 pg/mL
Testosterone, serum - 11 ng/dL
Testosterone, Free - 1 pg/mL
Pregnenolone, Ms - <10 ng/dL
TSH-ICMA - 1.2 uU/mL
Free T4 by Dialysis/Mass Spec - 1.1 ng/dL

### nonchalant — 2017-09-15T20:04:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/build-better-hormones-naturally.20158/post-223741>

I would say a lot more sun. When do you catch the sunlight? When it is still red or orange? I would say you need at least an hour of sunlight before the UV light shows up, because that's when the pituitary makes those hormones.

### Kathy1967 — 2017-09-15T20:22:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/build-better-hormones-naturally.20158/post-223743>

Thanks so much Nonchalant! I live in NJ and have to be at work at 7:15am. I usually have about 15 minutes that I can stand outside at work before I need to go in, I work at a lake and I put my feet in there often! It is not still red or orange by time I get to work but actually with the sun rising later now it will be! I'll make sure though to get up earlier and catch the sun at home. SO I'll add in more sun and retest in about 3 months. I'm kind of worried about starting supplemental hormones and then NEEDING to keep supplementing!

# Thread: Chris' Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris-journal.20166/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cjmorin — 2017-09-18T21:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris-journal.20166/post-223921>

Hello everybody!

I'm Chris, and have been lurking here for the past three months or so, absorbing much more information (and consequently full-spectrum sunshine) than I have in a long time. I value the communication that happens here and the true desire of other members to pursue their own personal growth while supporting the growth of one another! I am looking forward to adding to the discourse as time goes by.

**About Me**
I'm Chris, a 32 year old massage therapist, personal trainer and I have been a nutrition and health junkie for the better part of 14 years. I am tall, fair skinned with light blue eyes and medium brown hair, British and Italian heritage, as well as a bit of Native American in there. My 23&Me report says that I have more Neanderthal in me than most (ooga!).

I am generally quite energetic, I am physically fit and strong, very funny, quick-witted and engaging and have always enjoyed learning new things and keeping up on current science. I have also struggled for most of my adult life with social anxiety, depression, making and keeping friends and other fear/panic related challenges and low dopamine-type behaviors like alcohol and sex addiction and the never ending quest for "more" or "better".

This very well may have a lot to do with my birth trauma (more below), my job (picking up energetic baggage like some sort of Tantric trash man) and undoubtedly with my less-than-stellar environment (raised on video games, voluntarily surrounded myself with innovative lighting products that not only incorporate LEDs but also BT and WIFI!!, and I'm a recovering blue-light-at-night addict, all in the ironic pursuit of learning about health!)

Just last week I had a series of what I believe were my first real panic attacks, that is, the sudden and overwhelming feeling of intense anxiety and dread that started in my solar plexus and moved up into my chest, heart and head, leading to what felt like AFib and the convincing notion that I was surely going to keel over and croak at any second. This has happened only a handful of times so far, but my neurological system feels like it is now hyper vigilant and I have been feeling strong negative effects from stimuli that normally do not hit me so drastically (things like smoking weed, even strains with very low THC, staying up too late, eating crap foods, etc.)

Admittedly, I did not originally come here seeking answers to these problems, but found the site through a search on DHEA after reading much about it on the Ray Peat forums. I definitely feel as though my neuroses are directly linked to the fact that I have essentially become a creebling blue-lit cave dwarf that is relegated to a life of watching other people through electronic portals rather than actually meeting them in person. Isn't that what the kids are all about, nowadays?

My sleep is often quite terrible and I do not fall asleep quickly or get very tired until late at night. This has been a constant struggle for me.

Libido is high as it ever was, and perhaps even more so since experimenting with a few upstream topical hormones a la Peat. I will be tapering this off as I become more thoroughly steeped in the lessons here. I recognize (albeit not in an entirely graceful way, yet) that hormones are essentially a chemical translation of light and circadian rhythm signals. Thus, any exogenous hormones, aside from the fundamental building blocks provided by diet, should not be required as I become more sensitive to the natural cycles around me and produce what is needed inside.

**My Grandmother's History**
I know little about her or her life experience, but she did not seem to be a very resilient or energetic woman, and was prone towards narcissism. She liked to show us clothing and things that she bought for herself and pictures of where she went. I tend to think she probably suffered from social anxiety and some type of mood disorder.

She moved to Florida after having a double mastectomy and bypass surgery and lived for several more years, eventually passing in her late 70's or early 80's. I have to imagine that even the unintentional intense sunlight helped to prolong her life after her move.

**My Mother's History**
My mom is a lovely woman who has thankfully not struggled with many health-related challenges for her 70 years of life so far. She had a child at 18, another at 23 and finally me (with my dad) at 38. She has mentioned in passing that she had pretty severe postpartum depression after giving birth to me, and the process itself was not without it's challenges. I was breach, strangled by the umbilical cord and essentially my brain was dead, all before I even started. Not the best introduction to the world.

I believe that she has stayed healthy for so long because of her intense love of nature and animals, intentional time spent outside and lots of love and laughter at home. It was not without it's challenges, as helping to run a family business (as I was doing for the last decade or so) with my father is extremely stressful at times.

Lately, she has only really suffered from issues with balance and dizziness. I think this may be due to something in her inner ear, and perhaps more frequent usage of technology and nnEMF. She has become sort of a gadget fiend lately, too, finally matching my father's love of all things technological.

**My Initial Steps**
I just recently moved to Portland, ME from the Berkshires in NW MA. My girlfriend and I love it here, and I value the availability of fresh seafood and summertime sunshine. That said, the window for this season is quickly closing, and I am looking forward to getting more involved in CT this winter to compensate for it. I am currently seeking a very large animal drinking trough or watertight barrel to use as my CT lab this winter!

We live on the third floor of a three apartment house in a suburban neighborhood in a relatively dense school district. I do not know the light/nnEMF conditions for my downstairs neighbors, or if the house has a Smart Meter. I do know that I can pick up only 4 WIFI networks including my own from where we usually spend time. Those other signals are quite weak, as well, for which I am thankful.

I have replaced all but a few hardwired lamps with 2200K Edison-style incandescent bulbs, most on dimmers, for additional control.

I have begun turning off Wifi and Cable modem before bed.

We do not watch TV ever, but I work on my phone many hours per day and we occasionally watch movies on a 10' projector screen in ourliving room (lettin that Geek flag fly). I wear blue blocker sunglasses during any nighttime activity, especially those involving blue light like movie time. I downregulate blue as much as possible on all my devices, although I know this is only partially effective.

I use an app that is ironically called Appblocker to completely eliminate my usage of devices at night past 9pm. I even had my girlfriend set the password so that I cannot uninstall it! We set our phones on airplane at night or turn them completely off.

I keep my cell on airplane mode when in my pocket for extended periods of time.

I am in the process of reading John Ott's book and have already taken the time to watch several videos regarding his observations...fascinating stuff. I was browsing earlier and learning about potential cancer-promoting effects of "pink" light and thought a lot about the effects that colors and light frequencies have on us. I tend to wear very natural colors, drab or earthy tones with a bias towards green, blue or gray.

I have a couple NIR bulbs that I like to spend time under before bed. We own a fancy-schmancy Biomat, which I use in small doses, as I feel pretty crummy if I am on it too long while dehydrated.

I tried keto about a year ago during the winter and it did not serve me so well, and based on what I have read here so far I am starting to see why...poor sleep regulation, poor hydration from poor quality water, lack of frequent cold, terrible availability of natural light, little to no integration of DHA and mineral-rich foods from the sea, etc. I plan to try again this winter with the incorporation of loads of seafood, better hydration with RO water and a large reduction in nnEMF and out-of-cycle blue light.

I plan to install a countertop RO system so that I can better control my ingestion of fluoride. I have already been using a ZenWater system, which seems to be pretty fantastic for just about everything but Fluoride, so it's time to step up.

**My Concerns Moving Forward**
I am worried about this long Maine winter. Portland is at 43.6 degrees, which is higher than I have ever lived long-term, but not by much. That said, I believe that my fair complexion and pale eyes will make the most out of any light that is provided in this environment, and will do my best to expose my body when there are appropriate opportunities to do so.

I have considered getting a tanning membership, but lack the devices needed to check for nnEMF in the beds. I really would rather avoid tanning, but also cannot really lie out naked anywhere around my apartment to maximize wintertime light absorption. Guidance on this point is appreciated.

Thanks to Dr. Jack for his brilliant and nuanced insights into the true functioning of the world! I feel like I have been preparing to read these blogs for the last decade. Thanks again to all the highly active members here whose observations and n=1s have been so thoughtfully shared!

Chris


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Coming to a new beginning
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-to-a-new-beginning.20230/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sufoto — 2017-10-03T06:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-to-a-new-beginning.20230/post-224737>

It has been about a month since I began to explore the ideas put forth by Jack Kruse. Read his book. Getting up early to greet the day. Finding the sun. Looking for the sun light. Wearing my blue blockers while on the computer.
My basic info

An older woman ( a grandmother) who still feels like she is in her forties or maybe fifty. I am not retired rather I am a working person who has an on-going career.

I have noticed inflammation lately and high blood pressure for the past few years
.
The high blood pressure comes from my mother and maternal family. Mother had congestive heart failure and died too young at 78. Mother had pneumonia and pleurisy as an infant leaving her with weak lungs and she had many bouts on pneumonia during her life. Smoking for most her life did nothing to help.

Her mother died of heart failure as well in her 70's.

Father died of a heart attack even younger - in his late 50s.

I have managed my blood pressure by diet and exercise. I was doing well without meds until very recently.
Then in June was at the gym and blood pressure spiked and had me seeing double so I went to the ER. I thought I might be having a stroke. I wasn't and didn't. My doctor (integrative medicine) tried several different BP meds all of which made me feel worse. I cut them in half and then could barely tolerate them.
The last time I took them was several weeks ago. I recently took my BP and it was 160/82. Now trying to stay away from caffeine. Eating a Big Ass Breakfast. Getting the light.

Only last week I found that I had had a smart meter put on the house while I was in California in June. I think this may account for the spikes in BP and am addressing the meter with my electric company. I will have an opt out. Soon I hope!!

There is much more to add but it's getting past my bedtime. (This used to be an early bedtime)
So I will continue this tomorrow.

### Sufoto — 2017-10-04T04:24:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-to-a-new-beginning.20230/post-224879>

More

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=224777)
>
> welcome.

Good to be here!

### Sufoto — 2017-10-04T04:43:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-to-a-new-beginning.20230/post-224882>

Where I reside: Columbus, North Carolina. 35° 15′ 2″ N, 82° 12′ 8″ W
We have lived here since October 2005. The town has just under a 1000 people. We live in a house on 8 acres of mostly hillside forest. The three bedroom house is two stories. The main story is upstairs and the basement level built out and if I want to be "grounded" I would need to be downstairs. My office is downstairs, inside the wall where the meters are. This is not something I gave thought to , at the time.
The news on the smart meter. I spoke with them today. The electric company will remove the smart meter. It will cost $50 to remove it and $11 a month to have a meter reader human come to read it each month. But I am happy to do that to save my body.
Trying to find a place where the light in the morning and the grounding can happen. we have a deck that gets early light through the trees. But nowhere on the ground and because of the trees very little light on the ground until the sun is high. This is a dilemma I am trying to resolve. Forgot to put in the elevation of the town which is 1100 feet.

### Sufoto — 2017-10-04T05:07:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-to-a-new-beginning.20230/post-224889>

Trying the hardest to get the most light early in my eyes. My eye doc in NYC who wrote the book "Total VIsion", Richard Kavner, recommends looking at the clear blue sky. I used to do that and I worked with him on my eyes' convergence, a problem I didn't know I had but it was significantly improved with his exercises.
We had a well put in when we had been here a year and the water seems fine when we have had it tested. No chemicals.
Took me a time to find out that there is radon naturally occuring in this area and we had a very minimal reading in the cellar so put in the device to vent it.
Also making an effort to eat paleo. Pastured meat. (Recently found Butcher Box). No gluten for quite a few years and had been grain free but fell off that now going back to grain free. Starting the CT with the iced face in the sink which I did years ago when I read it was something that Paul Newman did every day. Kept him looking pretty great. Ah, vanity!
And I am reading reading reading to get up to speed.

# Thread: Wendy's journey..to her journal.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wendys-journey-to-her-journal.20356/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Wendy Harris — 2017-10-28T21:21:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wendys-journey-to-her-journal.20356/post-226838>

Hello ( second attempt.I "spellchecked" and lost the first journal...so this might be grammatically bad and under spelt ! )....

I have always been fit and fairly well ( aside from stomach issues from 20 onwards..took the NHS 17 years to diagnose duodenal ulcers, helico bacteria etc ).

12 years ago...
Had a funny turn one night ( never found out what that was, even after investigation but suffered bad insomnia and maybe it was part of that.)
Enter a spinal tap ( lumbar puncture in UK speak ) that leaked. It was left too late for a blood patch and it was pretty bad.
From there I had head pains that made me feel suicidal ( honestly ) that lasted for 11 years.
In that space of time my poor head would twitch ( not a good look ) and I was given numerous awful drugs and steroid injections. And more steroid injections plus botox into the skull, which crept and I couldn't swallow for nearly 4 weeks.
By then my whole back joined in the party..I had ( and still do ) awful upper trap spasms ( imagine Arnie here on a 5'2" woman. ) apparently this was pulling my scalp back.
And then the rest of my back muscles just didn't want to work properly.

I had awful shoulder issues and two operations..first for crystal formation and second for torn tendon ( from spur bones ) plus the bursa was infected and I had oedema into the bone marrow.
Boy I hurt so much.
The interesting thing here is that I was an IT project manager and had at least 3 PC screens open at one time on my desk, with a mobile phone constantly on the go plus a head set for the other ear...and yes in an open plan " modern" lite office.
BLUE LIGHT EVERY WHERE..plus all the defence antennas ( I worked for a global communication company that managed among other things, ministry of defence communications ) were on the roof and my office was at the top of the building.
All the hardware to run half the booming country was whizzing and pinging around me.
NO WONDER I COULD NEVER GET BETTER.
My body just couldn't regenerate.
Battery was flat.

After 4 years of me dragging myself from doctor to doctor a neuro surgeon said that I had 3 foramen in my neck that needed opening but he would only operate on one of them.
So..C4/c5 was opened and boy my head was 60% better.

So, I carried on with my fitness classes again and tried to be normal.
I was given drugs for the remaining pain- BuTrans patches, pregabalin and amitripyline ( which I stopped pretty quickly ) and a few drugs which I believed were for spasms but were anti depressants.
I was in pain and felt stoned and horrid all the time.
And then I just had to give up work...I loved my job ( a little woman telling loads of men what to do ..) I took my pension very early and just tried to get myself well again.
I tried all the exercise cures you can think of. And physio and osteotheraphy and nothing stopped the pains or spasms, I was in bed every night by 6am...with sleeping pills ( zopiclone ) to knock me out.
What a life ah ??

I decided if nothing else, I would stop all the drugs.
Pregabalin took me years to get off even on a minimum dose. Ended up in ER twice.
And then I stopped being able to eat ..lost 25 LBS in a month and felt so ill.
After tests they found SIBO, the belief is that it was down to the drugs.
Tried FODMAP and nothing worked.

And then I sequestered a disc in my lower back..boy that hurt, a piece of disc broke off and got stuck in the foramen ...I declined surgery but they said I had scolosis ( 28 degrees ) and I was always going to slip a disc.
I had no idea of any of these things. Maybe some knowledge might have stopped me doing some of my fitness workouts. ( some of them were pretty fast and furious ).

Then I was told I needed a VBT op on my lower back ( nuts and screws to straightened the little curve in the base)I asked if this would stop my head pains ( now back to 100% ) and my spasms that were never ending.
Nope was the answer.
I was so knocked out by all it by now.
Had rotten sciatica and muscle spasms in the leg at night ( still have that )
I ate well ( or so I thought, was fit, or so I thought and walked a lot and got out into the sunshine when I could - I live in London area ).

And then I met a young man ( no, not what you're thinking ) at a breath test for SIBO ( he'd broken his back in an accident and the morphine was believed to have caused his issues ).
And lo and behold ( it's a 3 hour test, so a long chat ), he helped people rehab their bodies.
He had had scolosis and fixed it, was having the metal work removed from his broken back as he had strengthened the muscles to do the job ( took a year ) and we talked about VBT..he said if I had it done all it would do is move the problem further up the spine..I had to get my movements right.
He uses the system of Movement for Humans - Naudi Aquilar.
So, he agreed ( arm twisted up his back ) to see me. He usually sees athletes and I must have been his token old person !
But this last year I have learnt so much.
It seems my 30 odd years of yoga were not right for my body..I am hyper mobile and I've over stretched and loosened my joints.
Plus pilates , body combat, weight training et al...all not good.
To think I could have stayed home, watched tv, ate chocolate and been fitter ! Life is funny.

I have managed to decrease the degree in my scolosis and my back is stronger.
And then after a while we discussed my continuing pains and my numb fingers and my arm pains and I showed him my latest MRI scans, and he suggested I return to my neurosurgeon as he couldn't fix the pinched nerves in my neck.
Seems I had stenosis at 2 levels in my neck in 4 joints.
No wonder I hurt...the surgeon said this shouldn't cause head pain, but the left side was completed closed around the nerves.
Had the op and head pain is 90 % better and I am now trying to ease the pains from my relentless upper trap spasm with exercise drills most days.
But fingers and arms are fine now.

PATH HERE........
The funny thing is, I had the op in March this year and Gordon, the young man, said watch this Utube of Naudi Aquilar as you have time ( no exercises for a few weeks ) and I did and what a wonderful utube it was...as I meet JACK KRUSE on that interview and I was totally in awe of the things he had to say. I was nodding and agreeing and thinking of cause, of cause...and I immediately read EPi Paleo ooh and realised I had a leaky gut.
I have osteopenia and could never understand why ( my D levels were okay and I weight trained and walked and moved ).
So I started to eat meat ( not done so for years but always ate fish..I love fish ) and dropped diary and night shades. Started K2.

And I had had high liver reading on previous blood work, but the last test was back to normal and the doctor asked how I had done that..I said Epi paleo, read it and learn. ( little women are bossy like that ).

I still have chronic pains ( but so much improved since the operation ) and am trying CT showers.
Read John Ott and was astounded that governments stand back while the people kill themselves with technology ( And I was a person who put the platforms in to run the bloody technology ).
Just got a copy of Robert O Becker.
I turn my wifi off at night, phone off too.
Stopped taking my phone out with me and looking at it in the mornings. Never use it to speak on.
I use PC for an hour a day ( to view this site actually and to read JK's FB bloggs).
I watch the sun in the morning, not sun rise as I have houses blocking that path, but I ground on the patio or grass and breath the light into my eyes and body...it actually feels so liberating).

So many paths have lead me here...and it took me along time to find you guys but I know the answer to my health and pain free living are here.
Thank you for listening to my tales.

I don't take any drugs. ( yippee )
I do take supplements as I know I am not absorbing well.

K2.
Sulgar V2000 multi.
Magnesium
Milk thistle ( liver )
Goto Kola
EC ( digestive enzymes)

Bio identical hormone cream
T alpha ( testosterone ) - short course to help the muscles.

Hope the speaking isn't too bad - I won't risk it again.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Maguel's optimal journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maguels-optimal-journal.20545/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Maguel — 2017-12-05T16:10:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maguels-optimal-journal.20545/post-229539>

Hello, it certainly feels good to be here. Listening to Dr Jack talk to Nathan the other night he suggested recording details of one's first 6 years to start the journal so briefly here they are...

I was born naturally at home on the shores of Lake Victoria, Tanzania to a mother of German/Jewish stock and a British father. My two grandfathers lives were cut short whilst my one grandmother died of cancer. My parents are in pretty good health at ages 90 and 87. We moved to the coast, on the Indian Ocean, soon after my birth and my first 6 years were happily spent mostly barefoot and semi-naked in our garden, with a view out to sea, along with 30 odd ducks, 2 dogs, a cat and several tortoises and guinea pigs.

My paradise was shattered however at age 6 when I was sent away to a Catholic boarding school. Luckily I was only there one year before we moved to the UK but in that time I was sexually abused by the priests. The trauma of this experience was such that the memories were completely repressed into my unconscious. I always knew something was wrong with me however, my inner sadness and inability to express real joy, and 50 years later I finally found someone who helped me recover the repressed memories and so start to heal my psyche.

That was earlier this year and just a couple of month's ago I discovered Dr Kruse. What a revelation. Some of what he writes about I always knew instinctively - loving the sun, going barefoot, circadian rythms - but never knew just how important these things are. Nor could I argue with or convince anyone of what I felt.

So looking back I see that my whole life has led to this point. My relentless search for meaning and the reasons for my dis-ease with myself has brought me here. I consider myself pretty healthy but my passion now is for optimal health - mental / emotional and physical. At the same time to share with others and hopefully help to heal the broken human condition itself.

I have no major health issue I am aware of but the one thing that has irritated me all my life is dry skin. If I could fix that I would be happy so if anyone has any suggestions?!

Thank you Dr Kruse and everyone in your support team.

Maguel

### Lahelada — 2017-12-05T16:21:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maguels-optimal-journal.20545/post-229540>

Welcome !!
Regarding your dry skin,in isolation it would be difficult to say anything about it. One thing to think about though is looking at where you live with regards to EMF and light pollution.
