Is dry skin connected to redox? I'm thinking about nnemf definitely
Did you measure your environment?

### Maguel — 2017-12-05T17:39:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maguels-optimal-journal.20545/post-229549>

Thanks both. I have had dry skin all my life and have lived in both Africa and northern Europe and Brazil. Have to say though that it did improve in Brazil, on the equator, with plenty of sunshine, sea, fruit and fresh coconuts. Currently I am living in Wales but planning to be on the road much of next year.

### Maguel — 2018-02-25T09:43:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maguels-optimal-journal.20545/post-235681>

So, I am now in northern Botswana for 3 months - Lat 20S; Long 23.4 E; Alt 3,100 ft. These are my health intentions. Any comments or advice appreciated...

1\. Watch the sunrise and sunset daily and be outdoors barefoot and undressed as much as possible.
2\. Drink freshly milked goats milk.
3\. Eat freshly laid eggs.
4\. Eat local free range beef.
5\. Drink natural borehole water.
6\. Drink South African red wine.
7\. Swim for exercise.
8\. Spend time with good friends.

# Thread: Jan and Sam’s optimum journal.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Janet Shold — 2017-12-21T06:49:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/post-230773>

I am Janet, a 58 year old mom and grandmother. Sam is my 15 year old daughter, so I’ll be her journalist.

Our haplogroup group is H1A1.

We both seem to have Lyme disease, and anybody familiar with that know that we are always on a search to find ways to fix that. Sam also has hashimotos and vitiligo, both are autoimmune issues. I’ve always had the feeling that Lyme is just something that takes advantage of a modern human who is not in optimal condition, and the failure to be optimal is in our lifestyle. Throwing antibiotics at the one organism (who is clever enough hide and morph And persist just isn’t the answer. That’s my gut feeling, and I feel it bears out in research and experience.

**Healing the Gut**
When I read a few of jack’s posts and blogs, something just clicked. Immediately we got supplies for healing the gut, and are still on that. Wow. Nice, regular poopies. Never underestimate the health power behind a good poop!

**Morning Sun**
Next, we started watching the sun rise. We love the time we spend doing that, as the winter birds are in the yard and surrounding shrubs and trees, and it just feels so good to be out there with them and with the sun. Now, we live on Vancouver island, so we get weeks of cloud.

**Red Fish Blue Fish**
Because we can’t see the morning light everyday, we hack light by avoiding blue light. Blue blocking glasses after sundown and we read by the light of red led bulbs. Holy cow, this alone has Changed our world. LET ME SAY that removing blue light, using blue blockers and reading in the evenings by red light only has already made a profound change for us. Sam (daughter) has been plagued from birth with incredible insomnia. Many nights she would be awake until daylight. She was constantly, chronically unable to maintain a healthy circadian rhythm. After a week of minimizing blue light and using blue blockers and red after sundown this girl is sleeping at night and waking with the sun. As for myself, I’m waking every day at 6:15 and I have never been anything but a “night owl”. **Sperti Red.** We just got the red Sperti for getting vitamin d. We haven’t had blood d levels taken yet, though. I have a sun burn. I got a little carried away. My bad.

**Lugols**
My daughter and I have both been taking lugols 37.5 mg a day, for a couple of years. I’ve wondered about increasing to 50 or more.

**Cold adaption**
We did some face dunking, daughter found it hard. I am working with her to get cold adapted, but it’s going to take a lot of encouragement. Repeating all the wonderful benefits of it is helping, because she is very motivated.

I have been taking cold showers daily for 9 days so far, and also cold packs 3 times a week. I find the showers are easier to tolerate if I leave my feet in a pool of relatively warm water. So I’ll do the cleaning under tepid 70f water with the plug in, and then lower the temp in increments until it’s at 45-55. The cold packs are already a park of my freezing to numb before bee stings for the bee venom therapy for Lyme, Ive just added more, larger jel packs! Perhaps once we are more accomplished motichondriacs (dare I say black swans?), I can forget the bee venom, because our bodies will be able to take care of things without it.

The cold showers are hard to do, but the payback is enormous! After a shower one has energy, a clear mind, the aches and pains are gone or diminished. The energy from a cold shower seems to last for hours and hours. I can’t wait to start using a tub and then the nearby ocean and glacier fed lake.

**Diet**
Our diet is low carb, but not absolutely keto just yet. Adding in the seafood.

\- I’m shopping for a nice sized livestock water trough for some good and icey soaks.

\- I’m getting ready to join and have access to all the information. The incredible changes we have already experience with light and cold is just WOW!

\*\*\*\* Thank you for being here, jack. Thank you, experience members for your input, help and guidance\*\*\*\*

### caroline — 2017-12-21T08:04:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/post-230778>

You are doing a fantastic job Janet - for you and your daughter! You are indeed a black Swan - in my opinion.

Have you thought of taking your daughter camping? or maybe a cabin in the woods? maybe she would put her feet in a cold stream? Nothing pre meditated ...just something fun to do.

One of the ladies here - Freesia - did amazing things for herself with C/T and had her kiddos doing it as well. They were allowed to shout/use a really bad word of there choice. Swearing and using disgusting language makes C/T so much easier!

Maybe plan a trip to the Yucatan ......

### Janet Shold — 2017-12-31T18:04:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/post-231342>

The last question in the Vermont talk about vitiligo made me shit the bed, just like jack said it would when he answered the question! Sam has several spots of **vitiligo**, and has since around the same time the malasma showed up. I have a black light and an infrared light in her room, and a red Sperti.

We get the morning light, come cloudy or clear weather. I need to get her into the cold, and Sam has been very slowly doing it by not using warm water for hand washing, wearing fewer clothes, and using ice packs, but she gets dizzy quickly when showering in **cool** water. I believe this means we have to go very slowly with her. We’ve begun eating seafood regularly.

I am going to get a membership. I’ve been watching and listening to every video and podcast I can find. I may be driving my husband around the bend with this, but I’m insisting he get on board.

### Janet Shold — 2018-01-07T18:03:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/post-231894>

I’m sitting on my iPad which is hard wired to the internet! It was a little tricky, but my husband - a non-geek - figured it out and got it done. Our internet is all hard wired as of yesterday, and the router’s wifi has been disabled. I plan to get a router with no wifi in the near future.

Even my grandchildren are avoiding blue light through apps, and one grandson is using blue blocking glasses while on the computer. I’m going to keep on working on them to get their light situation in the evenings hacked properly.

Sam and I always wear the blue blockers after supper. i turn off lights and light some old oil lamps and candles. A little stumbling block on this is my husband, who seems to need a lot of light before retiring for the night. I’ll keep encouraging him.

Sam is working on the cold thermogenesis and is coming along slowly. We all just went through a flu that is going around so I think that slowed her progress on the cold down. I’ve been doing the daily cold showers, and sometimes getting into the tub after the shower and filling it with cold water and some ice.

We’ve been adding more seafood into our diet, but had a setback last week when we had bad prawns. It took several days to clear that meal out!

Morning light: Vancouver island weather is not cooperating! Cloud, fog, rain...I understand that even when it’s overcast there is about 30% of the morning light there to absorb, but man!

I’m going to sign up as a member with skin in the game, but wonder if I can change my name on the forum to be a little more incognito, first. I don’t think I need to share our personal information with the whole interwebs going forward.

\----------------
**A word about CD57+ testing:**
\-------------------

**Treatment**

1.  **If you are dealing with Lyme, it must be treated with 3 weeks of antibiotics to kill the spirochete bacteria** and then try to clear the biotoxins. When this bacteria is killed, expect a serious Herxheimer reaction and your symptoms to get worse before they get better. There is a treatment option for this, however, to mitigate this biochemcal process.

\--------------------------------------

### Janet Shold — 2018-01-09T20:47:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/post-232117>

I’ve had lyme with symptoms that range wide, from sore joints, headaches and insomnia to too-tired-to-breath fatigue for years. Lyme persists after antibiotic treatment. I know of many people who have treated with antibiotics, had relief of their symptoms, only to relapse after treatment. This is why we have come here, because I know the only way back to health is jacks way.

It seems that many people are exposed to the spirochete, yet don’t get ill. I didn’t get Noticeably sick until years after a tick bite(s), and then it was a hard crash that put me in bed for a couple of years. As far as we can tell, my daughter was exposed in utero. It really sucks!

[http://ajp.amjpathol.org/article/S0002-9440(17)30894-5/pdf](http://ajp.amjpathol.org/article/S0002-9440\(17\)30894-5/pdf)

### Janet Shold — 2018-01-18T17:20:11+0200

We’ve been to get blood tests and waiting for the results. I sent an email off to support to upgrade our account - I think that’s how it’s done? I’ll put the blood tests results on here as soon as we have them, and post the 23andme reports on our journal.

It’s the dead of winter here, and there has been no morning sun in over two weeks, just feels like a waste of time to go out in the morning to get rained on because it is so DARK. I know we have to get out of here in the winters for sam’s health. Looking at Thailand, because my brother lives there and it’s super inexpensive to rent and eat - and the food is supposed to me incredible. But I’d love to get Sam to the member meetup.

Is there any way to get oysters to NOT taste like oysters? Lol. We’ve been having salmon for supper several times a week, and our dogs have been enjoying my oyster recipes...

# Thread: My Optimal Journal- Stacia Whitaker
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-stacia-whitaker.20623/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stacia426 — 2017-12-28T20:49:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-stacia-whitaker.20623/post-231189>

I am so excited to learn from Dr. Kruse and the members on this site! I want to provide some information about me and my past medical history. I am currently 33 with two children living in central Indiana. I live in a rural town and commute to Indianapolis for work. I work in a Neonatal ICU as a Neonatal NP.

PMH:
Infancy and early childhood: FTT, multiple food intolerances/allergies, multiple hospital admissions

Early childhood and teenage years: multiple broken bones (eight), continued infections with frequent antibiotic use, hospitalization for mono, removal of tonsils due to continued infections, go cart accident with TBI and LOC

Twenties: severe environmental allergies requiring five allergy medications, infertility with multiple ovarian cysts with one cyst being pre cancerous, IBS, continued sinus infections taking antibiotics at least monthly, sinus surgery to remove polyps, muscle weakness, chronic fatigue, joint pain/inflammation, cystic acne, POCS, car accident again with TBI and LOC. I have always been at a healthy weight.

I did become pregnant at 28 in 2013 and had my first child after taking metformin and starting a gluten free diet. I was sick my entire pregnancy with severe hyperemesis. My first child was born with torticollis. During his first years of life he was very sick with: FTT, feeding failure due to severe tongue tie, multiple food intolerances, multiple medication reactions, speech delay, SPD, autism, myopia, esotropia requiring glassess, multiple illnesses including an ICU admission for RSV. We stopped all vaccines after 6 months. He had made great improvements in the past two years with biomedical therapies.

I had my second child at 31 in 2016 without any fertility treatments. I again had hyperemesis. The hyperemesis was more severe this time requiring a zofran pump, three additional medications and home health nursing for home IV fluids. My second child has been healthier. I was able to take a more proactive approach with his diet and health. I was able to eliminate enough foods from my diet to be able to breast feed him until 16 mos. He is developing normally without evidence of autism. He has also had an ICU admission for RSV. He is completely unvaccinated.

Between my two children I became very ill with debilitating joint pain and chronic fatigue. Blood IgG testing reveled severe intolerances to many foods. This is when I started a GAPS/Paleo diet. I was able to heal my gut. I began to feel much better and had much more energy. I was finally able to lose the weight I gained during pregnancy. I was getting sick less often. My environmental allergies were improving after doing some biofeedback therapy.

Thirties: I feel that the improvements that I made in my health are fading. I feel that my hormones are a wreck. I need to lose about 25-30 pounds. I cannot seem to lose this weight despite eating the "perfect" paleo diet. I have felt for awhile now that changing my diet has helped but something else was missing.

My MGM had breast cancer at 40 and died of a sudden heart attack at 50. My mother has multile health challenges including: osteoporosis, weight gain, IBS, food intolerances, joint pain. My 23andme results are pending.

I first learned about Dr. Kruse from an interview that he did with Dr. Dan Pompa. For the last three months I have been listening to every You Tube Video with Dr. Kruse that I can find. I am excited to finally be able to put the missing information together. Any suggestions are greatly welcome! Thanks for reading my long post!

Stacia Whitaker

### Sheddie — 2017-12-29T02:41:47+0200

Welcome Stacia. It might not be positively exciting to ponder and evaluate your NICU work environment for a steady 'assault' by nnEMFs coming at and through you while you're there... Have you run across Dr Kruse mentioning some ornery health observations relative to his time in hospital surgery rooms? This is just what came to me reading your intro, and feeling great compassion. You sound ready to work on health armed with the 'new paradigm' and as an insider to the current state of medical health care. Again, a very big welcome to you. What I have learned has been very helpful, and hopeful, in just about a half a year.

# Thread: Mayuri's Optimal Journal - Been Following Dr. Jack Since Feb. 2017
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayuris-optimal-journal-been-following-dr-jack-since-feb-2017.20674/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mayuri — 2018-01-09T03:43:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayuris-optimal-journal-been-following-dr-jack-since-feb-2017.20674/post-232043>

Genetics:
Maternal Haplogroup J1c
86% Italian

Mother developed dementia, had sleep apnea, 8 small strokes - high carb high sugar diet, high stress and she was seldom outdoors. Neurologist gave her 5 years after diagnosis, rapid dementia - she died 5 years after diagnosis at 76 yrs. old. Grandmother was insulin diabetic - very active traveller, was head of a typing pool all her life, developed pancreatic cancer.

Prior to getting sick, my work was in a University town with mega-technology for 12 years, wifi and modem computers everywhere and very long work hours. I had flu every single winter, never any sunlight (even in the summers). Before diagnosis my doctor told me I had slight fatty liver disease, he said not to worry because everyone had it. I never ate meat or fish, only soya burgers and soya this and that.

Before that, I lived in the tropics for 21 years, never ever was sick. Was in the sunshine everyday with lots of water sports and swimming, although I was still working long hours with computers.

Live in Oz now, we've been following you faithfully and we are doing really well with your protocols. We're out everyday in the sunshine, even when it's freezing out. We're drinking deuterium free water and eating all the good things. Change is hard, the body revolts.

Doing real well, my blood work looks great, and hoping to continue to do well.
The prognosis scares the crap out of one, high recurrence rates - makes one value life more.
Your January 2018 lecture was great.

I am interested in stem cells. Your stem lecture is whoa, need to listen to it a few more times.
Thanks for E V E R Y T H I N G in a big big way!

# Thread: Seth's Story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seths-story.20732/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Greenie — 2018-01-21T01:58:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seths-story.20732/post-233012>

For the past year, I have been reading through many of the excellent threads and articles on this great forum. The following is an introduction-story that touches upon some of the most significant experiences; these have refreshingly led me to this community of mitochondriacs. (My apologies in advance to anyone who finds the story superfluous, verbose, or dull.)

"Everything changes and nothing remains still...you cannot step twice into the same stream." Heraclitus

August 1988:

I returned from my first overnight summer camp. Nestled along Lake Winnipesaukee, the all-boys camp was not an enjoyable experience. As a non-conformist, my attitude was offensive to some senior campers who expected newbies to grovel before them. My questioning of so-called authority would become a motif from this point on.

After four weeks, I returned to the Philadelphia suburb where my family lived. Exhausted from the return trip, I noticed an odd tingling sensation on my tongue. I told my mother that I did not feel so well. She recommended I go to bed. Upon awakening the next morning, I sat up in my bed—something was amiss: I had no feeling in my legs; it was as if they were not there. Alarmed as any 12-year-old kid in

Over the next several weeks, I felt as though I was part of an experiment; one test after another was performed, among them spinal taps, electroshock, IVG, plasmapheresis. The physically weaker I became, the more tubes and monitors were added. More than half a dozen doctors visited me over the next several weeks; not one of them seemed to have an idea as to the source of my condition. Most of them, if not all, were interested in helping, but they lacked insight and understanding.

My desire to get the hell out of the hospital grew stronger each day. Family and friends offered their unwavering support. Each friend who visited me served as a reminder of how much I wanted to get back to life. Eight months earlier, I started lessons on the guitar, an instrument that would become my sanctuary over the decades to come. I also wanted to get back to learning how to ice skate and get back to riding my bike with friends to the local pizza joints and arcades.

I might have been physically weak, but I was willing to fight, especially after a particular dream experienced at my lowest point. Ironic as it may sound, this was a point at which I was peace with dying.

Four weeks after being admitted to the hospital, I returned home. My body had atrophied; my legs were still unresponsive. The “let’s throw everything we have at the wall and hope that something sticks” approach was apparent and ineffective. By far, the person who helped me most throughout my recovery was a physical therapist named Dana.

Learning how to do something your brain remembers doing is a bizarre thing to experience. Over the next year, Dana helped me relearn how to balance while standing on my feet, walk, jog, and run.

About one month after my discharge from the hospital, I met with a neurologist who had diagnosed me as having Guillain–Barré Syndrome (GBS). He could not believe that I was able to walk at all. Surprised that I managed to stand on my own and walk a couple of steps, the neurologist said incredulously, “You aren’t supposed to do that.”

That evening, my parents help me make sense of the neurologist’s seemingly odd behavior. The doctors had told my parents that my paralysis would be permanent. Physical therapy was merely something to counteract the muscle atrophy through exercises. After a little over one year, I regained full use of my legs. Relearning how to stand, walk, jog, and run again was strange, especially as these were actions that used to be performed unconsciously with ease. Though I have had some great mentors, this experience is likely the one that has had the most profound impact.

During my recovery, I decided to pursue things for which I loved regardless of what others thought; this never changed, despite many efforts to steer me elsewhere. Such an attitude pissed off a lot of people, especially the administration of the all-boys prep school I attended. In retrospect, I am amazed that I made it through high school without getting expelled, though I came very close.

I kept to myself and didn’t give a damn about meeting anyone else’s expectations. Although Emerson, Whitman, Thoreau, and other proponents of self-cultivation were parts of the curriculum, most of the school’s administrators and teachers sure as hell didn’t encourage students to apply such philosophies to their own lives.

Music and the guitar were my sanctuaries. I had neither an interest in performing nor any concern about becoming accomplished. Playing music was an outlet, a pace of inspiration and expression free of judgment and bullshit. My primary interests were the guitar, ice hockey, and a few close friends. The rigors of academia were uninspiring, save for a few classes and professors I loved.

Beginning Freshman year in high school, I hardly ever slept before 3:00 am. The night was quiet and free of familial interruptions, seemingly a perfect setting for playing music. School grades were negatively affected, but then again, I fell asleep while taking the SAT, a fitting embodiment of what I felt about academia. School and the institutionalizing of students’ minds was an anathema to me.

Watching older musician-friends deal with drug addiction was the most effective anti-drug lesson I could have learned. (Ironically, the parents of many friends told their kids to stay clear of me because I had mullet and was intensely interested in playing the guitar and ice hockey.)

I also realized that making lots of money was not a prerequisite for happiness. The area was a very affluent area, yet most of the friends’ families were at least as dysfunctional and laden with melancholia as my own. Unlike most kids my age, I was well-aware of my mortality. I decided that I would focus on the quality of life and be less occupied by quantity.

Not until the age of 16 did I meet a doctor who impressed me with his integrity and lack of towing the ADA’s bullshit. Dr. Liu debunked many dental and medical fallacies. He advised not to use toothpaste, especially fluoride. “Do you really think it is wise to brush your teeth with something made up of water, sand, and sugar that contains an ingredient so toxic that you are asked to call the poison control hotline if you ingest it?” He said rhetorically.

I have followed Dr. Liu’s recommendations; to this day, I have never had a single cavity or dental issue, nor have I ever broken a bone; I am nearly 42.

Junior year in high school, I made it onto the varsity ice hockey team. Though I was a fast and agile skater, I was 6’0” and weighed a mere 149lbs. My coach urged me to gain at least 20 pounds over the offseason that summer.

I increased my already high caloric intake and frequented the gym at least three or four times a week for an hour each time. Grossly overtraining, I managed to gain two pounds despite my efforts, much to the chagrin of my coach and me.

At 18, I researched weight training approaches. For one, I had realized that my health was my responsibility first and foremost. I had no interest in ever seeing a medical doctor again if I could help it. (Understandable, considering my previous experience.) I read every book on the subject in the Central Library of the Free Library of Philadelphia. Nothing worked at breaking the strength and weight plateaus I kept experiencing time after time.

The last book I checked out was one I had dismissed earlier for being so unconventional that I figured its approach would not work. Ellington Darden’s High-Intensity Training (HIT) book turned out to be the most effective of all. I began using the SuperSlow protocol but realized I was not sure if I was properly performing the movements. I sought out help from a trainer certified in SS.

To say the HIT protocol produced results would be an understatement. I began at 149lbs; 14 months later I was 200lbs. My obsession with muscle gain got to the point where I hit 228lbs; however, I was eating a minimum of 8000 calories per day to maintain the weight. After a friend expressed the disgust he felt while watching me eat so much food, I asked my trainer if it was natural to be eating as much as I was. He looked at me oddly and said, “No, why?” “Well, why wouldn’t you tell me that before?” I asked. “Because your goal was to keep gaining, remember? It is up to you to know when to stop.” I then said I was only going to eat when I was hungry. Within two months 40lbs vanished.

While I continue to train this way, I am back to about 149lbs at 4-5% body fat. Having experienced many weights with body fat percentages from 4-15%, I feel best where I was and currently am weight wise on a diet comprised of fats mostly (60+%), 20-25% protein, and 15-20% carbs.

I have been eating 2-6 raw egg yolks (from free-range chickens, preferably no grain or corn) daily for over 15 years. Having eaten well over 20,000 raw egg yolks, I should be dead from coronary disease according to the response of many people.

Being want of faith in the typical medical doctor, I took it upon myself to be responsible for my health. The fact that I have been without health insurance from 26 years of age underscored this stance. I began reading and researching a lot, a habit that continues today. Of course, I have made many mistakes, but mistakes are essential in the learning process; fortunately, none of these ever materialized into the need to visit a doctor or hospital. In this respect, I have been very lucky.

After years of competitive tennis, I missed playing on an ice hockey team and joined a league five years ago. I make it a point to wear blue-blocking glasses under the helmet.

I have noticed over the years that I have a sensitivity to EMFs, which seems to be partly tied to my bout with GBS as a kid. My maternal haplotype is H5a1. (Paternal = E-M5021.) CT has been integral to battling nerve pain when encountering EMF hellholes.

Understanding the effects of an environment, whether they are on the macro (classical) or micro (quantum) level, provides a picture that is far more complete than the usual reductionist-mechanistic approaches—no organism exists independently of its environment. To ignore nature is to put oneself on a perilous path. While in graduate school, Alan Watts and Goethe inculcated in me how essential context-environment is; this perspective has become a mainstay in every facet of my life.

Dr. Kruse’s no-nonsense, empirical, ever-developing philosophy is a breath of fresh air. The about-face he made years ago stemmed from a realization: the conventional and prevailing wisdom is ineffective and unwise. He emphasizes the importance of questioning our notions and precepts of the orthodoxy. Dr. Kruse inspires us to tap into our courage and tear down the facades of bullshit and face the unsavory truths—this process is conducive to growth and transformation.

The vast number of Dr. Kruse’s resources-references have been incredibly helpful and fascinating. Without these resources, I would likely be ignorant of the marvelously illuminating works of those such as Ott, Becker, Marino, Wunsch, Wallace, Pollack, McFadden-Al-Khalili, and Hands. I sincerely hope that I can bring at least a fraction of a nanometer of light to the forum.

### Greenie — 2018-03-06T01:48:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seths-story.20732/post-236380>

A couple of weeks ago, I flew out to Denver to participate in the Pabst-Yaegermeister Pond Hockey Tournament with some teammates. The tournament itself is held in Silverthorne @ 9035 ft. I knew the change in altitude from living at around 1000 ft would be a major influence.

More UV, IR, less O2, among other differing factors, I was aware I had to stay hydrated, increase DHA, and do CT. I managed to succeed in those departments; however, in the sleep and wisdom department, I failed horribly. Sleeping in the condo with the other teammates posed more of a challenge than I had thought. Some sounding like they were about to suffocate; as a result, I could not get much sleep—a total of about 11-12 hours in three nights; this serious screwed me up.

I compounded this by being a moron and drinking two glasses of Malbec on the third night. (Damn was that stupid as f&#$.)

The weather was majestic though about 20s-30s during the day. I got CT with cold showers and stripping down to a Kiniki suit outside. The water from the shower must have been much colder than the low 50s I use. The intense cold made the session more like 10-12 minutes instead of 20+. I have no idea the temperature of the water, but it was cold enough to cause pain if you didn't rotate. In retrospect, I wonder if I was screwing myself even more by using such cold water.

One day had 20mph winds. I was quickly reminded of how wind affects water and skin per Dr. Pollack's "The Fourth Phase of Water."

My idiocy and the winds notwithstanding, the weather was lovely. Definitely glad I went, though I might make different living arrangement and not get caught up in drinking with the team, half of which was acclimated since they live nearby.

[Click here to check out a write-up and photos from the Gazette:](http://gazette.com/gallery/7002/pictures/1737677)

Also began shutting off all electricity (back home) as I noticed my quality of sleep was not what it had been. I used milligauss and body voltage meters to check the difference between the breakers being on and off: the body voltage dropped over tenfold! Waking up much more rested.

After hearing Dr. Jack talk about Elon Musk and 5G satellites, I read an article that interviewed Musk. After finishing the article, I could help but ponder: Musk is no moron by any means. It appears that three possibilities present themselves:

1.  He is ignorant of the ramifications of beaming 5G from thousands of satellites orbiting the planet.
2.  He knows about the ramifications but does not care or believe they are substantial.
3.  He knows about the ramifications but the dream he has a darker, more nefarious bend to it.

The first and third possibilities are difficult to believe. The first possibility would mean Musk has never encountered information in regards to EMF and biological systems in any form, directly or indirectly. Unless Musk is a sociopath, the third possibility makes no sense because 5G will invariably affect him. Would be curious to hear what others think about the implications of Musk's plan.

# Thread: Rebecca - getting Right
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rebecca-getting-right.20853/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RebbalforLight — 2018-02-10T18:58:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rebecca-getting-right.20853/post-234503>

Hello, Everybody from a tiny town north of Athens, GA, the birthplace of the rock bands REM and the B-52s. I have been attempting to sip from this firehose of knowledge for a few weeks and decided to jump in and join the tribe.

Jack, you have blown my mind and opened my eyes to a much greater reality. To be honest, it is a reality that I have always hoped was true, but my background in “science” always caused me to doubt. Earthing and living with nature always sounded so right but so New Age. Thank you, from the bottom of my heart, for all your hard work and willingness to share.

To all the mitochondriacs on this site, please bear with me as a low dopamine newby that is just trying to tread water in this very deep ocean. I have been reading the forum with awe.

I am a 51-year-old woman of the H1C3 haplogroup. I’m 5’8” and weigh 140lbs. The conditions that led me to seek alternative health answers are collagenous colitis and a very painful DVT (blood clot). Below is my abbreviated life story. Fifty years is a lot to trim, so I tried to focus on the salient points that may lead to insights as to how I got here and what works and what doesn't.

My nickname growing up was Rebecca of Dirty Foot Farm because I was always barefoot, rain or shine, summer or winter. I was a healthy, active youth. In 6th grade, I got glasses for astigmatism. My diet was partly healthy and mostly junk: protein and bad carbs, with a few raw veggies in the mix.

My recreation and education had me outside most of the time. I used sunscreen but not sunglasses (but did have coke-bottle glasses).

I grew up, got married, got my masters degree and we moved to Athens, GA. After giving birth by c-section to my wonderful son, my world crashed. I had postpartum depression. It was as if the real me was shoved up into a tiny corner of my mind while my negative emotions drove my body around. It was scary and I felt totally helpless.

I fought with deep depression for over a decade. Doctors tried cocktails of drugs. I tried ECT, which worked, apparently, but it totally erased my memory of the happy period that it yielded, as well as chunks of my past. By happenstance, I found a naturopathic doctor who cared. He was offended at the length of my current medication list and my state of ill health (105 lbs of misery). He got my adrenals working and was able to reduce my cocktails with pre enzymatic vitamins and nutrition.

In my mid-30s, a study of rTMS (repetitive Trans-Cranial Magnetic Stimulation) for mono-polar depression opened up in my hometown and I got off all my meds to participate. After the experiment, I was given 2 weeks of open treatment and fixed me. Within a week of open treatment, I was up to flat and after another week, I was human. It was a miracle.

In my late 40s, I developed collagenous colitis. The steroids stopped the pain but did not fix the problem. I tried many diets and supplements and then I tried a water fast to give my GI tract a rest. This was prior to Thanksgiving of last year. I fasted for 4 and a half days. My colitis improved greatly.

Just after the fast, I developed a DVT going from mid femur area to mid hip. Currently, I am on Xarelto blood thinner and Tramadol for pain and take serrapeptase on an empty stomach to break up the clot.

Thanks to Jack’s videos and a first read through Epi-Paleo Rx, I am wearing blue blockers at work and in front of computer/cell phone/tv and going sans glasses as much as possible. For about 2 weeks, I have been enjoying grounding as I watch the sunrise and as I eat lunch outside, with my glasses at off. I am eating seafood daily and currently following a Keto diet (with emphasis on seafood and pastured eggs, healthy fats and seasonal greens). My house is now lit with sunlight, a few red night lights, and candles. I am working on getting my family on board, too.

My CT exposure has been slow. I am doing face dips and ending my shower with cold, as well as being mostly bare during the very chilly sunrises. I’m going to try a cool bath today.

We have always wanted to have a permaculture farm and I now know that it needs to be sooner rather than later. Once I am ambulatory, I will head in that direction. It will need to be providing income before I can quit and go full-time farmer.

Thanks for reading my rambling. I look forward to learning more and interacting with you all.

### RebbalforLight — 2018-02-11T16:07:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rebecca-getting-right.20853/post-234547>

Hello, Caroline. Thank you for the welcome and pointers. I have listened to the Vermont talks once, which is not enough. I've listened to Jack and Nathan Walz, too. Can you please point me towards Jeremy's videos?

My great grandmother was healthy and lived to be 106 and was sharp as a tack. She never gave into the low-fat health craze. When I knew her (in her 80's and beyond), she didn't get outside, but she grew up on a farm. I am sure she dressed modestly, though.

My grandmother was healthy until she did succumb to the low fat craze. She changed her cooking and gradually slowed down and got cancer and died in her late 80s.

My mom is healthy, despite her low fat, processed food diet. She has low blood pressure and cholesterol. She gets lots of sun and is active. She will probably outlive my siblings. One sibling has T2D and the youngest had some sort of undiagnosed brain damage at birth and now has severe epilepsy. Unfortunately, I am seen as the crazy one who follows crazy trends and doesn't listen to normal doctors. I hope to get them to listen soon.

ETA: I know my mitochondria are from my mom but thought this might help: Father's mother was an enigma. She was abusive, paranoid and very hateful. My dad raised his sisters and got out ASAP. He has always struggled with health. High blood pressure, glaucoma, heart attack, gallbladder removed, half of colon removed. He is still kicking at 80, which is old for someone his height, 6'6". All of his peers in college (they were grouped by height) have long since passed.

### RebbalforLight — 2018-02-13T15:32:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rebecca-getting-right.20853/post-234673>

Sleep -

When I first got my blueblockers, I had almost a week of blissful sleep.

The last few nights, I am waking up multiple times - my usual pattern. I am fighting a cold with neti pot with a touch of both goldenseal extract and hydrogen peroxide and taking oil of oregano by mouth and in my ears. Grr. I don't need to be sick, again.

I wear layers of clothes so I can cover up under blue light at work and expose skin at lunch and on breaks. I wear my blueblockers all the time at work because the lights are all fluorescent. At home when not doing delicate/dangerous tasks like cutting/cooking, I go glassless unless using the computer or if I'm in the room with the TV, when I use blueblockers.

For the BAB, I can't eat more than 20g of protein without nausea. Does that get better?

What am I missing? I really want to heal.

Should I get some UV and or IR lights at home? it has been and will be cloudy for the next week.

Does one have to call the company for the Breathy test for D? I can't find any links for it. (I hate the phone, but will use when necessary). Or does a doctor have to order it? I am going to go get some Icelandic water. I need to save up for DDW.

### karenr — 2018-02-13T16:19:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rebecca-getting-right.20853/post-234674>

Hi Rebecca, the Breathy test can be ordered online from Cignature Health,
[https://www.cignaturehealth.com/product/breathy/](https://www.cignaturehealth.com/product/breathy/)

They don’t sell the water online. When I ordered it last month, I just called them, had a brief conversation with Anne Cooper about my situation, she advised me on how to use the water, and I ordered a case. Recently, I heard that someone called to order and was told that they need to go through a health care practitioner. So I'm not sure, maybe they've changed their policy. (I will be calling in the next week or so, and will find out).

I do know that CH allows practitioners like chiropractors and acupuncturists to become affiliates, to order tests and water for their patients.

CH seems to be busier than they used to be, and usually you'll need to leave a phone message. But they’re very responsive via email for simple questions.

I was using Icelandic Glacial water before I started the DDW, that seemed like a good way to go. With the DDW, the instructions are to dilute it with any (good quality) water, so now I don’t bother spending more for the Icelandic water to dilute the DDW with.
Good luck!

### RebbalforLight — 2018-02-16T16:22:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rebecca-getting-right.20853/post-234887>

Some progress with the leg that has the blood clot: we had a party at work and I had multiple loads of crock pots and goodies and arrived at work when everyone who could help me unload was busy or absent. Long story, short, I made multiple trips up and down stairs, carrying loads and then had to walk the 6 minutes from my parking area. My leg hurt like crazy afterward, but I was able to do it and felt safe. (I only started walking down stairs about a week ago, due to the stiffness in my lower leg and ankle.) And two other people have started eating keto. There has been interest in my blueblockers, but no one wants to join me down the rabbit hole, yet.

At the end of this month, I have a doctor's appointment and will schedule my physical and talk to him about getting specific tests. He's really good at figuring the justifications for various tests. With that and knowledge I am gaining from this forum, I should get plenty of good info.

I just found Jack's response to Hairless Samson \[[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...o-write-this-to-be-honest.18332/#post-200418\]](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/i-never-wanted-to-write-this-to-be-honest.18332/#post-200418]) and am digging into Time #21 and then rereading Jack's response about ferritin and iron and blood clotting disorders. More deep waters.

I have been watching and reading some of Doug Wallace's work and the mitochondria and disease connections. I have been having trouble locating how to find specifics about my haplotype, H1C3. People on the forum seem to be able to dig into their lineage with more aplomb than I. Just putting my haplotype into a search engine doesn't reveal much, or much that means something to me, anyway. How do people find this information? Do I start with H only or what preceded it?

And yes, I seem to be all over the map, trying to figure the best approach to learning all of this.

Sleep has been better, even with my cold. I woke up just once last night and just rolled over and went back to sleep.

CT is getting better. I usually do face dunks followed by a short warm and several minutes of cold shower. Unfortunately, I can't stand for more than a few minutes without my compression sock. Baby steps.

### RebbalforLight — 2018-02-25T19:06:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rebecca-getting-right.20853/post-235701>

The rest of the family now have blueblockers, though I have to remind them to wear them...

Does anyone have knowledge about pros/cons of compression socks for DVT recovery?

My husband proposed that my leg still hurts without the sock because the sock is a "crutch" and I should go without to get the leg to heal; kind of like wandering around without my glasses in hopes of improving my vision. But it hurts a lot after just a few minutes without the compression sock. My leg turns various shades of red, blue and purple and I would have to go back to being sedentary.

I am hoping that I don't have permanent vein damage or that "permanent" damage can be healed by light, water and magnetism intervention. Any knowledge would be appreciated.

On my third run-through of the Vermont talks, I am finally "getting" it or at least more bits.

### RebbalforLight — 2018-03-04T14:50:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rebecca-getting-right.20853/post-236257>

Yesterday was AWESOME. We are about to have some major earthwork done on our land to correct water flow and start our first major step to being a permaculture farm. The work will destroy the area that was our garden and we didn't want to lose that good soil, so the whole family was outside from mid-morning through the early afternoon moving soil and potting perennials that we wanted to keep. I was, of course, barefoot. Because if my leg, I was in and out, but was out for at least 2.5 hours.

I didn't burn! I was in a sports bra and shorts and have
Fitzpatrick skin type II. I. Didn't. Burn! Nada! No red, no pain, nothing! and it felt so good. My son, who is resisting sunrise and seafood did burn a little. My husband did not, but he has olive skin and laughs at winter sun.

So, the solar callous is real. I know I'm preaching to the choir here, but I can't wait to tell all those type I and II peeps at work. My n=1 is rocking along nicely, thanks to Jack Kruse and his Merry Mitochondriacs.

### RebbalforLight — 2018-03-10T19:46:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rebecca-getting-right.20853/post-236856>

Hormone fix? I'm post menopause (everything ended at 38) and I had these annoying thick hairs on my chinny chin chin. They have softened.

# Thread: Marshall's journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/marshalls-journal.20872/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### marshall mueller — 2018-02-14T08:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/marshalls-journal.20872/post-234715>

Hi . I'm brand new to Jack's work. Jumping in with both feet.
I'm 70 years old. 5'9" 145 lbs. light skin, red hair. I am currently residing temporarily in an apartment in Pasadena CA. My home is in Washougal Washington, but my wife and I are volunteering to be nearly full time babysitters for my 3 year old granddaughter here in Pasadena
The health issue I'm most concerned with is tinnitus and headaches. I've had tinnitus for about 30 years and the headaches for about 25 years. When the T first started I tried lots of stuff none of which worked. Acupuncture, herbs, diet, ayahuasca, meditation - - etc. etc. I finally resigned myself to it and tried just to live with it. Now both are getting worse and I've got to do something -- I'm often completely slammed by the tinnitus especially and am unable to function well.

Medications. I'm on no prescription meds. I take various supplements. vit C, B's iodine, trace minerals, coconut oil, red palm oil, C-60, magnesium citrate, ashwaganda.

Other issues. I notice I'm more forgetful than I ever was. My brain is often kind of foggy. My overall energy
level is pretty good for a 70 year old I guess, but it's slipping. Arthritis in a number of joints - - both thumbs, one big toe, a couple of fingers. Nothing really debilitating yet but not good signs.

Medical procedures. Had a hip replacement last year. completely degraded by arthritis. no complications and it's working very well. Had both eyes done for cataracts - one in 2009 and one in 2003. Unfortunately the implants are the type that filter UV. Asked my eye doctor today if they could be replaced - - heavy advice against - -(no surprise) Not sure how I will get the right lights into my body. am searching the website for some direction.

Family history . grandmothers both lived to nearly 100 and as far as I know had no big health issues. Mother died at 33 of colon cancer, and had ulcerative colitis and severe arthritis. Grandfathers both died in their mid 60s. Paternal died of heart attack, maternal of stroke. Father quite healthy for most of his life. prostate cancer in his late 70s. got radiation but it came back and he died at 83.

General life style and diet. I've done organic for 40 years. Tried vegan for a while 30 years ago but seemed to get anemic. Now I do lots of homemade probiotics including raw milk kefir, grass fed meat (not a lot) some eggs, nuts and seeds and lots of veggies. Not much carbs --occasional german rye bread toast with grass fed butter is about all I do. I'm looking into seafood. I haven't eaten it much in my life and never really loved it that much. I have been quite concerned with the toxins in it so I never felt I was missing anything. JK has changed my mind on that and I'm searching for a reliable source of clean seafood.

I should exercise more. I do a good brisk walk pretty much every day and when I'm home in Washington I walk in my neighborhood which has quite steep hills.

I'm just beginning with the work on emf's, sunlight etc. I have limited my cell phone and installed a shield on it, hard wired my computers at home, have RO water filter, wear blue blockers religiously, have been sunning lots especially in
the am.

# Thread: Cynthia Holmes - My Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cynthia-holmes-my-optimal-journal.20912/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cynthia Holmes — 2018-02-20T21:16:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cynthia-holmes-my-optimal-journal.20912/post-235212>

Basics

· Female. Married with 28 y.o. son and 23 y.o. daughter.

· I was born in February 1958 (just turned 60).

· I am the second child of three daughters.

· My mother smoked during all 3 pregnancies. She and my father smoked during the 18 years I lived at home. My mother did not breastfeed any of us. She died of COPD at age 80 after being on oxygen 24/7 for 8 years.

· My maternal grandmother lived to 104, outliving her daughter (my mother) who was her only child. Both my mother and her mother were mentally sharp until they died.

· I was raised in northern NJ (18 years), then moved to Philadelphia (6 years), then moved to San Francisco (6 years), and then to Tucson (28 years).

· I had plenty of sun exposure early in life but became neurotic about avoiding it when I moved to Tucson. Seriously! Hats, sunglasses, sun screen, long-sleeves and shade- seeking behavior for 28 years!

My health concerns (in order of importance)

· Dry macular degeneration (optometrist first noticed drusen when I was in my 30s)

· Dysthymia/depression since age 17 (on 20 mg fluoxetine for 20 years)

· HSV-2 since age 19 (on prophylactic acyclovir for 33 years)

· APOE-E 3/4 and MTHFR defects

How I found Jack

In September 2017, a google search on causes of macular degeneration brought up the link to Jack’s 2017 Nourish Vermont talk. I watched it at least 10 times because what he says about the eye clock is information I had never heard before! I transitioned quickly from watching various podcasts on YouTube to becoming a bronze then silver then gold member. Since my last membership upgrade I have been devouring the old webinars and should be current by the end of the month, at which time I will start on the blogs. I really look forward to a live event: I couldn’t do MX last year because we had family visiting and because I already had a trip to Playa De Carmen planned for this month (2/21-2/25). I will attend the member event in MX in 2018.

Prior to discovering JK

In July of 2017 I began self-paying to see an internal medicine doc who is fellowship-trained in Integrative Medicine because the PCPs on my insurance plan would not order the labs I wanted. A month before my first meeting with him I had eliminated all grains and dairy from my diet and in doing so lost some weight, which was a goal, but also had the unexpected benefit of completely eliminating my chronic neck and shoulder pain which I had treated for a long time (years) with 800 mg of ibuprofen every morning: I no longer do this. Also, on this diet, my stools went from type 2 to type 4 on the Bristol chart, albeit after some GI discomfort and diarrhea in the transition.

JK stuff I’m doing now:

· LIGHT: barefoot sungazing for about 10 minutes at dawn (not looking directly at the sun because I’m still nervous about retinal damage). No sunglasses outside ever. Have discontinued wearing contacts and take off my regular glasses as much as possible when outside. I am using daytime and nighttime blue blockers when in artificial light. I am nude sunbathing daily when D is available per D-minder app, 15 minutes each side, lying on a mat with my feet and hands in contact with the ground. I use an infrared sauna (Sunlighten) three times a week for about 45 minutes each session. I can make D in Tucson 365 days a year (32nd latitude).

· WATER: First I switched from Brita-filtered tap water to spring water and increased my consumption. I am currently drinking reverse osmosis water exclusively. Tucson water is not fluoridated.

· MAGNETISM: thinking about a Magnetico sleep pad but haven’t made that investment yet. Just trying to be barefoot outdoors more. Will spend time in a cenote in Cancun this coming week.

· CT: In Nov/Dec 2017 I did CT about a dozen times in my bathtub between 50 and 55 degrees for about 30 minutes each time. Currently, not doing it, but may start face-dunking. (Leptin on labs is currently 6).

· EMFs: After I do the EMF Boot Camp I will test my home, which is the environment I spend the most time in. I like the idea of eliminating wi-fi, hardwiring our computers and TV, and installing a kill switch for the master bedroom. In anticipation of worsening vision (but before I became aware of the negative health effects of artificial light) I spent a bundle upgrading our electric and installing a bunch more lights. Bummer!

· FOOD: I’m eating more sea food, although I think I injured my wrist shucking my own oysters to save money! My diet is ketogenic and relatively high fat. I buy fruits and veggies from the farmers’ market to ensure these items are local and seasonal. I am monitoring my blood sugar but rarely have morning fasting levels below 100. I am trying to keep my eating window as narrow as possible but still almost always have three meals a day.

· SUPPLEMENTS: My doc has me on quite a few things and I will discuss these in a later post. I started the JK practices and the supplements around the same time so of course it is impossible to know what is having what effect. The good news is my labs have improved. Since I didn’t make changes one at a time and monitor results with labs, I think I’ll try to do the reverse and start reducing or eliminating things one at a time (e.g., prescription drugs and supplements) and check labs after each step.

· EXERCISE: I should have waited to start exercising until I lost weight, but I didn’t and gave myself bone spurs on the tops of both feet. These are no longer painful and I currently walk/jog outdoors three days a week and do a Cybex circuit indoors three days a week.

Health Wins

· I’ve lost 35 pounds since June 1, 2017 (about 7 months). I started at 177 pounds and 47.2% body fat and am now at 141 lbs. and 31.4% body fat (using Bod Pod for body composition). 135 lbs is what I weighed for most of my 20s and 30s.

· Getting more REM sleep and Deep sleep as reported by Fitbit Alta HR.

· Mood has stabilized and improved above and beyond the gain I made 20 years ago with fluoxetine.

· Way less brain fog and way more energy.

· Feet don’t feel as cold as they used to and I am sometimes kicking off the covers at night.

· Labs: The attached PDF compares labs from September 2017 and January 2018 (about 5 months apart). The September labs, reported in the right-hand column of the PDF, show lots of red (high risk) and yellow (intermediate risk) results. My current labs, to the left, show a lot more green (optimal) results. A couple of highlights include the following improvements: cholesterol went down to 163 from 206, hs-CRP when down to 1.2 from 3.4, oxidized LDL went down to 49 from 74, CoQ10 went up from .69 to 1.38, and omega 3 index went up to 9.4 from 6.2. Areas of concern include possible insulin resistance, high adiponectin at 16, high ferritin at 156, high postprandial fatty acids, low free T4, and BUN: Creatinine ratio of 21.

Health Goals

· Do whatever can be done to reverse or slow the progression of my macular degeneration.

· Discontinue fluoxetine and find another way to manage depression.

· Discontinue acyclovir and find another way to prevent outbreaks of herpes.

· Optimize bioidentical hormones.

· Do whatever can be done to minimize risk of cognitive decline from APOE E 3/4 status.

I use an infrared sauna (Sunlighten) three times a week for about 45 minutes each session. I can make D in Tucson 365 days a year (32nd latitude).

# Thread: And so it begins...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndal — 2018-03-14T04:58:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237162>

I’ve been doing CT in the ocean each morning for about a week now, then have a cold shower when I get home. Amazing how warm the water is. I do find myself shivering afterwards for a while some days.

I’m struggling to only eat within the Epi-Paleo proticol. I keep giving into my sugar addiction...grrr. I’m trusting the more sunlight I get and the more CT I do, the more I’ll want to eat Epi-Paleo only and get my Leptin working properly. In the past I’ve been off all sugar for about 2 1/2 years. Never seemed to reach Leptin Sensitivity though. Back on about 6 months ago. Really screwed myself by giving in to it again.

I have an opportunity to go up North for a few months. Up near Byron. Here I’ll be able to focus fully on myself. I can spend hours at the beach from first light. Going in and out of the ocean as need be. I can rest and relax to my hearts content. I can live in an environment that has no one else bringing sugar in to the house. I can have full control over lights, wifi, sleep, etc. I can get educated in the realm of Optimal Health, applying, evaluating and adjusting accordingly. I can leave the daily stresses behind me.

It’s an opportunity to reconnect with myself as well. Work out who I truly be and what it is I would like to be doing with my life.

The only downside is I’ll be leaving my family behind. My husband will come up when he can, a few days at a time. Our kids (23 & 25) have said they’ll come up and visit too when they can. I’m thinking the benefits will far outweigh the negatives or so I keep telling myself.

I’m over living in survival mode. I want to have the energy to engage in life and stop sitting on the sidelines watching it pass me by. I’m sick and tired of being sick and tired!

And so it begins...

### caroline — 2018-03-14T06:32:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237168>

Hi Lyndal - You are making a fantastic beginning! go for it girl! Dr. K. always tells us to take care of ourself first .......and your family will reap all the benefits.

There is a clothing optional beach in Byron - probably lots of them!

Don't look back ...this is your time to shine - your journey to optimal!

Are you eating a big enuf BAB?? that should knock out all cravings. Don't give in because you will set yourself back to the beginning.

wishing you magic and sending you a Koala bear hug x

### Lyndal — 2018-03-14T07:16:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237169>

Thanks Caroline, got it!

I’ve been to the one at Tyagarah before, not the one at Kings Beach. Planning on hanging out there as much as I can whilst I’m up that way. I’ll have a look at getting some tan through bathers as well.

I’ll keep looking forward...keep my head towards the sunshine.

Hmm...maybe I’m not eating a big enough BAB. Having changed over to seafood I’m not yet sure what’s enough for me. Will play around with quantities and see what works best. Hadn’t thought about it stemming from breakfast. Makes sense now you’ve shone a light on it.

Thanks again Caroline, greatly appreciate all your assistance, encouragement and support, as well as the magic and Koala bear hug. Which I’m sending back to you too

### Lyndal — 2018-03-22T05:09:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237720>

Ok, so my accommodation near Byron fell through - oh well.

That’s had me have a rethink and I’m feeling up higher north might be a better option. I’m liking where Richelle is at Agnes Water.

The plan is to head off next week. My husband will drive up with me, then he’ll fly back a few days later.

It really is time to make my health and well-being a priority. That being said, I’m so fricken nervous and exhausted, it’s quite overwhelming. I’m trusting I can follow through with this plan. My mental health has taken a bit of a dive of late too. I’m sure it’s fear, patterning, guilt, shame, my saboteur trying to stop me, let alone lack of sunshine. I’ll cry my way there if I have to!

If anybody has any other suggestions of where to locate my self for a few months for Optimal health and healing in the land of Oz, I’d greatly appreciate the recommendations.

Still struggling with the sugar cravings. Working on the BAB and will give the pork belly and prawns a go, as Caroline suggested. Seafood alone, no matter the quantity, doesn’t appear to be able to knock those cravings off.

Continuing to CT and ground every day, mostly at the beach at sunrise, followed by a cold shower.

Been working on the oxytocin too

### caroline — 2018-03-22T08:17:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237724>

I think Agnes Waters sounds like a great idea ......baby steps. Rome definitely wasn't built in a day! Give yourself a break - cut yourself some slack.

Think of all the positives in your life ....enjoy the road trip up with your DH. Make some great memories while you are at it.

Have a lot of pork belly for BAB ........

One day at a time Lyndal........keep focusing on the three legged stool. You are already doing great things for yourself ...and for your family.

if you want to talk - pm me

It will be great - you only need to concentrate on yourself and getting well ....before long you won't recognise yourself!

Plan a trip to Mexico for next Christmas to meet Jack.......

> That’s had me have a rethink and I’m feeling up higher north might be a better option. I’m liking where Richelle is at Agnes Water.

> Richelle was planning on building holiday accommodation as well - don't know if she has done this - perhaps you could PM her.

### Alper — 2018-03-24T11:04:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237880>

> [Lyndal said:](/goto/post?id=237169)
>
> Thanks Caroline, got it!
>
> I’ve been to the one at Tyagarah before, not the one at Kings Beach. Planning on hanging out there as much as I can whilst I’m up that way. I’ll have a look at getting some tan through bathers as well.
>
> I’ll keep looking forward...keep my head towards the sunshine.
>
> Hmm...maybe I’m not eating a big enough BAB. Having changed over to seafood I’m not yet sure what’s enough for me. Will play around with quantities and see what works best. Hadn’t thought about it stemming from breakfast. Makes sense now you’ve shone a light on it.
>
> Thanks again Caroline, greatly appreciate all your assistance, encouragement and support, as well as the magic and Koala bear hug. Which I’m sending back to you too

Maybe you need more fat for your breakfast...that was my big help to stop the cravings. I usually eat 4 eggs (scrambled, fixing it with 2 tablespoons of butter) and some fish (in total about 50g of protein). As soon as the eggs are done I just put one or two extra tablespoons on top of the eggs and let it melt. It´s delicious. those 2 spoons made a big difference for me. I can easily stay without eating until 5 p.m.

# Thread: Dr. Hoeper's Journal- Looking for all inputs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-hoepers-journal-looking-for-all-inputs.21059/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gregory H Hoeper — 2018-03-18T03:11:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-hoepers-journal-looking-for-all-inputs.21059/post-237386>

I'll go on about my history... dissect away I am looking for any and all advice.

28 (almost 29) year old recovering func. medicine doctor working hard to convince those the way of the mitochodriac. Born cesarean section, my mother was on antibiotics while she breastfed me. When I was 2 I got a toenail fungus on both big toes in a tropical island. I still have it 26 years later. I started to get fat when I was about 8 or 9, was very fat until age 16 (S.A.D.) then lost weight and was very skinny (for 4 years, skinny from 16-19/20). I took all those garbage weightlifting supplements for a long time. Maybe into my 20's (mid 25 ish some of them). Video game player. Then got huge-fatass in college (up to about 280) drinking sometimes tons of video game binging really gross with energy drinks I hope I can attone for all of my sins and went paleo and lost it all by the time I was 22. I got fatter a few more times getting to around 220ish. I am fairly muscular and have had somewhat impresive lifts in the past. However now with joint aches and whatnot it gets hard (29 years old and joint aches). Anyway, I've always dug for answers and was in the world of diet arguing, so finally happy Dr. Kruse was finally able to make food easy (environment stupid).

Anyway, got into func. medicine, and working with patients. Helping people, but my problems seemed to not resolve. And actually when I think about it, I can't remember when my energy started getting so bad, but I remember in college and highschool and all of it being bad. And then I also manual labored for some time. So i was probably healthiest then. But when I think about how unhealthy I have been my whole life, I am surprised I am still alive. I get what would appear to white patches of skin with white hair that used to get irritated and red and itchy with alcohol. I have experimented with sooooo many supplements, super high doses etc etc. I got some blood work done april 2017, showed poor kidney function, low cholesterol (like 145 or someething?) highish liver enzymes, high bilirubin, low vitamin D, whacky electrolytes, and a bunch of other stuff. SO i tried really hard with supplements and the whole 9 yards. And then ended up going into zero carb towards august (end, and probably too early) and did that for some time. Got bloodwork in November, there was some good and bad. I attached it but if you're lazy Cholesterol 256 HDL 95 BUN/Creatinine 24 Reverse t3 22 bilirubin 1.3 MCV 94.

\*\*\*MY LARGEST REGRET EVER: Using PEMF device on my head in the 8-10hz for a few weeks before I discovered Dr. Kruse." Deathly affraid currently I will get brain cancer yesterday. I have also made 3 transpolar flights, and did ground myself the third flight (all in the last 6 months)

Anyway, symptoms: I think I need coffee to use the bathroom, I usually have it after I eat breakfast now so I neve know. Brainfog, discoordination t feels like of my extremities and shoulder pain and leg pain. Freezing hands and stuff. It is super weird, but I can't figure out when I get it. So random all day. I have had bloating with vegetables in the past.

What I am doing currently:

I live in an apartment that has Wifi that I can't turn off completely The wifi band doesn't make it into my room all the time (1-2 bands) but sometimes I see neighbor wifi's. I try to get it off. Flux/orange glasses on pc. Block blue light indoors all day and have minute 1-2 minutes exposure max. Apartment is red and UV bulbs. Eat withing 30 minutes of waking. I Eat usually red meat, now incorporating more fish, and I make a shake with whey protein collagen, differnet things and coconut milk bee pollen etc etc some raw eggs. Also some raw unpasteurized honey. I finish eating by 6:30-7pm. I have 3 meals.

I take 2 showers a day and end every single one with cold water no less than one minute. Getting used to the cold is hard. I am usually in bed around 9 or 10 and up at 5:30-7:00. I ionic foot detox 5 days a week, and infrared sauna 4x a week.

The only supplements I take (which actually ehlp and make a difference) Hexaniacin 2,360mg a day, licorice extract with ashwaganda rehmannia and chinese yam, and a fish oil (Polar Power) which is cold-pressed whole sockeye salmon with the brains whch yield vitamins A,D, Omega 3, 6, 9. I Recently got the X3 Bar to do outdoor training. I have been getting sunlight but it is hard in Pittsburgh.

### Lahelada — 2018-03-18T14:57:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-hoepers-journal-looking-for-all-inputs.21059/post-237405>

Welcome doc!
You are managing the light you have pretty well already!
The light you do not have is sun. Could you consider a trip to the beach? Playa del Carmen?

From what you say about needing coffee and the BUN/Creatinine you are very possibly dehydrated. Are you drinking low fluoride water?
Have you been following the story about deuterium and deuterium depleted water here? There may be mileage in that for you. We have also talked about here that supplements may contain a lot of deuterium so they may after all not be your best friend. You might want to look into the tests offered by Cignaturehealth to get an idea what your body's deuterium levels are.

One thing that will help immensely is to do a history of the maternal side of your family. What was your mother and grandmother like,what did they suffer from if anything ?

Finally, as you said your only 29... You can lick this !

### Lahelada — 2018-03-18T19:56:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-hoepers-journal-looking-for-all-inputs.21059/post-237447>

Read the water analysis. Spring is great but as with anything read the fine print.In my country we have a very much hyped mineral water form pristine springs in the middle of a nature reserve , taken care of by vestal Virgins( the latter is my exaggeration to describe the tone of advertising) and I was horrified to find that they have the highest fluoride content. Why? Their water has none so they decided to add it...I was worried about concussion with all the facepalms I gave myself reading that.

If you have your own consulting room ,maybe you can change the lighting to full spectrum lighting? Still not good but better.

Canned seafood is okay but is never ok when the real deal is there but just not convenient. However you are talking to the mother of all "seafood avoiders" . It is possible and would help so much especially as you are in the run up to summer now. Your results depend on how much seafood and good water you get now.

Great that you are taking most of your sun opportunities.

### WalterNL — 2018-03-19T09:10:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-hoepers-journal-looking-for-all-inputs.21059/post-237498>

Your history sounds very comparable to mine. Just keep incorporating more and more of the protocols. Change doesn't happen overnight. In my experience it's incremental, and every day adds up in either the right way or wrong way. Eventually you will have built a solid base and your life turns into the right direction naturally because every decision gets slightly better.

I'd drop all supplements and focus only on sun, water & magnetism. Epi-paleo helps, but at the least make all your food unprocessed. Supplements have a way of messing everything up by disturbing all the intricate feedback loops of our biology. And most of them are ultra-processed and high in deuterium. I still keep some mitochondrial optimizers at hand, but find I don't bother with them unless I go down the wrong way again. Of course this is my n=1.

I try to use supplements from whole-food companies like Standard Process, North American Herbs and Spices, PR Labs, Metagenics, and Orthom anolecular. Usually only the herbals. Of course I try to get everyone to eat healthier and implement the same strategies that I am implementing who are my patients.

# Thread: Nathan Australia's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathan-australias-optimal-journal.21066/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Duck — 2018-03-19T12:22:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathan-australias-optimal-journal.21066/post-237505>

My name is Nathan and I have always lived in Melbourne, Australia. Upon a private consultation with Jack in July 2017, I moved to Cairns in January 2018.

A little background into my Health and Journey:

**January 1992**: I was diagnosed with Bilateral Retinoblastoma, as a baby, at 8 months of age. I received Radiotherapy treatment for this. I have approx... 10% patchy camera vision in both eyes with badly scared retinas. I am classified as Legally Blind.

**2004 – 2006:** I underwent 2 Separate Operations on my teeth due to crowded mouth, removing 8 adult teeth around the back of the mouth.

**December 2011:** By the time I was 19 I had developed quite dense Cataracts in both eyes. In 2011, I underwent Cataract Surgery in my left eye only and they placed an untinted Perspex Hoya Intraocular lens implant. I still have my cataract in my right eye.

**December 2015**: I was diagnosed with Testicular Cancer and an enlarged lymph node in my retroperitoneal space. I underwent a single orchiectomy and 5 rounds of BEP Chemotherapy.

After this experience in 2015/2016 I realised my mitochondria were trashed and I quit my job at The ANZ Bank, 12 Floors up in the air, in a transition metal tower in the middle of Downtown Melbourne and began living a more natural life totally devoted to my health, ‘Pedal to the Metal’. I became a Blue Blocking Maverick, turned my bedroom into a faraday cage, ate Epi-Paleo, undertook months of CT during the Melbourne winter and have seen nearly every sunrise since.

Upon a consultation with Jack in July 2017, he strongly suggested I move up north to the Australian Tropics. I began packing and moved up to Cairns permanently 6 months later. I now drink Qlarivia (DDW) water mixed with Evian Spring Water or Voss Water and eat a great deal of seafood, coconuts, eggs and pork in addition to all I have mentioned above.

I proudly call myself a Jack Kruse Star Fish as I have already helped and will continue to help my family, friends and colleagues in my community to make big shifts in their lives towards slowing down their Heteroplasmy.

**Goals:** Although, I am far from out of the woods, it is my goal to heal my Scared Retinas with the sun and restore my RPE. I aim to heal my abdominal scaring from my orchiectomy to allow the electrical signalling to work over that area again and energise my organs again to make them stronger than ever to remedy all the damage 5 rounds of BEP Chemotherapy did. In Jack’s 2017 Vermont talk, when he mentioned how the Intraocular lens implant blocks UV frequencies reaching the RPE, I was heartbroken, but I still believe that despite this I can find a way to heal my RPE.

I am excited to share my journey with such an amazing group of individuals and welcome any comments or suggestions from Jack and or any member. I am an extremely motivated, passionate young man and take everything in my stride, so don’t hold back if there’s anything that comes to mind.

**Paternal Haplogroup:**

R-Z278

**Maternal Haplotype:**

J1

**Blood Type:**

O+

I'm also interested to hear what health improvements you've seen so far?

### Alper — 2018-03-20T18:29:08+0200

### Jude — 2018-03-21T00:53:39+0200

### Duck — 2018-04-06T06:38:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathan-australias-optimal-journal.21066/post-238820>

> [Earth Monkey said:](/goto/post?id=237570)
>
> Hi Nathan, Respect for the total life turnaround!
> I spent a month in Cairns last winter, was thinking about doing it again and just wondering where you get your seafood and pork from there?
> I'm also interested to hear what health improvements you've seen so far?

Seafood:
Ocean World Seafood Market - 379 Sheridan St, Cairns North
Captain Cook Seafoods - 351 Sheridan St, Cairns North
These two usually have all the best and freshest seafood in Cairns that I have found. Oysters are a little pricy, $25 per dozen, but the rest is affordable. Although this doesn't stop me from buying them every time I go for a swim at the pool accross the road from these 2 places!

Pork:
Edge Hill Butchery - 136 Collins Ave, Edge Hill
They source most of their meat from Tasmania. They also have a large selection of Grass Fed meats.

Health Improvements:
1\. I'm eating much less than I was living in Melbourne. Most days seafood omlette for breakfast and Fish/Pork/Lamb/Beef with veggies or bone broth for dinner. I don't feel the need to snak besides the occasional local coconut, because I can get away with it up here.
2\. More toned muscle mass and lean physique
3\. Better quality sun absorbtion: The sun feels more nurrishing here too, rather than a 'lazer beam' down south, it's a much slower and nicer cok throughout the day.
these were jsut the first 3 that came to mind, but I'll be doing labs at my 6 month mark and report back with scientific results then.

> [Brent Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=237590)
>
> Welcome Nathan , mate what percentage cut are you using with Qlarivia and Evian spring water ?

My Biohack is:
For the first 3 months I'm using 165ml of Qlarivia and 835ml of water. So I only use 1 x 500ml bottle per day.
For the second 3 months I'll use 300ml of Qlarivia and 700ml of water. Then compare the difference and see which one is Optimal for me.

This was my hypothesis based on my situation. If you are curring it differently, I'd be interested to hear what is Optimal for you.
