>
> Oh [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) I try really hard at being appreciative! I want to be grateful for the present so I can fully accept the future when its ready for me. I am a perfectionist, trend toward critical and beat myself up. I watched the movie "The Secret" about manifesting changes in your life hence what I wrote above. I may not have all the things I want or be where I want to be, but I have that picture in mind. I will make it happen!

I have been told that lately - a few times ....I am too critical of myself. I only want to be better....

We are learning so much here - so many life lessons......and I want to suck it all in!

### Christine_L — 2019-06-17T19:11:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-272791>

Update 6/17/19

Just a city girl livin' in a country world...

Came back from a conference in Nashville. I had a great time experiencing a new place, learning a new tool, sampling new people from around the country, buying my cowgirl boots  and bonding with my fam. In a word it was BUSY. **I feel overdone and swollen from the trip.** Maybe add fried in there too. It was EMF soup to the max everyday plus an early flight back home really screwed with me. But literally my feet were swollen for 2 days. That caused a panic in my mind. _(Do I have blood clots, am I going to get a stroke? Plus Fathers Day causes me to reflect on my dad...)_ I CT'd my wrists with cold water and ice, and took cold showers during the trip. Didnt get as much sunlight as I needed but saw the sunrise everyday. Thankfully I napped Friday afternoon back home to help with the mild jet lag (uhh epic party night before!), grounding as much as possible, and drinking mineral water (I only had evian the whole trip). My focus is on grounding, eating oysters and fish, and getting back into my routine.

Finding solace in the routine is magical... Except life is not always routine. What happens when the schedule changes or things don't run on time (as they normally don't)? **_Am I rising to the occasion or letting myself get pushed into the shit hole again?_** Unfortunately I am sorry to tell myself that too often its the later. **_Why do I let myself get nudged in negative ways or ways contrary to my beliefs/wants/desires?_** Am I sabotaging myself in order to be comfortable? If I want A-B-C, how am I making that happen? _**Am I building in an overall positive direction or do I too often let my weaknesses control my future?**_

I learned last week when talking to my sales rep _(random interchange, I know! Sometimes its easier to tell random acquaintances about my observations)_ that I try hard at meeting the "bare minimum". Least effort with maximum result. I'm naturally smart and push things to the limit of what I have to do in order to be successful and "win" at something. I'm not sure if its laziness, procrastination, or me being "too smart for my own good". I think its the later. My abilities are very high, I am a high performer and did well in school and now at work. My old boss/friend used to tell me the same thing, _"you are so fast at doing things you give yourself more time to goof off"_, _"you look like you're doing nothing and yet you've got it all done"_, to "_remember you're the smartest person in the room"_. Even when planning and packing for the trip, this minimalist, no frills, no extra time attitude gave me no extra clothes and no time to relax after the conference ended. I am pushing things to the max and not giving myself enough time.

Am I almost always late for things? Yup. Do I rush around all weekend to fit everything in? Yup. Whats my perfect day? Doing everything! getting everything in! What was my most relaxing day recently? Not. doing. a. damn. thing. Laying outside on a blanket in the sun. How do I normally feel after running around? Fucking tired. _(Thankfully my sleep is great, otherwise I'd be a burnt out mess again with shit cortisol and shit hormones.)_ My mom tells me I never leave myself time for anything. My BF tells me I am always late. (_He loves being early. But_ _Isn't there a 15 minute window for arriving at a party?)_

Well, they're right.

I know I beat myself up the last month and did not do as much as I could have to \*avoid\* the damage. After feeling like shit the past 2 days, I have a renewed sense of focus on getting myself back on track. I'm focusing on limiting my nnEMF exposure, doing more CT, and keeping stricter with food/carb intake. I'm in a good groove with morning sunrise, getting UV light, and light mitigation at night. **I named my phone "Beast" - Put the Beast back in its cage, I tell myself!** The cage is an RF blocking pouch (it does work, tested it with my cornet) My world is too nnEMF toxic, I know this. I have to do a better job of managing everything else I'm doing to limit the damage. I'm taking Jack as an example... **_if your job is killing you, everything else you do has to be on point!_**

### Christine_L — 2019-06-19T00:10:13+0300

Met a fellow mitohacker who lives 10 minutes from my house! Small world! We were the only ones who went outside in the sun during the break!

### drezy — 2019-06-21T02:24:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-273060>

There are lots of ways to work toward this goal.

The overall technique my wife and I use is boring as hell, but works well.

1.  Pack that retirement account like a fat piggy getting ready for slaughter
2.  Defer or simply do not purchase gizmos, doodads, new cars, big houses...
3.  Understand tax code (as you read federal and state tax code do not keep a loaded revolver nearby)

Along with the environment/health improvement the ideas on display in this nook of the internet save you money. You won't be buying a new car, 5 mobile devices, huge TVs, and expensive gaming platforms not even mentioning the supplement game or massive health bills.

If you even think about doing contract work you will want to look into starting an LLC(or another form of small business). I'm slowly shrinking my own footprint and growing my LLC. My LLC was formed in Texas and sat dormant for 3 years. The number of things that are tax deductible surprises me regularly. My wife was recently pissed at a $30 wire transfer business fee. Like a good husband I let her spout on for a while explaining how bad my decision was until I showed her the tax code and the file I keep on deductible costs. I enjoyed the look on her face when she saw a $900 convention cost (potential customer meet and greet) and then she fully grasped "deductible". This is not something I could do as an individual or if I worked for someone else.

### Phosphene — 2019-06-21T04:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-273063>

I like to frame the concept of ‘early retirement’ as simply living your current iteration on this planet as you would without the constraints of a full-time job. It doesn’t mean that you’re lazing about in the beachy waves all day (though my evolving plan includes a considerable amount of that). It means prioritizing what is crucial to YOU and (perhaps, hopefully) your current lover/spouse/family/friends.

Returning to health via Nature (and inspiring others to do the same) is where it’s at for me. I am thoroughly confused about just how long we have left on this planet, but I intend to enjoy it as long as possible, advocate for change where it makes sense, and prepare for the worst without freaking out.

It’s not so hard when we support each other and share some parts of the load.

### JanSz — 2019-06-21T15:41:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-273081>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=273060)
>
> There are lots of ways to work toward this goal.
>
> The overall technique my wife and I use is boring as hell, but works well.
>
>
> 1.  Pack that retirement account like a fat piggy getting ready for slaughter
> 2.  Defer or simply do not purchase gizmos, doodads, new cars, big houses...
> 3.  Understand tax code (as you read federal and state tax code do not keep a loaded revolver nearby)
>
> Along with the environment/health improvement the ideas on display in this nook of the internet save you money. You won't be buying a new car, 5 mobile devices, huge TVs, and expensive gaming platforms not even mentioning the supplement game or massive health bills.

Retire early assumes long periods of time.
Practically that is infinity.
In calculations assume infinity.
In location assume the most stable country in the world. I assumed USA.
It must work under Democrats, Republicans, wars, peace.
Any movement of funds cost, do not move, do not sell.
Financial helpers come with cost, do not use them.
Mutual funds come with cost, do not use them.
Fluctuations, keep 5 better 7 years expenses in cash.
Crashes come with recoveries, timing works all the time until it did not (just once).
When you sick, older, or on extended travel far from ability to change anything, it must work.
.
....

### caroline — 2019-06-22T07:08:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-273109>

The last few days I am obsessed with a family we met at the beach. Mom, Dad and three little boys - the oldest is 7 and then 5yo and 2yo. They have taken the oldest out of school this term and are homeschooling and travelling in their caravan.
Mom and Dad worked for 11 years - 7 days a week and very long hours. They had a cafe and gas station and another business. A guy came along and bought it all - for very good money. They have invested the money in rental properties and are now retired. They must be around 35 but look years younger.

Mom and Dad have a great attitude and the boys are so full of energy - it is staggering to watch them.

They have no iPhones, no iPads, no gizmos of any kind. They all spend their time outside playing football etc. etc. and playing in the ocean. They own a property on the ocean and are really happy there - except for the cold winters.....so they travel north too Queensland whenever they are sick of the cold.

I just can't get these folks out of my head .....they had so much smarts to do what they have done to enjoy the life they have now.

They were smart enuf to work their butt off early on and now can enjoy bringing up their boys and travelling in their caravan whenever they want to.

It hasn't been all work either .....They have already spent a year travelling around Australia ...with a toddler and a baby!

These people are so genuine and lovely and healthy and full of energy and they have left a lasting impression on us.

### JanSz — 2019-06-22T14:28:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-273113>

Whatever income you have, do not borrow, spend half or less, save the other.
At first, for long time, it is mostly about mind set.
.
.
Note. This is not advice for all citizens. Country's conomy would go down the drain if everybody did that.
.

### Marko Pollo — 2019-06-22T20:41:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-273129>

I believe one of the most important concepts of financial and true independence is that we cant predict what the world will be like in the distant future, and so preparing for every contingency is impossible. I would speculate that my grandparents wondered more about which kids would survive the winter than how they were going to pay for diabetes and dementia care. These fears and solutions are good for those selling solutions. Having the confidence in oneslf, that no matter what happens, i can deal with it and take care of my family. Most of the fear and solutions just weigh us down and clutter our heads with unnecessary stresses and worries. Being happy with less is the biggest promotion you can give yourself.

### Christine_L — 2019-06-24T04:29:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-273186>

Update 6/23/19

You guys. Tomorrow is my birthday!

I love celebrating my birthday. Usually about 2 months out I dread getting older and then a week or 2 before the big day its a full on constant celebration in my mind. My boyfriend teases me and says almost everyday is my birthday because I keep asking for treats and gifts from him because "its my birthday" LOL...  He pretends to be me "Cutie its almost my birthday... in 6 months!"  He's very endearing and probably right. He gets my weirdness and likes it. He must be crazy! I love the attention from him because I did not get any positive attention when I was younger. I was too big, too tall, too pale, too quiet, too many pimples, too many curls in my hair ... How much things can change in such a short period of time. This coming from a teenager who was made fun of and **wanted to speak up but could not**. I felt choked, stifled, and unable to fit in. So I stopped fitting in. Now I have no qualms talking or telling people what I think. Once in awhile I will give myself pause and think if I am being annoying or rude. Probably true but I modulate the tone depending on the person. I've gotten better at it. It's truly a craft. They call it emotional intelligence or some shit. Finally. The 6 year old me is back. She has emerged!

Saturday had a "pool" day at the state park I wanted to try for some time and spent hours in the sun with my mom and sis. I'm tan but not tan enough... yet. BF took me out to surf and turf and today we took the beagle for a walk in the woods and made dinner early. He bought me pearl earrings that look absolutely lovely (my birth stone and from oysters! Love that.), white roses, and 100% dark chocolate. Of course I didn't even eat the chocolate he got me last time but I love that he knew to try the one without sugar. He's so cute. It was a relaxing weekend. Tomorrow might get a massage and a pedicure after sitting in the sun all morning. Fam is taking me to happy hour for oysters. Yum.

Besides the glowing 2 paragraphs, I think about the quiet whispers that still haven't been able to shake.

The wounds from my Dad being gone and his physical pains reverberating throughout my neck and head giving me anxiety and stress that somehow I will suffer the same fate as him. Every tinge and soreness is a stroke. _But I do not live his lifestyle and I do not have his labwork. Use logic Christine._

The life I have versus the life I want.

Then I hear my sister...
"Christine do I have to keep the video of you dancing with that guy in Nashville? Because its weird... "
AND
"If she doesn't want to be by herself she has to go to the concert with the guy from KISS"

LOL Cant top that. Goodnight ya'll
​

### caroline — 2019-06-24T05:28:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-273192>

It sounds wonderful \[sort of\] to have endless birth.... days! Isn't that what we are doing here .....we are being reborn, and re energised and entangled and re focused.

We now find love and stimulation and kindness in endless and simple places, everyplace is our oyster because we know how to look for the simple truths of our existence.

YAY US ....for being smart enuf to be here and to live the three legged stool ......at least give it our best shot every single day!

Have the happiest of birthdays every single day Christine...xoxoxo

PS .....I am a little jealous ...you are so young and lovely. It is a real pleasure to read your journal with all your wonderful insights. You are a smart cookie.

How much things can change in such a short period of time. This coming from a teenager who was made fun of and **wanted to speak up but could not**. I felt choked, stifled, and unable to fit in. So I stopped fitting in. Now I have no qualms talking or telling people what I think. Once in awhile I will give myself pause and think if I am being annoying or rude. Probably true but I modulate the tone depending on the person. I've gotten better at it. It's truly a craft. They call it emotional intelligence or some shit. Finally. The 6 year old me is back. She has emerged!
>
> Saturday had a "pool" day at the state park I wanted to try for some time and spent hours in the sun with my mom and sis. I'm tan but not tan enough... yet. BF took me out to surf and turf and today we took the beagle for a walk in the woods and made dinner early. He bought me pearl earrings that look absolutely lovely (my birth stone and from oysters! Love that.), white roses, and 100% dark chocolate. Of course I didn't even eat the chocolate he got me last time but I love that he knew to try the one without sugar. He's so cute. It was a relaxing weekend. Tomorrow might get a massage and a pedicure after sitting in the sun all morning. Fam is taking me to happy hour for oysters. Yum.
>
> Besides the glowing 2 paragraphs, I think about the quiet whispers that still haven't been able to shake.
>
> The wounds from my Dad being gone and his physical pains reverberating throughout my neck and head giving me anxiety and stress that somehow I will suffer the same fate as him. Every tinge and soreness is a stroke. _But I do not live his lifestyle and I do not have his labwork. Use logic Christine._
>
> The life I have versus the life I want.
>
> Then I hear my sister...
> "Christine do I have to keep the video of you dancing with that guy in Nashville? Because its weird... "
> AND
> "If she doesn't want to be by herself she has to go to the concert with the guy from KISS"
>
> LOL Cant top that. Goodnight ya'll
> ​

### Christine_L — 2019-07-25T17:36:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-274987>

Update 7/25/19

Not really doing too great the past 2 weeks. There's so much stress and negative, harmful frequencies hurting my vibrations. Every key is like a blow to my soul. I'm taking tomorrow and Monday off for a long weekend. Going to the beach with my friend. There's much weighing down on me and making me feel stressed out. I'm too busy with things that really don't matter. Small things are adding up and defeating my will to have a good day. Where is my hope, happiness, strength, resolve, commitment? Its not gone its just stifled. What is stifling it? Despair and lack of action. It creates a stagnant body and destroys momentum. What momentum is there in schedules, stress, and routine? Routine is the gear in my bicycle. I cannot move faster if my gears only have capacity for a small change.

There are a lot of things I need to do to fix myself and my life. I want to escape for awhile to recharge. Why do I want to escape? Either to avoid the something or give solitude to my thoughts and heal. What is the remedy? How can I heal if I don't change?

### Christine_L — 2019-07-29T03:10:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-275088>

Update 7/28/19

Things are better. Thanks all for your kind words, songs and love. I am surrounding myself with more music and enjoyment. Had a really wonderful day yesterday. Made the trek down to the beach with my friend, showed her this delicious yummy paleo restaurant down by the beach, got some sun, saw the waves roar, belly laughed and chatted for hours! I'm so thankful for such a wonderful friend. We rocked out to Creed and my friends band later on. Rocking my ra optics of course. Its crazy how people were OK with the lights going bizzerko during the show. But I guess people are OK with a lot of insane things. Its the slow boil really...

Some other observations that are on my checklist:
\-Sunburn on my face yesterday but when I am home, I never get sunburn and have just tanned.
\-Mosquito bite today on my arm which always is in the sun
\-Weight gain
\-Feeling sleepy late in the afternoon today
….SO what's that mean for Christine? More evidence of the slow boil...yup... _What are you going to do about it?_

### Christine_L — 2019-07-29T04:48:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-275092>

part 2
What a Q&A. My mind feels full and validated. The more that you absorb like a sponge and mull things over in your mind away from the clutter of people, stress, work, family, etc. the more you will understand fully what you need to understand.

A+B=C think logically

Take a walk in the sun and think about it. Who is keeping your best interest in their mind? Who will help you and lift you up when things get bad or help you prevent it? Do they get it? What are their flaws? Will those flaws become your flaws? Do they even give a shit. A friend of mine tied the pieces together when she said "Show up for her like she shows up for you". Network everyday.

How can you remediate an environment that cannot be fixed? I am planning and sorting out in my head the potential chess moves before making a strike. I hope there is enough time left for me. I reference the checklist of warning signs. Its a little band-aid on a gaping wound. Pray it will turn out okay and do not to worry. Little steps everyday.

### caroline — 2019-07-29T07:17:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-275094>

Christine - you have so much great stuff ahead of you .......don't ever sell yourself short.

It has to be only about you .....take a critical look around yourself - what do you see?

It really isn't being selfish to take care of you.....it is how it has to be.

I think maybe it is like a jigsaw puzzle.......some pieces fit perfectly and others don't quite fit ....no matter how much you want them too.

We saw Fleetwood Mac two years ago and they were so fantastic. They are touring here again soon - without 2 key members......

### Christine_L — 2019-09-27T00:02:13+0300

9/26/19
I didn't really care about posting. Work has been busy and soul-sucking. I hate that I let it become like \*this\* again. Monotony. Routine. Irradiated. I'm doing my best in the logical way to get a different job and environment. It's taking too much time. Here I am talking about the "sameness". This is why I didnt post.

Continued...

This is somewhat bullshit. I've been busy because I want to be. I over schedule myself and exhaust myself. I need a day to rest, but I don't give it to myself. Why can't I be kind to myself? Because I'm pissed off that I let things become what they are. I knew all along and just let it go. What's the good in knowing if you let it go?

### Christine_L — 2019-10-01T23:07:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-279331>

Update 10/1/19

I read the most recent Relationship Redox blog and didn't find it to be anything that has't been posted online in the past 10 years. Likewise anything I haven't already experienced in my own life. I had difficulties fitting in when I did not physically so there immediately went my deep dive into all things people. I end up with the weirdos, strange-lings, and so-called self imposed outcasts. Some weren't at all necessarily self-aware or are _actually outcasts for a reaso_n. This causes some self-reflection. _**All people are weirdos.**_ The ones who do not fit in and the ones that do. **Everyone has an idiosyncrasy. There is always a gray area.**

Further, losing family members to death or broken relationships really has formed the concrete in the base of my life. **When you understand loss, grieving and hurt and look for the lesson to learn in that pain - that is when you grow.** The lesson sometimes is that you're the asshole. Sometimes the other person is. When its you, you have to do better. When its them, you have a choice to love them anyway or let them be assholes somewhere else.

There's so much more personal hurt in the destructive love or hate families can give each other. It's out of a place of hurt that most people hurt others. _**Why is it that we do not first fix the hurt within ourselves in order to love others more fully?**_

Therefore, what do I make of this new blog series? Hope it goes away quickly or make me look at the old content further? Working on relationships is a lot harder than watching the sunrise everyday, eating a lot of seafood, and reducing nnEMFs. Any of the later things you actually **_do on your own_**_. A_ny of the former things you must do through mastery of people and all of the potentially illogical things we do. Frankly, I've been through this before and it does not pique my interest. Hopefully others are getting more out of it.

### 5G Canary — 2019-10-02T02:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-279360>

> [Christine\_L said:](/goto/post?id=279331)
>
> **There is always a gray area.**

Sounds like you’ve maybe endured a storm Christine...if you are observant you will see the gray might really be gold. Families/relationships are very similar to these gray storms...

Families/relationships have all kinds of storms. Sometimes it’s a major lightning and thunderstorm which hopefully awakens us and other times it’s just a little rain. We like in nature will naturally grow from the rain the storm provides....but only when we are given some sunshine. Families tend to make up all the different colors in the rainbow. Each one is unique, special and important in their own way. Sometimes colors clash with each other and other times they blend well....but we need all these colors to create the rainbow. After a storm it’s important to have a little distance in order to see the rainbow....to appreciate how truly miraculous it is. How the colors are all different but blend and create such a beautiful rainbow. Without a storm there is no growth or rainbow and of course the gold that follows....

**What is the gold? Love...of course!**

### Christine_L — 2019-10-03T23:30:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-279506>

> [MITpowered26 said:](/goto/post?id=279366)
>
> Nice. Will definitely keep this guy on my list of people to read up at the cottage

Fair warning its not light reading!

One of my favorite writings is The Importance of Being Earnest and The Picture of Dorian Gray by Oscar Wilde. Its more of a fun read if you're interested there and its pretty cheap on Amazon.

[https://www.amazon.com/Picture-Dori...+picture+of+dorian+gray,stripbooks,139&sr=1-3](https://www.amazon.com/Picture-Dorian-Gray-Oscar-Wilde/dp/1515190994/ref=sr_1_3?crid=2YZSW06OUM359&keywords=the+picture+of+dorian+gray+by+oscar+wilde&qid=1570134569&s=books&sprefix=the+picture+of+dorian+gray%2Cstripbooks%2C139&sr=1-3)

[https://www.amazon.com/Importance-B...WSTV6QZ2EVZ&psc=1&refRID=K99BJTFT4WSTV6QZ2EVZ](https://www.amazon.com/Importance-Being-Earnest-Oscar-Wilde/dp/1503331741/ref=pd_sbs_14_28?_encoding=UTF8&pd_rd_i=1503331741&pd_rd_r=b4ce8cda-0cb0-4271-93c5-8dfda1f84848&pd_rd_w=7JS8l&pd_rd_wg=0s0fb&pf_rd_p=d66372fe-68a6-48a3-90ec-41d7f64212be&pf_rd_r=K99BJTFT4WSTV6QZ2EVZ&psc=1&refRID=K99BJTFT4WSTV6QZ2EVZ)

### Christine_L — 2019-10-04T00:49:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-279518>

Inspiration from Jessika LeCorre's Instagram

"There was once a businessman who was sitting by the beach in a small Brazilian village.
As he sat, he saw a Brazilian fisherman rowing a small boat towards the shore having caught quite few big fish.
The businessman was impressed and asked the fisherman, “How long does it take you to catch so many fish?”
The fisherman replied, “Oh, just a short while.”
“Then why don’t you stay longer at sea and catch even more?” The businessman was astonished.
“This is enough to feed my whole family,” the fisherman said.
The businessman then asked, “So, what do you do for the rest of the day?”
The fisherman replied, “Well, I usually wake up early in the morning, go out to sea and catch a few fish, then go back and play with my kids. In the afternoon, I take a nap with my wife, and evening comes, I join my buddies in the village for a drink — we play guitar, sing and dance throughout the night.”
The businessman offered a suggestion to the fisherman.
“I am a PhD in business management. I could help you to become a more successful person. From now on, you should spend more time at sea and try to catch as many fish as possible. When you have saved enough money, you could buy a bigger boat and catch even more fish. Soon you will be able to afford to buy more boats, set up your own company, your own production plant for canned food and distribution network. By then, you will have moved out of this village and to Sao Paulo, where you can set up HQ to manage your other branches.”
The fisherman continues, “And after that?”
The businessman laughs heartily, “After that, you can live like a king in your own house, and when the time is right, you can go public and float your shares in the Stock Exchange, and you will be rich.”
The fisherman asks, “And after that?”
The businessman says, “After that, you can finally retire, you can move to a house by the fishing village, wake up early in the morning, catch a few fish, then return home to play with kids, have a nice afternoon nap with your wife, and when evening comes, you can join your buddies for a drink, play the guitar, sing and dance throughout the night!”
The fisherman was puzzled, “Isn’t that what I am doing now?” —Classic Story

### Christine_L — 2019-10-23T04:14:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-281013>

Tomorrow is my Dad's birthday. He's been gone 3 years in November. Going to the cemetary to chat with him and do something in his honor.

I think about where I am and what choices were made to get here. Sometimes it feels like 1 step forward and 2 steps back. What does God have in store for me? Is it all a scam? Am I sabotaging the "plan"? Is my idea of the "plan" the problem?

I've been thinking of my greatgrandmother and grandmother often lately. How did they get where they were? A daughter of a Lady and Gentleman living in a small island in the Carribean. Mita, what happened there? What made your life go that way? That my grandmother isn't raised by her father. My mother loves you so much and held you in high regard, even though the "plans" didnt go your way. Or maybe they did and people let you down. Why is this all I've been thinking about? It is resonance from your soul to mine or the memory of the water from your body to mine? Is this a connection to tell me to turn left instead standing in the center? Or to force me to let go of my presumptions that will ultimately keep me alone? Tell me.

### Christine_L — 2019-10-24T16:27:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-281156>

10/24/19
Finding friends around me to support the journey of growth has been such a blessing. There's nothing like giving a part of yourself in conversation and have the other person return with their own gifts and knowledge. Having soul supporters that want the light, balance and human movement is really encouraging.

Lately I've been listening to a lot of Catholic and Christian run podcasts and its filled up my heart on the day to day. It helps to fill your mind with good things to think about. It draws you back to the straight and narrow. I'm focusing on where I can file my thoughts better and examine where my weakness leads me astray from my goals - sometimes even in something as simple as a food choice.

### Christine_L — 2019-10-29T18:37:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-281471>

Update 10/29
Rainy few days ahead of us here in NJ. I'm soaking up as much outside time as I can. This weekend was beautiful and fun! I movnat-ed outside with my coach, took a long walk with the beagle and visited some friends.

I kept all my plants outside and haven't moved them in yet. My Christmas Cactus will be blooming soon. Its' leaves turned a deep plum color but the buds are growing everyday. Its interesting to watch how they do when exposed to the sun, not the filtered through glass and confusing lights.

I missed the Q&A this weekend. I'm OK with that. Need to catch up on a few things here, but I am enjoying reading everyone's journals. There is a strong community. We need that. Without others, we are alone. it's like my plants being separated from the earth.

### Christine_L — 2019-11-27T00:14:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-282821>

11/26/19
Been away and busy with life - which is good! Focusing on my seasonal eating, light, cold, CT, meeting friends, and keeping consistent for my health. Its hard but worth it.

I don't think we talk about that enough. Not the struggle itself but the fact we are trying, doing, accomplishing. All the small things add up to something greater. I do not always give myself enough credit - a pat on the back so to speak - not in a prideful way but an accomplished way.

There's something to be said about progress, positivity, your good thoughts, and dreams. Having given myself stress beyond belief this past month, reflecting on what the past/present/future holds, praying for both the living and the dead's souls, reorganizing priorities, and then giving myself a break again - I have to say that the changes you make matter. Even if you fall- you MUST ASK for forgiveness from God and yourself. Without this, you are just holding yourself back.

I look at my own progress and from those around me. I look at all of you, my friends who I do not always talk to, or who I neglect to check back on. This is for you. I raise my glass of mineral water to you as well!

For those who did not want anything to do with blue blockers but now want a custom pair!
For those who only wanted carbs to eat but now are buying oysters and salmon for dinner!
For those who who hated the cold but now go outside in 30 degrees in a bathing suit!
For those who bundled up and did not expose an ankle to sunlight but now are basically naked all the time!
For those who only drank tap water but now only drink spring fed live water!
For those who thought they couldn't... but then they did!

Love you all...

### Christine_L — 2019-12-05T22:47:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-283155>

12/5/19
Reading the relationship redox #10.

This quote has struck an old chord.

_"I suffered deeply from relationship ambivalence—this is the agonizing process of feeling uncertain about a romantic partner even while going through the daily motions of a relationship."_

Back in March I felt so strongly in my heart about what Jack was going through. It echoed my own sentiments. It gave me fire. I made a decision to break out and seize my life back. Then the attempt failed. After that disappointment I rationalized myself back to my safe zone, thinking I could fix my problems from within that safety until my situation changed. I thought that plan would be my saving grace. Of course plans are only that. Plans dont make you happier, only actions can free you and thus give you space to grow.

Feelings are beautiful and emotionally charging. They can give us wind in our sails to do things we never thought we could do. Yet when crisis of health strikes, the fleeting feelings cant give us the energy back. The fleeting feelings cant give us the time back. Thats when you need a rock. A fellow human or circle of friends who understand deeper than the happy thoughts and vibes of temporary moments.

Discernment counseling. I think everyone needs it. Maybe not just for couples relationships but for life, friendships, or career.

### caroline — 2019-12-09T00:11:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-283314>

What Jack has been going thru has really affected me too......it is so hard to see people in so much pain who you really care about.

All of this has so many lessons intertwined.

When you take charge of your life there can be so much pain .....but then ....the sun comes out!

When we take responsibility for our own happiness and wellness ......doors close and then wonder of wonders - new doors open.

Why are we so often afraid to take charge? what are we afraid of exactly?

We are addicted to our comfort zone .....no matter how bad, unhealthy and miserable we are.

After my DH passed away I was seeing a therapist to try to make sense of all of it.

This young woman said one thing to me that I will never, ever forget ........

WE ARE ADULTS AND WE MUST ASK FOR WHAT WE NEED. If a partner doesn't get what we need and what is important to us .....we must ask!

### Christine_L — 2020-01-14T23:41:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-284586>

Update 2020

Happy New Year! A few thoughts on my mind lately, in no particular order:

1\. Everything you have or don't have in your life is a choice. Whether you actively or passively let it happen. Everything else is an excuse. You chose it!
2\. Time is a limited resource. Spend it doing things that enrich your being. The things you wanted to do, do them now. The past is over. Give tomorrow's "You" something to be proud of.
3\. Define what taking care of yourself means. Eating right, exercise, getting outside, seeing your friends, skin care, dressing with confidence, getting a haircut, getting a massage, having tea. Don't whither away due to the old "you".
4\. God is important. He is faithful, be faithful to Him. If you want to love God, then do it.
5\. Learn. Take it bit by bit to begin.
6\. Outlets for creativity help feed your soul.
7\. Are you proud of what you are today? If not, do something about it. Don't forget this. Don't let all of the " get through todays" make you comfortable.

### Dr. Jon Motley — 2018-08-20T06:21:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.21879/post-248823>

I have been doing the Leptin Rx protocols/CT for 7 days, been using BlueBlockers and just bought a new pair from Ra Optics. Here is what i have noticed:

1.  Sleeping easier going to bed at 9-10pm instead of 1-2am, mind does not race as much, don't toss and turn as much.
2.  Energy levels have increased, more awake in the morning, no brain fog, thinking clearly.
3.  I used to be very sensitive to noise, angered easily, now none of that! Totally amazing!!!
4.  Feel relaxed, always been a happy person, but just feel better in general, happier.
5.  Body feels tight, like it is shrinking, or like I have worked out even though I haven't
6.  I do not crave the snacks I used to, not hungry after my main meal.
7.  Getting up much earlier to catch the sunrise, when I used to sleep in to 9-10am.
8.  Staff and family notice my belly is getting smaller.

### Dr. Jon Motley — 2018-08-22T07:09:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.21879/post-249096>

So where does one start on this site? There is no reference, and the search bar has not been helpful.

Coffee, fresh brewed (Lavazza), Is it good or bad?

If good, when can you have it? If my routine is Light+grounding, Breakfast, CT.
I like to read while getting the light, and usually have coffee with mapel syrup and butter. cut out the maple syrup and replaced with VCO because not good for LR.

Same thing with Chocolate, 80% dark?

I am currently eating 4 fresh eggs with butter and Fish eggs on top. Ice cold water after (Perrier). Not hungry at all till 5pm, then have sauteed shrimp or muscles.

I read outside and get the sun as it sets, but 30 feet away there is a post with 3 big ass transformers on it... that can't be good. The bright halogen light come on just before the sun goes down, so I move to the back yard.

Try to make it as dark as possible, but family still uses the kitchen light which I changed to amber incandescent, and their cell phones and PC's, complete with 4G wifi. wear my Blueblockers inside, and the bedroom is always dark, so I have been trying to get to bed at 9pm or before. Body is much more relaxed but still do not sleep right away, so I am mindful. waking earlier every day to watch sunrise.

Doing what I can to get myself better, then family. Until then, they are going to use their electronics.

### Dr. Jon Motley — 2018-08-22T09:31:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.21879/post-249102>

OMG, I don't think Pierre is so good for me after meals. maybe too basic for my stomach, but right after I drank it this morning, I been bloated, gave me some acute low back pain and had diarrhea, 6 hours later I am feeling much better, so back to the regular filtered water.

### Dr. Jon Motley — 2018-09-08T07:24:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.21879/post-250514>

Update... 3 weeks into Leptin reset, all good. Then I got an inflatable tub that I could fill with ice, got it to 62 degrees, used it once at around 5pm, then in the morning at 5:45am, stayed in about 5 min, ate breakfast, drank water, got in it again for 8 min. (was unaware that should do the CT after eating/drinking.)

After the first time using CT I had a little diarrhea, which continued for another 48 hours. I lost 2-3 pounds of water. Is any of this normal?

For the first 3 weeks, cold showers and pools (empty stomach), eggs/bacon/butter breakfast, shrimp or sashimi for dinner, coffee with butter/coconut oil/maple syrup with breakfast. No problems whatsoever. lost 8 pounds.

### JanSz — 2018-09-08T17:04:41+0300

Angeles City, Pampanga, Philippines
Latitude 15.156311

You are in tropics.
Closer to equator more carbs
Closer to pole more fat
Generally eat what grows or have been raised locally, what's available now without refrigeration.
\--------------------------------
I think for you that also means coconut/palm oils tear round.

But not for those farther from equator.

# Thread: Janek's road

**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal

### Janek — 2018-08-20T11:31:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janeks-road.21880/post-248826>

Hello

It's always easy to visualize yourself writing all your stories and background, and seeing all these helpful posts, but when it comes to reality it's way harder to start then it seems to be. So it's for me now.

My name is Janek, I'm 25 years old and I'm living in Poland in really poor quantum yield. When did my story start? Like everyone's story, it started 9 months before my birth, but I will go through it in later posts because I'd like to start from now and go to my past.

Since I'm not getting a pension for being a student anymore I can call myself a professional poker player, because it's my source of income. Later I will write more about it, but now all you need to know it's not a gambling, it's like chess, but with a variance, so weaker player in short-term may win against me, but skill-wise and in long run I'm the winner.

In 2013 I worked as a builder's assistant for one month in July. I worked 10-hour a day in sunlight doing physical work. Work was pretty hard and my life was like working, taking a nap, hanging out for an hour or two and go sleep because I had to wake up at 5 am. Even so I felt really good, my mind was clean and I felt happy. A month later I flew to work in Denmark in a plastic factory. I worked on 2 shifts, morning (6am to 2pm) and afternoon (2pm to 10pm). Then my problems appeared. I had trouble to fall asleep, I was feeling really bad, my depression started and I started to google, and google, and google... Because I'm very critical to what I read It started the journey to where I'm now. I worked there for 3 months and go back to Poland for lovely winter. My dopamine was low, I was doing nothing, but playing video games and hanging out with energy vampires (actually my energy was so low I don't know could they drain anything). I was a little better because I didn't work in toxic environment anymore.

In 2015 I moved out from my parents and another dark chapter began. I'm living in block of flats on 9th floor with power line 50m from it. I started to see all my neighbors are in poor health. Many are abusing alcohol, some are kind of mental, so I know this is the worst possible place to live. Especially my town is high in population density. I think one of a problem in this flat was bright artificial light from outside that made my sleeping room bright enough to see everything. I hacked it, but I must change my place because hacking doesn't help much. I made my sleeping room as dark as possible, I'm taking care of my circadian rhythm and pay attention to blue light after dark. I'm doing better because I had a trouble to fall asleep again and sometimes could do it even till 4 am. Right now I'm in better condition but It's far away from being optimal. Every year is same story. In summer I can do well and I have some motivation, but in winter I'm depressed and feel really badly. This winter I was literally playing 12h a day video games. I was trying CT and only if I did it everyday I had a little motivation boost and once I didn't go CT-ing it all collapsed.

This summer I'm doing well and made some money, but I fell quantum yield is lowering (saw falling leaves recently that made me sure about it) and I start to feel worse. That's why I need this journal to measure a change and to keep me on track to change my environment. I want to move probably to Bali at the begining of November otherwise I will see a dark side of life this winter again. And I'm really feared by it.

I will write soon more about me, post my health history and my blood test to do a picture of me, because I'd like to become a member and get help from Dr Kruse. Please let me know what should I post here else.

Greetings
Janek

Your prolactin is high.
Prolactin is a pituitary hormone.
Have you been hit in the head?
Specially the area of the top of your nose and forehead.
You may want to do pituitary MRI.

I do not know of any other than you have already tried.
Parlodel Bromocriptine mesylate (slow)
Cycloset Bromocriptine mesylate (fast)

Easy with Cycloset, may cause low blood sugar.
Take it in the morning but do not forget eating food.
(I did once, not pretty)

### Maureen M MacDonald — 2018-08-31T17:19:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21971/post-249814>

4 years with a functional medicine (IM) doc to deal with menopause. (Age 55) My learning: just taking a bunch of supplements doesn't work and is a huge waste of money and just creates stress to get them all down. (I hate taking pills.) Hormone replacement helps, but these docs are still learning- and know nothing re: methylation cycle issues (COMT++) that can complicate their dosing. My HS-CRP has been high (>4.0) for 4 years despite downing more fish oil than I ever imagined. I finally asked to get a stool/ gut test-- yes leaky gut issues and dysbiosis. More pills to swallow.
So then I found Jack-- had no idea both sun and nnEMF were so important. Love his biology explanations of everything. My stress has been chronically high for at least 10 years (single parent and business owner); working on meditation and just relaxing much more-- not take life so seriously would be good for me. I can think my way to a hot flash or cortisol flush-- training my mind seems more important than any damn supplement I might take.
Leptin Rx makes sense-- very close to Terry Wahls Protocol.
So ready to go with Leptin Rx/ Wahls eating (3cups greens, 3 cups sulphur veggies 3 cups color veggies/berries) -- Paleo Rx at its best.
Last labs: Vit D 45 (had been 27); CRP 5.0; Ferritn 142 (had been 62); HDL 75; LDL 158; Triglycerides 117; Fasting glucose 90; HBA1C 5.3; DHEA 203; Pregnenolone <20. Omega 6:3 ratio in 2014 was 5:1; Reverse T3 11.
Taking desicated thyroid-- last labs: Free T4 1.14; T3 87; Free T3 2.9; Reverse T3 10.
Morning cortisol was high in '15 (8.5) then low in spring '18 (3.3) -- Weight down 10 lbs since 2015-- now 152; BMI 24.0
I get it. Time to reverse all this stuff.
Wish me luck- our modern world and most people's eating habits are not supportive to change; so will look to this group to keep me going. Thanks in advance.

# Thread: Survival Soup on a Budget - The protein shake / soup version
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/survival-soup-on-a-budget-the-protein-shake-soup-version.21973/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### James Lech — 2018-08-31T17:49:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/survival-soup-on-a-budget-the-protein-shake-soup-version.21973/post-249816>

I really found Jack's Survival Soup useful. It is simple.
([https://www.patreon.com/bePatron?c=1083965&rid=1915327&redirect\_uri=/posts/survivors-soup-15707564](https://www.patreon.com/bePatron?c=1083965&rid=1915327&redirect_uri=/posts/survivors-soup-15707564))

But I needed to find a way to make it on a budget. A growing platform but thought the below may be helpful with a ketogenic diet.

The most expensive piece of tech in all of this is a very high powered blender like a Blendtec or Nutribullet Rx.

1.  When using shellfish seafood, the best was hunting for mussels described in my previous thread in the next point.
2.  [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...ering-the-bs-gaining-new-understanding.21516/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/emfis-filtering-the-bs-gaining-new-understanding.21516/)

3.  When not possible, I would look at the supermarket for amazing deals on frozen seafood or thawed "fresh-fish", that is on a sale. Often frozen seafood like shrimp, mussles is pre-cooked. I would use frozen raw or pre-cooked. Throw them into the blender with a little bit of hot water, and blend into a very fine mush. On the blender there would be a soup mix function. Decant and use for later mixing. I would throw-in prawns with shell, head and all. For fish, no need to de-bone as the bones are broken into fine particles.
4.  Add your butter, coconut butter or olive oil depending on your area and season.
5.  Meat and fats. Purchasing bones for cooking is really expensive here per kg. Instead, I found a really good butcher and I use the sawdust. (It is the extract from the cutting machine catch tray). The content is a mixture of all the different meats within the butcher, cuttings from bone and fat. You can cook it with hot water in a pressure cooker or rice cooker for 20-40 minutes. This extracts and melts the fat. I then place this into the blender on soup function. Again, any bone fragments are liquidized. A real bonus is when you get fat sinues etc... P.S. I know the above visually is unappealing but the taste and mitochondria effect is amazing. Decant the mix into a side bowl.
6.  Vegetable soup and herbs. In this, I would place vegetables, especially green (kale is 1/3 the price of spinach here), seaweed/kelp, ginger, spices, herbs, vegetable stock etc.... blend again into a soup function. If you have hearty vegetables like pumpkin or sweet potatoes, I recommend cooking it. Use what works best for your seasonal environment.
7.  Now pour all blended mixes together to get a good consistency.

Now drink/chew drink. The taste is surprisingly great (especially if you use ginger). You get a tone of seafood, fats, proteins and vegetables with water.

I tell people, it is an effective protein shake compared to weigh. You are able to make liters (a gallon/s) of it in under an hour, refrigerate and consume over a couple of days. P.S. don't leave it for too long. It works very well as the big ass breakfast in the morning.

P.S. I re-iterate, the visual, intellectual idea of it is off-putting, but once you understand the science and mito mechanisms, it makes a lot of sense, and again, it is overwhelming how tasty it actually is.

Jack, let me know if you want me to make a quick video post of this?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Laura Cullen-Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laura-cullen-optimal-journal.21982/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Laura C — 2018-09-01T23:41:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laura-cullen-optimal-journal.21982/post-249899>

My Optimal Life Journal for Laura Cullen
Geographic Location: Small town 40 minutes south of Madison, Wisconsin. Planning on moving to Mexico, in two-four years.
Age: 45 years(1972), caucasian female, 166 lbs, 5'7"
Occupation History: Former medic in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, left job to raise my children in 1996
Current Occupation: self employed at home, writer, homemaker, homeschool teacher
Education: Public/Private schools until High School then Homeschooled, some College, EMT Certificate
Kyuki-do martial arts: eligible to test for 1st Dan black belt, Judo- Green Stripe, Juijitsu-rank forthcoming; post black belt test
Parents: Mom(deceased colon cancer aka bowel obstruction secondary to colon cancer) a former RN, later years recovered alcoholic, mitral valve prolapse. German ancestry, immigrated in 1841 to Wisconsin.

Father, alive, former engineer. Abusive, no contact. Super high cholesterol, literally off the charts. Dad's mom, German ancestry. Dad's dad was born in Hungry and came in US in 1930's.

Current problems: menorrhagia(heavy menstrual bleeding during my 3 day cycle), I like to loose about 15 lbs.
Current Supplements: SUN
Current Prescriptions: none, however, I was told the last two physicals to take statins due to my crappy high cholesterol. MY LDL is 211. I suspect my body is making cholesterol to turn into pregnenolone, one for my imbalanced hormones(toxic blue light), and two since I spent the better part of my life living in homes with an abusive dad, then emotionally abusive husband, emotional/physically abusive second husband. So suffice to say, my body was running high on cortisol. Then there is the electrical aspect as well.

Diet/Supplements: Standard american processed food diet until my mid twenties and had children. Proceeded to do an elimination diet for dairy sensitivities. Tried to heal gut via Weston Price food guidelines. After 5 years of examining and living their food dogma, I walked away to seek other answers. I proceeded down Paleo alley, and ended up on the doorstop of Magnesium Morley and his supplement guidelines for the last three years. Yet, again I didn't find the answers, I felt that his approach to supplementing health left me with unanswered questions and I was disenchanted with this all in one approach. I lean towards eating lots of meat, seafood, veggies, and fruits. Grains don't sit well in my stomach as I feel all bloated. I do consume dairy, currently A2 milk from a farm 3 hours north of me. I make my own kefir using A2 milk but usually drink that mostly in winter.

Medical History:
Eyes: high optical pressures reported since I was 7 years old, myopia, near -9.00 in each eye. I wore gas permeable contact wearing between the ages of 15 and 28. Currently wear glasses during day, spend time outdoors without my goggles, and wear blue blockers that I made with BPI tint diamond dye 550, inside when I am looking at tv or computer screens.

Ears: Normal, until recently. Hearing noise at night, maybe early tinnitus(I feel it's nnEMF related). Planning on getting a meter shortly to figure out what nnEMF I need to mitigate.

Heart: Diagnosed with mitral valve prolapse 1996, while pregnant. Experienced palpatations while resting. Short term prescription during pregnancy. No current issues. Mom had mitral valve prolapse too.

Gut health: Misdiganosed with antiperspirant allergy when I was 14, in the 1980's. Later on, a family doctor that diagnosed my firstborn with a dairy sensitivity(she had rashes on her face and creases of her fat folds as a baby, also concurrently diagnosed me with a dairy sensitivity and I stopped dairy consumption for several years during nursing my 1st daughter. My 2nd daughter, almost 17 yrs has a bad gut and can't eat eggs, or hazelnut family ingredients and for a long while just has issues with pain in her belly when she ate. I will add her issues later on and what she is doing in her journal. (look down below for more details on Brooke)

Spine/Skeletal: Spondylolesthesis L3 if memory serves. Chronic pain for 20 years, about 9 rating on a 1-10 pain scale, which I addressed with massive tons of ibuprofen, and chiropractor visits which got me to a 5 on the pain scale. Then I used acupuncture, a sports medicine chiropractor and a sports medicine surgeon who taught me how to use core muscle exercises to strengthen and support my core. I rarely have pain in my lower back now. I spend lots of time outside naked getting morning sun each day and laying out midday exposing my naked body to the sun. I've never had surgery.

Fertility: Moderate, I'd rate it poor(I know I had 4 kids)

Children: 4 (2 girls, 2 boys), short hard labors, vaginal births, 1 miscarriage

I have tracked my fertility through temperatures/cervical position/ an cervical mucus status since I was 20 yrs old.

Daily waking temps below 97.3 regularly in my 20's. Self treated with sea kelp tablets. (another fallout of not getting enough sun and seafood), 5 years spacing between first two children, 3 years before baby #3. Then 9 months spacing before conceiving number 4.

Breastfed all kids, until they weaned themselves at late toddler age. 3 yrs 2 months, 2.5 years, 3 years and 4 years(tandem nursed the boys together).

Menstrual difficulties: Menstruation began at 14 years old. At 37 yrs began having huge heavy clots(size of my hand) and heavier bleeding than normal for me, which is usually heavy. I use a silicone menstrual cup and cloth liners since I am part hippy(minus the weed experience). Vaginal ultrasounds, and doctor consult was negative for any abnormalities or fibroids. The GYN said it was normal for women my age to have heavy periods and that my only option was birth control or a hysterectomy. I chose neither and began to research ways of controlling heavy bleeding on my own. I tried supplements for women like evening primrose oil, vitex(chaste tree berry), magnesium, diffferent magnesium, acupuncture, vit d oil, no eating sugar, or drinking caffeine, using essential oils, eating liver, and nothing worked. Magnesium Morely seemed to think my bleeding was related to my body dumping iron from added iron to processed foods and grains however I haven't eaten breads for over 2 years now and still had the bleeding issue.

### Laura C — 2018-09-01T23:55:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laura-cullen-optimal-journal.21982/post-249900>

January(late) 2018- I was introduced on facebook by a friend who shared one of Dr. Jack's blog posts on deuterium. I went to read the blog post and I just couldn't figure out what deuterium was. I saved the webpage to return to it later. A week later, same friend shared another Dr. Jack post at which point I went to the website and started reading about this Dr. Jack fellow. The website was loaded with information and data and I am a lover of data, so I skimmed many webpages, blogposts and noticed a few guidelines. I ordered Epi-Paleo and Health and Light to be delivered to my local library. I started reading posts on Dr. Jack's facebook page. A couple weeks, later Health and Light by John Nash Ott arrived and I read half the book that morning. The next day, I went outside to get morning sun, in February, in Wisconsin, in my bikini top and my plaid flannel pajama bottoms(no dissing the plaid flannel please). I was surprised to not feel that cold, when the sun's ray were directly on my skin. A few weeks later, we had record high temps of 38 degrees and I was spring cleaning my flower garden in the front yard in shorts and my bikini top and feeling comfortable. I joined as a bronze member since I wanted to read posts on the forum and planned to read every post on the forum by May.

February-2018 Epi-Paleo Rx finally arrived at the library. I started reading it immediately. I was a few chapters into the book and I wanted to scream, cry, shout, throw up the window and yell outside some more at some passerby that I finally, had answers. Instead, I would spout out random paragraphs to whichever child was around to hear me. It was the first time, I felt a medical professional understood the idea behind root cause. It was the first time the puzzle pieces in my head started to make sense.

February 2018- Watch Dr. Jack's Vermont 2017 talk (three times), took notes. Read Light and Health again. Getting outside for morning sun. Read forum posts.
March 2018-Watch Dr. Jack's Vermont 2016 talk, took notes, rewind video, listen again. More notes. Found fish heads at the asian grocery, made bone broth with seafood. Get outside in the morning sun.
April 2018-Ordered Epi Paleo RX from the library, read it again. Ordered BPI tints to make blue blockers.
June 2018-spending more time in the sun, outside. Listening to podcast on Youtube with Dr. Jack while working online at home.
July 2018-Upgraded to gold membership so I can access more podcasts, talks. Ordered kniki swimsuit. Bought Vermont talk 2018, listened 2x. Took notes.
August 2018-I recently listed to Dr. Jack talk about heavy bleeding being a deuterium story in his youtube titled CT Talk 12/17/18 with Jeremy Thomley and Benjamin Smith at 54:30 and that sparked an idea about excess estrogen causing the heavy bleeding.

So to address my heavy bleeding, I eliminate exposure to blue light at all times, cover my skin(thyroid especially) if in stores. Wear blue blockers when exposed to tv, computer work at home, indoor lighting at other locations. My home has amber incandescence, or red lights. I usually don't need the light at night since I go to bed shortly after sunset. I don't eat ice(I was concerned with it being heavy in deuterium). I did eat ice a lot the last two years since I enjoying chewing it. I have sex to orgasm which offloads deuterium, every night. I aim for 2-3 15-20 second orgasms each night as the second and third seem to be more intense so I feel it is more effective at offloading deuterium. And oh hey, since I can't pee standing up I might as well enjoy the multiple orgasm gift. So sex isn't all about deuterium, that would be a lie. I wake just before sunrise and go expose my naked body to the rising sun for about 10-20 minutes. I eat within 30 minutes of waking, usually seafood bone broth(which I make from fish heads) with loads of shrimp with shells/tails on, clams, oysters, seaweed, sea lettuce. Some days I eat eggs and bacon(I'm having issues finding pork belly straight up) and other days if I run out of seafood bone broth I eat raw salmon. Lunch, I drink some A2 milk or flaxmilk or having more seafood broth. Supper is meat, and veggies. I then head to the bathroom and do CT after breakfast, fill up the tub with straight cold water and some ice, I can only get the temp down to 73.8 so I am looking at sticking my chest freezer in the basement for cooler CT. I've been spending time outside with no shoes and in my kniki suit for 2-3 hours per day, as I stain our cedar fence, pull weeds from the flower beds and yard. I did this for 3 weeks straight and noticed that I don't like being inside, I feel anxious and interested in coming up with reasons to be outside the house. I suspect nnEMF is causing my issues or maybe I just feel I need more sun medicine. I plan to purchase a trifield or cornette meter next month to see the areas I will need to mitigate nnEMF.

### countingstarsx — 2018-09-02T20:48:09+0300

Thank you for the "welcomes" Dr. Jack, Jenny, Counting Stars, and for the welcome and B-day wishes Phosphene! I will be posting a more detailed update here soon. Yesterday, I was listening to some Schumann resonance in music and wow, that was amazing to feel in my brain. I think I am going to play that during homeschool time and when we aren't hanging outdoors. I'm deep in the throes of researching a DIY mattress since my daughter needs a full bed. Mattresses aren't made nowadays to be long lasting(another fallout of demand for cheap crap) So I am aiming for 9 inches of rubber latex over magnetico pad, zipped in a wool cover special ordered from diymattress or rubber latex 3 inches over pocket coil springs also zipped in wool custom cover. Since her bedroom is on a floor 1/2 up(we live in a bilevel home where the basement is only half in the ground, 3 bedrooms up, 2 down), I wanted to increase the geomagnetic field for her while she sleeps. Oysters are back for the holiday, so I stocked up on those. The sun has been out consistently in the morning for a few hours during these two blackout months(Nov 30-Jan30) in Wisconsin, so I have been outside during that time since late September and October was so overcast.

And since it is my birthday today, I am now 46 years young, we are eating out at the seafood buffet(they have lots of oysters and sushi for me and other fare for other people) for brunch and then come home and play cards... usually, Sheepshead, Florida Rummy or Zioncheck, all good German pastime activities.

# Thread: It's been 3 years
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-been-3-years.21983/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aerose91 — 2018-09-02T00:48:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-been-3-years.21983/post-249907>

October 2015 i couldn't take how sick i was anymore. I got rid of everything i owned, bought a tent and a few new pieces of clothes, remediated my truck and drove out to the desert to live off the grid and start my doctor's protocols as well as Dr Kruse's.
Due to an untimely sand storm my camp got ruined but there was no way i was giving up only a few months in. Since then i have been living in my truck, relocating all around the country based on altitude, sun spectrum, air quality, EMF levels and any unknown factors that may be effecting me. I just know what areas feel right when i get there.
I have lived in 26 different places in these years, returning to some that felt the best as the seasons allow (Borrego Springs CA, Jenner CA, Seaside FL, Outer Banks).

My circadian rhythms are completely fixed and dead on now. I haven't missed a single sunrise or sunset while being grounded and drinking a glass of spring water in 3 years. Just the other day i was at a gas station and took my shoes off and laid in a little patch of grass by the curb so i could be grounded for sunset.

It took me about a year to build enough tolerance to the sun to not have regular crashes from it, but i did. I'm still at my worst mid day when the sun is strongest and it is hottest but i can tolerate it. Evening, about 2 hours prior to sunset is by far the best time of day for me.

At first cold was a killer- crashed me hard at the slightest amount. I can tolerate small amounts now like a cold dip in the ocean or river mid day on a hot day. Too long in there is still a problem, though. Hot and humid days are by far the worst for me.

My diet has always been good. I keep the seafood high and always have some seaweed and other marine foods around.

I drink only spring water. I have dozens of springs mapped out around the country that i fill up at whenever i pass by.

The only time i wear shoes is to go into a store. I'm on average grounded 8-10 hours a day and when i was camping, i stuck a copper wire in the sand and wrapped the other end around my toe to stay grounded while sleeping.

I'm only on my phone when grounding and preferably in the sun. I have a red light filter on it at all times.

I have a new doctor ive been seeing who just re-did a bunch of labs i had done just before leaving for the desert 3 years ago. My reverse T3 and TSH have been cut in half, my potassium is up and my bun/creatinine ratio went from 16 to 10.5. My manganese and molybdenum went from non existant to normal. My bilirubin for the first time ever is in normal range.

Good signs!

But...

I don't feel any better. Honestly nothing symptom wise has changed. I still have very thick depersonalization/derealization, my exercise intolerance is as bad as it ever was, I'm still equally sensitive to foods and environmental factors. Muscle weakness is unchanged and my memory & confusion issues are the same as they always were since getting encephalitis. I still border on psychosis often and small things like a full moon or over doing it can drop me into it. It's honestly very difficult to live like this.

I think I'm solidly in the lyme/CIRS category. I have tested positive for bartonella but both herbal and antibiotic treatments have failed, though they produced plenty of die off. I did a heavy methylation protocol while out in the desert for over a year and got nowhere with it. I will do infared saunas by zipping up my tent mid day and laying in there till i get a good sweat but i must be careful- if i sweat too much and lose too many electrolytes there will be hell to pay.

My current diagnoses are; toxic encephalopathy, bartonella encephalopathy, mast cell activation, secondary adrenal insufficiency, dysautonomia.

I'm on a bunch of antihistamines and bartonella abx with my current doc but they're not helping much. He suspects i may have a protozoan infection that has never come up on any tests but that is just speculation.

I sort of feel like I'm almost out of ideas now. The effects of everything I've done over the past 3 years haven't been cumulative at all. I can say my chemical sensitivites are noticeably better, but that's really all i got.

I'm not sure what to do next. I feel like there probably isn't a huge, major protocol that I'm missing but I'm all ears for any and all suggestions regardless. Thank you for your time if you've read this far.

### Evan Leck — 2018-09-04T18:57:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-been-3-years.21983/post-250146>

[@Aerose91](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/10465/) While my situation isn't as severe as yours, I understand the frustration that comes from having issues with mast cell activation and histamine intolerance. Since 2014 I have had issues with nocturnal itching. The summer of 2016 I made a big turn around from following Kruse's recommendations. But then I went away to school, and my symptoms came back twice as bad as they ever were. There was a good 15 months where my sleep was absolute trash: itching, waking up in the middle of the night, being anxious about going to bed, etc.

Now, the itchiness is less of a problem, but I think I have some neuro-degeneration from some of the things I did to keep myself going. I'm supposed to be looking for professional work now, but I feel so far away from having the capability to work an intellectual job that I've considered options like moving to North Dakota to work as a fracker.

While my situation is bad, I have improved a lot thanks to some guidance from a guy named Ryan Frisinger. He has an impressive knowledge base that includes biophysics, genetics, mold toxicity, homeopathics, functional medicine, and traumatic therapy.

In my experience, mold is devastating if you have the genetic susceptibility to it and it can't be ameliorated with the typical biophysical modalities.

### Sean Waters — 2018-09-05T17:48:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-been-3-years.21983/post-250245>

Ohhhh wow.... Yes, you guys all need to head to my Journal:

Mast Cell/ Histamine issues have been the topic of the last 3 or 4 pages: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/page-10](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/page-10)

In short:
NREM Sleep is the key to your nocturnal flare ups of histamine from my n=1. It completely overrides that histamine problem for some reason. NREM sleep is vital in T-Cell Clonal Selection.... you know how our immune cells are supposed to NOT attack our tissues? Crazy right, considering how that seems to be the norm these days.

Well NREM sleep has been shown to be key to that process. It stops that Mast Cell degranulation and starts recognising tissues as normal and stops the overreactions.

So most NREM sleep occurs before midnight. So the old wives tale: Every hour before 12 is worth 2. Well, that's actually true from what I've found.

To get to sleep that early, I made myself wake up earlier to increase the Adenosine build up @ 8pm (makes you tired) - but also, completely cut out all inflammatory factors. You need to dramatically lower Cortisol in the latter part of the day to stop any Wakefulness due to a high cortisol. That's a fairly simple task: Eat your carbs in the AM, and Zero carb in the PM with lots of Omega 3 (think fillet of fish with Ghee or coconut oil). This is basically the Leptin Rx and Epi Paleo Rx. "no eating after 5pm etc.".

Sunsets really help with lowering cortisol. Daylight UV exposure lowers Adrenaline. Which, if you have shitty cortisol receptors (inflamed for any reason) and low cortisol you will feel tired in the sun. The Leptin Reset + Epi Paleo Rx are key for this I've found.

But everything you are doing should target lowering inflamm = lowering cortisol = sleeping better. Sleep is 1/3 of every day of our lives, it is so underrated it is a fucking joke. It is where all the Immune Problems stem from in my view (I believe from n=1, not a fact).

I've started taking 2-3000mg of Curcumin, expensive yes @ £100 a month, but it has lowered my inflamm so much that my sleep is just incredible. Libido and Vivid Dreams will come flooding in and Histamine issues will just cease to happen when you get it right.

Message me for any Q's. My journal is a gold mine for this shit. Check it out

Honestly, I've been hacking SSKI for the past month and the results have been mind blowing (Brain Gut 12) - I would have both of those on hand to mitigate any infections that might come your way with the 5G - it sounds like mitophagy might still be \*off\* in your case If it wasn't, you could handle the cold - the methylene blue would help with the mitophagy - since you are doing some amount of fasting, you are making DDW when you burn your own fat - a water fast is my new go to suggestion for anyone - I go 12 hours at night ant 6-8 between breakfast and dinner - that seems to work great for me - IMHO - and really, who am I - the DDW might be a great move for you - it would at least answer the question if you were loaded with deuterium or not - probably you are - and - I think I vaguely remember anyway - you only have to do it for a couple of months - but I think it's $700.00 for those months - but janz is the go to guy for DDW and Scompy has played with it a bit - also, remember, it's been 3 years, but you've experimented with a lot of protocols and if they involved supplements, might not have been the way forward - sometimes, when I experiment I find myself taking 2 steps backward... when you were in these places, how long did you stay? Maybe you didn't stay long enough? The deuterium thing is huge - seafood/eggs/mushrooms (chaga/lions mane for the brain as it crosses the BBB) deplete deuterium -

the other thing is you might be sporting more heteroplasmy/deuterium/inflammation than other people because of your mom's heteroplasmy and her mom's heteroplasmy - like are they of northern european descent? And how was their health? I.e., it might take you a little longer to heal as you might have more deuterium than most - for Lyme, if you still think it's that, you might take cholestyramine which will clear the toxins that are stored in your fat - unless fasting does that too...

Also, I would get some l-serine - it will deplete deuterium and improve your mind - at least it did mine - you could do bone broths - but grass fed bones around here are $18.00 for 6 bones... R they nuts! Adding l-serine I can now remember my dreams, sweat at the proper times and recall stuff that astounds me - not to mention, if you take 3 grams prior to sleep, you have sleep onset within 15 minutes - and sleep is what repairs you - the stuff \*really\* takes the edge off -

The baking soda increases the potential of hydrogen - basically it knocks it up to a higher orbital - thus taking it out of ground state - this does a few things - it increases the bonding - creates an EZ and if you then add UV light, then you increase the size of the EZ - kinda like IR light is a direct hit to the ass end of the mitochondria and so it makes water, UV light then increases the PH of that water - something to that effect anyway - they have done studies with 4th stage kidney disease and all they do is 1tsp of baking soda 3 times a day and they've had reversals - I think this is also why ozone with UV would work better because it pops up the electrons to a higher orbital -

Take care and thanks for dropping by

### Dr. Deb — 2018-09-04T23:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-simply-treading-water.22004/post-250163>

I am Deb. Vibrant, active, mother of 10. Have been APRN since 1983 and realized about 8 years ago that everything I'd learned in school and in religion was "the wrong stuff." I have been playing catch-up ever since--almost frenetically--I might add. Finding Jack seems to be the treasure I found after digging and digging--at least down one of the holes! I am overwhelmed, have ordered 20 books, printed off countless blogs, listened to about 20 you tubes, wear my blue blocker glasses, lie neaked in 43 degree weather in the early am Anchorage sunlight on the deck. I need to figure out how to ground myself so I can stay naked--too many folks on the ground! Can I stick a wire into the earth and then around my wrist or ankle so that I can stay on the deck? The love of my life is not in on it, but does wear the glasses when using computer and some iphone testing and reading. I bought scallops and shrimp and salmon--looking for the oysters, which I have never tried! I work 2 weeks a month in SLC, then return home. I'm trying to figure out how to get all the light I need in AK. Right now, I know very little, so anyone with biohacks or mitohacks, please advise me! Also tryng to figure out if I need to do the whole reset (On chapter 3 Epi-Paleo RX). I am 5'7, 135, A1C 5.0, and up to now, a believer in supplements!!
My 25-OH vitamin D is 71
DHEA-S 41
TC 143
HDL 53
Trigs 63
LDL 76
HS CRP 1.2
BUN 15
Creat. 0.75
BUN/creat ratio 20
Just from what I've heard, I think you will be cringing!
